PT P
PN CN112763443-A; CN112763443-B
TI Carbon dioxide sensor for online detector, has channel provided with light filter for measuring light intensity of calibration gas chamber and measuring gas chamber, where calibration gas chamber is provided with light source and detector.
AU FENG L
   GU J
   MIAO C
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 2021542402
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor has a measuring gas chamber whose side is installed with a calibration gas chamber. The calibration gas chamber seals gas with required concentration, and is connected with a double-channel detector through a corresponding channel. The channel is provided with a light filter for measuring light intensity of the calibration gas chamber and the measuring gas chamber. The calibration gas chamber is provided with a single light source, a detector and an air chamber structure. A baffle is placed between the gas chambers made of light-tight material.
   USE - Carbon dioxide sensor for use in an online detector (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor eliminates drift generated by long-time operation of the sensor without adding an additional calibration device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for realizing calibration operation for a carbon dioxide sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide sensor. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PT P
PN JP2021043794-A
TI Separate type fire detector for use in wide space, e.g., tunnel, has photodetector having two optical filters which transmit infrared lights of different wavelengths from light transmission apparatus and two sensors.
AU SUZUKI K
AE NOHMI BOSAI KK (NOHM-C)
GA 202126368D
AB    NOVELTY - The fire detector (1) has a light transmission apparatus (11) emitting infrared lights of different wavelengths absorbed by carbon dioxide. A photodetector (12) has two optical filters transmitting the respective infrared lights, and two sensors (121,122) outputting respective sensor signals according to light receiving intensity. Using the sensor signals, the concentration of carbon dioxide between the transmission apparatus and photodetector is detected, and used to sense fire.
   USE - Separate type detector for detecting fires in wide space, e.g., tunnel, using infrared light absorption of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Regardless of optical path length between the light transmission apparatus and photodetector, the fire can be detected using concentration of carbon dioxide absorbing infrared light.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the fire detector with receiver. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Fire detector (1)
   Receiver (4)
   Light transmission apparatus (11)
   Photodetector (12)
   Sensors (121,122)

PT P
PN CN112479235-A
TI Preparation of hollow spherical lithium carbonate, involves mixing crude lithium carbonate, and introducing carbon dioxide into lithium-containing slurry, using carbon dioxide to carbonate lithium-containing slurry and reacting.
AU LI Q
   SHA Y
   YANG L
   ZHAO C
   LI N
AE JIANGSU RONGHUI GEN LITHIUM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202130630R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hollow spherical lithium carbonate, involves (S1) mixing crude lithium carbonate produced by the reaction of lithium sulfate solution and sodium carbonate with water to form a uniform lithium-containing slurry, and introducing carbon dioxide into the lithium-containing slurry while stirring at room temperature, using carbon dioxide to carbonate the lithium-containing slurry and react to generate a lithium bicarbonate solution, (S2) passing the lithium bicarbonate solution through the adsorption resin to remove calcium and magnesium to obtain a refined solution of lithium bicarbonate, and (S3) spraying and drying the obtained lithium hydrogen carbonate refined solution.
   USE - Preparation of hollow spherical lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the shortcomings of direct thermal decomposition and agglomeration of lithium bicarbonate and uneven pulverization of lithium carbonate. The lithium carbonate obtained by spray drying has higher purity, more uniform particles, higher activity and reduced process flow, pure lithium carbonate reduces production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solid-liquid ratio of crude lithium carbonate to water is 20:1-10:1, the stirring speed is 200-350 rpm, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 0.2-0.5 L/minute, and the carbon dioxide introduction time is 50-70 minute in the step (S1). The atomization temperature of the lithium bicarbonate refined liquid is 500-650 degrees C, the speed of the peristaltic pump is 50-200 rpm, and the compressed air pressure is 0.2-0.4 MPa in the step (S3). Preferred Components: The adsorption resin is Suqing D402 (RTM: macroporous aminophosphonic resin), Tulsion CH-93 (RTM: ion-exchange resin) and LS-1000 resin in the step (S2).
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC A11-C07; A12-M; E12-A13; E33-G
IP C01D-015/08

TI Calcining kiln for calcining small particle raw material and recycling generated carbon dioxide gas, has furnace body top part with feeding port and bottom with discharging port, carbon dioxide recycling device connected with furnace body.

GA 202113746K
AB    NOVELTY - The kiln has a furnace body whose top part is provided with a feeding port. A bottom part of the furnace body is provided with a discharging port. The furnace body is provided with a heating device. An inner side of the furnace body is provided with a material channel. Top and bottom parts of the material channel are respectively formed into a feeding end and a discharging end. The feeding port is located above the feeding end. The discharging port is located below the discharging end. A carbon dioxide recycling device is connected with the furnace body by a recycling pipeline.
   USE - The calcining kiln is useful for calcining small particle raw material, and recycling generated carbon dioxide gas.

PN CN112211609-A; CN112211609-B
TI Deep carbonate rock oil-gas reservoir heavy acid pressing method involves carrying high-strength proppant to support near-well etching crack through carbon dioxide foam ground cross-linking acid, and reducing loss degree of old crack.
AU JIANG T
   ZHOU J
   JIA W
   LUO P
   WU Z
   SHEN Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM ENG (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202108258B
AB    NOVELTY - Deep carbonate rock oil-gas reservoir heavy acid pressing method involves using carbon dioxide foam ground cross-linking acid to reduce the loss degree of carbonate rock storage layer acid pressure old crack, using two different viscosity and foam content carbon dioxide foam ground cross-linked acid to further increase the original acid corrosion joint length, and carrying out a high-strength proppant to support the near-well etched crack through carbon dioxide foam ground cross-linked acid.
   USE - Deep carbonate rock oil-gas reservoir heavy acid pressing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively increase the length of the original acid etching crack, and improve the supporting strength and flow guiding capability of the new crack, and increase the probability of communicating oil-gas seam hole and time of stable production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The medium proportion of carbon dioxide foam is 30-50 %mass. The low proportion of carbon dioxide foam is 20-30 %mass. The high-proportion carbon dioxide foam is 50 %mass or more, preferably 70 %mass or more.

PN JP2020173144-A
TI Gas detection apparatus for detecting physical quantity e.g. humidity of gas emitted from e.g. motor vehicle has sensor that detects carbon dioxide levels by correcting second signal using the first signal.
AU YUKI F
   ANDO A
   HOSOKAWA T
   AKIMOTO T
   NAKANO H
AE HITACHI AUTOMOTIVE SYSTEMS LTD (HISA-C)
GA 2020A2999F
AB    NOVELTY - The gas detection apparatus (100A) includes a sensor that detects humidity from primary signal based on the heat release of heat generating body (101) in primary temperature. Humidity is also detected from the second signal based on the heat release of the heat generating body in the second temperature. Carbon dioxide levels are detected by correcting the second signal using the primary signal.
   USE - Gas detection apparatus for detecting physical quantity e.g. density, humidity of gases e.g. vehicle suction air, carbon dioxide emitted from motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas detection apparatus can detect both humidity and carbon dioxide concentration accurately.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram which shows the circuit structure of the gas detection apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Gas detection apparatus (100A)
   Heat generating body (101)
   Resistance bridge circuit (102)
   Analog to digital converter (104)
   Gas detector (105)

PT P
PN JP6752328-B1; JP2020183470-A
TI Foaming a polyethylene terephthalate resin involves putting polyethylene terephthalate resin, the chain extender, and the dehydrated reaction product nucleating agent into foaming extruder to melt the polyethylene terephthalate resin.
AU YAMAURA H
   SHIMIZU K
AE NAKAMOTO PACKS KK (NAKA-Non-standard)
GA 2020881144
AB    NOVELTY - Foaming a polyethylene terephthalate (PET) resin involves putting PET resin, the chain extender MB, and the dehydrated reaction product nucleating agent MB into a foaming extruder to melt the PET resin. The nitrogen gas, carbon dioxide gas or a mixed gas of nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas is press-fitted into the mixed molten PET resin while maintaining the critical temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide gas or higher in the middle portion of the foaming extruder. The mixed molten PET resin is extruded from a die, and the mixed molten PET resin is foamed with the nitrogen gas, carbon dioxide gas or the mixed gas.
   USE - Method for foaming a polyethylene terephthalate resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to formed a polyethylene terephthalate resin, that is satisfactory in safety or security, sanitation property, the danger of an explosion, depletion of an ozone layer, etc.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: An extruder having two vent holes is used as the extruder, and the polyethylene terephthalate glycol-modified (PETG) resin or polyethylene resin is sucked and degassed from the vent holes under a high vacuum of 99.99 kilopascal or more in a heated and melted state. The reaction product nucleating agent MB is added so that the weight ratio of sodium benzoate or sodium citrate is 0.01-0.2 wt.%. The intrinsic viscosity (IV value) of the mixed PET resin at the time of foaming is 0.75 deciliter/g or more. A PET resin is kneaded with methyl styrene- (meth) acrylate-glycidyl methacrylate or pyromellitic anhydride having 9-10 epoxy groups/molecule. An ethylene-acrylic acid-glycidyl methacrylate copolymer is added as a compatibilizer between the PET resin and the PE resin.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)
MC A05-E04B; A05-E04C; A08-B04B; A08-M10; A08-P01; A11-B06B; A12-S04A3

AB    NOVELTY - Multi-purpose electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction electrolytic cell, comprises an autonomous module, cathode chamber module (8), anode chamber module (11) and conventional module (13); an ion exchange membrane (10) is set between the cathode chamber module and anode chamber module. PT P
PN CN111218624-A; CN111218624-B
TI Carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant seamless steel pipe comprises carbon, chromium, nickel, niobium, phosphorus, sulfur, nitrogen, iron and unavoidable impurities.
AU WU H
   GU Y
   ZHANG P
   SONG S
   ZHANG Z
   YUAN R
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202051436G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant seamless steel pipe comprises 0.03-0.08 wt.% carbon, 5.50-8.5 wt.% chromium, 0.1-2 wt.% nickel, 0.010-0.055 wt.% niobium, less than or equal to 0.012 wt.% phosphorus, less than or equal to 0.006 wt.% sulfur, less than or equal to 0.010 wt.% nitrogen, and iron and unavoidable impurities (balance amount).
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant seamless steel pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The seamless steel pipe has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant seamless steel pipe, comprising (i) smelting and casting each components into steel ingot, (ii) forging and performing primary annealing the steel ingot to obtain round billet, (iii) performing secondary annealing the round billet to obtain tube blank, (iv) rolling tube blank to obtain barren pipe, and (v) subjecting step (iv) product to heat treatment to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The performance indexes of the carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant seamless steel pipe are: at -20 degrees C to 50 degrees C, the carbon dioxide partial pressure is 0.15-0.3 MPa, chlorine-concentration is 1100-1500 mg/l, the crude oil content is greater than or equal to 25 wt.% and the corrosion rate of seamless steel pipe is less than 0.06 mm/a. In step (ii), the forging is carried out at not less than 150 degrees C and the forged billet is annealed for the first time at 800-900 degrees C. The heat preservation time of the primary annealing is adjusted based on diameter of the forging billet, the adjusted standard is 3-5 minutes/mm, and the cooling rate is less than or equal to 30 degrees C/hour during heat preservation. In step (iii), the specific parameters of the secondary annealing are: the annealing temperature is 650-750 degrees C and the heat preservation time is 3-4 hours. The step (iv) comprises placing tube blank after secondary annealing at 1150-1200 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, sizing and rolling into a barren pipe through a sizing machine or a tensioner using a tapered punch hole. In step (v), the heat treatment comprises quenching and tempering. The quenching process is heat preservation to austenite region water cooling and the tempering process is carried out at 630-750 degrees C and maintaining heat for 60-90 minutes. Preferred Components: The seamless steel pipe has a yield strength of greater than 450 MPa, a tensile strength of greater than 500 MPa and an impact energy of 90 J at -20 degrees C.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); M27 (Ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); P56 (Machine tools (B23P,Q).)
MC M24-D01A; M24-F03; M27-A01; M27-A01C; M27-A01N; M27-A04; M27-A04C; M27-A04N; M27-B04; M27-B04C; M27-B04N; M27-C02; P56-X

TI Megawatt-level supercritical carbon dioxide centripetal turbine device, has blade whose end is fixedly arranged with blade tip part, where another end of blade is arranged with blade root.
AU FENG Y
   XIE Y
   ZHAO J

   ZHANG Q
   YOU X
   JI W
   DU P
AE HARBIN ELECTRIC CO LTD (HRBE-C)
GA 201977062H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a turbine impeller (3) arranged at a center of a volute (1). A periphery of the turbine impeller is provided with a nozzle cascade (2). The turbine impeller is provided with a wheel disk and blades. Two ends of the blade are respectively provided with a blade inlet and a blade outlet. An end of the blade is arranged with a blade tip part. Another end of the blade is arranged with a blade root, where critical Carbon dioxide working medium pressure of the blade outlet is about 8.5MPa. The nozzle cascade is provided with an elliptic leading edge part.

   ADVANTAGE - The device is compact in structure, and has high working efficiency and wide application prospect.

PT P
PN TW201904433-A; TW653938-B1
TI Gas regulated preservation method for baked foods and product thereof capable of slowing down growth of microorganisms and reducing the bacteria count, so as to extend the expiration date of baked foods without using deoxidizer.
AU LIU B
AE UNI-PRESIDENT ENTERPRISES CORP (SNPH-C)
GA 201937147N
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention provides a gas regulated preservation method for baked foods, which comprises: preparing a baked product; and placing the baked product in a packaging container and filling a preservation gas into the packaging container and sealing the packaging container to form a sealed package, wherein the preservation gas is composed of nitrogen and carbon dioxide and the proportion of the carbon dioxide to the preservation gas is 35% to 80% based upon a total amount of nitrogen and carbon dioxide. Furthermore, this invention also comprises a gas regulated preservation sealing package produced by the aforementioned method. By controlling the proportion of carbon dioxide and nitrogen in the sealed environment, growth of microorganisms can be slowed down and the bacteria count can be reduced, so as to extend the expiration date of baked foods without using deoxidizer.
DC D11 (Baking - including bakery products, flour, doughs, bakery ovens, dough transporting and/or handling equipment, pies and pasta, but not flour milling (A21).); Q31 (Packaging processes and equipment (B65B, C)); Q32 (Container/closure types, special packaging features and transit packaging (B65D)); Q34 (Types of goods packaged/bottled/bound/labelled/unpacked (B65B, C, D))

PT P
PN CN109259051-A
TI Removing colloids in xylitol solution involves dissolving calcium oxide in water, preparing lime milk, heating xylitol solution, adding lime milk, introducing carbon dioxide gas, adjusting pH, reacting, filtering and centrifuging.
AU GUO X
   HUANG Q
   ZHANG H
   WEI K
AE ANYANG YUXIN XYLITOL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANYA-Non-standard)
GA 2019115334
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing colloids in xylitol solution using carbon dioxide gas, involves (i) dissolving calcium oxide in water to prepare lime milk, (ii) heating xylitol solution, adding the prepared lime milk to adjust pH of the xylitol solution to obtain mixed solution (A), and (iii) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the mixed solution (A), stirring and fully dissolving, adjusting the pH of the mixed solution (A), carrying out static reaction, filtering and centrifuging.
   USE - The method is useful for removing colloids in xylitol solution using carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, cost-effective and suitable for large-scale production, has obvious impurity removal effect, strong stability and easy operation, and effectively increases the content of colloidal impurities in xylitol solution and reduces the cost of subsequent xylitol purification when compared with traditional activated carbon adsorption or purification of anion and cation exchange resins.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of lime milk in step (i) is 20-40%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (ii), the concentration of xylitol solution is 30-45%. Preferred Method: The heating is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and the pH is 9.5-11. The step (iii) involves stirring at 30-50 rpm for 4-6 hours, and the pH is 6.5-7.5. The static reaction is carried out for 25-40 minutes. The centrifugation is carried out at 6000-8000 rpm for 20-35 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

AU SUN Y

AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 201904845H
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of partially oxidized tin disulfide sheets involves sequentially dissolving dodecylbenzenesulfonate, soluble tin salt and L-cysteine in a mass ratio of (3-5):1:(2-4) and a volume ratio of 5.5:(1-1):4.5, mixing water and glycol in a mixed solvent, carrying out reaction at 150-170 degrees C in a sealed reactor, separating, washing and drying.
   USE - Preparation of partially oxidized tin disulfide sheets used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The partially oxidized tin disulfide sheet has more excellent photocatalytic reduction of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. The highly-efficient photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide is achieved at normal temperature and normal pressure, and the rate of carbon monoxide generation by carbon dioxide under visible light irradiation is about 3 times with respect to unoxidized tin disulfide sheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble tin salt is tin nitrate or tin tetrachloride. Preferred Conditions: The reaction time is 5-15 hours, and the reaction is carried out at 160-16 degrees C. Preferred Process: The application process is carried out by using water as a reducing agent in photocatalytic reduction reaction, and continuously irradiating using visible light.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dodecylbenzenesulfonate is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate.

PN CN108686676-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide methanation catalyst involves adding mixed nitrate solution containing aluminum, copper and ruthenium ions to water with alkaline precipitant, aging and adding volatile organic solvent and reducing agent.
AU HUANG X
   XU B
   WU X
   ZHANG J
   WU L
   ZHU Y
   WANG J
   CAI J
   WEI S
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   RES INST NANJING CHEM IND GROUP (SNPC-C)
GA 2018851309
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of carbon dioxide methanation catalyst involves adding mixed nitrate solution containing aluminum ion, copper ion and ruthenium ion to water with alkaline precipitant, maintaining the solution pH to 7-8, placing above mixture in a microwave oven, adjusting the temperature to 60-90 degrees C, aging for 15-30 minutes, pouring off supernatant of mixture, adding volatile organic solvent and a reducing agent to the remaining wet material, putting the mixture in the microwave oven, adjusting the temperature inside the furnace to 50-100 degrees C, drying for 30-60 minutes, adjusting temperature to 120-300 degrees C, carrying out heat treatment for 60-120 minutes, and molding the remaining solid.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide methanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon dioxide methanation catalyst with high catalytic activity and excellent carbon deposition resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of amount of copper element substance to amount of aluminum element substance in nitrate solution is (0.2-1.0):1. The ratio of amount of ruthenium element substance to amount of aluminum element substance in nitrate solution is (0.005-0.02):1. The mixed nitrate solution contains magnesium ion, lanthanum, zirconium ion, cerium ion and gallium ion. The ratio of total amount of substance to amount of aluminum element is (0.01-0.2):1. Alkaline precipitant is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate and aqueous solution of potassium bicarbonate. The ration of amount of volatile organic solvent to amount of aluminum element in the wet material is (0.01-0.2):1. The reducing agent is oxalic acid. The ratio of amount of reducing agent to the amount of aluminum element in the wet material is (0.01-0.2):1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volatile organic solvent is ethanol, acetone, and diethyl ether.

The component A includes 60-70 pts.                wt. epoxy resin, 0.5-1 pts. wt. graphene and                magnesium oxide mixed powder, 15-20 pts. wt.                silver-coated copper powder, 0.5-1 pts. wt.                auxiliary agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. coupling agent, and                5-7 pts. wt. solvent. The component B includes                20-30 pts. wt. amine curing agent. The graphene and                magnesium oxide mixed powder is a mixed product                obtained through a combustion synthesis reaction,                and the combustion synthesis reaction comprises (1)                mixing nano-magnesium oxide powder and magnesium                powder, and placing in the reactor, (2) evacuating                inside of reactor and filling with carbon dioxide                gas, (3) heating reactant and igniting by tungsten                wire, and initiating the combustion synthesis                reaction of magnesium powder and carbon dioxide,                and (4) directly pulverizing the combustion                synthesis product to obtain the graphene and the                magnesium oxide mixed powder,
   USE - he electromagnetic shielding coating is useful                in electronic information industry, communication                device and electronic product. Can also be used in                aerospace field.
   ADVANTAGE - The electromagnetic shielding coating has low                volume of conductive filler, shielding efficiency                of greater than or equal to 65dB (150MHz to 10GHz),                electrical conductivity of square resistance of                less than or equal to 400 mOmega, and surface                density of less than or equal to 90g/m2; and                realizes the coordination and unity of high                shielding efficiency, electrical conductivity, low                density and construction technology of the                coating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Light broadband efficient electromagnetic                shielding coating comprises component A and                component B. The component A includes 60-70 pts.                wt. epoxy resin, 0.5-1 pts. wt. graphene and                magnesium oxide mixed powder, 15-20 pts. wt.                silver-coated copper powder, 0.5-1 pts. wt.                auxiliary agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. coupling agent, and                5-7 pts. wt. solvent. The component B includes                20-30 pts. wt. amine curing agent. The graphene and                magnesium oxide mixed powder is a mixed product                obtained through a combustion synthesis reaction,                and the combustion synthesis reaction comprises (1)                mixing the nano-magnesium oxide powder and                magnesium powder with a mass ratio of 80-86:10-15                to obtain a reactant, and is placing in the                reactor, (2) evacuating inside of reactor and                filling with carbon dioxide gas, where the pressure                of carbon dioxide gas is 0.5-1MPa, (3) heating                reactant and igniting by tungsten wire, and                initiating the combustion synthesis reaction of                magnesium powder and carbon dioxide, and (4)                directly pulverizing the combustion synthesis                product to obtain the graphene and the magnesium                oxide mixed powder, where the graphene and the                magnesium oxide mixed powder comprise the                nano-magnesium oxide powder of the dilution effect                in the reactant.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing electromagnetic shielding                coating comprising placing 60-70 pts. wt. epoxy                resin, 0.5-1 pts. wt. graphene and magnesium oxide                powder, 15-20 pts. wt. silver-coated copper powder,                0.5 to 1 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, 0.5 to 1 pts.                wt. coupling agent, and 5-7 pts. wt. solvent in a                container and mixing evenly to obtain component A,                providing 20-30 pts. wt. amine curing agent as B                component, and uniformly mixing the component A and                the component B when using.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, dispersing                  the component A in high speed when mixing. In the                  method, dispersing component A without grinding                  medium when mixing.Preferred Components: The particle size of                  the silver-coated copper powder is 10-14 mum. PT P
PN CN114409503-A
TI Preparing 1, 4-butanediol co-produced liquid            ammonia by using coke oven gas, involves pretreating            raw coke oven gas to obtain coke oven gas, and            performing carbon mono oxide conversion reaction on            coke oven gas and water vapor.
AU WU K
   GUO W
   JIN P
   WANG Z
   LI L
   GU X
   LI H
   ZHOU Y
   LI M
AE HENAN JIAHE ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY CO (HENA-Non-standard)
   HENAN PINGMEI SHENMA YUANDONG CHEM ENG (HENA-Non-standard)
   PINGDINGSHAN TIANAN COAL CO LTD (PING-Non-standard)
   CHINA PINGMEI SHENMA ENERGY & CHEM GROUP (CHPI-Non-standard)
GA 2022711774
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1, 4-butanediol (BDO) co-produced                liquid ammonia involves pretreating raw coke oven                gas to obtain coke oven gas, performing CO                conversion reaction on coke oven gas by using a                conversion catalyst at 190-480&#8451; and                pressure of 3-5 MPa, and converting carbon mono                oxide (CO) in the coke oven gas to generate                hydrogen. The coke oven gas, after CO conversion                contains 6-12 vol.% carbon dioxide, and a                decarburization solvent is added for                decarburization reaction, where the content of                carbon dioxide after decarburization is less than                0.1 vol.%. The coke oven gas is subjected to                catalytic reaction under the action of a                methanation catalyst at 350-450&#8451; to                convert CO and carbon dioxide (CO2) in coke oven                gas into methane. The obtained coke oven gas mixed                with methane is dried and dehydrated and liquefied                at (-)165-(-)162&#8451; with a mixed                refrigerant to produce liquid natural gas with a                methane content of more than 98 wt.%.
   USE - Method for preparing 1, 4-butanediol                co-produced liquid ammonia by using coke oven                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes coproduction of 1,                4-butanediol, liquefied natural gas, acetylene and                liquid ammonia, and also improves the production                efficiency, avoids the problems of serious process                pollution and energy consumption by reasonably                utilizing the raw material coke oven gas, and the                three wastes of the process with the national                emission standard, and further realizes energy                conservation and emission reduction of the coke                oven gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 1, 4-butanediol (BDO) co-produced                liquid ammonia involves pretreating raw coke oven                gas to obtain coke oven gas, performing CO                conversion reaction on coke oven gas and water                vapor by using a conversion catalyst at                190-480&#8451; and pressure of 3-5 MPa, and                converting carbon mono oxide (CO) in the coke oven                gas to generate hydrogen. The coke oven gas, after                CO conversion contains 6-12 vol.% carbon dioxide,                and a decarburization solvent is added for                decarburization reaction, where the content of                carbon dioxide after decarburization is less than                0.1 vol.%. The coke oven gas is subjected to                catalytic reaction under the action of a                methanation catalyst at 350-450&#8451; to                convert CO and CO2 in the coke oven gas into                methane. The obtained coke oven gas mixed with                methane is dried and dehydrated and liquefied at                (-)165-(-)162&#8451; with a mixed refrigerant                to produce liquid natural gas with a methane                content of more than 98 wt.%, and the by-products                are high-purity hydrogen and nitrogen. The prepared                liquefied natural gas is mixed and preheated to                650-750&#8451;, then mixed in an acetylene                cracking furnace mixer at 1300-1500&#8451;, and                methane is partially oxidized and cracked to obtain                a cracked gas containing acetylene. The acetylene                is separated from the cracked gas, after                purification, as the raw material for synthesizing                1,4-butanediol BDO, 1,4-butanediol BDO is prepared                by catalytic reaction. The reaction is divided into                two steps, in the first step: acetylene and                formaldehyde are raw materials, and 1,4-butynediol                is generated under the action of acetylene copper                complex catalyst, and in second step:                1,4-butynediol entered in hydrogenation reactor,                and hydrogenation is carried out with hydrogen                under the effect of nickel-based catalyst to                generate crude 1, 4-butanediol product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The shift catalyst is                  BASF K8-11-sulfur-tolerant type shift catalyst.                  Preferred Compositions: The mass ratio of water                  vapor and coke oven gas is 0.4-0.5. The                  decarbonizing solvent is consists of 45-55 wt.%                  nitromethyldiethanolamine, 2-wt.% activating                  agent and 43-53 wt.% water, the activating agent                  is piperazine or imidazole. The methanation                  catalyst is a nickel-based catalyst. The catalyst                  matrix is alumina. The catalyst promoter is                  lanthanum or cerium, and the catalyst contains                  20-30 wt.% nickel oxide, 4-6 wt.% catalyst                  promoter, and 64-76 wt.% alumina. The content of                  each component in the mixed refrigerant is 0-3                  vol.% nitrogen, 20-32 vol.% methane, 34-44 vol.%                  ethylene, 12-20 vol.% propane, 8-15 vol.% butane,                  and 3-8 vol.% pentane. Preferred Conditions: The                  purity of H2 is 95 wt.%, and the purity of N2 is                  5 wt.%. The reaction temperature of the first                  step is 50-75&#8451;, the pressure is                  1.5-2.5MPa, and the second step reaction                  temperature is 120-150&#8451;, the pressure                  is 1.15-25 MPa. The content of 4-butanediol                  obtained through two-step reaction is 32-35 wt.%.                  The specific pre-treatment process is to quench                  the cracked gas containing acetylene with water                  to 35-37&#8451;, and the quenching treatment                  time is 2-3 millisecond, the gas is compressed to                  1-1.5 MPa, and the temperature is controlled at                  75-80&#8451;. Preferred Process: The process                  of separating and purifying the cracked gas after                  pretreatment, includes purification, enrichment                  and desorption treatment processes, preferably                  N-methylpyrrolidone is used to gradually                  selectively adsorb the components in the cracked                  gas to obtain acetylene, and then obtain                  acetylene products through desorption, and                  discharges exhaust gas at the same time. The                  acetylene content in the acetylene product is                  greater than 98.8 vol.%. The acetylene product is                  recycled to the cracked gas as a recycle gas and                  mixed with the cracked gas to form a mixed gas                  with an acetylene content of 11 vol.%. The crude                  1,4-butanediol product is refined, and the crude                  1,4-butanediol products are first separated from                  low-boiling impurities and most of the water to                  obtain 1,4-butanediol solution with a                  concentration of 95 wt.%. The 1, 4-butanediol                  solution with a concentration of 95 wt.% is                  removed and a portion of the high-boiling point                  residue is removed and recovered to obtain                  high-purity 1, 4-butanediol solution, and further                  purification is performed to obtain a finished                  product of 1, 4-butanediol with a final                  concentration of over 99.5 wt.%. The synthetic                  ammonia is prepared by low pressure method, where                  the reaction pressure is 14 MPa, and the                  temperature is 450-500&#8451;, and ammonia                  synthesis is carried out in a synthesis                  column.

   USE - Method for measuring carbon isotope of shale gas storage layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses multi-stage pyrolysis, reduces the separation of complex cheese root and carbonate mineral, avoids the waste acid liquid produced by the preparation of phosphoric acid method carbon dioxide of kerogen and carbonates, and realizes the requirement of environment protection, device structure is simple, the device building cost is relatively small, and it is suitable for field fast building.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Measuring shale gas storage layer carbon isotope involves using shale gas storage layer carbon isotope measuring device by placing the sample to be tested in the quartz boat, dioxide signal, introducing inert gas to the reaction tube to discharge the oxygen in the reaction tube, introducing inert gas for a period of time, making the heating device opposite to the quartz boat, heating the quartz boat at the second decomposition temperature, the second decomposition temperature is greater than the complete decomposition temperature of the carbonate, and obtaining the shale carbonate carbon isotope value according to the detection data of isotope spectrometer, where the shale gas storage layer carbon isotope measuring device comprises a reaction tube, the air inlet end of the reaction tube is used for introducing oxygen or introducing inert gas, quartz boat, for bearing the sample to be tested, placed in the reaction tube, a heating device having a first heating state and a second heating state, when the heating device is located in the first heating state, the heating device is opposite to the quartz boat, and the quartz boat is heated by the first decomposition temperature, when the heating device is located in the second heating state, the heating device is opposite to the quartz boat and the quartz boat is heated by the second decomposition temperature, and an isotope spectrometer one end is connected with the air outlet end of the reaction tube through the connecting pipe, the other end is connected with the tail gas pipe.

AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide reactor comprises device main body (100) placed with soda bicarbonate liquid tank (300) in central position and citric acid liquid tanks (200) at equal interval with soda liquid tank as center. The device main body comprises annular slide rail (110) equidistantly connected at outer side with liquid pumping pipe (118) inserted into bottom end of citric acid liquid tank, and provided with liquid suction port at position of liquid pumping pipe, semicircular limiting groove at inner side at other end of liquid suction port and connecting pipe sliding along inner portion of sliding groove and absorbing inner liquid of citric acid liquid tank; pumping mechanism for pumping inner liquid of connecting pipe into sodium bicarbonate liquid tank; telescopic mechanism (150) having limiting base (151) for placing citric acid liquid tank, and fixed with annular fluted disk (130); and air storage cavity (120) provided with pressure sensor (123).
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor realizes effect of isobaric pressure, accelerates reaction effect of two solutions, can supplement citric acid liquid in time, and prevents problem with citric acid liquid not being replaced in time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide reactor comprises device main body (100) placed with soda bicarbonate liquid tank (300) in central position and citric acid liquid tanks (200) at equal interval with soda liquid tank as center. The device main body further comprises annular slide rail (110) connected through bearing and provided with sliding groove at inner side. The outer side of annular slide rail is equidistantly connected with liquid pumping pipe (118) inserted into bottom end of inner portion of citric acid liquid tank. The annular slide rail at position of liquid pumping pipe is provided with liquid suction port. The inner side of annular slide rail at other end of liquid suction port is provided with semicircular limiting groove. The inner side of annular slide rail is provided with connecting pipe sliding along the inner portion of sliding groove and absorbing inner liquid of citric acid liquid tank. A pumping mechanism comprises pump body for pumping the inner liquid of connecting pipe into inner portion of sodium bicarbonate liquid tank. A telescopic mechanism (150) comprises limiting base (151) for placing citric acid liquid tank, and telescopic rod for driving the limiting base to move up and down. The top portion of telescopic rod is fixed with bottom of annular slide rail. An annular fluted disk (130) is fixed on inner side of telescopic mechanism. The inner side of annular fluted disk is provided with driving motor (132) having output end connected with gear disk (131) meshed with the inner teeth of annular fluted disk. An air storage cavity (120) is arranged on inner top end of device main body. The top of air storage cavity is connected with exhaust pipe (122). The exhaust pipe is provided with control valve (121). The inner portion of air storage cavity is provided with pressure sensor (123).

EA    NOVELTY - The mask has a cover body (1) whose bottom                periphery is provided with an annular pad (2)                integrally formed with the cover body. An endoscope                operation hole pipe (14) is provided with a                negative pressure suction pipe (7). An end of the                negative pressure sucking pipe is located between                an endoscope sealing cap (15) and a four-blade                one-way valve (10). A negative pressure cap (12) is                provided at an opening of the endoscope operating                hole pipe. A breathing pipe is integrally formed at                an end of a cover body. The endoscope is provided                with an annular airbag. The annular airbag is                provided with a one-way inflation valve (6). The                cover body is connected with a tail end of a carbon                dioxide sampling pipe connector (8). A sampling                pipe cap (13) is connected to an end portion of the                carbon dioxide sample tube connector.
   USE - Painless endoscope anesthesia mask for use                during gastrointestinal examination of a                patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The main body structure of the anesthesia mask                is integrally formed without using adhesive, which                has no stimulation and no skin. The mask reduces                the possibility of air leakage, and is safer and                more reliable. The mask ensures that the tightness                of the jointed face is good, the exhaled air of the                patient is prevented from dispersing, and the gas                exhaled by the patient can be sucked out                continuously. The mask performs the main flow type                expiratory carbon dioxide and end inhalation                anesthetic gas concentration monitoring process,                and performs the inhalation anesthesia inducing                determination and adjustment of anesthetic depth                and early discovery patient ventilation                function.

TI Heat recovery system for carbon dioxide trans-critical refrigeration system, has middle-temperature heat exchanger whose output end is connected with input end of middle temperature heating system.
AU CHEN Y

   SI C
   LI F
   MA J
AE HUASHANG INT ENG CO LTD (HUAS-Non-standard)

GA 2021E76255
AB    (CN215002357-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a heat recovery system for carbon dioxide trans-critical refrigeration system, comprising: high temperature heat recovery sub-system and middle temperature heat recovery sub-system The high temperature heat recovery sub-system comprises a high temperature heat exchanger, a first liquid storage tank, a second liquid storage tank and a first heat exchanger, medium temperature heat recovery sub-system a middle temperature heat exchanger, a third liquid storage tank and a second heat exchanger. the heat recycling system can recycle the high quality heat generated by the refrigeration system, and through the high temperature heat exchanger and the medium temperature heat exchanger for multiple heat exchange, so that the heat is fully used, which improves the heat utilization rate of the refrigeration system, avoids energy waste, and the recycled heat can satisfy multiple requirements; The water supply system provided by the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger for multiple times of heating, comprehensive utilization of high temperature heat recovery sub-system and middle temperature heat recovery sub-system can provide more suitable temperature of the available water, further improves the energy utilization rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the heat recovery system for carbon dioxide trans-critical refrigeration system.
   First output end (2)
   Second input end (3)
   Second output end (4)
   High temperature heat exchanger (10)
   Medium temperature heat exchanger (20)
   First liquid storage tank (30)
   Second liquid storage tank (40)
   First heat exchanger (50)
   Third liquid storage tank (60)
   Second heat exchanger (70)
   High temperature water using system (100)
   Ice milling water system (200)
   Water supply system (300)
   Medium-temperature heating system (400)
EA    NOVELTY - The system has a middle-temperature heat exchanger (20) that is used for transmitting a second heat-carrying agent. The second output end of the medium-temperature heat exchanger is connected with the first input end of a second heat exchanger (70). The second output end of the middle temperature heat exchanger is further used for accessing the input end of a middle temperature heating system (400). The output end of a third liquid storage tank (60) is connected with the second input end of the medium temperature heat exchanger. The second input end of the second heat exchanger is used for accessing a water supply system (300). The second output end of the second heat exchanger is connected with the second input end of a first heat exchanger (50). The second output end of the first heat exchanger is used for accessing an ice milling water system (200).
   USE - Heat recovery system for carbon dioxide trans-critical refrigeration system such as remote refrigerator in national fast skating stadium.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat recycling system can recycle the high quality heat generated by the refrigeration system, and through the high temperature heat exchanger and the medium temperature exchanger for multiple heat exchange, so that the heat is fully used, which improves the heat utilization rate of the refrigerating system, avoids energy waste, and the recycled heat can satisfy multiple requirements. The water supply system provided by the first heat exchangers and the second heat exchanges for multiple times of heating, comprehensive utilization of high and middle temperature heat recovery sub-systems can provide more suitable temperature of the available water, further improving the energy utilization rate.

PN CN108251352-A; CN108251352-B
TI Culturing vascular endothelial stem cells by preparing vascular endothelial stem cells by selecting high-quality umbilical cord, culturing by introducing nitrogen, performing cell proliferation curve experiment and identifying stem cells.
AU WU J
   YE H
AE BESTLEY BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEST-Non-standard)
GA 201855328S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing vascular endothelial stem cells, involves selecting high-quality umbilical cord to detect infectious diseases and using umbilical cord for next experiment, if the five indicators of infection are negative, isolating vascular endothelial stem cells, washing the cells twice with phosphate-buffered saline, adding digestive juice, reconstituting the cells by placing in 37 degrees C for 1 minute and performing sub-bottle subculture, subjecting the cells for proliferation curve experiments, cultivating separately under 5% carbon dioxide conditions, digesting the first well of each plate after 24 hours of culture, collecting cells, counting cells, subjecting the cells collected for flow detection for identifying stem cells, collecting p9 cells, centrifuging, resuspending in 1xPBS, counting on cell counter, incubating with CD45 and CD34, directly labeling all antibodies, collecting cells, centrifuging, resuspending in 1xPBS and detecting by flow cytometry.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing vascular endothelial stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient to perform, and promotes proliferation rate of vascular endothelial stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing vascular endothelial stem cells, involves (a1) selecting high-quality umbilical cord to detect infectious diseases and using umbilical cord for next experiment, if the five indicators of infection are negative, (a2) isolating vascular endothelial stem cells by (i) using sterile gloves for removing the umbilical cord, disinfecting the inside of the hemostatic forceps with iodine and ethanol cotton balls, using sterile scissors to cut the umbilical cords at both ends, (ii) inserting the umbilical vein at one end of the umbilical cord, using two thick wires to tie and rinsing the venous cavity to wash the cord blood, (iii) removing the residual liquid in the venous cavity and other end of the umbilical cord is clamped with hemostat, connecting needle in 10 ml syringe, preheating 6-8 ml collagenase (0.1%) at 37 degrees C, injecting and adding in 37 degrees C cord buffer insulation for 6-10 minutes, (iv) absorbing the vein cavity and collagenase digested cell suspension, adding in centrifugal tube with 20 ml cord buffer, rinsing the venous cavity and adding in centrifuge tube and (v) centrifuging at 5000 revolutions per minute, discarding the supernatant and collecting the cells, (a3) preparing culture solution and (a4) resuspending the cells, (b1) culturing resuspended cells by charging nitrogen gas, adjusting oxygen to 5-20% and carbon dioxide to 5%, changing the liquid for every 3 days until the cells grow to 80-90% and (b2) washing the cells obtained in step (b) twice with phosphate-buffered saline, adding digestive juice, reconstituting the cells by placing in 37 degrees C for 1 minute and performing sub-bottle subculture, (c) subjecting the cells obtained in step (b) for proliferation curve experiments, taking vascular endothelial stem cells, inoculating 1x 103 cells/well in six 6-well plates, one for each of the two plates and 4 wells per plate, placing first group under 5% carbon dioxide and second group under 5% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide and third group in 20% oxygen, cultivating separately under 5% carbon dioxide conditions, digesting the first well of each plate after 24 hours of culture, collecting cells, counting cells, culturing for 48 hours and 72 hours and performing the same process after 96 hours separately to draw proliferation curve and (d) subjecting the cells collected in step (b) for flow detection for identifying stem cells, collecting p9 cells, centrifuging, resuspending in 1xphosphate-buffered saline (PBS), counting on cell counter, incubating with cluster of differentiation (CD)45 and CD34, directly labeling all antibodies, collecting cells, centrifuging, resuspending in 1xPBS and detecting by flow cytometry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a1), the five infections comprises hepatitis A, hepatitis B, hepatitis C, syphilis and AIDS. In step (b), the venous cavity is rinsed using 30 ml syringe with a cord buffer. In step (d), the CD34 is less than 5% in all samples.

PT P
PN CN107841764-A; CN107841764-B
TI Reduction of carbon dioxide comprises cathode structure as carbon paper, carbon felt or carbon cloth, where two layers of catalyst layers with different pore sizes are attached to one surface of substrate in sequence, and use as substrate.
AU ZHONG H
   ZHANG H
   QIU Y
   LI X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201825227H
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of carbon dioxide comprises cathode structure as carbon paper, carbon felt or carbon cloth. The two layers of catalyst layers with different pore sizes are attached to one surface of the substrate in sequence, and are sequentially used as a substrate. The large-aperture heteroatoms are doped nanocarbon layer, small pore size metal and heteroatom doped nanocarbon layer. The thickness of the base layer is 100-400 microns, the thickness of the large-aperture heteroatom-doping nanocarbon layer is 50-120 microns, preferably 50-80 microns, and thickness of the small-pore-size metal.
   USE - Reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The reduction of carbon dioxide high selectivity, increases reaction activity, and improves catalyst activity ratio surface area, reduces mass transfer resistance, and improves conversion rate of product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reduction of carbon dioxide comprises cathode structure as carbon paper, carbon felt or carbon cloth. The two layers of catalyst layers with different pore sizes are attached to one surface of the substrate in sequence, and are sequentially used as a substrate. The large-aperture heteroatoms are doped nanocarbon layer, small pore size metal and heteroatom doped nanocarbon layer. The thickness of the base layer is 100-400 microns, the thickness of the large-aperture heteroatom-doping nanocarbon layer is 50-120 microns, preferably 50-80 microns, and thickness of the small-pore-size metal and heteroatom-doped nanocarbon layer is 60-150 microns, preferably 70-100 microns. The large-aperture heteroatom-doped nano-carbon layer is the pore volume that is more than 50 nm-1 microns and the pore volume accounts for 30-60 vol.% total pore volume of large-aperture heteroatom-doped nano-carbon layer. The heteroatom in the large-aperture heteroatom-doped nano-carbon layer and surface area of the doped nano-carbon material is not less than 60 m2/g. The small-pore metal and heteroatom-doped nano-carbon layers refer to pore volume of 0.1-50 nm in total pore volume of small-pore-size metal and heteroatom-doped nano-carbon layer, and 50-90 vol.% total pore volume. The small pore size metal and heteroatom doping surface area of the carbon material in the hetero-nanometer carbon layer is not less than 600 m2/g.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a cathode, which involves:
   (A) processing the base layer is processed by carbon paper, carbon felt, or carbon cloth, for treatment in the air at 200-600 degrees C, and then in ethanol or acetone degreasing treatment through spraying on its surface 0.01-0.5mg/cm2 proton conductive agent or hydrophilic agent;
   (B) selecting proton conductive agent from perfluorosulfonic acid resin or a sulfonation degree of 20-70 wt.% sulfonated polyetheretherketone, and hydrophilic agent is nano-silicon dioxide;
   (C) adding heteroatom-doped nanocarbons to a dispersant is 10-50 times, mixing in an ultrasonic wave to uniform, adding a binder, and continuing to oscillate in ultrasonic waves mixing uniformly, and filtering to obtain slurry A;
   (D) coating the slurry A on the surface of the treat substrate layer, and then, the inert atmosphere is dried in nitrogen and/or argon to obtain a large pore heteroatom doped nanocarbon layer;
   (E) adding dispersant 10-50 times the weight of the heteroatom and metal co-doped catalyst, oscillate and mixing uniformly under ultrasonic wave, and then adding the proton conductor, mixing uniformly under ultrasonic wave and filtering to obtain slurry B; and
   (F) coating the slurry B on a nanoporous carbon layer with a large pore heteroatom doping and then drying under an inert atmosphere of nitrogen and/or argon to obtain cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The large-aperture heteroatom-doped nano-carbon layer is located on the surface of the base layer. The small-pore-size metal and heteroatom-doped carbon layer is located on the surface of a large-aperture heteroatom-doped nano-carbon layer. The base layer is treated carbon paper, carbon felt or carbon cloth. The treatment process is carried out under air at 200-600 degrees C treatment, and then in the ethanol or acetone degreasing treatment, and then spray 0.01-0.5 mg/cm2 proton conductor or hydrophilic agent on the surface. The nanocarbon has a particle size of 10-200 nm, and the heteroatom content in the nanoporous carbon layer with a large pore heteroatom doping is 0.5-20 wt.%. The mass ratio of the proton conductor to the co-doped nanocarbon is 1:3. The particle size of the nanocarbon is 10nm, and content of heteroatoms is 100 nm, small-pore size metal and heteroatom-doped nano carbon layer is 0.2-10 wt.%, and the metal content is 0.01-20 wt.%. The coating method is selected from one of a knife coating, a spraying, a screen printing, a roll pressing and a laser printing.Preferred Components: The proton conductive agent is selected from perfluorosulfonic acid resin or a sulfonation degree of 20-70 wt.% sulfonated polyetheretherketone. The hydrophilic agent is nano-silicon dioxide. The large-aperture heteroatom-doped nano carbon layer is made of heteroatom-doped nano carbon and a binder, where amount of the binder added is 5-20 wt.% heteroatom-doped nano carbon layer. The binder is selected from polytetrafluoroethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride, polyvinyl alcohol, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, polyacrylic acid, fluorosulfonic acid resin or a sulfonation degree of 20-70 wt.% sulfonated polyetheretherketone one or two or more types. The heteroatoms are selected from one or more than one of nitrogen, phosphorus, fluorine, and boron. The small-pore metal and heteroatom-doped carbon layer is made of metal, heteroatom-doped nanocarbon and a proton conductor. The proton conductor is selected from perfluorosulfonic acid resin or a sulfonation degree of 20-70 wt.% sulfonated polyetheretherketone. The metal is selected from one or more of iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, tin, and zinc. The dispersant is selected from one or a mixture of two or more of anhydrous ethanol, ethylene glycol, isopropyl alcohol, and N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF).

TI New iron pyridine carboxylic acid complex useful as catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, prepared by reacting 6-hydroxypyridine-2-carboxylic acid and ferric chloride hexahydrate under solvothermal conditions.
AU XU Q
   LI N
AE UNIV YUNNAN (UNYN-C)
GA 2021707964
AB    NOVELTY - Iron pyridine carboxylic acid complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): is cheap; has excellent catalytic effect. The preparation method: is simple; convenient and easy to implement; and has high product purity and yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Iron pyridine carboxylic acid complex of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use of (I) as a catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises reacting 6-hydroxypyridine-2-carboxylic acid and ferric chloride hexahydrate under solvothermal conditions to obtain yellow massive crystals i.e. final product. Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) dissolving 6-hydroxypyridine-2-carboxylic acid and ferric chloride hexahydrate in a solvent to obtain a mixed solution, (ii) placing the mixed solution obtained in step (i) in a reaction vessel and sealing for solvothermal reaction, completing the reaction, slowly cooling to room temperature to precipitate yellow block crystals and (iii) allowing the yellow block crystals obtained in the washing step (ii) to dry naturally to obtain a single crystal sample of the complex. Preferred Components: (I) belongs to the orthorhombic system, the space group is Pbca, and the unit cell parameters are as given in specification. In step (i), the molar ratio of 6-hydroxypyridine-2-carboxylic acid to ferric chloride hexahydrate is 1:1 and the solvent is a mixed solvent of ethanol and distilled water. Preferred Method: In step step (ii): the reaction temperature of the solvothermal reaction is 120-140 degrees C and the reaction time is 72-96 hours; the cooling rate is 10 degrees C/hour. In step step (iii), the yellow block crystals obtained in step (ii) were washed with ether. The use method is reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.

TI Extracting blast furnace slag active component and carbonation fixed carbon dioxide to produce calcium carbonate comprises e.g. grinding blast furnace slag, mixing with ammonium sulfate, and adding it to reaction kettle.
AU LI Z
   GAO X
   LI C
   WEN Z
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2019133404
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting blast furnace slag active component and carbonation fixed carbon dioxide to produce calcium carbonate comprises (i) grinding blast furnace slag to 37-300 mu m, mixing with ammonium sulfate according to the mass ratio of 1:1-1:5, adding it to reaction kettle, raising the temperature to 250-500 degrees C at 2-6 degrees C/minute, reacting for 0.5-3 hours, stirring at speed rate of 300-500 revolutions/minute, cooling to room temperature, taking out all solid products, and collecting gas product, (ii) dissolving all solid products in deionized water, maintaining liquid-solid ratio of 250:1-750:1, leaching for 1-3 hours, stirring at speed rate of 250-500 revolutions/minute, vacuum filtering eluate under vacuum, where primary filtrate is mixed solution containing iron(III) sulfate, magnesium sulfate and calcium sulfate, and the main component of the primary filter residue is silicon dioxide and unreacted calcium and magnesium silicate, (iii) adding aqueous ammonia solution to primary filtrate.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting blast furnace slag active component and carbonation fixed carbon dioxide to produce calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high efficiency, simple process, low energy consumption, no pollution and high economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting blast furnace slag active component and carbonation fixed carbon dioxide to produce calcium carbonate comprises (i) grinding blast furnace slag to 37-300 mu m, mixing with ammonium sulfate according to the mass ratio of 1:1-1:5, adding it to reaction kettle, raising the temperature to 250-500 degrees C at 2-6 degrees C/minute, reacting for 0.5-3 hours, stirring at speed rate of 300-500 revolutions/minute, cooling to room temperature, taking out all solid products, and collecting gas product, (ii) dissolving all solid products in deionized water, maintaining liquid-solid ratio of 250:1-750:1, leaching for 1-3 hours at 40-80 degrees C, stirring at speed rate of 250-500 revolutions/minute, vacuum filtering eluate under vacuum, where primary filtrate is mixed solution containing iron(III) sulfate, magnesium sulfate and calcium sulfate, and the main component of the primary filter residue is silicon dioxide and unreacted calcium and magnesium silicate, (iii) adding 5:15% aqueous ammonia solution to primary filtrate, adjusting pH to 8-10 to remove iron impurity ions, solid-liquid separating, where the secondary filtrate is mixed solution containing magnesium sulfate and calcium sulfate, and the secondary filter residue is precipitated by ferric oxyhydroxide, and calcining to obtain ferric oxide, (iv) continuously adding ammonia aqueous solution having mass fraction of 5-15% to secondary filtrate, adjusting pH to 10-12 to remove magnesium impurity ions, solid-liquid separating, where the third filtrate is a solution containing calcium sulfate, and the third filter residue is precipitated by magnesium hydroxide, (v) continuously adding ammonia solution with mass fraction of 5-15% to third filtrate, adjusting pH to 12.4-13 to precipitate calcium ion, solid-liquid separating, where the fourth filtrate is sulfuric acid solution, passing gas product collected in the step (i) to obtain ammonium sulfate solution, evaporating and concentrating to obtain ammonium sulfate crystal, which is recycled to the step (i), and precipitating the fourth filtration residue calcium hydroxide for subsequent carbonation reaction, and (vi) dissolving four filter residue in deionized water to form solution having calcium concentration of 0.12-0.28 mol/l, introducing gas containing 10-50% of carbon dioxide mass fraction at 10-50 ml/(min-gsample), reacting at 30-50 degrees C and 0.1-5 MPa for 0.5-2 hours, and filtering and drying the precipitate at 80-105 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (vi), the carbon dioxide-containing gas is the tail gas discharged from the coal-fired furnace, the oil-fired furnace or the gas furnace. Preferred Method: The method comprises determining content of calcium in the primary filter residue obtained in the step (ii) by X-ray fluorescence spectrometry, the calcium extraction rate calculation formula (I) is as given in the specification, where m1 is blast furnace slag quality (g), w1 is calcium mass fraction (%) in blast furnace slag, m2 is primary filter residue mass (g) obtained in the step (ii), w2 is calcium mass fraction (%) in the primary filter residue obtained in the step (ii), determining content of calcium in the precipitate obtained in the step (vi) by X-ray fluorescence spectrometry, the calcium carbonation conversion rate calculation formula (II) is as given in the specification, where m1 is blast furnace slag mass (g), w1 is calcium mass fraction (%) in blast furnace slag, m2 is primary filter residue quality obtained in the step (ii) (g), w2 is calcium mass fraction (%) in the primary filter residue obtained in the step (ii), m3 is mass of the precipitate obtained in the step (vi) (g), and w3 is calcium mass fraction (%) in the precipitate obtained in the step (vi).

PN CN113956905-A
TI Preparing ammonia and CNG from coke oven gas used in chemical industry, metallurgy, and environmental protection, involves compressing coke oven gas, and converting carbon monoxide into hydrogen and carbon dioxide through conversion gas.
AU TIAN L
   WANG J
   ZHANG Q
   ZHONG J
   YANG H
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 2022203015
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ammonia and CNG from coke oven gas involves compressing coke oven gas to 0.4-1.8 MPa, converting carbon monoxide into hydrogen and carbon dioxide through conversion gas. The concentration of the carbon monoxide in the conversion gas is less than or equal to0.5 wt.%. The hydrogen sulfide, organic sulfur, carbon dioxide, CnHm and portion of methane are removed through pressure swing adsorption, namely decarburization gas. The sum of the carbon monoxide and the carbon dioxide in the decarburization gas is less than or equal to1.0%. The decarburization gas is sent for fine desulfurization and purification until the total sulfur is less than or equal to0.1 ppm to obtain regenerated gas with the heat value within plus minus5 % of the coke oven gas. The regenerated gas is refluxed into the coke oven fuel gas. The fine degasification is sent into a methanation purification device to remove carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide until the sum is less than or equal to10 ppm, and the obtained product is called methanated gas.
   USE - Method for preparing ammonia and CNG from coke oven gas used in chemical industry, metallurgy, energy conservation and environmental protection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces waste discharge and resource consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ammonia and CNG from coke oven gas involves compressing coke oven gas to 0.4-1.8 MPa, converting carbon monoxide into hydrogen and carbon dioxide through conversion gas. The concentration of the carbon monoxide in the conversion gas is less than or equal to0.5 wt.%. The hydrogen sulfide, organic sulfur, carbon dioxide, CnHm and portion of methane are removed through pressure swing adsorption, namely decarburization gas. The sum of the carbon monoxide and the carbon dioxide in the decarburization gas is less than or equal to1.0%. The decarburization gas is sent for fine desulfurization and purification until the total sulfur is less than or equal to0.1 ppm to obtain regenerated gas with the heat value within plus minus5 % of the coke oven gas. The regenerated gas is refluxed into the coke oven fuel gas. The fine degasification is sent into a methanation purification device to remove carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide until the sum is less than or equal to10 ppm, and the obtained product is called methanated gas. The methanated gas is send to a pressure swing adsorption purification device to prepare CNG with the methane concentration of more than 95 %, where the concentration and the heat value of the CNG toxic gas meet the fuel gas standard, and simultaneously obtain crude degasification with the methane concentration of less than or equal to8 %. The coarse degasification enters a pressure swing adsorption fine degassing device to separate methane until the concentration is less than or equal to0.5 %. The coarse degasification is called as fine degasification and sent to an ammonia synthesis system, and the desorbed gas is compressed and sent to the coarse degassing device for cyclic separation.

Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN KR1986642-B1; WO2020045945-A1
TI Fuel cell system comprises reformer to produce hydrogen-rich reformed gas, hydrogen generator to generate hydrogen from carbon dioxide generated from reformer as raw material and fuel cell to receive hydrogen generated in hydrogen generator.
AU KIM G T
   KIM C M
   KIM J W
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 2019512071
AB    NOVELTY - Fuel cell system (1000) comprises: a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as a by-product; a hydrogen generator (1100) for generating hydrogen from carbon dioxide generated from the reformer as a raw material; and a fuel cell that receives hydrogen generated in the hydrogen generator and reforming gas produced by the reformer as fuel. The hydrogen generator comprises: a water-based electrolyte (1115) which is contained in a reaction space (1111) and is an alkaline solution containing potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide; a reducing electrode at least partially immersed in the aqueous electrolyte, and an oxidizing electrode at least partially immersed in the aqueous electrolyte in the reaction space; and a negative electrode electrically connected to the reduction electrode by applying direct current to hydrogen generator, and a power source having an anode electrically connected to oxidation electrode.
   USE - Used as fuel cell system.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel cell system generates hydrogen using renewable energy e.g. a solar cell, with the removal of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fuel cell system (1000) comprises: a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as a by-product; a hydrogen generator (1100) for generating hydrogen from carbon dioxide generated from the reformer as a raw material; and a fuel cell that receives hydrogen generated in the hydrogen generator and reforming gas produced by the reformer as fuel. The hydrogen generator comprises: a water-based electrolyte (1115) which is contained in a reaction space (1111) and is an alkaline solution containing potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide; a reducing electrode at least partially immersed in the aqueous electrolyte, and an oxidizing electrode at least partially immersed in the aqueous electrolyte in the reaction space; and a negative electrode electrically connected to the reduction electrode by applying direct current to the hydrogen generator, and a power source having an anode electrically connected to the oxidation electrode. The direct current power is applied to the hydrogen generator by the power source, and the carbon dioxide gas generated in the reformer is introduced into the aqueous electrolyte. The hydrogen ions and bicarbonate ions are generated by the reaction of the water of the aqueous electrolyte and the carbon dioxide gas. The hydrogen ions and the electrons of the reducing electrode are combined to generate hydrogen gas. The power source is a solar cell. In the daytime, hydrogen produced in the reformer is supplied as fuel to the fuel cell, hydrogen produced in the hydrogen generator is stored, and carbon dioxide generated in the reformer is supplied to the hydrogen generator, At night, the reformer does not operate and hydrogen stored in the daytime is supplied to the fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the fuel cell system.
   Fuel cell system (1000)
   Hydrogen generator (1100)
   Reaction vessel (1110)
   Reaction space (1111)
   Water-based electrolyte (1115)

TI Carbon dioxide safety phase change contaminant            separation device, has pressure release pipe connected            with top opening component for driving air release            valve to open, and liquid injection pipe equipped with            electromagnetic valve.
AU XIA J
   DING C
   DING S
   CHEN X
AE ANHUI YOUMING BAFANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2022104149
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a main phase change container                (8) whose side is connected with a secondary phase                change container (6). A pressure relief container                (3) is connected with a pressure release pipe (5).                An inner portion of a constant temperature medium                box (13) is provided with a heating pipe (14). An                end of a top portion of the constant temperature                medium box is connected with an extending pipe. An                electric guide pipe (11) and a mechanical force                guide pipe (12) are respectively with an                electromagnetic valve (1) and a safety valve (4).                The pressure release pipe is connected with a top                opening component for driving an air release valve                to open. A liquid injection pipe is equipped with                an electromagnetic valve. The main air outlet pipe                downstream of the liquid injection pipe is wound                with a heating ring.
   USE - Carbon dioxide safety phase change contaminant                separation device for use in a cement production                equipment technology field. Can also be used for a                cement industry, a production process and a                production environment of cement dust.
   ADVANTAGE - The device utilizes the liquid carbon dioxide                to change into gaseous state to boost the pressure                for cement production equipment descaling, so that                the carbon dioxide is not combustible and does not                support the characteristics of combustion for the                production environment of cement dust. The                characteristics of rapid phase transition under                temperature environment can shorten the energy                storage time. The device carries out the phase                transition of liquid carbon dioxide by using                multiple primary phase change vessels and secondary                phase change vessels that are connected in sequence                with the same structure in safe and efficient                manner. The device utilizes the stable phase change                temperature environment of the constant-temperature                medium box, thus limiting the influence of                temperature to the carbon dioxide phase change,                reducing the influence to carbon dioxide leakage,                increasing the use safety, and setting the                phase-change energy recovery component that changes                the traditional single phase change container using                limit.
