PN WO2021038374-A1; CA3143916-A1; EP4021873-A1; US2022251004-A1; BR112022003390-A2
TI Coproduction of ethanol and acetaldehyde during            production of ethylene, involves contacting ethane with            oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst, and discharging            reactor effluent containing ethylene, ethanol and            acetaldehyde.
AU SIMANZHENKOV V
   GOODARZNIA S
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 202122221G
AB    NOVELTY - Coproduction of ethanol and acetaldehyde                involves contacting ethane with an oxidative                dehydrogenation (ODH) catalyst in presence of                oxygen in a reactor (104) to dehydrogenate ethane                to ethylene and to form carbon dioxide and water,                discharging a reactor effluent (E1) from the                reactor (104) to a reactor (106), contacting the                reactor effluent (E2) with an ODH catalyst in the                reactor (106) to form ethanol and acetaldehyde, and                discharging a reactor effluent (E2) from the                reactor (106). The reactor effluent (E2) comprises                ethylene, ethanol, and acetaldehyde. The reactor                effluent (E1) comprises ethylene, carbon dioxide,                and water.
   USE - Coproduction of ethanol and acetaldehyde                during production of ethylene (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) coproduction in ODH system (100), which                involves feeding ethane, oxygen and a diluent to                the reactor (104), contacting ethane with the ODH                catalyst in the reactor (104) to produce ethylene,                acetic acid, carbon dioxide, and water, discharging                from reactor (104) of the reactor effluent (E1)                comprising ethylene, acetic acid, carbon dioxide                and water, removing oxygen from the reactor                effluent (E2), contacting the reactor effluent (E1)                with ODH catalyst in the reactor (106) to produce                ethanol and acetaldehyde, and discharging from the                reactor (106) of the reactor effluent (E2)                comprising ethylene, ethanol, acetaldehyde, acetic                acid, carbon dioxide and water;
   (2) coproduction in conversion of ethane to                ethylene, which involves contacting ethane with an                oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) catalyst in                presence of oxygen at a temperature of less than                450 degrees C in the ODH reactor (104) to generate                ethylene, acetic acid, carbon dioxide and water,                discharging from the ODH reactor (104) of the                reactor effluent (E1) comprising ethylene, acetic                acid, carbon dioxide, water and unreacted ethane                through an oxygen-separation vessel to a ODH                reactor (106), contacting the reactor effluent (E1)                with an ODH catalyst at a temperature of less than                450 degrees C in the ODH reactor (106) to generate                ethanol and acetaldehyde, and discharging from the                reactor (106) of the reactor effluent (E2) to a                separation column; and
   (3) ODH coproduction system, which consists of                the reactor (104) comprising the oxidative                dehydrogenation (ODH) catalyst (108) to                dehydrogenate ethane into ethylene and form carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide and water, and the reactor                (106) comprising the ODH catalyst (110) to form                ethanol and acetaldehyde from reactor effluent (E1)                and discharging the reactor effluent (E2)                comprising ethylene, ethanol and acetaldehyde as                coproducts.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the ODH                coproduction system.
   System (100)
   Reactor (104,106)
   Catalyst (108,110)
   Feed (112)
   Effluent (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ODH catalyst in                  the reactor (104) and the reactor (106) comprises                  a mixed metal oxide comprising                  molybdenum-vanadium-tellurium-niobium-palladium                  oxide of formula: MoaVbTecNbdPdeOf (I), where a                  is 1, b is 0.01-1.0, c is 0.01-1.0, d is                  0.01-1.0, e is 0.00-0.10, and f is at least the                  valence state of the corresponding elements in                  the catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  removing oxygen from the reactor effluent (E1)                  upstream of the reactor (106), contacting the                  ethane with the ODH catalyst in the presence of                  oxygen in the reactor (104) forms carbon monoxide                  and acetic acid, where the reactor effluent (E1)                  comprising carbon monoxide, acetic acid and                  unreacted ethane, maintaining an operating                  temperature of the reactor (104) and an operating                  temperature of the reactor (106) at less than 450                  degrees C, where the reactor effluent (E2)                  comprising carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and                  water, contacting the reactor effluent (E1) with                  the ODH catalyst in the reactor (106) forms                  acetic acid, where the reactor effluent (E2)                  comprising acetic acid, adding carbon dioxide to                  the reactor (106) to favor production of                  acetaldehyde over production of ethanol,                  separating the reactor effluent (E2) into a                  liquid stream and a gas stream, where the liquid                  stream comprising ethanol, acetaldehyde, acetic                  acid and water and the gas stream comprising                  ethylene, ethane, carbon dioxide and carbon                  monoxide, separating the reactor effluent (E2)                  into the gas stream and the liquid stream in a                  packed column, discharging the gas stream                  overhead from the packed column, discharging the                  liquid stream from a bottom portion of the packed                  column, and diverting a portion of the reactor                  effluent (E1) to the packed column instead of to                  the reactor (106). Preferred Condition: The                  operating temperature of the reactor (104) and                  the reactor (106) are less than 450 degrees                  C.

PN IN201744027143-A; CN108012693-A; VN57304-A; RU2017132173-A; RU2744297-C2
TI Device for increasing yield of crops e.g. dry crops, has time controller which controls opening and closing of electromagnetic transfer valve through customizing length of time.
AU LUAN J
   LUAN Y
AE SHENYANG JINGQUAN GAS CARBON TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENYANG JINGQUAN GAS CARBON TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENYANG JINGQUAN GAS CARBON TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201836044B
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a base station with a storage tank, a gasifier, and an output device. The storage tank has a carbon dioxide storage tank (1), a sulfur dioxide storage tank (2), nitrogen ammonia nitrogen or oxynitride storage tank (3) and a marsh gas natural gas methane liquefied gas storage tank (4). A time controller controls opening and closing of an electromagnetic transfer valve through customizing the length of time such that the gas-water transfer pipeline (21) and the gas-gas transfer pipeline are communicated with the gas-fertilizer applying pipe network (23).
   USE - Device for increasing yield of crops such as dry crops, greenhouse agricultural crops and paddy field crops through applying and adjusting concentration of carbon dioxide. Uses include but are not limited to high-stem crops e.g. sugarcanes, herbal medicine crops e.g. wheats, and soybeans, paddy and aquatic crops e.g. rice, vine plants e.g. grape trees, seasonal and anti-season vegetable economic crops e.g. cucumbers, eggplants, kidney beans, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has obvious yield-increasing, disease-resistant and sterilizing effects. The device and the system are safe, simple and convenient to operate. The purpose of high yield of crops is achieved. The carbon dioxide is obtained by recycling carbon dioxide generated in the process of industrial production so that the lot of carbon emission can be greatly reduced, and ecological restoration of lands is facilitated. The storage and transportation and usage manners can greatly reduce use cost so the device and the system are suitable for use and popularization on a large scale. The carbon dioxide and hydrogen are mixed with water to form carbonic water to prevent and reduce escape and drift of gaseous carbon dioxide or a mixed gas-carbon fertilizer and prevent from being blown away by wind so as to maintain an optimal carbon concentration of 600-2000 ppm required for plant photosynthesis, and to improve the photosynthesis rate to allow crops to be efficient and yield-increasing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for increasing yield of crops based on device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of the device for increasing yield of crops through applying and adjusting concentration of carbon dioxide.
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Sulfur dioxide storage tank (2)
   Nitrogen ammonia nitrogen or oxynitride storage tank (3)
   Marsh gas natural gas methane liquefied gas storage tank (4)
   Gas-water transfer pipeline (21)
   Gas-fertilizer applying pipe network (23)

TI Device for preparing synthesis gas by using solar energy to reform methane/carbon dioxide, comprises solar energy gathering device, photoelectric conversion device, solar automatic tracking device, reforming reaction chamber, and temperature control heater.

GA 2021C9076Y
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a photoelectric conversion device (6) fixed on a solar energy gathering device, vertical to a lens. The device has a solar energy automatic tracking device, a reforming reaction chamber, a gas buffer tank (17) and a heat exchanger (18). A transverse rotator (8) and a longitudinal rotator (9) are fixed at a bottom portion of a supporting frame. A temperature control heater is provided with a temperature sensor (13) and a heating resistance wire pipe. The temperature sensor measures and monitors temperature of heat conducting oil in a reforming reaction chamber. A heat exchanger heat inlet gas is synthesis gas prepared by the reforming reaction room. A mixed gas is entered into the heating exchanger and the synthesis gas to obtain pre-heated mixed gas to enter the reforming reactor to react. The solar energy gathering device is composed of a Fresnel lens (1), an annular stainless steel sheet (2), and a supporting frame (3).
   USE - Device for preparing synthesis gas by using solar energy to reform methane/carbon dioxide (claimed) used for downstream chemicals and fuel liquid as methanol, dimethyl ether, olefin, aromatic hydrocarbon, gasoline and diesel oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses free clean solar energy resource, provides high grade heat and high temperature reaction condition needed by methane/carbon dioxide reforming reaction process, realizing green conversion utilization of greenhouse gas methane and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing synthesis gas by using solar energy to reform methane/carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a device for producing synthesis gas by reforming methane/carbon dioxide using solar energy.

TI Syngas stream sweetening involves supplying syngas            stream to nonselective absorption unit, and then            separating syngas stream in nonselective absorption            unit to obtain overhead syngas stream and acid gas            stream.
AU VAIDYA M M
   DUVAL S A
   HAMAD F
   BAKER R
   MERKEL T

   HUANG I
   BAHAMDAN A A
   AL-OTAIBI F D

   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)

   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)

   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)

GA 2020B3372T
AB    NOVELTY - Syngas stream sweetening involves supplying a                syngas stream (401) to a nonselective absorption                unit, and then separating the syngas stream in the                nonselective absorption unit to obtain an overhead                syngas stream and an acid gas stream (412). The                syngas stream comprises syngas, carbon dioxide, and                hydrogen sulfide. The acid gas stream is introduced                to a membrane separation unit (420), where the acid                gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide. The acid gas stream is separated in the                membrane separation unit to produce a retentate                stream and a permeate stream, where the retentate                stream comprises hydrogen sulfide. The permeate                stream comprises carbon dioxide. The retentate                stream is introduced to a sulfur recovery                unit.
   USE - Method for sweetening syngas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method sweetens the syngas stream in a                simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                reduced amount of carbon dioxide in the residue gas                treatment process of the Claus plant, reduces                complexity and cost to operate of the residue gas                treatment process, and enhanced efficiency and                economics compared to a conventional process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for recovering sulfur and carbon dioxide from a                syngas stream, which involves:
   (A) supplying a syngas stream to a                nonselective absorption unit;
   (B) separating the syngas stream in the                nonselective absorption unit using an absorption                process to produce an overhead syngas stream and an                acid gas stream;
   (C) introducing the acid gas stream to a                selective absorption unit;
   (D) processing the acid gas stream in the                selective absorption unit to obtain an overhead                carbon dioxide stream and a recovered hydrogen                sulfide stream;
   (E) introducing the recovered hydrogen sulfide                stream to a membrane separation unit; and
   (F) separating the recovered hydrogen sulfide                stream in the membrane separation stage to produce                a retentate stream and a permeate stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic process flow                diagram for recovering sulfur and carbon dioxide                from syngas using a nonselective amine absorption                unit and a membrane separation unit.
   Syngas stream (401)
   Nonselective amine absorption unit                (410)
   Overhead gas stream (411)
   Acid gas stream (412)
   Membrane separation unit (420)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The membrane comprises                  a perfluorinated polymer. The membrane separation                  unit comprises a membrane separation stage and                  another membrane separation stage. The overhead                  carbon dioxide stream is discharged to the                  atmosphere without subjecting the overhead carbon                  dioxide stream to any additional hydrogen sulfide                  separation.

PT P
PN US2020041073-A1
TI Carbon dioxide processing system for dry cleaning of objects, has storage vessel that includes upper section for storing clean liquid and lower section for storing dirty liquid, and upper section and lower section are in flow communication.
AU KEGLER A
AE EMINENT TECHNOLOGIES LLC (EMIN-Non-standard)
GA 202010205F
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide processing system (10) has a processing vessel (14). A storage vessel (12) includes an upper section (30) for storing clean liquid and a lower section (32) for storing dirty liquid, and the upper section and lower section are in flow communication. The upper section includes a first heat exchanger (22), and the first heat exchanger condenses gas received from the lower section to form clean liquid to be stored in the upper section. An overflow height is defined between a bottom of the upper section and a top of a standpipe, and the upper section includes a storage portion that is configured to hold a predetermined volume of clean liquid. The bottom of the storage vessel is made of stainless steel, and an upper portion is made of carbon steel.
   USE - Carbon dioxide processing system for dry cleaning of objects.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature is maintained at a relatively steady rate and the pressure is held consistent with the distillation portion of the processing system, thus decreasing the density of the carbon dioxide gas. The auxiliary components reduce the overall operational cost of the process. The cost to produce the overall system is reduced, by reducing the number of components that require stainless steel as the material of construction. The more complete distillation and smaller storage requirements have reduced or eliminated the need for stainless steel storage tank components. The arrangement of the condensing section that allows the air to collect the condensing area where that is monitored and removed, thus eliminating the reduction in performance often associated with some carbon dioxide processing systems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for processing objects; and
   (2) a method for reducing pressure of a gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic or plumbing and instrumentation diagram of the carbon dioxide processing system.
   Carbon dioxide processing system (10)
   Storage vessel (12)
   Processing vessel (14)
   First heat exchanger (22)
   Upper section (30)
   Lower section (32)

PN EP3343219-A1; WO2018128271-A1; US2018188218-A1; EP3343219-B1; US10816521-B2
TI Sensor hub for users to visually check air            environment and power consumption, has main board with            processor that controls light emitting unit and diplay            unit based on data received from fine dust sensor and            carbon dioxide sensor.
AU SOHN S
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
   LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
   LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
   LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 201852343U
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor hub (1) has a housing (100) with                ventilaton ports located on the lateral and rear                sides and has first and second opening at the top                and bottom. A fine dust sensor is provided in the                housing which senses the concentration of fine                dusts introduced through the ventilation ports. A                carbon dioxide sensor senses the concentration of                carbon dioxide introduced through the ventilation                ports. Coupled to the first opening is a display                plate (530) mounted with a display unit (700). An                illumination plate is coupled to the second opening                mounted with light emitting units. A main board is                placed in the housing that includes processor which                controls the light emitting units and display units                based on data on fine dust concentration and carbon                dioxide concentration received from fine dust                sensor and carbon dioxide sensor.
   USE - Sensor hub for users to visually check air                environment and power consumption of a room.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor hub senses and displays fine dust                concentration and carbon dioxide concentration in                real time which allows the user to recognize the                point of time of using an air cleaner and the                required ventilation by opening and closing of a                window which improves user convenience. The sensor                hub allows the user to check the light from the                light emitting units through the edge portion of                the illumination plate which enables the user to                visually and easily grasp the schematic information                on fine dust concentration, carbon dioxide                concentration and power consumption at a remote                distance that improves user convenience and                satisfaction. The correction sensor detects the                temperature or humidity inside the housing and                corrects the temperature or humidity around the                housing which provides accurate temperature and                humidity information to the user.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for operating a sensor hub.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the perspective view of a                sensor hub.
   Sensor hub (1)
   Housing (100)
   Display plate (530)
   Top plate (560)
   Display unit (700)

TI Carbon dioxide sensing structure based on optical            fiber end face integrated super-surface, has gold film            etched with multiple micro-nano structures, each micro            nano structure composed of multiple circular holes,            where gold film covers with specific adsorption            material layer.
AU CHEN D
   ZHOU R
   LUO S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG NORMAL (UYZN-C)
GA 2022A13995
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide sensing structure based on                optical fiber end surface integrated super-surface                comprises an optical fiber terminal end surface                evaporated with a layer of gold film with a                sub-wavelength thickness, where the gold film is                etched with multiple micro-nano structures, each                micro nano structure is composed of multiple                circular holes, and each circular hole is provided                with multiple of circular holes. The gold film                covers with a specific adsorption material layer                for converting carbon dioxide gas concentration                change into spectrum change.
   USE - Carbon dioxide sensing structure based on                optical fiber end face integrated super-surface.                Uses include but are not limited to industry,                agriculture, national defense, medical sanitation,                environmental protection and aviation fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide sensing structure is                capable of performing gas sensing in narrow                environment or extreme environment, e.g. medical                invasive detection or gas detection in explosive                still environment. The detection process does not                need to heat, using more safe and capable of                real-time and fast sensing. It has simple                preparation, compatible with the preparation                process of the semiconductor complementary metal                oxide semiconductor (CMOS) device, can be on the                different optical fiber end face of multiple groups                of optical fiber bundle, batch preparation of other                micro-nano gas sensor, to reach a group of highly                integrated optical fiber bundle, at the same time                detecting the gas concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                carbon dioxide sensing system based on optical                fiber end face integrated super-surface.

   2Metal film layer

PT P
PN CN114810004-A
TI Method for mixing oil displacement with natural            gas and carbon dioxide, involves mixing and            pressurizing in air storage barrel and sending into            pipeline through pump body in barrel.
AU WANG B
AE TIANYI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A2350F
AB    NOVELTY - Mixing oil displacement with natural gas and                carbon dioxide comprises installing pipeline butt                joint device in the well net of gas injection oil                extraction area; setting each of pipeline joint                device above corresponding oil extraction zone and                connecting with pipeline; connecting gas inlet pipe                and pipeline butt device with natural gas source                device and carbon dioxide source device                respectively; before injecting gas, measuring                oxygen content in wells, opening pipeline valve                between air inlet tube and COG source device when                oxygen amount is higher than explosion point;                closing pipeline valves between gas outlet pipe and                natural gas supply device, if gas content is lower                than detonation point; and sending natural gas into                air storage tank through pipeline, mixing and                pressurizing in air tank and sending into pipeline                through pump body in the barrel.
   USE - The method is used for mixing oil displacement                with natural gas and carbon dioxide.

The collecting agent is added in the coal slurry under stirring for 2-3 minutes and then added foaming agent in coal slurry for 20-60 seconds. The carbon dioxide gas is filled into the coal slurry for scraping flotation, where the flotation time is 5-10 minutes.

PN CN113979472-A
TI Method for co-producing rutile with titanium-containing blast furnace slag mineralization carbon dioxide involves mixing titanium-containing blast furnace slag with green vitriol and additives, roasting, e.g. adding collector and foaming agent, and crystallizing to recover Glauber's salt.
AU LIU Q
   JIN X
   GAO Y
   HE M
   LIU W
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022233629
AB    NOVELTY - Method for co-producing rutile with titanium-containing blast furnace slag mineralization carbon dioxide involves (1) uniformly mixing titanium-containing blast furnace slag (which is finely ground below 150 mum) with green vitriol (iron(II) sulfate heptahydrate) and additives, roasting to obtain a solid product, (2) dissolving the roasting slag in water, adjusting the pH, adding a collector and a foaming agent, and using flotation equipment to extract the titanium dioxide by flotation, (3) adding the tailings after the flotation in step (2) to water to adjust the slurry, adding sodium hydroxide to adjust the pH to be alkaline, feeding carbon dioxide to generate calcium carbonate to realize the mineralization of carbon dioxide, evaporating the mineralized mother liquor and crystallizing to recover Glauber's salt, and returning to step (1) to use.
   USE - Method for co-producing rutile with titanium-containing blast furnace slag mineralization carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses titanium dioxide by-product green vitriol as additive, which avoids the high-energy-consumption recovery and circulation process in the mineral storage process. The method is carried out under mild condition with large amount of carbon dioxide sequestration. The raw material of the process is titanium white solid waste green vitriol, which has a wide range of sources and realizes the effective utilization of waste, which is environmentally-friendly and economical. The method utilizes green vitriol to modify titanium-containing blast furnace slag to form and enrich into a rutile phase that is easy to recover. The method is simple and convenient, and does not produce waste residue discharge and secondary pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the titanium-containing blast furnace slag to green vitriol is 1:1-8. The content of the additive is 2.5-25% of the total mass of material. Preferred Component: The additive is sulfur, sodium sulfate, and/or potassium sulfate.

PN CN113667698-A
TI Preparing microbial self-synthesized cadmium sulfide semiconductor useful for fixing carbon dioxide, by inoculating Thiobacillus thioparus into sulfur-containing culture medium, culturing, centrifuging, adding with cadmium nitrate and cysteine, culturing, and drying semiconductor.
AU ZHANG H
   GAO F
   LIU G
   GAO C
   XIONG Y
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2021E3233S
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for preparing microbial self-synthesized cadmium sulfide semiconductor involves (a) inoculating Thiobacillus thioparus into a sulfur-containing culture medium for expansion culture, and centrifuging to obtain wet bacteria, (b) adding a mixed solution of cadmium nitrate and cysteine to the wet bacteria, and performing adsorption culture to obtain a mixture, and (c) introducing the mixture into a mixed atmosphere of carbon dioxide and oxygen, culturing at a constant temperature, vacuum-freezing, and drying semiconductor. The method involves using T.thioparus, cadmium nitrate and cysteine in a mass ratio of (10-100):1:(5-10).
   USE - The method (M1) is useful for preparing microbial self-synthesized cadmium sulfide semiconductor, which is useful for fixing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method (M1) is performed in a simple, environmentally-friendly and cost-effective manner, and prepares cadmium sulfide nanoparticle with small size, uniform distribution, high photoelectron excitation ability, excellent reusability and excellent stability. The self-synthesized cadmium sulfide semiconductor improves efficiency of carbon dioxide storage and fixation under visible light irradiation when photogenerated electrons enter bacteria to participate in carbon fixation cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a microbial self-synthesized cadmium sulfide semiconductor being prepared by the above-mentioned preparation method (M1), and comprising T.thioparus, and cadmium sulfide nanoparticle being uniformly distributed on surface of T.thioparus, where the cadmium sulfide nanoparticle has a particle size of 20-40 nm; and
   (2) a method (M2) for fixing carbon dioxide by using the cadmium sulfide semiconductor involves mixing the cadmium sulfide semiconductor with a sulfur-free culture medium, introducing a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and oxygen, taking sodium lactate as a sacrificial agent, and performing a light irradiation reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The expansion culture is performed for 24-48 hours. The centrifugation is performed at a rotation speed of 6000-12000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5-20 minutes. The adsorption culture is performed at 26-30degreesC for 24-72 hours. The step (c) involves introducing the mixture into mixed atmosphere for 5-20 minutes, and performing vacuum-freezing and drying for 5-20 hours. The method (M2) involves using 0.08-0.12% sacrificial agent with respect to total mass of the reaction system.Preferred Component: The mixed solution has a pH of 6-8. The mixed solution comprises 1-10 g/l wet bacteria. The mixed atmosphere has carbon dioxide and oxygen in a volume ratio of 4.5-5.5:3.

TI Method for manufacturing and using electric frequency furnace with gas protection device, involves allowing carbon dioxide gas in steel bottle to enter into air storage cylinder along pipeline, where gas is filled with air outlet pipe.
AU WEI F
AE WEI F (WEIF-Individual)
GA 202116564J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting an air storage cylinder (6) with a steel bottle (9) of a carbon dioxide gas source through a pipeline (7) to properly open a control valve (8) of the steel bottle. Carbon dioxide gas in the steel bottle is entered into the air storage cylinder along the pipeline. The carbon dioxide gas is filled with the air storage cylinder and an air outlet pipe (4) by the pipeline. An oxygen-free space region (14) is formed in a hearth (5) of an electric frequency furnace (1).
   USE - Manufacturing and using method of electric frequency furnace with gas protection device.
   ADVANTAGE - The air outlet pipes of the air supply system are embedded or laid in the hearth of the electric frequency furnace, so as to ensure that the carbon dioxide gas overflow from different position of the hearth. The method enables forming an oxygen-free space area in the hearth after overflowing the carbon dioxide gas after extruding the air in the hearth. The heating process is finished in the hearth by the drill rod original piece needing to be heated. The method enables solving the technical problem that the drill rod is twisted off due to ash clamping generated in the process of heating, extruding and forming at the joint of the drill rod.

AU MOHAMMED O
AE ENGSL MINERALS DMCC (ENGS-Non-standard)
   GUSTAD T (GUST-Individual)
GA 202069075A
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon is captured through production of                carbon dioxide based on reaction of limestone by                (a) optionally and initially limestone mining,                crushing and screening (beneficiation), (b)                limestone calcination (V) and hydrated lime                production, (c) causticization (II) process, (d)                caustic soda separation, (e) calcium carbonate                (CaCO3) sludge separation, and (f) carbonation of                caustic soda and soda ash solution (I) recycling to                causticization in step (c).
   USE - Capturing a carbon through production of                carbon dioxide based on reaction of                limestone.
   ADVANTAGE - The caustic soda and calcium carbonate are                separated and the caustic soda is sent to a                carbonation reactor to produce soda ash, resulting                in a recycling of the soda ash. The carbon dioxide                (CO2) gas generated in calcination process is sent                to storage or used for enhanced oil recovery. The                process uses no energy from the power/industrial                plant (except waste heat) and makes available more                CO2 gas for storage or enhanced oil recovery than                the quantity of CO2 captured from the flue gas of                the power/industrial plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) use of carbon dioxide for storage/enhanced                oil recovery; and
   (2) plant or assembly of stages for producing                soda lime (NaOH) and carbon dioxide, comprising                optionally an initial section or stage for                limestone mining, crushing and screening                (beneficiation), and successive sections or stages                for limestone calcination (V) and hydrated lime                production, causticization (II) process, caustic                soda separation, calcium carbonate (CaCO3) sludge                separation, and carbonation of caustic soda and                soda ash solution (I) recycling to the                causticization section (II).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon capture process.
   Soda ash solution (I)
   Causticization (II)
   Thickener (III)
   Limestone calcination (V)
   Water (VI)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide gas                  from flue gas is converted into soda ash by                  carbonation of caustic soda which concentration                  of 10-11 wt.% and the soda ash solution produced                  has concentration of 12-13 wt.%. Carbonation of                  caustic soda is carried out in a pressurized                  carbonation reactor. Causticization is carried                  out at 85-95 degrees C. Calcination process is                  carried out at 900-1000 degrees C using waste                  heat of power/industrial plant. Preferred                  Components: The carbon dioxide (CO2) gas                  generated during calcination of calcium carbonate                  is greater than the quantity of CO2 gas captured                  during carbonation of caustic soda. The CO2 gas                  concentration in exhaust of calcination process                  is 72-74 wt.% with other gas component being                  nitrogen.

PN WO2019238268-A1; CA3103999-A1; AU2019286912-A1; CN112368464-A; IN202017056883-A; EP3803064-A1; US2021363900-A1; RU2020144004-A
TI Compound closed-loop heat Brayton cycle system,            for recovering waste heat from e.g. combustion engine,            has heat exchanger for circulating vapor from expander            to cooler in relationship with vapor from compressor to            heater.
AU NASINI E
   SANTINI M
   BELLANTONE F
   CHIESI F
   KEZI F
AE NUOVO PIGNONE TECHNOLOGIE SRL (NUOV-C)
   NUOVO PIGNONE TECHNOLOGIE SRL (NUOV-C)
   NUOVO PIGNONE TECNOLOGIE SRL (NUOV-C)
   NUOVO PIGNONE TECNOLOGIE SRL (NUOV-C)
GA 2019A61592
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a heater (16) for circulating                carbon dioxide vapor in heat exchange relationship                with hot fluid to heat the carbon dioxide vapor. An                expander (18) is coupled to the heater and expands                the carbon dioxide vapor. A compressor (22)                compresses the carbon dioxide vapor fed through a                cooler (20). A heat exchanger (17) circulates the                carbon dioxide vapor from the expander to the                cooler in heat exchange relationship with the                carbon dioxide vapor from the compressor to the                heater, where the expander and the compressor are                mechanically coupled volumetric machines, where                volumetric machines are selected from reciprocating                machines, radial expanders/compressors, axial                expanders/compressors, screw expanders/compressors,                impulse expanders/compressors or combination.
   USE - Compound closed-loop heat Brayton cycle system                for recovering waste heat from a waste heat source                e.g. combustion engine, gas turbine, geothermal,                solar thermal, industrial and residential heat                sources (all claimed), for generating                electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces cost and satisfies a                requirement of continuous supervision during                operation. The system improves efficiency and                provides a cost effective solution to convert waste                heat into mechanical energy. The system realizes                safe, environmental friendly solution and wide                operating range ability. The system avoids                influence by environmental conditions by avoiding a                need to reach condensed phase in ambient conditions                in a waste heat recovery Rankine cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a reciprocating compressor system
   (2) a waste heat recovery method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a compound closed-loop heat Brayton cycle                system.
   Heater (16)
   Heat exchanger (17)
   Expander (18)
   Cooler (20)
   Compressor (22)

TI Providing textured and porous polymeric film by providing organic polymeric film having opposing surfaces and is composed of material that is swellable with supercritical carbon dioxide, and applying a physical force.
AU GILLMOR J R
   OWUSU-NKWANTABISAH S
AE EASTMAN KODAK CO (EAST-C)
GA 2019574939
AB    NOVELTY - A textured and porous polymeric film is provided by (A) providing an organic polymeric film having 1st and 2nd opposing surfaces and dry thickness of less than or equal to 1 mm, and is composed of material that is swellable with supercritical carbon dioxide; (B) applying a physical force of greater than or equal to 0.05 Newtons to partial surface area of 1st and 2nd opposing surfaces of organic polymeric film; (C) subjecting organic polymeric film to supercritical carbon dioxide under a pressure of greater than or equal to 73 bar and temperature of greater than or equal to 31 degrees C for a time sufficient to swell the organic polymeric film with supercritical carbon dioxide; (D) removing the supercritical carbon dioxide; and (E) removing the physical force from partial surface area of 1st and 2nd opposing surfaces to provide a textured and porous organic polymeric film having (a) textured 1st and 2nd opposing surfaces in partial surface area, having wrinkles having an average peak to valley height of 1-3000 mu m, and (b) porosity of greater than or equal to 10% between 1st and 2nd opposing surfaces.
   USE - Provision of textured and porous polymeric film.
   ADVANTAGE - The organic polymeric films have internal pores and outer surface texture or wrinkles using physical pressure and supercritical carbon dioxide (ssCO2). The use of scCO2 creates a simple and complex textured and porous designs and environmentally friendly and is benign chemical, inexpensive, free of post-process extractions and precipitation, and allows for a facile control of the foam porosity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic, cross-sectional view of the amounts of opposing physical forces that can be applied to organic polymeric film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The textured and porous organic polymeric film is cooled to a temperature that is less than the temperature used in (C). The organic polymeric film is disposed on a substrate that is not swellable by supercritical carbon dioxide under the conditions of (C). Preferred Components: The organic polymeric film is composed of greater than or equal to 1 amorphous or semi-crystalline polymers. The organic polymeric film is composed of poly(vinylidene fluoride) or copolymer derived from vinylidene fluoride, acrylate homopolymer or copolymer, polycarbonate, polyurethane, or blend of greater than or equal to 2 polymers. The organic polymeric film is a composite composed of greater than or equal to 1 organic polymers and inorganic or organic filler material. The textured and porous organic polymeric film comprises both open pores and closed pores. The textured and porous organic polymeric film comprises predominantly closed pores. The organic polymeric film comprises a single organic polymeric layer. The organic polymeric film comprises a laminate of greater than or equal to 2 organic polymeric films of different composition. Preferred Parameters: The textured 1st and 2nd opposing surfaces in partial surface area have wrinkles having an average peak to valley height of 25-1500 mu m.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. PN CN107699342-A
TI Extracting walnut oil using supercritical carbon dioxide process, comprises e.g. subjecting walnut kernels to liquid nitrogen freezing, microwave thawing, removing skin, ultra-micro crushing, sieving, and ultrasonically processing.
AU CAO L
   ZHAO Y
AE HARBIN MEISEN FOOD MFG CO LTD (HARB-Non-standard)
GA 201815425N
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting walnut oil using supercritical carbon dioxide process, comprises (i) subjecting walnut kernels to liquid nitrogen freezing, microwave thawing, removing skin, ultra-micro crushing, sieving, ultrasonically processing; (ii) carrying supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to walnut powder obtained in the step (i) to obtain crude oil and cake; and (iii) subjecting crude oil obtained in step (ii) to pulsed electric field treatment; and ultra-micro filtering to obtain walnut oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting walnut oil using supercritical carbon dioxide process.
   ADVANTAGE - The walnut oil: maintains biological active substance property; has high oxidation resistance; prevents rancidity during the storage; has high extraction rate and short process time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting walnut oil using supercritical carbon dioxide process comprises (i) subjecting walnut kernels to liquid nitrogen freezing at -100to -120 degrees C for 5-7 minutes, microwave thawing at 75-85 degrees C for 5-8 minutes, removing the skin, ultra-micro crushing, sieving, ultrasonically processing at ultrasonic power of 450-550 W, ultrasonic temperature of 32-39 degrees C and ultrasonic frequency of 20-25 KHz for 8-12 minutes; (ii) carrying supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction to walnut powder obtained in the step (i) at CO2 flow rate of 12-18 l/minutes, extraction pressure of 37-39 MPa and extraction temperature of 40-50 degrees C for 40-45 minutes to obtain crude oil and cake; and (iii) subjecting crude oil obtained in step (ii) to pulsed electric field treatment for 80-100 ~ mseconds under electric field intensity of 20-25 kV/cm, pulse frequency of 300-400 Hz and pulse flow rate of 50-70 ml/minutes; and ultra-micro filtering to obtain walnut oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the liquid nitrogen freezing temperature is -100 degrees C, and liquid nitrogen freezing processing time is 5 minutes. In the step (i), the microwave thawing temperature is 80 degrees C, and the microwave thawing time is 6 minutes. In the step (i), the conditions for ultrasonic treatment is: ultrasonic power is 480 W, ultrasonic temperature is 37 degrees C, ultrasonic frequency is 25 KHz, and ultrasonic time is 10 minute. In the step (ii), the conditions for carrying supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is: carbon dioxide flow rate is 15 l/minute, extraction pressure is 38 Mpa, extraction temperature is 43 degrees C, and extraction time is 42 minutes. In the step (iii), carrying pulsed electric field treatment for 90 ~-mseconds under conditions of an electric field strength of 23 kV/cm, a pulse frequency of 340 Hz, and a pulse flow rate of 62 ml/minute. In the method, the ultra-micro filtering is carrying out by ultra-filtering film with the cut-off molecular weight of 80-120 kD. In the method, sieving is carrying out by using 60-mesh sieves.

PN EP4066936-A1; CN115193415-A; US2022323934-A1; JP2022159255-A
TI Matter composition useful in producing porous            material for capturing carbon dioxide, comprises,            quaternary ammonium ions in porous crosslinked polymer            network, and counter ion in polymer network that is            hydroxide or counter ion.
AU MECKLER S M
   CHINTAPALLI M
   MERKLE S
   MECKLER S
AE PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
   PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
   PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
GA 2022C7505E
AB    NOVELTY - Matter composition comprises: a porous                crosslinked polymer network; quaternary ammonium                ions in the crosslinked polymer network; and at                least one counter ion in the crosslinked polymer                network that is at least one of hydroxide or a                counter ion capable of forming hydroxide upon                reaction with water.
   USE - The composition is useful in producing porous                material for capturing carbon dioxide (all claimed)                as moisture swing sorbents.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: has high surface area and                improved pore characteristics, unparalleled                moisture swing adsorption performance due to the                sorbent physical and chemical structure resulting                from PARC's patented porous polymer stable free                radical polymerization and low cost; is scalable                processing without expensive supercritical carbon                dioxide; and enhances scalability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included                for:
   producing (M1) a porous material, comprising                polymerizing a compound containing quaternary                ammonium and a crosslinker using controlled                polymerization, and performing ion exchange in                presence of at least one of hydroxide or a counter                ion capable of forming hydroxide upon reaction with                water, optionally where the porous material is the                composition of matter; and
   method (M2) to capture carbon dioxide,                comprising employing a sorbent comprising                quaternary ammonium ions in a porous crosslinked                polymer network in an environment to adsorb carbon                dioxide, optionally where the sorbent is a                composition of matter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The counter ion in the                  crosslinked polymer network is forming hydroxide                  is one of bicarbonate, carbonate, phosphate,                  hydrogen phosphate or dihydrogen phosphate. The                  porous crosslinked polymer network includes                  residual chemical moieties from controlled                  radical polymerization, optionally where the                  residual chemical moieties are formed from at                  least one of nitroxides, chain transfer agents,                  thiols, (2,2,6,6-Tetramethylpiperidin-1-yl)oxyl                  hydroxy (TEMPO-OH), nitroxide species derived                  from the decomposition of alkoxyamine,                  4-hydroxy-TEMPO, TEMPO derivatives,                  2,2,5-trimethyl-4-phenyl-3-azahexane-3-nitroxide                  (TIPNO), TIPNO derivatives, chlorobenzyl-TIPNO,                  3-nitro-4-(2-oxo-pyrrolidin-1-yl)-benzenesulfonamide                  (SG1), SG1, thiocarbonylthio compounds,                  xanthates, trithiocarbonates, dithioesters,                  dithiocarbamates, dithiobenzoates, metal                  catalysts used in ATRP, copper, and iron. The                  porous crosslinked polymer network includes                  residue of at least one additional monomer,                  optionally where the residue is formed from at                  least one of styrene, styrenic compounds, n-vinyl                  pyrrolidone, vinyl acetate, vinyl chloride,                  methacrylates, acrylates, methyl methacrylate,                  acrylonitrile, methyl acrylate, ethylene,                  propylene, dimethylsiloxane, fluorinated vinyl,                  acrylic, or methacrylic compounds,                  hexa-iso-fluoroisopropyl methacrylate,                  hexa-iso-fluoroisopropyl acrylate,                  2,2,2-trifluoroethyl methacrylate,                  2,2,2-trifluoroethyl acrylate, and                  1H,1H,2H,2H-heptadecafluorodecyl methacrylate,                  and/or optionally where the residue comprises a                  hydrophobic monomer that contains at least one of                  fluorinated or aliphatic groups. The quaternary                  ammonium ions comprise moieties that result from                  polymerization of diallyldimethylammonium,                  trimethylammonium ion,                  2-(Methacryloyloxy)ethyl]trimethylammonium,                  2-(acryloyloxy)ethyl]trimethylammonium,                  3-(methacryloylamino)propyl]-trimethylammonium,                  (3-acrylamidopropyl)trimethylammonium, or                  trimethyl-ammonium functional monomers. The                  composition of matter includes a crosslinker,                  optionally where the crosslinker is                  divinylbenzene, 1,6-hexanediol diacrylate, groups                  including methyl, ethyl propyl, isopropyl, tri,                  tetra, penta or hexa-acrylates and methacrylates,                  trimethylolpropane triacrylate,                  trimethylolpropane ethoxylate triacrylate,                  di(trimethylolpropane) tetraacrylate,                  dipentaerythritol penta-/hexa-acrylate,                  trimethacryl adamantane, dipentaerithritol,                  trimethylolpropane trimethacrylate, phenylene                  dimethacrylate, phenylene diacrylate, and                  perfluorohexanediol diacrylate, where the                  cross-linker is hydrophobic and contains at least                  one of fluorinated or aliphatic groups. The                  crosslinked polymer network comprises an aerogel,                  optionally where the aerogel comprises one of a                  powder, a structured monolith, a self-supporting                  structure or includes a separate structural                  material. In the composition: (i) where the                  composition of matter has a surface area of                  greater than 100 m2/g, greater than 200 m2/g, or                  greater than 500 m2/g; and/or (ii) the                  composition of matter has an average pore                  diameter of 2-50 nm; and/or (iii) the mass                  fraction of the quaternary ammonium groups in the                  composition of matter has a range of 5-90%,                  20-70%, 50-70%, or 50-90%; and/or (iv) at least                  80% of the quaternary ammonium ions are linked                  directly to at least one other quaternary                  ammonium ion; and/or (v) the quaternary ammonium                  ions in the in the crosslinked polymer network                  are derived from monomers that have molecular                  weight of below 200 g/mol, when the monomers are                  in a chloride ion-exchanged form. In the method                  (M1): the counter ion in the crosslinked polymer                  network capable of reacting with water to produce                  hydroxide is one of bicarbonate, carbonate,                  phosphate, hydrogen phosphate, or dihydrogen                  phosphate, and/or optionally where the                  crosslinker contains quaternary ammonium groups;                  the compound contains residue of at least one                  additional monomer, optionally where the residue                  of the at least one additional monomer comprises                  at least one of styrene, styrenic compounds,                  n-vinyl pyrrolidone, vinyl acetate, vinyl                  chloride, methacrylates, acrylates, methyl                  methacrylate, acrylonitrile, methyl acrylate,                  ethylene, propylene, dimethylsiloxane,                  fluorinated compounds, hexaisofluoroisopropyl                  methacrylate, hexaisofluoroisopropyl acrylate,                  2,2,2-trifluoroethyl methacrylate,                  2,2,2-trifluoroethyl acrylate, and                  1H,1H,2H,2H-Heptadecafluorodecyl methacrylate.                  Preferred Method: In the method: the controlled                  polymerization comprises one of stable free                  radical polymerization (SFRP), atom-transfer                  radical polymerization, nitroxide-mediated                  polymerization, reversible addition-fragmentation                  chain-transfer polymerization, and sequential                  polymerization, optionally where the controlled                  polymerization includes addition of acetol,                  dicumyl peroxide or tertbutyl peroxide, the SFRP                  process optionally includes dissolving the                  quaternary ammonium-containing monomer,                  crosslinkers, and optional other monomers in a                  solvent to form a precursor solution, with                  initiators, a nitroxide mediator, and optional                  reducing agents. The method further comprises                  first reacting the quaternary-ammonium-containing                  monomers together to form                  quaternary-ammonium-containing oligomers, and the                  crosslinker and any additional monomers to form a                  polymer gel containing the                  quaternary-ammonium-containing oligomer residues,                  optionally where the                  quaternary-ammonium-containing oligomer residues                  are 2-10 quaternary-ammonium-containing monomer                  units long. In the method (M2): (i) where                  adsorption occurs in one of ambient conditions or                  with active water removal, and/or (ii) where the                  method further comprises desorbing carbon dioxide                  from the sorbent, where desorption occurs through                  one of addition of water vapor, addition of fog,                  addition of mist, or addition of liquid water,                  and/or (iii) where desorption occurs at a higher,                  actively controlled temperature than                  adsorption.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113953468-A
TI Method for preventing molten steel from absorbing nitrogen in tapping process by pre-adding dry ice to ladle, involves placing dry ice at bottom of prepared ladle.
AU YUN X
   HAN C
   DIAO W
   ZHANG H
   MA X
   ZHANG Y
AE BAOGANG GROUP CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2022343331
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing the dry ice at the bottom of the prepared ladle, where the density of carbon dioxide produced by sublimation of dry ice is greater than that of air. The sublimated carbon dioxide completely replaces the residual air in the ladle. The sublimation of dry ice increases with the impact of molten steel during the tapping process, and the carbon dioxide overflows in large quantities, thus forming an air-isolating effect on the molten steel. The carbon dioxide bubbles improve the dynamic conditions of molten steel flowing in the ladle, thus effectively reducing the amount of nitrogen absorbed.
   USE - Method for preventing molten steel from absorbing nitrogen in tapping process by pre-adding dry ice to ladle used for smelting process of modern top-bottom double blowing converters in field of iron and steel metallurgy.
   ADVANTAGE - The dynamic condition of the steel liquid flowing in the steel ladle is improved by the carbon dioxide bubble, which effectively reduces the nitrogen absorption amount. The method prevents steel liquid nitrogen absorption in steel tapping process by using ladle pre-adding dry ice, thus solving the problem of low cost and high efficiency, and easy operation control tapping process molten steel nitrogen absorbing method. The dry ice price is lower than the argon gas without additional device.
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)

PN CN113429650-A
TI Modifying rubber using nano filtration seawater to capture carbon dioxide products comprises grinding crushed recovered magnesium and calcium carbonate of nano filtration carbon dioxide, drying wet grinded magnesium and calcium carbonate and mixing with rubber, dispersing agent and antioxidant.
AU LIU Y
   CAO S
   WANG Z
   WANG R
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B4245X
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying rubber using nano filtration seawater to capture carbon dioxide products comprises (i) using a crusher to crush the recovered product magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate of the nano filtration carbon dioxide, where crushing size is 1-10 mm, using the Raylemill to grind to the size of 0.2-1 mm, grinding the recovered magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate by wet grinding to size of 1-100 microm and drying the wet grinded magnesium and calcium carbonate to obtain the micro-sized mixture of magnesium and calcium carbonate; and (ii) mixing the 2.2-14.7 pts. wt. micro-sized magnesium and calcium carbonate mixture, 85-95.3 pts. wt. rubber, 0.5-2 pts. wt. dispersing agent and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. antioxidant in the mixing machine uniformly to prepare the modified rubber.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying rubber using nanofiltration seawater to capture carbon dioxide products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can avoid the dispersion problem of nano materials in the rubber system; and applies the physical and mechanical properties of micro-calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate to rubber which improves the mechanical and chemical properties of the rubber and increases the service life of rubber products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ball-to-material ratio in the step (i) is 10-16:1, the ball material is alumina and/or zirconium oxide and the milling time is 6-10 hours and the milling temperature is less than 60degrees Celsius.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The mixing conditions in the mixing machine are rotating speed at 10-100 revolutions/minutes for 5-25 minutes at 100-170degrees Celsius. The method of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate and the recovered products of carbon dioxide captured by nano filtration seawater, are specifically comprises (A) subjecting the product wastewater containing carbon dioxide captured by nano-filtered seawater to sedimentation treatment comprises adding sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate to adjust the pH value of waste water to 7-9, adding continuously 0.05-2% basic polyaluminum chloride, the concentration of 0.5-100 ppm anionic polyacrylamide (APAM) and 0.05-0.1% sodium methylene dinaphthalene sulfonate until the product wastewater containing carbon dioxide captured by nanofiltration seawater forms large flocculent calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, cooling the product wastewater to 5-10, accelerating the precipitation of calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, and discharging the settled calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate wastewater into the thickening tank through the pipeline and using a filter press to dry the calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate to obtain blocky calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate; (B) cutting the block-shaped calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate obtained in step A mechanically and using a pulverizer to break the block-shaped calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate into small block-shaped magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate; and (C) transporting the small lumps of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate obtained into the thermal infrared drying heating chamber through the material transfer system for thermal infrared drying to obtain calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate which are the products of nanofiltration seawater trapping carbon dioxide. The step (C) comprises the residence time of the small pieces of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate in the thermal infrared drying heating chamber is 0.1-0.5 hours and the moisture content of the magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate obtained by drying is 0.5-5%.Preferred Components: The molecular weight of the anionic polyacrylamide (APAM) in the step (A) is 10-18 million.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas-liquid two-phase carbon dioxide corrosion inhibitor comprises 10-25 wt.% main agent and 75-90 wt.% composition, where the composition is composed of 10-25 wt.% pyridine quaternary ammonium salt, 3-10 wt.% diphenylthiourea, 3-5 wt.% propynol, 4-5 wt.% surfactant and 30-70 wt.% solvent, and the main agent is 2-isopropyl-5,5-dimethyl-2,5-dihydrothiazole of formula (I). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing corrosion inhibitor comprising mixing (I), pyridine quaternary ammonium salt, diphenylthiourea, propynol, surfactant and solvent by stirring evenly to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The main agent is prepared by pouring 28% aqueous ammonia into a three-necked flask, maintaining the temperature at 33-36 degrees C, adding isobutyraldehyde dropwise for 3-4 hours, reacting for 10-12 hours after completion of the dropwise addition, cooling the reaction product, then pouring the reaction product into a separatory funnel and allowing to stand for layering, separating the upper organic phase, pouring the upper organic phase and sulfur into a three-necked flask, stirring, heating at a constant temperature of 130-150 degrees C, and cooling after 6 hours of reaction to obtain solution, subjecting the solution to distillation under reduced pressure, and collecting fraction to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The surfactant is Tween 20 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monolaurate) or OP-10. The solvent in composition is isopropyl alcohol or a mixture of methanol and dimethylformamide. The corrosion inhibitor comprises 20-25 wt.% main agent. The mass ratio between aqueous ammonia, isobutyraldehyde and sulfur is 0.8-1:0.8-1:0.2-0.25.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining type of chromatography separation column to be used in a chromatography system. PN CN114874088-A
TI Preparing acetylenic acid, involves placing            molecular sieve, auxiliary agent alkali and end group            alkyne in carbon dioxide atmosphere for reaction,            mixing reaction product with inorganic solvent, and            extracting by using extracting agent to obtain water            phase.
AU WEI W
   SHI J
   SUN N
   ZHANG L
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 2022A5752A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing acetylenic acid, involves (a)                placing the molecular sieve, the auxiliary agent                alkali and the end group alkyne in a carbon dioxide                atmosphere for reaction, (b) mixing the obtained                reaction product with an inorganic solvent, and                extracting by using an extracting agent to obtain a                water phase, and (c) acidifying the water phase,                then removing impurities and extracting, where                removing impurities and extracting for three times                to obtain an organic phase, drying the organic                phase, and then carrying out solid-liquid                separation to obtain acetylenic acid.
   USE - Method for preparing acetylenic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the direct carboxylation of                carbon dioxide and terminal alkyne, which                effectively utilizes carbon dioxide, is                eco-friendly, and also has high research value and                economic and social environmental benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                acetylenic acid is prepared by method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molecular sieve is                  4A molecular sieve and 3A molecular sieve. The                  particle size of the molecular sieve is 0.5-3 mm.                  The bulk density of the molecular sieve is                  0.69-0.75 g/mL. The static water adsorption                  capacity of the molecular sieve under conditions                  of 25 &#8451; and 50% RH is &#8805;21.5                  wt.%. Preferred Components: The terminal alkyne                  is phenylacetylene, 4-methylphenylacetylene,                  4-ethylphenylacetylene, 4-propylphenylacetylene,                  4-butylbhenylacetylene, 4-pentylphenylacetylene,                  4-methoxyphenylacetylene,                  3-methylphenylacetylene, 2-methylphenylacetylene,                  3-methoxyphenylacetylene,                  2-methoxyphenylacetylene, 3- (trifluoromethyl)                  phenylacetylene, 3, 4-dimethoxyphenylacetylene,                  3, 5-dimethoxyphenylacetylene, 4-                  (trifluoromethyl) phenylacetylene,                  4-chlorophenylacetylene, 4-fluorophenylacetylene,                  4-bromophenylacetylene, 3-chlorophenylacetylene,                  3-bromophenylacetylene, 2-chlorophenylacetylene,                  2-fluorophenylacetylene, 2-bromophenylacetylene,                  2-ethynylnaphthylene, 1-ethynylnaphthalene,                  ethynylcyclopropane, cyclohexylacetylene,                  3-cyclohexylpropyne, methylpropynyl ether,                  n-butylacetylene, 1-heptyne, 1-octyne,                  3-ethynylthiophene, 2-ethynylthiophene,                  4-nitrophenylacetylene, 2-ethynylpyridine,                  3-dimethylbutyne, 4-tert-butylacetylene,                  2-ethynylfuran, 3-hydroxyphenylacetylene,                  acetylene propionate, phenylpropargyl ether,                  propynyl cyclopentane, 1, 4-diethynylbenzene,                  4-ethynylphenetole, 4-ethynylanisole,                  4-ethynylbenzonitrile, 3,                  5-bistrifluoromethylphenylacetylene,                  4-ethynylbiphenyl,                  2-acetylene-6-methoxynaphthalene, methyl propynyl                  acetate, methyl methacrylate, ethyl methacrylate,                  butyl methacrylate. The auxiliary base comprises                  cesium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium                  carbonate, cesium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  sodium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide, cesium                  fluoride, potassium fluoride, sodium fluoride,                  cesium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, lithium bicarbonate, cesium acetate,                  potassium acetate, sodium acetate, lithium                  acetate, potassium phosphate, sodium phosphate,                  cesium phosphate, dipotassium hydrogen phosphate,                  disodium hydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, lithium                  dihydrogen phosphate, cesium dihydrogen                  phosphate, potassium tert-butoxide, sodium                  tert-butoxide, lithium tert-butoxide, sodium                  methoxide, potassium methoxide, cesium methoxide,                  lithium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, potassium                  ethoxide, cesium ethoxide, lithium ethoxide,                  potassium hydride, sodium hydride, cesium                  hydride, sodium amide, potassium amide, cesium                  amide, lithium amide, 1,5, 7-triazabicyclo                  [4.4.0] dodec-5-ene, 1, 5-diazabicyclo [4.3.0]                  -5-nonene, cesium fluoride, potassium fluoride,                  sodium bicarbonate, potassium tert-butoxide,                  lithium methoxide, sodium methoxide, potassium                  methoxide, lithium ethoxide, lithium methoxide,                  lithium ethoxide, lithium hydroxide, cesium                  hydride, lithium hydroxide, sodium amide, lithium                  hydroxide, 1, 8-diazabicyclo [5.4.0] undec-7-ene,                  triethylamine, triphenylamine, 2,6,                  6-tetramethylpiperidine, and diethanolamine. The                  organic solvent comprises toluene, acetonitrile,                  tetrahydrofuran, N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl                  sulfoxide, propylene carbonate, sulfolane,                  N-dimethylacetamide, N-methyl-pyrrolidone, 1,                  3-dimethyl-2-imidazoline, dichloromethane,                  chloroform, dioxane, dimethylamine, dimethyl                  carbonate, diethyl carbonate, ethylene carbonate,                  dioxane, hexane, dichloroethane, diethyl ether,                  methanol, ethanol, N-propanol, and isopropanol.                  The inorganic solvent is water. The extracting                  agent is dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon                  tetrachloride, dichloroethane, trichloroethane                  and tetrachloroethane. The acid is hydrochloric                  acid, sulfuric acid or phosphoric acid,                  preferably hydrochloric acid. The first                  extractant is ethyl acetate, diethyl ether or                  diisopropyl ether. The second extractant is water                  and sodium chloride aqueous solution. The third                  extractant is sodium chloride aqueous solution                  and water. Preferred Compositions: The molar                  ratio of the auxiliary agent alkali and terminal                  alkyne is (0.1-5): 1. The mass ratio of the                  molecular sieve and terminal alkyne is                  (0.1-0.75): 1. The mol volume ratio of the                  terminal alkyne and organic solvent is 1 mmol:                  (0.5-10) mL. The volume ratio of the inorganic                  solvent and organic solvent is (1-2): 1.                  Preferred Conditions: The acidification is                  carried out to a pH of 1.5 or less. The                  solid-liquid separation is vacuum filtration. The                  solid-liquid separation also comprises                  post-treatment, and the post-treatment comprises                  solvent removal. Preferred Process: The first                  extraction agent is used to extract the acidified                  solution to obtain a first organic phase. The                  second extracting agent is used to extract the                  first organic phase to obtain a second organic                  phase. The third extraction agent is used to                  extract the second organic phase to obtain a                  third organic phase.

PN CN112931813-A
TI Preparation of polysaccharide dry pulp ultra-micro            powder by extracting polysaccharide dry pulp in            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, taking            out, crushing into coarse powder, and performing            ultra-micro crushing treatment.
AU LIN L
   LIU J
   LIU Y
AE AGRIC PROD PROCESSING RES INST CHINESE A (CTAG-C)
GA 202170965N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polysaccharide dry pulp                ultra-micro powder comprises adding polysaccharide                dry pulp into material kettle of supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction device, performing                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, taking out                polysaccharide dry pulp after extracting, putting                in universal crusher, crushing to obtain                polysaccharide dry pulp coarse powder, and                performing ultra-micro crushing treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                polysaccharide dry pulp ultra-micro powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method processes the polysaccharide dry                pulp by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                extraction technology under the specific condition;                it can remove the sticky substance in the                polysaccharide dry pulp, so as to prepare the                polysaccharide dry pulp ultra-micro powder; and the                preparation method is simple; the used instrument                is less, the impurity is less, and no organic                solvent residue; it can effectively keep the                polysaccharide content; and the preparation of the                polysaccharide dry pulp ultra-micro powder has                important value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Extraction is done at                  35-60 degrees C at 10-20 MPa for 1-3 hours.                  Ultra-micro crushing is done at - 4 to - 15                  degrees C for 5-15 minutes. Preferred Components:                  The polysaccharide dry pulp comprises longan                  pulp, litchi pulp, fig pulp, red jujube pulp                  and/or grape pulp.

PN CN112897525-A
TI Preparing nitrogen-rich carbon material for capturing carbon dioxide, involves fully grinding biomass waste bean dregs, transferring to hydrothermal kettle followed by repeatedly washing activated carbon material and drying.
AU XIE W
   ZHANG Y
   LI H
   YE F
   CUI X
   WANG L
   HE L
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
   TIANJIN NANDA CASTOR ENG TECHNOLOGY CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202165271T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-rich carbon material involves fully grinding the biomass waste bean dregs, mixing the bean dregs powder with distilled water and transferring to a hydrothermal kettle for hydrothermal carbonization. The carbon material obtained by hydrothermal carbonization is washed with water for several times, mixed and ground with an activator after being fully dried, and activated under an inert atmosphere to obtain an activated carbon material. The activated carbon material is repeatedly washed with dilute hydrochloric acid and distilled water to neutrality, followed by fully drying to obtain nitrogen-doped porous activated carbon.
   USE - Method for preparing nitrogen-rich carbon material for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon material has a high specific surface area and a good pore structure, and exhibits excellent adsorption capacity of 3.3-6.3 mmol/g for carbon dioxide, when carbon dioxide partial pressure is 1 bar at 273K.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activator is potassium hydroxide, where the ratio of potassium hydroxide and bean dregs powder is mixed and ground at 1-3, the inert atmosphere is argon and the activation temperature is 600-800 degrees C for 1-3 hours.

TI Refrigerant composition used in transcritical heat            transfer system and refrigeration, air-conditioning or            heat pump system, comprises carbon dioxide and            difluoromethane.
AU LOW R E
