GA 202014446R
AB    NOVELTY - Refrigerant composition comprises carbon                dioxide and 1-25 wt.% difluoromethane.
   USE - The composition is useful in transcritical                heat transfer system and refrigeration,                air-conditioning or heat pump system (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is non-flammable; has global                warming potential of below 150; exhibits improved                energy efficiency relative to carbon dioxide;                reduces operating pressure relative to carbon                dioxide; and exhibits temperature glide in the                evaporator of less than 10 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a transcritical heat transfer system for                providing heating and/or cooling comprising the                composition; and
   (2) a refrigeration, air-conditioning or heat                pump system comprising the composition;
   (3) an electric vehicle equipped with a                transcritical air-conditioning and/or heat pump                system;
   (4) producing cooling which comprises                evaporating the composition in the vicinity of a                body to be cooled; and
   (5) producing heating which comprises                condensing the composition in the vicinity of a                body to be heated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: The transcritical heat                  transfer system: is an automotive                  air-conditioning system; is an automotive heat                  pump system; provides for both heating and                  air-conditioning in automotive applications; is a                  heat pump system for the production of hot water;                  is a supermarket refrigeration system; is a                  residential air-conditioning system; refrigerated                  transport system; and further comprises a                  lubricant, preferably a polyolester or                  polyalkylene glycol lubricant. The transcritical                  heat transfer system uses: a single stage                  compression cycle; and a dual stage compression                  cycle.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 20-25 wt.% difluoromethane, preferably                  less than 21 wt.% difluoromethane. The                  composition is a binary refrigerant composition                  comprising 75-99 wt.% carbon dioxide, preferably                  79 plus minus 1 wt.% carbon dioxide, and 25-1                  wt.% difluoromethane, preferably 21 plus minus 1                  wt.% difluoromethane. The composition further                  comprises: 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane (R-134a);                  tetrafluoropropene, preferably a                  tetrafluoropropene comprising                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoroptopene (R-1234yf) and                  1,3,3,3-tetranuoropropene (R-1234ze(E)); and                  R-1234yf, R-1234ze(E), where the amounts of the                  R-134a and the selected tetrafluoropropene                  compound is so that the binary mixture of                  R-1234yf, R-1234ze(E) with R-134a is                  non-flammable. The composition is a binary                  refrigerant composition comprising 86 plus minus                  1 wt.% carbon dioxide, 7 plus minus 1 wt.%                  difluoromethane and 7 plus minus 1 wt.%                  1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The refrigeration,                  air-conditioning or heat pump system further                  comprises lubricant, preferably a polyolester or                  polyalkylene glycol lubricant.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 86% carbon                dioxide; 7% difluoromethane; and 7%                1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane.

TI Beam transport system for carbon dioxide laser for use in extreme UV lithography apparatus, has enclosure formed from set of rigid, tubular components, where carbon dioxide laser beam propagates through cavity defined by enclosure.

PT P
PN CN110128587-A; CN110128587-B
TI Preparing fluorine-containing polymer material by emulsion template method comprises e.g. adding oil phase into the high pressure kettle, sealing, vacuuming, filling carbon dioxide gas, magnetically stirring, and discharging carbon dioxide.
AU GENG B
   CHEN J
   WANG Y
   WAN X
AE UNIV JINAN SHANDONG (UNJN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UNJN-C)
GA 201973556Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fluorine-containing polymer material by emulsion template method comprises (i) adding fluorine-containing acid ester monomer, hydrophobic crosslinking agent and oil-soluble monomer, and surfactant into a serum bottle, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain uniform oil phase and adding an evocating agent; (ii) dissolving polyvinyl alcohol into deionized water to obtain aqueous polyvinyl alcohol solution and adding into a high pressure kettle; (iii) adding oil phase into the high pressure kettle containing the polyvinyl alcohol solution, sealing, vacuuming, filling carbon dioxide gas, magnetically stirring and reacting under constant temperature; and (iv) discharging carbon dioxide after completing the reaction and taking out the cylindrical fluorine-containing polymer material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fluorine-containing polymer material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fluorine-containing polymer material, prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of fluorine-containing acid ester monomer, hydrophobic crosslinking agent, evocating agent, and surfactant to the total weight of the oil phase is 50-90:10-50:1-3:5-20 (wt.%). The polyvinyl alcohol comprises polyvinyl alcohol 1788, 1799, 2488 and/or 2699. The amount of carbon dioxide is 2-6 times the amount of oil-soluble monomer, where the oil-soluble monomer is a fluorine-containing acid ester monomer, and hydrophobic crosslinking agent oil-soluble monomer. The surfactant is an amphiphilic block polymer with a hydrophilic end and a carbon dioxide end, where the hydrophilic end is polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether 1000, polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether 2000, polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether 5000 or polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether 10000, and the carbon dioxide end is trifluoroethyl methacrylate, hexafluorobutyl methacrylate or dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate. The fluorine containing acid ester monomer is trifluoroethyl methacrylate, hexafluorobutyl methacrylate, dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate, trifluoroethyl acrylate, hexafluorobutyl acrylate and/or dodecafluoroheptyl acrylate. The hydrophobic cross-linking agent is divinylbenzene, ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, ethylene glycol diacrylate, butylene glycol diacrylate and/or butylene glycol dimethacrylate. The evocating agent is azodiisobutyronitrile, benzoyl peroxide, potassium persulfate and/or ammonium persulfate. Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises dissolving polyvinyl alcohol into deionized water at 80-90 degrees C to obtain polyvinyl alcohol solution, where the amount of aqueous polyvinyl alcohol solution is 0-3 wt.%, and the amount of aqueous polyvinyl alcohol solution added into the kettle is 30-80 wt.% (v/v). In the step (iii), the magnetically stirring is carried out by pre-stirring for 10-120 minutes, slowly heating to 65-80 degrees C, continuously stirring for 10-60 minutes, stopping magnetic stirring, and reacting at 65-80 degrees C for 6 hours until the pressure in the kettle reaches 9-20 MPa. The material is prepared by carbon dioxide-water-in-water emulsion (W/C), water-in-water-carbon dioxide multiple emulsion (W/C/W) and carbon dioxide-water-packed carbon dioxide-water multiple emulsion (W/C/W/C).
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.)
MC A02-A03; A04-E10D; A08-S01; A08-S06; A10-B03; A10-E08A; A10-E09A

PT P
PN WO2019096190-A1; CN111315716-A; EP3710420-A1; EP3710420-A4
TI Producing formic acid, comprises reacting carbon            dioxide with hydrogen in presence of solvent,            optionally substituted quinone compound and/or            optionally substituted hydroquinone compound and            catalyst comprising metal e.g. palladium and gold.
AU ORDOMSKY V
   STREIFF S
   HERNANDEZ ENCISO W Y
   SCHWIEDERNOCH R
   HERNANDEZ E W Y
AE SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   SOLVAY & CIE (SOLV-C)
GA 2019457700
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) formic acid, comprises reacting                carbon dioxide with hydrogen in the presence of a                solvent, optionally substituted quinone compound                and/or optionally substituted hydroquinone compound                and a catalyst comprising a metal comprising                palladium, platinum, ruthenium, rhodium, gold,                silver, iridium, nickel or cobalt.
   USE - The processes are useful for producing formic                acid (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing (P2) formic acid, comprising (i)                mixing optionally substituted quinone compound                and/or optionally substituted hydroquinone compound                with the catalyst in the presence of a solvent,                (ii) removing the solvent from the mixture obtained                at step (i) to obtain a solid composite, and (iii)                reacting carbon dioxide with hydrogen in the                presence of a solvent, the solid composite obtained                at step (ii), and optionally a basic compound;                and
   (2) a composition comprising carbon dioxide,                hydrogen, a solvent, optionally substituted quinone                compound and/or optionally substituted hydroquinone                compound, and the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  a supported metal catalyst comprising at least                  one metal of palladium, platinum, ruthenium or                  gold. The catalyst is Raney nickel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process (P1): the                  weight ratio of the optionally substituted                  quinone compound and/or the optionally                  substituted hydroquinone compound to the                  supported metal catalyst is 0.5-1.5; the reaction                  medium comprises a basic compound; and the                  solvent comprises a non-polar solvent, where the                  reaction results in the obtention of a solid                  comprising the catalyst and a reaction product,                  and the process comprises additional steps of (i)                  separating the solid obtained from the reaction                  medium, (ii) adding a polar solvent to the                  separated solid to dissolve the reaction product,                  (iii) separating the catalyst from the solution                  so obtained, and (iv) exposing the solution                  obtained at step(iii) to a basic compound and                  then heating the solution. Preferred Components:                  The optionally substituted quinone compound, or                  optionally substituted compound: has 5-20 carbon                  atoms; and comprises at least one cyclic ring                  having at least one five-membered or six-membered                  ring. The non-substituted quinone compound                  comprises 1,2-benzoquinone, 1,4-benzoquinone,                  1,4-naphthoquinone, 9,10-phenanthraquinone or                  9,10-anthraquinone. The non-substituted                  hydroquinone compound comprises benzene-1,4-diol,                  benzene-1,2-diol, naphthalene- 1,4-diol,                  phenanthrene-9,10-diol or anthracene-9,10-diol.                  The substituted quinone compound has substituted                  quinone compounds of formula (I), preferably                  1-methyl-9,10-anthraquinone,                  2-methyl-9,10-anthraquinone,                  1-ethyl-9,10-anthraquinone,                  2-ethyl-9,10-anthraquinone,                  1-amyl-9,10-anthraquinone or                  2-amyl-9,10-anthraquinone. The substituted                  hydroquinone compound has substituted                  hydroquinone compounds of formula (II),                  preferably 1-methylanthracene-9,10-diol,                  2-methylanthracene-9,10-diol,                  1-ethylanthracene-9,10-diol,                  2-ethylanthracene-9,10-diol,                  1-amylanthracene-9,10-diol or                  2-amylanthracene-9,10-diol.At least one of R1-R8 = 1-12C alkyl.Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises the basic compound.
   EXAMPLE - Catalyst was prepared by addition of 50 mg of                40 wt.% palladium/carbon in the solution of 100 mg                2-ethyl-9,10-anthraquinone dissolved in 1 ml of                xylene. Solvent was removed afterwards in the                rotavapor and the catalyst was dried at 80 degrees                C in vacuum. Catalyst was added in 50 ml stainless                steel reactor together with 0.9 g of trimethylamine                in 6 g of water as formic acid extracting solution.                Reactor was sealed and pressurized with 30 bar of                hydrogen and 30 bar of carbon dioxide. The reaction                was heated to 60 degrees C for 5 hours under                continuous stirring. After reaction the products                were analyzed and ionic conductivity method to                obtain formic acid with a yield of 2234 mg.

PN CN109059167-A
TI Seed production workshop ventilating method, involves performing seed production workshop ventilation by starting fan to reach purpose of ventilation when corresponding carbon dioxide concentration reaches preset threshold value.
AU INVENTOR U
AE HEFEI YANGYANG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201900768E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves arranging an air inlet pipeline and an air outlet pipeline on inner and outer sides of a seed production workshop. The air inlet pipeline is fixed to a filter plate, where the filter plate comprises a gauze, a filter screen, an active carbon layer and a clear resin layer. Carbon dioxide concentration of a seed in the seed production workshop is detected by using a carbon dioxide concentration detector. Seed production workshop ventilation is performed by starting a fan to reach purpose of ventilation when the carbon dioxide concentration reaches preset threshold value.
   USE - Seed production workshop ventilating method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing the filter screen to filter bacteria, absorbing peculiar smell in the air by using the active carbon layer, and killing residual spore in the seed production workshop so as to ensure air cleanliness and avoid workshop pollution without affecting seed production.

PT P
PN CN108690095-A
TI Synthesizing supercritical carbon dioxide liquid based on ferrocene formic acid synthesis, comprises preparing catalyst for carbon nanotubes loaded with aluminum trichloride, adding, hydrolyzing, filtering, freezing, purifying, and melting.
AU CHEN D
AE DONGGUAN LIANZHOU INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY (DOGN-C)
GA 201885067Y
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing supercritical carbon dioxide liquid based on ferrocene formic acid synthesis, comprises (i) adding aluminum trichloride to the solvent in an ice bath environment, adding carbon nanotubes, heating to completely dissolve, and stirring uniformly to obtain a catalyst for carbon nanotubes loaded with aluminum trichloride; (ii) dissolving the ferrocene in toluene, dissolving completely, transferring to a supercritical device, introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, adding slowly catalyst of the prepared aluminum chloride-loaded carbon nanotube during the slow heating process, reacting at constant temperature, adding strong acid, hydrolyzing by ice bath, filtering to obtain crude ferrocene carboxylic acid; and (iii) extracting the crude ferrocene carboxylic acid with toluene, and subjecting to cryostat freezing, purifying and melting.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing supercritical carbon dioxide liquid based on ferrocene formic acid synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves reaction rate; and improves yield and purity of product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide or N,N-dimethylacetamide. In the step (i), the heating temperature is 30-50 degrees C. In the step (i), the carbon nanotubes are multi-walled carbon nanotubes. In the step (i), the mass ratio of the aluminum trichloride to the carbon nanotubes is 1:2.5-4. In the step (ii), the ratio of the amount of the substance of the ferrocene and the aluminum trichloride is 1:1.6-2.2. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the rate of slow temperature rise is 0.3-0.5 degrees C/minute. In the step (ii), the temperature of the constant temperature reaction is 40-45 degrees C, the pressure is 7.5-9 MPa, and the reaction time is 5-60 minutes. In the step (iii), the temperature of the cryostat freezing and purifying treatment is minus 10 to minus 5 degrees C, and the time is 30-60 minutes.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E05-L02A; E11-F02B; J04-E01; J04-E04; J04-E11; J04-F02B; N01-C03; N06-F; N07-D02B

PN KR1833804-B1; WO2018151526-A1
TI Manufacture of complex calcium carbonate used as            e.g. mineral filler, involves immersing bottom ash from            power plant in water, introducing carbon dioxide at            preset flow rate into obtained mixture, and performing            solid-liquid separation.
AU WHAN A J
   AHN J
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 201833310C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of complex calcium carbonate                involves (a) immersing a bottom ash from a                circulating fluidized bed power plant in water to                produce a mixture, (b) introducing carbon dioxide                into the prepared mixture to form calcium oxide in                the bottom ash with calcium carbonate, and (c)                performing solid-liquid separation of the obtained                mixture. The bottom ash comprises magnesium oxide,                potassium oxide and sodium oxide. The carbon                dioxide generated from the circulating fluidized                bed power plant is introduced at a flow rate of                0.1-2.0 L/minute.
   USE - Manufacture of complex calcium carbonate used                as concrete admixture and mineral filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of reducing the amount                of carbon dioxide generated from power plants,                thereby minimizing the generation of environmental                pollutants such as greenhouse gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The bottom ash is                  immersed in 10-50 wt.% water. The step (a)                  involves stirring the bottom ash at 300-600 rpm                  under normal temperature and pressure conditions                  after immersion step. The step (b) is terminated                  when the pH of the mixture is 6.5 or less. The                  solid-liquid separation is carried out using an                  aspirator having a pressure of 20-500 mm                  mercury.

PN US2021147231-A1
TI Production of gas involve dynamically mixing dry particles comprising carbon dioxide precursor and dry particles comprising proton-generating species, maintaining static, further dynamically mixing and produced gas at preset controlled rate.
AU ISAAC T
   ERNST W
AE ICA TRINOVA LLC (ICAT-Non-standard)
GA 2021538710
AB    NOVELTY - Production of gas involve dynamically mixing dry particles comprising a precursor and dry particles comprising a proton-generating species for time (t1), maintaining static for time (t2), further dynamically mixing for time (t3), producing gas at a rate controlled by controlling the time (t1), (t2) and/or (t3). The dry precursor particles comprise a carbon dioxide precursor, and the gas comprises carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas can be produced efficiently at variable rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: 1-75% Precursor is converted to the gas. The carbon dioxide precursor comprises a carbon-containing compound selected from carbonates, bicarbonates and sesquicarbonate. The dry particles comprising the precursor further comprise a porous carrier selected from zeolite crystals, silica, pumice, diatomaceous earth, bentonite, and clay, and the precursor is impregnated in the porous carrier. The dry particles comprising the proton-generating species further comprise a porous carrier selected from zeolite crystals, silica, pumice, diatomaceous earth, bentonite, and clay, and the proton-generating species is impregnated in the porous carrier. The dry particles contain 1-50 wt.% precursor. The dry particles comprise 1-50 wt.% proton-generating species chosen from organic acid and/or an inorganic acid selected from acetic acid, citric acid, hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, propionic acid and sulfuric acid. The proton-generating species comprises a metal salt selected from ferric chloride, ferric sulfate, calcium chloride, zinc sulfate, zinc chloride, cobalt sulfate, cobalt chloride, manganese sulfate, manganese chloride, copper sulfate, copper chloride, magnesium sulfate, sodium acetate, sodium citrate, sodium sulfate, sodium bisulfate, hydrogen phosphate and disodium hydrogen phosphate.

PT P
PN CN112366340-A; CN213212195-U; CN112366340-B
TI Fuel cell for separating carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, comprises cathode layer, composite electrolyte layer and anode layer, where composite electrolyte layer is composed of carbonate ion transport layer and oxygen ion transport layer.
AU LU Y
   YAN S
   MA L
AE UNIV NANJING XIAOZHUANG (UNJX-C)
   UNIV NANJING XIAOZHUANG (UNJX-C)
GA 202118623Y
AB    NOVELTY - Fuel cell based on molten carbonate and solid oxide composite electrolyte comprises a cathode layer, a composite electrolyte layer and an anode layer. The composite electrolyte layer comprises a carbonate ion transport layer and an oxygen ion transport layer.
   USE - Fuel cell used in separating carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel cell combines molten carbonate fuel cell with solid oxide fuel cell, realizes separation of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and has wide market application value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate ion transport layer is samarium doped cerium oxide-carbonate electrolyte layer. An oxygen ion transport layer is samarium doped cerium oxide. The cathode layer is porous metal nickel. The surface of the anode layer is coated with NCAL foam nickel. Preferred Process: The carbonate is immersed into the samarium-doped cerium oxide through a high-temperature dipping method by mixing carbonate and samarium-doped cerium oxide at a molar ratio of 1:1 to obtain mixed powder materials, after ball milling, adding the mixed powder material to the polyvinyl alcohol solution, stirring to obtain a slurry, immerse the pretreated organic foam in the slurry, and drying the organic foam containing the slurry when the slurry is full of foam, after obtaining the porous ceramic body. The porous ceramic sheet material is obtained, and then the porous ceramic sheet material is ground into nano-scale composite powder material. The composite powder material is hot pressed to obtain a samarium-doped cerium oxide-carbonate electrolyte layer. The carbonate is made by mixing 62 wt.% Li2CO3 and 38 wt.% K2CO3. The pretreatment of the organic foam includes putting the washed polyurethane sponge into a NaOH solution and soaking it in a water bath at 60-65 degrees C for 30-35 minutes.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN BR102018003076-A2
TI Preparing oil materials by using brown leaves as source of silicon in carbon dioxide gas adsorption, involves collecting and burned banana leaves, selecting leaf ash, and extracting silicon of leaf ash by sodium hydroxide leaching method.
AU RADOVANOVIC E
   FAVARO ROSA S L
   ARROYO P A
   BARON O
   MOISES M P
AE UNIV ESTADUAL MARINGA (UYES-Non-standard)
   UNIV PARANA TECNOLOGICA FEDERAL (UYPA-Non-standard)
GA 201982295F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oil materials by using brown leaves as source of silicon in carbon dioxide gas adsorption, involves collecting and burned banana leaves, selecting leaf ash, extracting silicon of leaf ash by sodium hydroxide leaching method and synthesizing zeolitic material by hydrothermal method.
   USE - Method for preparing oil materials in carbon dioxide gas adsorption and in catalytic processes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare oil materials that has thermal capacity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The biomass burner is used at 110-550 degrees C. The selection and separation of grains of ash of leaf with grain size of less than 250 micrometres. Preferred Process: The sodium hydroxide solution is used for leaching with concentrations ranging from 1-2 mole/L for 30-60 minutes at 90-110 degrees C. The sodium aluminate is dissolved in distilled water with aluminum concentration corresponding to a silicon/aluminum ratio equal to 1 and mixed with the leached extract, under agitation to form amorphous gel. The crystallization of the zeolite is perfromed at 80-95 degrees C for a time of 24-168 hours. The zeolites obtained from banana leaf ash is used as a source of disillusion for the removal of metallic cations in water to remove pollutants in water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Microorganism cultivation method comprises (S1) designing microorganism cultivation device, (S2) when cultivating, the staff opens the box door of the microorganism cultivation device and placing the configured aerobic microorganism culture dish in the groove on the eccentric wheel, then closing the box door, and turning on the power supply of the control motor, (S3) driving the drive shaft of the motor to the eccentric wheel to rotate, (S4) rotating plate alternately contacts the push plate when it swings alternately, and (S5) aerobic microorganisms gradually consume oxygen to produce carbon dioxide, and the water in the box can also settle after the water is sprayed from the atomizing nozzle to prevent the dust from entering the petri dish and affecting the growth of microorganisms.
   USE - The method is useful for microorganism cultivation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Microorganism cultivation method comprises (S1) designing microorganism cultivation device, (S2) when cultivating, the staff opens the box door of the microorganism cultivation device and placing the configured aerobic microorganism culture dish in the groove on the eccentric wheel, then closing the box door, and turning on the power supply of the control motor, (S3) driving the drive shaft of the motor to the eccentric wheel to rotate, the eccentric wheel drives the first connecting rod to rotate with the eccentric wheel as the center of the circle, when the first connecting rod rotates, pulling the rotating rod through the third connecting rod to make the disk rotate on a fixed axis, when the rotating rod rotates following the disc to the farthest position from the eccentric wheel, pulling the first link to the third link so that the first link and the third link are in the same straight line state, at the same time, in the process that the third connecting rod and the first connecting rod are gradually approaching the same straight line, the rotating rod rotates, therefore the rotating plate is driven to rotate, the rotating plate disturbs the gas flow in the box, uniformly distributing oxygen and carbon dioxide in the box to avoid all carbon dioxide generated by the metabolism of aerobic microorganisms from accumulating in the lower part of the box to affect the reproduction of aerobic microorganisms, (S4) rotating plate alternately contacts the push plate when it swings alternately, pushes the push plate alternately, when the push plate is pushed, the piston rod will slide in the direction of the box body, when the pressure in the box body is less than the elastic force of the second spring, the push plate pushes the mounting ring to move toward the box body through the second spring, the cylindrical ratchet on the mounting ring slides into the box body through the mounting cylinder, the rectangular plate on the cylindrical ratchet is slid in the same direction as the cylindrical ratchet in the box body, the rectangular plate can pressurize the oxygen in the box body when sliding, at the same time, when the cylindrical ratchet slides into the box, the teeth on the cylindrical ratchet first push the two symmetrically arranged blocks to overcome the elastic force of the first spring and move away from each other, the cylindrical ratchet continues to move in the direction of the box body, so that the teeth of the cylindrical ratchet that push away the blocking block run to the inside of the installation cylinder, at this time, the two blocks are no longer subjected to the force of the cylindrical ratchet, then the elastic force of the first spring brings the two blocks closer to each other, and blocks the teeth on the cylindrical ratchet, the cylindrical ratchet will not slide in the direction away from the box body, and the pressurized oxygen entering the box body can maintain a certain pressure, and (S5) aerobic microorganisms gradually consume oxygen to produce carbon dioxide, when the carbon dioxide concentration reaches a predetermined value, the carbon dioxide concentration sensor controls the solenoid valve to open, the oxygen under high pressure in the box body can flow through the gas injection pipe at this time, when the oxygen flows in the gas injection pipe, the pressure in the gas injection pipe will increase, the control rod is in contact with the inner wall of the gas injection pipe, in this state, the control rod can overcome the elastic force of the third spring to slide downward, when the control rod slides down, the through hole on the control rod first connects with the water outlet pipe, at this time, the water in the water tank with internal pressure will be sprayed into the tank through the water outlet pipe and the atomizing nozzle under the action of its pressure, the humidity in the box can be maintained within the range required by aerobic microorganisms, and the water in the box can also settle after the water is sprayed from the atomizing nozzle to prevent the dust from entering the petri dish and affecting the growth of microorganisms.

PN CN112662313-A; CN112662313-B
TI Preparation method of polyphosphazene modified polyester imine water-based thermal conductive coating comprises e.g. dissolving materials, adding tetrabutyl titanate catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide gas and adding grafting catalyst.
AU LI Z
AE ANHUI HIPP POLYMER MATERIALS CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202150940Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of polyphosphazene modified polyester imine water-based thermal conductive coating comprises (i) adding 4-bromonaphthalene-1,8-dicarboxylic anhydride, maleic anhydride, hexamethylene diamine, dimethylpropanediol, dimethylolpropionic acid and spirodiol, heating, dissolving the materials in the reactor, adding tetrabutyl titanate catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heating, heat preserving, stopping the carbon dioxide, adding dicyclopentadiene and dimethylthiotoluenediamine, heating the material in the kettle, stirring, adding hydroquinone, stirring and heat preserving, cooling, adding grafting catalyst, dropping glycidyl methacrylate, and (ii) combining polyester imine emulsion, urushiol polyphosphazene emulsion, curing agent, inorganic polymer thermal conductive particles and accelerator, heating, incubating, cooling, adding emulsifier EM90, stirring, heating, adding deionized water, continuously stirring, stirring, and adding water.
   USE - The coating is useful in parts that require heat transfer and heat dissipation in insulation and flame retardant applications and for heat dissipation or heat conduction in insulation and flame retardant applications e.g. electronic appliances.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating is non-toxic and environmentally friendly, odorless, minimal volatile matter, non-flammable and non-explosive and has high safety, good insulation, corrosion resistance and thermal conductivity, strong bonding force with the coated substrate, resistant to mechanical shock and thermal shock, high strength wear resistance, corrosion resistance, high temperature resistance and flame retardant properties. The method has no need to use additional metal thermal pads and carbon thermal conductive materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method of polyphosphazene modified polyester imine water-based thermal conductive coating comprises (i) adding 157.8-161.5 pts. wt. 4-bromonaphthalene-1,8-dicarboxylic anhydride, 34.5-37.8 pts. wt. maleic anhydride, 62.5-64.8 pts. wt. hexamethylene diamine, 68.5-71 pts. wt. dimethylpropanediol, 27.3-31.9 pts. wt. dimethylolpropionic acid and 18-21 pts. wt. spirodiol, heating to 130-135 degrees C, dissolving the materials in the reactor, turning on the stirring for 0.5-1 hour, adding 0.47-0.51 pts. wt. tetrabutyl titanate catalyst, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas with flow rate of carbon dioxide gas is 0.1-0.2 m3/minute, heating to 180-185 degrees C, heat preserving for 4-5 hours, stopping the carbon dioxide, adding 48-53 pts. wt. dicyclopentadiene and 25-27 pts. wt. dimethylthiotoluenediamine under stirring, heating the material in the kettle to 185-190 degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/hour, stirring for 0.5-1 hour, adding 12-14 pts. wt. hydroquinone, stirring and heat preserving at 195-200 degrees C for 1-2 hours, cooling to 145-150 degrees C, adding grafting catalyst under the protection of nitrogen atmosphere, dropping 38-41 pts. wt. glycidyl methacrylate, stirring to react, completing the dropping within 35-40 minutes, stopping the reaction, stirring for 1-2.5 hours, cooling to 60-65 degrees C, adding 12-14 pts. wt. dimethylaminoethoxyethanol and 0.02-0.05 pts. wt. emulsifier, slowly adding deionized water, dispersing at high speed for 15-20 minutes, adjusting the pH of the system to 7-8 and the solid content to 46.5-48% to obtain polyester imine emulsion, and (ii) combining 45-53 pts. wt. polyester imine emulsion, 35-55 pts. wt. urushiol polyphosphazene emulsion, 1-2 pts. wt. curing agent, 14-16 pts. wt. inorganic polymer thermal conductive particles and 0.3-0.6 pts. wt. accelerator, uniformly stirring at 65-70 degrees C and adding water to adjust to a solid content of 36-38% to obtain a polyphosphazene modified polyester imine aqueous-based thermal conductive coating. The synthesis method of the urushiol polyphosphazene emulsion comprises drying ampoules, washing the ampoules with chromic acid lotion and distilled water three times in order to be clean before use, drying in a 150 degrees C drying oven for 2 hours, and cooling to 25 degrees C under a vacuum of -0.08 MPa, placing the hexachlorocyclotriphosphazene in an ampoule, vacuuming at room temperature for 20-30 minutes, using an alcohol blowtorch to melt and seal the mouth of the bottle under a vacuum of -0.08 MPa, placing the sealed ampoule in a heating furnace with a temperature control device, performing a polycondensation reaction at 240-250 degrees C for 6-10 hours to obtain a white polyphosphazene prepolymer, adding 478-491 pts. wt. 45-50% urushiol solution by into the mixer, stirring in the reactor of the thermometer and condenser, adding the obtained 280-285 pts. wt. white polyphosphazene prepolymer, 53-56 pts. wt. 33-34% formaldehyde solution and 1.2-1.5 pts. wt. urotropine, stirring 18-20 pts. wt. 14-15% aqueous ammonia uniformly, stirring and reacting at 80-90 degrees C for 50-60 minutes, heating to 120-130 degrees C, incubating for 1-2 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding 0.2-0.4 emulsifier EM90, stirring evenly, heating to 50-60 degrees C, stirring at a rate of 1000-2000 revolutions/minute, adding deionized water at the rate of pts. wt./minute, continuously stirring for 0.5-1 hours until the reverse rotation occurs, stirring evenly at 65-70 degrees C, and adding water to adjust to a solid content of 36-38%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the thermally conductive particles are polyphosphazene nanotubes or polyphosphazene microspheres and curing agent is cumene hydroperoxide or potassium persulfate and/or quaternary ammonium-dimercapto-1,3,4-triazole. The accelerator is potassium acetate, triethylenediamine, or zinc diethyldithiocarbamate. The emulsifier is sodium itaconic acid monododecyl propyl sulfonate, sodium acrylamidoisopropyl sulfonate, or sodium allyloxy hydroxy sulfonate. The grafting catalyst is ammonium persulfate or azobisisobutamidine hydrochloride.

PN IN201911018669-A
TI System for generation of bio-energy from industrial waste, has integrated bottling chamber that is configured to enable liquefaction of food grade carbon dioxide, and double membrane storage that is fluidically coupled to purification unit.
AU TIWARI K K
   TIWARI Y
AE TIWARI Y (TIWA-Individual)
GA 2020B59259
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a mixing tank (102) that is provided for receiving the industrial waste from sources. The mixing tank is arranged to mix the received industrial waste with water in a predefined ratio. A screening chamber (104) is fluidically coupled with the mixing tank and is arranged to receive the industrial waste mixed water from the mixing tank. The floating objects are segregated from the industrial waste mixed water. The purification unit (108) is configured to receive the bio gas from the multi-feed digester and allows the received bio gas to pass through a membrane configured to remove one or more impurities including hydrogen sulphide from the bio gas. The purified bio gas is processed to generate food grade carbon dioxide. The integrated bottling chamber is configured to enable liquefaction of the food grade carbon dioxide. A double membrane storage (110) is fluidically coupled to the purification unit and stores the bio gas, if the produced bio gas is in excess.
   USE - System for generation of bio-energy from industrial waste such as press mud, molasses, spent wash, dairy waste, paper-mill waste, and agricultural waste (all claimed). Uses include but not limited to agricultural waste, manure, municipal waste, plant material, sewage, green waste, and food waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The spectrum of production ability is increased. The received bio gas is allowed to pass through a membrane to remove impurities. The purification tank cleans the bio-gas for high efficiency of the bio-gas obtained free from impurities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The multi-feed digester is fluidically coupled with the screening chamber and is arranged to receive the industrial waste to form a composition. The composition is treated with heat, enzymes, or a chemical solvent to promote decomposition of the composition in a predefined pressure and a predefined temperature to produce bio gas. A purification unit is fluidically coupled with the multi-feed digester and an integrated bottling chamber. The composition is provided with a pressmud in a predefined ratio in a certain range in combination with spentwash in a predefined ratio. A pressmud is in a predefined ratio in a specific range in combination with molasses in a predefined ratio in a specific range. A pressmud in a predefined ratio in a specific range is in combination with spentwash in a predefined ratio in combination with dairy effluent in a predefined ratio in a specific range in combination with paper mill waste in a predefined ratio in a predetermined range. The predefined temperature is in a range of 37 -41 degrees Celsius where the predefine pressure in a range of 1 - 3 pound-force per square inch. The sensor is arranged in the multi-feed digester to monitor a temperature value or a pressure value in the multifeed digester. A pressure releasing valves is provided to release excess pressure from the multi-feed digester when the sensor records a pressure value above a threshold pressure value. A flare system is arranged to burn the excess bio gas. The industrial waste is mixed with water in a specified ratio. The multi-feeder digester is arranged to induce anaerobic digestion to generate the bio-gas. A fertilizer tank is fluidically coupled with multi-feeder digester, where the fertilizer tank is arranged to receive slurry from the multi-feeder digester. The slurry is processed to generate fertilizers. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for generation of bio-energy from industrial waste, involves receiving the industrial waste from sources in a mixing tank for mixing the received industrial waste with water in a predefined ratio. The industrial waste mixed water is received from the mixing tank in screening chamber fluidically coupled with the mixing tank. The floating objects are segregated from the industrial waste mixed water. The industrial waste is received from the screening chamber to form a composition in a multi-feed digester fluidically coupled with the screening chamber. The composition comprises a combination of elements selected in a predefined ratio from a group of elements comprising of press mud, molasses, spent wash, dairy waste, paper-mill waste, and agricultural waste. The composition with heat, enzymes, or a chemical solvent is treated to promote decomposition of the composition in a predefined pressure and a predefined temperature in the multi-feed digester to produce bio gas. The bio gas are received from the multi-feed digester in a purification unit fluidically coupled with the multi-feed digester. The received bio gas is allowed to pass through a membrane configured to remove impurities including hydrogen sulphide from the bio gas. The purified bio gas is processed to generate food grade carbon dioxide and liquefying the food grade carbon dioxide to bottle the food grade carbon dioxide. The excess bio gas is stored in a double membrane storage fluidically coupled to the purification unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a system for generating bio-energy from industrial wastes.
   System for generation of bio-energy from industrial waste (100)
   Mixing tank (102)
   Screening chamber (104)
   Purification unit (108)
   Double membrane storage (110)

PT P
PN CN110590509-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide cyclic decomposition coking crude phenol co-production desulfurizer involves distilling high temperature coal tar to obtain mixed fraction of phenol content naphthalene oil washing oil and washing.
AU LIU D
   SU G
   LIU J
   FAN Y
   YAN M
AE SHANXI YONGDONG CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A7596X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide cyclic decomposition coking crude phenol co-production desulfurizer involves distilling high temperature coal tar to obtain a mixed fraction of phenol content naphthalene oil washing oil with a phenol content of 4-6%, and washing and dephenolizing with a sodium hydroxide (NaOH) aqueous solution in a washing tower, and the medium temperature is maintained at 80-90 degrees C. The NaOH aqueous solution and phenol oil naphthalene oil washing oil mixed fractions are brought into reverse flow contact, and the phenol and NaOH in the mixed fractions react to form 80-90 degrees C. The phenol sodium salt solution obtained in the previous step is sprayed into the steam blowing tower from the upper portion of the steam blowing tower, and 0.3 megapascal saturated steam is added to the bottom of the steam blowing tower. The temperature of the bottom of the steam blowing tower is maintained at 105-110 degrees C, and the sodium phenol sodium salt solution is purified.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide cyclic decomposition coking crude phenol co-production desulfurizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide cyclic decomposition coking crude phenol co-production desulfurizer involves distilling high temperature coal tar to obtain a mixed fraction of phenol content naphthalene oil washing oil with a phenol content of 4-6%, and washing and dephenolizing with a sodium hyfroxide (NaOH) aqueous solution in a washing tower, and the medium temperature is maintained at 80-90 degrees C. The NaOH aqueous solution and phenol oil naphthalene oil washing oil mixed fractions are brought into reverse flow contact, and the phenol and NaOH in the mixed fractions react to form 80-90 degrees C. The phenol sodium salt solution obtained in the previous step is sprayed into the steam blowing tower from the upper portion of the steam blowing tower, and 0.3 megapascal saturated steam is added to the bottom of the steam blowing tower. The temperature of the bottom of the steam blowing tower is maintained at 105-110 degrees C, and the sodium phenol sodium salt solution is purified. The purified phenol sodium salt solution flows out from the bottom of the steam blowing tower. The purified phenol sodium salt solution is transported to the upper portion of the primary decomposition tower with two layers of fillers, flows from top to bottom through the filler layer, and is in reverse contact with the rising air and carbon dioxide (CO2) mixed gas in the tower, and most of the phenol sodium salts. The CO2 in the mixed gas is decomposed to produce crude phenol and sodium carbonate (Na2CO3), and then flows into the bottom of the primary decomposition tower. The static separation is performed for 6-8 hours according to the specific gravity difference between the crude phenol and the Na2CO3 solution, and the separation of the crude phenol and the Na2CO3 solution is completed. After the separation, the lower Na2CO3 solution is transferred to the extraction tower using a centrifugal pump, and the upper crude phenol flows to the decomposition intermediate buffer tank. The crude phenol in the intermediate buffer tank is transported to the upper portion of the secondary decomposition tower with three layers of filler through the secondary decomposition tower raw material pump at a pressure of not less than 0.4 megapascal, and flows from the top to the bottom through the filler layer. It is in reverse contact with air and CO2 mixed gas from the bottom of the column, and a small amount of sodium phenol which is not decomposed in the primary decomposition column contained in the crude phenol is decomposed by CO2 in the mixed gas and flows into the secondary decomposition together with the decomposed crude phenol. The static separation is performed for 6-8 hours according to the specific gravity difference between the crude phenol and the Na2CO3 solution, and the crude phenol and the Na2CO3 solution are separated. The lower Na2CO3 solution is sent to the extraction tower using a centrifugal pump, and the upper crude phenol flows to the crude phenol finished product tank. The CO2 air mixed gas that has not participated in the reaction escapes from the top of the primary decomposition tower and the secondary decomposition tower and enters the inlet of the circulating fan to mix with the newly supplemented CO2 gas. After being pressurized by the circulation fan, they are respectively sent to the bottom of the primary decomposition tower and the secondary decomposition tower for recycling, hence establishing a gas circulation system for carbon dioxide decomposition. The Na2CO3 solution is mixed with the dephenolized phenol oil in a mass ratio of 1:0.4, and the layers are statically separated to obtain a purified Na2CO3 solution containing less than 0.05 wt.% phenol content. The upper phenol oil is returned to the washing tower, and mixed with NaOH aqueous solution to wash and dephenolize again. The purified Na2CO3 solution in the lower portion is pumped to the causticizer. In the causticizer, the Na2CO3 solution reacts with quicklime to generate Na2CO3, sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution, and calcium carbonate (CaCO3) precipitation. The solid CaCO3, Na2CO3, and NaOH solutions are separated by a centrifuge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The extraction and causticization reactions are in a closed 0.5-5 kilopascal negative pressure state. The volume of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the mixed gas of air and CO2 is 16-22%, and in order to maintain the circulation of the system CO2, the volume of the CO2 gas with a volume concentration greater than 95% is replenished at the inlet of the circulation fan. The mixed fractions of phenol oil naphthalene oil washing oil are washed and dephenolized in a washing tower with a mass concentration of 10-13 wt.% NaOH water. The oil content of the phenol sodium salt flowing out from the bottom of the steam blowing tower is less than 0.2%, that is, the phenol sodium salt solution is purified. The decomposition rate of phenol sodium salt to more than 99.5% through secondary decomposition.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC A01-E13; E10-E02B1; E11-F11; E11-Q02B; E31-F01A; E31-N05C

PT P
PN IN202221001395-A
TI Making biocomposite useful as e.g. packaging materials from lignocellulosic agricultural waste by sterilizing substrate, drying, adding nutritious materials e.g. sugars, inoculating e.g. liquid spawn, incubating and allowing to grow in carbon dioxide rich environment, molding and drying.
AU TOTALA S
   TOTALA M S
   KATARIYA S D
AE TOTALA S (TOTA-Individual)
   TOTALA M S (TOTA-Individual)
   KATARIYA S D (KATA-Individual)
GA 2022343669
AB    NOVELTY - Method for making a biocomposite from a lignocellulosic agricultural waste involves collecting the substrate, sterilizing to get free from microorganism, allowing the sterilized material to dry by sun drying, shed drying or machine drying, or their combinations as per requirement, adding nutritious materials such as sugars, sucrose, maltose, dextrose and maltodextrin or their combinations or the nutrition in the form of rice bran, wheat bran, calcium carbonate and calcium sulfate or their combinations, inoculating wheat, millet, any grain based spawn or liquid spawn or pure/sub culture grown on e.g. yeast starch medium of e.g. Pleurotus ostreatus, incubating the complete mixture to grow in e.g. grow bag, introducing the obtained mycelium rich substrate inside a mold, allowing to grow in carbon dioxide rich environment and drying the composite, material or structure in hot air exchanger, sun drying or oven drying to eliminate the complete moisture.
   USE - The method is useful for making biocomposite of any desired shape from a lignocellulosic agricultural waste such as wheat straw, rice straw, cotton waste, corn stover, corn curbs, rice husk, sugarcane leaves, soyabean waste, banana leaves, bagasse and saw dust or their combinations, which is useful as packaging materials, cushioning packaging, protective packaging, shock absorbing packaging, impact resistance material, foam materials, acoustic tiles, fire insulation panels, sound insulation panels, construction bricks, shoe soles, coffins and furniture.
   ADVANTAGE - The biocomposite is an environmentally-friendly, sustainable, biodegradable, eco-friendly, self-reliant, water-resistant, fire-resistant, compact, rigid, breathable, insulating, biodegradable and durable biocomposite and the process is carried out in completely sterile environment with utmost sincerity. The method uses mycelium (root structure of mushroom) which naturally digests the agricultural waste, thus acting as a natural binding polymer under specific environmental conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for making a biocomposite from a lignocellulosic agricultural waste involves (a) collecting the substrate such as lignocellulosic agricultural waste and sterilizing to get free from microorganism and prohibiting the mycelial hyphae growth by performing chemical sterilization using 5-10 weight/weight% carbendazim and 1-2 volume/volume% formalin and/or pasteurizing at 50-75 degrees C for 3-5 hours and/or autoclaving at 115-125 degrees C and at 14-17 psi for 2-5 hours and/or combination depending upon the type of lignocellulosic agricultural waste used as substrate, (b) allowing the sterilized material to dry until the moisture in the substrate becomes 60-80 weight/weight% of the sterilized substrate either by sun drying, shed drying or machine drying, or their combinations as per requirement, (c) adding 2-8 weight/weight% nutritious materials such as sugars, sucrose, maltose, dextrose and maltodextrin or their combinations to enhance the mycelial hyphae growth in the inoculated substrate or adding 2-8 weight/weight% nutrition in the form of rice bran, wheat bran, calcium carbonate and calcium sulfate or their combinations to provide sufficient nutrition for the growth, (d) inoculating 2-20 weight/weight% wheat, millet, any grain based spawn or liquid spawn or pure/sub culture grown on yeast starch medium, malt extract medium, potato dextrose agar medium, Thermomonospora, Thermoactinopolyspora, CM Medium, compost extract medium, nutrient agar medium, Hans medium, Ken Knight medium of Pleurotus ostreatus/Ganoderma lucidum/Pleurotus florida/Lentinula edodes/Pleurotus eryngii/Trametes versicolor or their combinations, (e) incubating the complete mixture to grow in a grow bag/autoclave bag/polypropylene bag/filter patch bag having a thickness of more than 50 mu m or high-density polyethylene (HDPE)/polypropylene/silicon containers for 5-15 days under constant temperature of 23-27 degrees C and 60-95% humidity at an elevated carbon dioxide concentration of 5000-20000 ppm, (f) opening the bags after the completion of incubation and breaking the mycelium bound substrate into tiny pieces mechanically by manual/semi-automatic/automatic processes, (g) introducing the obtained mycelium rich substrate inside a mold of any shape and size desired made of HDPE, low density polyethylene (LDPE), polypropylene, glass, metal, stainless steel or silicon and sealing completely to cut off any gaseous exchange, at 23-27 degrees C and 50-99% humidity at an elevated carbon dioxide concentration of 10000-25000 ppm for 4-14 days, (h) popping out the obtained mycelium bounded substrate of agricultural waste from the molds and allowing to grow in carbon dioxide rich environment with 10000-35000 ppm for 1-5 days and (i) drying the composite, material or structure in hot air exchanger, sun drying or oven drying to eliminate the complete moisture and achieving a constant weight by subjecting to continuous heat for longer duration of time to get desired product.
TF EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Experimental device for replacing gas in a coal            seam with mixed gas, comprises flow meter that is            connected with air inlet of one-way valve and air            outlet of one of drying tanks, and confining pressure            pump connected to coal sample holder.
AU BAI G
   FU X
   ZHU Z
   LI S
   ZHOU L
   WEN H
   LAI X
   FAN C
AE UNIV LIAONING TECHNICAL (ULNT-C)
GA 202298463N
AB    NOVELTY - Experimental device comprises a methane gas                storage tank (1), a carbon dioxide air storage tank                and a nitrogen gas storage-storage tank are                arranged in parallel. The gas outlet of the methane                gas storage tank is communicated with the gas inlet                of the first one-way valve (4). The air outlet of a                first flow meter, a second flow meter and a second                outlet valve, sodium hydroxide adsorption tank                (15), a third flow meter, a second air inlet and a                fourth flow meter are sequentially communicated                with each other. The methane gas concentration                sensor, the carbon dioxide concentration sensor is                connected with the nitrogen storage tank to provide                a shaft pressure load for the coal sample. The                confining pressure pump (9) is connected to a coal                sample holder (7) and is used to provide the coal                samples with a confined pressure load.
   USE - Experimental device for replacing gas in a                coal seam with mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The device replaces and displaces coal bed by                mixed gas, which changes the defect that the                original only uses single carbon dioxide gas as the                research object, and can apply tri-axial stress to                the coal sample in the experiment device, further                improving the methane gas displacement replacing                efficiency at the same time, where the experiment                result is closer to the actual test result of the                field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for replacing and displacing gas in the coal seam                by using the mixed gas, which involves cutting the                coal blocks into cylindrical coal samples according                to vertical textures, horizontal textures, or                oblique textures, pasting a strain gauge on the                surface of a coal sample, sending the sample                assembly into the pressure chamber cylinder and                placing the sample assembly at the upper end of the                piston rod of the actuator, installing a sealing                cover plug and a counter-force ejector rod on the                top end of the pressure chamber cylinder body,                setting the outlet pressure of the first reducing                valve and the fourth reducing valve, starting a                confining pressure pump, closing the confining                pressure unloading function of the confining                pressure pump, setting the outlet pressure of the                second pressure reducing valve, and then analyzing                all the collected data by the computer until carbon                dioxide gas is obtained.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the experimental device for replacing                gas in a coal seam with mixed gas.

PN WO2021244727-A1
TI Producing precipitated calcium carbonate and silica concentrate by adding solid material bearing calcium and silica into extraction solution, separating solid residual material, carbonating, separating calcium carbonate precipitate from solution, directing residual material, and separating residual.
AU MOHAMED H A
   KIHL A
   SALUSTE A
   UIBU M
   TRIKKEL A
   KUUSIK R
   TAMM K
   LEIER A
   MOTLEP R
   KIRSIMAEE K
AE R-S OSA SERVICE OUE (RSOS-Non-standard)
GA 2021E0413V
AB    NOVELTY - Multistage method for producing precipitated calcium carbonate and silica concentrate involves (a) adding solid material bearing calcium and silica into the extraction solution in a continuously stirred extraction reactor, (b) separating the solid residual material from the calcium rich solution, (c) subjecting the calcium rich solution to carbonation, in the carbonation reactor, to precipitate calcium carbonate and inducing the carbonation by introducing carbon dioxide gas directly into the calcium containing aqueous solution in a closed reactor, (d) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from solution, (e) directing the residual material and the recovered extraction solution to an extraction reactor, (f) separating the solid residual material from calcium rich solution, (g) subjecting the calcium rich solution to carbonation in the carbonation reactor, to precipitate calcium carbonate, and (i) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from solution of the previous step.
   USE - The method is useful for producing precipitated calcium carbonate and silica concentrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces extraction of finite material resources, achieves degree of circularity in industries that produce and utilize precipitated calcium carbonate and silicate minerals, reduces mass of output residual material, reduces energy consumption per unit mass of calcium carbonate product produced, maximizes carbon dioxide captured and utilized per unit mass of input material, reduces the chemical consumption per unit mass of calcium carbonate precipitate produced, is cost-effective, reduces the alkalinity of the residual material, consequently lowering the ecotoxicity of the material, and concentrates the silica and magnesium in the residual material for after extraction or use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multistage method for producing precipitated calcium carbonate and silica concentrate from calcium and silica containing waste and by-products involves (a) adding solid material bearing calcium and silica into the extraction solution in a continuously stirred extraction reactor, in which the solid material bearing calcium and silica is an oil shale ash or coal ash or waste cement, (b) separating the solid residual material from the calcium rich solution, (c) subjecting the calcium rich solution to carbonation, in the carbonation reactor, to precipitate calcium carbonate and inducing the carbonation by introducing carbon dioxide gas directly into the calcium containing aqueous solution in a closed reactor, (d) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from solution, (e) directing the residual material and the recovered extraction solution to an extraction reactor, where a second calcium extraction step takes place, (f) separating the solid residual material from calcium rich solution, (g) subjecting the calcium rich solution to carbonation, in the carbonation reactor, to precipitate calcium carbonate, in which the carbonation is induced by introducing carbon dioxide gas directly into the calcium containing aqueous solution in a closed reactor, and (i) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from solution of the previous step.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium extracting agent comprises an ammonium based aqueous solution, preferably ammonium chloride, and ammonium acetate.

TI Composition used to treat e.g. airway disease e.g. pulmonary disease, preferably recurrent airway obstruction in horses, comprises solvent of mixture of water and ethanol, glucocorticoid of ciclesonide, and long-acting muscarinic antagonist.
AU ALBRECHT B
   AVEN M
   LAMAR J
AE BOEHRINGER INGELHEIM VETMEDICA GMBH (BOEH-C)
GA 201974445C
AB    NOVELTY - Composition in the form of a liquid formulation, comprises: a solvent comprising a mixture of water and ethanol, where the ethanol comprises 90-95 vol.%; a glucocorticoid comprising ciclesonide or its salts; and a long-acting muscarinic antagonist (LAMA) or its salts of the cation having substituted tertiary nitrogen compounds (I), and the LAMA comprises 0.2-7 mass% or vol.% of the total composition, and ciclesonide or its salt provided in the composition of 3 g/100 ml.
   USE - The composition is useful for treating airway disease, e.g. pulmonary disease, preferably recurrent airway obstruction, summer pasture associated obstructive pulmonary disease and inflammatory airway disease in animals, preferably horses.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: provides a medication for horses which allows the treatment of airway disease in horses with a quick onset of action, while reducing the risk of side effects for the treated animals; and utilizes the LAMA that improves lung function, increases blood, reduces partial pressure of carbon dioxide, and improves lung function and clinical score. The ability of long-acting muscarinic antagonist to reduce partial pressure of carbon dioxide was tested in cross-over, blinded moldy hay challenge study in 8 horses by measuring change in partial pressure of carbon dioxide using biological assays. The results showed that 800 mu g of 2-((R)-2-(2,2-diphenylpropanoyloxy)butyl)-3,3-dimethyl-1,3-oxazetidin-3-ium bromide exhibited reduces partial pressure of carbon dioxide on day 7 as compared to day 0.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composition in the form of a liquid formulation, comprises: a solvent comprising a mixture of water and ethanol, where the ethanol comprises 90-95 vol.%; a glucocorticoid comprising ciclesonide or its salts; and a long-acting muscarinic antagonist (LAMA) or its salts of the cation having substituted tertiary nitrogen compounds of formula (I), optionally in the form of the individual optical isomers, mixtures of the individual enantiomers or racemates, and the LAMA comprises 0.2-7 mass% or vol.% of the total composition, and ciclesonide or its salt provided in the composition of 3 g/100 ml.
   A = double-bonded group comprising -CH2-CH2-, -CH=CH- or substituted cyclopropyl moieties of formula (II);
   X- = anion with a single negative charge;
   R1, R2 = 1-4C alkyl (optionally substituted by OH or halo);
   R3-R6 = H, 1-4C alkyl, 1-4C alkyloxy, OH, CF3, CN, NO2 or halo; and
   R7 = H, 1-4C alkyl, 1-4C alkyloxy, 1-4C-alkylene-halo, halo-1-4C-alkyloxy, 1-4C-alkylene-OH, CF3, -1-4C-alkylene-1-4C-alkyloxy, -O-CO-1-4C-alkyl, -O-CO-1-4C-alkyl-halo, -O-COCF3 or halo, if A denotes -CH2-CH2-, R1, R2 denote methyl and R3-R6 denote H.

Preferred Components: The LAMA comprises 2-((R)-2-(2,2-diphenylpropanoyloxy)butyl)-3,3-dimethyl-1,3-oxazetidin-3-ium bromide. The LAMA comprises 0.3-6 (preferably 0.4-5) mass% or vol.% of the total composition. The glucocorticoid further comprises budesonide or its salts. 8. The liquid formation comprises a solution for inhalation. The liquid formulation is a partially ethanolic formulation.

   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Muscarinic antagonist.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the composition is by inhalation (claimed), parenteral or oral routes. Dosage of the composition is 100-3000 (preferably 200-800) mu g.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 0.446 g/100 ml 2-((R)-2-(2,2-diphenylpropanoyloxy)butyl)-3,3-dimethyl-1,3-oxazetidin-3-ium bromide; 0.01 g/100 ml benzalkonium chloride; 0.01 g/100 ml edetate disodium; adding hydrochloric acid (0.1 M); and balance of water to make up to 100 ml.

PN CN109621699-A
TI Carbon dioxide capture method based on chemical absorption and biotransformation enhanced by wastewater culture by inoculating chlorella L38 separately, adding BG-11 medium, culturing, centrifuging tofu wastewater, filtering, and heating.
AU SONG C
   QIU Y
   XIE M
   QI Y
   LIU Q
   JI N
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201938738Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide (CO2) capture method based on chemical absorption and biotransformation enhanced by wastewater culture comprises inoculating chlorella L38 separately in a container, adding 10 vol.% BG-11 medium, and culturing in light incubator using white fluorescent illumination under filtered CO2 conditions; centrifuging tofu wastewater, filtering, heating, and adding different concentrations of ammonium bicarbonate; and performing chlorella culture experiment using two modes comprising direct feeding and batch feeding. The batch feeding is done by adding 20 mL and 10 mL tofu wastewater respectively with absorption liquid, adding 6.6 mL and 3.3 mL culture medium respectively on the 6th day, 12th day and 18th day of culture cycle, feeding once every 6 days for 3 times, inoculating separately the chlorella L38, controlling initial absorbance to 0.1-0.2, and culturing at 22-25 degrees C using white fluorescent illumination of 4000-6000 Lx.
   USE - Method for carbon dioxide capture based on chemical absorption and biotransformation enhanced by wastewater culture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method replaces nutrient source provided by BG 11 with wastewater which is more economical, and has potential advantages compared with traditional processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide (CO2) capture method based on chemical absorption and biotransformation enhanced by wastewater culture comprises inoculating chlorella L38 separately in a container, adding 10 vol.% BG-11 medium, and culturing in light incubator for 7-10 days at 22-25 degrees C using white fluorescent illumination of 4000-6000 Lx under 20-40 mL/minute 15% filtered CO2 conditions; centrifuging tofu wastewater, filtering, heating to 100-121 degrees C, and adding different concentrations of ammonium bicarbonate at ratio of 1:4 or 1:9 until final concentration of ammonia nitrogen is 248 mg/; and performing chlorella culture experiment using two modes comprising direct feeding and batch feeding. The direct feeding is done by adding 200 mL tofu wastewater samples to six 250 mL erlenmeyer flasks each with a dilution ratio of 3 bottles. The batch feeding is done by adding 20 mL and 10 mL tofu wastewater respectively with absorption liquid, adding 6.6 mL and 3.3 mL culture medium respectively on the 6th day, 12th day and 18th day of culture cycle, feeding once every 6 days for 3 times, inoculating separately the chlorella L38, controlling initial absorbance to 0.1-0.2, and culturing at 22-25 degrees C using white fluorescent illumination of 4000-6000 Lx. The tofu wastewater has chemical oxygen demand (COD) of 21.1 plus minus 0.26 g/L, total nitrogen of 0.34 plus minus 0.01 g/L, total phosphorus of 0.085 plus minus 0.002 g/L, and ammonia nitrogen of 0.12 plus minus 0.015 g/L.

PT P
PN CN109095474-A; CN109095474-B
TI Removing impurity of potassium feldspar comprises crushing potassium feldspar raw ore by a crusher, washing, removing sludge, dehydrating, ball-milling, filling, heating, stirring, filtering, and drying feldspar particles.
AU DENG P
AE HEZHOU JUNXIN MINERAL PROD CO LTD (HEZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019024751
AB    NOVELTY - Removing impurity of potassium feldspar comprises (1) crushing potassium feldspar raw ore by a crusher, washing the spiral chute, removing sludge and dehydrating, (2) placing washed feldspar ore into first electromagnetic mineral separation device, removing the magnetic impurities, (3) ball-milling above-mentioned magnetic separation treatment of potassium feldspar by a first ball mill, (4) adding feldspar particles, water and a capturing agent into the reactor, filling carbon dioxide, heating, starting stirring rod, stirring, vacuum drying and filtering, (5) ball-milling high pressure treated K-feldspar particles by a second ball mill, (6) passing feldspar particles of the secondary milling process through a second electromagnetic mineral separation device, (7) mixing above-mentioned secondary magnetic separation treated K-feldspar particles in a sulfuric acid solution, stirring, filtering, water washing till the filtrate is neutral and (8) drying feldspar particles.
   USE - The method is useful for removing impurity of potassium feldspar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method high whiteness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing impurity of potassium feldspar comprises (1) crushing the potassium feldspar raw ore by a crusher until the granularity is 5-8 cm, washing the spiral chute, removing sludge and dehydrating, (2) placing washed feldspar ore into first electromagnetic mineral separation device, removing the magnetic impurities, (3) ball-milling above-mentioned magnetic separation treatment of potassium feldspar by a first ball mill to a particle size of 30-50 mesh to obtain potassium feldspar particles, (4) adding feldspar particles, water and a capturing agent into the reactor, where the reactor is vacuumized to vacuum degree of 0.06-0.08MPa, filling carbon dioxide which purity is 99% or more and the reactor pressure is 13-18MPa, and heating to 55-60 degrees C, starting stirring rod for 40-50 minutes, continuously inletting carbon dioxide in the stirring process to make the reactor pressure is maintained at 13-18MPa, finishing stirring, vacuum drying and filtering, (5) ball-milling high pressure treated K-feldspar particles by a second ball mill to a particle size of 100-120 mesh, (6) passing the feldspar particles of the secondary milling process through a second electromagnetic mineral separation device, (7) mixing above-mentioned secondary magnetic separation treated K-feldspar particles in a sulfuric acid solution, pouring into a high pressure reactor, where thereactor is vacuumized to vacuum degree of 0.06-0.08MPa, filling carbon dioxide which purity is 99% or more and the reactor vacuum degree is 0.02-0.04MPa, and heating to 55-60 degrees C, starting stirring rod for 60-80 minutes, stirring, filtering, water washing till the filtrate is neutral and (8) drying the feldspar particles at 100-110 degrees C to obtain potassium feldspar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the first electromagnetic mineral separation device is 0.7-0.9T, the rotating speed is 100-110 revolutions/minutes. In the step (4), the mixing ratio of the potassium feldspar particles, water, and the capture agent is 1Kg:2-3Kg:0.5-0.7 g. In the step (4), the capture agent is mainly prepared by mixing sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, linolenic acid and dodecylamine at a mass ratio of 3:1:1. In the step (6), the second electromagnetic mineral separation equipment of the magnetic field intensity is 1.1-1.2T, the rotating speed is 50-60 revolutions/minutes. In the step (7), the volume fraction of the sulfuric acid solution is 40%, mixing ratio of the potassium feldspar particles and the sulfuric acid solution is 100 g:35-45 ml.

PN CN108585093-A
TI System for processing garbage percolation liquid            to recover ammonium bicarbonate, comprises an ammonia            removing tower, and a steam inlet of the ammonia            removing tower which is in communication with the steam            pipeline.
AU SHI L
   ZHU J
   REN Z
   TAN J
AE JIANGSU KAILIMEI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201883831N
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises an ammonia removing tower                (1), and steam inlet (11) of the ammonia removing                tower is in communication with the steam pipeline                (2). The air outlet is located between the sewage                inlet of the ammonia removing tower and the liquid                outlet of the ammonia removing tower. The outlet of                the ammonia removing tower is located below the                sewage inlet of the ammonia removing tower. The                outlet of the ammonia removing tower is located at                the top of the ammonia removing tower. The inside                of the ammonia removing tower is provided with a                W-shaped tower plate and a spray head.
   USE - System for processing garbage percolation                liquid to recover ammonium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively improves processing                efficiency of garbage percolation liquid, reduces                processing cost, and directly recycles ammonia into                ammonium bicarbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for processing garbage percolation liquid to                recover ammonium bicarbonate, which involves:
   (A) adding garbage percolation liquid into                ammonia removing tower, heating it through heat                exchanger, feeding it into the ammonia removing                tower from sewage inlet of tower, heating inside of                tower through steam in steam pipe at a temperature                of 70-80 degrees C, flowing percolation liquid from                left end of W-shaped tower plate to right end under                diversion of the W-shaped tower plate, flowing it                from right end of the W-type plate to left end,                reciprocating it from top to bottom, decomposing                ammonium bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate,                magnesium bicarbonate and other substances in the                percolation liquid, exchanging calcium carbonate,                magnesium carbonate, water and organic matter from                outlet of tower, sending heater to discharge tank,                and sending ammonia gas, carbon dioxide and a small                amount of water vapor to condensate separation tank                through gas outlet of tower;
   (B) cooling ammonia gas and carbon dioxide                entering the condensate separation tank by                condenser, sending it to the separation tank,                refluxing small amount of ammonium carbonate                mixture to tower for deamination, and entering                ammonia gas and carbon dioxide into ammonia                recovery unit to form ammonium hydrogencarbonate                solution; and
   (C) entering ammonia gas and carbon dioxide                continuously into ammonia recovery unit, where                ammonia recovery unit is cooled by the cooler, and                concentration of ammonium hydrogencarbonate                solution is higher, exceeding saturated                concentration, precipitating ammonium carbonate                crystal salt, pumping it into pump, stirring and                cooling crystal slurry tank, separating ammonium                hydrogencarbonate solid by a centrifugal separation                device, sending remaining solution to mother liquid                pool buffer and then sending it to an ammonia                recovery device for recycling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system                for processing garbage percolation liquid to                recover ammonium bicarbonate.
   Ammonia removing tower (1)
   Steam pipeline (2)
   Cleaning pipe (3)
   Cleaning liquid tank (4)
   Steam inlet (11)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The ammonia removing                  tower is used for 2-3 months, and solution in                  cleaning tank is sprayed through spray head to                  clean the scales such as calcium carbonate and                  magnesium carbonate on the W-shaped tower                  plate.
