PT P
PN CN108160737-A
TI Aluminum profile extrusion die caustic washing waste liquid recycling process by adjusting concentration of waste liquid, separating solid and liquid, and separating sodium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide from mother liquor.
AU YU P
   LI J
AE YU P (YUPP-Individual)
GA 201849289S
AB    NOVELTY - An aluminum profile extrusion die caustic washing waste liquid recycling process includes adding waste liquid to mold alkali washing tank, pumping to mold waste storage tank, pumping to mold waste liquid adjustment tank, adjusting concentration while stirring to promote separation of alloy components from aluminum ions and sodium ions, separating solid and liquid to obtain alloy oxide mixture and sodium aluminate mother liquor, and directly selling alloy oxide mixture; dividing sodium aluminate mother liquor into two portions, separating one portion to sodium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide by using temperature control condition, and separating other portion to aluminum hydroxide and sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate by using carbon dioxide; and directly selling separated aluminum hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide, and recycling sodium hydroxide solution and /)6% sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate into mold alkali washing tank.
   USE - Method for aluminum profile extrusion die caustic washing waste liquid recycling process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large-scale industrial production, does not need to add new substances waste solution, generate waste residue and increase generated solid matter and types of harmful substances in original waste liquid, and has greatly reduced cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An aluminum profile extrusion die caustic washing waste liquid recycling process comprises:
   (A) adding waste liquid to mold alkali washing tank, pumping to mold waste storage tank until processable amount is reached, and pumping to mold waste liquid adjustment tank, adjusting concentration to 30 plus minus 5% at 50 plus minus 5 degrees C while stirring at speed of 5 meters/minute to promote separation of alloy components from aluminum ions and sodium ions, separating solid and liquid traditional solid-liquid separation technology to obtain alloy oxide mixture and sodium aluminate mother liquor, and directly selling alloy oxide mixture;
   (B) dividing sodium aluminate mother liquor into two portions, separating one portion to sodium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide by using temperature control condition, and separating other portion to aluminum hydroxide and sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate by using carbon dioxide; and
   (C) directly selling separated aluminum hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide, and recycling sodium hydroxide solution and /)6% sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate into mold alkali washing tank.
   The separation method using temperature control condition is done by heating sodium aluminate mother liquor to 70 plus minus 5 degrees C while stirring at speed of 3 meters/minute, converting aluminum ions into aluminum hydroxide and precipitating, and converting sodium ion to sodium hydroxide retention solution. The separation method using carbon dioxide is done by adding sodium aluminate mother liquor with carbon dioxide gas, and separating aluminum hydroxide and sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate.

PN CN113912843-A
TI Porous metalloporphyrin polymer containing benzimidazole ionic liquid used as catalyst for reaction of cycloaddition of epoxide and carbon dioxide to synthesize cyclic carbonate, has benzimidazole ionic liquid compound.
AU JI H
   LIN D
   DENG C
   FENG Z
   QIU Y
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEM TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 202221975F
AB    NOVELTY - Porous metalloporphyrin polymer containing benzimidazole ionic liquid has benzimidazole ionic liquid unit compound (I) used as bridge unit.
   USE - Porous metalloporphyrin polymer containing benzimidazole ionic liquid used as catalyst for reaction of cycloaddition of epoxide and carbon dioxide to synthesize cyclic carbonate under condition of no solvent and no auxiliary agent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous metalloporphyrin polymer containing benzimidazole ionic liquid has high porosity and high stability and is easy to separate and reuse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Porous metalloporphyrin polymer containing benzimidazole ionic liquid has benzimidazole ionic liquid unit compound of formula (I) used as bridge unit.
   M=aluminum chloride, zinc, cobalt, cobalt chloride, copper, iron chloride, magnesium, manganese chloride, nickel, chromium and chromium chloride. X=halogen;
   m=1-10;
   R1 and R2=methyl, ethyl, butyl and benzyl; and
   R3=H, (CH2)0-13CH3, CH2Cl, CH2Br, CH2OCH2CH=CH2, OH, CH2OPh, Ph, PhpCH3, PhpOCH3, PhpC, and PhpBr.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1)a method for preparing the porous metalloporphyrin polymer containing the benzimidazole ionic liquid, which involves: (a)adding 0.1-10 mmol dialdehyde compound containing benzimidazole ionic liquid and 0.2-20 mmol freshly distilled pyrrole into 10-1000 mL organic acid to obtai first mixed solution, reacting the obtained first mixed solution at 100-140degrees Celsius for 1-96 hours to obtain first precipitate;(b)collecting, washing the obtained first precipitate with N,N-dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, methanol and dichloromethane, performing vacuum-dried to obtain the ionic liquid-based porous porphyrin polymer (Por-PiP);(c)sending the obtained Por-PiP and metal salt in a ratio of 1:10 and 10-100 mL anhydrous acetonitrile with specific molar ratio;(d) reacting at 60-100degrees Celsius for 1-24 hours to obtain a second precipitate;(e) collecting, washing the obtained second precipitate with anhydrous tetrahydrofuran and anhydrous dichloromethane;(f) drying in vacuum to obtain the porous metalloporphyrin polymer (MPor-PiP) of the benzimidazole ionic liquid; and
   (2)an application method for porous metalloporphyrin polymer catalyzing epoxide and carbon dioxide cycloaddition to synthesize cyclic carbonate, which involves: (a)adding epoxide compound of formula (II) and porous metalloporphyrin polymer catalyst at first time in a 10 mL stainless steel reactor without adding any solvent and additive;(b)discharging the air in the still;(c)filling with carbon dioxide;(d)reacting at 5-140degrees Celsius for 0.1-96 hours;(e) filtering, extracting, drying reaction mixture;(f)purifying by micro-column chromatography to obtain the product of cyclic carbonate compound of formula (III), and recovering the catalyst by filtration after each reaction is washed and dried for reuse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the prepared porous metalloporphyrin polymer catalyst (MPor-PiP) containing benzimidazole ionic liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt is selected from zinc acetate, manganese acetate, manganese chloride, cobalt acetate, cobalt chloride, copper acetate, ferrous chloride, diethylaluminum chloride, magnesium bromide, chromium chloride and nickel acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid is selected from acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid and lactic acid. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of metal to epoxide compound (II) in the catalyst MPor-PiP is 0.05-10%. The initial pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-15 megapascal, and the initial concentration of carbon dioxide is 1-100%.

AB    NOVELTY - Processing high-efficiency pyrolysis of coal to produce clean energy by classification and quality involves assembling dry distillation furnace section and gasification furnace section are from top to bottom to form furnace body, operating the material dynamically from top to bottom, reforming he low-rank coal into briquette in the briquette section, drying the external water, putting the dried briquette into the dry distillation furnace section, generating indirectly exchange heat with the high-temperature water gas by the gasification furnace section, generating the the high-temperature carbon dioxide flue gas by the combustion of coal gas, performing preheating and low-temperature oxygen-free dry distillation to produce high-temperature clean carbon and raw gas after heat exchange, cooling, washing to obtain low-temperature coal tar and dry distillation gas after heat exchange, and collecting high-temperature carbon dioxide flue gas.
   USE - Method for processing high-efficiency pyrolysis of coal to produce clean energy by classification and quality.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing high-efficiency pyrolysis of coal to produce clean energy by classification and quality involves assembling dry distillation furnace section and gasification furnace section are from top to bottom to form furnace body, operating the material dynamically from top to bottom, reforming he low-rank coal into briquette in the briquette section, drying the external water, putting the dried briquette into the dry distillation furnace section, generating indirectly exchange heat with the high-temperature water gas by the gasification furnace section, generating the the high-temperature carbon dioxide flue gas by the combustion of coal gas, performing preheating and low-temperature oxygen-free dry distillation to produce high-temperature clean carbon and raw gas after heat exchange, cooling, washing to obtain low-temperature coal tar and dry distillation gas after heat exchange, collecting high-temperature carbon dioxide flue gas, sealing, putting high-temperature clean carbon into the gasification furnace section, reacting with the oxygen and steam mixture provided by the oxygen, subjecting steam mixing section to produce high temperature water gas, sending high-temperature water gas into the dry distillation furnace section, exchanging heat indirectly with the briquette after heat exchange to obtain purified water gas, cooling, washing, using purified water gas to produce hydrogen and other chemical raw material, performing in the gasification furnace section, producing gasification slag by the reaction of high-temperature clean carbon with the mixture of oxygen, discharging steam from the furnace after cooling by water, and producing oxygen and steam mixture by oxygen and steam mixing section.

PT P
PN CN112359374-A
TI Use of copper-based composite material in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, where copper-based composite material is prepared by pretreating copper-based alloy, and performing electrochemical deposition, reduction and oxidation.
AU ZHAO Y
AE ZHAO Y (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 2021182917
AB    NOVELTY - Use of copper-based composite material in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is claimed. The copper-based composite material is prepared by (i) performing pretreatment of copper-based alloy, (ii) using copper-containing solution as electrolyte and copper-based alloy as cathode to perform electrochemical deposition and reduction, (iii) performing high temperature chemical reduction of copper nanowire seed crystals, (iv) performing hydrothermal treatment, and (v) oxidizing, where pretreatment is carried out by sanding and inorganic degreasing, copper-containing electrolyte comprises copper sulfate, sulfuric acid, chloride ions and surfactant, and anode is inert electrode or pure copper, solution used for high-temperature chemical reduction in step (ii) is mixture of copper acetate and glycerol, solution used in step (iv) for hydrothermal is same as solution in step (iii), and oxidizing solution used in step (v) is mixed solution of aqueous ammonia and copper carbonate.
   USE - The copper-based composite material is used in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based composite material significantly increases the specific surface area and active sites of the electrode material, where the electrode is stable, and the thorny copper obtained is not easy to peel off. The copper-based composite material increases its lifespan while ensuring high ethylene conversion rate. The copper-based alloy has Faraday efficiency of greater than 60%, and ethylene selectivity of greater than or equal to 40% under the electrolysis voltage of -1.8V vs silver/silver chloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of copper-based composite material in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is claimed. The copper-based composite material is prepared by (i) performing pretreatment of copper-based alloy, (ii) using copper-containing solution as electrolyte and the copper-based alloy as cathode to perform electrochemical deposition and reduction at a very high negative bias, (iii) performing high temperature chemical reduction of copper nanowire seed crystals, (iv) performing hydrothermal treatment to obtain thorn-like copper-based composite material, and (v) oxidizing, where the pretreatment in step (i) is carried out by sanding and inorganic degreasing, the copper-containing electrolyte used in step (ii) comprises copper sulfate, sulfuric acid, chloride ions and surfactant, and the anode is an inert electrode or pure copper, the solution used for the high-temperature chemical reduction in step (ii) is a mixture of copper acetate and glycerol, the solution used in step (iv) for hydrothermal is the same as the solution in step (iii) in a closed state, the oxidizing solution used in step (v) is a mixed solution of aqueous ammonia and copper carbonate, the copper-based alloy is at -1.8 V vs silver/silver chloride electrolytic voltage, the Faraday efficiency of the electrochemical conversion method is more than 60%, and the ethylene selectivity is more than 40%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sanding is carried out by sequentially sanding with No. 200, No. 600 and No. 1200 sandpaper. The degreasing is carried out by using 15 g/l sodium carbonate, 10 g/l sodium phosphate dodecahydrate and 10 g/l sodium silicate at 50 degrees C for 2 minutes. The method involves before step (iv), using nitrogen to evacuate the air in the reaction kettle in step (iii). Preferred Conditions: The copper-containing electrolyte is maintained at a temperature of 25-30 degrees C, direct current (DC) constant voltage of -13 V for 5-10 seconds. The solution used for the high-temperature chemical reduction in step (iii) is maintained at 300-350 degrees C in a non-sealed state for 15-20 minutes. In step (iv), the hydrothermal treatment is carried out at 110-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours. In step (v), the reaction is carried out at 15-20 degrees C for 3-5 minutes. Preferred Components: The copper-based alloy is a copper-nickel cupronickel alloy, and the shape is a rod shape or a plate shape. The reaction kettle of step (iii) and step (iv) is a stainless steel non-lined hydrothermal reaction kettle. Preferred Composition: The copper-containing electrolyte uses 140-150 g/l copper sulfate pentahydrate, 30-35 g/l sulfuric acid, 40-45 g/l chloride ion and 0.5-1 g/l alkylphenol ethoxylates (APEO). The solution used for the high-temperature chemical reduction in step (iii) is 3-5 M anhydrous copper acetate glycerol solution. The step (v) uses 100-120 ml/l 25 wt.% aqueous ammonia and 40 g/l copper carbonate.

PT P
PN CN111494469-A; WO2021218945-A1; CN111494469-B
TI Medicinal composition used for providing            antibacterial and antiviral effect, clearing away heat            and treating fever and respiratory diseases, comprises            patchouli, rhizoma atractylodis, Chinese mosla herb,            Artemisia leaves, clove and mint.
AU LIN R
   DONG X
   CUI T
   WANG J
   SUN M
AE YUNNAN BAIYAO GROUP CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
   YUNNAN BAIYAO GROUP CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202077332S
AB    NOVELTY - Medicinal composition comprises 4-14 pts. wt.                patchouli, 9-28 pts. wt. rhizoma atractylodis, 5-18                pts. wt. Chinese mosla herb, 11-33 pts. wt.                Artemisia leaves, 0.1-3 pts. wt. clove and 2-10                pts. wt. mint.
   USE - The medicinal composition, medicinal volatile                oil or medicinal aromatic water is used in                preparation of medicine for providing antibacterial                and antiviral effect, clearing away heat and                treating fever and respiratory diseases. The                respiratory diseases include nose, pharynx, larynx,                trachea, bronchus or lung diseases. The medicine                for treating respiratory diseases include                anti-inflammatory medicine, cough suppressant,                anti-asthmatic medicine, phlegm reducing medicine                or allergic rhinitis treating medicine (all                claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (1)medicinal volatile oil is prepared by                (a) (i) distilling and extracting the patchouli,                rhizoma atractylodis, Chinese mosla herb, Artemisia                leaves, clove and mint respectively with water to                obtain the volatile oil of each raw material, and                mixing the volatile oil of each raw material to                obtain a mixed volatile oil, (ii) adding the                patchouli, rhizoma atractylodis, Chinese mosla                herb, Artemisia leaves, clove and mint into a                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device,                passing liquid carbon dioxide for supercritical                extraction to obtain the volatile oil of each raw                material and mixing the volatile oil of each raw                material to obtain mixed volatile oil, (iii)                uniformly mixing the patchouli, rhizoma                atractylodis, Chinese mosla herb, Artemisia leaves,                clove and mint and distilling the mixture with                water to obtain the total volatile oil or (iv)                uniformly mixing the patchouli, rhizoma                atractylodis, Chinese mosla herb, Artemisia leaves,                clove and mint, adding the mixture into a                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and                introducing liquid carbon dioxide to perform                supercritical extraction to obtain total volatile                oil and (b) adding natural borneol to the mixed                volatile oil or total volatile oil to dissolve the                natural borneol to obtain medicinal volatile oil or                (ii) adding natural borneol to a solvent at 50-60                degrees C, evenly stirring the mixture until                complete dissolution, cooling to room temperature,                and then adding the mixed volatile oil or total                volatile oil to obtain medicinal volatile oil; (2)                medicinal aromatic water prepared by uniformly                mixing the patchouli, rhizoma atractylodis, Chinese                mosla herb, Artemisia leaves, clove and mint,                adding 5-12 times water, soaking overnight and                distilling to obtain medicinal aromatic water; and                (3) use of the medicinal composition, medicinal                volatile oil or medicinal aromatic water is used in                preparation of medicine for providing antibacterial                and antiviral effect, clearing away heat and                treating fever and respiratory diseases. The                respiratory diseases include nose, pharynx, larynx,                trachea, bronchus or lung diseases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises 0.01-0.2 pt. wt. natural                  borneol. The composition further comprises                  auxiliary material. Preferred Method: The step                  (a) (i) involves (A) adding 5-8 times water to                  patchouli, soaking overnight and extracting for                  4-8 hours by steam distillation, (B) adding 5-8                  times water to rhizoma atractylodis, soaking for                  20-60 minutes and extracting by steam                  distillation for 4-6 hours, (C) adding 5-8 times                  water to Chinese mosla herb, soaking for 1-3                  hours and then extracting by steam distillation                  for 2-4 hours, (D) adding 6-10 times water to                  Artemisia leaves, soaking for 4-8 hours and                  extracting for 4-6 hours by steam distillation,                  (E) adding 5-8 times water to cloves, soaking                  overnight and extracting for 4-8 hours by steam                  distillation and (F) adding 5-8 times water to                  mint, soaking for 1-3 hours and extracting it by                  steam distillation for 4-6 hours. The step (ii)                  involves performing supercritical extraction at                  extraction pressure of 20-40 MPa, extraction                  temperature of 40-70 degrees C and separation                  temperature of 30-60 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours.                  The step (iii) involves adding 5-10 times water                  to the mixture, soaking for 1-8 hours and                  extracting by steam distillation for 2-8 hours.                  The step (iv) involves performing supercritical                  extraction at extraction pressure of 20-40 MPa,                  extraction temperature of 40-70 degrees C and                  separation temperature of 30-60 degrees C for                  0.5-10 hours. The spray is prepared by (a)                  diluting the medicinal volatile oil with a                  diluent, evenly stirring the mixture and                  filtering to obtain a spray or (b) adding natural                  borneol and auxiliary material into a solvent at                  50-60 degrees C, evenly stirring the mixture to                  dissolve, cooling to room temperature, then                  adding mixed volatile oil or total volatile oil,                  diluting with diluent, evenly stirring the                  mixture and filtering to obtain a spray. The                  lotion is prepared by adding auxiliary material                  to medicinal aromatic water and evenly stirring                  the mixture to obtain a lotion. The aerosol is                  prepared by (a) diluting the medicinal volatile                  oil with a diluent, evenly stirring the mixture,                  filtering, filling the filtrate into a                  pressure-resistant container, pressing the cap,                  and filling with a propellant to obtain an                  aerosol or (b) adding natural borneol and                  auxiliary material into a solvent at 50-60                  degrees C, evenly stirring the mixture to                  dissolve, cooling to room temperature, then                  adding mixed volatile oil or total volatile oil,                  diluting with diluent, evenly stirring the                  mixture, filtering, filling the filtrate into a                  pressure-resistant container, pressing the cap,                  and filling with a propellant to obtain an                  aerosol. The dripping pill is prepared by                  subjecting the medicinal volatile oil to                  microencapsulation to obtain volatile oil                  microcapsule powder, and mixing with the dripping                  pill matrix to make the dripping pill. The soft                  capsule is prepared by using soft capsule                  materials to wrap medicinal volatile oil to make                  soft capsules. The nose drops are prepared by                  diluting the medicinal volatile oil with a                  diluent, evenly stirring the mixture and                  filtering. The atomizer liquid is prepared by                  diluting the medicinal volatile oil with a                  diluent, evenly stirring the mixture, filtering                  and filling the filtrate. Preferred Components:                  The solvent is chosen from one or more                  combinations of medium chain triglycerides, 1-98%                  alcohol, absolute ethanol, propylene glycol,                  glycerin and edible oil. The diluent is chosen                  from one or more combinations of medium-chain                  triglycerides, 1-98% alcohol, absolute ethanol,                  propylene glycol, glycerin and edible oil. The                  propellant is chosen from one or more                  combinations of nitrogen, carbon dioxide,                  heptafluoropropane, tetrafluoroethane,                  1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene,                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene and compressed air.                  The dropping pill matrix is chosen from one or                  more combinations of glycerin-gelatin mixture,                  polyethylene glycol 4000, polyethylene glycol                  6000 or sodium stearate. The soft capsule                  material comprises mixture of gelatin, glycerin                  and purified water.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Virucide; Antipyretic;                Respiratory-Gen.; Antiinflammatory; Antitussive;                Antiasthmatic; Antiallergic.
   ADMINISTRATION - The medicine is in the form of spray, lotion,                aerosol, drop pill, soft capsule, nose drop or                atomizer liquid (claimed).
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN EP3666369-A1; FR3089821-A1; US2020188843-A1; CN111321020-A; FR3089821-B1
TI Installation used to treat by membrane permeation of feed gas stream having methane and carbon dioxide comprises compressor A, first separation unit, second membrane separation unit, third membrane separation unit, means and compressor B.
AU BARRAUD F
   CHAREYRE J
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2020534214
AB    NOVELTY - Installation comprises: a compressor A making it possible to compress the feed gas stream; a first separation unit by membrane able to receive the gas flow coming from the compressor and to provide first permeate and first retentate; second membrane separation unit able to receive the first retentate and to provide a second permeate and second retentate; third membrane separation unit capable of receiving the first permeate and providing third permeate and a third retentate; means for measuring the pressure of the feed gas flow at the inlet of the first membrane separation unit; at least one means of measuring the methane concentration in the second retentate; and compressor B making it possible to adjust the suction pressure of the third permeate as function of the pressure measured in the feed gas flow and the methane concentration measured in the second retentate, with each membrane separation unit comprising membrane more permeable to carbon dioxide than to methane.
   USE - The installation is useful for treating by membrane permeation of feed gas stream having methane and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Installation comprises: a compressor A making it possible to compress the feed gas stream; a first separation unit by membrane able to receive the gas flow coming from the compressor and to provide a first permeate and a first retentate; a second membrane separation unit able to receive the first retentate and to provide a second permeate and a second retentate; a third membrane separation unit capable of receiving the first permeate and providing a third permeate and a third retentate; at least one means for measuring the pressure of the feed gas flow at the inlet of the first membrane separation unit; at least one means of measuring the methane concentration in the second retentate; and at least one compressor B making it possible to adjust the suction pressure of the third permeate as a function of the pressure measured in the feed gas flow and the methane concentration measured in the second retentate, with each membrane separation unit comprising at least one membrane more permeable to carbon dioxide than to methane. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for controlling the installation comprising (a) measuring the pressure of the feed gas flow at the inlet of the first separation unit by membrane, (b) measurement of the methane concentration in the second retentate, (c) comparison of the pressure measured in step (a) and of the concentration measured in step (b) with reference values, and of determination of the deviation from these set values and (d) a step of adjusting the suction pressure of the third permeate using the compressor B.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The third retentate is recycled into the supply gas flow upstream of the compressor A. The second permeate is recycled to the feed gas stream upstream of the compressor A. The membranes used in the membrane separation units have the same selectivity, where at least one membrane separation unit comprises at least two membranes. The feed gas flow is biogas. Preferred Method: In step (d) the suction pressure of the third permeate is adjusted both using compressor B and the pressure of the feed gas flow using compressor A or using a cut-off and progressive pressurization valve. The adjustment of the pressure of the supply gas flow comprises an increase or a decrease in the pressure. In the adjustment step the compressor B undergoes acceleration or a deceleration. The comparison step and the adjustment step are performed automatically by means of data transmission and data processing.

PT P
PN CN111118639-A; CN111118639-B
TI Titanium dioxide matting agent preparation involves dissolving phosphoric acid in ethanol, adding hydrochloric acid dropwise under heating, and then pendant aminopropyl polysiloxane.
AU GE C
   BAI Y
   QIU Y
AE SHANGHAI HUALY SUPERFINES SCI & TECHN CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020413488
AB    NOVELTY - Titanium dioxide matting agent preparation involves dissolving phosphoric acid in ethanol, adding hydrochloric acid dropwise under heating at 45-45 degrees C for 30 minutes, and then pendant aminopropyl polysiloxane in 3-5 minutes. The side aminopropyl polysiloxane is completed at 60-65 degrees C for 3 hours. Formaldehyde is added to the system with stirring for 50-60 minutes. The hydrochloric acid, pendant aminopropyl polysiloxane, phosphoric acid, formaldehyde and ethanol are provided in mol ratio of 1:10:5.2:9.8:20. The titanium dioxide is added in a mixed solution of water and hydrochloric acid with a mass ratio of 10:1 for 1 hour at 50 degrees C, and then placed at 200 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain a treated product. The treated product is pulverized to obtained powder with a particle size of 400-600 mesh, and then put the processed product in a carbon dioxide pressure vessel under carbon dioxide atmosphere at 80-100 degrees C for 3 hours to get pretreated titanium dioxide.
   USE - Method for preparing titanium dioxide matting agent for highly dispersible textile fabrics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the titanium dioxide matting agent in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with effectively improved dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Titanium dioxide matting agent preparation involves dissolving phosphoric acid in ethanol, adding hydrochloric acid dropwise under heating at 45-45 degrees C for 30 minutes, and then pendant aminopropyl polysiloxane in 3-5 minutes. The side aminopropyl polysiloxane is completed at 60-65 degrees C for 3 hours. Formaldehyde is added to the system with stirring for 50-60 minutes. The hydrochloric acid, pendant aminopropyl polysiloxane, phosphoric acid, formaldehyde and ethanol are provided in mol ratio of 1:10:5.2:9.8:20. The titanium dioxide is added in a mixed solution of water and hydrochloric acid with a mass ratio of 10:1 for 1 hour at 50 degrees C, and then placed at 200 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain a treated product. The treated product is pulverized to obtained powder with a particle size of 400-600 mesh, and then put the processed product in a carbon dioxide pressure vessel under carbon dioxide atmosphere at 80-100 degrees C for 3 hours to get pretreated titanium dioxide. The kaolin, white solid and molybdenum disulfide are added to the pre-treated titanium dioxide with stirring for 30 minutes at 150 degrees C, and then mixed with phenolic resin in compression molding device for compression to obtain pre-treated tablet. The obtained pre-treated tablets are put in a muffle furnace at 400 degrees C for 90 minutes to obtain reprocessed titanium dioxide. The surface of the retreated silica is coated with alumina to obtain the matting agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The kaolin is subjected to modification treatment before adding the retreated titanium dioxide, where the modification treatment is performed by preparing hydrochloric acid solution, adding kaolin at 50-55 degrees C to obtain pretreated kaolin. The pretreated kaolin is mixed with water and silane coupling agent at 50-55 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain modified kaolin. The silane coupling agent is a vinyl trialkoxysilane coupling agent.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.)

PN CN110172479-A; CN110172479-B
TI Constructing plasmid capable of simultaneously            expressing latent membrane protein 1 and CD30            double-target CARs comprises e.g. ligating light chain            of anti-CD30 and synthesizing gene sequence            fragment.
AU LIAO X
   WANG J
   XIANG Y
   LI J
   ZHANG H
   HUANG F
   DAI Z
   GU C
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 201975555P
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing plasmid capable of simultaneously                expressing latent membrane protein 1 (LMP1) and                CD30 double-target CARs comprises (i) sequentially                ligating light chain of anti-CD30, light chain of                anti-LMP1 single-chain antibody, heavy chain of                anti-LMP1 single-chain antibody, heavy chain of                anti-CD30, CD8 alpha hinge region, CD28                transmembrane and intracellular regions, 41BB                intracellular region and CD3 intracellular region,                and synthesizing gene sequence fragment, (ii)                ligating gene sequence fragment obtained in the                step (i) to the lentiviral expression vector PTK881                to obtain plasmid PTK-hCD30-hLMP1 capable of                simultaneously expressing LMP1 and CD30                double-target CAR.
   USE - The plasmid is useful for simultaneously                expressing LMP1 and CD30 double-target CARs, and                preparing kit or reagent for treating EB virus                infection (claimed), killing Hodgkin lymphoma cells                infected with EB virus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) constructing CAR-T cell, comprising (a)                constructing plasmid capable of simultaneously                expressing LMP1 and CD30 dual-target CARs using the                construction method, (b) lentiviral packaging,                culturing 293T cells in DEME complete medium at 37                degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, mixing                DEME low sugar medium with the plasmid obtained in                the step (a), then mixing with polyethyleneimine                and shaking, and then incubating at 37 degrees C,                culturing the incubated mixture in 5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, collecting the                cell culture supernatants, and filtering through                filter to obtain virus initial solution, (c)                concentrating virus solution, mixing the virus                initial solution with 5x PEG 8000 sodium chloride,                and allowing to stand for into four                layers from bottom to top, i.e. red blood cell,                granulocyte layer, layered liquid layer, single                nuclear cell layer and plasma layer, removing the incubator containing carbon dioxide, and                transducing to obtain CAR-T cells; and

GA 2019288086

   USE - The systematic method is useful for improving the quality of fresh frozen pork.

PN ES2909954-A1
TI Producing at least one organic carbonate comprises reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide, catalyzing by hydrophobic ionic liquid, and recovering organic carbonate obtained after the reaction in an aqueous phase.
AU NAVARRO TEJEDOR P
   HERNANDEZ MUNOZ E
   VELA GALLEGO S
   MOYA ALAMO C
   SANTIAGO LORENZO R
   HOSPITAL BENITO D
   LEMUS TORRES J
   PALOMAR HERRERO J
AE UNIV AUTONOMA MADRID (UMAD-C)
GA 2022809602
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one organic carbonate comprises reacting carbon dioxide and at least one epoxide, catalyzing by at least one hydrophobic ionic liquid, where the reaction is carried out in the presence of 6-34C alkyl comprising at least one functional group including hydroxy and/or acid, recovering organic carbonate obtained after the reaction in an aqueous phase by means of a process comprising a separation step between an organic phase formed by alkyl, and aqueous phase.
   USE - The method is useful for producing organic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows both the separation of the product with high purity and the reuse of the catalyst, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of 6-34C alkyl comprising at least one functional group including hydroxy and/or acid in a liquid catalyzed synthesis of organic carbonates hydrophobic ionic liquids, where the synthesis comprises a separation step between an aqueous phase and an organic phase for the recovery of carbonates and ionic liquids, in phases, respectively.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process comprises adding water after obtaining the at least one carbonate and prior to the separation step. The method comprises: (b1) adding water to the reaction medium so as to form an organic phase comprising the hydrophobic ionic liquid and an aqueous phase comprising the at least one organic carbonate, and (b2) separating the organic phase comprising the hydrophobic ionic liquid from the aqueous phase comprising the at least one organic carbonate. In the method, the steps (b1) and (b2) are carried out simultaneously. In the method, water added is in a water:reaction medium volume ratio of 5:1-0:1, preferably 3:1-1:3. The hydrophobic ionic liquid is recovered with alkyl after the separation step between the organic phase and the aqueous phase and is fed into the reaction. The carbonate is obtained by (a1) mixing carbon dioxide, at least one epoxide and at least one hydrophobic ionic liquid pair-6-34C alkyl comprising at least one functional group including hydroxy and/or acid, and (a2) providing thermal energy for the insertion reaction of carbon dioxide in the at least one epoxide, where the molar ratio between carbon dioxide and the at least one epoxide is 10:1-1:10, preferably 2:1-1:1, and the molar ratio of epoxide and hydrophobic ionic liquid is 1000:1-2:1. The reaction is carried out in the absence of water. The method is carried out in a closed cycle. The synthesis of carbonates is a continuous process. Preferred Components: The 6-34C alkyl comprising at least one functional group including hydroxy and/or acid is from an acid or an alcohol, and the 6-34C alkyl comprising at least one functional group including hydroxy and/or acid includes 6-18C alcohol. The molar ratio between alkyl and the at least one hydrophobic ionic liquid is 1000:1-1:10, in the reaction to obtain the at least one carbonate. The molar ratio of alkyl and the at least one hydrophobic ionic liquid is 100:1-5:1. The molar ratio of alkyl and the at least one hydrophobic ionic liquid is 50:1-10:1. The water added is an aqueous stream. The molar ratio of epoxide and hydrophobic ionic liquid is 100:1-20:1, preferably 100:1-50:1. The water of the aqueous phase is an aqueous stream.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of biological methods that do not support the claims.

PN EP3988600-A1; WO2022084115-A1
TI Preparing polyether carbonate alcohols useful e.g.            in detergent and drilling fluid, comprises e.g. adding            cyclic carbonate into H-functional starter substance in            presence of catalyst A to form mixture containing            polyether carbonate alcohol.
AU WOLF A
   LAEMMERHOLD K
   HOFMANN J
   WALKER K
   GUERTLER C
   SCHUETZE M
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 202255622A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyether carbonate alcohols,                comprises (a) adding cyclic carbonate into an                H-functional starter substance in presence of a                catalyst A to form a mixture containing polyether                carbonate alcohol, (b) optionally separating off                the catalyst A from the mixture, and (c) reacting                alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide with the obtained                polyether carbonate alcohol in the presence of a                catalyst B.
   USE - The polyether carbonate alcohol is useful in                detergents, drilling fluids, fuel additives, ionic                and non-ionic surfactants, dispersants, lubricants,                process chemicals for paper or textile manufacture,                cosmetics or in production of polyurethanes (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is carried out in an efficient and                eco-friendly manner as it enables conversion of                greenhouse gas to polymer; is a continuous process                that enables both continuous copolymerization and                continuous addition of raw materials                simultaneously; achieves better mixing of reactants                in reactor, which improves mixing and heat                dissipation simultaneously; and attains optimum                miscibility of components, preferably carbon                dioxide in its liquid or supercritical form.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method includes at                  least one compound (i) of titanium compounds, tin                  compounds, alkali metal and alkaline earth metal                  salts, molybdate, tungstate, vanadate and at                  least one compound of tribasic alkali metal                  phosphates, alkali metal carboxylate, alkali                  metal acetates, where phosphate is used as the                  catalyst A, preferably compounds of formula (MnX)                  (II). The addition of the cyclic carbonate onto                  the H-functional starter substance is carried out                  at 130-200 degrees C, preferably 140-190 degrees                  C. The method includes adding 15-40 wt.%,                  preferably 15-30 wt.% polyether carbonate alcohol                  in step (a) or (b). At least one double metal                  cyanide catalyst or a metal complex catalyst                  based on zinc and/or cobalt is used as the                  catalyst B. Preferred Components: The catalyst A                  is at least one compound including trisodium                  phosphate, tri-potassium phosphate, sodium                  vanadium oxide, potassium vanadium oxide, caesium                  orthovanadate, sodium metavanadate, potassium                  metavanadate and caesium vanadium oxide. The                  cyclic carbonate includes propylene carbonate                  and/or ethylene carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The H-functional                  starter substance includes at least one compound                  including water, ethylene glycol, propylene                  glycol, 1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol,                  1,5-pentanediol, 2-methylpropane-1,3-diol,                  neopentyl glycol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol,                  diethylene glycol, di-propylene glycol, glycerol,                  trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol or sorbitol.                  Preferred Method: The method in step (c)                  includes: (i) adding H-functional starter                  substance or a mixture of at least two                  H-functional starter substances or a suspending                  agent and optionally removing water and/or other                  readily volatile compounds by drying at elevated                  temperature and/or reduced pressure, where the                  catalyst B is added to the H-functional starter                  substance or to the mixture of at least two                  H-functional starter substances or the suspending                  agent before or after drying, (ii) optionally                  adding a subset of alkylene oxide (based on the                  total amount of alkylene oxides used in the                  activation and copolymerization) to the mixture                  obtained in step (i) for activation of a double                  metal cyanide catalyst, where step (ii) can                  optionally be carried out in the presence of                  carbon dioxide, in such a manner that the                  temperature peak (hotspot) is achieved as a                  result of subsequent exothermic chemical reaction                  and/or a pressure drop is achieved in a reactor,                  and the step (ii) is carried out multiple times                  for activation, and (iii) adding alkylene oxide,                  carbon dioxide and optionally an H-functional                  starter substance to the mixture obtained from                  step (i) or optionally (ii), where the polyether                  carbonate alcohol obtained after step (a) or (b)                  is added as an H-functional starter substance in                  at least one of the steps (i) or (iii). The                  polyether carbonate alcohol obtained after step                  (a) or (b) is used at least in (a). The cyclic                  carbonate formed in step (c) is reused in step                  (a). The propylene oxide and/or ethylene oxide is                  used as the alkylene oxide.M = alkali metal cations including lithium                  ion, sodium ion, potassium ion and cesium                  ion;X = anions e.g. VO3-, WO42-, MoO42- and                  VO43-; andn = 1 when X = VO3-, n = 2 when X = WO42- or                  MoO42-, n = 3 when X = VO43-.
   EXAMPLE - 500.5 g Cyclic propylene carbonate, 59.6 g                diethylene glycol and 5.68 g potassium vanadium                oxide were added into a 500 ml four-necked glass                flask. Then, 10 litre/hour nitrogen was introduced                for 30 minutes to obtain a suspension and stirred                at 300 rpm. Later, the suspension was heated to 180                degrees C and reacted with the formation of gaseous                carbon dioxide. The reaction mixture was kept at                the set temperature until the evolution of gas came                to a standstill to obtain polyether carbonate                alcohol, which was then dissolved in 150 ml toluene                and extracted using water. The combined organic                phase was washed with magnesium sulfate, dried and                the solvent was removed under vacuum at 60 degrees                C. The step (c) was carried out, which included (i)                81 mg double metal cyanide catalyst was suspended                in 120.1 g of polyether carbonate alcohol obtained                from step (b) and the suspension was then                introduced into a 2 l pressure reactor with a gas                metering device, then suspension was heated at 130                degrees C and 30 litre/hour of nitrogen was                introduced for 30 minutes, (ii) nitrogen was added                at 130 degrees C, 1000 rpm and at a pre-pressure of                2 bar, then 8 g propylene oxide was added and was                allowed to react, then again 15 g propylene oxide                was added, and 214.9 g propylene oxide was                continuously added for the third time into the                reactor at 1.23 g/min at 108 degrees C to obtain                the mixture, (iii) stirred and was freed from                traces of monomeric propylene oxide to obtain                polyether carbonate alcohol.

TI Preparation method of polymer supported            synergistic catalyst for carbon dioxide photo reduction            catalysis, involves preparing dinitrogen coordination            compound of ruthenium into a first monomer containing a            terminal alkenyl group, carrying out acylation reaction            on a pyridine compound.

   ZHANG S
   ZHU Y
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 202263231C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of a polymer supported                synergistic catalyst, involves(1)preparing                dinitrogen coordination compound of ruthenium into                a first monomer containing terminal alkenyl group,                (2) carrying out acylation reaction on a pyridine                compound containing terminal amino or a bipyridine                compound containing terminal amino and an acyl                chloride compound containing terminal alkenyl to                prepare a second monomer containing terminal                alkenyl, (3) carrying out free radical                polymerization on first monomer and second monomer                (4) and (4) coordinating the polymer obtained in                step (3) with transition metal salt, and then                carrying out anion exchange on the reaction product                and hexafluorophosphate to obtain the polymer                supported synergistic catalyst.
   USE - Preparation method of polymer supported                synergistic catalyst for carbon dioxide photo                reduction catalysis(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The distance between photosensitizer and                transition metal catalyst in polymer is pulled in,                so that transfer of energy and electrons between                photosensitizer and transition metal catalyst is                promoted, and reaction efficiency of photocatalysis                is greatly improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: Preparation method of a                  polymer supported synergistic catalyst,involves                  (1) dissolving a bipyridine compound containing                  terminal alkenyl and a dinitrogen coordination                  compound of ruthenium in a solvent, performing                  tube sealing reaction, purifying an obtained                  crude product, dissolving the purified crude                  product in methanol, adding a saturated ammonium                  hexafluorophosphate aqueous solution until no                  solid is precipitated, and washing and drying the                  obtained solid to obtain a first monomer (2)                  dissolving a pyridine compound containing                  terminal amino groups or a bipyridine compound                  containing terminal amino groups and                  triethylamine in a solvent, adding an acyl                  chloride compound containing terminal alkenyl                  groups at low temperature, stirring for reaction                  at room temperature, then stirring for reaction                  at 40-50 &#8451;, collecting a product after                  the reaction is finished, and drying to obtain a                  second monomer,(3) under the protection of inert                  gas, adding a first monomer, a second monomer, an                  initiator and a solvent into a Schlenk tube,                  reacting at 75-85 &#8451;, adding ether into                  the Schlenk tube after the reaction is finished                  and cooled to separate out a product, and                  centrifuging and drying to obtain a                  polymerization product, (4) dissolving the                  polymerization product in a solvent, adding a                  methanol solution of transition metal salt,                  stirring for reaction at room temperature, then                  adding a saturated ammonium hexafluorophosphate                  aqueous solution into the solvent until no solid                  is precipitated, and washing and drying solid to                  obtain polymer supported synergistic                  catalyst.Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  first monomer to second monomer to initiator is                  1-100:100-1: 1-500. The initiator is AIBN or                  BPO.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pyridine compound                  containing terminal amino is                  4-aminopyridine,bipyridine compounds containing                  terminal amino groups, 4-amino-2, 2'                  -bipyridine.The acyl chloride compound containing                  terminal alkenyl is acryloyl chloride,                  undecylenic chloride, 5-hexenoyl chloride,                  p-phenylethenoyl chloride and vinyl                  chloroformate.The solvent is chloroform,                  methanol, N-dimethylformamide, dichloromethane,                  ethanol, petroleum ether, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  tetrahydrofuran, ethyl acetate, toluene,                  acetonitrile and benzene, tube sealing reaction                  condition is that tube sealing reaction is                  carried out for 10-15 h at 110-130                  &#8451;.

PN CN114392744-A
TI Preparing copper-iron bimetallic catalyst comprises mixing ferric nitrate nonahydrate and copper nitrate trihydrate with water, adding alkaline solution and propionaldehyde, stirring, reacting, cooling, centrifuging, washing, drying, tableting, sieving copper iron oxide and carbonizing.
AU ZENG J
   WU W
   LI H
   LI Z
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202268869V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-iron bimetallic catalyst comprises (a) mixing ferric nitrate nonahydrate and copper nitrate trihydrate with water to obtain a mixed solution, adding alkaline solution drop wise to the mixed solution, adding propionaldehyde, stirring, performing hydrothermal reaction, cooling, centrifuging, washing, drying to obtain copper iron oxide and (b) tableting, sieving the copper iron oxide, reducing and carbonizing to obtain the copper-iron bimetallic catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing copper-iron bimetallic catalyst
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains a copper-iron bimetallic catalyst with copper iron carbide interface through the control of the reaction conditions and prepares long-chain olefins with high selectivity in the normal pressure carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a copper-iron bimetallic catalyst prepared by the above method; and
   (2) a long-chain olefin comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen and the copper-iron bimetallic catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight and volume of the ferric nitrate nonahydrate, copper nitrate trihydrate, deionized water and propionaldehyde is 3.54-4.54 g, 1.92-2.92 g, 40-60 ml: 0.5-1.5 ml. In the step (a) the alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide. The concentration of the sodium hydroxide is 0.2-0.3 g/ml.Preferred Method: In the step (a) the dropping rate of the alkaline solution is 1 ml/minute and the magnetic stirring speed is 400revolutions/minute. In the step (a) the hydrothermal reaction process is carried out at 160-180degrees Celsius for 20-24 hours. In the step (a) the stirring time after adding alkaline solution drop wise is 20-30 minutes. The stirring time after adding propionaldehyde is 1-2 minutes. In the step (b) the reduction and carbonization treatment comprises performing hydrogen reduction and carbon dioxide-hydrogen mixed gas treatment, where the temperature of the hydrogen reduction is 350-400degrees Celsius, the reaction time is 1-2 hours, the hydrogen flow is 50-100 ml/minute and the pressure is 3-4 bar, the temperature of the carbon dioxide-hydrogen mixed gas treatment is 300-320degrees Celsius, the reaction time is 1-2 hours and the volume ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:3 and the mixed gas flow quantity is 15-20 ml/minute. In the step (a) the centrifugation process is carried out at 10000revolutions/minute for 2-3 minutes. The washing is deionized water for 2-3 times. The drying is vacuum drying at 50-60degrees Celsius for 10-12 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

In the step (3), the cleaning method is alternately cleaning by de-ionized water and methanol, and the drying condition is vacuum drying for 8-10 hours at room temperature. In the step (3), the mass ratio of isopropanol to ethyl acetate to normal hexane in the mixed solution is 5:3.8-4.2:4.8-5.2. The molar ratio of the fullerenol to sodium tetrachloropalladate is 1: 6.5-7.5; the solvent B is methanol solution with concentration of 2-2.5 mol/l, the concentration of fullerenol in the solvent B is 1.5-2 g/l. In the step (4), the reaction is as follows: under the condition of room temperature, stirring under the condition of visible light, the reaction time is 2-3 hours.

PN WO2021227079-A1
TI Producing urea through energy radiation involves e.g. combining nano-structured catalyst with carbon-containing source, nitrogen-containing source, which is in contact with hydrogen-containing source and irradiating nano-structured catalyst.
AU WANG C
   HUO H
AE BEIJING GUANGHE ORIGINAL TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0651J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing urea through energy radiation comprises combining the nano-structured catalyst with at least one carbon-containing source, at least one nitrogen-containing source which is in contact with at least one hydrogen-containing source, and irradiating the nano-structured catalyst, the carbon-containing source, the nitrogen-containing source, and the hydrogen-containing source to produce urea molecules, and the nano-structured catalyst includes at least one first component and at least one second component.
   USE - The method is useful for producing urea through energy radiation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the solar energy to convert the carbon dioxide into hydrocarbon, alcohol and other organic compound form to realize the sealing and utilization of carbon dioxide and the regeneration of the energy. The catalyst exhibits the effect of increasing the rate of chemical reaction by reducing the activation energy of the reaction, does not consumed in the catalytic reaction, which can continue to further react with a small amount of the catalytic reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The energy radiation is at least one of optical radiation and thermal radiation, preferably optical radiation. The nano-structured catalyst is formed by physically mixing at least one first component and at least one second component. The energy radiation causes the reaction to proceed at a temperature between 20-500 degrees C, preferably 90-100 degrees C, and the energy conversion rate for urea production is greater than 0.1%, and greater than 1% in the preferred temperature range. The energy radiation makes the reaction proceed at a temperature between about 20-300 degrees C, preferably 120-270 degrees C. The light radiation or thermal radiation is used to initiate the reaction, and light radiation or thermal radiation is used to make the reaction to proceed, where the light radiation power of the light radiation is 200-1500 W/m2, preferably 500-1000 W/m2. The light radiation increases the temperature of the nano-structured catalyst, the nitrogen-containing source and the hydrogen-containing source, and is preferably the only source for increasing the temperature. Preferred Components: The distance between the first component and the second component is less than or equal to 200 nm, preferably less than or equal to 100 nm, and preferably the two are in close contact. The nano-structured catalyst contains a chemical element that serves as the first component and the second component, or it contains at least two chemical element, alloy or compound. The nano-structured catalyst provides the at least one first component and the at least one second component in a nanostructure. The first component is consisting of cobalt, iron, aluminum, silver, platinum, copper, nickel, zinc, titanium, manganese, carbon, lead, rhodium and its alloy, preferably rhodium, cobalt or zinc. The second component is cobalt, iron, ruthenium, rhodium, osmium, iridium, lanthanum, cerium, copper, nickel, titanium, molybdenum, bismuth, vanadium, carbon and/or its oxide, sulfide, carbide, hydroxide, chloride, carbonate and bicarbonate, preferably cobalt, copper, carbon or cobalt carbide (CoC). The nano-structure catalyst is cobalt catalyst, cobalt-ruthenium catalyst, cobalt carbide catalyst or copper-zinc catalyst. At least one dimension of the length, width, and height of the nanostructure is about 1-1000 nm. The nanostructures are each independently about 1-3000 nm in length, width, and height, preferably 200-500 nm. The nanostructures each independently have an aspect ratio of 1-20, preferably 2-8. The shape of the nano-structure catalyst is spherical, spine, sheet, needle, grass, cylindrical, polyhedron, three-dimensional cone, square, sheet, semi-spherical, irregular three-dimensional shape, and/or porous structure. The multiple nanostructures are arranged in a pattern on the substrate, preferably arranged in multiple layers, or multiple nanostructures are randomly dispersed in the medium. The carbon-containing source consists of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, 1-4C hydrocarbons, synthesis gas, bicarbonate, and/or air, industrial flue gas, and tail gas containing more than one of these carbon sources or emissions, preferably carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide. The nitrogen-containing source is consists of nitrogen, air, ammonia, nitrogen oxide, nitro compound, and/or air, industrial flue gas, exhaust gas or emission containing more than one of these nitrogen-containing sources, preferably nitrogen. The hydrogen source consists of water, hydrogen, 1-4C hydrocarbon, and/or one of the hydrogen-containing source of air, industrial flue gas, tail gas or emission, preferably water.
   EXAMPLE - 8.1 g Cobalt chloride hexahydrate and 0.042 g ruthenium trichloride were added into a 250 ml three-necked flask. 100 ml Water was added and stirred to dissolve, and heated to 80 degrees C, and condensation and reflux reactions were performed. 10.88 g Sodium hydroxide was dissolved in 30 ml water and 30 ml hydrazine hydrate was added, and then the mixture was slowly dripped into a three-necked flask at a rate of 1.8 ml/minute with a titration pump, reacted for 60 minutes, the solution was cooled to room temperature, and subjected to ultrasonic washing with deionized water for 3 times and centrifuged to separate at 5000 revolutions/minute, and dried in a vacuum oven at 70 degrees C to obtain the cobalt-ruthenium catalyst.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anti-infective medical adhesive material involves: (1) uniformly mixing alkyl- alpha -cyanoacrylate compound with polylactic acid to obtain a medical adhesive matrix; (2) adding aerogel particles and plant anti-infective ingredients to the matrix, uniformly stirring and mixing to gel state to obtain an anti-infection matrix material; (3) adding the matrix material to an airtight container filled with an ionic liquid, removing air, filling the container with carbon dioxide, controlling the temperature and pressure in the container to make the carbon dioxide gas in a supercritical state, and when the matrix material is not in contact with the ionic liquid, carrying the ionic liquid to process the matrix material to obtain required medical adhesive material; and (4) sterilizing the adhesive material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing anti-infective medical adhesive material (claimed) used in clinical application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides anti-infective medical adhesive material, which has excellent anti-infective performance and long-term bonding strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkyl- alpha -cyanoacrylate compound includes n-butyl alpha -cyanoacrylate and n-octyl alpha -cyanoacrylate in a mass ratio of (50-60) wt.%:(40-50) wt.%. The mass ratio of the alkyl- alpha -cyanoacrylate compound to polylactic acid is (60-80) wt%:(20-40) wt.%. The ionic liquid includes choline chloride ionic liquid or choline bromide ionic liquid. The usage amount of the ionic liquid is 10-20 wt% of the mass of the matrix material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The plant anti-infective ingredients include dandelion extract, Aloe vera extract or Portulaca oleracea extract. The usage amount of the plant anti-infective ingredients is 20-30 wt.% of the medical adhesive matrix.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The aerogel particles include gum arabic aerogel particles. The usage amount of the aerogel particles is 10-30 wt.% of the medical adhesive matrix.

TI Producing chabazite (CHA) zeolite separation membrane with controlled size of defects, comprises e.g. adding carrier on which CHA particle seed layer is deposited to synthesis solution for zeolite growth containing organic structure.
AU CHOI J
   LEE K Y
   HONG S
   LEE K
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202102561F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing chabazite (CHA) zeolite separation membrane with a controlled size of defects, comprises (a) adding a carrier on which a CHA particle seed layer is deposited to a synthesis solution for zeolite growth containing an organic structure inducing agent and silicon and aluminum as raw materials, and performing hydrothermal synthesis, (b) performing rapid calcination at 700-1200 degrees C for 10 seconds to 5 minutes, and (c) performing low temperature calcination by heating at 450-550 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for producing chabazite zeolite separation membrane with a controlled size of defects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) chabazite (CHA) zeolite separation membrane with a controlled size of defects prepared by the above method; and
   (2) method for separating carbon dioxide from a mixture containing carbon dioxide and a molecule including methane, dinitrogen, oxygen, ethylene, ethane, propene and propane using the CHA zeolite separation membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The low temperature calcination of step (c) is slow calcination in which heating is increased at a rate of 0.1-5 degrees C/minute for 12-100 hours. The separations are carried out at 30-200 degrees C under dry conditions and 30-200 degrees C under humid conditions. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of silicon/aluminum is 5-1000.The support comprises alumina including alpha -alumina or gamma -alumina, polypropylene, polyethylene, polytetrafluoroethylene, polysulfone, polyimide, silica, glass, mullite, zirconia, titania, yttria, ceria, vanadium oxide, silicon, stainless steel and/or carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic structure directing agent comprises (N,N,N-trimethyladamantylammonium hydroxide), (N,N,N-trimethyladamantylammonium bromide), (N,N,N-trimethyladamantylammonium fluoride), (N,N,N-trimethyladamantylammonium chloride), (N,N,N-trimethyladamantylammonium iodide), tetraethylammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium fluoride, tetraethylammonium fluoride, tetraethylammonium fluorine, tetraethylammonium fluoride, tetraethylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium iodide and/or cyclohexylamine. The synthesis solution for zeolite growth has molar ratio of N,N,N-trimethyladamantylammonium hydroxide, silicon dioxide, water, sodium oxide and aluminum oxide of 1-100:100:1000-20000:0.5-50:0-10.

PT P
PN TW202100242-A; TW718568-B1
TI Preparation of perovskite composite oxide visible light catalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to manufacture alcohol, by calcining calcium-containing waste to form calcium source, and dissolving template material in water.
AU JEHNG J M
   DAI Y M
AE UNIV NAT CHUNGHSING (UCHN-C)
GA 2021799650
AB    NOVELTY - The present disclosure provides a method for fabricating a calcium titanium composite oxide photocatalyst. The method includes following steps: providing a calcium source, performing a template agent preparing step, providing a titanium source, performing a mixing step, performing an aging step, performing a calcining step. The calcium source is formed by calcining a calcium-containing waste. The template agent preparing step is for preparing the template agent. The titanium source is formed by adding tetrabutyl titanate to anhydrous alcohol. The mixing step is for forming a first mixture. The aging step is for forming a second mixture. The calcining step is for obtaining a calcium titanium composite oxide photocatalyst. Accordingly, the present disclosure can be controlled by the template agent, aging and calcining temperature to prepare the photocatalyst. The calcium titanium composite oxide photocatalyst can be used to perform photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to manufacture alcohol.

PN CN112062202-A
TI Removing glyphosate from water by adsorbing and removing calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite comprises e.g. adding calcium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide into a reactor filled with water, stirring, mixing and passing carbon dioxide.
AU ZHOU X
   PENG G
   TANG B
AE UNIV SHAOYANG (USAY-C)
GA 2020C6455F
AB    NOVELTY - Removing glyphosate from water by adsorbing and removing calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite comprises (i) adding calcium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide into a reactor filled with water, where the molar ratio of calcium hydroxide to aluminum hydroxide is 3-5:1 and the mass ratio of water to calcium hydroxide is 10-50:1, stirring, mixing and passing carbon dioxide, raising the temperature to 60-90 degrees C and reacting for 0.5-3 hours, (ii) stop stirring, continue to reacting at 60-90 degrees C for 10-30 hours and then dehydrating and drying to obtain calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite, (iii) calcining the resultant calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite at 400-600 degrees C for 1-5 hours and crushing.
   USE - The method is useful for removing glyphosate from water by adsorbing and removing calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is suitable for large-scale application; has clean preparation process and reduces the production cost; and the obtained product has high adsorption to glyphosate in water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components:The pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.1-0.5MPa. The adding amount of the calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite is 0.5-5g/l. The concentration of the glyphosate in the water is 10-10 mg/l. Preferred Method: The adsorption time is 0.5-2 hours.

   USE - The method is useful for extracting essential oil from Mongolian medicine womb warming seven-ingredient powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: lays a foundation for the subsequent research on the mechanism of medicine action and the development of dosage forms of Mongolian medicine enriched with active ingredients.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the temperature of the extraction is 45-50 degrees C, preferably 45 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa, preferably 28 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow is 48-52 l/hour, preferably 50 l/hour, and the extracting time is 80-120 minutes, preferably 90 minutes. In the step (2), the first separating pressure is 8-10 MPa, preferably 9 MPa, first separating temperature is 60-70 degrees C, preferably 65 degrees C; the second separating pressure is atmospheric pressure, and the second separating temperature 30-40 degrees C, preferably 35 degrees C. In the step (3), the volume ratio of the crude oil to water is 1:0.8-1.2, and the extraction is performed until the volatile oil no longer increases. AU LI X
   LI H
   TIAN H
   LI S
   WANG J
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST FORESTRY (USWF-C)
GA 202069405Q
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritically extracting truffle oil, comprises (i) pre-treating fresh truffle slices, where the pre-treatment is enzyme treatment or ultrasonic treatment to obtain processed truffle raw material, (ii) drying the pretreated truffle raw material, crushing the dried truffle, and classifying the powder to obtain the raw material, (iii) transferring the truffle powder to a supercritical extraction kettle, and using carbon dioxide as the supercritical fluid and ethanol as the cosolvent for extraction and separation, (iv) collecting the extract, adding into a freezer, and freezing, (v) centrifuging the extract, collecting the supernatant, and discarding the precipitate, and (vi) performing decompression treatment, and separating truffle oil from the extract.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritically extracting truffle oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not utilize organic solvents e.g. n-hexane and petroleum ether; solves the problem of residual organic solvents; achieves effective extraction of truffle oil; reduces extraction temperature controlled below 60 degrees C the loss of heat sensitive nutrients in truffle oil; and prepares product which has excellent quality and stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the enzymatic treatment comprises using enzymes e.g. cellulase, pectinase, protease and/or amylase at 20-60 degrees C with an ultrasonic frequency of 2 times for 5-30 minutes. In the step (ii), the drying treatment of the truffle raw material is microwave, roasting, drying, or vacuum freeze drying. In the step (iii), the extraction temperature in the supercritical extraction kettle is carried out at 30-60 degrees C with an extraction pressure of 10-35 MPa, where the amount of carbon dioxide/truffle raw material amount is 1-30/1. In the step (iv), the freezing treatment is -17 to 15 degrees C. In the step (vi), the separation of truffle oil is carried out by thin film evaporation equipment, molecular distillation equipment or countercurrent separation equipment. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the crushed truffle raw material has a particle size of 10-80 mesh. In the step (ii), the amount of ethanol cosolvent/the amount of truffle raw material is 0.2-4/1.

Clinacanthus nutans essential oil, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. eclipta prostrata extract. The mist generating agent is prepared from carbon dioxide and water with molar ratio of 1:0.4-0.8. The method involves preparing herba ecliptae extractive, taking ink drought lotus herb, cleaning, putting in steam explosion tank for 50-80 second, explosion time under the pressure condition of 1.1-1.3 megapascal after extracting for 2-4 times, combining extracting liquid, decompressing and concentrating until the relative density is 1.0-1.1 to obtain the herba ecliptae extractive.

AU CHEN K
AE ANHUI JINYI FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020226682
AB    NOVELTY - Producing salt soda water involves preparing                raw materials for salt and soda production and                dissolving the white sugar at 60-80 degrees C for                20 minutes. The dissolved solution is decolorized                by activated carbon and filtered through                diatomaceous earth. The sodium benzoate, common                salt, potassium sorbate, citric acid, sodium                citrate, vitamin-C, and soda flavor are dissolved                in the above proportions, filter them through a                200-300 mesh filter, and then add them to the                mixing tank. The solution of constant volume is                mixed with carbon dioxide through the steam-water                mixer according to the above ratio, and the                temperature of the carbon dioxide and the solution                is not higher than 10 degrees C during mixing to                obtain the finished product after filling and                sterilization inspection.
   USE - Method for producing salt soda water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enable to produce salt soda water                which has best solubility and also improves the                mixing effect and optimize the process.

Preferred Conditions: The stirring                  mechanism is designed in the mixing tank in the                  second step, and the mixing tank is a heating                  tank, and its temperature is maintained at 40                  degrees C. The heating tank is heated by a heater                  and is equipped with a control switch to control                  temperature. Preferred Components: The filter                  screen is a stainless steel filter module, which                  is a circular filter module. The stirring                  mechanism is designed in the soda-water mixer,                  and there is a heating element. The heating                  element is controlled by a controller, and the                  temperature is adjusted. The activated carbon and                  diatomaceous earth are wrapped by cotton cloth to                  form a filter knot.
