PN CN110777272-A
TI Applying organic chlorine oxidant in neutral leaching of uranium involves injecting carbon dioxide and oxygen into groundwater to prepare extractant, and controlling pH of extractant.
AU DENG J
   YUAN Y
   CHENG H
   XU Y
   ZHAO L
   LI J
   ZHOU G
   CHENG W
AE BEIJING CHEM ENG & METALLURGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 202015687S
AB    NOVELTY - Applying organic chlorine oxidant in neutral leaching of uranium involves injecting carbon dioxide and oxygen into groundwater to prepare an extractant, and controlling 6.8-7.5 pH of extractant, when the concentration of uranium in leachate reaches its peak value and no longer rises, stopping adding oxygen, adding sodium dichloroisocyanurate as an oxidant, and adding prepared 200-300 mg/L sodium dichloroisocyanurate solution to the injection manifold through a metering pump, monitoring the free chlorine concentration of leaching solution, when the free chlorine concentration more than 20 mg/L, reducing the concentration of oxidant sodium dichloroisocyanurate to 30-40%, i.e., 60-120 mg/L, when the leaching rate of block more than 70%, stopping adding oxidant until the leaching process ends.
   USE - Method for applying organic chlorine oxidant in neutral leaching of uranium (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can overcome effects of using oxygen as an oxidant for neutral leaching uranium mining, effectively oxidize tetravalent uranium in ore, increase the uranium concentration in leachate, and shorten the leaching cycle of ore.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Applying organic chlorine oxidant in neutral leaching of uranium involves adding 200-1200 mg/L carbon dioxide and 300-600 mg/L oxygen. It is determined based on the content of bicarbonate in the groundwater of ore-bearing aquifer. The addition of oxygen is stopped after 10 days.

PN CN110684731-A
TI Performing for ex vivo expansion and culture of natural killer (NK) cell body by adding stimulating factors and ratio regulator to blood containing NK cells after treatment, and culturing cells, adding cytokines, and culturing cells.
AU ZHANG Z
   ZHUO Y
   ZHANG Y
   YANG F
AE CHENGDU EXAB BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202007401S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing for ex vivo expansion and culture of natural killer (NK) cell body involves (a) adding stimulating factors and a ratio regulator with a final concentration of 0.1-10 nM to blood containing NK cells after treatment, and culturing the cells at 36.5-37.5 degrees C for 3-4 days under 5-10% carbon dioxide environment, and (b) adjusting the cell density to 0.5-1.5x 106 cells/ml while performing step (a), and continually adding a ratio regulator with a final concentration of 0.1-10 nM, and adding cytokines at a final concentration of 100-500 U/ml at 36.5-37.5 degrees C under 5-10% carbon dioxide environment, and continually culturing for 20-30 days.
   USE - The method is useful for performing for ex vivo expansion and culture of NK cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is performed in a stable, simple and feasible manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecule: The stimulating factor is a factor that functions to transmit a proliferation signal or an activation signal in a cell of the NK cell by specifically binding to a receptor on the surface of the NK cell, or it binds to a cell surface receptor of monocytes other than NK cells, and induces humoral factors to produce released factors. The stimulating factor comprises an anti-cluster of differentiation (CD)-16 antibody, OK432, and a cytokine, where the final concentration of the anti-CD16 antibody is 0.1-10 mu g/ml, the final concentration of OK432 is 0.008-0.015 KE/ml, and the final concentration of the cytokine is 50-100 ng/ml. The cytokine is at least one of interleukin, TNF or membrane cofactor protein (MCP). The anti-CD16 antibody is immobilized on a support by a solid phase method. The ratio regulator is rapamycin. Preferred Method: The method involves processing the blood by (a) transferring peripheral whole blood collected from vein to a sterile centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 900-1200 g for 10 minutes, collecting the supernatant as plasma, inactivating plasma by treating at 56 degrees C for 30 minutes, centrifuging at 900-1200 g for 10 minutes, and storing at 4 degrees C to obtain autologous plasma, (b) adding twice the volume of sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to components after plasma separation as peripheral whole blood before separation to prepare a blood cell solution, (c) superimposing blood cell solution on the lymphocyte separation solution, and removing the red blood cells and granulocytes by density gradient centrifugation to obtain peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC), washing the isolated PBMCs with PBS for 2-3 times, (d) resuspending the PBMCs cells in X-VIVO 15 medium and inoculating it into a petri dish, adhering for 30 minutes, transferring the suspended cells into a centrifuge tube, and using X-VIVO 15 medium supplemented with autologous plasma to adjust cell density of PBMCs to 1-3x 106 cells/ml, where the amount of the autologous plasma added is 5-10% of volume of the X-VIVO 15 medium. The step (b) involves adding X-VIVO 15 medium comprising 700 U/ml interleukin (IL)-2 for every 2-4 days until the end of the culture. The method involves adjusting the cell density by using X-VIVO 15 medium containing autologous plasma, where the amount of autologous plasma added is 5-10% of the volume of X-VIVO 15 medium.

PN CN110152743-A; CN110152743-B
TI Preparing loaded solid super acid catalyst comprises adding carrier and metal oxide to sulfuric acid solution, stirring, filtering, drying, and calcining.
AU HUANG J
   XU J
   SHANG X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201977316X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing loaded solid super acid catalyst comprises adding carrier and metal oxide to sulfuric acid solution, stirring, filtering, drying, and calcining at 450-650 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing loaded solid super acid catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The 5-hydroxymethylfurfural: uses catalyst soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide, uses low-boiling methanol as the organic phase; and can achieve supercritical state together with carbon dioxide. The method: can avoid hydration side reaction of 5- hydroxymethylfurfural and formation of solid insoluble matter; and can maintain both selectivity and yield at high level.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing 5-hydroxymethylfurfural by using the above method comprising (i) adding raw material, anhydrous methanol, and catalyst to reaction kettle, (ii) heating and pressurizing to the temperature and pressure required for the reaction, then sealing the reaction kettle, and stirring the reaction, and (iii) cooling the reaction mixture to room temperature after completing the reaction, then filtering the product solution, and evaporating for removing methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carrier is silicon-containing and/or fluorine-containing polymer. The metal oxide is zirconium dioxide, titanium dioxide, cobalt oxide, cobalt trioxide, molybdenum oxide, bismuth oxide, or nickel oxide. The dosage ratio of carrier, metal oxide and sulfuric acid solution is 1 g:1-5 g:10-40 ml. The sulfuric acid solution has a concentration of 0.5-2 M. The silicon-containing polymer comprises silicone rubber, or silicone resin, preferably polymethyl silicone resin, amino silicone resin, fluorosilicone resin, silicone-epoxy resin, methyl silicone rubber (MQ), methyl vinyl silicone rubber (VMQ), methyl phenyl vinyl silicone rubber (PVMQ), nitrile silicone rubber, or fluorosilicone rubber. The fluorine-containing polymer is fluorinated acrylic polymer, preferably poly(1,1-dihydroperfluorooctyl acrylate) (PFOA), or polytetrafluoroethylene. The raw material comprises glucose, fructose, chitosan, chitin, cellulose, hemicellulose, lignin, lactose, and raw biomass. The raw biomass comprises tree, peel, bamboo, and crop straw. The dosage ratio of catalyst, raw material and anhydrous methanol is 1 g:10-20 g:50-100 ml. Preferred Method: The stirring is carried out for 6-12 hours. The reaction for synthesizing 5-hydroxymethylfurfural is carried out at 240-320 degrees C under the pressure of 8.1-22 MPa for 3-10 hours.

TI Processing urban green waste based on composting process comprises e.g. collecting and classifying urban green waste, pre-treating, disinfecting, drying, crushing, controlling environmental conditions, and determining maturity of compost.
AU LIU S
AE LISHUI VOCATIONAL & TECH COLLEGE (LISH-Non-standard)
GA 2019286627
AB    NOVELTY - Processing urban green waste based on composting process comprises (i) collecting and classifying urban green waste in a unified manner for removing toxic and hazardous substances and reserving; (ii) pre-treating qualified urban green waste, removing composting interference, disinfecting and drying, crushing green waste using a crusher, and mediating appropriate moisture content, carbon/nitrogen ratio and pH value; (iii) controlling the environmental conditions of the treatment process, regulating oxygen, carbon dioxide and moisture content, simultaneously ventilating, turning over, and aerating to provide sufficient oxygen for biological reaction; and (iv) determining the maturity of the compost by physical indicators, chemical indicators, biological indicators and microbial indicators.
   USE - The method is useful for processing urban green waste based on composting process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the environmental conditions of nature, regulates the oxygen, carbon dioxide and moisture content suitable for composting, promotes the recycling of urban green waste and sustainable development of agriculture, improves urban environmental sanitation, and protects and develops ecological construction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the disinfecting is carried out using 75% alcohol and the crushing is carried out to a particle diameter of 10-50 mm. The step (iii) comprises performing proper ventilation, turning, controlling stack temperature, removing redundant moisture and carbon dioxide, providing oxygen for biological aeration. In the step (iv), the physical indicators are odor and temperature, the chemical indicators are carbon/nitrogen ratio, cation exchange capacity, and nitrification, the biological indicator is a seed germination index, and the microbial indicator is respiration.

PN CN109174072-A
TI Supercritical preparation method of nano titanium dioxide, involves subjecting suspension emulsion to temperature-reducing pressure-reducing reaction and then cooling to room temperature while being purged with air.
AU WU Y
AE WU Y (WUYY-Individual)
GA 201909569X
AB    NOVELTY - A supercritical preparation method of nano titanium dioxide involves (1) adding n-butyl titanate to absolute ethanol, ultrasonically reacting for 20-30 minutes to obtain a titanium alcohol liquid, (2) flushing the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into the titanium alcohol liquid, sealing and stirring until completely mixed to obtain an organic titanium mixed liquid, (3) maintaining the organic mixture at a constant temperature and constant pressure state and adding a trace amount of distilled water for microwave reaction for 1-3 hours to obtain a suspension emulsion, (4) subjecting the suspension emulsion to a temperature-reducing pressure-reducing reaction for 2-5 hours and then cooling to room temperature while being purged with air to obtain a white precipitate.
   USE - Supercritical preparation method of nano titanium dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of supercritical carbon dioxide fluids can reduce the density and viscosity of countless ethanol, ensuring uniform dispersion of n-butyl titanate and nano-titanium dioxide. The method is provided the uniform particle size distribution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in the step (2) is 40-50 degrees C, the pressure is 8-10 MPa, the pressure of the sealing stirring is 8-10 MPa, the temperature is 45-50 degrees C, and the stirring speed is 1000-2000 rpm. The temperature in the constant temperature and constant pressure state in the step (3) is 50-60 degrees C, and the pressure is 8-10 MPa. The power of the microwave reaction is 500-1000 W, and the temperature is 0-70 degrees C. The temperature rising rate of temperature rising and pressure releasing reaction in the step (4) is 2-5 degrees C, a temperature of 80-85 degrees C, and the pressure releasing speed of 0.1-0.2 MPa/minute. The speed of the temperature drop in the step (4) is 2-5 degrees C/minute and the flow rate of the purged air is 10-20 mL/minute. Preferred Composition: The amount of the trace distilled water in the step (3) is 0.5-1.5 %mass of anhydrous ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonic frequency of the ultrasonic reaction is 40-60 kHz, and the temperature is 50-60 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The concentration of n-butyl titanate in the step (1) is 90-130 g/L in absolute ethanol.

PT P
PN WO2019009754-A1
TI Preparation of nickel-cerium-zirconium mixed oxide-based catalyst used for e.g. reforming methane with carbon dioxide to produce syngas, involves performing solvothermal supercritical synthesis in supercritical alcohol.
AU SADYKOV V A
   SMIRNOVA M Y
   MEZENTSEVA N V
   PAVLOVA S N
   FEDOROVA Y E
   SIMONOV M N
AE AS RUSSIA PHYSIOLOGICALLY ACTIVE SUBSTAN (ASPH-Non-standard)
GA 2019047873
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst (I) involves performing solvothermal supercritical synthesis in a supercritical alcohol. The catalyst comprises a nickel oxide as active component, and a nanocrystalline cerium-zirconium mixed oxide (II) as a carrier comprising single-phase oxide with a fluorite-like structure of composition.
   USE - Preparation of catalyst used in chemical industry for producing single-phase oxide materials, and for reforming methane with carbon dioxide to produce syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst comprising mixed cerium-zirconium oxide and nickel oxide exhibits increased catalytic activity and stability in the reforming reaction of methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of catalyst of formula: aNiO+bCeyZr1-yO2 (I) involves performing solvothermal supercritical synthesis in a supercritical alcohol. The catalyst comprises a nickel oxide as active component, and a nanocrystalline cerium-zirconium mixed oxide of formula: CeyZr1-yO2 (II) as a carrier comprising single-phase oxide with a fluorite-like structure of composition.
   a = 5-20;
   b = 80-95;
   a+b = 100%;and
   y = 0.2-0.8.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process, nickel oxide is introduced into cerium zirconium oxide at the stage of supercritical synthesis, or by impregnation method. The nanocrystalline single-phase fluorite-like mixed cerium-zirconium oxide is prepared from a solution obtained by dissolving cerium and zirconium salts in supercritical alcohol e.g. isopropanol or butanol in a flow reactor, and adding acetylacetone to the solution. Preferred Component: The zirconium salt acts as a zirconium oxide precursor and is preferably zirconium butoxide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of cerium to zirconium in the single-phase cerium-zirconium oxide is 2-8.

PT P
PN CN108862319-A; CN108862319-B
TI Processing melamine by-product ammonia by            synthetic ammonia raw material gas by heating urea with            silica gel, boiling, cooling and crystallizing melamine            powder, and bubbling synthetic ammonia raw material gas            into carbonization tower.
AU XU Y
AE ANHUI HUAERTAI CHEM IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201895956D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing melamine by-product ammonia by                synthetic ammonia raw material gas comprises                heating urea with silica gel as catalyst to 390-410                degrees C, boiling to prepare mixed gas of                melamine, ammonia and carbon dioxide, cooling and                crystallizing melamine powder, absorbing ammonia                gas and carbon dioxide gas by demineralized water,                generating ammonia with high carbide and synthetic                ammonia raw material gas by synthetic ammonia                device, entering synthetic ammonia raw material gas                into carbonization tower by compressor inlet,                bubbling with ammonia with high carbide to generate                ammonium hydrogen carbonate suspension,                centrifugally separating to obtain dilute aqueous                ammonia, entering into fixed tower and composite                tower, separating by gas-water separator,                collecting triamine-aqueous ammonia by-product,                mixing and stirring with fresh water, fully                contacting, reducing degree of carbide, cooling,                entering into concentrated ammonia tank and washing                in washing tower.
   USE - Method for processing melamine by-product                ammonia by synthetic ammonia raw material                gas.

PT P
PN EP3403806-A1
TI Additive manufacturing process for obtaining customized three-dimensional structure, involves saturating thermoplastic material with supercritical gas, extruding and transforming material into filament, and depositing filament in strands.
AU MARASCIO M
   BOURBAN P
   PIOLETTI D
AE EPFL ECOLE POLYTECHNIQUE FEDERALE LAUSAN (EPFL-C)
GA 201891143J
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves saturating thermoplastic material with supercritical gas such as carbon dioxide. The thermoplastic material is extruded and transformed into filament, powder or granules. The filament is deposited in smaller spaced strands. Microporosity is adjusted with extrusion nozzle geometry, temperature and deposition speed, where molten material forms the filament that is subsequently transformed into a set of strands. The microporosity is induced within the strands by supercritical gas foaming i.e. carbon dioxide foaming. The macroporosity is generated by spaces between the strands.
   USE - Additive manufacturing process for obtaining a customized 3D structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables producing polymers with reproducible properties and market availability, thus producing structures at high strength/density ratio and high surface area with defined porosity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a three-dimensional (3d) structure
   (2) a 3d printing machine for obtaining 3D structures
   (3) a foam printing head.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating an additive manufacturing process for obtaining a customized 3D structure.

AU DU S

AE ZHEJIANG JIZHU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201878008N
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing bamboo activated carbon, involves (S1) performing dicing process of bamboo into small pieces of 30-50 mm, and length of not exceeding 50 mm, (S2) rapidly drying at temperature of 150-350 degrees C, controlling the moisture content of bamboo at 28-32%, (S3) carbonizing at temperature of 200-550 degrees C for 60-120 minutes, (S4) performing activation treatment of bamboo in the activation furnace, introducing mixed gas of carbon dioxide and water vapor into activation furnace, controlling flow rate of mixed gas at 18-22 m3/hour, activating at 850-950 degrees C for 80-120 minutes, (S5) cooling, grinding the milled bamboo, and passing through 325 mesh filter screen to obtain bamboo powder with fineness of 99%.

PT P
PN US2018202272-A1; US10316631-B2
TI Increasing oil recovery rate for recovering heavy oil, involves injecting an effective amount of carbon monoxide into reservoir at natural temperature, comparing rate of oil recovery which is increased, injecting pure carbon dioxide.
AU TROST P
AE TROST P (TROS-Individual)
GA 201855931F
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing oil recovery rate involves injecting an effective amount of carbon monoxide into a reservoir at natural temperature, comparing rate of oil recovery which is increased as compared to a rate of oil recovery which is achieved by injecting pure carbon dioxide into the reservoir.
   USE - Increasing oil recovery rate for recovering heavy oil, protecting oilfield tubulars from corrosion and increasing oil and gas production (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for recovering heavy oil from reservoir, which involves injecting an effective amount of carbon monoxide into a reservoir at natural temperature; and
   (2) a method for protecting oilfield tubulars from corrosion, which involves injecting an effective amount carbon monoxide into a reservoir to protect tubulars from corrosion; and
   (3) a method of increasing oil and gas production, which involves: adding an effective amount of carbon monoxide to a fracking fluid and injecting the fracking fluid with carbon monoxide into a reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Condition: The effective amount of carbon monoxide is at least 10% gas mixture which is injected into the reservoir. The effective amount of carbon monoxide is at least 20% gas mixture which is injected into the reservoir. The injection takes place during a primary phase, a secondary phase or a tertiary phase of oil recovery operations. Thecarbon dioxide and the carbon monoxide are injected into the reservoir. The gas mixture comprises nitrogen, carbon dioxide and the carbon monoxide which are is injected into the reservoir,and amount of carbon monoxide is at least 10% of the gas mixture. The carbon monoxide is injected into a reservoir at natural temperature which is less than 250 degrees F. The rate of heavy oil recovery from the reservoir is increased. Preferred Components: The gas mixture comprises carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, which is injected into the reservoir at natural temperature. The gas mixture comprises carbon monoxide and other hydrocarbon gases and non-hydrocarbon gases which are injected into the reservoir at natural temperature. The gas mixture comprises carbon monoxide and one or more of carbon dioxide, methane, ethane, propane, butane, and pentane. Preferred Composition: The effective amount of carbon monoxide in a gas mixture is at least 20% carbon monoxide, preferably 30%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PT P
PN WO2018096199-A1; ES2669429-A1; CA3044347-A1; ES2669429-B1; EP3545772-A1; EP3545772-A4; MX2019006007-A1; US2021212331-A1; US11363823-B2
TI Preservation of fresh juice of citrus fruits and            pomegranates for conservation of other juices and            mixtures of fruit and vegetable juices involves            introducing carbon dioxide in dry ice form to container            and introducing juice to container.
AU CUEVAS VILLOSLADA E
   DEZA SAN BAUDILIO F
   SAN BAUDILIO F
   VILLOSLADA E
   CUEVAS V E
   DEZA S B F
AE XEXPRIMIR SL (XEXP-Non-standard)
   XEXPRIMIR SL (XEXP-Non-standard)
   XEXPRIMIR SL (XEXP-Non-standard)
GA 201842161U
AB    NOVELTY - Preservation of fresh juice of citrus fruits                and pomegranates for conservation of other juices                and mixtures of fruit and vegetable juices is                performed by cooling and disinfecting fruit from                which juice is intended to be obtained, by                squeezing or extruding fruit, subjecting container                to gasification process, introducing carbon dioxide                (CO2) in the form of dry ice until oxygen in                container is evacuated, introducing natural juice                pumped from conservation container, and immediately                sealing container tightly for storage in cold room                at 1-4 degrees C.
   USE - Preservation of fresh juice of citrus fruits                and pomegranates for conservation of other juices                and mixtures of fruit and vegetable juices                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preservation of juice,                without any type of thermal treatment prior or                subsequent to packaging, for a minimum of 9 days,                deferring its oxidation and maintaining all the                natural properties of fresh juice. The method is                stable and ensures absence of oxygen in the                container, thus preventing oxidation of food                product in container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Processes: Optionally, immediately                  after filling the container with juice and before                  sealing it, a drop of liquid nitrogen is                  introduced into container. Liquid nitrogen is                  added under the condition that sugar content of                  juice has Brix degree of greater than or equal to                  12.5 and/or juice has pH level of greater than or                  equal to 3. Application of CO2 in the form of dry                  ice is carried out in proportion of 0.01-0.2%                  with respect to total volume of juice to be                  introduced into container. Application of liquid                  nitrogen is carried out in proportion of                  0.01-0.2% with respect to total volume of juice                  to be introduced into container.

PT P
PN WO2018097222-A1; EP3546936-A1; JP2018552961-X; US2020182856-A1; EP3546936-A4
TI Evaluating subject's nutritional status by calculating expiration discharged from subject to which labeled amino acid is administered and calculating ratio of carbon dioxide to unlabeled carbon dioxide and comparing with index value.
AU INADA M
   KAWATA K
   SUDOU K
AE OTSUKA PHARM CO LTD (SAKA-C)
   OTSUKA PHARM CO LTD (SAKA-C)
   OTSUKA PHARM CO LTD (SAKA-C)
GA 201842822Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for evaluating a subject's nutritional status involves (a) calculating expiration discharged from a subject to which a 13C-labeled amino acid is administered as a test sample and calculating a ratio of 13carbon dioxide (CO2) amount to the amount of unlabeled CO2 in the test sample or the total CO2 amount and (b) comparing the ratio with the index value to determine the subject's nutritional status.
   USE - The method and the composition are useful for evaluating subject's nutritional status and diagnosing sarcopenia of subject(all claimed), which is useful for providing information for the selection of diagnostic and treatment methods for diseases such as liver diseases and metabolic diseases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method conveniently and quickly evaluates the real-time nutritional status of subject, than a known respiratory quotient method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a composition; and
   (2) a method for diagnosing sarcopenia of subject.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecules: The 13C-labeled amino acid is chosen from 13C-labeled alanine, 13C-labeled glycine, 13C-labeled serine, 13C-labeled threonine, 13C-labeled cysteine and 13C-labeled tryptophan. The 13C -labeled amino acid is chosen from 13C-labeled glutamic acid, 13C-labeled glutamine, 13C-labeled arginine, 13C-labeled histidine and 13C-labeled proline. The 13C -labeled amino acid is chosen from 13C-labeled leucine, 13C-labeled ricin, 13C-labeled threonine, 13C-labeled isoleucine, 13C-labeled tyrosine, 13C-labeled phenylalanine and 13C-labeled tryptophan. The 13C-labeled amino acid is chosen from 13C-labeled alanine, 13C-labeled glutamic acid and 13C-labeled leucine. Preferred Subject: The subject is suffering from a liver disease or a metabolic disease. The subject is afflicted with cirrhosis. The subject is suffering from diabetes. Preferred Method: The index value is a value obtained from a ratio calculated in accordance with the step (a) in a group of animals of a normal nutritional state of the same species as the subject. The method involves determining that the subject is in a malnutrition state and has sarcopenia, when the ratio is higher than the index value and determining that the subject is in a normal nutritional state, if it is less than or equal to the index value.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN108085270-A; CN108085270-B
TI Preparing a fecal fungi liquid comprises e.g. adding sterile sodium chloride solution to fecal sample, adding lithium-based kaolin, uniformly mixing, filtering feces slurry and taking out filtrate under protection of carbon dioxide gas.
AU CHEN T
   CAI X
   ZHANG Z
   MA D
AE GUANGDONG LONGSEE MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018451330
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a fecal fungi liquid comprises (i) adding a sterile sodium chloride (NaCl) solution to a fecal sample, adding lithium-based kaolin and uniformly mixing to obtain a fecal slurry; (ii) initially filtering the feces slurry, and taking out the filtrate under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, nitrogen, helium, neon, or argon, stirring at intervals of 1800-2200 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 3-6 minutes in every 0.8-1.2 hours and for 4-7 hours to obtain fecal fungi slurry; and (iii) filtrating fecal fungi slurry and filtering to obtain the crude fecal fungi solution, centrifuging coarse fecal fungi liquid, collecting a precipitate, resuspending the suspension to a colorless and odorless liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a fecal fungi liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes iron, titanium, and barium ions in human feces, and makes feces donated by feces donors whose iron, titanium, and barium ions are exceeds the standards.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The lithium-based kaolin is prepared by adding water and lithium metaphosphate to kaolin mineral, heating to 70-80 degrees C, adding sodium carbonate (Na2CO3), and stirring for 2.5-3.5 hours, allowing to stand for 20-30 hours, washing with deionized water by centrifugation, and recovering and drying the solid. thE cation exchange capacity of lithium-based kaolin is 70-90 mmol/100 g. In the step (ii), the process of the primary filtration is: removing the large particulate matter by filtering the fecal slurry through a filter with a pore size of 4.5-5.5 mm. In the step (iii), the process of the filtration of fecal slurry is: the pore size is 1.5-2.5 mm, 0.8-1.2 mm, 0.4-0.6 mm and 0.2-0.3 mm filter. In the step (iii), the rotation speed of centrifuge is 5500-6500 rpm and the time is 12-18 minutes. Preferred Components: The ratio of kaolin mineral, lithium metaphosphate, Na2CO3 and water is 80-120 g:8-12 g:18-22 g:1 l. In the step (i), the dosage ratio of stool sample, NaCl solution and lithium-based kaolin is 1g: 3-7 ml: 2-3 g. The concentration of NaCl solution is 0.85-0.95% (w/v).

PN CN107759812-A; WO2019072105-A1; CN107759812-B
TI Preparing crosslinked polymer based on waste            polystyrene used for adsorption and separation of            carbon dioxide, involves stirring waste polystyrene,            organic solvent, crosslinking agent and catalyst under            protection of atmosphere.
AU LIU C
   FU Z
   LEI X
   GU J
   WANG L
   JIA J
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
   UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 201819972M
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of crosslinked polymer                based on waste polystyrene involves: (A) weighing                1-6 wt.% waste polystyrene, 80-90 wt.% organic                solvent, 1-6 wt.% crosslinking agent and 2-8 wt.%                catalyst, stirring the weighed components at room                temperature for 10-50 minutes under the protection                of atmosphere, and then heating at 80-90 degrees C                for 6-24 hours; and (B) adding an alcohol solution                to resulting mixture obtained in step (A) to stop                the reaction, then cooling to room temperature, and                carrying out purification treatment to obtain the                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing crosslinked                polymer based on waste polystyrene, which is useful                for adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of                crosslinked polymer based on waste polystyrene with                highly three-dimensional network porous structure,                and can provide effective way for the recovery of                waste polystyrene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                crosslinked polymer based on waste polystyrene                prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is chosen                  from ferric chloride and/or aluminum                  chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent                  is carbon tetrachloride, dimethoxymethane,                  p-xylylene dichloride, bis(chloromethyl)biphenyl                  and/or m-trichlorotoluene. The organic solvent is                  toluene, o-xylene, ethylbenzene, dichloromethane,                  dichloroethane, chloroform and nitrobenzene. In                  step (B), the concentration of alcohol solution                  is 40-60 vol.%/vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparation method                  further involves (C) initially cleaning and                  drying the waste polystyrene before the step (A).                  The purification treatment in step (B) involves                  filtering the cooled reaction solution,                  respectively washing the cooled reaction solution                  with ethanol and dilute hydrochloric acid                  solution, then washing with deionized water until                  the filtrate is colorless, finally extracting the                  filtrate with a Soxhlet extractor containing                  methanol for 18-30 hours, and vacuum drying at                  40-60 degrees C for 18-30 hours.

   USE - Methyl anthranilate ethylenediamine derivative Schiff base zinc complex used as catalyst for synthesis of poly(cyclohexene carbonate) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound has catalytic efficiency of 1316 g polymer/g zinc, copolymer polycarbonate content of more than 90%, simple preparation, and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A methyl anthranilate ethylenediamine derivative Schiff base zinc complex of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I); and
   (2) application of the compound (I) as catalyst for synthesis of poly(cyclohexene carbonate) comprising adding (I) into autoclave equipped with stirrer, vacuum-drying at 80 degrees C for 2 hours, cooling to room temperature, injecting cyclohexene oxide into autoclave, heating to 80-160 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide at 1-5 MPa, stirring system to react for 8-40 hours, releasing carbon dioxide after reaction, dissolving obtained viscous mixture in chloroform, removing catalyst in system using 5% hydrochloric acid solution, washing product with water, and precipitating with alcohol as white product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The (I) is prepared by dissolving methyl anthranilate and ethylenediamine separately in methanol, adding methyl anthranilate solution dropwise to ethylenediamine solution with stirring at room temperature, heating mixture under reflux with stirring for 1-2 hours, filtering precipitate off, drying to obtain methyl anthranilate-ethylenediamine complex, placing complex in flask, filling flask with nitrogen, cooling on ice salt bath, adding diethylzinc, stirring mixture for 1 hour, removing from ice salt bath, and heating to 60 degrees C with to continuous stirring for 1-2 hours. Preferred Component: The molar ratio of methyl anthranilate and ethylenediamine is 1:0.4-0.6. The molar ratio of methyl anthranilate-ethylenediamine complex and diethylzinc is 1:1-1.2. The mass ratio of (I) and cyclohexene oxide is 1:200-400.

AU MATSUMOTO Y
   CHIBA M
AE KOBE STEEL LTD (KOBM-C)
GA 201807363P
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of steel wire involves preparing a rolled material containing 0.20-0.60 %mass carbon, 1.0-3.0 %mass silicon, 0.10-1.5 %mass manganese, 0.015 %mass or less phosphorus, 0.015 %mass or less sulfur, 0.3-1.5 %mass chromium, 0.001-0.10 %mass aluminum, 0.001-0.02 %mass nitrogen, and remaining iron and impurities, spheroidizing annealing the rolled material in an atmosphere containing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide at 700-830 degrees C for 5-15 hours, cold working and low-temperature annealing the rolled material at 600-700 degrees C for 3-10 hours in an atmosphere of 90 vol.% inert gas.
   USE - Manufacture of steel wire used for manufacturing bolt (claimed) used for motor vehicle and various industrial machines.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of producing steel wire having excellent delayed fracture resistance and fatigue resistance, and high strength.

PN WO2022010769-A1
TI Performing dilute methane oxidation (DMO) to convert methane to carbon dioxide, by mixing first gas stream having methane with second gas stream having oxygen to form third gas stream and contacting third stream with DMO catalytic material.
AU RADAELLI G
   PINAUDA L
   SCHER E
AE ICARUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (ICAR-Non-standard)
GA 202210283A
AB    NOVELTY - Performing dilute methane oxidation (DMO) to convert methane into carbon dioxide, comprises: mixing a first gas stream comprising methane with a second gas stream comprising oxygen to form a third gas stream comprising methane and oxygen, the third gas stream contains less than 5 mol.% methane; and performing DMO reaction by contacting the third gas stream with a DMO catalytic material in a fixed bed reactor to produce a fourth gas stream comprising carbon dioxide in an amount greater than that present in the third gas stream, the third gas stream enters the fixed bed reactor at a pressure not greater than 5 barg and at a temperature not greater than 600 degrees C, where the DMO reaction using the DMO catalytic material as a heterogeneous catalyst has a methane conversion of at least 10% and a selectivity to carbon dioxide of at least 30% in the fixed bed reactor under the conditions of it.
   USE - The method is useful for performing DMO to convert methane into carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is catalytic processes preferably high performance and stable catalysts useful as heterogeneous catalysts in catalytic reactions; enables complete oxidation of diluted methane, ethane, and higher hydrocarbons in off-gas streams at more favorable conditions, i.e. lower temperatures or without requirement for additional fuel gas; and increases stability, decreased susceptibility to poisons and/or inhibitors, and/or decreased costs relative to existing materials are all favorable, which can have significant positive impact on the environment and economics of operators and is very exothermic nature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) performing dilute alkane oxidation (DAO) to convert alkanes into carbon dioxide, comprising mixing a first gas stream comprising alkanes with a second gas stream comprising oxygen to form a third gas stream comprising alkanes and oxygen, where the third gas stream contains less than 5 mol.% alkanes and performing a DAO reaction by contacting the third gas stream with a heterogeneous DAO catalytic material in a fixed bed reactor to produce a fourth gas stream comprising carbon dioxide in an amount greater than that present in the third gas stream, the third gas stream enters the fixed bed reactor at the pressure and the temperature, the DAO reaction using the DAO catalytic material as a heterogeneous catalyst has an alkane conversion of at least 10% and a selectivity to carbon dioxide of at least 30%;
   (2) performing dilute hydrocarbon oxidation (DHO) to convert hydrocarbons into carbon dioxide, comprising mixing a first gas stream comprising hydrocarbons with a second gas stream comprising oxygen to form a third gas stream comprising hydrocarbons and oxygen, the third gas stream contains less than 5 mol.% hydrocarbons and performing a DHO reaction by contacting the third gas stream with a heterogeneous DHO catalytic material in a fixed bed reactor to produce a fourth gas stream comprising carbon dioxide in an amount greater than that present in the third gas stream, the third gas stream enters the fixed bed reactor at the pressure and the temperature, where the DHO reaction using the DHO catalytic material as a heterogeneous catalyst has a hydrocarbon conversion of at least 10% and a selectivity to carbon dioxide of at least 30%;
   (3) DHO catalytic material comprising a perovskite, mixed lanthanide oxide, nanowires and nanoparticles, where the DHO catalytic material further comprises at least one dopant from group 2 and at least one dopant from groups 4, 9, 10, and/or 11, the DHO catalytic material comprises a hydrocarbon conversion of at least 10% and a selectivity to carbon dioxide of at least 30% when the DHO catalytic material is employed as a heterogeneous catalyst contacting a gas stream comprising oxygen and a hydrocarbon in a fixed bed reactor at the pressure and the temperature, the gas stream contains less than 5 mol.% hydrocarbons;
   (4) DAO catalytic material comprising a perovskite, mixed lanthanide oxide, nanowires and nanoparticles, where the DAO catalytic material further comprises dopants from group 2 and at least one dopant from groups 4, 9, 10, and/or 11, the DAO catalytic material comprises an alkane conversion of at least 10% and a selectivity to carbon dioxide of at least 30% when the DAO catalytic material is employed as a heterogeneous catalyst contacting a gas stream comprising oxygen and an alkane in a fixed bed reactor at the pressure and the temperature, the gas stream contains less than 5 mol.% alkanes; and
   (5) DMO catalytic material comprising a perovskite, mixed lanthanide oxide, nanowires, and nanoparticles, where the DMO catalytic material further comprises dopants from group 2 and at least one dopant from groups 4, 9, 10, and/or 11, where the DMO catalytic material comprises a methane conversion of at least 10% and a selectivity to carbon dioxide of at least 30% when the DMO catalytic material is employed as a heterogeneous catalyst contacting a gas stream comprising oxygen and methane in a fixed bed reactor at the pressure and the temperature, where the gas stream contains less than 5 mol.% methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dopant comprises boron, aluminum, gallium, arsenic, phosphorus, antimony, bismuth, copper, cobalt, iron, zirconium, lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium, neodymium, promethium, samarium, europium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium, terbium, ytterbium or lutetium. TI Producing eco-friendly dried persimmon, comprises            loading astringent persimmons on resin film, spraying            hot water and carbon dioxide in particulate form inside            the resin film in state in which astringency persimmon            are loaded in chamber.
AU PARK M
   JUN B S
AE PARK M (PARK-Individual)
GA 2022E13832
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sealing a resin film after                spraying hot water and carbon dioxide in                particulate form inside the resin film in a state                in which astringent persimmon is loaded on an inner                side of a de-intercalation chamber. The resin film                is heated to an internal temperature of the                chamber, and the sealed film is maintained at 45-50                degree Celsius for 24-36 hours. The persimmons are                inserted into an aseptically-treated drying                chamber, and cold and hot air is alternately                supplied into the drying chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                environmentally friendly dried persimmon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce eco-friendly                dried persimmons having chewy texture like jelly,                prevents generation of contamination sources such                as browning, mold, and fine substances, and                shortens drying time.

   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium carbonate/talc powder composite material, which is useful in field of paper making filler, plastic and rubber modification.

In the process, using calcium carbonate/talc composite material as a template, and coating chemically deposited calcium carbonate on the surface of the lamellar talc to form a structural powder.

PN CN112007482-A
TI Electrolytic volatile organic compound method involves oxidizing volatile organic compound in solution into soluble intermediate substance, carbon dioxide, water and other small molecular products.
AU HUANG F
   DU J
AE JIANGMEN YICHAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C3387J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves continuously introducing volatile organic compound (VOC) gas, entering into collecting box (001) to be absorbed by absorption liquid, and entering into electrolytic cell of electrolytic tower under action of catalyst. The large amount of strong oxidizing hydroxyl free radical is generated through electrolytic reaction of anode (007) and cathode (008). The VOC in solution is oxidized into soluble intermediate substance, carbon dioxide, water and other small molecular products. The VOC flow speed in solution is reduced, the efficiency of gas to be electrolyzed is enhanced, and the absorption liquid is recycled.
   USE - Electrolytic volatile organic compound method.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables reducing VOCs flow speed in solution, enhancing efficiency of gas to be electrolyzed, and reducing cost of changing solution for multiple times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electrolytic volatile organic compound system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the electrolytic volatile organic compound method and system.
   Collecting box (001)
   VOC inlet (003)
   Anode (007)
   Cathode (008)
   Electrolytic tower (013)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The absorption liquid is water, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium acetate, potassium acetate, sodium sulfate, propylene carbonate, and glycerin.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN111943162-A; CN111943162-B
TI Microalgae-based biomass nitrogen-doped carbon            material useful in gas adsorption, e.g. carbon dioxide,            methane, sulfur dioxide, and preparing electric double            layer supercapacitors, preferably electric double-layer            supercapacitor.
AU FANG Y
   GE L
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2020B7239R
AB    NOVELTY - Microalgae-based biomass nitrogen-doped carbon                material, which is porous carbon material doped                with nitrogen, and its pore structure belongs to a                mesoporous structure.
   USE - The microalgae-based biomass nitrogen-doped                carbon material is useful in gas adsorption,                preferably, the gas includes carbon dioxide,                methane, sulfur dioxide, and preparing electric                double layer supercapacitors, preferably electric                double-layer supercapacitor prepared with 6 mol/l                potassium hydroxide aqueous solution as electrolyte                and polypropylene as diaphragm (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing microalgae-based biomass nitrogen-doped                carbon material, comprising (i) mixing the dried                microalgae powder with glucose and water,                performing first carbonization, filtering and                separating product, and drying the solid phase                product to obtain semicoke, (ii) mixing and                grinding semi-coke and solid potassium hydroxide,                heating to carbonization temperature under the                nitrogen atmosphere, performing second                carbonization to obtain microalgae base biomass                nitrogendoped carbon material precursor, and (iii)                placing microalgal-based biomass nitrogen-doped                carbon material precursor in hydrochloric acid,                stirring, filtering, washing, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen content                  of the microalgae-based biomass nitrogen-doped                  carbon material is 2.9 wt.%, the specific surface                  area is 1893.26 m2/g, the maximum carbon dioxide                  adsorption capacity at 0 degrees C is 4.18-5.72                  mmol/g, in the electric double layer                  supercapacitor system, at high current density is                  1 A/g, and the specific capacitance is 303.75-                  335.7 F/g. In step (i), the mass ratio of                  microalgae powder and glucose is 1:1 based on the                  total amount of microalgae powder and glucose,                  and the amount of water added is 10-12 ml/g. In                  the step (ii), the mass ratio of the semi-coke to                  potassium hydroxide is 1: (3-4), the heating rate                  is 10 degrees C/minute, the carbonization                  temperature is 750 degrees C, and the second                  carbonization time is 1 hour, the drying                  temperature is 60 degrees C, and the drying time                  is /- 12 hours. The microalgae-based biomass                  nitrogen-doped carbon material is a porous carbon                  material doped with nitrogen, and its pore                  structure belongs to a mesoporous structure. The                  nitrogen content of the microalga-based biomass                  nitrogen-doped carbon material is 2.9 wt.%, the                  specific surface area is 1893.26 m2/g and the                  carbon dioxide adsorption capacity is 4.18-5.72                  mmol/g at 0 degrees C, in the electric double                  layer supercapacitor system, at high current                  density of 1 A/g, and the specific capacitance is                  303.75-335.7 F/g. Preferred Method: In the step                  (i), the first carbonization temperature is                  150-200 degrees C, the first carbonization time                  is 24-28 hours, the drying temperature is 60                  degrees C, and the drying time is not less than                  12 hours.

PN KR2020126681-A; KR2285948-B1
TI Production of electrochemical catalyst for e.g. water electrolysis, involves fabricating titanium-based support e.g. carbon-doped titanium support, and depositing noble metal atom on support surface using atomic layer deposition equipment.
AU TAE K Y
   KIM J H
AE POSTECH ACAD-IND FOUND (UYPO-C)
GA 2020B1252K
AB    NOVELTY - Production of electrochemical catalyst involves fabricating a titanium-based (carbon, nitrogen, and oxygen-doped titanium) support, and depositing a noble metal atom on the surface of the carrier by performing 50 cycles or less using an atomic layer deposition equipment.
   USE - Production of electrochemical catalyst e.g. gold-doped titanium carbide, ruthenium-doped titanium dioxide, and iridium-doped titanium nitride catalyst used in carbon dioxide reduction, and water electrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of electrochemical catalyst having excellent stability of a single atom structure with small usage of the precious metals. The gold-doped titanium carbide catalyst increases carbon dioxide reduction reaction, and reduces the amount of precious metal by more than 95%. The ruthenium-doped titanium dioxide catalyst has excellent activity and chlorine generation efficiency of 96% or more. The iridium-doped titanium nitride catalyst has excellent oxygen generation reaction efficiency of 90%, and reduces the usage of the precious metals by 99%.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN MY179153-A
TI Producing precipitated calcium carbonate,            comprises dispersing calcium ion bearing source in            water, introducing solid elastomeric material to            solution, and feeding stream of carbon dioxide to            solution with stirring to carbonize calcium ions.
AU OTHMAN A B
   OTHMAN R B
   ISA N B
   SABRI S N B M
AE PUSAT PENYELIDIKAN MINERAL (PUSA-Non-standard)
GA 202293786E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC)                comprises: dispersing a calcium ion bearing source                in water to obtain a solution containing calcium                ions; introducing a solid elastomeric material to                the solution at an amount sufficient for complete                carbonization; feeding a continuous stream of                carbon dioxide at a controllable flow rate to the                solution with stirring to carbonize the calcium                ions so that crystalline particles of PCC are                formed; and obtaining the PCC particles through                isolating the PCC particles from the                solution.
   USE - The method is useful for producing PCC                (claimed) which is used as filler in paper-making                and production line e.g. manufacturing of glass,                rubber, detergent, and paint, and as drug carrier                in drug-making, polymer-making, coating, and                painting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces PCC shall which have                smaller particle size and improved morphology, and                prevent abnormal growth of PCC particles; is more                environmental friendly; and produces PCC which has                improved physical and chemical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ion containing                  solution is prepared by mixing an oxide or                  hydroxide of calcium with a disaccharide and                  water. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is                  200-6000 cm3/minute (0.2-6 l/minute). The                  solution is stirred at 500-1500 rpm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The elastomeric                  material is waste rubber. The amount of the                  elastomeric material sufficient for complete                  carbonization is 0-100 g/1000 cm3(1 l) of the ion                  containing solution.
   EXAMPLE - 36 g Carbide lime was mixed with 100 g sucrose                and 1000 cm3(1 l) water and filtered the mixture to                obtain a calcium ion containing solution. 20 g                Rubber band of a predetermined weight was added to                the solution, introduced carbon dioxide to the                solution at 200 cm3/minute (0.2 l/minute), filtered                the solution to obtain crude precipitated calcium                carbonate (PCC). PCC was cleaned with hot water to                obtain pure solid PCC.

TI Preparing paraffins involve introducing a feed            stream comprising hydrogen gas and a carbon-containing            gas selected from the group consisting of carbon            monoxide, carbon dioxide, and mixtures into a reaction            zone of a reactor.
AU KIRILIN A
   CHOJECKI A
   POLLEFEYT G
   NIESKENS D L S
   ANDREWS K C
   SANTOS CASTRO V P
   DEWILDE J F
   YANCEY D F

   MAREK A
   NIESKENS D L

AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 202059425K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing C2 to C5 paraffins involve                introducing a feed stream comprising hydrogen gas                and a carbon-containing gas selected from the group                consisting of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and                mixtures into a reaction zone of a reactor and                converting the feed stream into a product stream                comprising C2 to C5 paraffins in the reaction zone                in the presence of a hybrid catalyst, the hybrid                catalyst comprising a microporous catalyst                component and a metal oxide catalyst component                selected from the group consist of a bulk material                consisting of gallium oxide, gallium oxide present                on a titanium dioxide support material and a                mixture of gallium oxide and at least one promoter                present on a support material, the support material                comprising at least one oxide of a metal selected                from group 4 of the IUPAC periodic table of                elements.
   USE - Method for preparing C2 to C5 paraffins.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare C2 to C5                paraffins with good conversion of carbon and high                yield of desired products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred components: The metal oxide                  catalyst component is a bulk material consist of                  gallium oxide. The metal oxide catalyst component                  is gallium oxide present on a titanium dioxide                  support material. The metal oxide catalyst                  component is a mixture of gallium oxide and at                  least one promoter present on a support material,                  the support material comprising an oxide of a                  metal selected from group 4 of the IUPAC periodic                  table of elements. The support material is                  selected from the group consisting of titanium                  dioxide, zirconium dioxide, hafnium dioxide, and                  mixtures. The support material is titanium                  dioxide. The support material is monoclinic                  zirconium oxide. At least one promoter is a metal                  selected from group 10 of the IUPAC periodic                  table of elements. At least one promoter is a                  metal selected from the group consist of nickel,                  palladium, platinum, and mixtures. Preferred                  Conditions: The weight percent of the at least                  one promoter in the metal oxide catalyst                  component is from 0.01-10 wt.%. The weight                  percent of gallium in the metal oxide catalyst                  component is from 0.1-10 wt.%. The microporous                  catalyst component is a molecular sieve having                  8-MR pore openings.

AU GAREAU F

Preferred Components: The copper source comprises                  copper sulfate pentahydrate, copper nitrate                  trihydrate, and copper acetate monohydrate.                  Preferred Conditions: The hydrothermal reaction                  is carried out for 8-10 hours. The temperature of                  the ice water bath is 0-4 degrees C. The                  low-temperature liquid phase reduction reaction                  is carried out for 0.5-3 hours.

   Common synthesis gas production (10)
   Direct dimethyl ether synthesis (20)
   Hydrocarbonylation (40)
   Process for producing ethanol (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The dimethyl ether contained in the feed mixture is provided by direct dimethyl ether synthesis (20), where the dimethyl ether direct synthesis is subjected to further feed mixture containing at least carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The synthesis gas is provided by common synthesis gas production (10) and is used to form a first part for forming the feed mixture subjected to hydrocarbonylation and to a second part for forming the further feed mixture subjected to dimethyl ether direct synthesis. The common synthesis gas production comprises steam reforming, dry reforming, autothermal reforming, partial oxidation and/or electrolytic process, where carbon dioxide or water and carbon dioxide are reacted. The synthesis gas is provided by synthesis gas production with first hydrogen-carbon monoxide ratio, and the hydrogen-carbon monoxide ratio of at least the second part of the synthesis gas is changed before it is used as hydrocarbonylation feed mixture. The methanol from the crude product mixture from the hydrocarbonylation is subjected to dehydration to form dimethyl ether separately from the dimethyl ether direct synthesis, the dimethyl ether formed in the dehydration is used to form hydrocarbonylierung feed mixture. The crude product mixture provided by dimethyl ether direct synthesis is formed by distillation and stepwise condensation of at least a predominant proportion of the higher-boiling components or exclusively carbon monoxide and hydrogen-containing fraction.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE BEIJING BAOFENGCHUN PETROLEUM TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201987325L
AB    NOVELTY - Fracturing fluid comprises 0.55 %mass thickening agent, 1.5 %mass conditioning agent, 0.8 %mass crosslinker, 0.5 %mass discharge aiding agent, 0.5 %mass clay stabilizing agent, 0.1 %mass antiseptic agent, 0.1 %mass demulsifier and reminder being water.
   USE - The fracturing fluid is used for carbon dioxide fracturing construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The fracturing fluid system can be used under acidic conditions, avoids the damage of alkaline material to formation and is suitable for well temperature up to 160 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the fracturing fluid, involving (i) taking water in a mixer, start stirring, adding thickening agent, clay stabilizing agent, discharge aiding agent, demulsifier and antiseptic agent, adding conditioning agent after sticking, continuously stirring for 5 minutes, placing at 30 degrees C for 4 hours to stabilize the viscosity to obtain a base liquid, and (ii) taking the base liquid, adding temperature-controlled cross-linker under stirring, uniformly stirring and evaluating temperature and shear resistance by 6000 rupees high-temperature high-pressure rheometer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thickening agent comprises guar gum, hydroxypropyl guar gum and/or carboxymethyl hydroxypropyl guar gum. The discharge aiding agent comprises fluorocarbon surfactant, hydrocarbon surfactant and/or low molecular alcohol, preferably fluorocarbon surfactant. The low molecular alcohol comprises methanol or ethanol. The clay stabilizing agent comprises potassium chloride, ammonium chloride and/or a quaternary ammonium salt type clay stabilizer. The antiseptic agent comprises formaldehyde, glutaraldehyde and/or quaternary ammonium salt. The demulsifier comprises alkyl phosphate, alkoxy carboxylic acid, and/or polypropylene ether. Preferred Conditions: The thickening agent is carboxymethyl hydroxypropyl guanidine gum, which is an anionic and non-ionic double derivative of guar gum, which reacts with guar gum in tetrahydrofuran medium by double chemical modification, which is epoxy propane and sodium chloroacetate, and is prepared by introducing hydrophilic group carboxymethyl and hydroxypropyl in a guanidine gum molecular structure.

PN CN110205301-A
TI Cloning hybridoma cell, by adding Dulbecco's modified Eagle's complete culture medium, resuspending cell, pipetting cell suspension, placing dish under inverted microscope, pipetting single cell and culturing in carbon dioxide incubator.
AU CHEN Z
AE UNIV YANGZHOU (UYYZ-C)
GA 2019791784
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cloning hybridoma cell, involves (a) taking a sterile flat dish and adding a Dulbecco's modified Eagle's complete culture medium (DMEM), which is preheated at 37 degrees C, (b) gently blowing and resuspending the cell using a gun head and pipetting 5-10 mu l cell suspension into the dish, (c) gently shaking the dish to mix the cell, leaving still for 3 minutes, placing the dish under an inverted microscope and ensuring that only 1-2 visible cells are in the field of view, (d) finding a bright and round cell and pipetting a single cell using a pipette, (e) adding the single cell into 96 well plate paved with feeding layer cell, and (f) placing the single cell at 37 degrees C and culturing in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator.
   USE - The method is useful for cloning hybridoma cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves experiment material required for dilution process, does not require special equipment, and is simple in operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred device: The sterile flat dish is a circular plastic dish having a diameter of 6 cm, and has no adsorption to cells. The pipette in step (d) is a 10 mu l pipette. Preferred Cell: The cell in step (b) is a hybridoma cell in a positive well screened after cell fusion. Preferred Method: The step of ensuring that only 1-2 visible cells are in the field of view are obtained by diluting the cell suspension until one or two cells are visible in the field of view. The step (d) involves finding the bright and round cell in order to ensure that the selected cells are hybridoma cells with good states instead of feeding layer cells or dead cells.

PT P
PN CN109880085-A; CN109880085-B
TI New high nitrogen-enriched microporous organic polymer useful in industrial fields e.g. capturing carbon dioxide and volatile organic vapor, and recovering heavy metal and removing organic dye contaminants in water systems.
AU WANG Z
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201954943L
AB    NOVELTY - High nitrogen-enriched microporous organic polymer i.e. N,N',N''-tris-(amino-((1,3,5)triazin-2-ylamino)-methyl)-(1,3,5)triazine-2,4,6-triaminecompounds (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in industrial fields e.g. heterogeneous catalyst matrix materials, capturing carbon dioxide and volatile organic vapor, and recovering heavy metal and removing organic dye contaminants in water systems.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has large specific surface area. The method: is simple and easy to operate; utilizes cheap and easily available raw materials; and does not require catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High nitrogen-enriched microporous organic polymer i.e. N,N',N''-tris-(amino-((1,3,5)triazin-2-ylamino)-methyl)-(1,3,5)triazine-2,4,6-triaminecompounds of formula (I) is new.
   R1, R2 = H, amino group, or 1-10C alkyl group, alicyclic ring, heterocyclic ring or aromatic group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises taking melamine and formamide or its derivative of formula (II) in a ratio of 1:3-3:2, dissolving melamine and (II) in organic solvent A, polymerizing by heating to 150-250 degrees C for 1-6 days under nitrogen and/or air atmosphere, cooling to room temperature, extracting the resulting solid using organic solvent B for 2-48 hours, and drying at 30-150 degrees C under vacuum to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The mass ratio between melamine, (II) to organic solvent A is 1:100-30:100 (g/ml). The formamide or its derivative is in the form of mixture. The organic solvent A is N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide and/or diphenyl sulfone. The organic solvent B is fatty alcohol, fatty ketone, alkane, halogenated hydrocarbon, tetrahydrofuran, dioxane, N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide and/or N,N-dimethylacetamide.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN109776370-A
TI Extracting carotene from saffron comprises e.g. placing the saffron in clean water for cleaning, drying the washed saffron, disposing dried saffron in disinfection cabinet for disinfection, crushing sterilized saffron, and placing in kettle.
AU YUAN H
AE YUAN H (YUAN-Individual)
GA 201948275S
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting carotene from saffron comprises placing the saffron in clean water for cleaning, drying the washed saffron, disposing the dried saffron in a disinfection cabinet for disinfection, crushing the sterilized saffron into a powder having a particle diameter of 20-100 mu m, placing the crushed saffron into powder in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle; in an extraction environment with an analytical temperature of 20-60 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 6-12 MPa, introducing a carbon dioxide with flow rate of 40-60 l/hour, followed by extraction for 5-8 hours; allowing to stand the extracted material is at room temperature for 2-5 hours to take the upper layer; adsorbing the upper layer object, and analyzing by a resin, and finally placing the analyzed object in a drying box and drying to obtain the carotene extract.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting carotene from saffron (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can improves the extraction efficiency of carotene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The extraction temperature is 20-60 degrees C, and the analytical pressure is 6-12 MPa, carrying out the extraction for 5-8 hours after the carbon dioxide flow rate is 40-60 l/hour, including: the analysis temperature is 20 degrees C, and the analytical pressure is a 6 MPa extraction environment, introducing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 40 l/hour and extracting for 5 hours. In the extraction environment where the analysis temperature is 20-60 degrees C and the analytical pressure is 6-12 MPa, the carbon dioxide flowing at a flow rate of 40-60 l/hour, extracting for 5-8 hours including: at an analytical temperature of 60 degrees C under an extraction environment, and an analytical pressure of 12 MPa, introducing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 60 l/hour, and extracting for 8 hours. Preferred Components: The crushing of the sterilized saffron into powder having particle diameter is 20-100 mu m, and includes pulverizing the sterilized saffron into powder having a particle diameter of 20 mu m. The sterilizing cabinet is the UV-sterilizing cabinet.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)
MC A12-W11D; B11-B03C; B15-A00

PT P
PN CN109734637-A; CN109734637-B
TI Method of treating methionine crystal mother liquor, involves using methionine crystallization mother liquor as raw material, subjecting potassium bicarbonate filtrate to bipolar membrane electrodialysis treatment, and incinerating.
AU ZHOU R
   LIAO C
   SU Y
   PENG Q
AE LUFENG TIANBAO PHOSPHORUS CHEM CO LTD (LUFE-Non-standard)
   TIANBAO ANIMAL NUTRITION TECHNOLOGY CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201945176G
AB    NOVELTY - Method of treating methionine crystal mother liquor, involves (1) using methionine crystallization mother liquor produced during the production process of the methionine potassium salt process as a raw material, heating mother liquor of methionine and concentrating to obtain a mixture of potassium methionate and potassium carbonate, and introducing carbon dioxide gas, obtaining potassium bicarbonate solids containing methionine and potassium bicarbonate filtrate, respectively, and returning the solids to 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin hydrolysis process, (2) subjecting the potassium bicarbonate filtrate obtained in the step (1) to bipolar membrane electrodialysis treatment, and obtaining an aqueous solution containing the organic matter in the acid chamber, and concentrating and incinerating, diluting alkali chamber with potassium hydroxide solution, and returning to 5-( 2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin hydrolysis process.
   USE - Method of treating methionine crystal mother liquor.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide acidification and bipolar membrane electrodialysis are used to fully recover the methionine and potassium ions in the mother liquor, to avoid the loss of valuable compounds in the mother liquor and reduce production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The methionine crystallization mother liquid is a crystallization mother liquid obtained by separating carbon dioxide and neutralizing methionine in a potassium salt process. The mother liquor is composed of methionine, potassium hydrogen carbonate, potassium formate, potassium propionate, potassium citrate, potassium acetate, potassium butyrate, potassium carbonate, and methionine dipeptide. Other organic substances are polyvinyl alcohol, sorbitan laurate or hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose. The solid obtained is potassium hydrogen carbonate solid containing a small amount of methionine. The filtrate is one or more of aqueous solution of potassium hydrogen carbonate containing methionine, methionine dimer, potassium formate, potassium propionate, potassium acetate, potassium butyrate, potassium carbonate, potassium citrate, polyvinyl alcohol, sorbitan laurate, and hydroxypropyl methylcellulose. Preferred Composition: The raw material comprises 4.0-9.0 wt.% methionine, 4.0-11.0 wt.% potassium ion, 0.01-1.0 wt.% methionine dipeptide, and 0.01-0.5 wt.% other organic impurities. Preferred Property: The pH of the mother liquor is 7.5-9.0. The pressure of introducing carbon dioxide is 0.1-1.0 MPa. The pH is 6-7 and the potassium ion concentration is less than 0.10% by weight. The concentration of potassium hydroxide is 2.0-10.0% by weight. Preferred Process: The mother liquor of the methionine crystallization is concentrated to a potassium ion concentration of 20.0-30 wt.%, and a concentration temperature of 80-140 degrees C. The carbon dioxide is neutralized. An aqueous solution of an organic compound is obtained in the acid chamber of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis. The aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide is obtained in the alkali chamber. The material entering the bipolar membrane electrodialysis treatment is pretreated, that is, the potassium bicarbonate is decomposed into potassium carbonate by heating, and cooled and passed through a precision filter and into a bipolar membrane electrodialyzer. The material pretreatment and bipolar membrane electrodialysis operations are carried out in one or more cycles until the potassium ion concentration in the acid chamber in the bipolar membrane electrodialyzer is reduced to within 0.10% by weight. The potassium hydroxide in the alkali chamber is directly or after being concentrated and returned to the process of hydrolyzing 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin. Preferred Condition: The neutralization temperature is 0-10 degrees C. The bipolar membrane electrodialysis working temperature is 20-35 degrees C.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC A03-A04A; A10-E09B; A12-W11
IP C07C-319/20; C07C-319/28; C07C-323/58

PN CN109663512-A
TI Ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedral filled mixed matrix membrane for separating carbon dioxide/methane mixture, comprises polyether-polyamide block copolymer as matrix, and ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedron filled in matrix.
AU LI X
   DING S
   ZHANG H
   BAO S
AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 201940019X
AB    NOVELTY - An ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedral filled mixed matrix membrane comprises a polyether-polyamide block copolymer as a matrix, and an ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedron filled in the matrix, where the mass ratio of the polyether-polyamide block copolymer to the ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedron is 1:0.02-1:0.08, the ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedron with a core-shell structure, the ionic liquid is a core, and the hollow polyhedron is a shell.
   USE - Ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedral filled mixed matrix membrane for separating carbon dioxide/methane mixture (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedral filled mixed matrix membrane, which involves mixing an ionic liquid and a hollow polyhedron in a solvent, and drying to obtain an ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedron, adding the ionic liquid dope hollow polyhedron to the polyether-polyamide block copolymer solution, mixing and molding to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material of the shell is nickel-cobalt layered double hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is 1-hexyl-3-methylimidazolium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide salt.
