PN CN109336134-A; CN109336134-B
TI Processing carbonization useful for e.g. carbonizing ammonium hydrogencarbonate, comprises entering pre-carbonization liquid into carbonization tower, vaporizing, discharging, pre-carbonizing, compressing, cooling and recycling.
AU TANG Y
   FENG R
   GUO L
   LIU C
   WANG M
AE SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019188252
AB    NOVELTY - Processing carbonization comprises entering the pre-carbonization liquid from the pre-carbonization tower into the carbonization tower from the upper end, in reverse-flow contacting heat exchange with the liquid phase refrigerant entering the carbonization tower at the lower portion, and vaporizing the refrigerant by heat, taking away the heat generated by the carbonization reaction, discharging the carbonized suspension from the bottom of the carbonization tower to the solid-liquid separation section, sending the gas-phase refrigerant and the unreacted carbon dioxide into the pre-carbonization tower to wash and absorb the carbon dioxide in the mixed gas, and sending the remaining gas-phase refrigerant to the refrigeration compressor group, compressing and cooling to a liquid phase, and returning to the carbonization tower for recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for carbonizing ammonium hydrogencarbonate, baking soda and soda ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low investment structure, simple equipment structure, small temperature difference between refrigerant and heat transfer mother liquid and high heat exchange efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid solution ratio of the suspension discharged from the bottom of the carbonization tower is 10-80%. The CO2 concentration for carbonization is more than 90%. The refrigerant is a mixture of butene, n-butane, pentane, cyclopentane and isopentane or neopentane. The weight ratio of the liquid phase refrigerant to the carbonized liquid is 1:0.5-10. Preferred Method: The carbonization tower has an operating temperature of 10-60 degrees C and an operating pressure of 10-100 KPa.

PN CN108928844-A; CN108928844-B
TI Preparation of regular cubic calcium carbonate involves adding calcium carbonate suspension to reaction kettle and reacting, introducing carbon dioxide-enriched gas, adding calcium hydroxide emulsion and coating agent and stirring.
AU WU Q
   CHEN G
   FANG G
AE JIANDE HUAMING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A00309
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of regular cubic calcium carbonate involves adding less than or equal to 240 g/l calcium carbonate suspension to a reaction kettle and then controlling temperature, reacting at greater than or equal to 50 degrees C and then adjusting pH value to 6.4-10, introducing carbon dioxide-enriched gas into the reaction kettle, adding calcium hydroxide emulsion and then carbonizing, filtering the mixture and then carrying out surface treatment at 45-80 degrees C, adding 0.75-4% or 5-20% coating agent and then stirring at 70-95 degrees C and activating for 1-2 hours. The coating agent comprises fatty acid and alkali metal salt or water-soluble titanate coupling agent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of regular cubic calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy and economical preparation of regular cubic calcium carbonate with high yield and purity and excellent dispersibility and thixotropic property in environmentally-friendly manner. The regular cubic calcium carbonate does not contain additional additive.

The iron raw material is iron(II) chloride;
   (2) hydrogenation catalyst, which comprises copper and iron and has a triangular-pyramidal form. The catalyst has a specific surface area of 10 m2/g or more; and
   (3) manufacture of hydrogenation catalyst, which involves reducing the prepared catalyst precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The precursor comprises a trigonal form. The copper raw material is copper(II) nitrate and/or copper(I) chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of hydrothermally synthesizing the solution is carried out at a temperature of 150-200 degrees C for 30 minutes to 5 hours. Preparation of solution involves adding 5-10 pts. wt. raw material with respect to 100 pts. wt. solvent by injecting the raw material into a solvent, and further involves adding 6-11 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide with respect to 100 pts. wt. solution into the solvent. The catalyst precursor is reduced in a temperature range of 200-500 degrees C for 1-5 hours in a hydrogen gas atmosphere at a flow rate of hydrogen gas of 50-200 sccm. Preferred Composition: The reducing agent is propionaldehyde and/or ethylene glycol. The usage amount of reducing agent is 0-3 pts. wt. with respect to 100 pts. wt. solution. The catalyst comprises spherical copper particles on its surface. The catalyst is used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide for producing product comprising at least 59 wt.% hydrocarbons containing 5C or more with respect to 100 wt.% hydrocarbons in the product. Preferred Properties: The catalyst precursor has a particle diameter of 800 nm or less, a porosity of 0.12-0.17 cm3/g, a load ratio of copper with respect to iron of 594 or more, a distance of iron metal (110) lattice plane of at least 0.2 nm, an amount of change of carbon dioxide per iron per unit time of 1.7x 10-6 mol carbon dioxide/g iron/second or more, and a ratio of paraffins to olefins of at least 9.0. The catalyst has a three-way geometry and a specific surface area of at least 10 m2/g.

PN CN108467788-A
TI Supercritically extracting camphor essential oil comprises e.g. extracting camphor wood as raw material, peeling, smashing, sieving, and transferring sieved camphor powder into extraction vessel.
AU PENG W
   YU D
AE FOSHAN POLYTECHNIC (FSPL-C)
GA 2018700621
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritically extracting camphor essential oil comprises: (a) extracting camphor wood as raw material, peeling, smashing, and sieving through 40 mesh sieve, (b) transferring the sieved camphor powder into the extraction vessel of the supercritical extraction device, and separately heating the extraction kettle and separator t 28-40 degrees C, is introducing the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide, adjusting the flow rate to 10-30 kg/hour, and open the high pressure pump for boosting to 20-40 MPa, and cyclic extracting for 1-4 hours, (c) flowing the obtained volatile oil from step (b) into the separator with carbon dioxide, where the separation pressure is 6-10 MPa, and the separating at 25-35 degrees C. and (d) incubating the separated volatile oil obtained in the step (c) at 3-6 degrees C for overnight, and filtering to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritically extracting camphor essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, and easy to operate; has high extraction rate; and is safe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the extraction temperature is 30-35 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 28-35 MPa, and the extraction time is 1-2 hours. In the step (c), the separation pressure is 7-9 MPa, and the separation temperature is 28-32 degrees C. The volatile oil separated in the step (d) is at 3-5 degrees C for overnight, and filtering to obtain product.

PN CN108276808-A; CN108276808-B
TI Preparation method of reinforcing filler, involves mixing fly ash-based raw material with alkali metal hydroxide solution, pre-desiliconizing, solid-liquid separating, contacting liquid phase and carbon dioxide, and solid-liquid separating.
AU WANG X
   SUN Q
   WANG B
   LI H
AE SHENHUA GROUP CORP LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
   CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
GA 2018560818
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of reinforcing filler involves (1) mixing fly ash-based raw material with alkali metal hydroxide solution, performing pre-desiliconization treatment, and subjecting the pre-desiliconized product to solid-liquid separation to obtain a solid phase (I) and a liquid phase (I), (2) contacting the liquid phase (I) and carbon dioxide, and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain a solid phase (II) and a liquid phase (II), (3) mixing the solid phase (I) and water, subjecting the obtained slurry to flotation in a presence of a collector and foaming agent to obtain carbon black, and (4) drying the solid phase (II), and contacting with the carbon black in the presence of coupling agent.
   USE - Preparation method of reinforcing filler (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of reinforcing filler, and which effectively realizes the resource utilization of the fly ash and avoids the resource waste, by environmentally-friendly process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of the fly ash-based raw material to the alkali metal hydroxide is 100:(40-80). The concentration of the alkali metal hydroxide solution is 5-20 wt.%. In the step (3), the amount of water used in the slurry adjustment is 300-1200 mL with respect to 100 g of solid phase (I). In step (3), the amount of the collector is 100-1500 g and the amount of the foaming agent is 10-200 g relative to 1 ton slurry. In the step (4), the weight ratio of solid phase (II) and the carbon black is 100:(200-400). Preferred Components: The alkali metal hydroxide is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and/or lithium hydroxide. Preferred Properties: In the step (2), the volume flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 200-500 mL/minute. Preferred Conditions: In the step (1), the pre-desiliconization treatment is performed at 80-100 degrees C for 30-120 minutes. In step (2), the contact reaction is performed at 50-100 degrees C for 1-5 hours. In the step (4), the contact reaction is performed at 100-200 degrees C for 30-200 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The collector is diesel, kerosene, motor oil and/or fuel oil. The foaming agent is octanol, pine oil, fuel oil and/or GF oil. The coupling agent is silane coupling agent, aluminate coupling agent, titanate coupling agent, and/or zirconate coupling agent. The solvent is oil, ethanol, isopropanol, triethanolamine and/or glycidyl ether. Preferred Composition: In the step (4), the coupling agent is used in an amount of 0.2-5 wt.% based on 100 wt.% of the total amount of the solid phase (II) after drying and the carbon black. The coupling agent is provided in a form of a coupling agent solution having concentration of 0.5-3 wt.%.

PT P
PN IN201611044559-A; IN371485-B
TI New perovskite used for syngas generation, dry and tri-reforming of methane and lower hydrocarbons, steam reforming of hydrocarbons and lower alcohols, partial oxidation of hydrocarbons and oxidative dehydrogenation of alkanes.
AU CHILUKURI S V
   DAMA S
   GURAV H R
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
   COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 201853647V
AB    NOVELTY - A perovskite is new.
   USE - New perovskite used for syngas generation, dry and tri-reforming of methane and lower hydrocarbons, steam reforming of hydrocarbons and lower alcohols, partial oxidation of hydrocarbons and oxidative dehydrogenation of alkanes and alkyl aromatics, and used as catalyst for reforming biogas containing greater than or equal to 50 vol.% carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Perovskite is resistant to coke formation, has excellent performance in high methane and carbon dioxide conversions and long term stability, and helps stabilize high catalytic activity for very long hours of reaction time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A perovskite of formula A1-xA'xZr1-(y+z)MyM'zO3-delta (I) is new.
   A,A'=alkaline earth metals, such as Ca, Sr and Ba;
   x=0-0.5;
   My=Ni, Co and Fe;
   y=0-0.5;
   M'z=precious metal such as Pt, Rh, Ru and Pd;
   z=0-0.2; and
   delta =0-0.5.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing perovskite type oxides.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Perovskite is chosen from compound of formulae CaZr1-yNiyO3-delta, SrZr1-yNiyO3-delta, BaZr1-yNiyO3-delta, MgZr1-yNiyO3-delta, CaZr1-yRuyO3-delta, SrZr1-yRuyO3-delta, BaZr1-yRuyO3-delta, MgZr1-yRuyO3-delta, CaZr1-yRhyO3-delta, SrZr1-yRhyO3-delta, BaZr1-yRhyO3-delta, MgZr1-yRhyO3-delta, CaZr1-yPtyO3-delta, SrZr1-yPtyO3-delta, BaZr1-yPtyO3-delta, MgZr1-yPtyO3-delta, Ca1-xBaxZr1-yNiyO3-delta, Ca1-xSrxZr1-yNiyO3-delta or Ca1-xMgxZr1-yNiyO3-delta. Preparation: (Claimed). Perovskite type oxide is prepared by adding nitrates of metals in minimum amount of distilled water to citric acid solution under constant stirring at 353-360 K, evaporating and drying solution at 453-460 K for 12-14 hours to obtain fluffy amorphous citrate dry gel, and crushing fluffy material into powder and calcining at 1023-1030 K for 6-7 hours in air flow to get perovskite type oxides.
   Preferred Definitions:
   A,A'=Ca, Sr or Ba;
   x=0-0.5;
   My=Ni, Co or Fe;
   y=0-0.5;
   M'z=Pt, Rh, Ru or Pd;
   z=0-0.2; and
   delta =0-0.5.
   EXAMPLE - Perovskite was prepared by dissolving 1 mole nitrates of alkaline earth metal, 0.8 mole zirconium metal and 0.2 mole nickel in minimum amount of distilled water which were dropwise added to citric acid solution under constant stirring at 353-360 K. After complexation, evaporating and drying at 453-460 K for 12-14 hours to obtain fluffy amorphous citrate dry gel. Fluffy material was crushed into powder and calcined at 1023-1030 K for 6-7 hours in air flow to get perovskite type oxides.

(ii) inoculating microalgae strain into microalgae culture solution and carrying out small-scale cultivation in light incubator to obtain microalgae algae solution and carrying out microalgae cultivation using air carbon dioxide as carbon source at 45-50 degrees C; (iii) mixing microalgae algae solution and microalgae culture solution according to volume ratio of 1:20-1:30 and conveying from No. 1 liquid feeding tube to culture chamber and turning on inside of first LED lamp and second LED lamp of irradiation culture chamber to provide constant light intensity and maintaining illumination time at 14-16 hours/day and simultaneously introducing higher concentration flue gas carbon dioxide after desulfurization and denitrification treatment into culture chamber through first air supply hose with concentration of higher concentration flue gas carbon dioxide of 2.5-5%, which is higher, spraying concentration of flue gas carbon dioxide from first aerator and fully contacting with mixed liquid of microalgae algae liquid and microalgae culture liquid to carry out enrichment culture in culture chamber for 4-5 days to obtain higher concentration of microalgae algae liquid in logarithmic growth phase; (iv) transferring higher concentration microalgae liquid to mixing chamber through liquid transfer tube and allowing microalgae liquid solution to reach conversion chamber through third liquid supply tube and when liquid level sensor detects liquid level height reaches set value, turning off pump and introducing nitrogen-free microalgae culture solution from second liquid supply tube, turning on No. 1 LED lamp and No. 2 LED lamp of illumination conversion chamber to provide constant light intensity and maintaining illumination time at 14-16 hours/day and simultaneously through second air supply hose, introducing low concentration flue gas carbon dioxide after desulfurization and denitration treatment, and allowing low concentration flue gas carbon dioxide top to enter conversion chamber through gas passage, where low concentration flue gas carbon dioxide concentration is 0.5-2%, and spraying low concentration flue gas carbon dioxide from second aerator and fully contacting with microalgae liquid and nitrogen-free microalgae culture liquid to be in conversion chamber promoting microalgae to produce bio-oil, transforming more biomass and culturing for 7-10 days to obtain microalgae algae liquid and during conversion of microalgae into biomass in transformation chamber, continuously feeding mixed liquid solution of microalgae solution and microalgae culture solution into culture chamber through No. 1 liquid supply tube; (v) collecting microalgae liquid enriched with biological oil in transformation chamber through liquid collection tube, centrifuging and concentrating to obtain sedimentation of microalgae cell sediment; (vi) repeatedly freeze-thawing microalgae cell sediment for 3-4 times, drying, processing ultrasonically, and extracting biological fat oil in the crushed material using extracting agent; (vii) mixing biological oil extract with anhydrous methanol according to mass ratio of 4:0.5-2:1, adding acidic catalyst and performing reaction at 55-60 degrees C to obtain pre-esterified grease and continuing trans-esterifying pre-esterified oil to obtain crude biodiesel; and (viii) rectifying and purifying crude biodiesel to remove impurities to obtain higher concentration of biodiesel.

The first                detector is configured to measure the flow rate of                the supply gas flowing through the first flow path                to generate a first data signal. The second                detector is configured to measure the flow                rate.
   USE - Carbon compound manufacturing system                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system has saved plant construction costs,                it can be manufactured at a low cost, and it has                increased the utilization efficiency of the raw                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon compound manufacturing system comprises                a recovery unit configured to separate carbon                dioxide from a supply gas containing the carbon                dioxide, a conversion unit configured to convert                the carbon dioxide supplied from the recovery unit                into an intermediate compound, and a synthesis unit                configured to synthesize a carbon compound using                the intermediate compound supplied from the                conversion unit, a first flow path configured to                supply the supply gas to the recovery unit, a                second flow path connecting the recovery unit and                the conversion unit, a third flow path connecting                the conversion unit and the synthesis unit, at                least one detector selected from a first detector,                a second detector, and a third detector. The second                detector is configured to measure the flow rate of                the carbon dioxide flowing through the second flow                path to generate a second data signal, and the                third detector is configured to measure a value of                voltage or current supplied to the conversion unit                to generate a third data signal, and an integration                controller configured to collate measured data in                at least one data signal selected from the first to                third data signals with a plan data corresponding                to the measured data, and generate, according to a                collation result, at least one control signal                selected from a first control signal for adjusting                an operation condition of the recovery unit, a                second control signal for adjusting an operation                condition of the conversion unit, and a third                control signal for adjusting an operation condition                of the synthesis unit.

PN CN109173592-A
TI Device useful for capturing carbon dioxide in flue            gas by using ionic liquid, comprises dehydration unit,            multistage compressors, mixers, absorption tower, flash            tanks, heat exchanger, condensers, compressor, and            circulation pumps.
AU WANG Y
   LIU X
   LI G
   ZHU Z
   CUI P
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201908388S
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a dehydration unit (D1), a                multistage compressor (M-CP1), a multistage                compressor (M-CP2), a mixer (M1), a mixer (M2), an                absorption tower (T1), a flash tank (F1), a flash                tank (F2), a heat exchanger (H1), a condenser (C1),                a condenser (C2), a compressor (CP1), a circulation                pump (P1), and a circulation pump (P2). The                multistage compressor (M-CP1) is connected to the                inlet of the dehydration device (D1), and a gas                outlet is connected to the inlet of the absorption                tower via the mixer (M1) and the multistage                compressor (M-CP2). The circulation pump (P1) is                connected to the inlet of the absorption tower (T1)                via the mixer (M2), nitrogen gas at the top of the                absorption tower (T1) is evacuated, and the bottom                carbon dioxide-nitrogen-absorbent is connected to                the inlet of the flash tank (F1). The upper gas                outlet of the flash tank (F1) is connected to the                compressor (CP1), and then enters the mixer (M1)                through the condenser (C1).
   USE - The device useful for capturing carbon dioxide                in flue gas by using ionic liquid as                absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses an imidazole-type ionic                liquid as an absorbent with good absorption effect                on carbon dioxide, thus realizing the capture of                higher purity carbon dioxide and emission standards                from the flue gas; reduces the solvent loss, energy                consumption and corrosion; and saves equipment cost                when compared with the traditional chemical                absorption method of monoethanolamine; has no side                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A device comprises a dehydration unit (D1), a                multistage compressor (M-CP1), a multistage                compressor (M-CP2), a mixer (M1), a mixer (M2), an                absorption tower (T1), a flash tank (F1), a flash                tank (F2), a heat exchanger (H1), a condenser (C1),                a condenser (C2), a compressor (CP1), a circulation                pump (P1), and a circulation pump (P2). The                multistage compressor (M-CP1) is connected to the                inlet of the dehydration device (D1), and a gas                outlet is connected to the inlet of the absorption                tower via the mixer (M1) and the multistage                compressor (M-CP2). The circulation pump (P1) is                connected to the inlet of the absorption tower (T1)                via the mixer (M2), nitrogen gas at the top of the                absorption tower (T1) is evacuated, and the bottom                carbon dioxide-nitrogen-absorbent is connected to                the inlet of the flash tank (F1). The upper gas                outlet of the flash tank (F1) is connected to the                compressor (CP1), and then enters the mixer (M1)                through the condenser (C1), and the bottom carbon                dioxide-rich absorbent is connected to the inlet of                the flash tank (F2) via the heat exchanger (H1).                The top gas phase outlet of the flash tank (F2)                discharges carbon dioxide, and the bottom absorbent                enters the mixer (M2) through the circulation pump                (P2) and the condenser (C2). The method for                capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas by using ionic                liquid as absorbent involves: (1) introducing the                flue gas into the dehydration device (D1) via the                multistage compressor (M-CP1) and introducing the                dehydrated gas into the bottom of the absorption                tower (T1) via the mixer (M1) and the multistage                compressor (M-CP2); (2) transporting the ionic                liquid to the top of the absorption tower (T1) via                the circulation pump (P1), evacuating the overhead                gas and entering the bottom liquid carbon                dioxide-nitrogen-absorbent to the flash tank (F1);                (3) subjecting the flash tank (F1) to one-time                flash evaporation, discharging nitrogen and carbon                dioxide mixed gas from the top gas-phase outlet of                the tank through the compressor (CP1), connecting                the condenser (C1) with the inlet of the mixer                (M1), then conveying to the tower top of the                absorption tower (T1) through the multistage                compressor (M-CP2) and connecting the tank bottom                rich carbon dioxide absorbent with an inlet of a                flash tank (F2) through the heat exchanger (H1);                and (4) desorbing carbon dioxide through the flash                tank (F2), directly extracting the carbon dioxide                from the top of the tank, flowing out the ionic                liquid through the bottom of the tank, and                recycling the ionic liquid flows through the                circulation pump (P2), the condenser (C2), the                mixer (M2) and the dehydration device (D1) to the                absorption tower (T1). The dehydration device (D1)                has a pressure of 13-17 and a temperature of 15-25                degrees C. The absorption tower (T1) has a pressure                of 25-35 bar. The ionic liquid enters from the top                of the tower, and the gas enters from the bottom of                the tower. The feed temperature at the top of the                tower is 30-60 degrees C. The feed temperature at                the bottom of the tower is 30-60 degrees C. The                flash tank (F1) has an operating pressure of 3-5                bar and a temperature of 25-65 degrees C. The flash                tank (F2) has an operating pressure of 0.05-1.5 bar                and a temperature of 100-130 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the nitrogen-carbon                  dioxide-water mixture: the mass fraction of                  nitrogen is 78%; the mass fraction of carbon                  dioxide is 12.5%; and the mass fraction of water                  is 9.5%. The purity of carbon dioxide emitted                  from the top of the flash tank (F2) is higher                  than 90%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent is an                  imidazole-type ionic liquid in an amount of                  12500-14500 kg/hour.

PN CN212688115-U; CN113512620-A
TI Converter smelting full process end point carbon            dynamic control system of gas analysis and sublance,            has converter smelting process state and information            detecting device that is connected with converter base            automatic programmable logic controller device.
AU GAO Y
   GAO J
AE BEIJING KAIDE HENGYUAN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202130268R
AB    (CN212688115-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a converter smelting                full process end carbon dynamic control system of                gas analysis and sublance, belonging to the                converter smelting control technology; the system                is provided with a converter field gas detection                probe, collecting before each smelting; analyzing                the gas mol fraction for gas analysis in the air;                ensuring the accuracy of the gas analysis basic                data; constructing a converter smelting full                process gas analysis decarburization dynamic model;                constructing dynamic end point carbon time                prediction model. The constructed system and                dynamic mathematical model overcomes the defect of                the existing technology, which can realize                converter smelting full process end carbon dynamic                control, widely suitable for new, various converter                steelmaking system for expansion or                reconstruction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing dynamic control of end point carbon                in converter smelting by gas analysis and sublance                involves arranging converter field gas detection                probe, and using the mole fraction of gas for gas                analysis in the air is collected and analyzed                before each smelting, so that the accuracy of basic                data of gas analysis is ensured. A gas analysis                decarburization dynamic model is constructed in the                whole process of converter smelting, and the mole                fraction of carbon dioxide in air entering is                determined from a furnace mouth by adopting the                helium mole fraction obtained by analyzing flue gas                detection gas. The mole fraction of carbon dioxide                contained in the air entering is subtracted from                the furnace mouth from the mole fraction of carbon                dioxide in the flue gas obtained by analyzing the                flue gas detection gas, thus obtaining the total                amount of the mole fraction of carbon dioxide                generated in the smelting process and the mole                fraction of carbon dioxide generated in the                combustion of the air entering from the furnace                mouth. The total mole fraction of carbon dioxide                and the mole fraction of carbon monoxide are added                in the flue gas obtained, then analyzing the flue                gas detection gas, then multiplying the total                amount of the flue gas obtained by the total amount                of the carbon monoxide and the carbon dioxide                generated in the smelting process. The method                further involves calculating the carbon amount                removed in the oxygen blowing smelting process                according to the total amount, then subtracting the                carbon removed this time from the previous residual                carbon in the molten steel to obtain the residual                carbon in the molten steel this time. Thr process                is the calculation method of the gas analysis                decarburization dynamic model in the whole process                of the converter smelting. The carbon content in                the molten steel measured by the sublance is used                for replacing the residual carbon content in the                molten steel calculated by the dynamic model, so                that the calculation error of the carbon content in                the molten steel is fundamentally corrected. A                dynamic endpoint carbon time prediction model is                constructed by obtaining three groups of data after                iterative calculation of a gas analysis                decarburization dynamic model in the whole process                of three times of converter smelting, then                constructing a unitary quadratic equation model for                expressing the dynamic decarburization process by                using the three groups of data, replacing a first                group of data in the previous three groups of data                with a new group of data acquired next time,                constructing a new unitary quadratic equation model                for expressing the dynamic decarburization process,                and forming a dynamic curve of the decarburization                process and a moment used for forecasting endpoint                carbon by using the dynamic equation model                continuously updated along with the smelting                process. The gas analysis decarburization dynamic                model in the whole process of converter smelting                using an equation Cpi=(Ci-1-(Pgc2i-Pac2X                Pghi/Pah+Pgc1i)X QgiX(12/22.4))/G, where Cpi is the                carbon content in the molten steel of the ith                calculation result is percent; Pgc2i is the mole                fraction of carbon dioxide in the flue gas of the                ith gas analysis is percent; Pac2 is the mole                fraction of carbon dioxide in air, stored in gas                analysis computer database; Pghi is the helium mole                fraction in the flue gas of the ith gas analysis;                Qgi is total flow of flue gas; Pah is mole fraction                of helium in air, constant, is stored in a gas                analysis computer database; Pgc11 is the mole                fraction of carbon monoxide in the flue gas of the                ith gas analysis is percent; g is weight of molten                steel, kg; Ci-1 is used for calculating the carbon                content in the molten steel in kg in the previous                time; the value at the time of initial calculation                is calculated by equation Ci-1=Ce+Cs+Cm, Ce is the                carbon content and constant in the molten iron are                stored in a gas analysis computer database, kg; Cs                is the carbon content and the constant in the scrap                steel are stored in a gas analysis computer                database, and are kg; Cm is the carbon amount and                constant in the pig iron block are stored in a gas                analysis computer database, kg; a dynamic endpoint                carbon time prediction model of equation                Cf=k(At2+Bt+C), Cf is end point carbon content in                the molten steel; t is end point carbon prediction                time (second); a, B, C is a constant; and k is                engineering coefficient of 0.5-1.5, and in equation                in gas analysis decarburization dynamic model                residual carbon in the molten steel is dynamically                calculated in the whole smelting process.. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for                performing dynamic control of end point carbon in                converter smelting by gas analysis and sublance,                comprises computer workstation 1 of the converter                main control room, which is a human-computer                interaction interface of the converter smelting                whole-process end point carbon dynamic control                system based on gas analysis, and is a digital                device composed on the basis of the computer                workstation, and is provided with the setting and                modification functions of the converter smelting                whole-process end point carbon dynamic control                parameters of gas analysis and a dynamic curve                display picture, and is connected with the gas                analysis computer 2, to obtain the gas analysis                data and information in real time, and is connected                with the converter basic automation programmable                logic controller device 8 to obtain the                interlocking information of production and                operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of system                for performing dynamic control of end point carbon                in converter smelting by gas analysis and sublance                (The drawing include non-English language                text).
   Computer workstation (1)
   Gas analysis computer (2)
   Gas analysis computer (3)
   Gas detection device (4)
   Programmable logic controller device                (8)
EA    NOVELTY - Performing dynamic control of end point carbon                in converter smelting by gas analysis and sublance                involves arranging converter field gas detection                probe, and using the mole fraction of gas for gas                analysis in the air is collected and analyzed                before each smelting, so that the accuracy of basic                data of gas analysis is ensured. A gas analysis                decarburization dynamic model is constructed in the                whole process of converter smelting, and the mole                fraction of carbon dioxide in air entering is                determined from a furnace mouth by adopting the                helium mole fraction obtained by analyzing flue gas                detection gas.
   USE - Method for dynamically controlling end point                carbon in a converter smelting full process of gas                analysis and sub-lance of a converter steelmaking                heating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing all kinds of                scale, different working condition of converter                steelmaking full automatic control. The method                ensures the accuracy of the gas analysis basic                data. The carbon content in the molten steel                measured by the sublance is replaced to replace the                residual carbon content. The calculation error of                the carbon content is fundamentally corrected and                is used in various converter steel making systems                for building, expanding or improving.

PN EP3985093-A1
TI Transfer system for biological samples, comprises rapid transfer port (RTP) container and filter, where filter is configured to protect biological samples from external and cross contamination and allow gas exchange through RTP container, and gyroscopic flask-holder is housed inside RTP container.
AU SEVERINA F
   ZANINI C
   BONIFACIO M
AE BIOAIR SPA (BIOA-Non-standard)
GA 2022528213
AB    NOVELTY - A transfer system (1) for biological samples (2) comprises a rapid transfer port (RTP) container (3) defining a container opening (11) which is closable by a container lid (12), a high efficiency particulate air or ultra-low particulate air filter, positioned at the container lid and an RTP door associated with a door flange of a grade A isolator. The filter is configured to (a) protect the biological samples from external contamination during the transfer of the RTP container, (b) protect the biological samples from cross contamination when the RTP container is inserted into standard external carbon dioxide incubators, and (c) allow a gas exchange through the RTP container inserted into standard external carbon dioxide incubators. The door flange defines a door opening which is closable by the RTP door, the geometrically connected container lid and RTP door are jointly separable from the door flange, and a gyroscopic flask-holder (4) is housed inside the RTP container.
   USE - The transfer system is used for biological samples, preferably transferring biological samples from a grade A isolator to standard external carbon dioxide incubators.
   ADVANTAGE - The transfer system is economical and eliminates the risk of contamination during the transfer from the Grade A isolator to the standard external carbon dioxide incubators, eliminates the risk of cross contamination between biological samples from different patients, and allows the growth of the cells of the biological samples inside standard carbon dioxide incubators outside the Grade A isolator, without breaking the isolation continuity of the biological samples, preserves the biological samples, and avoiding the risk of overturning the biological samples. The system provides ventilation of the interior of the system more efficiently. The container lid ensures excellent gas permeability. The transfer system can be simply placed inside the standard external carbon dioxide incubators, without including connection systems between the standard external carbon dioxide incubators and the transfer system and it is possible to transfer more samples inside a single standard external carbon dioxide incubator, even containing biological samples from different patients, and ensures increasing the number of samples which are insertable into the same incubator. The seals ensure tightness between the door flange and the container lid and the RTP door, and reduces the contamination risk. The gyroscopic flask-holder keeps the orientation of the biological samples in stable manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A transfer system (1) for biological samples (2) comprises an rapid transfer port (RTP) container (3) defining a container opening (11) which is closable by a container lid (12), a high efficiency particulate air or ultra-low particulate air filter, positioned at the container lid, where the filter is configured to (a) protect the biological samples from external contamination during the transfer of the RTP container from a grade A isolator to standard external carbon dioxide incubators, (b) protect the biological samples from cross contamination when the RTP container is inserted into standard external carbon dioxide incubators, and (c) allow a gas exchange through the RTP container inserted into standard external carbon dioxide incubators, an RTP door associated with a door flange of a grade A isolator, where the door flange defines a door opening which is closable by the RTP door, the container lid and the RTP door are configured to geometrically connect to each other, and the geometrically connected container lid and RTP door are jointly separable from the door flange, and a gyroscopic flask-holder (4) is housed inside the RTP container. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a transfer assembly for biological samples; and
   (2) a method of transferring biological samples.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a transfer system for biological samples.
   Transfer system (1)
   Biological samples (2)
   RTP container (3)
   Gyroscopic flask-holder (4)

PN KR2313745-B1
TI Preparing decaffeinated coffee beans involves soaking coffee beans in water, filtering water and coffee beans with activated carbon filter, separating filtered water, and transferring coffee beans into carbon dioxide injection chamber.
AU LEE J K
   OH D J
AE RUDDO CO LTD (RUDD-Non-standard)
GA 2021C1196T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing decaffeinated coffee beans involves immersing coffee beans in water at 60-80 degrees C, filtering coffee beans using activated carbon filter, separating filtered water, transferring separated water to carbon dioxide injection chamber, adding water to coffee beans and injecting carbon dioxide, cooling mixture to 15 degrees C or less, aging mixture for 24 hours, and drying decaffeinated coffee beans.
   USE - Method for preparing decaffeinated coffee beans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to have economical operation, does not require expensive equipment such as supercritical carbon extraction, and maintains taste and aroma of coffee beans.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The water added is 0.5-1.5 times volume of green beans. The aging is performed at 30-50 degrees C. Preferred Process: The dried decaffeinated beans are roasted, and freeze-dried extract of roasted decaffeinated beans. The roasted decaffeinated beans are crushed into bean powder, added water at 80-100 degrees C to coffee bean powder only enough to wet coffee bean powder, steaming for 30 seconds-1 minute, added water at 80-100 degrees C to steamed bean powder again and extracted coffee within 30 seconds-1 minute at pressure of 10-12 bar, and cooled and dried extracted coffee to -5-4 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Bioreactor used to convert organic residues into            organic nutrient solution used as absorbent for storing            e.g. carbon oxide comprises reaction vessel having feed            line, ventilation device by which oxygen can be            introduced and carrier element.

AE JASSEN-KUNST ZENT GMBH APP ZUSCHNITTE (JASS-Non-standard)

GA 2020C5682F
AB    NOVELTY - Bioreactor (1) comprises a reaction vessel (5)                having a feed line (6) through which a suspension                (4) can be introduced into the reaction vessel, and                reaction vessel has a discharge line through which                suspension leaves the reaction vessel; a                ventilation device by means of which oxygen can be                introduced into reaction vessel and suspension; and                at least one carrier element in a receiving space                of reaction vessel with a settlement surface for                formation of a biofilm (12) from microorganisms,                where carrier element with the introduced                suspension and the introduced oxygen can be flushed                around and/or flushed through, and that a                surface-to-volume ratio of the carrier element is                greater than a surface-to-volume ratio of receiving                space. The ventilation device has a hydrogen                peroxide reservoir and catalyst, by which hydrogen                peroxide flowing out of hydrogen peroxide reservoir                during an aeration process can be converted into                water and oxygen in a catalytic reaction.
   USE - The bioreactor is useful for converting                organic residues and/or waste materials into                organic nutrient solution used as absorbent for                storing carbon oxide, nitrogen oxide and/or sulfur                oxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bioreactor: efficiently separates the                carbon dioxide gas resulting from the process of                anaerobic fermentation of organic residues and                waste and/or from the combustion of biogas; and                washes carbon dioxide out of the gas stream and                absorbs carbon dioxide in a stable manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing an organic nutrient solution                with a proportion of at least 10% plant-available,                mineralized nitrogen based on the total nitrogen                content of the organic nutrient solution,                preferably a nitrate portion of the                plant-available, mineralized nitrogen is higher                than an ammonium portion, comprising inoculating                carrier element with an inoculum containing                ammonifying and/or nitrifying bacteria, forming a                biofilm with ammonifying and/or nitrifying bacteria                on the support element, incubating an organic                residue and/or waste material with the biofilm, in                a reaction vessel, where the ammonifying and/or                nitrifying bacteria is organically bound nitrogen                residual and/or convert waste material into                mineralized nitrogen. The oxygen is introduced into                the reaction vessel and/or into the carrier element                by a ventilation device during the implementation                of individual or all steps. The organic nutrient                solution produced by the previous steps with a                carbon dioxide-containing gas, preferably a biogas                and/or an exhaust gas from a combined heat and                power plant, is treated such that gaseous carbon                dioxide is bound by the organic nutrient solution,                preferably the carbon dioxide storage step is                carried out in an externally sealed carbon dioxide                storage space;
   (2) organic nutrient solution, preferably                organic plant fertilizer, produced by the above                method, comprising at least 10%, preferably at                least 25%, preferably at least 75% plant-available                mineralized nitrogen based on the total nitrogen                content of the organic nutrient solution,                preferably with a nitrate portion of the                plant-available, mineralized nitrogen which is                higher than an ammonium portion, preferably with an                nitrate: ammonium ratio of 2: 1, preferably at                least 3:1, preferably 50:1;
   (3) use of an organic nutrient solution,                preferably an organic nutrient solution produced in                the bioreactor, as an absorbent for storing carbon                oxide (COx), nitrogen oxide (NOx) and/or sulfur                oxide (SOx), where the organic nutrient solution is                treated with COx-, NOx- and/or SOx-containing gas,                preferably in an externally closed and/or sealed                room, where gaseous COx and/or NOx and/or SOx are                bound through the organic nutrient solution;                and
   (4) nutrient generation and carbon dioxide                storage system comprising a fermentation device,                preferably a biogas plant, for generating an                organic residual and/or waste material, optionally                a combined heat and power unit for burning one                generated by the fermentation device, a bioreactor                for converting organic residual and/or waste                materials into an organic nutrient solution,                preferably into a nutrient solution, and a carbon                dioxide storage device with a carbon dioxide                storage space, preferably an externally sealable                carbon dioxide storage space, in which at least one                scrubber device is arranged which is used to carry                out a liquid-gas reaction between a carbon                dioxide-containing gas, preferably a biogas and/or                exhaust gas from a block-type thermal power station                and the organic nutrient solution produced by the                bioreactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of the                bioreactor.
   Bioreactor (1)
   Suspension (4)
   Reaction vessel (5)
   Feed line (6)
   Biofilm (12)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least one catalyst                  is arranged between the hydrogen peroxide                  reservoir and an outlet opening of the                  ventilation device. The catalyst is manganese                  dioxide and/or manganese oxide, preferably                  manganese dioxide and/or manganite. The organic                  nutrient solution has an ammonium content of at                  least 10% of the total nitrogen content of the                  organic nutrient solution and/or a potassium                  content of at least 10% of the total solids                  content of the organic nutrient solution and/or a                  calcium content of at least 5% of the total                  solids content of the organic nutrient solution                  and/or a magnesium content of at least 1% of the                  total solids content of the organic nutrient                  solution and/or a protein content of at least 15%                  total solids content of the organic nutrient                  solution, preferably where a pH value of the                  organic nutrient solution is not greater than                  7.5, preferably not greater than 6. Preferred                  Method: In the carbon dioxide storage step, the                  surface area of the organic nutrient solution is                  increased, preferably by passing the organic                  nutrient solution over a scrubber device,                  preferably the scrubber device being a device or                  a combination of two or more devices including                  bubble column reactor, tubular reactor, jet                  nozzle reactor, stirred tank, thin-film reactor                  and/or spray tower. The carbon dioxide storage                  space is divided into at least two chambers, a                  washing device is arranged in a first chamber and                  the second chamber being designed as a collecting                  chamber for the organic nutrient solution, and                  that the organic nutrient solution is transferred                  from the first chamber to the second chamber                  and/or by at least one pumping device or can be                  pumped from the second chamber into the first                  chamber.

PN WO2019156442-A1; KR2019096808-A; NL2020407-B1; US2020398219-A1; JP2021513353-W; KR2269393-B1
TI Bioreactor used to e.g. convert gaseous carbon dioxide and liquid culture medium to organic acids, comprises e.g. cavity, outer wall, bottom, top, and pipe connected to first liquid outlet located at bottom for discharging liquid.
AU SHIN H Y
   YONG S H
AE SHIN H Y (SHIN-Individual)
   INWOO CORP (INWO-Non-standard)
   KONG S W (KONG-Individual)
   SHIN H Y (SHIN-Individual)
   INWOO CORP (INWO-Non-standard)
   SHIN H Y (SHIN-Individual)
GA 201970822D
AB    NOVELTY - Bioreactor (1) comprises: a cavity (2a); an outer wall (2b); a bottom (2c); a top (2d); a pipe connected to a first liquid outlet located at the bottom of the bioreactor for discharging liquid; a pipe connected to a second liquid outlet located at the bottom of the bioreactor and to the inlet of a first pump; a pipe connected to an outlet of the first pump and to a first liquid inlet located at the top of the bioreactor for recycling liquid culture medium over the at least one plate; a pipe connected to a first gas inlet for providing carbon dioxide-containing gas to the bioreactor; a pipe connected to a first gas outlet for discharging gas from the bioreactor; and a pipe connected to a second liquid inlet for supplying fresh liquid culture medium to the bioreactor. The cavity comprises at least one plate having at least one perforation, where the plate is positioned perpendicularly to the outer wall.
   USE - The bioreactor is useful for: anaerobic conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide and a liquid culture medium to organic acids; and biogas production facility (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bioreactor converts larger fraction of the organic material originally present in the digester to methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) biogas production facility comprising (i) a digester for the production of carbon dioxide-containing biogas from organic material, and (ii) the bioreactor, where the digester comprising a gas outlet connected to pipe of the at least one bioreactor for supplying carbon dioxide-containing biogas to the bioreactor and a liquid inlet connected to pipe of the bioreactor for supplying organic acid-containing liquid medium to the digester via a second pump;
   (2) anaerobic (M1) conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide and liquid culture medium to organic acids, comprising (a) providing the bioreactor, (b) adding anaerobic organic acid-producing microorganisms to the upper surface of the plate or to the upper surface of the most upper plate of the bioreactor, (c) adding fresh liquid culture medium via pipe and second liquid inlet and carbon dioxide-containing gas via pipe and first gas inlet to the bioreactor, (d) circulating liquid culture medium over the plates by collecting the liquid carbohydrate medium at second liquid outlet located at the bottom of the bioreactor and supplying it to the first liquid inlet located at the top of the bioreactor via pipe, first pump, and pipe, to obtain an organic acid-containing liquid medium and a gas depleted in carbon dioxide, (e) discharging the organic acid-containing liquid medium obtained in step (d) via the first liquid outlet and pipe, and (f) discharging the gas depleted in carbon dioxide obtained in step (d) via the first gas outlet and pipe; and
   (3) anaerobic (M2) conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide and liquid culture medium to organic acids, comprising (a1) providing the biogas production facility according, (b1) anaerobically digesting organic material in digester, resulting in carbon dioxide-containing biogas, (c1) adding anaerobic organic acid-producing microorganisms to the upper surface of the plate or to at least the upper surface of the most upper plate of each bioreactor, (d1) adding fresh liquid culture medium via pipe and second liquid inlet to each bioreactor and adding the carbon dioxide-containing biogas of step (b1) from the digester via pipe and first gas inlet to each bioreactor, (e1) circulating liquid culture medium over the one or more plates by collecting the liquid culture medium at liquid outlet located at the bottom of each bioreactor and supplying it to the first liquid inlet located at the top of each bioreactor via pipe, pump and pipe, to obtain an organic acid-containing liquid medium and a gas enriched in methane, (f1) discharging the organic acid-containing liquid medium obtained in step (e1) via the first liquid outlet, pipe and second pump, and liquid inlet to the digester, and (g1) discharging the gas enriched in methane obtained in step (e1) via the first gas outlet and pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the bioreactor.
   Bioreactor (1)
   Cavity (2a)
   Outer wall (2b)
   Bottom (2c)
   Top (2d)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The plate comprises on its upper surface anaerobic organic acid-producing microorganisms. The anaerobic organic acid-producing microorganisms comprise: organic acid-producing microorganisms including e.g. Acetobacter, Gluconoacetobacter, Acidomonas, Gluconobacter, Sporomusa ovata, Clostridium ljungdahlii, Clostridium aceticum, Moorella thermoacetica, and Acetobacterium woodii; succinic acid-producing microorganisms including e.g. Actinobacillus succinogenes, Anaerobiospirillum succiniciproducens, and Corynebacterium glutamicum. The fresh liquid culture medium comprises a carbon source including glycerol and/or starch, maize steep liquor as a nitrogen source, and optionally salts. The gas enriched in methane contains at least 90 mole% methane, preferably at least 98 mole% methane. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide-containing gas in step (c) is biogas, off-gas from a natural gas power plant, off-gas resulting from crude oil extraction, carbon dioxide-containing gas from waste-water treatment, and/or carbon dioxide-containing gas from bio-ethanol production, preferably the carbon dioxide-containing gas in step (c) is biogas, and the gas depleted in carbon dioxide is biogas enriched in methane. In step (d1) also at least one carbon dioxide-containing gases including off-gas from a natural gas power plant, off-gas resulting from crude oil extraction, carbon dioxide-containing gas from waste-water treatment, and carbon dioxide-containing gas from bio-ethanol production are added via pipe and first gas inlet.

GA 2022E0451N
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical                field of angiography, especially relates to a                angiography system, comprising an injector, a                three-way valve, a radiography catheter and a gas                storage bottle stored with liquid carbon dioxide;                the three-way valve is provided with a first port                capable of switching the connection state, a second                port and a third port, the first port is connected                with the air outlet of the gas storage bottle, the                second port is connected with the injection port of                the injector, the third port is connected with the                angiography catheter, the connection state of the                three-way valve is switched so that the first port                is connected with the second port, the liquid                carbon dioxide in the gas storage bottle is                gasified and then filled into the injector, a                heating device sleeved on the outer side of the                cylinder of the injector for heating the carbon                dioxide gas entering the injector, then the second                port and the third port are connected, the carbon                dioxide gas after heating is injected into the                target blood vessel, therefore, the angiography                system of the utility model, the temperature of the                carbon dioxide gas injected into the patient is                suitable, avoiding the discomfort of the                patient.

PN CN115186314-A
TI Predicting solid carbon content of storage layer            with improved wedging, involves providing chemical            mechanical coupling mathematical model in carbon            dioxide mineralization sealing process.
AU GAO H
   LI S
   LUO X
   CAO S
   JIANG Q
   ZHENG H
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 2022D1260Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing a                three-dimensional geometric model of a reservoir                representative unit mineralization capture. A                chemical mechanical coupling mathematical model is                established in carbon dioxide mineralization                sealing process. Loss volume of a spherical is cut                in a certain main stress direction based on time. A                rate of change is equal to pressure dissolving                reaction volume dissolution rate of mineral                particles in the stress direction. A mineral                reaction area is updated along the three main                stress directions according to the loss volume of                the spherical along the main stress. The mineral                pressure is determined to start or not. A final                carbon dioxide molar amount is obtained.
   USE - The method for predicting solid carbon content                of a storage layer with improved wedging in a                carbon dioxide geology sequestrate field using                pressure of wedging.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a feasible evaluation                method for improving the storage layer mineralizing                solid carbon quantity, which has a great                application prospect in the carbon sequestrate                field. The method for predicting solid carbon                content of the reservoir by using the wedging                establishing a three-dimensional geometric model of                the mineralization and capture of reservoir                representative unit, providing a chemical                mechanical coupling mathematical model in the                carbon dioxide mineralization sealing process,                based on the stratum physical property parameter,                stress parameter, thermal chemical parameter                calculating solid carbon amount of unit volume                reservoir rock mass wedging the pressure                driving.

PN WO2022167714-A1
TI Manufacturing elevator element for elevator, by            filling mold with mixture comprising material            comprising compound comprising silicon and specific            divalent metal, and water, adding carbon dioxide in            mixture in mold and allowing water and carbon dioxide            to react with material under overpressure.
AU TALONEN T
AE KONE CORP (KONL-C)
GA 2022A0894J
AB    NOVELTY - Elevator element manufacturing method                comprises providing a material comprising a                compound comprising silicon and a second divalent                metal in the powder and/or granulate form, filling                a mold with a mixture comprising the material and                water, adding carbon dioxide in the mixture in the                mold, and allowing water and carbon dioxide to                react with the material under overpressure, thus                creating bonding structures based on metal                carbonates. The reaction of water, carbon dioxide                and the material powder/granulate takes place at a                room temperature. The overpressure is 1-2 bar. The                elevator element is a filler-bit of a                counterweight.
   USE - The method is used for manufacturing an                elevator element, which is used in an elevator                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of effectively and                economically manufacturing the lift element with                high strength, reduced environmental load and                reduced energy consumption, thus reducing the                weight of the elevator element without increasing                the volume of the element and achieving a compact                counterweight or balance weight and promotes the                tendency of branching of the polymer at the bonding                structure, thus achieving an even stronger                structure of the component and achieving an                energy-saving process, and reduces the                manufacturing cost of the elevator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the elevator element; and
   (2) an elevator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The second divalent                  metal is selected from calcium, copper, iron,                  nickel, cobalt, manganese, magnesium, silicon,                  zinc, palladium, cadmium, tin, platinum and                  lead.

PN CN114317053-A
TI Co-producing liquefied natural gas by using coke oven gas and carbon dioxide for producing low carbon methanol, comprises performing temperature swing adsorption, desulfurization and decarburization to coke oven gas and processing first gas.
AU ZHANG Y
   YUN Z
   XIAO Y
   LIU X
AE BEIJING BEIKE OUYUAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022562729
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing liquefied natural gas by using coke oven gas and carbon dioxide, comprises using a variable temperature adsorption device, a desulfurization device and a decarbonization device to perform temperature swing adsorption, desulfurization and decarburization to coke oven gas to obtain first gas, conveying first gas to a cryogenic separation device, processing first gas by cryogenic separation device to obtain liquefied natural gas and second gas, using trapping device to capture industrial tail gas to obtain carbon dioxide gas, conveying captured carbon dioxide gas and second gas to synthesis gas compression device to obtain third gas, and conveying the third gas to green low-carbon methanol synthesis unit to obtain green low-carbon methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for producing low carbon methanol and manufacturing coke oven gas and industrial tail gas into green low carbon methanol and liquefied natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need pure oxygen gas and matched air separation device for preparing synthesis gas to provide carbon source, meets the conventional requirement of synthesizing green low carbon methanol by carbon monoxide and hydrogen, needs to recycle carbon dioxide from the tail gas to realize the production of green low-carbon methanol, and has simple process. The device has good total economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for producing green low carbon methanol co-production liquefied natural gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The decarburization device adopts methyldiethanolamine method for decarburization. When the decarbonization device obtains the first gas, the carbon dioxide gas is generated simultaneously, conveying the carbon dioxide generated by decarbonization device to the synthesis gas compression device, the carbon dioxide generated by the decarburization device, the carbon dioxide captured by trapping device, and second gas obtained by the synthesis gas compression device to obtain third gas. The coke oven gas is conveyed to the temperature swing adsorption device. The method further comprises using the gas cabinet to collect and store the coke oven gas, conveying the stored coke oven gas to first pressurizing device, conveying the coke oven gas pressurized by first pressurizing device to temperature swing adsorption device, using second pressurizing device to pressurize the coke oven gas adsorbed by variable temperature adsorption device, and conveying the coke oven gas pressurized by second pressurizing device to desulfurization device. The green low-carbon methanol synthesis unit comprises conveying the third gas to the green low-carbon methanol synthesis reactor, obtaining crude green low-carbon methanol by green low-carbon methanol synthesis reactor, conveying the crude green low-carbon methanol to first rectifying tower, and rectifying rough green low-carbon methanol by first rectifying tower to obtain green low-carbon methanol. The crude green low carbon methanol before conveying to the first rectifying tower further comprises separating the gas and liquid from the crude green low-carbon methanol by first phase separating device, separating the circulating gas, fourth gas and liquid phase rough green low-carbon methanol, discharging fourth gas separated by first phase separating device by purge gas recycling device, pressurizing circulating compressor to the circulating gas and purge gas, and conveying to the green low-carbon methanol synthesis reactor. The crude green low carbon methanol before conveying to the first rectifying tower further comprises a coke oven gas pre-processing module, a synthesis gas module and a green low-carbon methanol module, conveying the liquid phase rough green low-carbon methanol separated by first phase separating device to the second phase separating device for separating again, conveying the liquid phase rough green low-carbon methanol separated by second phase separating device to second rectifying tower, and orderly passing the liquid phase rough green low carbon methanol through second rectifying tower and first rectifying tower to obtain green low carbon methanol.

PN CN113151855-A
TI Copper nano-electrode rich in twin boundaries used for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, comprise conductive substrate and copper nano-catalyst particles supported on surface, and there are large number of twin grain boundaries on surface of copper nano-catalyst particles.
AU KUANG Y
   SONG S
   SUN X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2021912433
AB    NOVELTY - Copper nano-electrode rich in twin boundaries comprise a conductive substrate and copper nano-catalyst particles supported on the surface, and there are a large number of twin grain boundaries on the surface of the copper nano-catalyst particles, and the crystal faces constituting the twin grain boundaries are copper(111) and copper(200).
   USE - The copper nano-electrode rich in twin boundaries is useful for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-electrode increases the selectivity of the carbon dioxide reduction product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing copper nano-electrode rich in twin boundaries, involving supporting mixture of copper hydroxide and basic copper salt on the conductive substrate, pretreating the electrode by constant potential polarization to obtain the copper nano-electrode; and
   (2) use of the copper nano-electrode for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, involving using the copper nano-electrode to improve the selectivity of the multi-carbon product of the carbon dioxide reduction product, where the multi-carbon products comprise ethylene, ethanol and/or isopropanol, and the copper nano-electrode can reduce the Faraday efficiency of hydrogen to 17% or less, and increases the Faraday efficiency of multi-carbon products to more than 68%, and increases the Faraday efficiency of ethylene to more than 62%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The angle between the two crystal planes constituting the twin boundaries is 53 degrees . The potentiostatic polarization pre-reduction treatment adopts a three-electrode system, and the working electrode is the electrode loaded with a mixture of copper hydroxide and basic copper salt, where the potentiostatic polarization pre-reduction treatment time is longer than 1 minute, and the potentiostatic potential is lower than minus 1 volt, compared with the standard hydrogen electrode, the electrolyte is saturated with carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The conductive substrate comprises gas diffusion layer carbon paper, hydrophobic carbon paper, hydrophilic carbon paper and/or carbon cloth. The electrolytic solution of the potentiostatic polarization pre-reduction treatment process comprises sodium bicarbonate solution, potassium bicarbonate, potassium sulfate solution, sodium sulfate solution, potassium chloride solution, sodium sulfide solution, potassium bromide solution, sodium bromide solution, potassium iodide solution and/or sodium iodide solution, and the above electrolyte is saturated with carbon dioxide. The precursor of the mixture of copper hydroxide and basic copper salt is basic copper salt, when the basic copper salt is basic copper sulfate, and the preparation method involves adding 0.1-0.3 mol/l aqueous ammonia solution to 1-3 mol/l copper sulfate pentahydrate solution, stirring to obtain basic copper sulfate dispersion, adding 0.5-4 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, reacting to obtain the dispersion of copper hydroxide and basic copper sulfate mixture, solid-liquid separating, washing, and drying to obtain the mixture of copper hydroxide and basic copper sulfate powder, and when the basic copper salt is basic copper nitrate, and the preparation method involves dripping 0.5-2 mol/l potassium hydroxide solution into boiling 2.5-5.5 mol/l copper nitrate trihydrate solution, stirring to obtain basic copper nitrate dispersion, solid-liquid separating, washing, drying to obtain light green basic copper nitrate powder, dissolving the basic copper nitrate powder in water to obtain 0.2-3 mol/l basic copper nitrate solution, adding 0.1-2 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution to 0.2-3 mol/l basic copper nitrate solution, reacting to obtain a dispersion of copper hydroxide and basic copper nitrate mixture, solid-liquid separating, washing, and drying to obtain a mixture powder of copper hydroxide and basic copper nitrate, when the basic copper salt is basic copper carbonate, and the preparation method involves dripping 0.5-2 mol/l copper sulfate pentahydrate solution into 0.5-2 mol/l anhydrous sodium carbonate solution, evenly stirring, adding to an oil bath at 50-100 degrees C, heating for 5-30 minutes, where the solution changed from blue to emerald green, centrifugally washing, drying to obtain an emerald green basic copper carbonate powder, dissolving the basic copper carbonate powder in water to obtain 0.2-3 mol/l basic copper carbonate solution, adding 0.1-2 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution to 0.2-3 mol/l basic copper carbonate solution, reacting to obtain a dispersion of a mixture of copper hydroxide and basic copper carbonate, solid-liquid separating, washing, and drying to obtain copper hydroxide and basic copper carbonate powder. Preferred Properties: The particle size of the copper nano-catalyst particles are 1-200 nm. Preferred Method: The copper nano-catalyst particles are formed by performing electrochemical pre-reduction of a mixture of copper hydroxide and basic copper salt by applying a reduction potential, where the basic copper salt comprises basic copper sulfate, basic copper nitrate, basic copper carbonate, basic copper chloride and/or basic copper acetate.

PT P
PN US2019329227-A1; US11266980-B2
TI Carbon dioxide hydrogenation via reverse water-gas shift reaction to selective form carbon monoxide, comprises e.g. co-impregnating molybdenum and alkali-metal precursors onto gamma-alumina support and carburizing dried and calcined support.
AU POROSOFF M
   WILLAUER H D
AE US SEC OF NAVY (USNA-C)
   US SEC OF NAVY (USNA-C)
GA 201990072L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide hydrogenation via the reverse water-gas shift (RWGS) reaction for the selective formation of carbon monoxide, comprises: co-impregnating molybdenum and alkali-metal precursors onto a gamma -alumina support, where the alkali metal is potassium, sodium or lithium; drying and calcining impregnated gamma -alumina support; carburizing the dried and calcined gamma -alumina support to form alkali metal-promoted molybdenum carbide supported on gamma -alumina (A-molybdenum2C/ gamma -alumina), where A is potassium, sodium or lithium; and reacting the A-molybdenum2C/ gamma -alumina with carbon dioxide and hydrogen to form carbon monoxide.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide hydrogenation via RWGS reaction for the selective formation of carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides the carbon dioxide hydrogenation via the RWGS reaction that achieves a carbon monoxide yield of greater than or equal to 12% and a carbon monoxide selectivity of greater than or equal to 90% (claimed); and utilizes the alkali metal-promoted molybdenum carbide supported on gamma -alumina that is a low-cost, stable and highly selective catalyst for RWGS over a wide range of conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a supported heterogeneous catalyst material for catalyzing RWGS reaction for the selective formation of carbon monoxide, comprising a support material comprising gamma -alumina, and an active material comprising alkali-metal doped molybdenum carbide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The loading of molybdenum is 1-70 wt.%. The loading of the alkali metal is 0.1-15 wt.%. The carburization is performed at 400-1000 degrees C. The reaction is performed while applying external heat. The reaction is performed at 250-1000 degrees C. The reaction is performed at a pressure of 0-350 psig. The reaction is performed while flowing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen gas, over the A-molybdenum2C/ gamma -alumina catalyst material. The reaction is performed while applying external heat and flowing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen gas over the A-molybdenum2C/ gamma -alumina catalyst material. Preferred Components: The alkali-metal component of the active material comprises at least one alkali-metal precursor in elemental form or in the form of oxides, where the metal comprises potassium, sodium and/or lithium. The molybdenum component of the active material comprises at least one molybdenum precursor in the form of carbides, oxycarbides, oxides and/or elemental molybdenum.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E31-N05A; E31-N05B1; J04-E11; N01-A; N03-D02; N04-A; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-J
IP B01J-021/02; B01J-027/22; B01J-037/02; B01J-037/08; C01B-032/40; C01B-032/949; B01J-021/04

The discharge port of the decompression unit is connected to the storage carbon dioxide tank.
