TI Wet oxidation treatment system used for            high-concentration organic wastewater, comprises            pressurized high-concentration wastewater storage tank,            high-concentration wastewater pump, heat exchange            reaction device, oxygen supply unit, and post-reaction            wastewater pressure storage tank.
AU MENG J
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2022986831
AB    NOVELTY - A wet oxidation treatment system comprises a                pressurized high-concentration wastewater storage                tank used to receive and store wastewater to be                injected into the system, where reaction gas rich                in carbon dioxide generated by wet oxidation                reaction of the system is introduced into                wastewater to generate carbon dioxide rich quenched                and tempered high-concentration wastewater, a                high-concentration wastewater pump, a heat exchange                reaction device, which is connected to the pump to                receive the wastewater and is used for                supplementing heating according to the situation to                make it reach the set value wet oxidation reaction                temperature at the highest temperature, and then                wet oxidation is carried out to generate                post-reaction hot wastewater, which is subjected to                heat exchanging and cooling to obtain post-reaction                cooling wastewater, an oxygen supply unit, and a                post-reaction wastewater pressure storage                tank.
   USE - The system is useful for wet oxidation                treatment of high-concentration organic wastewater                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The wastewater in the pressurized                high-concentration wastewater storage tank absorbs                carbon dioxide in the incoming reaction gas, and                quenches and modifies the wastewater, so that                carbon dioxide can be used to significantly enhance                anti-scaling effect of the wastewater treatment.                The stability and reliability of the process is                improved. The backflow of the reaction gas in the                wastewater pressure storage tank after reaction                achieves recovery and utilization of residual                pressure energy, and reduces process energy                consumption and treatment cost to certain                extent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A wet oxidation treatment system comprises a                pressurized high-concentration wastewater storage                tank used to receive and store wastewater to be                injected into the system, where reaction gas rich                in carbon dioxide generated by wet oxidation                reaction of the system is introduced into                wastewater to generate carbon dioxide rich quenched                and tempered high-concentration wastewater, a                high-concentration wastewater pump connected to the                storage tank and used for pumping the wastewater, a                heat exchange reaction device, which is connected                to the pump to receive the wastewater and is used                for heat exchanging and temperature rise of the                wastewater, and for supplementing heating according                to the situation to make it reach the set value wet                oxidation reaction temperature at the highest                temperature, and then wet oxidation is carried out                to generate post-reaction hot wastewater and a                reaction gas rich in carbon dioxide, where the hot                wastewater is subjected to heat exchanging and                cooling to obtain post-reaction cooling wastewater,                an oxygen supply unit, which is used for providing                an oxidant for the quenched and tempered wastewater                that undergoes wet oxidation, and a post-reaction                wastewater pressure storage tank, which is used for                separating the post-reaction cooling wastewater and                the carbon dioxide-rich reaction gas in the storage                tank and temporarily storing. The reaction gas rich                in carbon dioxide is introduced into the                pressurized wastewater storage tank under the                pressure of the post-reaction wastewater pressure                storage tank for conditioning high-concentration                wastewater. The post-reaction cooling wastewater is                subjected to subsequent treatment or external                discharge under the action of the pressure of the                post-reaction wastewater pressure storage                tank.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for wet                oxidation treatment process for high-concentration                organic wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of carbon                  dioxide in the reaction gas is 20-80 vol.%.

TI Preparing molding sand useful for casting hook body for preparing railway hook slowing device, comprises e.g. taking sand and quartz, laying gas eyes on surface of plastic cloth, carrying out blow-hardening process and pulling out air pipe.
AU LI M
   MU J
   SUN X
   YU H
   YU C
AE QINGDAO LIANSHAN CASTING CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019205004
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing molding sand comprises (i) taking sand type ratio and quartz sand, inorganic binder, reinforcing agent, stirring evenly, adding reinforcing agent and inorganic binder, mixing quartz sand and covering plastic cloth with mixed quartz sand; (iii) laying five gas eyes on the surface of the plastic cloth one of which is in the middle and one on each side; (iv) carrying out blow-hardening process comprises connecting air blower to the side of the carbon dioxide gas source with a valve, draining water in the wind bag, opening five of the air outlet valves of the air blower control device, the remaining one air outlet valve judges the gas flow rate by blowing operator, opening the side ball valve on the carbon dioxide pipe, opening gate valve, blowing carbon dioxide into the quartz sand, closing carbon dioxide side ball valve, opening simultaneously compressed air side ball valve and opening to one-third of the stroke; and (vi) pulling out the air pipe.
   USE - The molding sand is useful for casting hook body for preparing railway hook slowing device (claimed), preferably for American railway hook slowing device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing molding sand comprises (i) taking sand type ratio and quartz sand at 100 wt.%, inorganic binder, reinforcing agent, where amount of adding inorganic binder is 2.8-3.4 wt.% of quartz sand and adding reinforcing agent is 18-24 wt.% of inorganic binder, the inorganic binder comprises cement and clay at mass ratio of 1-3:0.8-1.5; (ii) adding quartz sand and water, stirring evenly, adding reinforcing agent, covering with plastic cloth on mixed quartz sand, adding inorganic binder, mixing quartz sand, taking out quartz sand and covering plastic cloth with mixed quartz sand; (iii) laying five gas eyes on the surface of the plastic cloth one of which is in the middle and one on each side, and the middle eye is smoothed with wet quartz sand, slanting four eyes on the side of the plastic sheet, inserting four air holes into the air blowing tube, preventing front end of the air blowing tube from entering the quartz sand during the intubation and compacting at cannula; (iv) carrying out blow-hardening process comprises connecting air blower to the side of the carbon dioxide gas source with a valve, before using compressed air, draining water in the wind bag, opening five of the air outlet valves of the air blower control device, four of which correspond to the air blowing pipe inserted into the quartz sand, the remaining one air outlet valve judges the gas flow rate by the blowing operator during the blowing process, opening the side ball valve on the carbon dioxide pipe, opening gate valve, blowing carbon dioxide into the quartz sand, closing carbon dioxide side ball valve, opening simultaneously compressed air side ball valve, opening to one-third of the stroke, blowing compressed air and closing compressed air ball valve and carbon dioxide side gate valve; and (v) pulling out the air pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reinforcing agent in step (i) is a silane coupling agent, preferably, the silane coupling agent comprises vinyltriethoxysilane, vinyltrimethoxysilane and vinyltris( beta -methoxyethoxy)silane. Preferred Composition: The amount of adding water in step (i) is 5-8 wt.% of quartz sand. Preferred Method: The mixing of quartz sand in step (ii) is carried out for 30-40 seconds. The mixing of quartz sand in step (iii) is carried out for 90-100 seconds. The step (i) comprises setting gas pressure of the carbon dioxide to 0.3-0.35 MPa. The blowing of carbon dioxide in step (iv) is carried out by blowing first for 8-10 seconds at 5-10 m3/h and blowing for 40-45 seconds at 12-15 m3/hour. The step (v) comprises blowing the compressed air is carried out for 25-28 seconds, where air pressure is 0.3-0.35 MPa. The step (v) further comprises cleaning the hardened quartz sand in the gas pipe comprises sprinkling dry quartz sand on the smooth surface, flipping core box over it, opening core box clamp, and knocking both operators simultaneously on the appropriate part of the core box.

PN CN110975813-A
TI Preparation of Salix mongolica-based porous carbon used for adsorbing carbon dioxide, by mixing Salix mongolica, ammonia and water to perform hydrothermal reaction and mixing with activator potassium hydroxide to perform activation reaction.
AU LEI Y
   SU H
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA (UNMO-C)
GA 202031486S
AB    NOVELTY - A Salix mongolica-based porous carbon is prepared by mixing Salix mongolica, ammonia and water to perform hydrothermal reaction to get nitrogen-doped hydrated carbon, and another mixing nitrogen-doped hydrated carbon and activator to perform activation reaction.
   USE - The method is used for preparing Salix mongolica-based porous carbon for carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product can adsorb carbon dioxide up to 110.1 mg/g, is graded porous carbon, and has high specific surface area, developed pore structure and surface containing oxygen and nitrogen functional groups.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activator is potassium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dosage ratio of Salix mongolica, 10-30 wt.% ammonia and water is 1-2g:10-30 mL:35-55 mL. The mass ratio of activator to nitrogen-doped hydrated carbon is 1:1-8. The carbon has specific surface area of 1210.62-1518.2 m2/g, total pore volume of 0.5238-0.7834 cm3/g and nitrogen content of 2.8-7.6%. Preferred Method: Mixing is carried out under stirring condition at room temperature for 2 hours at speed of 1000 revolutions/minute (rpm). Hydrothermal reaction is done at 150-220 degrees C for 14-26 hours. Activation reaction is done at 400-900 degrees C for 2-4 hours at rate of 5 degrees C/minute.

PN WO2019004507-A1
TI System useful for monitoring fungus, comprises first measuring unit, second measuring unit, and control unit.
AU KIM D C
AE FIELDSOLUTION CO LTD (FIEL-Non-standard)
GA 2019020730
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises (i) a first measuring unit for measuring the relative humidity and the carbon dioxide concentration of a reference region, (ii) a second measuring unit for measuring the relative humidity and the carbon dioxide concentration of a target region, and (iii) control unit for determining the possibility of mold occurrence in the target region by assigning a predetermined variable according to the differences between each of the relative humidities and the carbon dioxide concentrations, which are measured by the first measuring unit and the second measuring unit.
   USE - The system is useful for monitoring fungus (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for monitoring a fungus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred System: The control unit calculates a predetermined value of a predetermined humidity variable according to a difference between the relative humidity measured by the first measuring unit and the second measuring unit when the relative humidity measured by the second measuring unit is 60%. The control unit sets the humidity variable to '0' when the relative humidity measured by the second measuring unit is less than 60%. The control unit is configured to control the first measuring unit and the second measuring unit to measure the carbon dioxide concentration measured by the first measuring unit and the second measuring unit when the carbon dioxide concentration measured by the second measuring unit shows a rising trend over a predetermined range, and a predetermined value of a predetermined carbon dioxide parameter is given according to the difference. The control unit sets the carbon dioxide parameter to '0' if the carbon dioxide concentration measured by the second measuring unit doesn't show the rising trend or doesn't reach the stable state. The system comprises an alarm unit for notifying the possibility of fungus formation if the control unit determines that fungus is likely to occur in the target area. The alarm unit comprises a display device for displaying the possibility of fungus as an image or a speaker device for outputting a sound.

Preferred Components: The molar ratio between butterfly hexa(hydroxy)tricyclic quinazoline and terephthalic acid is 2:1-10. The washing solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, acetone, N,N-dimethylacetamide and/or 1,4-dioxane. Preferred Method: The heating crystallizing reaction temperature is 80-180 degrees C. The reaction time is 72-168 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogel composition manufacturing involves dissolving polymer hydrogel in an alkali solution supplying an alkaline solution in which the hydrogel is dissolved to a calcium solution in which calcium is dissolved, and then forming a gel-state hydrogel composition containing basic calcium. The calcium is combined with the hydrogel to form the gel-like hydrogel composition.
   USE - Method for manufacturing hydrogel composition for removing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures the hydrogel composition in a simple and cost-effective manner with rapid removal of carbon dioxide, excellent shape stability, easy storage and transport.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution is a hydroxide combined with an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal. The calcium solution is calcium chloride, calcium sulfate, calcium carbonate, and calcium hydroxide. The alkali hydrogels are dissolved in the alkali solution at a negative pressure to inhibit the formation of bubbles in the alkali solution. The hydrogel composition is provided in the form of beads separated from each other.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer hydrogel is a synthetic polymer material selected from polyacrylic acid, polyacrylamide, kappa-carrageenan, karaya gum, amide pectin, carboxymethylcellulose, polytetrafluoroethylene, polyethylene, polyurethane or polyethylene glycol. The polymer hydrogel is a natural high molecular substance of hyaluronic acid, heparin, dextra, collagen, albumin, gelatin and chitosan.

AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorbent comprises a chain                amine (I), a cyclic amine, and an acid.
   USE - Absorbent is used in apparatus for separating                and recovering carbon dioxide (claimed) in                coal-fired power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent has improved                carbon dioxide absorption performance and low                corrosivity, and enables economical separation and                recovery of carbon dioxide with reduced energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide absorbent comprises a chain                amine of formula (I), a cyclic amine, and an                acid.
   R1 = H or 1-7C alkyl comprising at least one                hydroxy;
   R2 = 1-7C alkyl comprising at least one                hydroxy;and
   R3 = H, 1-7C linear alkyl, 1-7C branched                alkyl, or 5-7C cycloalkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus                (1) for separating and recovering carbon dioxide,                which comprises the carbon dioxide absorbent which                is a carbon dioxide unabsorbed carbon dioxide                absorbent, an absorption tower (2) configured to                separate and recover carbon dioxide from a gas                containing carbon dioxide by contacting the gas                containing carbon dioxide with the carbon dioxide                unabsorbed carbon dioxide absorbent to allow carbon                dioxide to be absorbed in the carbon dioxide                unabsorbed carbon dioxide absorbent to obtain a                carbon dioxide absorbed carbon dioxide absorbent,                and a regeneration tower (3) configured to                regenerate the carbon dioxide absorbed carbon                dioxide absorbent by desorbing carbon dioxide from                the carbon dioxide absorbed carbon dioxide                absorbent which has absorbed carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus for separating and recovering carbon                dioxide.
   Apparatus (1)
   Absorption Tower (2)
   Regeneration Tower (3)
   Carbon Dioxide Absorbent Supply Hole                (5)
   Reflux Condenser (12)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  cyclic amine with respect to acid is 1:0.1-1:1.9.                  The cyclic amine is of formula (II) and/or (III).                  The acid is hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid,                  sulfuric acid, nitric acid, hydroiodic acid,                  acetic acid, citric acid, formic acid, gluconic                  acid, lactic acid, oxalic acid, tartaric acid,                  and/or a sulfo group-containing organic acid of                  formula: R10-SO3H (IV) or HO3S-R11-SO3H (V),                  preferably 1,2-ethanedisulfonic acid,                  1,3-propanedisulfonic acid, 2-aminoethanesulfonic                  acid,                  N,N-bis(2-hydroxyethyl)-2-aminoethanesulfonic                  acid, 2,2-iminobis-ethanesulfonic acid,                  2-dimethylaminoethanesulfonic acid, and                  3-dimethylamino-1-propanesulfonic acid.R4 = 1-3C alkyl optionally comprising                  organic group including nitrogen as side chain,                  and optionally hydroxy as side chain;R5 = 2-3C alkyl optionally comprising                  organic group including nitrogen as side chain,                  and optionally hydroxy as side chain;R6,R7 = H or 1-4C linear or branched alkyl                  optionally comprising hydroxy;R8 = 4-7C linear alkyl optionally comprising                  organic group including nitrogen as side chain,                  and optionally hydroxy as side chain;R9 = H or 1-4C linear or branched alkyl                  optionally comprising hydroxy;R10 = 1-5C organic group including alkyl                  and/or aryl, and optionally including amino                  and/or hydroxy;andR11 = 2-5C organic group comprising alkyl,                  or amino and alkyl.Preferred Properties: The pH of the carbon                  dioxide absorbent before absorbing carbon dioxide                  is 9.5-10.8. The acid has an acid dissociation                  constant of 6 or less.

AU HU P

GA 202105617M
AB    (CN212298528-U)   NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide pressurizing device, comprising a box body; the box body is fixedly installed with a booster pump; the input end of the booster pump is fixedly connected with one end of the input pipeline; the other end of the input pipeline passes through the side surface of the box body and is connected with the carbon dioxide output source; the output end of the booster pump is fixedly connected with one end of the output pipeline; the other end of the output pipeline passes through the side surface of the box body and is connected with the external device; the driving gas inlet end of the booster pump is fixedly connected with one end of the compressed air inlet pipe; the other end of the compressed air inlet pipe passes through the side surface of the box body and is connected with the external compressed air source; the compressed air inlet pipe is orderly fixedly installed with a filter, a pressure regulating valve and a switch valve. The utility model overcomes the disadvantages of the existing technology, and can pressurize the pressure of the input end to the specified range. when the pressure of the output end reaches the specified pressure when working, it can automatically stop when the booster pump is required; when the output end is lower than the specified pressure, the booster pump pressurizes automatically; after the output end exceeds the required pressure, it can automatically release pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide pressurizing device.
   Booster pump (2)
   Input pipe (3)
   Carbon dioxide output source (4)
   Output pipe (5)

   Pressure regulating valve (8)
   On-off valve (9)

   Electric control box (12)
   Pneumatic valve (13)
   External compressed air source (14)
   Input branch pipe (31)
   Relief valve (32)
   Liquid carbon dioxide output source (41)
   Gaseous carbon dioxide output source (42)
EA    NOVELTY - The device has an input pipe (3) whose end passes through side surface of a box and is connected with a carbon dioxide output source (4). The output end of a booster pump (2) is fixedly connected to one end of an output pipe (5). The other end of the output pipe passes through side surface of the box frame to communicate with external equipment. A driving gas inlet end of the booster pump is fixedly connected to one end of a compressed air intake pipe (6). The other end of the compressed air intake pipe passes through the side surface of the box and is connected to an external compressed air source (14). The compressed air intake pipe is fixedly installed with a filter (7), a pressure regulating valve (8) and an on-off valve (9) in sequence.
   USE - Carbon dioxide pressurizing device used in petroleum, chemical, food and laboratories.
   ADVANTAGE - The booster pump is required to automatically stop when the pressure at the output end reaches the specified pressure during work. The booster pump automatically pressurizes when the output end is lower than the specified pressure, and automatically release the pressure when the output end exceeds the required pressure.

AB    NOVELTY - A reaction gas full temperature range-pressure swing adsorption separating method involves taking medium temperature pressure swing adsorption concentrate-1 and a non-condensable gas as a feed gas, and pressurizing the medium temperature multi-column pressure swing adsorption concentration system. The effluent methane-rich non-adsorbed phase gas is entered into the next process and taken as the intermediate temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration-2, blasted the effluent carbon dioxide-rich adsorption phase gas into a subsequent process, followed by heat exchanging the medium temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration-2, blowing the carbon dioxide-rich adsorption phase gas, entering the carbon dioxide absorption tower and heating. The non-condensable gas is returned to the ethylene oxide reactor, heated the bottom of the potassium carbonate solution and mixed with supplemented potassium carbonate solution and recovered carbon dioxide from the direct discharge.
   USE - Method for separating reaction gas full temperature range-pressure swing adsorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A reaction gas full temperature range-pressure swing adsorption separating method involves taking medium temperature pressure swing adsorption concentrate-1 and a non-condensable gas from the top of an ethylene oxide scrubber tower as a feed gas, and pressurizing or heat exchanging or directly entering the medium temperature multi-column pressure swing adsorption concentration system with an operating temperature of 60-120 degrees C and an operating pressure of 0.3-3.0MPa. The effluent methane-rich non-adsorbed phase gas is entered into the next process and taken as the intermediate temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration-2, blasted or pressurizing the effluent carbon dioxide-rich adsorption phase gas into a subsequent process to complete the absorption decarburization, followed by heat exchanging or directly entering the medium temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration-2, a methane-rich non-adsorbed phase gas from the pressure swing adsorption concentrate-1 system to a multi-column pressure swing adsorption with an operating temperature of 20-80 degrees C and an operating pressure of 0.3-3.0MPa, directly discharging the argon-rich non-adsorbed phase gas flowing out from methane ethylene concentration system or entering the fuel gas pipe network as a fuel gas or sending to the boundary area to recover argon gas, returning the effluent methane and ethylene-rich adsorption phase gas to the ethylene oxide reactor for reaction after passing through the recycle gas compressor as an ethylene oxide reaction cycle gas, blowing or pressurizing the carbon dioxide-rich adsorption phase gas from the pressure swing adsorption concentrate-1 system during absorbing decarburization, after the heat exchange, entering the carbon dioxide absorption tower at the absorption temperature of 20-80 degrees C and the absorption pressure of 0.3-1.0MPa, heating and entering the potassium carbonate solution as an absorbent from the top of the absorption tower. The non-condensable gas flowing out from the top of the absorption tower is returned to the ethylene oxide reactor for recycling as an ethylene oxide reaction cycle gas through a heat exchange and a recycle gas compressor. The carbon dioxide-rich absorption liquid flowing out from the bottom of the absorption tower is entered into the desorption tower through a circulation pump, heated the bottom of the potassium carbonate solution and mixed with a portion of supplemented potassium carbonate solution to return to the absorption tower for recycling, and the carbon dioxide-rich desorbed gas flowing from the top of the desorption tower and recovered carbon dioxide from the direct discharge or output boundary.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide trans-critical thermodynamic                cycle power storage system comprises a cold storage                unit connected on a heat pump circulation loop. The                heat pump circulation loop utilizes redundant                electric energy in an electric network to convert a                working substance in the heat pump circulation loop                into a supercritical state of high temperature and                high pressure and send the working substance to a                heat storage unit. The heat storage unit is                provided with multiple heat storage mediums. A low                temperature and high pressure working medium after                absorbing heat by the heat storage unit enters into                the heat pump circulation loop again to finish a                working medium circulation.

   TOMITA I
   ABE H
AE NIPPON MINING & METALS CO LTD (NIHA-C)
   NIPPON MINING & METALS CO LTD (NIHA-C)
   NIPPON MINING & METALS CO LTD (NIHA-C)
GA 2020981091
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing lithium carbonate,                involves (a) dissolving crude lithium carbonate in                a liquid while supplying carbon dioxide gas, and                (b) performing decarboxylation by heating the                lithium solution obtained in step (a), and removing                carbon dioxide. In step (a), the liquid mixed with                crude lithium carbonate is retained in a reaction                tank, and the liquid is stirred using a stirrer.                The stirrer has: a cylindrical body arranged in an                upright position in the reaction tank; rotating                blades located at an end of the cylindrical body,                that are driven to rotate about the axial line of                rotation, and that extend radially; fixed blades                that extend radially and that are fixed and                arranged radially outside the rotating blades at                the end of the cylindrical body; and a gas supply                tube that blows gas toward the rotating blades and                the fixed blades.
   USE - The method is used for producing lithium                carbonate which is used for manufacturing lithium                ion batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases reaction efficiency of                carbon dioxide gas when dissolving crude lithium                carbonate, achieves effective utilization of                resources of lithium-ion battery waste, and                suppresses the loss of lithium due to the                generation of undissolved crude lithium carbonate                to a small value. The method shortens the reaction                time, effectively reduces production cost, and                produces lithium carbonate grade (purity) of 99.2                %mass or more, preferably 99.5 %mass or more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (a), the                  slurry concentration before dissolving the crude                  lithium carbonate in the liquid is 20-90                  g/l.

PN WO2019185622-A1; EP3571331-A1; CA3088833-A1; EP3571331-B1; US2021218036-A1
TI Multilayer composite catalyst used for reducing            carbon dioxide in fuel cell for electrochemical            production of hydrocarbon product e.g. ethylene,            comprises catalyst layer, hydrophobic gas-diffusion            layer, and current collection structure.
AU SARGENT E
   DINH C
   PELAYO GARCIA DE ARQUER F
   IP A
   PELAYO G D A F
AE TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
GA 201983393X
AB    NOVELTY - A multilayer composite catalyst comprises a                catalyst layer comprising at least one metal                compound, and having opposing sides (A) and (B), a                hydrophobic gas-diffusion layer provided on the                side (A) of the catalyst layer, and a current                collection structure provided on the side (B) of                the catalyst layer.
   USE - Multilayer composite catalyst used for                reducing carbon dioxide in fuel cell for production                of hydrocarbon product e.g. ethylene (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The multilayer composite catalyst enables                production of products with excellent activity,                selectivity, and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) electrochemical production of a                hydrocarbon product, which involves contacting                carbon dioxide gas and an electrolyte with an                electrode comprising the catalyst, such that the                carbon dioxide gas diffuses through the hydrophobic                gas-diffusion layer and contacts the catalyst                layer, applying a voltage to provide a current                density in the current collection structure of the                catalyst to cause the gas contacting the catalyst                layer to be electrochemically converted into the                hydrocarbon product;
   (2) fuel cell, which comprises reaction cell                receiving an electrolyte comprising 6-8M potassium                hydroxide, oxygen evolution catalyst comprising                nickel iron oxide, and composite multilayer                catalyst;
   (3) system, which comprises electrolytic cell                to receive liquid electrolyte and carbon dioxide,                anode, and cathode comprising catalyst; and
   (4) manufacture of multilayer composite                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst layer                  comprises copper, preferably copper ion and                  cupric ion and/or copper alloys chosen from                  copper-silver alloy, copper-palladium alloy,                  copper-aluminum alloy and/or auricupride alloy.                  The current collection structure comprises a                  stabilization layer arranged on the catalyst                  layer, current collection layer arranged on the                  stabilization layer, and at least one                  electro-conductive carbon compound. The                  stabilization layer comprises carbon                  nanoparticles, current collection layer comprises                  graphite and forms a graphite electrode layer,                  and electro-conductive carbon compound comprises                  carbon nanoparticles and/or graphite, preferably                  carbon nanoparticles and graphite having a                  multilayered structure. The electrolyte used in                  electrochemical production comprises 3-12M                  potassium hydroxide and further comprises                  potassium iodide to replace missing potassium.                  Preferred Properties: The catalyst layer has                  thickness of 1-2000 nm, and current collection                  structure has thickness of 1-100 mu m, preferably                  10-50 mu m, determined by scanning electron                  microscope. The current collection structure has                  current density of at least 100 mA/cm2,                  preferably 250-800 mA/cm2 for selective                  electrochemical conversion of the carbon dioxide                  into a target hydrocarbon product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic                  gas-diffusion layer comprises an                  electro-insulating material which is a polymeric                  material comprising fluorocarbon polymer,                  preferably PTFE. Preferred Properties: The                  hydrophobic gas-diffusion layer has porosity with                  pores having diameter of 0.01-2 mu m, and                  thickness of 20-500 mu m, determined by scanning                  electron microscope.

TI Integrated control pre-point circumferential seam welding machine has frame whose left and right sides are provided with translation frame which is provided with main shaft, clamp, carbon dioxide welding torch and argon arc welding torch.
AU XIE J
AE FOSHAN SHUNDE JIEFENG IND AUTOMATION CO (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201970174H
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an integrated type pre-point circular seam welding machine, comprising a machine seat, the left and right sides of the machine seat is provided with a translation, the translation frame is provided with a main shaft, the translation frame is provided with a clamp connected with the main shaft; the translation frame is provided with a carbon dioxide welding gun and argon arc welding gun, the carbon dioxide welding gun is in turn up and down the first Y axes shifting frame drop through the can, can first Z axis moving frame to move front and back, left and right first X axis movement of the frame is connected with the translation. the argon-arc welding gun on and raising the second Y axes shifting frame drop through the can, can front and back second Z-axis moving bracket connected with the translation to move; the seam welder, arrangement of the carbon dioxide welding gun and argon arc welding gun is more reasonable, compiling of welding gun travelling program more simple and convenient, it avoids welding gun and collide, the dispersing of the end cover and a cylinder disposable coupling is good, the welding is good, it is convenient to the carbon dioxide welding gun and argon arc welding gun and improves welding efficiency.

PT P
PN NL2020163-B1; WO2019125151-A1; CA3084335-A1; KR2020091433-A; CN111615510-A; IN202047025910-A; SG11202005008-A1; EP3728213-A1; BR112020012638-A2; US2020399239-A1; JP2021508736-W; US11053210-B2; EP3728213-B1; ES2903541-T3; IN398124-B; KR2452490-B1
TI Continuously preparing cyclic carbonate product            comprises e.g. reacting epoxide compound with carbon            dioxide in suspension of liquid cyclic carbonate and            supported dimeric aluminum salen complex which is            activated by halide compound.
AU LAURENTIUS S
   VERGUNST F
AE ALTA INNOVATION SUPPORT BV (ALTA-Non-standard)
   NEW GREEN WORLD BV (NEWG-Non-standard)
   NEW GREEN WORLD BV (NEWG-Non-standard)
   NEW GREEN WORLD BV (NEWG-Non-standard)
   ALTA INNOVATION SUPPORT BV (ALTA-Non-standard)
   NEW GREEN WORLD BV (NEWG-Non-standard)
GA 201950999X
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously preparing a cyclic carbonate                product by reacting an epoxide compound with carbon                dioxide in the presence of a supported dimeric                aluminum salen complex which is activated by a                halide compound comprises (a) contacting carbon                dioxide with the epoxide compound in a suspension                of liquid cyclic carbonate and the supported                dimeric aluminum salen complex, where the epoxide                compound reacts with the carbon dioxide to from the                cyclic carbonate product and part of the supported                dimeric salen complex deactivates, (b) separating                part of the cyclic carbonate product from the                complex to obtain a mixture comprising of the                cyclic carbonate product, carbon dioxide, epoxide                compound and halide compound, (c) separating the                halide compound from the cyclic carbonate product                to obtain purified cyclic carbonate product, and                (d) using all or part of the halide compound as                obtained in step (c) to activate deactivated                supported dimeric salen complex.
   USE - The process is useful continuously preparing                the cyclic carbonate product.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: yields cyclic carbonate products                having a very high purity e.g. provides propylene                carbonate with purities of above 99 wt.% and even                above 99.999 wt.%; and avoids a build-up of                compounds boiling in the same range as the epoxide                which may be present in any one of the feedstocks                or which may have formed in the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The temperature in step                  (a) is 20-150 degrees C and the pressure is                  0.1-0.5 MPa, where temperature is below the                  boiling temperature of the product at the chosen                  pressure. The step (a) is performed in a                  continuously operated stirred reactor, where                  carbon dioxide and epoxide compound are                  continuously supplied to the reactor and part of                  the cyclic carbonate product is continuously                  withdrawn as part of a liquid stream. The                  separation step (b) makes use of the different                  mass density and/or size between the cyclic                  carbonate and the complex. The separation step                  (b) is performed by means of a filter and using                  centrifugal forces. The step (c) is performed by                  distillation and in the distillation step a                  mixture comprising of carbon dioxide, halide                  compound, the epoxide compound and the cyclic                  carbonate product is separated into separate                  streams of carbon dioxide, halide compound,                  epoxide compound and the cyclic carbonate                  product. The content of epoxide compound in the                  mixture as obtained in step (b) is reduced to                  obtain a mixture having a reduced epoxide content                  which obtained mixture is separated in the                  distillation step (c). The reduction of the                  epoxide compound is achieved by contacting the                  epoxide compound with carbon dioxide in the                  presence of the complex in a second reaction                  step. The deactivated supported dimeric aluminum                  salen complex is activated by contacting with the                  halide compound while performing step (a). The                  deactivated supported dimeric aluminum salen                  complex is activated in a separate step (e) by                  contacting the deactivated supported dimeric                  aluminum salen complex with the halide compound                  in the presence of the cyclic carbonate product.                  The molar ratio of halide compound and the                  complex is greater than 5:1 in step (e). The                  steps (a) and (e) are performed in at least two                  parallel operated reactors, where when step (e)                  is performed in at least one reactor, step (a) is                  performed in at least one of the remaining                  reactors. Preferred Components: The epoxide                  compound has 2-8 carbon atoms, and is ethylene                  oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, pentene                  oxide, glycidol or styrene oxide. The supported                  dimeric aluminum salen complex is of formula (I).                  The support S is composed of particles having an                  average diameter of 10-2000 mu m. The support S                  is a particle consisting of silica, alumina,                  titania, siliceous MCM-41 or siliceous MCM-48.                  The halide compound is benzyl halide. The benzyl                  halide is benzyl bromide.S1 = solid support connected to N via                  alkylene, where the supported dimeric aluminum                  salen complex is activated by halide                  compound;X1 = tert-butyl;X2 = H; andE1 = 1-10C alkyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)

PN CN109529838-A
TI Flower-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for preparing methane, comprises small particles of copper oxide loaded on flower-shaped tin oxide carrier.
AU WU J
   ZENG S
   YAN L
   XUE L
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA (UNMO-C)
GA 201931163T
AB    NOVELTY - A flower-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst comprises small particles of copper oxide loaded on flower-shaped tin oxide carrier.
   USE - Flower-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst used for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for preparing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The flower-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst is prepared using cheap raw materials by simple and easy process, and has high selectivity of carbon dioxide electroreduction, and high Faraday efficiency of 67.9%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (i) production of flower rod-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst, which involves using tin chloride as raw material, sodium hydroxide as precipitant, ethanol aqueous solution as solvent, and carrying out hydrothermal synthesis of flower-shaped tin oxide carrier, using copper nitrate as raw material, synthesizing copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst with different copper oxide loadings by sedimentation precipitation process; and (ii) usage of flower-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce methane, which involves adding flower-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst to mixture of deionized water and absolute ethanol, adding Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, ultrasonically forming the uniformly dispersed solution, applying solution to copper sheet and drying at room temperature, and carrying out electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide using copper sheet as a working electrode to produce methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The flower-shaped tin oxide carrier is prepared by using tin salt as raw material and inorganic base as precipitating agent by hydrothermal process. The flower-shaped copper oxide/tin oxide catalyst is prepared by dissolving sodium hydroxide and tin chloride in water, ultrasonically treating and stirring to form uniform colorless and transparent solution, dripping ethanol, stirring evenly, transferring to reaction kettle, heating at 200 plus minus 20 degrees C for 1-3 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, collecting the product, washing, and drying to obtain flower-shaped tin oxide carrier, adding flower-shaped tin oxide carrier to copper nitrate solution and stirring for 20-40 minutes, dripping sodium hydroxide solution, stop dripping when pH of solution is 8.5-9.5, stirring mixture for 2-4 hours, collecting the product, washing, drying, and calcining. Preferred Composition: The loading amount of copper oxide is 5-20%. The molar ratio of tin chloride to sodium hydroxide is 1:(10-15). The volume ratio of water to ethanol is 1:(0.8-1.2). The mass ratio of copper nitrate to flower-shaped tin oxide carrier is (0.123-0.586):1. Preferred Properties: The diameter of rod-shaped tin oxide carrier is 0.5-5 mu m. Preferred Conditions: The calcination temperature is 300 plus minus 50 degrees C, calcination time is 1.5-2.5 hours, and calcination rate is 1.8-2.2 degrees C/minutes.

TI Method for offshore transport of carbon dioxide, involves coupling transfer tank to carbon dioxide inlet in order that carbon dioxide is emptied from transfer tank through carbon dioxide inlet.
AU KOEIJER G M D
AE STATOIL PETROLEUM AS (DENO-C)
   EQUINOR ENERGY AS (DENO-C)
GA 2018594230
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing at least one transfer tank for transport of CO2. An offshore installation is provided with a CO2 inlet configured for fluid communication with the at least one transfer tank. At least one transfer tank is transported to the offshore installation using a floating vessel whilst the at least one transfer tank contains CO2 (130). At least one transfer tank is conveyed between the floating vessel and the offshore installation using a transfer device. At least one transfer tank is coupled to the CO2 inlet in order that the CO2 can be emptied from the transfer tank through the CO2 inlet.
   USE - Method for offshore transport of CO2 installation in sea.
   ADVANTAGE - The capital expenditures of the system which can be greatly raised due to material cost of long risers is reduced. The waiting time for the floating vessel can be significantly reduced, since all that is required is for the transfer tanks containing CO2 to be removed from the floating vessel and to be replaced with transfer tanks containing hydrocarbons and optionally produced formation water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for offshore transport of CO2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a first CO2 transfer apparatus at a stage of removing a transfer tank containing CO2 from a floating vessel using a crane.

   Crane (112)
   Engagement pad (114)
   Floating vessel (120)
   Transfer tank contains carbon dioxide (130)

TI Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate useful in papermaking applications, involves calcining limestone, adding calcium oxide, sodium bicarbonate, stearic acid, carbon dioxide, maleic acid and calcium carbonate dispersant, and sieving.
AU LI W

   AERODYNE RES INC (AGAC-C)

GA 201800090G
AB    NOVELTY - The instrument has a laser source (150) applying a beam to a sample cell (130). An optical detector (160) receives the beam after passing though the sample cell and measures signal intensity. A controller (170) is coupled to the laser source and the optical detector, and performs measurements at measurement frequency using the laser source and the optical detector to produce a dataset. The controller analyzes the dataset to detect changes in carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration that are not anti-correlated with atmospheric oxygen (O2) concentration and identifies a potential CO2 leak.
   USE - Instrument for detecting CO2 leaks from CO2 sequestration facilities during fossil fuel combustion processes, photosynthesis processes and plant and soil respiration processes.
   ADVANTAGE - The instrument analyzes rapidly-captured measurements over short time intervals by high speed and high precision detection of atmospheric oxygen and carbon dioxide such that calibration can be avoided when concentration difference determinations are completed before instrument drift occurs. The instrument provides availability of rapid concentration measurements for water to compensate water concentration, thus avoiding a need to remove water vapor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a CO2 leak detection method.

PN CN114956873-A
TI Co-production process of rice straw-based            potassium humate and activated carbon comprises washing            rice straw with water, crushing into rice straw powder,            preheating oxidant, mixing oxidation residue with            potassium hydroxide solution, and introducing mixed gas            of carbon dioxide and superheated steam.
AU LIU P
   LI Y
   ZHONG X
   ZHANG Y
   LEI T
   LU A
   LIAO G
AE SUNTIME ENVIRONMENTAL REPAIRING CO LTD (SUNT-Non-standard)
GA 2022B53994
AB    NOVELTY - Co-production process of rice straw-based                potassium humate and activated carbon involves (a)                washing rice straw with water, airing until surface                is dry, and crushing into rice straw powder; (b)                preheating oxidant to 70-90&#8451;, adding rice                straw powder, stirring, reacting for 60-90 minutes,                cooling, and filtering to obtain fulvic acid mother                liquor and oxidation residue; (c) mixing oxidation                residue with potassium hydroxide solution, heating                to 70-80&#8451;, stirring and reacting for                90-120 minutes; (d) stopping heating, centrifuging                to obtain potassium humate mother liquor and                reaction residue; (e) dropping potassium hydroxide                in fulvic acid mother liquor, adjusting pH to 9-11,                adding potassium humate mother liquor, stirring,                mixing, and drying to obtain rice straw-based                potassium humate; and (f) placing reaction residue                in nitrogen atmosphere, heating to                500-900&#8451;, introducing mixed gas of carbon                dioxide and superheated steam for physical                activation and keeping for 20-90 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for co-production process                of rice straw-based potassium humate and activated                carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accelerates the oxidation of                components in rice straw to humic acids, provides                chemical activation for the carbonization process                of the later reaction residue, increases the pore                structure of the activated carbon, and improves                specific surface area, utilizes rice straw                resources at a high value, fixes carbon and reduces                carbon, and helps achieve carbon peaking and carbon                neutralization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  grain size of the rice straw powder is 2-5 mm. In                  the step (b), the oxidant is a nitric acid                  solution with a concentration of 8-12 wt.%. The                  solid-liquid ratio of the oxidant and the rice                  straw powder is (1-1.5): (15-25). In the step                  (c), the solid-liquid ratio of the oxidation                  residue and the potassium hydroxide solution is                  (1-1.2): (10-15). The concentration of the                  potassium hydroxide solution is 3-5 wt.%. The                  mass ratio of carbon dioxide and superheated                  steam in the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and                  superheated steam is (1-1.5):(0.8-1).Preferred Method: The steps when using the                  reaction residue to produce and prepare activated                  carbon are specifically placing reaction residue                  in nitrogen atmosphere, and heating to                  500-900&#8451; at a rate of                  5-20&#8451;/minute, introducing the mixed gas                  of carbon dioxide and superheated steam, keeping                  for 20-90 minutes, and cooling to room                  temperature after the heat preservation is                  completed to obtain activated carbon material. In                  the step (e), the hot air drying temperature is                  55-65&#8451;.

PN US2022204653-A1
TI Bioreactor system for producing carbon-negative            bioplastic, has process chamber containing volume of            water, and electronic controller configured to direct            production of bioplastic in process chamber and to            control release of hydrocolloid.
AU MCCURDY C T
AE CHARLOTTE MCCURDY RES LLC (CHAR-Non-standard)
GA 202286338G
AB    NOVELTY - Bioreactor system for producing a                carbon-negative bioplastic, has a process chamber                (100) containing a volume of water (106); and an                electronic controller configured to direct                production of a bioplastic in the process chamber.                The electronic controller configured to: control                release of hydrocolloid (105), which is derived                from macroalgae, to the volume of water in the                process chamber through an inlet (103) on a surface                of the process chamber to form a solution (120);                cause a rotor stator disperser (110) to homogenize                the solution in the process chamber, where the                rotor stator disperser is submerged into the                solution via the inlet on the surface of the                process chamber; control temperature in process                chamber; direct a sieve (107) to facilitate                transport of the solution in the process chamber to                a mold (130); control cooling the solution; cause                the solution to rest for a time sufficient for                component of the solution to saturate and disperse;                and cause dehydration of the solution.
   USE - The bioreactor system is useful for producing                a carbon-negative bioplastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The bioreactor system or method provides for                the production of algae-based plastic that is                carbon-negative in the broadest scope; and provides                for the production of a bioplastic where there is                less carbon dioxide in the atmosphere because the                bioplastic was produced and the carbon dioxide                remains removed for timescales meaningful to                combatting the worst effects of climate                change.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                carbon-negative bioplastic production                process.
   Process chamber (100)
   Inlet (103)
   Hydrocolloid (105)
   Volume of water (106)
   Sieve (107)
   Rotor stator disperser (110)
   Solution (120)
   Mold (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Device: The bioreactor system                  further comprises the controller directing                  culturing of the hydrocolloid in a liquid that is                  not normally configured to harbor photosynthesis                  in nature, providing, at least in portion, for                  the bioplastic's carbon-negative property. The                  controller directs the sieve to facilitate                  transport of the solution in the process chamber                  to a mold further includes the controller                  directing transfer of the volume of the solution                  into the mold forms a seal at an area within the                  mold. It causes the solution to rest further                  includes resting the solution for up to 15                  minutes for the component of the solution to                  saturate and disperse. The controller is powered                  by renewably sourced electricity, providing the                  bioplastic's high material consistency and                  preserving zero emissions output from production.                  The bioreactor system further comprises coating                  the mold with a material, where the material has                  a binding affinity to the component in the                  solution. The controller causing dehydration of                  the solution further includes the controller                  controlling airflow, temperature, and relative                  humidity.Preferably, the controller monitors                  temperature in the process chamber using                  temperature sensors; and the controller                  monitoring pressure via pressure sensors in the                  process chamber. During a cooling operation, the                  controller directs cooling of the solution in                  communication by controlling the release of                  refrigerant in a refrigerator coupled to the                  process chamber; the controller causing the                  temperature of the process chamber to be reduced                  by varying speed of a drive motor of the                  refrigerator based on pressure and temperature                  readings from sensors in the process chamber. The                  controller is configured to respond to an over                  pressure condition by decreasing a temperature                  setpoint of the process chamber. The bioreactor                  system also comprises a compressor bank having at                  least one compressor configured to supply the                  refrigerant to the refrigerator, the refrigerator                  arranged to consume the refrigerant; the                  controller configured to regulate refrigerant                  supply to the refrigerator in response to                  temperature and pressure readings from the                  process chamber; and the controller causes a                  rotor stator disperser to homogenize the solution                  in the process chamber using one or more air lift                  pumps configured to create a radially outward                  flow within the process chamber to mix the                  solution. It further includes the controller                  receiving instructions from a remote server                  system that processes predictive algorithms to                  control a variable rate compressor motor based on                  temperature, pressure, and air quality readings                  in the process chamber to regulate the rate of                  release of heat, refrigerant, purge gas or oxygen                  in the process chamber via the variable rate                  compressor motor.Preferred Components: The hydrocolloid is at                  least one of: alginate, agar, or carrageenan. The                  mold is a glass and is configured to provide a                  high material reflectivity; and the mold has a                  smooth surface configured to resist capillary                  action, thus reducing shrinkage during the                  dehydrating of the solution. The macroalgae is                  cultured in a dead zone of the ocean.Preferred Composition: The solution has a                  concentration of 0.1-10%, where the concentration                  is a measure of dry mass per solution volume. The                  bioreactor system further comprises adding a                  second component to the solution at a                  concentration of 0.1-5%, where the concentration                  is a measure of the mass per solution                  volume.Preferred Parameter: The binding affinity to                  the component in the solution is a property of a                  material composition of the component or a                  geometry of the component.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Parameters: The bioplastic has an                  optical transparency, a tensile strength of                  100-300 KPa; and a carbon-negative footprint,                  where all operational energy inputs to produce                  the carbon-negative bioplastic are renewably                  sourced. The bioplastic having substantially                  optically transparency allows transmission of                  about 70-100% of light.

PN WO2021262078-A1; SE202050771-A1; WO2021262078-A9; SE544421-C2
TI Method for producing direct reduced metal material, involves supplying additional hydrogen gas to achieve and/or maintain predetermined pressure in furnace space.
AU MURRAY H E H
   MURRAY E H
AE GREENIRON H2 AB (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0928R
AB    NOVELTY - Producing direct reduced metal material involves: (a) charging metal material (142) to be reduced into a furnace space (120), onto a gas-permeable floor (151); (b) providing heat and hydrogen gas into the furnace space, so that heated hydrogen gas heats the charged metal material to a temperature high enough so that metal oxides present in the charged metal material are reduced, in turn causing water vapor to be formed; and (c) condensing and collecting the water vapor formed in step (c) in a condenser. The hydrogen gas is circulated in a closed loop upwards through the floor, through the charged metal material, and further via the condenser and a gas forced circulation device. The method further involves: (d) supplying additional hydrogen gas to achieve and/or maintain a predetermined pressure in the furnace space.
   USE - The method is useful for producing direct reduced metal material (claimed) e.g. carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, in a gas-permeable floor of a furnace space.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves a thermally and energy efficient method for direct reducing and carburizing of metal material that does not lead to the release into the atmosphere of carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide. The method is scalable to large throughputs and be capable of handling metal material of different constitutions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for producing direct reduced metal material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a simplified furnace.
   Upper portion (110)
   Furnace space (120)
   Fluidized bed (141)
   Charging metal material (142)
   Gas-permeable floor (151)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The gas-permeable floor comprises or is formed by a perforated ceramic plate or of woven metal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The method further involves: (e) continuously discharging reduced metal material from the furnace space. It also involves an initial metal material provision step in which the metal material is provided, such as by grinding and/or sifting, in a powder form. It further involves the initial step of evacuating an existing atmos phere from the furnace space so as to achieve a gas pressure of less than 1 bar, such as at the most 0.5 bar, inside the furnace space. In step (d), the provision of additional hydrogen gas is performed so that a pressure of greater than or equal to 1 bar builds up inside the furnace space. The method also involves evacuating gasses from the furnace space back to atmospheric pressure after the charged metal material has been reduced. It further involves a carburization step performed before an evacuation of gasses from the furnace space back to atmospheric pressure, in which carburization step a carbon-containing gas such as a gaseous hydrocarbon is provided to the furnace space, so that the heated and reduced metal material is carburized by the carbon-containing gas. Step (b) involves a first heating sub step, in which either the hydrogen gas or an inert gas is circulated through the charged metal material to heat the charged metal material, and a second reduction step, in which the hydrogen gas is circulated through the charged metal material to achieve the reduction. In the first heating substep, the charged metal material is heated to a temperature above the boiling temperature of water contained in the charged metal material, causing the contained water to evaporate. The hydrogen gas is preheated in a heat exchanger, where heat exchanger is arranged to transfer thermal energy from water evaporated from the charged metal material to the hydrogen gas to be provided in step (b). Provision of additional hydrogen gas in step (d) is performed until no additional hydrogen gas is required to maintain the predetermined pressure and/or until a predetermined amount of water has been collected in the condenser. Hydrogen gas circulated in the closed loop is heat exchanged to an external medium which is heated by the hydrogen gas. The hydrogen gas circulated in the closed loop is further dried in a gas dryer. The method further comprises a cooling step, in which cooling gas is circulated in the closed loop past the reduced metal material, cooling the reduced metal material, before removing the reduced metal material from the furnace space. Preferred Components: The flow of the hydrogen gas upwards through the floor and further through the charged metal material is arranged so that the charged metal material together with the hydrogen gas forms a fluidized bed (141). The fluidized bed is a bubble bed in which the charged metal material stays on the gas-permeable floor. It is a circulating bed in which the charged metal material is suspended above the gas-permeable floor, inside the furnace space. In step (a), the metal material is continuously charged into the furnace space. Preferred Parameter: The material after the initial metal material provision step has a mean particle size which is 10 mu m to 20 mm. Preferred Condition: The predetermined pressure is a pressure of greater than or equal to 2.3 bar, such as greater than or equal to 2.5 bar, such as greater than or equal to 3 bar. Steps (b) and (c) are performed during greater than or equal to 0.25 hour. Heat is provided in step (c) until the charged metal material reaches a temperature of 700-1100 degrees C, such as 800-1100 degrees C.

PN CN113769712-A
TI Preparing covalent organic framework compound and open caged fullerene composite material for e.g. absorbing carbon dioxide, comprises dissolving open-cage C60 and 3-(2-pyridyl)-5,6-diphenyl-1,2,4-triazine in solvent, heating, purifying, adding covalent organic framework compound, ultrasonicating.
AU SU Y
   LI Q
   QIAO S
   DONG X
   YANG J
   LI X
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 2021E8705R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing covalent organic framework compound and open caged fullerene composite material comprises (a) placing 1,3,6,8-tetra(4-carboxyphenyl)pyrene and p-phenylenediamine in a container, adding acetic acid and solvent-X for ultrasonic dissolution, pumping the container to a degassed state, and sealing, heating the sealed container at room temperature and reacting to obtain reactant-A, washing the reactant-A, soaking and drying in sequence to obtain covalent organic framework compound, (b) taking open-cage C60 and 3-(2-pyridyl)-5,6-diphenyl-1,2,4-triazine, adding solvent Y to dissolve, heating to reflux, cooling, eluting, purifying, secondary eluting to obtain reactant-B, dissolving reactant B in solvent Z, carry out high-pressure mercury lamp irradiation, rotary evaporating, eluting to obtain open-cage fullerene, and (c) dissolving the open cage fullerene in solvent W, adding covalent organic framework compound, ultrasonicating, stirring, washing, soaking, and drying.
   USE - The composite material is used prepare hydrogen in industrial electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction and absorbing carbon dioxide (all claimed) and as electrocatalyst for efficient hydrogen evolution.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material can solve the problem that the traditional electro-catalyst caused by dependent noble metal cost is too high, at the same time improves the catalytic activity of the electrocatalyst; has higher nitrogen element doping rate, larger specific surface area and hole volume, so it has better adsorbability, selectivity and repeatability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the solvent X is 1,4 dioxane or o-dichlorobenzene, the ultrasonic time is 0.5-1 hour, pumping is carried out under liquid nitrogen condition, the molar ratio of 1,3,6,8-tetra(4-formaldehyde phenyl) pyrene, p-phenylenediamine and acetic acid is 1:1-3: 10-20. The heating rate is 3-5degrees Celsius/hour, and the temperature after heating is 200-300degrees Celsius and the reaction time is 40-50 hours. The washing is to sequentially wash with acetone, methanol and 1,4-dioxane, the soaking is immersed in acetone for 10-15 hours, the drying is performed under vacuum conditions at 70-90degrees Celsius for 8-15 hours. In the step (b), the solvent Y is o-dichlorobenzene, toluene or chlorobenzene; the temperature of the heating and refluxing is 150-200degrees Celsius, and the time is 20-30 hours, the temperature after cooling is 20-30degreesC, the elution is to use carbon disulfide as the eluent, purifying, using silica gel column, the eluent of described secondary elution is that ethyl acetate and carbon disulfide are mixed with the volume ratio of 1:40-80 and the molar ratio of the 3-(2-pyridyl)-5,6-diphenyl-1,2,4-triazine and C60 is 10:3-8. In the step (c), the solvent W is at least one of chloroform, carbon tetrachloride and carbon disulfide, the temperature of described ultrasonic is 20-30degrees Celsius, and the time is 10-15 minutes, the temperature of the stirring is 20-30degrees Celsius, the rotating speed is 1000-2000 revolutions//minute, and the time is 20-30 hours, the washing is to sequentially wash with acetone, methanol and 1,4-dioxane, the soaking is soaking in acetone for 10-15 hours, the drying is carried out at 70-90degrees Celsius under vacuum conditions, and the time is 8-15 hours, and the mass ratio of the caged fullerene and the covalent organic framework compound is 1:0.5-2.

AB    NOVELTY - Cobalt-ruthenium bimetallic single-atom                photocatalyst comprises two-dimensional porous                network structure with a pore diameter of 2-10 nm,                it is composed of a carbon-nitrogen polymer carrier                and a carbon-nitrogen polymer carrier anchored on                the carbon-nitrogen polymer carrier, cobalt                ruthenium bimetallic monoatomic composition, the                chemical formula of the carbon nitrogen polymer is                g-C4N3, and the mass ratio of the carbon nitrogen                polymer, cobalt and ruthenium is (100 -150):                (2-3):1.
   USE - The cobalt-ruthenium double-metal mono-atom                photocatalyst is useful in photocatalytic reducing                carbon dioxide and reducing carbon dioxide                photocatalytic (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, is suitable for                large-scale industrial production, and the obtained                double-metal cobalt-ruthenium single atom                photocatalytic the reduction CO2 activity is                obviously improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cobalt-ruthenium doublemetal                single-atom photocatalyst comprises (1) adding the                ruthenium salt into formamide solvent, stirring                uniformly to obtain mixed solution A, (2) adding                cobalt salt in the mixed solution A obtained in                step (1), continuously stirring to obtain the mixed                solution B, (3), transferring the mixed solution B                obtained in the step (2) into the reaction kettle,                and placing it in the hydrothermal kettle for                solvent thermal reaction, the reaction temperature                is 160-200degrees Celsius, and the time is 16-20                hours, after finishing the reaction, naturally                cooling to room temperature, taking out, (4)                centrifuging the product obtained in the step (3),                cleaning, drying to obtain the cobalt-ruthenium                doublemetal single atom photocatalyst.

The                  cobalt salt is cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt                  dichloride, cobalt carbonate or cobalt oxalate.                  The mass ratio of the ruthenium salt to the                  formamide solvent to the cobalt salt in the step                  (2) is (2-6): (300-800): 1. The drying                  temperature in step (4) is 60-80degrees Celsius                  and the drying is 12-18 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Pyrazole-carboxylic acid organic ligand i.e. 3',5'-di(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)biphenyl-4-carboxylic acid of formula (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing metal-organic framework material as catalyst for cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): produces metal-organic framework material which has potential application value in molecular magnetic and catalysis aspects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pyrazole-carboxylic acid organic ligand i.e. 3',5'-di(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)biphenyl-4-carboxylic acid of formula (I) is new.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   1. preparation of (I);
   2. triangular pyrazole-carboxylic acid ligand based nickel metal-organic framework material i.e. nickel-3,5-bis(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-(1,1'-biphenyl)-4-carboxylic acid complex of formula ((Ni(HDBPC)(H2O)2)) (II). The crystal structure of the metal-organic framework material belongs to the monoclinic crystal system, the space group is C2/c, and the unit cell parameters are: a is 8.1550(9)Angstrom, b is 29.6221(3)Angstrom, c is 7.5149(3)Angstrom, alpha and gamma is 90degreesand beta is 93degrees; and
   3. preparing (II) comprisingcarrying out solvothermal reaction of organic ligand 3',5'-di(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)biphenyl-4-carboxylic acid and nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate in a mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and deionized water under sealed conditions to obtain final product.
   H3DBPC = 3,5-bis(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-(1,1'-biphenyl)-4-carboxylic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises firstly reacting 3,5-dibromophenylboronic acid with methyl 4-iodobenzoate to obtain 3',5'-dibromo-(1,1'-biphenyl)-4-carboxylate, then adding 3',5'-dibromo-(1,1'-biphenyl)-4-carboxylate and 1-(4-methoxybenzyl)pyrazole borate, potassium carbonate, tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0) and 1,4-dioxane into reactor, sealing, vacuumizing, carrying out inert gas protection, heating and reacting to obtain 3,5-bis(1-(4-methoxybenzyl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-(1,1'-biphenyl)-4-carboxylate, refluxing 3,5-bis(1-(4-methoxybenzyl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-(1,1'-biphenyl)-4-carboxylate with hydrochloric acid-ethanol solution, after the reaction is completed, and then hydrolyzing in a mixed solution of sodium hydroxide, water, methanol and tetrahydrofuran, and filtering to obtain final product.Preferred Components: Each nickel (II) ion in the metal-organic framework material is coordinated with oxygen atoms in 2 axial water molecules and 4 pyrazolyl N atoms from different ligands to form an octahedral configuration, each ligand is connected to two metal nickel (II) ion, and the pyrazolyl group of the ligand is connected to nickel (II) ion in the coordination mode of micro1-eta1:eta0, the metal-organic framework forms a one-dimensional chain structure with metal through nitrogen atoms on two different pyrazoles on the same ligand.Preferred Method: The molar ratio of organic ligand 3',5'-di(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)biphenyl-4-carboxylic acid to nickel chloride is 1:1-3, each 0.03 mmol of nickel chloride corresponds to 1-4 ml N,N-dimethylformamide and 0.1-1.6 ml deionized water, the temperature of the solvothermal reaction is 80-150degrees Celsius, and the solvothermal reaction time is 12-48 hours.
