AU LUO J
   FU S
AE SUZHOU DUCHAMPS NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8225Q
AB    NOVELTY - A reinforcing masterbatch comprises phase solvent containing 80-90 %mass polypropylene (PP) graft copolymer and 10-20 %mass graphene PP composite, where the ratio of graphene and PP in graphene-PP composite is 1:9, and the reinforcing masterbatch is graphene-PP-polyethylene terephthalate composite system.
   USE - Reinforcing masterbatch for BOPP film (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Since PP, PET and graphene are chemically bonded to form molten blend, enhanced reinforcing effect is achieved, and the BOPP film produced by using the reinforcing masterbatch has higher rigidity and ensures increased processing speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation method of BOPP film reinforcing masterbatch, which involves (1) surface-modifying graphene by surfactant, drying modified graphene to form stable dispersed modified graphene powder, and blending the modified graphene powder using PP high-speed mixer to obtain uniformly mixed graphene-PP composite material, (2) adding PP graft copolymer to graphene-PP composite, and mechanically blending to form phase solvent blend, (3) uniformly mixing phase solvent blend, homo polypropylene and polyethylene terephthalate (PET), and adding to two-stage extruder connected in series, heating and melting to form melt, and dispersing PET in graphene-PP for forming graphene-PP-PET composite system, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into front section of second-stage extruder and mixing with melt uniformly, and extruding the foaming and reinforcing masterbatch through die, where the injection volume of supercritical carbon dioxide is 10-50 g/minute, and injection pressure is 15-25 MPa; and
   (2) a BOPP film, which comprises 10-30 %mass reinforcing masterbatch for BOPP film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The extruder is tandem double-stage single-screw extruder or tandem twin-screw-single-screw double-stage extruder. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of extruder head is controlled at 200-260 degrees C, the speed of single-screw extruder is controlled at 20-60 rpm, and the speed of twin-screw extruder is controlled at 210 rpm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graphene is graphene solid powder modified by surfactant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (1), the surfactant is silane coupling agent KH-560 (RTM: gamma -glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The PP graft copolymer is at least one of maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene (PP-g-MAH), maleimide grafted polypropylene (PP-g-MI), acrylic acid grafted polypropylene (PP-g-AA), polypropylene grafted with glycidyl methacrylate (PP-g-GMA) and derivative of PP grafted maleic anhydride.

PN CN113144872-A
TI Fixing carbon of non-ferrous smelting flue gas            desulfurization gypsum comprises e.g. drying and            pre-treating non-ferrous smelting flue gas            desulfurization gypsum, removing free water, mixing            with lignite or high-sulfur coal, and filtering.
AU MA L
   ZHANG W
   NING P
   SUN X
   DAI Q
   TIAN S
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202191745S
AB    NOVELTY - Fixing carbon of non-ferrous smelting flue gas                desulfurization gypsum comprises (i) drying and                pre-treating the non-ferrous smelting flue gas                desulfurization gypsum for 0.5-1.5 hours at 100-110                degrees C, removing more than 97 wt.% free water                and mixing with lignite or high-sulfur coal in a                ratio of 1:3-5, adding into reactor for reduction                decomposition reaction, (ii) adding the solid phase                product obtained in the step (i) into a sealed                stirring hydrolysis reactor for hydrolysis reaction                to obtain alkaline solution containing calcium ion                and hydroxide ion, and (iii) precipitating the                hydrolyzed turbid liquid in the step (ii),                filtering to obtain clear solution, capturing                carbon dioxide in the flue gas by using the clear                liquid phase and generating light calcium carbonate                process.
   USE - The method is useful for fixing carbon of                non-ferrous smelting flue gas desulfurization                gypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low energy consumption, and the                prepared calcium carbonate product has high purity.                The method realizes low energy consumption                treatment of the non-ferrous smelting                desulfurization gypsum and reaches the purpose of                collecting and mineralizing the carbon dioxide in                the industrial tail gas, and producing the light                calcium carbonate with high added value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                device for fixing carbon of non-ferrous smelting                flue gas desulfurization gypsum.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                device for fixing carbon of non-ferrous smelting                flue gas desulfurization gypsum (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight percentage                  of calcium sulfate in the non-ferrous smelting                  flue gas desulfurization gypsum is not less than                  80%. The purity of calcium sulfide in the solid                  phase product obtained by the reduction                  decomposition reaction is greater than 90%.                  Preferred Method: The method ensure that the                  reaction device is in a sealed state before the                  flue gas carbon dioxide is captured, so as to                  prevent the acid gas in the air from reacting                  with the liquid phase.

PN CN112899220-A
TI Human tubal fluid (HTF) droplet useful for enhancing fertilization ability of mouse sperm, comprises mouse serpin family A member 5 (SERPINA5) artificially synthesized protein.
AU YAO B
   CAO S
   ZHAO S
   JING J
   WU R
   ZHANG G
AE PLA GEN HOSPITAL EASTERN THEATER COMMAND (PLAG-Non-standard)
GA 202168074X
AB    NOVELTY - Human tubal fluid (HTF) droplet (1 ml) comprises 1-2 mu g mouse serpin family A member 5 (SERPINA5) artificially synthesized protein.
   USE - The human tubal fluid (HTF) droplet is useful for enhancing fertilization ability of mouse sperm.
   ADVANTAGE - The HTF droplet improves ability of mouse sperm in vitro fertilization. The method provides a reference method for optimizing sperm quality during the in vitro capacitation process of assisted reproductive technology (ART).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing HTF droplets to enhance the fertilization ability of mouse sperm involves (a) making 200 mu l/drop HTF droplets in a Petri dish, covering the HTF droplets with tissue paraffin oil and then placing in an incubator to balance, (b) preparing mouse SERPINA5 synthetic protein powder with serum-free HTF into mouse SERPINA5 synthetic protein concentrate, where the concentration of mouse SERPINA5 synthetic protein concentrate is 240-260 mu g/ml and (c) adding mouse SERPINA5 artificial synthetic protein to the HTF droplets to make the concentration of mouse SERPINA5 artificial synthetic protein in the HTF droplets 1-2 mu g/ml; and
   (2) method for using HTF droplets to enhance the fertilization ability of mouse sperm involves preheating the HTF droplets with the mouse SERPINA5 synthetic protein concentration of 1-2 mu g/ml in the incubator with a temperature of 37 degrees C and a carbon dioxide content of 5%, mixing the mouse sperm, adding to the preheated HTF droplets and then placing with the mouse sperm in an incubator at 37 degrees C and a carbon dioxide content of 5% for 0.5-1.5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The HTF droplet (1 ml) comprises 1 mu g mouse SERPINA5 artificially synthesized protein. Preferred Components: The concentration of mouse SERPINA5 artificially synthesized protein is 250 mu g/ml. The concentration of mouse SERPINA5 synthetic protein in the HTF droplet is 1 mu g/ml. Preferred Method: The environmental control of the incubator is temperature of 37 degrees C and carbon dioxide content of 5%. The time to equilibrate in the incubator is 12-24 hours. The preheating time in the incubator is 0.5-1.5 hours. The duration of energy acquisition in the incubator is 1 hour.

PN CN112852148-A
TI Micro-foamed polyamide magnetic plastic used for            e.g. automotive application, comprises polyamide resin,            carbon dioxide affinity additive, phenolic hydroxyl            resin, filler, compatibilizer, nucleating agent, and            foaming agent.
AU LI S
   JIANG T
   ZHU N
   ZENG X
   SHEN C
   HE L
   GONG W
   ZHANG C
AE GUIZHOU MATERIALS IND TECHNOLOGY RES INS (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2021627028
AB    NOVELTY - Micro-foamed polyamide magnetic plastic based                on organic fluorine modification comprises basic                raw materials comprising polyamide resin, carbon                dioxide affinity additive, phenolic hydroxyl resin,                and filler and additive raw materials comprising                compatibilizer, nucleating agent, foaming agent and                other additives.
   USE - The micro-foamed polyamide magnetic plastic                based on organic fluorine modification is used for                automotive, electronic and electrical                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The micro-foamed polyamide magnetic plastic                saves material costs, reduces water absorption of                polyamide resin, and overcomes poor product                dimensional stability, poor surface quality, poor                foaming effect and reduced mechanical properties                caused by high water absorption of polyamide                composite materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the micro-foamed polyamide magnetic                plastic based on organic fluorine modification,                which involves (i) drying the polyamide resin raw                materials at 105 degrees C for 4 hours, taking the                basic raw materials and additive raw materials, and                uniformly mixing the raw material components except                the foaming agent in a high-speed mixer to obtain a                mixture, (ii) pelletizing the mixture prepared in                step (i) in an internal mixer and a single-screw                extruder unit to obtain a modified polyamide                composite material, where the extrusion temperature                is 200-280 degrees C, (iii) uniformly mixing the                dried modified polyamide composite material                prepared in step (ii) and the foaming agent to                obtain a foaming mixture, and (iv) performing an                injection molding process on the foaming mixture                prepared in step (iii) in an injection molding                machine to obtain an organic fluorine-modified                micro-foamed polyamide magnetic plastic, where the                molding temperature of the injection molding                process is 210-320 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler comprises                  MXene, ferrite magnetic powder, rare earth                  permanent magnetic powder, metal, metal oxide,                  carbon black, carbon nanotube, graphene, talc                  powder and/or glass fiber. The nucleating agent                  comprises silicon oxide, molecular sieve,                  sorbitol nucleating agent and/or rosin nucleating                  agent. The foaming agent comprises azo compound,                  nitroso compound, citrate, carbonate and/or                  bicarbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The pro-carbon dioxide                  additive is                  tetrafluoroethylene-hexafluoropropylene                  copolymer, PTFE resin, polyvinylidene fluoride,                  polychlorotrifluoroethylene,                  ethylene-chlorotrifluoroethylene copolymer,                  vinylidene fluoride-hexafluoropropylene                  copolymer, and/or vinylidene                  fluoride-tetrafluoroethylene-hexafluoropropylene                  terpolymer. The phenolic hydroxyl-containing                  resin comprises general phenolic resin, bisphenol                  A type boron phenolic resin an/or lignin resin.                  The polyamide resin comprises polyamide6,                  polyamide66, branched polyamide and/or                  hyperbranched polyamide. The other additive                  comprises light stabilizer, antioxidant, and/or                  scratch-resistant agent. Preferred Composition:                  The basic raw material comprises 60-90 %mass                  polyamide resin, 1-20 %mass pro-carbon dioxide                  additive, 5-10 %mass phenolic hydroxyl resin, and                  4-12 %mass filler. The amount of the                  compatibilizer and the basic raw material is                  0.05-5%. The amount of the nucleating agent and                  the basic raw material is 0.01-2 %mass. The                  amount of the foaming agent and the basic raw                  material is 0.05-4 %mass. The amount of the other                  additive and the basic raw material is 0.02-2                  %mass. The mixing ratio of the light stabilizer,                  antioxidant, and scratch resistant agent is                  1:1:1. Preferred Conditions: In the composition                  of the magnetic plastics, the dissolution and                  diffusion of carbon dioxide in the resin matrix                  are controlled by adding a carbon dioxide-philic                  additive to reduce gas escape, improving the                  foaming effect of polyamide composites, and                  adding phenolic hydroxyl resin to reduce the                  water absorption of polyamide resin. The                  compatibilizer is a maleic anhydride graft                  compatibilizer.

PN CN112844438-A; CN112844438-B
TI Erythrocyte-shaped bismuth vanadium            oxide/hm-C(CN)3Z heterojunction comprises            three-dimensional hollow skeleton bismuth vanadium            oxide and semi-metallic hm-C(CN)3 coating layer.
AU YANG Y
   XIONG L
   WU J
   TANG Z
   SHEN J
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2021626333
AB    NOVELTY - Erythrocyte-shaped bismuth vanadium                oxide/hm-C(CN)3Z heterojunction comprises                three-dimensional hollow skeleton bismuth vanadium                oxide and a semi-metallic hm-C(CN)3 coating layer.                The three-dimensional hollow skeleton bismuth                vanadium oxide is formed by the self-assembly of                BiVO4 nanoparticles. The semi-metallic hm-C(CN)3 is                uniformly coated on the surface of the                three-dimensional hollow skeleton bismuth vanadium                oxide, and the content of semi-metallic hm-C(CN)3                in the Z-type heterojunction is 1.5-4 wt.%.
   USE - The Z-type heterojunction is useful in                photocatalytic pollutant degradation, water                splitting or carbon dioxide reduction conversion                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Z-type heterojunction: has excellent                carbon dioxide capture, activation and                photo-generated electron, hole separation ability,                which can be applied to catalytic and energy                conversion field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the erythrocyte-shaped bismuth vanadium                oxide/hm-C(CN)3Z heterojunction comprising (i)                vigorously stirring, adding the ammonium                metavanadate dispersion into the bismuth nitrate                dispersion, stirring for 0.5-1 hours, adjusting the                pH value to neutral, continuously stirring for                0.5-1 hours, then the obtained mixed body at 200                degrees C water heat reaction for 3 hours,                naturally cooling to room temperature, centrifuging                and separating, washing, freezing and drying to                obtain erythrocyte-shaped bismuth vanadium oxide,                and (ii) adding the erythrocyte-shaped bismuth                vanadium oxide in ethyl acetate solution of                tricyanomethane imidazole ion liquid ultrasonically                processing for 30 minutes, steaming to remove ethyl                acetate, under the atmosphere of nitrogen, heating                for 1 hours at 400 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The scale of the                  Z-type heterojunction is between 2-3 mu m. In the                  step (i), the dispersants in the ammonium                  metavanadate dispersion and the bismuth nitrate                  dispersion are both polyvinylpyrrolidone. The                  molar ratio of ammonium metavanadate to bismuth                  nitrate is 1:1. The concentration of ammonium                  metavanadate in the dispersion of ammonium                  metavanadate is 0.5mol/l, and the concentration                  of polyvinylpyrrolidone is 25 g/l. The                  concentration of bismuth nitrate in the bismuth                  nitrate dispersion is 0.5 mol/l, and the                  concentration of polyvinylpyrrolidone is 25 g/l.                  In the step (i), the ammonium metavanadate                  dispersion is obtained by adding                  polyvinylpyrrolidone to a 0.5 mol/L ammonium                  metavanadate solution and stirring for a period                  of time. The ammonium metavanadate solution is a                  sodium hydroxide solution of 0.5 mol/l ammonium                  metavanadate. In the step (i), the bismuth                  nitrate dispersion is obtained by adding                  polyvinylpyrrolidone to a 0.5 mol/l bismuth                  nitrate solution and stirring for a period of                  time. The bismuth nitrate solution is a nitric                  acid solution of 0.5 mol/L bismuth nitrate. In                  the step (ii), the mass concentration of the                  ethyl acetate solution of the tricyanomethanated                  imidazole ionic liquid is 4 -10 g/l. In the step                  (ii), the mass ratio of bismuth vanadium oxide                  and tricyanomethanated imidazole ionic liquid is                  25:1-10:1.

GA 2021309539
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a thermal imaging camera (110) installed in a switchboard at a distribution facility to obtain thermal image information inside the switchboard. A photographing camera (120) is installed in the switchboard to take a picture of the inside of the switchboard. An image comparison and analysis unit (140) determines occurrence of fire in the switchboard by applying the image captured by the photographing camera to a neural network model built to determine whether the fire is occurred in the switchboard. An injection unit injects carbon dioxide into a packing tube. A control module (170) operates a compressor when the fire occurs inside the switchboard to discharge the air inside the switchboard to the outside and to expand the packing tube, where the injection unit is operated such that carbon dioxide is injected into the packing tube.
   USE - Artificial intelligence function featured switchgear fire monitoring and extinguishing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system analyzes an image or thermal image information captured inside the switchboard and determines whether the fire occurs in the switchboard based on detection information for detecting an internal environment of the switchboard so as to detect occurrence of fire more accurately.

AB    NOVELTY - The photoacoustic gas sensor device comprises                a measurement cell (2), an electromagnetic                radiation source (7) and a pressure transducer (6).                The measurement cell encloses a measurement volume                (3). The electromagnetic radiation source is                arranged to emit electromagnetic radiation into the                measurement volume. The pressure transducer is                arranged to measure a sound wave, which is                generated by the component in response to an                absorption of electromagnetic radiation by the                component in the measurement volume. A gas                permeable area (4) is provided in the measurement                cell for a gas to enter the measurement volume. The                gas permeable area is represented by a porous gas                permeable membrane. The porous gas permeable                membrane includes a sintered metal, a ceramic, a                polymer such as Polytetrafluoroethylene                (PTFE).

The gas                permeable area constitutes a non-reflective area                which leads to a decrease of the overall                reflectivity of the measurement cell, while                reducing the average optical path length and                reducing the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).

PN CN112342544-A
TI Low-temperature acid and alkali-resistant cast iron enamel processing includes selecting gray cast iron with comprising carbon, manganese, silicon, phosphorus and sulfur as castings, and applying enamel for bottom glaze and top glaze.
AU LV J
   ZHEN Q
AE QINGDAO YU JIN JINGTANG COATING CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202117467B
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature acid and alkali-resistant cast iron enamel processing includes selecting gray cast iron with comprising 3.4-3.7% carbon, 0.4-0.6% manganese, 2.3-2.5% silicon, 0.2-0.4% phosphorus and 0.01-0.1% sulfur, as castings; repairing sand hole, filling sand hole of less than 2 mm with semi-dry bottom glaze or repairing sand hole between 2-5 mm with carbon dioxide shielded welding; treating iron particles and sticky sand, using a grinding wheel to smooth iron particles, using carbon dioxide shielded welding to fill up exposed bubbles of casting, and fining to smooth molding sand and burrs that adhere to surface of casting; annealing, placing castings horizontally and securely in heating device, and heating continuously for 15-20 minutes at 750-800 degrees C; using small-capacity ball mill to grind glaze slurry for preparation of glaze slurry and glaze powder; applying enamel for bottom glaze and top glaze; and checking and packaging.
   USE - The method is for low-temperature acid and alkali-resistant cast iron enamel processing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, automatic operation, small labor intensity, and high working efficiency. The enamel surface is not light loss, after impact test, within 24 hours, the outer ceramic layer is not damaged, and has good strength and compactness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for low-temperature acid and alkali-resistant cast iron enamel coating formulation.

PN WO2021007848-A1
TI Preparing foamed microchannel film, comprises e.g. cutting microchannel film produced by mixing polylactic acid/polybutylene succinate, and blocking both ends of microchannel film, and placing microchannel film in carbon dioxide environment.
AU XU Z
   ZHAO J
   ZHENG S
   XU N
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021083549
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing foamed microchannel film in which cells and microchannels are interconnected, comprises (a) cutting a microchannel film produced by mixing polylactic acid/polybutylene succinate into predetermined length, and blocking both ends of microchannel film, (b) placing the microchannel film in carbon dioxide environment at 5-100 degrees C and pressure of 0.1-3 Mpa for predetermined time until the microchannel film is saturated, (c) raising the temperature to the foaming temperature of 110-135 degrees C and the pressure to the foaming pressure of 10-20 Mpa for predetermined time to make saturated microchannel film, and (d) releasing pressure for 1-30 seconds, cooling, and obtaining a microchannel film with foamed pores communicating with the microchannel.
   USE - The foamed microchannel film is useful in tissue engineering scaffolds, biomedicine and bionic engineering field.
   ADVANTAGE - The foamed microchannel film can be produced in simple forming and processing process with low cost and excellent performance, and has broad application prospects in the fields of tissue engineering scaffolds, biomedicine and bionic engineering.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for foamed microchannel film produced by the above method, where the foamed microchannel film includes multiple long straight microchannels (AA) and foamed pores (BB) interconnected with the microchannels, the diameter of the microchannel is 10-500 mu m, the foamed pores have diameter of 3-200 mu m, and open porosity of greater than or equal to 75%, and the diameter of the foamed pores is uniform.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the foamed microchannel film (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Microchannels (AA)
   Foamed pores (BB)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The pressure in the supercritical carbon dioxide environment in step (b) is 0.1-1.5 Mpa. The foaming temperature in the step (c) is 118-127 degrees C. The foaming pressure in the step (c) is 13-17 Mpa. The pressure relief time in the step (d) is 3-9 seconds. Preferred Components: The mass fraction of polybutylene succinate in the microchannel film is 5-35%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation of diaryl ether compound by            carbon-fluorine bond activation based on carbon dioxide            participation involves adding acetonitrile solution of            2-fluoropyridine compound in reaction container with            e.g. catalyst palladium acetate.
AU QIU J
   XU C
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 202101463X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of diaryl ether compound (I) by                carbon (C)-fluorine (F) bond activation based on                carbon dioxide (CO2) participation comprises adding                acetonitrile solution of 2-fluoropyridine compound                (A1) into a reaction container, sequentially adding                catalyst palladium acetate (Pd(OAc)2), alkali                cesium carbonate and phenylsilane (PhSiH3), using                CO2 gas to replace for 3 times, where CO2 pressure                is 1 atm, reacting for 24 hours at 25 degrees C,                after finishing the reaction, adding ice water                mixture to extract and extinguish the reaction,                extracting by methylene dichloride, combining the                organic phases and drying, decompressing and                distilling, and conducting silica gel column                chromatography to obtain target product white solid                diaryl ether compound.
   USE - The method is for preparation of diaryl ether                compound (I).
   ADVANTAGE - Compared with the prior art, the method has                mild reaction temperature, good selectivity, high                yield and easy industrialization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of diaryl ether compound of                formula (I) by C-F bond activation based on CO2                participation comprises adding acetonitrile                solution of 2-fluoropyridine compound of formula                (A1) into a reaction container, sequentially adding                catalyst Pd(OAc)2, alkali cesium carbonate and                PhSiH3, using CO2 gas to replace for 3 times, where                CO2 pressure is 1 atm, reacting for 24 hours at 25                degrees C, after finishing the reaction, adding ice                water mixture to extract and extinguish the                reaction, extracting by methylene dichloride,                combining the organic phases and drying,                decompressing and distilling, and conducting silica                gel column chromatography to obtain target product                white solid diaryl ether compound.
   R=CN or NO2.

AU DORIN M
AE OFF DORIN SPA MARIO (DORI-Non-standard)
   OFF DORIN SPA MARIO (DORI-Non-standard)
GA 2020854291
AB    NOVELTY - The multi-cylinder reciprocating compressor has housing (1) that is developed along a horizontal longitudinal axis and is accessible through an end cover. An electric motor is coaxially housed in the housing to rotate a crankshaft that actuates several pistons by the respective connecting rods that are slidably coupled in the respective cylinders. The cylinder is defined in the housing and is closed on the top by a respective hollow cylinder head (2) that is hermetically adjacent to a corresponding opening. The discharge openings are defined on one of the outer walls of the pressure chambers of two of the cylinder heads. The pressure chambers of the cylinder heads are connected to one another in a sealed manner by a distributor (3). The distributor is partially not in contact with the housing and defines a channel through which the ambient air flows and removes heat. The distributor is provided with a high pressure tap (4) and an overpressure safety valve (31).
   USE - Multi-cylinder reciprocating compressor used in carbon dioxide cooling circuit.
   ADVANTAGE - The multi-cylinder reciprocating compressor dissipates the heat generated by operation even when heat carriers reach high temperatures. The compressor is manufactured without using the components that are complex to manufacture.

PN CN110975918-A; CN110975918-B
TI Indium-zinc sulfide-nitrogen-doped graphene            composite photocatalytic material useful in catalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises            three-dimensional nitrogen-doped graphene foam and            two-dimensional indium-zinc sulfide nanosheets.
AU YU J
   XIA Y
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2020443312
AB    NOVELTY - Indium-zinc sulfide-nitrogen-doped graphene                composite photocatalytic material comprises                three-dimensional nitrogen-doped graphene foam and                two-dimensional indium-zinc sulfide nanosheets                which are vertically arranged on the surface of                nitrogen-doped graphene foam, where the indium-zinc                sulfide nanosheets and nitrogen-doped graphene foam                forms a two dimensional/three dimensional                heterojunction, the three-dimensional                nitrogen-doped graphene foam has a porous                structure, and indium-zinc sulfide has a hexagonal                phase.
   USE - The composite photocatalytic material is                useful in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite photocatalytic material:                exhibits excellent photocatalytic activity under                simulated sunlight; has excellent catalytic effect                and sufficient ultraviolet-visible-near-infrared                light-to-heat conversion efficiency; and enhances                utilization efficiency of sunlight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing indium-zinc sulfide-nitrogen-doped                graphene composite photocatalytic material,                comprising (i) subjecting surface of polyurethane                sponge after impurity removal pre-treatment to                hydrophilic treatment by ultraviolet irradiation,                (ii) uniformly dispersing graphene oxide in a mixed                solvent to obtain graphene oxide suspension,                soaking step (i) product in graphene oxide                suspension, taking out the sponge adsorbed with                graphene oxide, drying, calcining and oxygen plasma                cleaning to obtain three-dimensional nitrogen-doped                graphene foam, and (iii) dissolving inorganic zinc                salt and inorganic indium salt in a mixed solvent                to obtain salt solution, soaking step (ii) in salt                solution, stirring, adding sulfur-containing                organic matter, stirring uniformly, performing                hydrothermal reaction, two-dimensional indium-zinc                sulfide nanosheets grown on three-dimensional                nitrogen-doped graphene foam and post-treating to                obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between                  nitrogen-doped graphene foam and indium-zinc                  sulfide is 0.1-25:100. The thickness of the                  indium-zinc sulfide nanosheets is 5-20 nm and the                  pore size of the porous structure is 0.1-1 mm. In                  step (ii), the concentration of graphene oxide                  suspension is 0.02-2 mg/ml, and the mass-volume                  ratio between polyurethane sponge and graphene                  oxide suspension is 1:20-200 (g:ml). In step                  (iii), the mass ratio between nitrogen-doped                  graphene foam and indium-zinc sulfide nanosheets                  is 0.1-25:100. In step (iii): the inorganic                  indium salt is indium(III) nitrate, indium(III)                  sulfate, indium(III) chloride and/or indium(III)                  acetate, the inorganic zinc salt is zinc                  chloride, zinc sulfate, zinc nitrate and/or zinc                  acetate, the sulfur-containing organic matter is                  L-cysteine, thioacetamide, urea and/or dimethyl                  sulfoxide, the inorganic zinc salt is calculated                  based on zinc, the inorganic indium salt is                  calculated based on indium and the                  sulfur-containing organic matter is calculated                  based on sulfur, the molar ratio between                  inorganic zinc salt, inorganic indium salt and                  sulfur-containing organic matter is 1:2:4-10, and                  the concentration of zinc ions in salt solution                  is 0.005-0.1 mmol/ml; the salt solution further                  comprises ionic liquid, where the ionic liquid is                  1-carboxymethyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride,                  N-butylpyridinium hexafluorophosphate,                  1-(2-hydroxyethyl)-3-methylimidazolium                  tetrafluoroborate and/or sodium                  3-allyloxy-2-hydroxy-1-propanesulfonate, and the                  addition volume of ionic liquid accounts for 1-5%                  of the total volume of salt solution. Preferred                  Method: The pre-treatment of polyurethane sponge                  for removing impurities, comprises soaking                  polyurethane sponge in organic solvent, placing                  in an ultrasonic cleaner, ultrasonically cleaning                  using deionized water, and drying, where the                  organic solvent is methanol, ethanol or acetone.                  The ultraviolet irradiation conditions are: the                  power is 100-300 W and the irradiation time is                  1-60 minutes. In steps (ii) and (iii), the mixed                  solvent is composed of deionized water and                  absolute ethanol in a volume ratio of 1:1-9. In                  step (ii): the calcination temperature is 100-400                  degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-60                  minutes; the oxygen plasma cleaning time is 1-60                  minutes, and the power is 500-3000 W. In step                  (iii), the stirring rate is 100-300                  revolutions/minute, the hydrothermal reaction                  temperature is 90-200 degrees C, and the                  hydrothermal reaction time is 1-24 hours.

   USE - The culture medium is useful for production of blood culture (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing the blood culture bottle, which involves preparing a first reagent, preparing a second reagent, and filling a bottle, where first reagent is prepared by obtaining first reagent, adding purified water to prepare silica gel mixture, stirring the silica gel mixture, filling the stirred silicone mixture into culture bottle with carbon dioxide sensor and membrane that selectively penetrates carbon dioxide at the bottom, drying, heating the culture bottle to dry and solidifying the silica gel mixture at bottom of the culture bottle, sterilizing the dried and solidified culture flask, and the second reagent is prepared by adding purified water to reagent mixed solution, stirring the reagent mixture solution, adding solution to the culture medium preparation, sterilizing in a damp heat environment, filling the bottle for second filling, obtaining sterilized medium by the first reagent and the sterilized medium prepared by the second reagent, filling the sterilized medium to post-bacterial culture bottle, sealing the culture bottle after filling, labeling, attaching a label on the surface of the culture bottle after capping and capping product.

PN CN110849200-A; CN110849200-B
TI Guide structure of supercritical carbon dioxide pipeline heat exchanger, has guide vane whose both ends are provided with circular arc portion, such that arc direction of guide vane is set to face bending direction of U-shaped elbow.
AU CHEN W
   LUO X
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020198682
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a guide straight pipe (1) which is provided with a guide cavity inside and U-shaped elbow pipe (2) that is connected between the two guide straight pipes. A guide vane (3) is provided with arc-shaped structure, and embedded in the U-shaped elbow pipe. The integrated structure is formed with the U-shaped elbow pipe. The both ends of guide vane are provided with a circular arc portion (4) such that the arc direction of the guide vane is set to face the bending direction of the U-shaped elbow pipe.
   USE - Guide structure of supercritical carbon dioxide pipeline heat exchanger.
   ADVANTAGE - The impact of the centrifugal force of the fluid is reduced effectively on the pipe wall surface through the cooperative setting of the U-shaped elbow and the guide vane. The flow resistance is reduced between the fluid and the pipe wall surface so that the flow loss in the entire pipeline circuit is reduced, and the local heat exchange capacity is enhanced. The guide vane effectively restrains the drastic changes in physical properties brought by the supercritical flow loss received in the quasi-critical region so that the stability of the supercritical is maintained in the quasi-critical region.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the guide structure.
   Guide straight pipe (1)
   U-shaped elbow pipe (2)
   Guide vanes (3)
   Circular arc portion (4)

TI Thermal spraying system for use in manufacture of electrodes for thiation, used in lithium-ion secondary battery, has surface treatment unit that stabilizes surface of film, and winding-up unit that wounds base material after stabilization.
AU YOSHII N
   KAJI K
   KADOSAWA K
AE TOKYO ELECTRON LTD (TKEL-C)
GA 202012981L
AB    NOVELTY - The thermal spraying system (100) comprises a thermal spraying unit (31) used for thermal spraying of powder material, so that a film is formed on the first surface of a base material (W). A surface treatment unit (50) stabilizes the surface of the film. A winding-up unit (71) wounds the base material after stabilization of the surface of the film. The thermal spraying unit thermally sprays lithium powder material and forms a lithium film on the first surface of base material. The surface treatment unit stabilizes the surface of the film by changing the surface of the lithium film to the mixture of lithium oxide, lithium carbonate or lithium oxide, and lithium carbonate. The surface of lithium film is stabilized using water and inert gas, or by exposing carbon dioxide, water and inert gas or oxygen, water and inert gas or carbon dioxide, oxygen, water and inert gas.
   USE - Thermal spraying system for use in manufacture of electrodes for thiation, used in lithium-ion secondary battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The base material with which film is formed is wound up easily without lowering the quality of the film formed by thermal spraying.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a thermal spraying method; and
   (2) a manufacturing method of lithium-ion secondary battery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the structure of thermal spraying system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN110624550-A; CN110624550-B
TI Preparing in situ carbon-coated copper-nickel            alloy nanoparticles photocatalyst for photocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, involves preparing ethanol            solution of trimesic acid and aqueous solution copper            nitrate trihydrate.
AU MIN Y
   JIANG H
   SHI P
   FAN J
   XU Q
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ELECTRIC POWER (UYSI-C)
GA 2020026443
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing in situ carbon-coated copper-nickel                alloy nanoparticles photocatalyst involves                preparing ethanol solution of 0.01-0.05 g/mL                trimesic acid, aqueous solution of 0.1-0.2 g/mL                copper nitrate trihydrate (Cu(NO3)2.2H2O) and                0.1-0.2 g/mL nickel nitrate hexahydrate                (Ni(NO3)2.6H2O) in water. After mixing the three                together, pour it into a stainless steel reaction                kettle lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, put it                in an oven at 160-180 degrees C, and reacted for                8-12 hours. The product is collected and washed by                centrifugation, and then dried in vacuum oven at 80                degrees C to obtain the product copper-nickel 1, 3,                5-benzenetricarboxylic acid (CuNi-BTC), which was                taken out and stored in oven at 60 degrees C. The                CuNiBTC is put into the quartz tube, and after the                pressure in the quartz tube is evacuated, and                sealed the quartz tube. The sealed quartz tube is                heated to 450-750 degrees C at temperature rising                rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute in air atmosphere,                and calcined for 2-5 hours to obtain copper nickel                /carbon (CuNi/C).
   USE - Method for preparing in situ carbon-coated                copper-nickel alloy nanoparticles photocatalyst                used in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare in situ                carbon-coated copper-nickel alloy nanoparticles                photocatalyst, that has the advantages of simple                operation, low cost, non-toxic raw materials, and                products with certain economic value and production                in accordance with environmental protection                concepts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The centrifugal                  collection and washing are specifically                  centrifuging the precipitate for 10 minutes                  through a rotation speed of 8000                  revolutions/minute, and the obtained precipitate                  is sequentially washed, and centrifugal washing                  is performed at a controlled speed of 8000                  revolutions/minute for 10 minutes, and the                  centrifugal washing is repeated 7-8 times. The                  vacuum degree of the quartz tube is 1x 10-4-2x                  10-4 kilopascal, the calcination temperature is                  450-750 degrees C, and the heating rate is 2-5                  degrees C/minute. The copper-nickel alloy in the                  in-situ carbon-coated copper-nickel alloy                  nanoparticle photocatalyst has a nano-particle                  structure, and a graphite carbon layer is coated                  on the surface of the copper-nickel alloy. The                  particle diameter of the nanoparticles is 10-12                  nanometer, the thickness of the graphite carbon                  layer is 1-2 nanometer, and the particle diameter                  of the solid sphere formed by the aggregation of                  the nanoparticles is 2-5 micrometer.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

The solenoid valve, the flow regulating valve, and the nozzle are communicated in sequence. The use of toxic reagents is                prevented so as to improve environmental                protection. The energy consumption is saved. The                protection process cost is reduced. The protection                process is performed easily without harming the                operator.

PN CN110432478-A
TI Preparing beverage thickener by washing tender stems and leaves of Corchorus capsularis with distilled water, defatting with supercritical carbon dioxide, extracting flavonoids, filtering, decolorizing and drying in vacuum drying oven.
AU WANG M
   YANG J
   WANG Y
AE INST BAST FIBER CROPS CHINESE ACAD AGRIC (CAGS-C)
GA 2019969556
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a beverage thickener involves (a) washing the tender stems and leaves of Corchorus capsularis with distilled water, drying in a vacuum drying oven, pulverizing and sieving, (b) defatting the vegetable powder with supercritical carbon dioxide, adding defatted powder to an ethanol solution, ultrasonically extracting flavonoids, and suction filtering to obtain a filtrate and a filter residue, where the filtrate is a solution containing flavonoids, (c) drying the filter residue, mixing with distilled water, heating in a water bath, filtering, and concentrating by rotary evaporation to obtain a concentrate; adding a precipitation purification agent to the concentrate, mixing, refrigerating, precipitating crude polysaccharides, filtering, and mixing with the filtrate of step (b) for the subsequent extraction of flavonoids; and decolorizing and drying the filter cake in a vacuum drying oven to obtain crude polysaccharides.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a beverage thickener, which is used in a beverage (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not undergo the chemical synthesis process of the thickener in the beverages. The thickener contains a large amount of dietary fibers, proteins and trace elements, which further increases the function and nutrients of the beverage, and is safer and more reliable in the food field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing a beverage from the beverage thickener, comprising heating the beverage thickener, dissolving in water, adding to a 3-20% aqueous solution, and formulating into a beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The decolorization treatment step involves: dissolving the filter cake in water, and performing decolorization through a column packed with granular activated carbon at 50-65 degrees C, where the number of decolorization is 2-4 times, and concentrating the mixture by rotary evaporation at 44-55 degrees C and using the concentrate in the subsequent drying step. Preferred Components: The precipitation purification agent is an aqueous ethanol solution having an ethanol content of greater than or equal to 70%. Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the vacuum drying temperature is 45-55 degrees C, and the mesh number of the sieve is 50-100 mesh. In the step (b), the liquid-solid ratio is (10-30):1, the ultrasonic extraction power is 300-600 W, and the extraction time is 0.5-1 hour. In the step (c), the liquid-solid ratio is (15-25):1, the heating temperature is 70-90 degrees C, the heating time is 2-4 hours, and the rotary evaporation temperature is 45-55 degrees C. In the step (c), the precipitation purification agent is an aqueous solution of ethanol, the volume ratio of the concentrated liquid to the precipitation purification agent is 1:(1-5), the refrigerating temperature is 3-5 degrees C, and the refrigerating time is 12-48 hours. The vacuum drying temperature is 45-55 degrees C.

TI Method for catalytic oxidation treatment of exhaust gas from pressure-swing adsorption (PSA) decarburization device, involves entering outlet gas into heat exchanger and discharging at high altitude after heat exchange reaches standard.
AU LI Y
   ZHENG Y
   CHEN L
   ZHU D
   YU L
AE SICHUAN DKT ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201992229H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing exhaust gas from PSA decarburization device containing carbon monoxide, volatile organic compounds, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and sulfide with the calculated air into a heat exchanger (9) and mixing well. The mixed gas is heated by a heater (10) and passed into a desulfurization tower (11) to be contacted with an organic sulfur catalyst and an inorganic sulfur desulfurizing agent for desulfurization treatment. The desulfurized gas enters catalytic oxidation reactor (12) and reacts to form water and carbon dioxide. The outlet gas after the reaction of the catalytic oxidation reactor reaches the emission standard, in which the total hydrocarbon content of non-methane and carbon monoxide content have specified range. The outlet gas enters the heat exchanger to exchange heat with the PSA decarbonized exhaust gas, and is discharged at high altitude after the heat exchange reaches the standard.
   USE - Method for catalytic oxidation treatment of exhaust gas from pressure-swing adsorption decarburization device.
   ADVANTAGE - The problem of environmental protection compliance can be solved. The exhaust gas from the PSA decarburization device can be continuously and stably discharged.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for method for catalytically treating gas discharged from pressure swing adsorption decarburization device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the PSA decarburization device.

   Heat exchanger (9)
   Heater (10)

TI Magnetically stable support as support or carbon dioxide transporter, as oxygen transporter for active phase in reactions, is prepared by mixing stoichiometric amounts of manganese oxide and ferric oxide with respect to final support weight.
AU ADANEZ ELORZA J
   DE DIEGO POZA L F
   GARCIA LABIANO F
   GAYAN SANZ P
   ABAD SECADES A
   IZQUIERDO PANTOJA M T
   ABIAN VICEN M
   PEREZ VEGA R
AE CONSEJO SUPERIOR INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNSJ-C)
GA 2019784767
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetically stable support is represented by linear formula.
   USE - Magnetically stable support as a support or carbon dioxide transporter, as an oxygen transporter for an active phase in reactions with carbon di-oxide capture by carbonation/calcination cycles or in combustion reactions with oxygen transporters or in combustion reactions with solid oxygen transporters with generation of gaseous oxygen (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Magnetically stable support is represented by linear formula that is (MnxFei-xMy)3O4+5, where M is selected from Ti, Mg, Ca or Si, x has a value between 0.1 and 0.75 and has a value between 0.01-0.06, d has a value between 0.03-0.36 and support has a spinel-like crystalline structure. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for obtaining the support, which involves:
   (A) mixing stoichiometric amounts of manganese oxide and ferric oxide with respect to the final support weight;
   (B) adding one compound selected from a titanium oxide precursor, a magnesium oxide precursor, a calcium oxide precursor, a silicon oxide precursor, a silicon oxide precursor onto obtained mixture in a weight of 1-6 wt.% with respect to the final support weight;
   (C) thermally treating the product at a temperature between 1300-1400 degrees C for a period of 2-10 hours;
   (D) grinding obtained calcined product to obtain a particle size between 2-10 pm;
   (E) mixing and agglomerating the product with an active phase; and
   (F) grinding the product and selecting the particles of size between 100-300 pm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The value of x is 0.33-0.66. The support is taken in a weight ratio of 40-85 wt.% with respect to the final system. The active phase FA is an active phase in the form of particles selected from calcium oxide, lithium oxide, magnesia, or copper oxide, manganese oxide, ferrous oxide, and one cobalt oxide or nickel oxide, where particles have a size of 1-20 pm, or active phase FA is supported on a support which is taken in a weight of 15-60 wt.% with respect to the final system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 201968755A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a conduit arranged between an input unit (45) and an output unit for providing airflow (78) communication from the input unit to the output unit. An input part is in airflow communication with the conduit for providing additional input material i.e. carbon dioxide, to the conduit. A cross-sectional area of the output unit is equal to a cross-sectional area of the input unit for maintaining consistent airflow within a chamber. A first portion of the conduit between the input unit and a conditioning system is equal in volume to a second portion of the conduit between the conditioning system and the output unit. A cleaning orifice is arranged in a main body for forcefully providing cleaning fluid i.e. cleaning chemical, to the chamber.
   USE - Cultivation system for controlling airflow in a plant cultivation chamber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system achieves benefits of a windy environment suffer from drawbacks including uneven airflow resulting in bent stalks, so that cold air and carbon dioxide can be effectively or counterproductively can be supplied along an upward direction and contamination of a grow area can be prevented. The system avoids pushing of plants and induces morphological changes.

   (1) a cultivation method for controlling airflow in a plant cultivation chamber
   (2) a plant cultivation chamber
   (3) an airflow system

PT P
PN CN109772246-A
TI Metastable graphene-based photocatalytic reactor has defect-free densely packed graphene submicron composite film that is obtained by spraying layer of silicon nanoparticles on surface of graphene film by magnetron sputtering.
AU GAO C
   PENG L
   XU Z
   LIU Y
AE HANGZHOU GAOXI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019470293
AB    NOVELTY - The reactor has a defect-free densely packed graphene submicron composite film (2) that is posirtioned horizontally in the columnar insulating casing (1). The electrode plates are respectively connected to the positive electrode and the negative electrode of the power source. The side wall of the columnar insulating casing is provided with a gas injection hole (5). The defect-free densely packed graphene submicron composite film is obtained by heat-treating a graphene film having a carbon-oxygen ratio of 1.8-2.2, gradually increasing the temperature to 300 degrees at 1-5 degrees C/minute for 1-2 hours, gradually increasing the temperature to 1500 degrees C at 1-20 degrees C/minute for 1-2 hours after cooling to room temperature, continuously heating up to 3000 degrees at 1-5 degrees C/minute. The layer of silicon nanoparticles is sprayed on the surface of the graphene film by magnetron sputtering after maintaining for 1-2 hours.
   USE - Metastable graphene-based photocatalytic reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The strength of the graphene film is increased, thus improving the operability of the graphene film, the recombination time of the electron holes, the internal quantum efficiency, and light utilization efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for catalytically reducing carbon dioxide in photocatalytic reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of device for photoreduction of carbon dioxide by defect-free densely packed graphene submicron composite film.
   Columnar insulating casing (1)
   Defect-free densely packed graphene submicron composite film (2)
   Negative plate (3)
   Positive plate (4)
   Gas injection hole (5)

AU WANG G
   YAN C
   YE Y
   BAO X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201939344C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 0.5-10 wt.%, preferably 2-7                wt.% nickel, 0.5-10 wt.%, preferably 0.5-4 wt.%                zinc and 2-15 wt.%, preferably 6-10 wt.% nitrogen                three elements which are evenly distributed in the                carbon skeleton.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electroreduction of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high carbon mono oxide                Faraday effect and current density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving zinc                nitrate and nickel nitrate in methanol in specific                ratio, where molar ratio of zinc/nitrate is                1/8-8/1, preferably ranging from 1/1-4/1; (ii)                adding specific amount of 2-methylimidazole                methanol solution under stirring, where molar                amount of 2-methylimidazole is 2-10 times of the                total molar amount of metal zinc and nickel,                preferably in the range of 4-8 times and stirring                at room temperature for 12-36 hours, preferably for                18-26 hours; (iii) centrifuging, washing with                methanol for more than 1 time, preferably 2-3 times                and drying under vacuum to obtain a solid powder;                and (iv) taking 0.1-0.8 g, preferably 0.4-0.6 g                above solid powder in a quartz boat, placing in a                tube furnace under the protection of an inert                atmosphere of 50-100 ml/minute, preferably 80-100                ml/minute under inert gas (argon gas and/or                nitrogen gas), heating at 1-10&#176; C/minute                (preferably at 3-8&#176; C/minute) from room                temperature to 600-1000&#176; C (preferably at                a temperature of 800-1000&#176; C), maintaining                for 1-12 hours (preferably 4-10 hours) and cooling                to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar                  concentration of 2-methylimidazole in the                  2-methylimidazole methanol solution is                  0.067-0.334 mol/liter, preferably 0.134-0.267                  mol/liter.

A light irradiation part (13) irradiates light to the growth tank. AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency amplification reagent comprises natural-killer cell amplification reagent, gamma -ray inactivated recombinant K562 feeder cells, CD52 antibody, interleukin-2, CD16 antibody, CD3 antibody and CD56 antibody.
   USE - The reagent is useful for peripheral blood natural-killer cells in vitro operation (claimed) and killing tumor cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The reagent has good effect on targeted.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for in vitro operation instruction of peripheral blood natural-killer cells, comprising (i) isolating the separating peripheral blood mononuclear cell and peripheral blood mononuclear cell of human peripheral blood mononuclear cells from peripheral blood by lymphatic separation, and inoculating the peripheral blood mononuclear cell into the culture flask, and adding 10% autologous plasma and IL-2 with final concentration of 100 IU/ml, at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide; (ii) Induction and expansion of NK cells, resuspending the peripheral blood mononuclear cell in medium, adding natural-killer cell amplification reagent and adding 10% autologous plasma, placing in a 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator until the third day, adding half fresh supplement medium and IL-2, where the final concentration of IL-2 is 50 ng/ml; and (iii) adding fresh medium and IL-2 every 2 or 3 days later, maintaining the cell concentration at 5105 cells/ml or more, and recording the cell expansion factor and natural-killer cell purity every seven days, and collecting cultured cells by centrifugation on the 17th or 21st day of culture.

In the step (i), the inoculum concentration of peripheral blood mononuclear cell is 1106-1.5106 cells/ml. The natural-killer cell in vitro high-efficiency amplification reagent is recommended to use volume of 20 ml.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PT P
PN US2019039955-A1; US10787390-B2
TI Producing article of aerated composite material involves forming wet mixture, where wet mixture comprises water, grounded calcium silicate having synthetic wollastonite, pseudowollastonite, rankinite, gehlenite, belite, and alite.
AU DEO O
   VUONG D
   RAVIKUMAR D
   SAHU S
   ATAKAN V
AE SOLIDIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SOLI-Non-standard)
GA 201913816L
AB    NOVELTY - Producing an article of an aerated composite material involves forming a wet mixture, where wet mixture comprises water, a grounded calcium silicate having one or more of synthetic wollastonite, pseudowollastonite, rankinite, gehlenite, belite, and alite having a specific particle size, d10 in range from 0.25-5 mu m, d50 in the range from 5-20 mu m, and d90 in range of 25-100 mu m, and filler particles having calcium oxide or silica having a particle size (d50) in the range from 0.25-200 mu m, and an aerating agent. The method involves casting wet mixture in a mold, providing conditions for generation of a gaseous product from aerating agent by causing volume expansion of the mixture, pre-curing the expanded mixture at a temperature of 20-100 degrees C under an atmosphere of water and carbon dioxide for a time sufficient to result in a pre-cured object, and then de-molding and/or cutting the pre-cured object to desired dimensions.
   USE - Method for producing an article of an aerated composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce an article of an aerated composite material, which has excellent physical and performance characteristic of aerated concrete, wide availability of material, low cost, and is suitable for large-scale production with improved energy consumption, desirable carbon footprint and minimal environmental impact.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method further involves curing de-molded and/or cutting pre-cured object at a temperature of 20-100 degrees C for 6-60 hours under an atmosphere of water vapor and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The wet mixture further comprises a set-controlling agent, a bubble stabilizing agent, and a dispersing/viscosity-modifying agent. The wet mixture further comprises an additive selected from dispersing, rheology modifying admixtures, pigments, retarders, and accelerators. The grounded calcium silicate is selected from wollastonite, pseudowollastonite, rankanite, belite, and alite. The filler particles are selected from fly ash, bottom ash, slag having particle sizes ranging from 0.5-300 mu m. The filler particles are selected from limestone, miro-silica, and quartz having particle sizes ranging from 1-500 mu m. The filler particles are selected from aggregates having particle sizes ranging from 20-500 mu m. The aerating agent is selected from aluminum, iron, zinc in powder and or paste form, preferably aluminum paste. The aerating agent is hydrogen peroxide in liquid form. The set-controlling agent is selected from a gluconate and sucrose, preferably gluconate. The dispersing/viscosity-modifying agent is a polycarboxilate based material. The activating-agent comprises grounded lime. Preferred Conditions: The method further involves drying pre-cured object to remove residual water at a temperature of 20-110 degrees C for 12-96 hours after curing the pre-cured object. The curing of expanded mixture is performed under a pressure of ambient atmospheric pressure to 30psi above ambient and under a carbon dioxide concentration ranging from 25-99.5% to produce an aerated composite material. The pre-curing and curing is carried out under a dynamic carbon dioxide circulating flow condition. The wet mixture is formed by mixing the ingredients in specified order of addition, adding calculated portion of water, adding and mixing the set-controlling agent, adding and mixing the dispersing/viscosity-modifying agent, adding and mixing grounded calcium silicate, adding and mixing the filler particles to form a uniform slurry, and then adding and mixing the aerating agent. The pre-curing step is performed at a temperature of 20-80 degrees C, preferably 60 degrees C for 1-10 hours, preferably 5 hours under a vapor having water and carbon dioxide. The curing of pre-cured object is performed at a temperature of 20-80 degrees C, preferably 30-70 degrees C for 5-40 hours, preferably 10-30 hours under a vapor having water and carbon dioxide and having a pressure from ambient atmospheric pressure to 30psi, preferably 60psi above ambient atmospheric pressure. The strength imparting components consist primarily of calcium carbonate and amorphous calcium-silicate phases, calcium-silicate hydrate gel and calcium hydroxide at end of curing step. Preferred Compositions: The wet mixture comprises 40-95 wt.% grounded calcium silicate, 0-30 wt.% filler particle, 0-0.05 wt.% set-controlling admixture, 0.05-0.25 wt.% dispersing/viscosity-modifying agent and 0.05-0.30 wt.% aerating agent of wet mixture.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

TI Gas-adsorbing device comprises e.g. orderly connected gas-liquid separator, first reaction tower, second reaction tower, absorber and chimney, first reaction tower and second reaction tower has the same structure.
AU CAI Y
   CHEN H
AE CAI Y (CAIY-Individual)
   HANGZHOU YEGUANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201899542X
AB    NOVELTY - Gas-adsorbing device comprises orderly connected gas-liquid separator, a first reaction tower, second reaction tower, absorber and chimney. The first reaction tower and second reaction tower has the same structure and each comprises glass fiber reinforced polystyrene of tower body. The tower body is installed on the upper part of stainless steel chimney, the first reaction tower of steel chimney and the second reaction tower inlet through the absorption pipe. The absorbing pipe is close to one side of the second reaction tower is provided with a control valve. The control valve is controlled by the controller.
   USE - Used as gas-adsorbing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the adsorption capability of the adsorption tube and the absorber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing absorption main body comprising mixing copper nitrate, cerium nitrate, zirconium nitrate and absolute ethyl alcohol to form a first solution, mixing de-ionized water and citric acid to form a second solution, adding first solution into the first reactor, and the first reactor direct carbon dioxide pressure in the first reactor reaches to 15 MPa, controlling the first reactor temperature is 150 degrees C, adding supercritical carbon dioxide solution slowly into the first reactor and fully stirring to obtain the mixed liquid, immersing mordenite with honeycomb-structure in the mixed solution for 1 hour under the environment of 450 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain the absorption body.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the gas-adsorbing device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction tower and a second reaction tower is further provided with an absorbing tube. The adsorption pipe is formed by roasting through a continuous tunnel kiln of cylindrical pipe which is composed of hydroxymethyl cellulose and phenolic aldehyde active carbon fiber formed by the monomer mixture is sintered. The phenolic aldehyde active carbon fiber monomer length is less than 7 mm to facilitate the microscopic structure of generating fibrous. The absorbing pipe is set between the first reaction tower and a second reaction tower to the adsorption gas impurity and water vapor. The absorber in silk light zeolite as absorbing main body, the mordenite microscopic structure is a honeycomb surface on the cells of the honeycomb structure to form a nitrogen oxide adsorbent powder coating.

PN CN108827386-A
TI Indoor building model natural ventilation testing method, involves storing temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration data information in data storage module by display module to display data information.
AU WANG H
AE UNIV CHANGSHA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSG-C)
GA 2018917928
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves confirming initial value parameters to select a beta value. Five frequency points are selected as data points in a full rank matrix to judge whether the full rank matrix is in a reversible state. An initial value of the full rank matrix is iterated to estimate a matrix value. Intelligent indoor ventilation performance is adjusted by a ventilation control module. Ventilation performance parameters are analyzed by numerical calculation and linear fitting process to obtain indoor ventilation performance parameter temperature and relative humidity of condition change. Missing data is supplemented to generate different continuous data. The supplemented data is evaluated by linear fitting function. Temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration data information are stored in a data storage module by a display module to display data information.
   USE - Indoor building model natural ventilation testing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating total amount of indoor air by a ventilation amount calculating module so that a user intuitively knew ventilation quantity in a room so as to achieve ventilation effect by the ventilation control module and improve room temperature, and finely dividing an area in the room so as to adjust small divided regions in information collecting environment and small region adjusting direction, thus ensuring environment safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) an indoor building model natural ventilation testing device
   (2) a computer readable storage medium for storing set of instructions for indoor building model natural ventilation testing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating an indoor building model natural ventilation testing method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PT P
PN CN108728375-A; CN108728375-B
TI Culture of rumen flora in dairy cows, by culturing            rumen flora of dairy cows by double external flow            culture system, injecting fresh cow rumen fluid as            inoculum to saturated fermenter, fermenting, and            performing gauze filtration.
AU LI S
   LIU J
   GUO C
   JI S
   YAN H
   JIANG T
   WANG Y
   CAO Z
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
   UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 201888826T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for in vitro culture of rumen flora in                dairy cows, involves culturing rumen flora of dairy                cows in vitro by double external flow continuous                culture system, injecting 750 ml fresh cow rumen                fluid as inoculum to preheated and carbon dioxide                saturated fermenter, where the effective volume of                the fermenter is 780 ml, turning on the stirring                and carbon dioxide aeration device, where the gas                flow rate is 35-45 ml/minute, using total mixed                ration (TMR) diet of the rumen liquid donor cattle                as fermentation substrate, where the amount is                36-80 g/day, dividing feed into 4-6 times,                inputting the buffer into double external flow                continuous culture system, controlling the liquid                phase digestion enthalpy flow rate and the solid                phase digestion enthalpy flow rate to 4-10%/hour                and 2-7%/hour, fermenting the system at 38.5-40                degrees C for 4-10 days, collecting the solid phase                and liquid phase digestion, and performing gauze                filtration to obtain rumen-containing flora                culture.
   USE - The method is useful for in vitro culture of                rumen flora in dairy cows (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient for rumen                transplantation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preserving rumen flora in dairy cows, which                involves adding skimmed milk powder to the rumen                fluid of the cow or its in vitro culture,                freeze-drying the mixture to obtain rumen liquid                freeze-dried powder, which is sealed and protected                from light at 4 degrees C or room temperature,                where in vitro culture is prepared according to the                above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The agitation is performed                  at rotational speed of 25-30 revolutions per                  minute (rpm) in manner of agitation for 25                  minutes and intermittent for 5 minutes. The flow                  rate of the input buffer is 1.3 ml/minute, and                  the liquid phase digestion enthalpy flow rate of                  the flow filter is 0.65 ml/minute. The rumen                  fluid donor cow is Holstein cow that produces 3-4                  fetuses and has a lactation day of 60-80 days.                  The method involves collecting effluent solid                  phase and the liquid phase digestion enthalpy,                  intermittently shaking the mixture for 3-4 times                  in an oscillator, each time oscillating for 10-15                  seconds through a double layer and filtering the                  gauze to obtain culture containing the rumen                  flora. The formulation of the standard buffer A                  comprises 24.5 g/l sodium bicarbonate, 29 g/l                  potassium bicarbonate, 9 g/l monosodium phosphate                  and 10.2 g/l monopotassium phosphate. The mineral                  salt solution B comprises 47 g/l sodium chloride,                  57 g/l potassium chloride, 12.8 g/l magnesium                  chloride hexahydrate and 5.3 g/l calcium chloride                  dihydrate. The inoculum is obtained by batching                  freshly collected rumen chyme under a condition                  of carbon dioxide saturation and heat                  preservation in an oscillator, oscillating the                  mixture for 3-5 times, each time for 10-15                  seconds, filtering with gauze, and collecting the                  filtrate, which is inoculum. The amount of the                  skimmed milk powder is 4%. The method involves                  freeze-drying the mixture at -80 degrees C for                  2-4 hours, and vacuum-drying the mixture at                  degree of 10-12 Pa and -40 degrees C to -60                  degrees C for 48-60 hours.

The second circulating bed material is conveyed into a reducing furnace and an oxidation furnace. The gasification furnace conveys the first circulating bed material to the combustion furnace by a first circulation pipeline. A second circulation pipeline is inserted into circulating solid and gas branches through a gas-solid separator i.e. cyclone gas-solid separator. First and second circulating gas branches convey reaction gas i.e. Carbon dioxide-rich gas or oxygen-containing combustion-supporting gas to the reduction and the combustion furnaces. TI Detection device for gas hydrate e.g. carbon-dioxide, and/or ice in water containing medium, has analysis unit using characteristic values of filtered signals and temperature measurement to determine presence of hydrates and/or ice.
AU DELPOUX O
   FROT D

GA 2018485457
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a laser source (S1) arranged to irradiate a measuring point in a water containing medium, and two band pass filters (F1, F2) to extract the light intensities corresponding to the Raman spectra of two vibration modes of OH bonds, where the filters are placed on a path of a signal reflected by the measurement point. Avalanche photodiode (APD) detectors (D1, D2) store the signals filtered by the band pass filters, and a temperature sensor measures the temperature at the measuring point. An analysis unit analyzes the presence or absence of hydrates and/or ice at the measuring point, where the analysis unit uses the characteristic values of the filtered signals combined with the temperature measurement to determine the presence or absence of hydrates and/or ice.
   USE - Device for detecting the presence of gas hydrate e.g. carbon-dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and nitrogen, and/or ice in a water containing medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The analysis unit uses the characteristic values of the filtered signals combined with the temperature measurement to determine the presence or absence of hydrates and/or ice, thus allowing simple and early detection of gas hydrate formation, and hence enabling to apply effective remedial actions to mitigate hydrate problems.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device for detecting the presence of gas hydrates and/or ice in a medium.
