PT P
PN US2018169718-A1; WO2018118427-A1
TI Method for cleaning contaminants in pipeline located in e.g. underground, involves feeding first gas and second gas into pipeline, where cleaning gas is selected from group consisting of nitrogen and supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU ASLAM N
AE ASLAM N (ASLA-Individual)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   ASLAM N (ASLA-Individual)
GA 201848517X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding first gas e.g. cleaning gas, into a pipeline (10) and feeding second gas e.g. motive gas, into the pipeline, where the cleaning gas is selected from a group consisting of nitrogen and supercritical carbon dioxide, and the motive gas is lighter than the cleaning gas. The first gas and the second gas are injected into the pipeline by an injector, where the injector is located along length of the pipeline, the first gas and the second gas are injected at pressures ranging from 200 psig to 1400 psig and at temperatures of 15 to 35 degree Celsius and the first gas and the second gas are traveling through the pipeline at 30 to 70 feet per second.
   USE - Method for cleaning contaminants in a pipeline located in various locations such as underground, undersea or above ground. Uses include but are not limited to corrosion products, heavy solids e.g. asphaltene types of depositions, sand, dirt, salts and hydrocarbon gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing impurities or contaminants in size to dissolvable in reverse micro emulsion particles, and exit the pipeline when the gases are removed. The method enables easily venting gas at end of cleaning operations over solid pigging operations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for pigging a pipeline
   (2) a micro carrier composition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a gas pipeline to be cleaned.
   Pipeline (10)
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC P43-B99

PN CN108132683-A
TI Automobile interior air quality detecting device,            has main control unit whose output end is connected            with alarming device, air conditioning control end and            interface circuit, where output end of interface            circuit is connected to PC machine.
AU HAN L
AE DALIAN HANLIANG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 201846366G
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a sensor unit (1) provided with                a temperature sensor for detecting temperature                information in a vehicle, where the temperature                sensor utilizes a DS18b20-type temperature sensor.                A carbon dioxide concentration sensor detects                carbon dioxide information. An illumination sensor                detects illumination intensity information of the                vehicle, where the illumination sensor is selected                as a XDC-009-type light intensity sensor. The                sensor unit transmits data information to a main                control unit (2), where the main control unit                adopts a STC12C5A60S2-type single chip. An input                end of the main control unit is connected with a                key-press circuit (27), a clock circuit (26) and a                power supply circuit (25). An output end of the                main control unit is connected with a wireless                communication module (23), a display screen (24),                an alarming device (22), an air conditioning                control end (4) and an interface circuit (28). An                output end of the interface circuit is connected                with a PC machine (21).
   USE - Automobile interior air quality detecting                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device ensures better air environment in                the vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an                automobile interior air quality detecting device.                '(Drawing includes non-English language                text)'

PT P
PN WO2018096101-A1; JP2018083928-A; CN110023363-A; EP3545015-A1; US2021115181-A1; JP6921505-B2
TI Production of open cell rigid polyurethane foam used as e.g. building material, involves foaming composition containing polyol and foaming agent comprising water and adduct of amine compound and carbon dioxide, and polyisocyanate component.
AU WADA H
   ODANI K
   TANABE T
   YASUDA Y
   KOTANI K
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO GERMANY AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 201842842A
AB    NOVELTY - Production of open cell rigid polyurethane foam involves foaming a mixed liquid of a polyol-containing composition comprising a polyol mixture, a catalyst and a foaming agent, and a polyisocyanate component. The foaming agent comprises 10-80 parts mass water and 1-20 parts mass adduct of an amine compound having a primary or secondary amino group(s) and carbon dioxide, based on 100 parts mass of the polyol mixture.
   USE - Production of open cell rigid polyurethane foam (claimed) used as construction and building material, and as thermal insulation material for refrigerators.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables rapid, easy and efficient production of open cell rigid polyurethane foam having stable foam density, and excellent thermal insulation property, appearance and mechanical characteristics. The polyol-containing composition has excellent initial foaming property when mixed with the isocyanate component, and the reaction is not easily affected by changes in the conditions such as the thickness of the open cell rigid polyurethane foam, the ambient temperature and the mixed liquid temperature, during foaming. The open cell rigid polyurethane foam exhibits excellent performance in terms of contractile property. The method uses ethylene oxide which prevents contraction of the open cell foam. The compatibility of the polyol and water used as the foaming agent is improved. The polyurethane foam is lightweight and exhibits excellent forming workability and hygiene in the working environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for polyol-containing composition for producing open cell rigid polyurethane foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound is an alkylamine compound or an alkanolamine compound. The catalyst is an amine catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyol mixture comprises a polyol (A) and a polyol (B). Preferred Process: The polyol (A) is a polyol obtained by subjecting alkylene oxide to ring-opening addition polymerization using an initiator having 2-8 functional groups, and has a hydroxyl value of 100-900 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The polyol (B) is a polyether polyol which is obtained by subjecting alkylene oxide to the ring-opening addition polymerization using the initiator which does not contain a nitrogen atom, and has a hydroxyl value of 10-80 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The foaming is carried out by a spraying method. Preferred Properties: The mixed liquid of the polyol-containing composition and the polyisocyanate component has a cream time of 1.5 seconds or less. The open cell rigid polyurethane foam has a core density of 25 kg/m3 or less, a thermal conductivity of 40 mW/m.K or less, a closed cell ratio of 10% or less, and a compressive strength of 10-40 kPa. Preferred Composition: The mixture ratio (volume ratio) of the polyol-containing composition to the polyisocyanate component is 1:0.5-1:1.5. The polyol-containing composition further comprises a flame retardant and a foam stabilizer.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.)
MC A02-A; A08-B; A08-B03; A11-B06A; A12-R06; A12-S02D; A12-S02F

PT P
PN CN107937038-A; CN107937038-B
TI Polygeneration system for coal or biomass gasification and methane cracking, has steam generator which utilizes heat carried by high-temperature gas generated by gasifier to convert liquid water into gaseous steam.
AU ZHANG J
   REN M
   XIE W
   LI X
   MA X
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 201832742F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a steam generator (4) and a material tank (2) which are respectively connected to gas separator (5,7). The porous carbon-supported elemental metal catalyst is used in a methane cracking furnace (6) as a methane cleavage reaction catalyst, and methane is cracked to generate hydrogen and carbon fibers. The steam generator utilizes heat carried by high-temperature gas generated by the gasifier (3) to convert liquid water into gaseous steam. The hydrogen, methane, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide are separated and formed by first gas separator.
   USE - Polygeneration system for coal or biomass gasification and methane cracking.
   ADVANTAGE - The polygeneration system utilizes the technological conditions of coal or biomass gasification reaction to realize the preparation of the catalyst, and expands the preparation route of the catalyst, and has an important guiding role for the design and synthesis of the novel catalyst. The polygeneration system realizes the extension of the industrial chain of coal or biomass gasification reaction by catalyst preparation use of catalyst. The polygeneration system provides a pathway for collaborative conversion of coal and natural gas two kinds of carbon-based resources, and realizes multi-generation technology of co-producing various chemicals such as hydrogen, methane, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and carbon fiber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of polygeneration system for coal or biomass gasification and methane cracking. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Gasifier (3)
   Steam generator (4)
   Gas separator (5,7)
   Methane cracking furnace (6)

PN CN107844659-A; CN107844659-B
TI Method for constructing agent model for coal gasification slurry gasification reaction process, involves selecting oxygen-coal ratio, coal slurry concentration, selecting carbon monoxide content followed by utilizing optimization algorithm.
AU QIAN F
   ZHONG W
   DU W
   LI Z
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2018251760
AB    NOVELTY - An agent model constructing method involves selecting oxygen-coal ratio, coal slurry concentration, flow rate of coal slurry, molar ratio of hydrogen/carbon elements in coal, and oxygen/carbo in coal. The molar ratio of the elements and the ash content in the coal are utilized as input variables. The carbon monoxide content, carbon dioxide content, hydrogen content, and carbon conversion rate in the coal are selected and Latin hypercube is utilized followed by performing sampling method and utilizing improved particle swarm optimization algorithm, so as to construct the proxy model.
   USE - Method for constructing agent model for a coal gasification slurry gasification reaction process.
   ADVANTAGE - The model has high fitting accuracy, better tracking effect and strong model generalization ability, and better industrial operation guidance significance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An agent model constructing method involves selecting oxygen-coal ratio, coal slurry concentration, flow rate of coal slurry, molar ratio of hydrogen/carbon elements in coal, and oxygen/carbo in coal. The molar ratio of the elements and the ash content in the coal are utilized as input variables. The carbon monoxide content, carbon dioxide content, hydrogen content, exit temperature, and carbon conversion rate in the coal are selected as the target output variables, and Latin hypercube is utilized. Followed by performing sampling method on samples input variables, analyzing the input data, and utilizing Kriging agent model to establish the data model between the input variables and the output variables, and utilizing improved particle swarm optimization algorithm to solve the optimal agent model parameters, so as to construct the proxy model.

PN CN107814458-A
TI Treating municipal solid waste percolate involves            pretreating municipal solid waste percolate, performing            anaerobic bioreactor treatment and electrocatalytic            membrane reactor treatment to decompose organic matter            and ammonia nitrogen.
AU LI J
   WANG H
   HUI H
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2018239040
AB    NOVELTY - Method of treating municipal solid waste                percolate involves pretreating municipal solid                waste percolate by removing colloid, oil, suspended                solid and heavy metal from percolate through                coagulation sedimentation, performing anaerobic                bioreactor treatment by performing aerobic                bioreactor treatment and external ultrafiltration                (UF) membrane separation and performing                electrocatalytic membrane reactor (ECMR) treatment,                decomposing the organic matter into organic small                molecules or carbon dioxide and water and the                ammonia nitrogen into nitrogen, reducing the heavy                metals and further removing.
   USE - The method is useful for treating municipal                solid waste percolate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of treating municipal solid waste                percolate involves (a) pretreating the municipal                solid waste percolate having high concentration of                organic matter by removing the colloid, oil,                suspended solid and heavy metal from the percolate                through coagulation sedimentation, (b) performing                anaerobic bioreactor treatment by removing organic                pollutants under the action of anaerobic                microorganism and converting organic ammonia into                inorganic ammonia, (c) performing membrane                bioreactor (MBR) treatment by performing aerobic                bioreactor treatment and external ultrafiltration                (UF) membrane separation to remove high                concentrations of ammonia nitrogen, chemical oxygen                demand (COD), biochemical oxygen demand (BOD) and                suspended solids (SS), and (d) performing                electrocatalytic membrane reactor (ECMR) treatment                by subjecting the MBR effluent to electrochemical                oxidation and reduction of the ECMR, decomposing                the organic matter into organic small molecules or                carbon dioxide and water and the ammonia nitrogen                into nitrogen, reducing the heavy metals and                further removing, where the quality of the effluent                treated by the waste percolate can be discharged or                reused according to the relevant national or                industry standard.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In step (b), the                  concentration of the sludge is 5-40 g/l and the                  hydraulic retention time is 5-8 days. In step                  (c), the aerobic bioreactor treatment includes                  anoxic denitrification and aerobic nitrification                  (A/O), the ultrafiltration membrane is separated                  into an external system, and the concentrated                  liquid is returned to the anoxic or aerobic stage                  of the aerobic bioreactor, the intermediate                  sedimentation tank is provided between the                  aerobic reactor and the external ultrafiltration                  membrane and the sludge is returned to the                  pre-anaerobic reactor. In the aerobic bioreactor,                  the aerobic part uses blast aeration, the                  concentration of the dissolved oxygen is 2-5                  mg/l, the concentration of the sludge is 5-15                  g/l, the hydraulic retention time is 2-4 days and                  the sludge age is 10-30 days. In the UF membrane                  separation system, the pore size of all the                  separation membranes are less than 0.1 mu m. In                  step (d), the ECRM comprises a cathode, an anode                  and a direct-current stabilized voltage power                  supply and the electrode spacing is 5-30 mm. In                  the ECMR electrolysis device, the cathode is an                  auxiliary electrode and its shape is a tubular or                  a plate type, and it is in the form of a mesh, an                  arc, a cylinder or a hole and the material is at                  least one of carbon, graphite, stainless steel,                  metal titanium and metal alloy, the anode is a                  microporous membrane electrode having a pore size                  of 0.1-10 mu m, the shape of the anode is tubular                  or plate type and the material or the matrix                  material is at least one of carbon, graphite,                  metallic titanium, metallic nickel, metallic                  copper and metal alloy, the voltage of the direct                  current stabilized voltage power supply is 1-5 V                  and the current density is 0.1-10 mA/cm2, and the                  driving force of the membrane electrode (anode)                  is provided with the pump and the retention time                  of the feed liquid reaction is 2-50 minutes, the                  contaminants in the feed liquid are                  electrochemically decomposed or reduced during                  the process of permeating through the membrane                  electrode surface and from one side of the                  membrane to the other and the water quality is                  purified.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Processing co-producing syngas with carbon fiber or carbon micro-sphere comprises loading main catalyst component with carbon-based material, introducing raw gas, reacting, collecting, washing catalyst and drying.
AU ZHANG J
   SI X
   REN M
   WANG M
   TANG H
   MA X
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 201823095S
AB    NOVELTY - Processing co-producing syngas with carbon fiber or carbon micro-sphere comprises (i) loading main catalyst component on the carbon-based material or mixing uniformly with carbon-based material to obtain target catalyst, and (ii) introducing raw gas into fixed bed or fluidized bed reactor, carrying out reaction in fixed bed or fluidized bed reactor at constant temperature for 1-10 hours, collecting gas produced in the reaction process to obtain syngas, washing the catalyst, and drying, where the raw material gas comprises methane and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for processing co-producing syngas with carbon fiber or carbon micro-sphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes high catalytic activity and stability, the catalyst design is simple, economical and suitable for industrial application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing co-producing syngas with carbon fiber or carbon micro-sphere comprises (i) taking main catalyst component and carbon-based material in a mass ratio of 1:(1-20), and loading main catalyst component on the carbon-based material or mixing uniformly with carbon-based material to obtain target catalyst, and (ii) introducing raw gas into fixed bed or fluidized bed reactor under the action of target catalyst, the reaction pressure is normal pressure or micro-positive pressure, the reaction temperature is 750-950 degrees C, the reaction air speed is 5-100l/(gcat.h), carrying out reaction in fixed bed or fluidized bed reactor at constant temperature for 1-10 hours, collecting gas produced in the reaction process to obtain syngas, washing the catalyst, and drying, where the raw material gas comprises methane and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The main catalyst component is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide. The carbon base material is activated carbon, porous carbon, coal pyrolysis coke, coke and carbon black or graphite. The feed volume ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 15:(1-20). The methane and carbon dioxide are partially or totally derived from coke oven gas, biogas, natural gas, and oil field gas or coal mine pit gas. Preferred Method: The loading main catalyst component on the carbon-based material or mixing uniformly with carbon-based material by immersing or mechanical mixing to obtain target catalyst. The pressure is less than 2 kPa. The reaction catalyst is washed with 1-3 mol/l hydrochloric acid, nitric acid or sulfuric acid, filtering and washing with de-ionized water or distilled water to neutralize. The catalyst is separated by sieving or centrifuging for purification after drying.

TI Producing kvass involves mixing rye malt, barley malt, rye flour, sugar, concentrate of leavened wort, and drinking water, heat-infusing, adding lactic acid and baker's yeast, fermenting, and blending separated liquid phase with sugar syrup.
AU KVASENKOV O I
AE KVASENKOV O I (KVAS-Individual)
GA 2018128807
AB    NOVELTY - Kvass is produced by preparing ingredients, mixing rye malt, barley malt, rye flour, sugar, concentrate of leavened wort, and drinking water, heat-infusing in drinking water, adding lactic acid and baker's yeast, fermenting, phase-separating, blending separated liquid phase with sugar syrup, and bottling, where prepared fennel is extracted with liquid carbon dioxide, corresponding miscella is separated, prepared oat roots are dried in a microwave field to a residual humidity of approximately 20% at a microwave field power that heats oat root to internal temperature of 80-90 degrees C for greater than or equal to 1 hour.
   USE - Production of kvass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Kvass is produced by preparing ingredients, mixing rye malt, barley malt, rye flour, sugar, concentrate of leavened wort, and drinking water, heat-infusing in drinking water, adding lactic acid and baker's yeast, fermenting, phase-separating, blending separated liquid phase with sugar syrup, and bottling, where prepared fennel is extracted with liquid carbon dioxide, corresponding miscella is separated, prepared oat roots are dried in a microwave field to a residual humidity of approximately 20% at a microwave field power that heats oat root to internal temperature of 80-90 degrees C for greater than or equal to 1 hour, and oat roots are impregnated with separated miscella with a simultaneous increase in pressure, followed by de-pressurizing to atmospheric pressure with simultaneous freezing of oat root, crushed, and fed for infusion in an amount of approximately 14.4% of sugar consumption rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: Instead of prepared fennel, prepared ingredients may include, but not limited to, ginger, hop, coriander, thyme, tarragon, basil, cardamom, anise, motherwort, juniper, rosehip, hawthorn, thyme, vanilla, honeysuckle, oregano, viburnum, cinnamon, cocoa liquor, and lavender.

PN CN113563975-A
TI Separating and purifying fresh pepper oleoresin            involves subjecting fresh pepper oleoresin to dynamic            countercurrent extraction by supercritical carbon            dioxide fluid dynamic countercurrent extraction            separation system.
AU QIN G
   LI J
   REN K
AE SICHUAN DIDIR FOODS DEV CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021C7769G
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and purifying fresh pepper                oleoresin involves subjecting fresh pepper                oleoresin to dynamic countercurrent extraction by                supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid dynamic                countercurrent extraction separation system. The                fresh pepper oleoresin feeding speed is 40-60                kg/hour, CO2 flow rate is 400-600 L/hour,                extraction temperature is 40-50degrees Celsius and                extraction pressure 15-25 megapascals (MPa). The                separation I temperature is 35-45degrees Celsius                and pressure is 6-11 MPa. The separation II                temperature is 50-60degrees Celsius and pressure is                4-6 MPa (determined by the gas source, not                adjustable, and dynamic value is automatically                controlled by the system). The supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid dynamic countercurrent extraction                separation system includes a CO2 gas source unit                (100), a solid matter extraction unit (400), which                is connected to the CO2 gas source unit through a                pipeline, and reversely transports supercritical                CO2 to the solid matter extraction unit through the                pipeline.
   USE - Method for separating and purifying fresh                pepper oleoresin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate and purify                fresh pepper oleoresin with a separating efficiency                of 98.59-99.24 % and total yield of                99.08-99.17%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and purifying fresh pepper                oleoresin involves subjecting fresh pepper                oleoresin to dynamic countercurrent extraction by                supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid dynamic                countercurrent extraction separation system. The                fresh pepper oleoresin feeding speed is 40-60                kg/hour, CO2 flow rate is 400-600 L/hour,                extraction temperature is 40-50degrees Celsius and                extraction pressure 15-25 megapascals (MPa). The                separation I temperature is 35-45degrees Celsius                and pressure is 6-11 MPa. The separation II                temperature is 50-60degrees Celsius and pressure is                4-6 MPa (determined by the gas source, not                adjustable, and dynamic value is automatically                controlled by the system). The supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid dynamic countercurrent extraction                separation system includes a CO2 gas source unit                (100), a solid matter extraction unit (400), which                is connected to the CO2 gas source unit through a                pipeline, and reversely transports supercritical                CO2 to the solid matter extraction unit through the                pipeline. The liquid substance extraction unit                (700) is connected to the CO2 gas source unit                through a pipeline, and the supercritical CO2 is                reversely transported to the liquid substance                extraction unit through the pipeline. The                separation unit is connected to the solid material                extraction unit and the liquid material extraction                unit. The extracted material is separated from the                supercritical CO2 and the separated CO2 is passed                through the pipeline through the CO2 filter (102)                and the cooler (103) circulates back to the storage                tank (104).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid dynamic                countercurrent extraction separation system.
   Carbon dioxide gas source unit (100)

PN CN115032113-A
TI Measuring hydrogen-rich blast furnace coke            deterioration process used in e.g. technical field of            steel metallurgy industry development challenge,            comprises loading bottom and upper layer of material            layer with iron-containing raw material, paving coke            sample, and introducing inert gas.
AU LI L
   LAN C
   BI Z
   HU Q
   ZHAO J
   LV Q
   CHEN S
   YAO H
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNNS-C)
GA 2022B87635
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring hydrogen-rich blast furnace coke                deterioration process comprises (i) preparing and                loading sample by placing coke sample and                iron-containing raw material into crucible, loading                bottom layer and upper layer of the material layer                with iron-containing raw material, paving coke                sample in middle layer, charging, and placing                crucible in high temperature pit furnace, (ii)                testing by applying a load of 0.4-0.6 kg/cm2 to the                sample in crucible, introducing inert gas into                crucible to form a protective atmosphere for sample                in crucible, and heating to 400-600&#176;C,                switching the introduced inert gas into a reducing                gas comprising nitrogen, carbon monoxide, hydrogen,                carbon dioxide and water, stopping to feed carbon                dioxide into crucible, switching reducing                atmosphere to inert gas atmosphere, cooling the                sample to room temperature, and removing the load,                and (iii) calculating gasification rate of coke as                given in the specification.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring                hydrogen-rich blast furnace coke deterioration                process used in technical field of steel metallurgy                industry development challenge and important                requirement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: truly reflects quality change of                coke under hydrogen-rich smelting conditions;                provides technical support for selection of                hydrogen-rich blast furnace coke; simulates the                dissolution loss behavior of gasification reaction                of coke with carbon dioxide and water under the                condition of hydrogen-rich blast furnace gas;                detects the influence of different temperatures,                loads and atmospheres on the thermal properties of                the coke; measures coke degradation performance by                synthesizing multiple test results to obtain the                coke gasification rate; more realistically reflects                the deterioration process of coke in the                hydrogen-rich blast furnace; and provides a new way                for blast furnace coke quality detection.

PN US2022235931-A1
TI Converting liquid-hydrocarbon fuel into thermal            energy, comprises e.g. vaporizing fuel into air by            passing fuel and air through vaporizer, and passing            mixed hydrocarbon-fuel vapor and air into catalytic            combustor.
AU YELISHALA S C
   THOMPSON C D
   COUTINHO D H
   FOKEMA M D
AE ASPEN PROD GROUP INC (ASPE-Non-standard)
GA 202295255X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing                liquid-hydrocarbon fuel (12) and air (14) to a                vaporizer (100). The liquid-hydrocarbon fuel is                vaporized in the vaporizer to produce                hydrocarbon-fuel vapor. The hydrocarbon-fuel vapor                and air are blended to form a                hydrocarbon-fuel-vapor-and-air mixture. The                hydrocarbon-fuel-vapor-and-air mixture is                introduced to a catalytic combustor (102) including                a catalyst (118). The catalyst promotes oxidation                of hydrocarbon-fuel vapor to form a carbon-dioxide-                and water-vapor-containing exhaust and generate                thermal energy. The carbon-dioxide- and                water-vapor-containing exhaust and air are                introduced to a recuperator (101). The recuperator                transfers thermal energy from the carbon-dioxide-                and water-vapor-containing exhaust to the air to                produce heated air.
   USE - Method for converting liquid-hydrocarbon fuels                into thermal energy efficiently directed to                thermal-to-electric conversion device.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus can operate on a variety of                liquid hydrocarbon fuels, such as heating oil,                diesel, jet fuel, gasoline, kerosene, naphtha, and                alcohols, over a wide range of fuel throughputs.                The vaporizer readily evaporates the                liquid-hydrocarbon fuel into the air stream at low                temperatures, thus minimizing the risk of coke and                residue formation, and simultaneously mixes the                evaporated hydrocarbon vapor into air stream, and                reducing the likelihood of fuel-rich pockets within                the combustor. The porous structure within the                vaporizer/recuperator enhances the transfer of                thermal energy into the hydrocarbon-air mixture by                increasing the thermal conductivity on the                vaporization side of vaporizer or recuperator and                by promoting hydrocarbon stream turbulence as the                stream flows through tortuous pathways within the                porous structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                combustion apparatus for converting                liquid-hydrocarbon fuels into thermal energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional side view                of the liquid-hydrocarbon combustion                apparatus.
   Liquid-hydrocarbon fuel (12)
   Air (14)
   Vaporizer (100)
   Recuperator (101)
   Combustor (102)
   Catalyst (118)

PT P
PN CN113578047-A
TI Method for fast eliminating of carbon monoxide            poison product in long and narrow limited space            explosion, involves quickly capturing and oxidizing            monoxide to carbon dioxide gas to realize fast            elimination of monoxide.
AU WANG Y
   WANG T
   SHEN Z
   ZHOU F
   LI J
   CHEN X
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021C5613W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves identifying a dangerous                area with possibility of explosion accident in a                long and narrow limited space. The carbon monoxide                elimination section is set between an explosion                source point and a personnel concentrated area, a                refuge place or an escape route. The horizontally                arranged supporting plate (3) is built in the                carbonmonoxide eliminating section. The supporting                plate is hung on a top plate of a lane. The support                plate is provided with a limiting plate (2).                Multiple powder grooves (1) with carbon catalyst                powder are placed on the supporting plate. The                carbon catalyst in the powder groove is thrown in                the laneway to form a high-concentration carbon                monoxide catalyst powder cloud belt. The carbon                monoxide is oxidized to carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for fast eliminating method of carbon                monoxide poison product in long and narrow limited                space explosion such as gas outburst and                accumulation of suspended coal dust.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing explosion shock                wave as power source for throwing carbon monoxide                catalyst powder, thus avoiding use of power device,                high reliability, suitable for explosion disaster                accident, simple structure, convenient to use, and                easy to manufacture. The method allows the powder                groove to be crushed or turned over instantaneously                when the explosion accident happens in the long and                narrow limited space, so that precursor shock wave                generated by the explosion is crushed or lifted                over the powder grooves, thus quickly capturing the                carbon monoxide and oxidizing the carbon monoxide                to carbon dioxide gas, and hence realizing fast and                efficient elimination of the carbon monoxide in an                environment after explosion, and providing more                sufficient escape time for a trapped person.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the method for fast eliminating of carbon                monoxide poison product in long and narrow limited                space explosion.
   Powder grooves (1)
   Limiting plate (2)
   Supporting plate (3)
   Anchor rod (4)
   Fixing nut (5)

PT P
PN DE102019218297-A1
TI Electrolysis system for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, has control device that is designed to regulate back pressure valve such that definable overpressure is generated in middle chamber in relation to two outer chambers.
AU ECKERT H
   KRAUSE R
   LANDES H
   MAGORI E
   RELLER C
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2021582482
AB    NOVELTY - The electrolysis system (100) has an electrolysis cell (102) which is divided into three parallel chambers (2,12,10) by an anode structure and a cathode structure. Two outer chambers (2,10) comprise a support structure (23,24) which is arranged to mechanically support the anode structure and cathode structure. The middle of the three parallel chambers (12) is provided with an electrolyte inlet (16) and an electrolyte outlet (8) for a liquid electrolyte or an electrolyte-gas mixture to flow through. A back pressure valve (17) is provided in an electrolyte line going out from the electrolyte outlet. A control device is designed to regulate the back pressure valve such that a definable overpressure is generated in the middle chamber (12) in relation to the two outer chambers.
   USE - Electrolysis system for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - An outwardly acting force generated in the middle chamber acts on both the anode structure and the cathode structure in the respective direction of the outer chamber, so as to ensure good mechanical contact between the anode and the anode contact structure and the cathode with the cathode contact structure. Thus, the entire electrolysis cell is made non-positive due to the overpressure. The support structure ensures that the electrolysis cell is constructed in a non-positive manner even when no overpressure is generated in the middle chamber, while creating a certain shadowing effect on the electrodes and creating a flow resistance, the mechanical stability and resilience of the electrolysis cell are improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating electrolysis system for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrolysis system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Chambers (2,10,12)
   Electrolyte inlet (6)
   Electrolyte outlet (8)
   Back pressure valve (17)
   Support structures (23,24)
   Electrolysis system (100)
   Electrolysis cell (102)

PT P
PN CN112570004-A; CN112570004-B
TI Catalyst for catalytic conversion of gasoline to            produce more benzene, toluene and xylene (BTX)            comprises natural minerals, inorganic oxides,            faujasite-type structure molecular sieve and noble            metal.
AU WANG L
   LIN W
   TIAN H
   YAN J
   SUN M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202137198C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst for catalytic conversion of gasoline                to produce more benzene, toluene and xylene (BTX)                comprises 15-70 wt.%. natural minerals, 5-60 wt.%.                inorganic oxides, 10-70 wt.%. faujasite-type                structure molecular sieve and 0.01-10 wt.%. noble                metal.
   USE - Used as catalyst for catalytic conversion of                gasoline to produce more benzene, toluene and                xylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst realizes the catalytic conversion                of gasoline and utilization of carbon dioxide under                mild conditions; has higher carbon olefin yield and                it can produce benzene, toluene and xylene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing catalyst for catalytic                conversion of gasoline to produce benzene, toluene                and xylene comprising mixing the noble metal salt,                supported type noble metal, faujasite-type                structure molecular sieve, natural mineral,                inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor                mixing, pulping, spraying drying, roasting and                preferably introducing auxiliary agent, noble metal                salts and/or supported noble metals, faujasite-type                structure molecular sieves, natural minerals,                inorganic oxides and/or inorganic oxide precursors,                mixing, pulping and/or supported noble metals                containing auxiliary agent, FAU structure molecular                sieve, natural minerals, inorganic oxides and/or                inorganic oxide precursors are mixing and                pulping;
   (2) catalyst for the catalytic conversion of                gasoline to produce benzene, toluene and xylene;                and
   (3) catalytic conversion of gasoline                comprising contacting the gasoline and carbon                dioxide, reacting with a catalyst and optionally a                diluent gas, where the catalyst comprises catalyst                for catalytic conversion of gasoline to produce                more BTX, preferably the conditions of the contact                reaction including a temperature of 350-850 degrees                C, preferably 500-650 degrees C, a pressure of                0.1-2 MPa, preferably 0.1-0.3 MPa, a ratio of                carbon dioxide and gasoline of 0.22-3:1, preferably                0.2-2:1 and a gasoline mass space velocity of                0.3-10h-1, preferably 0.5-5 h-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  20-60 wt.%. natural minerals, preferably 20-55                  wt.%., 8-50 wt.%. inorganic oxides, preferably                  10-45 wt.%., 10-55 wt.%. faujasite-type structure                  molecular sieve, preferably 30-40 wt.%. and                  0.1-5wt.%. noble metal, preferably 1-3 wt.%.. The                  noble metal comprises gold, silver, ruthenium,                  rhodium, palladium, platinum, iridium and/or                  osmium, preferably gold, palladium and ruthenium.                  The faujasite-type structure molecular sieve is Y                  molecular sieve. The natural mineral comprises                  kaolin, montmorillonite, diatomite,                  attapulgite,sepiolite, halloysite, hydrotalcite,                  bentonite and/or rectorite. The inorganic oxide                  comprises silicon oxide, alumina, alumina-silica,                  zirconia, titanium oxide, boron oxide, amorphous                  silicon aluminum, aluminum phosphate, tungsten                  oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconium oxide, molybdenum                  oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconium oxide,                  molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten                  oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide,                  copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide,                  vanadium oxide and/or niobium oxide. The catalyst                  comprises 0.5-10 wt.%. auxiliary agent comprising                  group consisting of IIA group, IIIA group, IVA                  group, VA group, lanthanides, sccandium, yttrium                  , hafnium , tantalum, copper, manganese, rhenium                  , iron and cadmium, preferably calcium, iron,                  gallium, indium, bismuth, lanthanum and/or                  manganese. The supported-type noble metal                  comprises a 0.5-20 wt.%. noble metal supported on                  the carrier comprises alumina, silicon oxide,                  alumina-silicon oxide, zirconium oxide, tungsten                  oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconium oxide, molybdenum                  oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconium oxide, titanium                  oxide, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten                  oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide,                  copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide;                  vanadium oxide and/or niobium oxide, preferably                  aluminum oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide                  and/or tungsten oxide-zirconium oxide. The                  roasting atmosphere comprises air atmosphere,                  inert atmosphere and/or water vapor atmosphere.                  Preferred Method: The preparation method                  comprises (a) mixing noble metal salts and/or                  supported noble metals, natural minerals,                  inorganic oxides and/or precursors of inorganic                  oxides, spray drying to obtain solid product I,                  (b) mixing the FAU molecular sieve, remaining                  part of natural minerals, inorganic oxides and/or                  inorganic oxide precursors, spray drying to                  obtain the solid product II and (c) mixing the                  solid product I and the solid product II and                  roasting, preferably in the step (a) the amount                  of natural minerals 1-15 wt.%. of the natural                  minerals in steps (a) and (b), in the step (a)                  the amount of inorganic oxide and/or inorganic                  oxide precursor is 1-20 wt.%. of the inorganic                  oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor in step                  (a) and step (b). The preparation method further                  comprises (m) loading noble metals on FAU                  structure molecular sieves to obtain FAU                  structure molecular sieves containing noble                  metals and (n) mixing, pulping the noble                  metal-containing FAU structure molecular sieve,                  natural minerals and inorganic oxides and/or                  inorganic oxide precursors, spray drying,                  roasting, preferably the method further includes                  introducing an auxiliary agent into the catalyst,                  loading noble metals and auxiliary agent on FAU                  structure molecular sieves to obtain FAU                  structure molecular sieves containing noble                  metals and auxiliary agent. The method comprises                  (x) mixing, beating, spray-drying the FAU                  structure molecular sieve, natural minerals,                  inorganic oxide precursors and/or inorganic oxide                  precursors to obtain solid product a, supporting                  the noble metal on the solid product a, roasting,                  preferably introducing an auxiliary agent into                  the catalyst and loading the noble metal and the                  auxiliary agent on the solid product a. The                  roasting process is carried out at 400-800                  degrees C for 0.5-8 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-sheet array structure                copper sulfide/brass net electrode material                comprises dissolving sodium sulfide, sodium                hydroxide, and sulfur powder into deionized water,                and continuously introducing nitrogen to prepare                mixed solution, where sodium sulfide concentration                in mixed solution is 0.05-0.50 mol/L, sodium                hydroxide concentration is 0.05-0.50 mol/L, and                sulfur powder concentration is 0.01-0.25 mol/L;                placing pretreated brass net substrate in 20-65                degrees C constant temperature water bath for 16-30                hours, growing copper sulfide on brass net                substrate, taking out, washing with deionized                water, and drying in 40-80 degrees C oven, where                grown copper sulfide is a two-dimensional nanosheet                array structure, copper sulfide nanosheet is                hexagonal and uniformly dispersed, the loading                amount of active component sulfide is 0.50-0.60                mg/cm2, the brass net substrate is a net woven by                brass wire and of formula copper zinc                (Cu0.64Zn0.36) and has mesh size of 180-250                mesh.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) nano-sheet array structure copper                sulfide/brass net electrode material comprising                brass net as substrate, where the copper sulfide is                grown on the brass net substrate to form the                electrode material; and

PN CN111909651-A
TI Acrylic modified polyurethane resin glue comprises e.g. solvent, initiator, acrylic ester monomer, amino resin, bio-modified polyester polyol, carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol, isocyanate monomer and catalyst.
AU CHEN T
AE SHENZHEN IBOX TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3969L
AB    NOVELTY - Acrylic modified polyurethane resin glue comprises component A and component B. The component A comprises 50-70 wt.% solvent, 1-3 wt.% initiator, 5-10 wt.% acrylic ester monomer, 1-5 wt.% amino resin, 1-10 wt.% bio-modified polyester polyol, 1-5 wt.% carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol, 5-10 wt.% isocyanate monomer and 0.05-0.1% wt. catalyst. The component B comprises 5-10 wt.% isocyanate curing agent.
   USE - Used as acrylic modified polyurethane resin glue.
   ADVANTAGE - The glue improves = bonding performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the glue, comprising (1) adding the solvent to the reaction flask and stirring, raising the reaction flask to 75-77 degrees C, adding the initiator, stirring until it is completely dissolved, mixing the acrylate monomer and the initiator together and uniformly stirring, so that the initiator is dissolved in the acrylate monomer, adding the acrylic monomer to the reaction flask to keep warm for a certain period of time, continuously adding the acrylate monomers for a certain time for heat preservation reaction after the viscosity of the solution in the reaction flask rises and the temperature stabilizes, continuously dripping the remaining acrylate monomers for a certain time during the heat preservation reaction, adding the remaining initiators, continuing the heat preservation reaction for a certain period of time, and lowering the temperature to 60 degrees C to obtain a prepolymer with self-crosslinking properties, (2) adding the amino resin, the bio-modified polyester polyol, and the carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol to the above-mentioned prepolymer at one time, uniformly stirring, adding the catalyst and stirring, gradually adding the isocyanate monomer dropwise and controlling the temperature at 60 plus minus 2 degrees C to obtain the prepolymer containing terminal hydroxy and amino groups, and (3) adding the isocyanate curing agent to the prepolymer containing terminal hydroxy and amino groups.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) adding the solvent to the reaction flask and stirring, raising the reaction flask to 75-77 degrees C, adding 15%the initiator, stirring until it is completely dissolved, mixing the acrylate monomer and 70% the initiator together and uniformly stirring, so that the initiator is dissolved in the acrylate monomer, adding 1/3 of the acrylic monomer to the reaction flask to keep warm for 30 minutes, continuously adding 1/3 of the acrylate monomers into the heat preservation reaction for 1 hour after the viscosity of the solution in the reaction flask rises and the temperature stabilizes, continuously dripping the remaining acrylate monomers for the insulation reaction for 1 hour during the heat preservation reaction for 1 hour, adding the 15% initiators, continuing the heat preservation reaction for 1 hour, and lowering the temperature to 60 degrees C to obtain a prepolymer with self-crosslinking properties, (2) adding the amino resin, the bio-modified polyester polyol, and the carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol to the above-mentioned prepolymer at one time, uniformly stirring, adding 80% of the catalyst and stirring, gradually adding the isocyanate monomer dropwise within 2 hours and controlling the temperature at 60 plus minus 2 degrees C,adding the chain extender and remaining 20 % catalyst, heating the reaction for 1 hour, adding the coupling agent and antioxidant, stirring, and cooling to obtain the prepolymer containing terminal hydroxy and amino groups, and (3) adding the isocyanate curing agent to the prepolymer containing terminal hydroxy and amino groups. Preferred Components: The glue further comprises 1-3 wt.% chain extender, 0.5-1 wt.% coupling agent and 0.2-0.5 wt.% antioxidant.

TI Fuel cell system for production of electricity,            has electrolyzer cell whose anode is configured to            receive portion of anode exhaust stream and another            portion of hydrocarbyl feed, and to generate hydrogen            stream.
AU JAHNKE F C
AE FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 2020739446
AB    NOVELTY - The fuel cell system (100) has a fuel cell                (170) that includes a cathode (172) and an anode                (174) configured to receive a portion of a                hydrocarbyl feed and to output an anode exhaust                stream comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and                water. An electrolyzer cell includes a cathode and                an anode. The anode of the electrolyzer cell is                configured to receive a first portion of the anode                exhaust stream and another portion of the                hydrocarbyl feed, to generate a hydrogen stream,                and to remove carbon dioxide from the first portion                of the anode exhaust stream.
   USE - Fuel cell system for production of                electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The molten carbonate electrolyzer (MCE)                purifies the anode exhaust hydrogen by removing                carbon dioxide and water, thus hydrogen in the                anode exhaust to be recycled is allowed to produce                power, the fuel required is reduced and the                efficiency is increased. The proper small amount of                methane added to the MCE feed prevents additional                methane generation, reduces the heat release in the                MCE and a more even temperature profile. The high                efficiency is achieved, since the power produced                from the higher fuel utilization and hydrogen                produced from water in the MCE is greater than all                of the power used by the MCE. The fuel cell system                achieves an overall electrical efficiency greater                than 70%, by using the purified anode exhaust to                generate power increases the efficiency of the                system. Thus, the fuel cell system is provided                where clean, reliable energy is supplied and the                high efficiency reduces harmful CO2                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of operating fuel cell system to increase                efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                fuel cell system.

   Fuel cell (170)

   Anode of fuel cell (174)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The formulation comprises 1 g dry ice, and 30 g salt. Preferred Components: The dry ice is carbon dioxide and the salt is sodium chloride. Preferred Method: The pressure of the production equipment is 6250.5498 kPa.

PN CN110655647-A
TI New poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film is used as a gas separation membrane for gas membrane separation such as nitrogen separation in air separation, biogas separation, or separation of carbon dioxide gas in natural gas process.
AU CAO Y
   YE L
   WANG L
   JIE X
   YU C
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202006509D
AB    NOVELTY - Poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film (I) is new.
   USE - Poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film is used as a gas separation membrane for gas membrane separation such as nitrogen separation in air separation, biogas separation of carbon dioxide gas, or separation of carbon dioxide gas in natural gas process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film with better mechanical properties can be prepared by heat-treating a polyamide copolymer prepolymer membrane containing an ortho-position hydroxyl group at a lower temperature and under less severe heat treatment conditions. Adjust the proportion of diamine monomers and heat treatment conditions to obtain thermally rearranged poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) gas separation membranes with different properties. The permeability and selectivity of most membranes are close to or exceed the Robeson upper limit, and show excellent thermal stability and plasticization resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film of formula (I) is new.
   Q=single bond, or O, S, CO, SO2, Si(CH3)2, CH(OH), C(CH3)2, C(CF3)2, substitution or unsubstituted phenylene;
   A=aromatic ring group selected from the group consisting of a substituted or unsubstituted divalent 6-24C arylene group and a substituted or unsubstituted divalent 4-24C heterocyclic aromatic group, where the aromatic ring group, group can be aromatic group or two aromatic groups are fused and form a condensed ring, or two aromatic ring groups are bonded by a single bond, O, S, CO, SO2, Si(CH3)2 , CH(OH), C(CH3)2, C(CF3)2 and other functional groups; and
   m and n=molar fractions of the corresponding repeating units, and satisfy m greater than or equal to 0.1, less than or equal to 0.9, and n greater than or equal to 0.1, less than or equal to 0.9, and sum of m and n is 1.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film of formula (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): Method for preparing Poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film of formula (I) involves polymerizing an ortho-hydroxydiamine and an aromatic diamine with a terephthaloyl chloride solution, and sedimenting the resulting polymer solution in a non-solvent to form an ortho-hydroxy polyamide copolymer. The o-hydroxy polyamide copolymer is dissolved in an organic solvent and casting it into a film. The obtained film is heat-treated. Preferred Conditions: The poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film (I) has weight average molecular weight of 10000 to 200000. The poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer film (I) may be a block copolymer or a random copolymer. The solution polymerization is a reaction in an ice bath at -10 to 5 degrees C, preferably -4 to 4 degrees C for 1-5 hours, the solvent used may be N-methylpyrrolidone, N, N' -dimethylformamide and/or N, N'-dimethylacetamide. The non-solvent used in the sedimentation process is ethanol/deionized water, or a mixed solution of methanol/deionized water, and the volume ratio of alcohol to water is 1:4-4:1. The organic solvent is aprotic polar solvents N, N-dimethylformamide, N, N-dimethylacetamide and/or N-methylpyrrolidone. The heat treatment can be performed in an inert atmosphere (nitrogen and/or inert gas), or in an air atmosphere, the temperature is 1 to 20 degrees C/minute. The rate is increased from room temperature to 200-400 degrees C and the temperature is maintained for 1 to 3 hours.

PN WO2019204779-A1; US2021116144-A1; US11226128-B2
TI Indoor air quality system for use in building, has correlation module selectively identifying presence of correlation between occurrence of user in condition and one of temperature, amount of particulate, compounds, and carbon dioxide.
AU MORGAN S K
   PHAM H M
   BUTLER B R
AE EMERSON CLIMATE TECHNOLOGIES INC (EMEL-C)
   EMERSON CLIMATE TECHNOLOGIES INC (EMEL-C)
GA 201988255E
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a condition identification module indicating an occurrence of a user in a physical condition based on output from a microphone and a camera. A correlation module selectively identifies presence of a correlation between the occurrence of the user in the physical condition and one of temperature, relative humidity (RH), amount of particulate, amount of volatile organic compounds, and amount of carbon dioxide based on the occurrence of the user in the physical condition and one of the temperature, the RH, the amount of particulate, the amount of volatile organic compounds, and the amount of carbon dioxide.
   USE - IAQ system for monitoring IAQ and occupant conditions for a mitigation device for a residential or light commercial heating, ventilation, and/or air conditioning (HVAC) system in a building. Uses include but are not limited to a home security system, a proprietary handheld device for use by a contractor, a mobile computing device, a smart household device such as smart speaker, smart TV, and video gaming system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces the time when a HVAC system is out of operation and improves flexible scheduling efficiency for a customer and a contractor, where a monitoring service allows the customer to remotely monitor real-time data within the building, outside of the building, and/or control components of the system such as setting temperature and RH setpoints and IAQ setpoints for enabling or disabling heating, cooling, ventilation, and air purification operations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for realizing operation for an indoor air quality (IAQ) system for a building.

PT P
PN RU2692007-C1
TI METHOD FOR CYCLIC NITRIDING OF ARTICLES MADE FROM STEEL 08U IN GASEOUS MEDIA.
AU ALEKSANDROV V A
   OSTAEVA G YU
   ISAEVA I YU
AE UNIV MOSC STATE TECH AUTOMOBILE ROADS (UYMO-Non-standard)
GA 201964658Q
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to chemical heat treatment, namely, to gas nitriding of steels using nanotechnologies, and can be used in machine building, instrument making and other industries. Method for cyclic nitriding of articles from 08U steel in gaseous media involves depositing a copper-containing nanoparticle layer on a surface of articles as a catalyst, subsequent heating of articles n in furnace in atmosphere of ammonia and carbon dioxide to saturation temperature of 540650C, followed by isothermal exposure, during which saturation atmosphere is replaced cyclically in two stages in each cycle. First stage of the isothermal holding cycle is carried out in a saturating atmosphere of ammonia in a mixture with water vapor at a ratio of volumes of said components of 1:1, and the second step of said cycle is carried out in a saturating atmosphere of ammonia in a mixture with carbon dioxide at a ratio of volumes of said gases of 1:1. Said cyclic replacement of saturating atmosphere is repeated until nitrided layer of specified thickness is obtained, after that, products are cooled together with furnace. At the preliminary stage, a layer of copper-containing nanoparticles in the composition of the nanocomposite combined with macromolecules of the polymer pseudomatrix with further drying of the layer with formation of the nanostructure is applied on the surface of steel articles by dipping. Prior to beginning of said heating in atmosphere of ammonia and carbon dioxide, copper-bearing nanoparticles have spherical shape and diameter of 315nm, and after said heating and decay of polymer pseudomatrix form on surface of articles copper-containing layer consisting of discrete particles located at a distance from each other. In particular cases, poly-N-vinylpyrrolidone with different molecular weight is used as polymer pseudomatrix. Copper-containing nanoparticles used are copper or its oxides.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Intensified process of nitriding, as well as obtaining on steel surface uniform reinforced layer with increased thickness of monolithic zone of cermets while maintaining high strength characteristics.
3 cl, 9 dwg, 1 tbl

PN KR2019037523-A; KR2061914-B1
TI Methane gas reduction device of waste landfill, has gas neutralization layer and methane oxidation layer that are maintained in closed state, and gas distribution layer, gas neutralization layer, and methane oxidation layer are kept closed.
AU LEE N H
   KYU P J
   MOON S H
   PARK J
AE UNIV ANYANG ACAD IND COOP FOUND (UYAN-Non-standard)
   ECOWILL PLUS (ECOW-Non-standard)
GA 201934443F
AB    NOVELTY - The device (30) has several landfill layers that are arranged in a partial region of the waste buried layer to disperse the landfill gas recovered in the waste buried layer into a predetermined region. A gas dispersion layer (31) removes moisture contained in the buried gas. A gas neutralization layer (33) neutralizes one of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide contained in the buried gas. A methane oxidation layer (35) is arranged on the gas neutralization layer and contains methane oxidizing microorganisms which oxidizes methane gas contained in the landfill gas to carbon dioxide. A gas diffusion layer (37) is connected inside the gas dispersion layer. An air injection unit (40) injects air necessary for methane oxidation. The gas neutralization layer and methane oxidation layer are maintained in a closed state, and gas distribution layer, gas neutralization layer, and methane oxidation layer are kept closed.
   USE - Methane gas reduction device of waste landfill for reducing methane gas generated in waste decomposition process of waste buried layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The pH reduction by carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide is suppressed and the methane oxidation conditions are optimized, by providing gas neutralization layer composed of alkaline substance. Since the gas diffusion layer is operated in closed state, and the influence from outside environment such as rainfall and temperature condition is minimized. Since the negative pressure is kept constant by the air injection unit and the ventilation fan, and the gas diffusion layer controls the amount of injected air and the flow rate of the landfill gas constantly. Therefore, methane gas generation is reduced by the biological methane oxidation process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross-sectional view of the methane gas reduction apparatus of waste landfill.
   Methane gas reduction device of waste landfill (30)
   Gas dispersion layer (31)
   Gas neutralization layer (33)
   Methane oxidation layer (35)
   Gas diffusion layer (37)
   Air injection unit (40)

PN CN108659136-A; CN108659136-B
TI Preparing high-efficiency and low-toxic potato starch involves peeling potato tuber, slicing, and soaking in living water, taking out, placing in ultrafine dispersing machine, followed by adding deionized water, and stirring.
AU WANG Y
   ZHANG Y
   YOU X
   LI M
   ZHOU K
   WEI P
   YAN H
   XIE X
AE CHINESE ACAD TROPICAL AGRIC SCI AGRO-PRO (CTAG-C)
GA 201883629S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-efficiency and low-toxic potato starch involves peeling potato tuber, slicing, and soaking in living water for 10-12 hours, taking out, placing in an ultrafine dispersing machine, followed by adding 10 pts. wt. deionized water, and stirring at a speed of 2000-3000 revolution per minute for 35-45 minutes with viscosity of 800 mega Pascal, and forming sweet potato slurry. The enzymatic hydrolysis involves placing 1000 pts. wt. sweet potato slurry and 3-5 pts. wt. cellulase in an enzymatic hydrolysis tank, and introducing carbon dioxide gas and nitrogen gas at a pressure of 0.2-0.3 mega Pascal and 55-70 degrees C for 1 hours, and then adding 1-3 pts. wt. protease at 40-55 degrees C in constant pressure enzymatic hydrolysis 2-3 hours. The viscosity of material is reached at 150 mega Pascal to obtain enzymatic hydrolysate. The enzymatic hydrolysate is placed in a centrifuge, centrifuged at 1000 revolution per minute for 10 minutes.
   USE - Method for preparing high-efficiency and low-toxic potato.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-efficiency and low-toxic potato which has high efficiency, low toxicity, and provides industrial popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The precipitate is washed and placed in a vacuum spray dryer at a vacuum of (-)0.06 mega Pascal and inlet air temperature. The spray drying is carried out at 85 degrees C and a feed rate of 1 meter per minute to obtain potato starch having a water content of 0.3-0.8 wt.%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for enzymatic hydrolysis tank, which comprises tank body, tank lid, heating layer, a carbon dioxide gas inlet pipe, a nitrogen gas inlet pipe and an gas outlet pipe, and lid is disposed at top of tank body, where heating layer which is covered outer surface of body, and carbon dioxide gas inlet pipe, nitrogen gas inlet pipe and gas outlet pipe are all passed through lid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide gas and nitrogen gas is 3:1. The enzyme activity is equal to 7000 microgram cellulase, 8000 microgram cellulase protease. The washing is first washed with ethanol and then washed with pure water.

PN WO2018116993-A1; TW201834565-A; CA3045979-A1; CN110099575-A; KR2019097224-A; SG11201905494-A1; US2019297933-A1; JP2018557745-X; EP3560347-A1; HK40006885-A0; EP3560347-A4; TW748023-B1; JP7000344-B2
TI Manufacturing thickened carbonated beverage packed in container, by mixing aqueous solution of thickener with another thickener, dissolving carbon dioxide gas in stock liquid, filling and sealing container, and heating carbonated solution.
AU BATORI H
   ENDOU H
   SUGANUMA Y
   AIZAWA T
   ENDO H
AE DAIWA CAN CO LTD (DAIW-C)
   DAIWA CAN CO LTD (DAIW-C)
   DAIWA CAN CO LTD (DAIW-C)
GA 201850968N
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing a thickened carbonated beverage packed in a container, involves (a) mixing an aqueous solution of a first thickener at a temperature of 0-35 degrees C with a second thickener, which is insoluble in water at 0-35 degrees C but soluble in water at 60 degrees C or more, to obtain a stock liquid in which the second thickener is dispersed in the aqueous solution, (b) dissolving carbon dioxide gas in the stock liquid to obtain a carbonated aqueous solution, (c) filling the carbonated aqueous solution into a container and sealing the container to obtain the carbonated aqueous solution packed in the container, and (d) heating the carbonated aqueous solution packed in the container, so that the carbonated aqueous solution attains a temperature of 60 degrees C or more to dissolve the second thickener in the carbonated aqueous solution.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing a thickened carbonated beverage packed in a container (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method stably produces thickened carbonated beverage with a sufficient amount of carbon dioxide gas and without requiring special equipment. The thickened carbonated beverage does not spill out of the container while filling, hence the amount of beverage in the container does not vary, and is easily swallowable, hence it is suitable for swallowing disabled people.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the thickened carbonated beverage packed in container, produced by the method, where the carbonated beverage has viscosities (A) and (B) (MPa.second) when measured by an E-type viscometer at a temperature of 20 degrees C and at a shear rate of 50/second, and at a temperature of 20 degrees C and at a shear rate of 100/second, respectively, where the viscosity (A) is 100-300 and the viscosity (B) is greater than or equal to (0.75x A).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step of dissolving the carbon dioxide gas in the stock liquid is carried out by cooling the stock liquid to 5 degrees C or less. The heating step is carried out by heating the carbonated aqueous solution packed in the container for 1-40 minutes, so that the carbonated aqueous solution attains a temperature of 80-110 degrees C. In all the steps prior to heating, all the liquids including the second thickener are maintained at a temperature below 60 degrees C, preferably 0-35 degrees C. Preferred Components: The first thickener is chosen from welan gum, guar gum, cold water-soluble iota carrageenan, lambda carrageenan, xanthan gum, cold water-soluble tamarind seed gum, tara gum, and psyllium seed gum. The second thickener is chosen from locust bean gum, heat-dissolvable iota carrageenan, native gellan gum, low-methoxyl pectin, and heat-dissolvable tamarind seed gum. The stock liquid further comprises an additive. Preferred Property: The stock liquid/beverage has a pH of 3.5-4.6. The first thickener aqueous solution and the stock liquid have a viscosity of 20-150 MPa.second when measured by an E-type viscometer at a temperature of 20 degrees C and at a shear rate of 50/second.

PN CN107841496-A; CN107841496-B
TI Bio-energy two-step microalgae production involves inoculating microalgae to culture medium, illuminating, pumping carbon dioxide and oxygen, culturing, harvesting microalgae, drying, grinding, and adding hot water and phosphoric acid.
AU CHEN T
   WANG L
   YANG S
AE UNIV HEXI (UHXI-C)
GA 2018252325
AB    NOVELTY - A bio-energy two-step microalgae production comprises inoculating microalgae to culture medium, illuminating, pumping carbon dioxide and oxygen, culturing, harvesting microalgae, drying, grinding, adding hot water and phosphoric acid, stirring, centrifuging, taking supernatant, adjusting pH value, adding butanone, ethyl acetate and tetrahydrofuran mixed solution, and adsorbent, heating, stirring, filtering, taking aqueous phase, extracting, combining organic phase, drying, and decompressing and concentrating.
   USE - Bio-energy two-step microalgae production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is short, low-cost, environment-friendly and suitable for industrialization. The product has high combustion calorific value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A bio-energy two-step microalgae production comprises inoculating microalgae to culture medium, illuminating for 10-12 hours, pumping carbon dioxide and oxygen at volume ratio of 10:1, culturing at magnetic field intensity of 30 A/m from 0 A/m at rate of 2-5 A/m/min, reducing to 0 A/m, harvesting microalgae, drying, grinding, adding 70-80 degrees C hot water and phosphoric acid, stirring for 30-50 minutes, centrifuging for 20-40 minutes, taking supernatant, adjusting pH value to 8 using sodium hydroxide solution, adding 1:30:15 weight ratio of butanone, ethyl acetate and tetrahydrofuran mixed solution, and adsorbent, heating at 40-45 degrees C, stirring for 20-30 minutes, filtering, taking aqueous phase, extracting 2 times at 40-45 degrees C, combining organic phase, drying, and decompressing and concentrating. The culture medium comprises 200-300 pts. wt. sodium nitrate, 300-400 pts. wt. urea, 100-150 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 30-80 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 30-80 pts. wt. calcium chloride, 0.1-0.4 pt. wt. cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, 0.1-0.4 pt. wt. disodium ethylene diamine tetraacetate, 0.02-0.08 pt. wt. gum arabic, 0.01-0.05 pt. wt. seaweed polysaccharide, 0.01-0.05 pt. wt. carrageenan, 0.001-0.005 pt. wt. microelement and 5000-8000 pts. wt. tourmaline activated water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae is Chlorella, golden algae, Microcystis or Scenedesmus. Preferred Method: The bio-energy two-step microalgae production comprises inoculating algae to culture medium, illuminating for 10-12 hours at illumination intensity of 300-500 Lux, pumping carbon dioxide and oxygen at volume ratio of 10:1, culturing at 25-30 degrees C at magnetic field intensity of 30 A/m from 0 A/m at rate of 2-5 A/m/min, reducing to 0 A/m, harvesting microalgae, drying, grinding, adding 70-80 degrees C hot water and 60-80 wt.% phosphoric acid, stirring for 30-50 minutes, centrifuging for 20-40 minutes, taking supernatant, adjusting pH value to 8 using 50-60 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution, adding 1:30:15 weight ratio of butanone, ethyl acetate and tetrahydrofuran mixed solution, and adsorbent comprising 5-15 pts. wt. zeolite, 10-20 pts. wt. activated clay and 40-50 pts. wt. coconut shell activated carbon, heating at 40-45 degrees C, stirring for 20-30 minutes, filtering, taking aqueous phase, extracting 2 times at 40-45 degrees C, combining organic phase, drying, and decompressing and concentrating.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
