PN CN114568513-A
TI Aerated beverage useful for hydration of            macromolecular substances, comprises zero fat milk            component, concentrated lactic acid bacteria            fermentation liquid, alpha-cyclodextrin, soluble            soybean polysaccharide, carbon dioxide,sweetness            modifiers and at least one of acidity regulators.
AU MENG D
   YAN P
   HOU T
   FAN Q
   BAGENNA
   LI J
   WEI Z
AE INNER MONGOLIA YILI IND GROUP CO LTD (IMYL-C)
GA 202284964S
AB    NOVELTY - Aerated beverage comprises 3-18.5 wt.% zero                fat milk component, 1-5 wt.% concentrated lactic                acid bacteria fermentation liquid, 0.2-0.6 wt.%                alpha-cyclodextrin, 1.5-4 wt. soluble soybean                polysaccharide, and 2-4 wt.% carbon dioxide. The                milk protein content of the milk-containing aerated                beverage is 0.3-1.2%.
   USE - The aerated beverage is useful for hydration                of macromolecular substances e.g. protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerated beverage: has good air bubble,                flavor and good taste, improved good flavor and                nutritional value and high protein content; and                maintains the quality and taste of product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing aerated beverage, comprising (i) heating                the ingredient water to 60-65degrees Celsius,                adding soluble soybean polysaccharide in a fully                stirred state, using chemical equipment for 15-20                minutes to make the material liquid into a uniform                mixed liquid, and cooling the material to below                20degrees Celsius after mixing; (ii) mixing skim                milk or reduced milk and gum liquid evenly in the                state of turning on stirring, adding concentrated                lactic acid bacteria fermentation liquid and                alpha-cyclodextrin, diluting the volume of the                mixture to 70% of the batching amount with the                batching water, and mixing and stirring the                material and liquid evenly; (iii) diluting the                above-mentioned mixed materials to the batching                amount with the batching water, and stirring                evenly; (iv) sterilizing the material by                homogenization and ultra-high temperature, where                the homogenization temperature is 70-75degrees                Celsius, adjusting the homogenization pressure to                the secondary pressure at 40 bar, and the primary                pressure to 200 bar and sterilizing at                110-112degrees Celsius for 4 seconds; (v) cooling                the feed liquid to 2-7degrees Celsius and mixing                carbon dioxide gas online to carry out carbonation                and inflation according to the gas content                requirements of the final product; and (vi) filling                at 2-10degrees Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The zero-fat milk                  component comprises at least one of skimmed milk                  powder and/or skim milk. The milk-containing                  aerated beverage further comprises at least one                  of sweetness modifiers comprising 0-80 wt.% white                  granulated sugar, 0-50 wt.% erythritol, 0-0.3                  wt.% sucralose and 0-60 wt.% fructose syrup and                  at least one of acidity regulators comprising                  0.5-3 wt.% citric acid, 0-3 wt.% lactic acid,                  0.2-0.5 wt.% potassium sorbate and 0-2 wt.%                  edible flavor.Preferred Method: The step (i) before                  comprises heating the ingredient water to                  50-55degrees Celsius according to skim milk                  powder and drinking water is 1:4-1:9, adding skim                  milk powder under full stirring, stirring the                  mixture for 15-20 minutes to fully dissolve the                  material liquid, adding the dissolved reduced                  milk at 45-55degrees Celsius for hydration for                  25-30 minutes, filtering the reduced milk with a                  60-80 meshes single or double filter after                  hydration, and cooling it to 1-12degrees Celsius                  and step (i) comprises heating the ingredient                  water that accounts for 20-25% of the ingredient                  amount to 60-65degrees Celsius, adding white                  sugar, erythritol and soluble soybean                  polysaccharide in a fully stirred state, using a                  mixture with mixing, dispersing, shearing and                  emulsifying effect of the material chemical                  equipment, chemical material for 15-20 minutes,                  so that the material liquid becomes a uniform                  mixture without visible particles, and cooling                  the material to below 20degrees Celsius after                  chemical materialization. The step (ii) comprises                  adding sucralose, potassium sorbate while adding                  concentrated lactic acid bacteria fermentation                  broth and alpha-cyclodextrin. The preparation of                  aerated beverage between step (ii) and step (iii)                  comprises preparing acid solution comprising                  dissolving the citric acid with the mixing water                  equivalent to 10 times the volume of the acid for                  10-15 minutes and carrying out on-line acid                  adjustment by mixing the acid solution and the                  milk glue mixture on-line according to the ratio                  of 1:2.5-1:3.5 and cooling to 1-12degrees Celsius                  and step (iii) comprises adjusting the mixed                  material obtained by preparing the acid solution                  and adjusting the acid on-line to the batching                  amount with the batching water and stirring well                  after adding the flavoring.

PN CN114486794-A
TI Device for detecting co-gasification reaction of coke, steam and carbon dioxide, has gas supply device, steam generating device, gasification reaction device, cooling device gas purifying device, infrared gas detector and processor.
AU HAN J
   LIANG Y
   GUO R
   DOU M
   SUN Z
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNNS-C)
GA 2022706405
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a gas supply device that is provided with nitrogen storage tank (2) and a carbon-di-oxide storage tank (1). Gas drying device (4) and electromagnetic mass flow meter (5) are arranged on an air outlet pipeline of the carbon-di-oxide storage tank. A continuous injection pump (7) is connected to the first inlet of a high temperature heating device (6). The reaction outlet of a corundum reaction tube (11-1) is connected with a gas cooling device (13) through a pipeline. The gas cooling device is connected with a gas purification device (14). Air outlet of the gas purification device is connected with the infrared gas detector (15). An infrared gas detector detects the concentration of carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon-di-oxide in real time. The infrared gas detector, the temperature-controlling thermocouple, and the electromagnetic mass flow-meter are electrically connected to a processor (16).
   USE - Device for detecting co-gasification reaction of coke, steam and carbon dioxide for blast furnace ironmaking in steel metallurgy industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The device accurately distinguishes the reaction of the carbon solution reaction and the water gas, and avoids water vapor condensation in the gas transmission process in the steam condensation problem.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for detecting the co-gasification reaction of coke, steam and carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure view of the co-gasification reaction detection device.

   Corundum reaction tube (11-1)
   Temperature control thermocouple (12)
   Gas cooling device (13)
   Gas purification device (14)
   Infrared gas detector (15)

TI Preparing sterile fruit-grain carbonated beverage, by heating concentrated syrup and reverse osmosis water, sterilizing sugar water, cooling aseptic sugar water, dissolving carbon dioxide gas in sugar water, and adding carbonated beverage and aseptic fruit granules.
AU SHI C
   LIU B
   LI Q
AE JIANGSU NEWAMSTAR PACKAGING MACHINERY CO (JSXM-C)
GA 2021912795
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sterile fruit-grain carbonated beverage involves (a) mixing concentrated syrup and reverse osmosis (RO) water, heating, sending to a degassing tank for performing negative pressure deoxygenation, performing sterilization, and delivering sterilized aseptic sugar water into a first buffer tank, (b) performing sterilization for fruit particle, transporting fruit particles, sending sterilized fruit pieces into a second buffer tank with back pressure for temporary storage, and stirring, (c) cooling aseptic sugar water in first buffer tank by a sterile centrifugal pump with steam shield and sending to a first mixing tank, and passing sterilized and filtered carbon dioxide (CO2) gas to dissolve in the sterile sugar water to prepare a sterile carbonated beverage, and (d) sending aseptic carbonated beverage and aseptic fruit granules for mixing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sterile fruit-grain carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares carbonated beverages with low fruit particle damage rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sterile fruit-grain carbonated beverage involves (a) mixing concentrated syrup and reverse osmosis (RO) water, heating, sending to a degassing tank for performing negative pressure deoxygenation, performing ultra-high temperature instantaneous sterilization, and delivering sterilized aseptic sugar water into a first buffer tank for temporary storage, (b) performing ultra-high temperature instantaneous sterilization for fruit particle, using cross-sectional area of sterilization pipeline to transport the fruit particles, sending sterilized fruit pieces into a second buffer tank with back pressure for temporary storage, and stirring, (c) cooling aseptic sugar water in first buffer tank by a sterile centrifugal pump with steam shield and sending to a first mixing tank, and passing sterilized and filtered carbon dioxide (CO2) gas to dissolve in the sterile sugar water to prepare a sterile carbonated beverage, and (d) sending aseptic carbonated beverage output in the first mixing tank and the aseptic fruit granules output in the second buffer tank to a second mixing tank with back pressure for mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The back pressure in the second mixing tank is greater than or equal to 4 bar, and the back pressure in the first mixing tank and the second buffer tank are respectively greater than the back pressure in the second mixing tank. In step (a), the degassing tank is evacuated by a vacuum pump until the gauge pressure is less than or equal to -0.8 bar. The step (a) involves obtaining high temperature and high pressure hot water through heat exchange of high temperature steam and high pressure water when sterilizing, performing heat exchange through the mixture material after the high temperature and high pressure hot water and the negative pressure deoxygenation, increasing temperature and sterilizing mixture. In step (c), heat exchange of the sterile sugar water output from the first buffer tank by a tubular heat exchanger reduces the temperature of the sterile sugar water to 10 degrees C or below. The method involves sending aseptic finished beverage to a third buffer tank with back pressure for temporary storage and stirring, where back pressure in the third buffer tank is greater than or equal to 4 bar, after step (4).

A bag-to-vent flow diverter (36) is then moved to an intermediate position to simultaneously open both a bag channel (48) and a ventilator channel (49). The inspiratory port (26) of the anesthesia breathing system is then connected to the expiratory port of the anesthesia breathing system. A dry gas source (12) is connected to an absorber outlet channel. A plug is then inserted into the absorber inlet port. A dry gas flow is then provided from the dry gas source through the bag channel and ventilator channel so as to dry out moisture from the bag circuit (40) and ventilator circuit (50) of the anesthesia breathing system.
   USE - Method for drying an anesthesia breathing system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a practical method that enables thorough drying of the breathing system of an anesthesia machine in order to reduce the likelihood of mold growth in the breathing system. Ensures that an anesthesia breathing system can be dried out between patient cases or during long periods of non-use, such as overnight, so as to prevent mold growth and allow more effective cleaning and sterilization of the entirety of the gas path in the breathing system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) an anesthesia breathing system; and
   (2) a kit for drying an anesthesia breathing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a breathing system in a drying mode and utilizing the drying method for an anesthesia breathing system.

TI Deep denitration method for supercritical coal-fired boiler in power generation system involves using injection burner to inject fuel and ammonia into combustion zone and form low-oxygen zone to reduces temperature and nitrates generation.
AU LIN Y
   GU M
   WANG J
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
   UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 202072806W
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical carbon dioxide coal-fired boiler deep denitrification involves using ammonia injection burner (110) to inject fuel and ammonia reduction into main combustion zone of burner in furnace and form an high-temperature low-oxygen reduction zone (116) with temperature of fourteen hundred degrees Celsius. The two-stage ammonia injection device (120) and three-stage ammonia injection device (140) respectively inject an amino reductant into main combustion zone and burn-out zone on upper unit of annular high temperature and low oxygen reduction zone perform two-stage denitration and three-stage denitrification of exhaust gas. The Dust removal, desulfurization and denitrification after three-stage denitration in the furnace enters denitration unit (200) for SCR denitration, and enters the desulfurization unit (300) for cooling treatment. After the flue gas temperature drops to ninety degrees Celsius, it is sent to the furnace for cyclic denitration treatment.
   USE - Deep denitration method for supercritical carbon dioxide coal-fired boiler in power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the temperature in the furnace and reduces the generation of thermal nitrates in the boiler itself to reduce pollution. Reduces the furnace exhaust Emission of nitrates concentration, and reduce oxygen concentration in recirculated flue gas. Reduce the nitrogen oxides produced by the combustion of pulverized coal, and improves the reduction and denitrification efficiency. Eliminates secondary pollution caused by ammonia escape. Reduces the load of the selective catalytic reduction (SCR) system and reduces the amount of catalyst used. Increases the service life of the catalyst, reduces the investment of the SCR system. Prevents the clogging of the tail wet electrostatic precipitator.

PN KR2128698-B1
TI Air purifier with built-in hydrocarbon-based gas saving device, has LED lighting unit including LEDs installed to light or flash, and auxiliary power supply that supplies power selectively in event of power failure or instead of power.
AU JANG S
   LEE H
AE JANG S (JANG-Individual)
   LEE H (LEEH-Individual)
GA 202060617C
AB    NOVELTY - The air purifier (1) has an air cleaning unit (110) that generates negative electrons and positrons and radiates them in the air to supply the electrons to the oxygen molecules and hydrogen molecules in the air to produce oxygen anions and hydrogen cations in the air, and combines the oxygen anion and hydrogen cation generated to form a hydroxyl radical anion in the air. An LED lighting unit (130) includes one or more LEDs installed to light/flash according to an on/off driving signal. An auxiliary power supply supplies power selectively in the event of a power failure or instead of the power. The hydroxyl anion is combined with ethylene gas to promote food decay, thus making water and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide and hydroxyl radical anion are formed, thus combining to form hydrogen carbonate ions. The generated water and carbon dioxide are combined to become carbonic acid, so that the carbonic acid is discharged into the outside air the door of the food storage is opened.
   USE - Air purifier with built-in hydrocarbon-based gas saving device for removing various food odors such as kimchi and side dishes stored in room temperature or refrigerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purifier does not generate harmful impurities such as ozone, and generates hydroxy radicals that are harmless to the human body, thus removing harmful substances such as odors, harmful bacteria and viruses. The air purifier sterilizes various microorganisms generated in food storage such as refrigerators, and reduces/removes hydrocarbon-based gas including ethylene gas that is a decay factor of food, thus creating an effect to clean the air in the storage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an air purifier with a hydrocarbon-based gas saving device. (Drawing includes non-English Language text)

   Air cleaning unit (110)
   LED lighting unit (130)

PN CN111013661-A; CN111013661-B
TI Metal-organic framework-porous organic polymers            core-shell material useful in preparing cyclic            carbonate from carbon dioxide and epoxide by            cycloaddition reaction comprises hafnium-based metal            organic frameworks and porous organic polymer.
AU HAN Z
   ZHANG Y
   LIU L
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 202034073Q
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework-porous organic                polymers core-shell material comprises                hafnium-based metal organic frameworks (NH2-UiO-66                (Hf)) as core, and porous organic polymer of cobalt                5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)-21H,23H-porphine with                2,4,6-tris (bromomethyl) trimethylbenzene                (CoTPy-CAP) produced by reacting cobalt                5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)-21H,23H-porphine                (CoTPy) and 2,4,6-tris (bromomethyl)                trimethylbenzene (TBMBr) as shell i.e.                NH2-UiO-66(Hf)@CoTPyCAP.
   USE - The core-shell material is useful in preparing                cyclic carbonate from carbon dioxide and epoxide by                cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions,                utilizes catalyst which has high catalytic                activity, single product and excellent circulation                performance; and does not use co-catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the core-shell material                comprising (i) mixing hafnium(IV) chloride,                2-aminoterephthalic acid, formic acid and                dimethylformamide evenly, placing it into a PTFE                high-pressure reaction kettle, sealing, placing                into an oven, heating at 398 K, reacting for 40-45                hours, slowly cooling to room temperature,                filtering, washing with dimethylformamide and                methanol in sequence, and drying to obtain metal                organic framework material NH2-UiO-66 (Hf), and                (ii) adding (Co-TPy), (TBMBr) and tetrahydrofuran                into a container, ultrasonic treating at room                temperature, adding (NH2-UiO-66(Hf) and continuing                to ultrasonic treating for 60 minutes, heating the                reaction mixture at 60-70 degrees C under nitrogen                conditions, heating to reflux for 90 hours, washing                with tetrahydrofuran and chloroform, filtering, and                drying to obtain final product; and
   (2) preparing cyclic carbonate comprising                placing epoxide into a reactor under action of                NH2-UiO-66 (Hf)@CoTPy-CAP as catalyst without                solvent, introducing carbon dioxide until the                reaction pressure is 0.5 MPa, performing                cycloaddition reaction at 80 degrees C for 10 hours                to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxide is                  2-(allyloxymethyl)oxirane,                  2-(butoxymethyl)oxirane, 2-ethyloxirane,                  2-methyloxirane or 2-phenyloxirane. In the                  method, the addition amount of catalyst is 50                  mg/10 mol of epoxide compound.

PT P
PN CN110627100-A; CN110627100-B
TI Two-stage series method for producing micron-sized calcium carbonate by a carbonization method in which liquid phase is continuous phase, and comprises two-stage series reactor, solid suspension of reactor is continuously fed and discharged.
AU WU Q
   CHEN G
   LI W
   XUAN S
   HONG J
AE JIANDE HUAMING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202002589W
AB    NOVELTY - Two-stage series method for producing micron-sized calcium carbonate by carbonization method in which liquid phase is continuous phase, comprises two-stage series reactor. Solid suspension of each stage of reactor is continuously fed and discharged. Calcium hydroxide raw material is continuously fed from primary reactor. Calcium carbonate precipitation suspension is discharged from secondary reactor, and carbon dioxide gas raw materials are continuously supplied to primary and secondary reactors, respectively. Secondary reactor consists of two reactors operating in rotation. Secondary reactor receives effluent from primary reactor for carbonization operation. Another secondary reactor performs operations of degassing and discharging calcium carbonate suspension. Operating conditions of reactors at all levels are adjusted and controlled according to technical indicators of crystal form, morphology and particle size of target micron-sized calcium carbonate product.
   USE - Two-stage series method for producing micron-sized calcium carbonate by a carbonization method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Two-stage series method for producing micron-sized calcium carbonate by carbonization method in which liquid phase is continuous phase, comprises two-stage series reactor. Solid suspension of each stage of reactor is continuously fed and discharged. Calcium hydroxide raw material is continuously fed from primary reactor. Calcium carbonate precipitation suspension is discharged from secondary reactor, and carbon dioxide gas raw materials are continuously supplied to primary and secondary reactors, respectively. Secondary reactor consists of two reactors operating in rotation. Secondary reactor receives effluent from primary reactor for carbonization operation. Another secondary reactor performs operations of degassing and discharging calcium carbonate suspension. Operating conditions of reactors at all levels are adjusted and controlled according to technical indicators of crystal form, morphology and particle size of target micron-sized calcium carbonate product. Operating conditions include average residence time ( tau ), operating temperature (T), calcium hydroxide feed concentration (C0), and carbon dioxide supply (Q). Unit of Q is mole carbondioxide/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide gas is a kiln gas collected by calcination of lime. The carbon dioxide volume concentration is 25 to 48 vol.%. The reactor is selected from stirred carbonization kettle, bubble column, and circulating jet carbonization kettle. The upper portion of the primary reactor is provided with an overflow port. The supply point of the calcium hydroxide raw material is set in the area with the highest mixing strength. The supply of carbon dioxide passes from the bottom of the reactor into the reactor. The operating conditions of the primary reactor with an operating volume (V1) are tau 1 is 20-60 minute, T1 is 55-70 degrees C, C0 is 0.6-2 mole/L, and Q1/A is 0.4-1. Among them, Q1 is the carbon dioxide (CO2) supply rate mole CO2/minute of the first stage reactor. The A is the supply rate of calcium hydroxide mole calcium hydroxide/minute. That is, A is C0asteriskV1/ tau 1. Rotate the two secondary reactors A and B with an operating volume (V2). The operating conditions are: V2 less than or equal to V1, T2 is 58-75 degrees C, Q2/V2 is 0.02-0.04 mole/minute.L. Feeding and carbonization time is 10 + tau 1 minute. Degassing and discharging for 20 minutes. The total operating time of a single reactor is less than or equal to 2 tau 1 minute. The unit of V1 and V2 is L. Access to micron spindle calcium carbonate products. The weight average particle diameter D50 of the particles is 1 to 6 mu m. The free calcium oxide content is less than 0.05 wt.%.

PN RU2684420-C1
TI Method of producing synthetic oil from natural / associated gas and compact plant for producing synthetic oil from natural / associated gas.
AU MIKHAYLOV M N
   GRIGOREV D A
   MAMONOV N A
   PROTASOV O N
   BESSUDNOV A E
AE ROSNEFT OIL PUBLIC STOCK CO (ROSN-Non-standard)
GA 2019374841
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to petrochemistry. Invention relates to a method for producing synthetic oil from natural / associated petroleum gas, suitable for transportation through trunk pipelines together with natural oil. Initial feed gas is mixed with water, the resulting water-gas mixture is heated and mixed with pre-heated air enriched with oxygen. Carry out a single-stage catalytic conversion of the heated vapor-gas mixture in the process of autothermal reforming with the obtaining of synthesis gas, its cooling, compressing, preheating and single-stage catalytic conversion in a compact mini-channel Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor into a mixture of water and synthetic liquid and gaseous hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide. Mixture of unreacted synthesis gas and water obtained in the Fischer-Tropsch process and synthetic liquid and gaseous hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide is cooled out of the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor. Hydrogen-containing gas, which is a mixture of gaseous synthetic hydrocarbons, unreacted synthesis gas and carbon dioxide, is separated from the mixture of water and synthetic liquid hydrocarbons. Water is separated from synthetic liquid hydrocarbons and mixed with a part of hydrogen-containing gas to form a gas-liquid mixture. Resulting gas-liquid mixture is heated and conduct a single-stage catalytic conversion of unsaturated hydrocarbons contained in the gas-liquid mixture into saturated hydrocarbons and synthetic oil is separated, which is a mixture of saturated synthetic hydrocarbons synthesized in a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor, and saturated synthetic hydrocarbons obtained as a result of the conversion of unsaturated hydrocarbons from unreacted hydrogen-containing gas. Invention also relates to a compact plant for the production of synthetic oil from natural / associated petroleum gas, suitable for transportation through trunk pipelines together with natural oil in one technological scheme.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing the yield of synthetic oil by increasing the yield of synthetic liquid hydrocarbons at the stage of Fischer-Tropsch synthesis and ensuring the production of synthetic oil from natural / associated petroleum gas, suitable for transportation through trunk pipelines together with natural oil.

AB    NOVELTY - Solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) system comprises                steam reformer (12) which comprises catalytic                reactor tubes (14) which are operable to convert                hydrocarbons of hydrocarbon feed into hydrogen to                produce reformer syngas, that comprises hydrogen                and a combustion chamber (16) which is thermally                coupled to catalytic reactor tubes, and is operable                to receive hydrocarbon feed, oxygen feed and anode                exhaust gas, and is operable to produce flue gas                which comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen                purification system that is operable to receive the                reformer syngas and is operable to produce a                purified hydrogen gas and an off-gas stream which                comprises methane, a pre-reformer and anode exhaust                gas and is operable to produce pre-reformer syngas                which comprises methane and solid oxide fuel cell                that is used to convert methane and water into                hydrogen and carbon oxides to produce electrical                power, where solid oxide fuel cell comprises anode                side which comprises hydrogen and carbon oxides and                anode side inlet.
   USE - Solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) system.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid oxide fuel cell system lowers its                overall utility requirements and improves                operational efficiency for converting fuel into                electricity, provides flexibility in operating SOFC                system to optimize hydrogen, electricity and carbon                dioxide production.

   (C) introducing the hydrocarbon feed to a                pre-reformer and anode exhaust gas to pre-reformer;                and
   (D) introducing anode feed to a solid oxide                fuel cell.

PN KR2018013658-A; KR1862363-B1
TI Producing sausage by mixing cycloartenyl ferulate with water, pulverizing pork, immersing in mixture, dehydrating and mixing with green tea and onion powder, aging, adding primary and secondary sauce, secondarily aging and filling.
AU KANGKYUN C
   CHOI K K
AE ARARI CO LTD (ARAR-Non-standard)
   CHOI K K (CHOI-Individual)
GA 201813101U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing sausage involves (a) mixing cycloartenyl ferulate with distilled water, (b) pulverizing the pork, placing the pulverized pork into a net bag, and immersing the mixture in the mixed water, (c) dehydrating the treated pork and mixing with a green tea powder and a onion powder, (d) aging the mixture, (e) adding primary sauce to the aged mixture, (f) adding a secondary sauce, (g) secondarily aging the mixture, (h) removing fat and cholesterol using a supercritical carbon dioxide solvent and (i) filling the mixture into the collagen casing.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sausage, which is useful for lowering the cholesterol level caused by intake of sausage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the sausage having a good texture and taste. The method prevents the consumer from intake of high cholesterol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing sausage involves (a) mixing cycloartenyl ferulate with distilled water at 0-10 degrees C, (b) pulverizing the pork, placing the pulverized pork into a net bag, and immersing the mixture in the mixed water for 30 minutes to 2 hours, (c) dehydrating the treated pork for 1-3 minutes and mixing with a green tea powder and a onion powder, (d) aging the mixture at 0-10 degrees C for at least 24 hours in a refrigerated state, (e) adding primary sauce comprising ice, salt, isolated soy protein (ISP), polyphosphate and corn starch to the aged mixture for more than 10 minutes, (f) adding a secondary sauce comprising sugar, pepper powder, curry powder, garlic, ginger, carrot, and green onion to the treated mixture for more than 20 minutes, (g) secondarily aging the mixture for at least 24 hours in a refrigerated state at 0-10 degrees C, (h) removing fat and cholesterol using a supercritical carbon dioxide solvent under a pressure of 100-400 bar and a temperature of 30-80 degrees C from the aged mixture and (i) filling the supercritical carbon dioxide-treated mixture into the collagen casing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method further involves (j) placing the filled casing on a trolley and putting it in a hopper, drying at 50-60 degrees C for 30 minutes, (k) fumigating the dried product at 55-65 degrees C for 30 minutes, (l) heating the smoked product at 65-75 degrees C for 30 minutes in a drier maintaining a humidity of 15-25%, (m) washing the heated product with distilled water at 5-10 degrees C, and repeating steps (j)-(l) and (n) refrigerating the heated product in a refrigerated state at 0-10 degrees C for at least 24 hours and then vacuum-packing the product. The step (a) involves mixing 10-30 pts. wt. cycloartenyl ferulate with 100 pts. wt. distilled water. The step (c) involves mixing 100 pts. wt. treated pork with 10-20 pts. wt. green tea powder and 5-15 pts. wt. onion powder. The step (e) involves adding primary sauce comprising 10-30 pts. wt. ice, 0.2-2 pts. wt. salt, 1-5 pts. wt. isolated soy protein (ISP), 0.001-0.1 pts. wt. polyphosphate and 2-20 pts. wt. corn starch to 100 pts. wt. aged mixture. The step (f) involves adding the secondary sauce comprising 0.2-2 pts. wt. sugar, 0.2-2 pts. wt. pepper powder, 1-15 pts. wt. curry powder, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. garlic, 0.1-1 pts. wt. ginger, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. carrot and 0.5 to 2.5 pts. wt. green onion to the 100 pts. wt. mixture.
   ACTIVITY - Antilipemic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN NL1043601-B1
TI Method for transporting carbon dioxide over water from carbon dioxide source to carbon dioxide storage location, involves unloading injection water and carbon dioxide from high-pressure reservoir at carbon dioxide storage location.
AU STIGTER H T
   VAN HIJFTE L J
AE FIZZY TRANSITION VENTURES BV (FIZZ-Non-standard)
   WAGENBORG OFFSHORE HOLDING BV (WAGE-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4363N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves charging a high-pressure reservoir of a barge at the counting site of the carbon dioxide source such that carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide source is stored in the form of liquid carbon dioxide in the high-pressure reservoir. The water reservoir of the barge is loaded at the location of the carbon dioxide source. The barge is pushed forward through a pusher boat, with the liquid carbon dioxide stored in the high pressure reservoir and the injection water stored in the water reservoir from the carbon dioxide source to the carbon dioxide storage location. The injection water from the water reservoir and the carbon dioxide from the high-pressure reservoir of the barge are unloaded at the location of the carbon dioxide storage location. The carbon dioxide storage site is an offshore carbon dioxide storage site.
   USE - Method for transporting carbon dioxide over water from carbon dioxide source to carbon dioxide storage location.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is transported at low cost from different carbon dioxide sources to different carbon dioxide storage locations, with scalable transport capacities where no frostbite or hydrate formation during the washing and injection process is made and with minimal carbon dioxide emissions from the transport system itself.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for transporting carbon dioxide over water from carbon dioxide source to carbon dioxide storage location.

PN US2021299591-A1; WO2021202262-A1; AU2021246724-A1
TI Continuously separating desublimatable compound            from hydrocarbons involves e.g. providing vessel,            passing feed fluid stream having hydrocarbon and            desublimatable compound, cooling to desublimate portion            of compound, collecting solid compound, and removing            liquid and gas streams.
AU BAXTER L
   BURT S
   FRANKMAN D
   HOEGER C
   MANSFIELD E
   CHAMBERLAIN S
   STITT K
AE SUSTAINABLE ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (SUST-Non-standard)
   SUSTAINABLE ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (SUST-Non-standard)
   SUSTAINABLE ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (SUST-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2144Q
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously separating desublimatable                compound from hydrocarbons comprises: providing                vessel comprising upper chamber and lower chamber;                passing feed fluid stream comprising hydrocarbon                and desublimatable compound in upper chamber;                cooling the feed fluid stream in the upper chamber                to desublimate portion of desublimatable compound                out of the feed liquid stream to form solid                desublimatable compound snow and product gas                stream; collecting the solid desublimatable                compound snow in the lower chamber; melting lower                portion of the solid desublimatable compound snow                to form liquid desublimatable compound stream so                that upper portion of the solid desublimatable                compound snow remains as insulative barrier between                upper chamber and liquid desublimatable compound                stream; removing the liquid desublimatable compound                stream at a rate that matches a production rate of                the solid desublimatable compound snow to maintain                insulative barrier; and removing the product gas                stream from the upper chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously                separating desublimatable compound from                hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) continuously separating (M2) components                comprising providing a vessel comprising upper                chamber and lower chamber, passing feed fluid                stream into the upper chamber, flashing a cold                liquid stream into the upper chamber to reduce                temperature of the upper chamber, where the cold                liquid stream comprises methane, desublimating the                carbon dioxide out of the feed fluid stream to form                solid carbon dioxide snow and product gas stream,                collecting the solid carbon dioxide snow in the                lower chamber, melting lower portion of the solid                carbon dioxide snow to form liquid carbon dioxide                stream so that upper portion of the solid carbon                dioxide snow remains as insulative barrier between                the upper chamber and the liquid carbon dioxide                stream, removing the liquid carbon dioxide stream                at a rate that matches a production rate of the                solid carbon dioxide snow to maintain insulative                barrier, and removing the product gas stream from                the upper chamber; and
   (2) system for continuously separating                components comprising a vessel (400) comprising an                upper chamber (10) and a lower chamber (12), where                the upper chamber comprising a feed gas inlet, a                cooling source, and a product gas outlet, the lower                chamber comprising a product liquid outlet and a                heat source (14), the feed fluid inlet configured                to pass a feed gas stream through the feed gas                inlet into the upper chamber, the feed fluid stream                comprising as per se, the cooling source cools the                feed gas stream to desublimate portion of the                desublimatable compound out of the feed gas stream                to form solid desublimatable snow and product gas                stream, the solid desublimatable compound snow                falling into the lower chamber, heat source melts a                lower portion of the solid desublimatable compound                snow in the lower chamber to form liquid                desublimatable compound stream in a manner that                upper portion of the solid desublimatable compound                snow remains as insulative barrier between the                upper chamber and the liquid desublimatable                compound stream, the product liquid outlet                configured to remove the liquid desublimatable                compound stream at a rate that matches a production                rate of the solid desublimatable compound snow to                maintain insulative barrier, and the product gas                outlet configured to remove the product gas stream                from the upper chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an isometric cutaway view                of the system for separating compounds.
   Upper chamber (10)
   Lower chamber (12)
   Heat source (14)
   Insulative barrier (33)
   Vessel (400)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further                  comprises passing a portion of the product gas                  stream through a refrigeration loop to produce                  the cold liquid stream. The method (M2) further                  comprises passing portion of the product gas                  stream through a refrigeration loop to produce                  the cold liquid stream. In the method (M1): the                  cooling the feed fluid stream comprises flashing                  cold liquid stream into the upper chamber to                  reduce the temperature of the upper chamber, and                  the cold liquid stream comprising the                  hydrocarbon; and the melting comprises injecting                  a warm liquid desublimatable compound stream into                  the lower chamber, and heating the lower chamber                  with a heating element. In the method (M2): the                  feed fluid stream comprises at least 20 wt.%                  carbon dioxide is at least 25 bar, and is less                  than -55degrees Celsius and C2+ natural gas                  components; the recycle liquid stream comprises                  not greater than 3 wt.% carbon dioxide is at                  least 50 bar, and is less than -102degrees                  Celsius; melting comprises injecting a warm                  liquid carbon dioxide stream into the lower                  chamber and heating the lower chamber with a                  heating element.Preferred Components: In the method (M1):                  the desublimatable compound comprises carbon                  dioxide, sulfur oxides, nitrogen oxides and/or                  carbon monoxide; and the hydrocarbon comprises                  methane, ethane and/or propane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Composition: The system further                  comprises a refrigerant loop configured to                  receive, cool, and compress a portion of the                  product gas stream to form cold liquid stream,                  where the cooling source is a result of flashing                  the cold liquid stream into the upper                  chamber.Preferred Components: In the system: the                  heat source inductive resistance heater embedded                  in or attached to walls of the lower chamber; the                  heat source is indirect-contact heat exchanger;                  the lower chamber comprises auger configured to                  convey the solids towards the product liquid                  outlet as the solids melt; and the desublimatable                  compound comprises carbon dioxide, sulfur oxides,                  nitrogen oxides and/or carbon monoxide.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN CN112410215-A
TI Dynamic adjustment of pH in reactor for cell culture, involves passing culture solution through pH sensor fixing block, reducing pH of culture solution by feeding carbon dioxide and vortex stirring, and increasing pH using nitrogen gas.
AU CHEN H
   SHUAI J
AE INNOVEL INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY SUZHOU CO (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 202127149F
AB    NOVELTY - Dynamic adjustment of pH in a reactor involves setting pH value detected by a pH sensor of the reactor, pumping a culture solution from bottom of a reactor glass tank using a peristaltic pump, passing through a pH sensor fixing block, controlling a carbon dioxide mass flow controller of the reactor when the pH sensor fixing block detects that the pH value of the culture solution is too high, feeding a certain amount of carbon dioxide through the inlet of oxygenator and dissolving into the culture solution to form carbonic acid and vortex stirring without stirring blade to make pH of the culture solution in the glass tank low and more uniform, controlling a nitrogen mass flow controller of the reactor to allow a certain amount of nitrogen to pass through when the pH sensor fixed block detects that the pH value of the culture solution is low, and dissolving the nitrogen gas into the culture solution to squeeze out the carbon dioxide gas in the culture solution to increase pH.
   USE - Dynamic adjustment of pH in reactor for cell culture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can quickly adjust pH in reactor with high stability and safety without the need to directly add acidic and alkaline liquids to the reactor to balance the pH value, thus avoiding risk of pollution. The entire pH-adjustment device is fully automated which greatly improves efficiency of cell culture and saves time and cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dynamic adjustment of pH in a reactor involves setting pH value detected by a pH sensor of the reactor, pumping a culture solution from bottom of a reactor glass tank using a peristaltic pump, passing through a pH sensor fixing block and through a liquid inlet of an oxygenator, flowing the liquid outlet of the oxygenator to top of the glass tank so that a vortex is formed in the glass tank, that is, the entire loop keeps circulating, controlling a carbon dioxide mass flow controller of the reactor through a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) closed loop when the pH sensor fixing block detects that the pH value of the culture solution is too high, feeding a certain amount of carbon dioxide through the inlet of oxygenator and dissolving into the culture solution to form carbonic acid and vortex stirring without stirring blade produced by the peristaltic pump to make the pH of the culture solution in the glass tank low and more uniform, controlling a nitrogen mass flow controller of the reactor by the PID closed loop to allow a certain amount of nitrogen to pass through when the pH sensor fixed block detects that the pH value of the culture solution is low, dissolving the nitrogen gas into the culture solution to squeeze out the carbon dioxide gas in the culture solution to increase pH. The reactor has the reactor glass tank, peristaltic pump, pH sensor fixing block, oxygenator, nitrogen mass flow controller, nitrogen pneumatic quadruple, carbon dioxide mass flow controller, carbon dioxide pneumatic quadruple, silicone tube, and pH sensor.

PN WO2018224763-A1; FR3067261-A1; AU2018279228-A1; FR3067261-B1; BR112019025828-A2
TI Purifying natural gas feed stream comprising methane, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons, comprises introducing feed gas stream in purification unit, and separating stream in membrane permeation unit having stage of main membrane separation.
AU TERRIEN P
   MARTY P
   CHANTANT N
   GERARD S
   RAVENTOS M
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201899633H
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying a natural gas feed stream comprising methane, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons having at least 2C atoms, comprises (a) introduction of the feed gas stream (1) in a purification unit at a pressure P1, and (b) separation of the gas stream in a membrane permeation unit comprising at least one stage of main membrane separation from which at least one gas enriched in carbon dioxide has a pressure P3 and a gas stream depleted in carbon dioxide has a pressure P2 greater than P3, where: the difference between P2 and P3 is greater than or equal to 20 bara; and when P1 is greater than or equal to to a threshold pressure Ps, the step (b) is preceded by the following steps that are (b1) Joule Thomson relaxation of the feed stream from the pressure P1 to a pressure between P2 and Ps and (b2) introduction of the stream from step (b1) into a phase separator pot (7) to produce at least two phases, including a liquid phase and a gas phase (10).
   USE - The method is useful for purifying the natural gas feed stream comprising methane, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons having at least two carbon atoms.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes carbon dioxide contained in the stream of natural gas to be liquefied by minimizing methane losses during this withdrawal and while minimizing the costs required to deploy this type of purification process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying a natural gas feed stream comprising methane, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons having at least 2C atoms, comprises (a) introduction of the feed gas stream (1) in a purification unit at a pressure P1, and (b) separation of the gas stream in a membrane permeation unit comprising at least one stage of main membrane separation from which at least one gas enriched in carbon dioxide has a pressure P3 and a gas stream depleted in carbon dioxide has a pressure P2 greater than P3, where: the difference between P2 and P3 is greater than or equal to 20 bara; when P1 is greater than or equal to to a threshold pressure Ps, the step (b) is preceded by the following steps that are (b1) Joule Thomson relaxation of the feed stream from the pressure P1 to a pressure between P2 and Ps and (b2) introduction of the stream from step (b1) into a phase separator pot (7) to produce at least two phases, including a liquid phase comprising at least 10 (preferably at least 50) mole%, of hydrocarbons having at least 4C atoms initially contained in the feed stream and a gas phase (10); when P1 is less than or equal to Ps, the membrane separation unit used in step (b) comprises a preliminary membrane separation stage (25) producing a hydrocarbon-enriched stream (26) having at least 4C atoms at pressure P4 and a stream whose hydrocarbon dew point is at most equal to the hydrocarbon dew point of the feed gas of this step (b) at pressure P2; and P1 decreases with time and the number of membrane units used increases over time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the method.
   Feed gas stream (1)
   Phase separator pot (7)
   Gas phase (10)
   Preliminary membrane separation stage (25)
   Hydrocarbon-enriched stream (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The membranes implemented in the membrane separation unit are inverse-selectivity membranes for the preliminary stage and membranes more selective for carbon dioxide than for methane and more selective for methane only for heavy hydrocarbons for the main separation stage. The gas stream depleted of carbon dioxide at a pressure P2 from step (b) contains less than 10 mole% of carbon dioxide. The feed stream to be purified comprises at least 15 mole% of carbon dioxide, where Ps is equal to 65 bara and the difference between P2 and P4 is greater than 20 bara.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN113413777-A; CN113413777-B
TI Matrix film comprises polyimide            4,4'-(hexafluoroisopropylidene)diphthalic            anhydride-Durene and filler i.e. veiled zeolitic            imidazolate framework-67 or zeolitic imidazolate            framework-67.
AU LI J
   CHEN K
   QI J
   WANG C
   SUN X
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2021B2996P
AB    NOVELTY - Matrix film comprises polyimide                4,4'-(hexafluoroisopropylidene)diphthalic                anhydride-Durene (6FDA-Durene) (remaining amount)                and 10-30% filler i.e. veiled zeolitic imidazolate                framework-67 (VZIF-67) or zeolitic imidazolate                framework-67 (ZIF-67).
   USE - The matrix film is useful as gas separation                membrane, preferably separates carbon                dioxide/methane mixed gas (all claimed) and in                purifying natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The matrix film has significant separation                performance for separating carbon dioxide/methane                mixed gas (PCO2 more than 1210Barrer, alpha carbon                dioxide/methane more than 32.5) and higher carbon                dioxide gas permeability and separation                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the matrix film, comprising (a)                dissolving the polyimide 6FDA-Durene in a solvent                to obtain a 6FDA-Durene solution, adding VZIF-67 or                ZIF-67 into the 6FDA-Durene solution, and                dispersing in the 6FDA-Durene solution by                ultrasonic and stirring to obtain a casting liquid;                (b) degassing of the casting liquid; and (c)                pouring the casting liquid into the mold,                evaporating the solvent to form a film, and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The film thickness of                  the mixed matrix film is 40-100 mu m, and the                  film area is not limited. In the step (a), the                  solvent is chloroform, dichloromethane,                  N,N-dimethylformyl or N-methylpyrrolidone.                  Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises adding                  polyimide 6FDA-Durene powder twice into the                  uniformly dispersed VZIF-67 solution to dissolve,                  adding one-tenth of the required amount of                  6FDA-Durene for the first time, and adding                  remaining amount of 6FDA-Durene powder after it                  is completely dissolved. In the step (b), the                  method of degassing adopts allowing to stand,                  vacuum or ultrasonic deaeration. The step (c)                  comprises drying the mixed matrix film at room                  temperature, drying at 60-80 degrees C for 8-12                  hours, and vacuum-drying at 130-170 degrees C for                  2-4 days.

PN CN113413779-A
TI Preparing mixed matrix film used for carbon            dioxide-nitrogen high-efficiency separation, involves            mixing one-dimensional linear composite material            ZIF-8-carbon nanotubes and polymer matrix material            suitable for mixing matrix film.
AU TONG Y
   ZHANG Y
   LI X
   CHEN X
   HE W
   GUO S
   LIU Y
   ZHANG H
   CHENG L
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA WATER RESOURCES & ELECT (UNCW-C)
GA 2021B22743
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mixed matrix film involves mixing                one-dimensional linear composite material                ZIF-8-carbon nanotubes and polymer matrix material                suitable for mixing matrix film in mixed solvent,                dissolving fully, volatilizing and removing the                mixed solvent to obtain the mixed matrix film for                carbon dioxide/nitrogen (CO2/N2) high-efficiency                separation, where the one-dimensional linear                composite material ZIF-8-carbon nanotubes is                prepared by dispersing ultrasonically acidified                carbon nanotubes and 2-methylimidazole into                methanol solution at room temperature, adding zinc                nitrate hexahydrate methanol solution, mixing,                stirring to appear turbid, washing, centrifuging,                and drying to obtain the one-dimensional linear                composite material ZIF-8-carbon nanotubes.
   USE - Method for preparing mixed matrix film used                for carbon dioxide/nitrogen high-efficiency                separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare mixed matrix                film in simple and cost-effective manner, realizes                complete mixing with polymer matrix, overcomes                problem of interface defect in mixed matrix film,                and is good for carbon dioxide/nitrogen                separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The mixed solvent                  includes 70 wt.% absolute ethanol and 30 wt.%                  deionized water. Preferred Process: The method                  for acidification treatment involves mixing                  concentrated sulfuric acid and concentrated                  nitric acid in a volume ratio of 3:1 to obtain a                  mixed acid, adding carbon nanotubes to the mixed                  acid for treatment for 20 hours at the same time                  of stirring in the oil bath at 50 degrees C,                  collecting carbon nanotubes, washing and drying.                  Preferred Conditions: The mixed solvent comprises                  45 mg/ml Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) 1657.                  The mass ratio of the one-dimensional linear                  composite material ZIF-8-carbon nanotubes to the                  polymer matrix material is 1-15%. The mass ratio                  of the one-dimensional linear composite material                  ZIF-8-carbon nanotubes relative to the polymer                  matrix material is 3-8%. The molar ratio of zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate to 2-methylimidazole is 1:8.                  The mass ratio between carbon nanotubes and                  2-methylimidazole is 1:10.815. The stirring time                  after mixing the two methanol solution is 1                  hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer matrix                  material is Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide)                  1657.

PN WO2021133523-A1; CN114845979-A; CA3162634-A1; EP4081495-A1
TI Preparing hydrocarbons by combining feed stream            comprising hydrogen gas and carbon-containing gas and            second stream comprising carbon dioxide, and converting            combined stream into product stream in presence of            hybrid catalyst.
AU POLLEFEYT G
   NIESKENS D L S
   KIRILIN A
   CHOJECKI A
   DEWILDE J F
   FISH B B
   MALEK A
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 202174162X
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbons containing 2-4 carbon atoms                (2-4C) are prepared by introducing feed stream                comprising hydrogen gas and carbon-containing gas                comprising carbon monoxide into a reaction zone of                a reactor; introducing 2nd stream comprising carbon                dioxide into reaction zone of reactor; forming                combined stream from feed and 2nd stream; and                converting combined stream into a product stream                comprising 2-4C hydrocarbons in the reaction zone                in the presence of a hybrid catalyst which                comprises mixed metal oxide catalyst component and                microporous catalyst component, where gas hourly                space velocity (GHSV) is greater than or equal to                2500/hour, net carbon dioxide (CO2) selectivity is                less than or equal to 5%, and 2-4C hydrocarbon                productivity is greater than or equal to 75 g                hydrocarbon/kg catalyst per hour.
   USE - Preparation of 2-4C hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The hybrid catalyst efficiently converts                various carbon-containing streams to 2-4C                hydrocarbons, while limiting the amount of carbon                dioxide that is produced in the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The 2nd stream                  comprises up to 80 vol.% carbon dioxide (CO2).                  The 2nd stream consists of CO2. Combined stream                  comprises greater than or equal to 6.3 vol.% CO2                  and 50-80 vol.% hydrogen. Combined stream                  comprises CO2/CO volume ratio of 0.05-1.5. Mixed                  metal oxide catalyst component comprises zirconia                  (ZrO2). Mixed metal oxide catalyst component                  comprises ZrO2 and gallium oxide (Ga2O3).                  Microporous catalyst component is a molecular                  sieve having 8-MR pore openings. Microporous                  catalyst component is SAPO-34. Preferred                  Properties: Net CO2 selectivity is less than or                  equal to 1%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Reaction zone operates                  at 380-450 degrees C and pressure of 20-70 bar.                  Gas hourly space velocity (GHSV) is greater than                  or equal to 4800/hour. Preferred Component: The                  2-4C hydrocarbons consist essentially of 2-4C                  olefins.

TI Orthopedic splint for curing using carbon dioxide            in air, comprises support, and cured body formed on            support, where cured body comprises polyurethane resin            and water generating agent, where resin has            polyisocyanate functional group.
AU BAE J W
   PEI Z
AE BLTECH CO LTD (BLTE-Non-standard)
GA 202135636X
AB    NOVELTY - Orthopedic splint (1) comprises a support                (10), and cured body (20) formed on support, where                cured body comprises polyurethane resin and water                generating agent. The polyurethane resin comprises                poly-isocyanate functional group that generates                urea and carbon dioxide by reacting with moisture                in the air. The water generating agent reacts with                carbon dioxide present in the air to generate                water, and the water generated by the water                generating agent reacts with poly-isocyanate                functional group.
   USE - Orthopedic splint for curing using carbon                dioxide or moisture in air.
   ADVANTAGE - The orthopedic splint is used to fix the                affected area by wrapping it partially or entirely                tightly to stabilize the affected area and limit                the mobility of the affected area in case of an                emergency such as a trauma, fracture, dislocation                or sprain of body.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                orthopedic splint
   Orthopedic splint (1)
   Support (10)
   Cured body (20)
   Silica layer (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water generating                  agent contains calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2). The                  orthopedic splint comprises first and second                  surface in contact with the support and further                  comprises a silica layer positioned on second                  surface. Preferred Composition: The cured body                  comprises 15-30 pts. wt. water generating agent                  based on 100 pts. wt. polyurethane resin.                  Preferred Conditions: The average particle size                  of the Ca(OH) 2 is 0.5 to 5 mu m. The silica                  layer is formed by spraying silica onto the                  second surface. The silica layer is 1-10 pts. wt.                  based on 100 pts. wt. cured body.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The CO2 in the CO2 containing gas is absorbed by the material in the reaction system, and created a non-oxidizing atmosphere in the reaction system and heated to 350 degrees C or higher and 700 degrees C or lower, and decomposed CO2 absorbed by the material in the reaction system into carbon.

PN CN111551643-A
TI Detecting carbon-containing compound based on catalytic conversion technology comprises oxidizing and combusting sample to-be-detected, hydrogenating and reducing carbon dioxide, and detecting methane by hydrogen flame ionization detector.
AU AO X
   ZHANG Y
AE BEIJING SDL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202081821F
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting carbon-containing compound based on catalytic conversion technology comprises (i) catalytically oxidizing and combusting sample to-be-detected at greater than or equal to 390 degrees C in the presence of oxygen to generate carbon dioxide, where the combustion is a flameless combustion; (ii) catalytically hydrogenating and reducing the carbon dioxide generated in step (i) in the presence of hydrogen to generate methane; and (iii) detecting the methane generated in step (ii) by a hydrogen flame ionization detector.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting carbon-containing compound based on catalytic conversion technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses the catalytic oxidation combustion and catalytic hydrogenation reduction technology for indirectly realizing the detection of micro or trace carbon-containing compound using hydrogen flame ionization detector, and simultaneously measuring carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and organic matter; and has very high detection sensitivity for formaldehyde, formic acid and formamide, that are difficult to detect by conventional method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sample to-be-detected comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and/or organic matter. The organic matter is comprises formaldehyde, formamide and formic acid.

   USE - The sulfide scavengers are useful for: scavenging sulfides and carbon dioxide from petroleum and natural gas; removing carbon dioxide or a sulfide from oil, water or natural gas; and scavenging of hydrogen and organic sulfides.
   ADVANTAGE - The sulfide scavengers: exhibit good scavenging capacity and are cost-effective; exhibit increase stoichiometric capacity and reaction time kinetics; and exhibit less aquatic toxicity than the aquatic toxicity with use of triazines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming the sulfide scavenger (S1) comprising blending an aromatic imine with an amine; and
   (2) a sulfide scavenger (S2) comprising a cold blend of an aromatic aldehyde and an amine, where the blend of aromatic aldehyde and amine is 5-80 wt.% of the total blend.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The aromatic imine is formed by reacting an aldehyde with a first amine, where the aldehyde is in stoichiometric excess. Preferred Components: The amine is diethylamine, 2-methylpiperidine, 2-ethylpiperidine, dibutylamine and/or N-methylcyclohexylamine. The aromatic aldehyde is benzaldehyde. The pre-reaction mixture of aromatic aldehyde and amine is present at 5-50 wt.% of the total pre-reaction mixture. The first amine and the second amine are the same or different, preferably different. The aromatic aldehyde is benzaldehyde, the first amine is propylamine, and the second amine is diethylamine. The cold blend of aromatic aldehyde and amine is 5-50 wt.% of the total blend.
   EXAMPLE - Typical sulfide scavenger comprised of reaction product of 90% benzaldehyde and 10% diethylamine.

TI Preparation of catalyst for producing olefin,            involves grinding metal precursor, promoter precursor            and/or support precursor to form mixture, calcining at            one or more temperatures, and flowing methane over            obtained pre-catalyst.
AU BASSET J
   HUANG K
   HENGNE A M
   ENAKONDA L R
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 201954051T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst involves grinding                (101) a metal precursor, a promoter precursor                and/or a support precursor to form a mixture,                calcining (102) at one or more temperatures to form                (100) a pre-catalyst, and flowing methane over the                pre-catalyst to form a catalyst active in the                production of olefins by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation.
   USE - Preparation of catalyst for producing chemical                species e.g. olefin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst having excellent catalytic                activity can be formed efficiently by simple                method, and enables manufacture of chemical species                with high selectivity and yield. The methane                formation during carbon dioxide hydrogenation can                be suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                producing chemical species, which involves flowing                carbon dioxide and hydrogen over methane or                methane/hydrogen mixture or natural gas activated                catalyst to produce hydrocarbons by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of preparation                of pre-catalyst.
   Preparation of pre-catalyst (100)
   Grinding (101)
   Calcining (102)
   Particle size reduction (103)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal precursor                  includes iron, cobalt or nickel, and contains                  metal oxide, metal hydroxide, metal nitrate,                  metal oxalate, metal chloride, metal carbonyl                  and/or metal sulfate. The promoter precursor                  includes an alkali metal and/or alkaline earth                  metal, preferably sodium, calcium, potassium,                  cesium, manganese and/or copper. The support                  precursor includes aluminum, titanium, zirconium,                  silicon, magnesium, carbon, carbon nanotubes,                  graphene, zinc and/or zeolite. The metal                  precursor, promoter precursor, and support                  precursor are each salts, in which anion is                  nitrate. The mixture of the metal precursor,                  promoter precursor, and support precursor is                  fused. The methane and methane/hydrogen mixture                  reduces metal oxides to metallic metals and                  provides a carbon source for forming metal                  carbides. The methane/hydrogen mixture and                  natural gas provide a source for forming metal                  carbides. The catalyst is supported on alumina,                  silicon oxide, titanium oxide, zirconium oxide,                  magnesium oxide, carbon, carbon nanotubes or                  graphene. Preferred Conditions: Calcining is                  performed at 350-450 degrees C. The grain size of                  the pre-catalyst is reduced to 25-500 pm.                  Alternately, calcining is carried out at 500-1000                  degrees C and 1-20 bar for 1 minute to 1 hour.                  Preferred Composition: The catalyst contains                  10-100 wt.% metal, 0-20 wt.% promoter and 0-90                  wt.% support. Preferred Method: The catalyst is                  prepared by grinding iron nitrate nonahydrate,                  sodium nitrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate                  to form a homogeneous powder mixture, calcining                  under static air at one or more temperatures, and                  flowing methane over the pre-catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The hydrocarbons                  include one or more 2-9C linear olefin.

In step (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process conditions are: the pressure is 10-30 Mpa, the temperature is 20-60 degrees C and the time is 30-120 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Reaction device based on self-generating                technology comprises turbine power generation                device (3) and injection device, where stored                sodium bicarbonate water solution is changed into                steam after primary heating and distillation, solid                sodium bicarbonate is further heated by a secondary                stage and decomposed into carbon dioxide, sodium                carbonate and water, carbon dioxide is passed into                injection device, steam generated after primary                heating is injected into injection device and mixed                in the injection device, and steam is injected into                turbine power generation device at a high speed for                power generation.
   USE - Reaction device based on self-generating                technology used in power generation and chemical                engineering industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical characteristics of the sodium                carbonate and the carbon dioxide are utilized, and                the generated carbon dioxide is utilized to inject                the water vapor through chemical reaction, so that                the power generation is finally realized. The                generated carbon dioxide and the water vapor are                mixed for power generation by utilizing the                chemical reaction and mutual conversion between the                sodium bicarbonate and the sodium carbonate, so                that the effect of reducing the environmental                pollution is achieved, the raw materials is                obtained in a large amount at low cost, and the                effect of low power generation cost is                achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reaction device based on self-generating                technology comprises turbine power generation                device (3) and injection device, where stored                sodium bicarbonate water solution is changed into                steam after primary heating and distillation, solid                sodium bicarbonate is further heated by a secondary                stage and decomposed into carbon dioxide, sodium                carbonate and water, carbon dioxide is passed into                injection device, steam generated after primary                heating is injected into injection device and mixed                in the injection device, steam is injected into                turbine power generation device at a high speed for                power generation, generated power is stored in an                energy storage device and partially used, redundant                portion is output to the outside to finish the                mixed fluid for pushing the turbine generating                device to generate electricity, sodium bicarbonate                and the water decomposed by the secondary heating                are mixed and reacted in the water solution by                utilizing waste heat to generate the sodium                bicarbonate water solution, and circularly                decomposing for generating electricity.
