TI Production device useful for producing Mucuna extract comprises e.g. ultrasonic extraction kettle, carbon dioxide gas storage tank, collecting bottle, air purifying tank, condenser, ultrasonic wave generator, and first separation kettle.
AU LI Y
   LU H
   LI M
AE NAPO KANGZHENG NATURAL PLANT EXTRACTION (NAPO-Non-standard)
   NAPO KANGZHENG NATURAL PLANT EXTRACTION (NAPO-Non-standard)
GA 201926531M
AB    NOVELTY - Production device comprises ultrasonic extraction kettle, carbon dioxide gas storage tank, collecting bottle, air purifying tank, pump, heater, condenser, ultrasonic wave generator, first separation kettle, and second separation kettle. The carbon dioxide gas storage tank, collecting bottle, air purifying tank, and pump are connected through pipeline. The pump is provided with two outlets, which are respectively connected to condenser and heater. The condenser and heater are combined by a pipeline to form a pipeline, and connected to a carbon dioxide inlet pipe of ultrasonic extraction kettle. The side wall of ultrasonic extraction kettle is connected to the top of first separation kettle through a pipe. The side wall of first separation kettle is connected to the top of the second separation kettle through a pipe. The second separation kettle is connected to the collecting bottle through a pipe. The ultrasonic extraction kettle outer wall is provided with an ultrasonic generator.
   USE - The production device is useful for producing Mucuna extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has strong corrosion resistance, simple operation, and strong practicability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production device comprises ultrasonic extraction kettle, carbon dioxide gas storage tank, collecting bottle, air purifying tank, pump, heater, condenser, ultrasonic wave generator, first separation kettle, and second separation kettle. The carbon dioxide gas storage tank, collecting bottle, air purifying tank, and pump are sequentially connected through a pipeline. The pump is provided with two outlets, which are respectively connected to the condenser and heater. The condenser and heater are combined by a pipeline to form a pipeline, and connected to a carbon dioxide inlet pipe of ultrasonic extraction kettle. The side wall of ultrasonic extraction kettle is connected to the top of first separation kettle through a pipe. The side wall of first separation kettle is connected to the top of the second separation kettle through a pipe. The second separation kettle is connected to the collecting bottle through a pipe. The ultrasonic extraction kettle outer wall is provided with an ultrasonic generator. The bottom of the ultrasonic extraction kettle body is provided with an outflow pipe, the top is provided with a lid that matches the body of the kettle, and the inner wall of the ultrasonic extraction kettle is provided with an acid-resistant coating.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the production device for producing Mucuna extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The acid-resistant coating is titanium nano-polymer coating. The device further comprises centrifugal separator, and leaching liquor preparation tank. The outflow pipe at the bottom of the ultrasonic extraction kettle body is connected to the centrifugal separator. The centrifugal separator is connected to the leaching liquor preparation tank through a pipe. The leaching liquor preparation tank is connected to the ultrasonic extractor through a pipe. The extraction kettle is also provided with an observation window. The ultrasonic extraction kettle inner wall is provided with an ultrasonic transducer. The ultrasonic transducer is electronically connected to the ultrasonic wave generator. The liquor preparation tank and ultrasonic extraction kettle connecting pipeline is provided with a water meter. The carbon dioxide inlet port of the ultrasonic extraction kettle is provided with a flow meter. The connection pipe of the ultrasonic extraction kettle, and first separation kettle and connection pipe of first separation kettle, and second separation kettle are each provided with a pressure gauge and a heater. The titanium nano-polymer coating layer comprises 80-100 pts. wt. titanium powder, 2-8 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, 5-10 pts. wt. dispersing agent, 1-5 pts. wt. protective agent, 10-15 pts. wt. acrylic resin, 2-8 pts. wt. solvent, 5-15 pts. wt. curing agent, and 5-10 pts. wt. of modifier. Preferred Method: The titanium nano-polymer coating is prepared by placing titanium powder, auxiliary agent, dispersing agent, and protective agent into crusher, crushing into nano titanium powder having a particle diameter of 50-100 nm, mixing with an acrylic resin, solvent, curing agent, and modifier to obtain the mixture, placing the mixture in a stirring kettle, and rotating at 90-100 degrees C, and at a high speed of 1000 revolutions per minute to obtain the product.

PN CN109454329-A; CN109454329-B
TI Controlling linkage rotary marking comprises e.g. adjusting emitter of carbon dioxide laser, adjusting height of galvanometer assembly, determining motion track by determining shape of the graphic and controlling laser control movement.
AU TANG G
AE FOSHAN FULAN LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2019254282
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling linkage rotary marking comprises (I) adjusting the emitter of carbon dioxide laser to make spot of the laser entering center of the entrance aperture of the focus lens of the galvanometer assembly, (II) adjusting height of the galvanometer assembly to make the focus lens center of the galvanometer assembly coincides with the spot of the carbon dioxide laser, (III) reading upper computer graphic marking, determining motion track by determining the shape of the graphic, and sending motion track to the laser control card, and (IV) controlling laser control card movement of the galvanometer assembly relative to the rotating shaft in real time by controlling correcting algorithm during the marking process according to the motion trajectory and controlling the carbon dioxide laser to emit laser for marking.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling linkage rotary marking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is more efficient and stable, improves marking accuracy and reduces reference circle generated by the drift.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for controlling linkage rotary marking device comprising memory, processor, and computer program stored in the memory and operable on the processor, where the processor executes computer program to operate in a unit of laser focusing unit for adjusting a transmitting head of the carbon dioxide laser to make a spot of the laser enters a center of the entrance aperture of the focusing lens of the focusing lens of the galvanometer assembly, the galvanometer adjusting unit for adjusting height of the galvanometer assembly to make a focus lens center of the galvanometer assembly coincides with a spot of the carbon dioxide laser, the control card reading unit is configured to read the graphic to be marked by the upper computer and determine the motion track by determining the shape of the graphic to be marked, and send the motion track to the laser control car, controlling a correction unit for the laser control card according to the motion trajectory, in the process of marking, the movement of the galvanometer assembly relative to the rotating shaft is controlled in real time by controlling the correcting algorithm, and controlling carbon dioxide laser to emit laser light for marking.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow chart for linkage rotary marking control method (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (III), importing graphic marking to the upper computer is to importing PLT file outputted in CorelDRAW software into the marking software of the upper computer for editing or processing, where the import file types comprises high performance and low overhead logging library (HOGL), bitmap (BMP), autocad, adobe illustrator, pc-format file of mark, portable document format (pdf), mcl, pcx, and sjf. In the step (IV), performing laser control card marking method according to the motion trajectory, marking laser control card on the work piece by the galvanometer assembly according to the motion trajectory, emitting laser by controlling the carbon dioxide laser for marking and driving rotating shaft the work piece to rotate to achieve rotary marking. Preferred Components: In the step (IV), the control correction algorithm is as specified in the equation. The speed ratio of the galvanometer assembly and rotating shaft is kp, i.e. kp = omega (r)/ omega (l) where, when kp=1, there is omega l= omega r, and the laser linear marking path is issued by the carbon dioxide laser, when kp greater than 1, there is omega l less than omega r, and the laser left turn marking path issued by the carbon dioxide laser, and when kp less than 1, there is omega l greater than omega r, and the laser right-turning marking path is issued by the carbon dioxide laser. The mathematical model for controlling the correction is as specified in the equation. The default value of the offset threshold is 100 mm. The incremental rotary encoder is to reconstruct the state of the galvanometer assembly by counting pulses, which is disposed at the rotational positions of the first reflecting mirror and the second reflecting mirror of the galvanometer assembly for performing angle measurement and angular velocity measurement.

PN JP2020045430-A
TI Renewable energy utilization system for use with renewable energy electric power generating apparatus, has methanation installation that synthesizes carbon dioxide and hydrogen to manufacture hydrocarbon fuel including methane.
AU HAGINO T
AE TOHO GAS KK (TOHG-C)
GA 2020238008
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a water electrolysis apparatus (2) which manufactures hydrogen and oxygen using a surplus electric power of a renewable energy electric power generating apparatus (1). A methanation installation (3) synthesizes the hydrogen manufactured with the water electrolysis apparatus, and the carbon dioxide generated within a system, to manufacture hydrocarbon fuel including methane. A biogasification installation (4) includes an activated sludge tank (41), a fermentation tank (42), and a carbon dioxide separation apparatus (43). The water electrolyzer uses the surplus power to produce oxygen together with hydrogen, for supplying the activated sludge tank. The carbon dioxide separated by the carbon dioxide separation apparatus and the hydrogen obtained by the water electrolyzer are synthesized by the methanation installation to manufacture a hydrocarbon fuel including methane.
   USE - Renewable energy utilization system for use with renewable energy electric power generating apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The renewable energy utilization system effectively utilizes oxygen obtained together with hydrogen from surplus electricity in the system. The surplus power is supplied to the water electrolysis apparatus more stably and at low cost, and more stable and inexpensive hydrocarbon fuel such as methane is produced. The renewable energy utilization system with still few environmental loads is realizable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A biomass power generation apparatus generates power by burning biofuel discharged from the biogasification installation, and a carbon dioxide separation and recovery apparatus separates and recovers carbon dioxide from waste gas of the biomass power generation apparatus. The water electrolysis apparatus supplies the oxygen manufactured with hydrogen using the surplus electric power to the combustor of a biomass power generating apparatus or the power-generation-from-waste apparatus. The surplus power is selectively supplied to the water electrolysis device from the renewable energy power generation device, the biomass power generation device, and power-generation-from-waste apparatus. The renewable energy electric power generating apparatus is provided as solar power generation device. The daytime surplus power is supplied from the solar power generation device and nighttime surplus power is supplied from the biomass power generation device or the power-generation-from-waste apparatus. The biomass power generation device or the power-generation-from-waste apparatus is arranged in the same premises as the water electrolysis apparatus. A portion of a hydrocarbon fuel produced in the methanation installation is supplied to the biomass power generation apparatus or the power-generation-from-waste apparatus as an auxiliary fuel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the configuration of the renewable energy utilization system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Renewable energy electric power generating apparatus (1)
   Water electrolysis apparatus (2)
   Methanation installation (3)
   Biogasification installation (4)
   Activated sludge tank (41)
   Fermentation tank (42)
   Carbon dioxide separation apparatus (43)

AB    (CN208446534-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model belongs to the technical field of beverage production, specifically to a steam-water mixing device, comprising a degassing tank, water pump, a carbon dioxide storage bottle, a mixing tank, a chilled water through the degassing tank degassing, freezing water enters into the mixing tank, the water fog sprayed by 2 nozzles for the first time with carbon dioxide gas, second time mixing by film dispersing and storing of system multiple times, to mix the water and carbon dioxide. This utility model structure simple, convenient operation, using the steam-water mixing device water beverage, carbonated beverage according to B/T10792 (steam) to the standard detection, carbon dioxide gas volume (20) reaches 2.5-3.5 degrees centigrade.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of steam-water mixing device.
   Water pump (1)
   Degassing tank (2)

EA    NOVELTY - Steam-water mixing device comprises a degassing tank (2), a water pump (1), a carbon dioxide storage bottle (3), and a mixing tank (6). The mixing tank is connected by a bottom plate and a tank body into a closed container in a flange form, and the upper side portion of the tank body is connected to the bottle through a one-way valve (5). The top of the tank is equipped with a pressure gauge and a safety valve and the side is equipped with an exhaust valve and a liquid level display controller. A water supply pipe runs through and is fixed to the bottom plate, and runs through and is connected to a membrane system. Two nozzles are installed on the top of the water supply pipe. A discharge valve is mounted on the lower portion of the bottom plate of the mixing tank. The membrane system comprises layers of disk-shaped membrane plate and absorption disk. The diameter of the absorption disk matches the membrane plate, whose diameter matches the inner diameter of the mixing tank.
   USE - As steam-water mixing device for beverage production.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has a simple structure and a convenient operation. The device provides a carbon dioxide gas capacity (20 degrees C) of 2.5-3.5 times when soda beverage mixed with the steam-water mixing device is tested according to the B/T10792 (carbonated beverage (soda) standard).

   ADVANTAGE - The method: realize resource utilization of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate having wide industrial use; has high catalytic activity, high chemical selectivity, fast conversion speed, low initial pressure of carbon dioxide, mild reaction condition, low energy consumption, simple catalyst preparation and high stability.

TI New heterogeneous pyridyl salicylimine organic catalyst used for preparing cyclic carbonate.
AU YAVUZ C T
   SUBRAMANIAN S
   KIM D Y
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202088479U
AB    NOVELTY - A heterogeneous pyridyl salicylimine organic catalyst (I), is new.
   USE - New heterogeneous pyridyl salicylimine organic catalyst used for preparing cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterogeneous pyridyl salicylimine organic catalyst provides cyclic carbonate with high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A heterogeneous pyridyl salicylimine organic catalyst of formula (I), is new.
   R1,R2 = H, hydroxy, 1-20C alkyl, or amino;and
   n = 100-100000.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I); and
   (2) production of cyclic carbonate, which involves reacting epoxide and carbon dioxide using the heterogeneous pyridyl salicylimine organic catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is prepared by condensing a pyridine-based aromatic diamine and an aromatic aldehyde in the presence of a solvent. Preferred Components: The compound (I) is of formulae (Ia) or (Ib). The pyridine-based aromatic diamine is 2,6-diaminopyridine. The aromatic aldehyde is chosen from 4-tert-butyl-2,6-diformylphenol and isophthalaldehyde. The solvent is an alcohol. The epoxide is one or more chosen from epichlorohydrin, propylene oxide, epibromohydrin, and 4-fluorostyrene oxide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the pyridine-based aromatic diamine and the aromatic aldehyde is 1:0.1-10. Preferred Conditions: The cyclic carbonate is prepared at atmospheric pressure and temperature of 80-120 degrees C without cocatalyst. Preferred Property: The cyclic carbonate is prepared with selectivity of 99% or more.

   ADVANTAGE - The fat-rich Scenedesmus sp. strain HY-D3 is rich in oil and fat, and has high carbon fixation efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (claimed): No preparation method given. Preferred Conditions: The Scenedesmus HY-D3 is green under microscope, and algae cells often gather into groups. The population cells are arranged in straight line. The single cell of Scenedesmus sp. HY-D3 is oblong, oval, with smooth cell walls and pigment bodies. The single cell is 3-6 micrometer long and 2-3 micrometer wide. The upper and lower ends of cells on both sides of colony each has long, straight or slightly curved spur and has no spines on both ends of middle portion of cell and on free side of side of cell on both sides. The 4-cell population is 10-24 microns wide and 10-13 microns long. The Scenedesmus HY-D3 tolerates concentration of carbon dioxide (CO2) up to 40 vol.% and concentration of silicon dioxide (SO2) up to 400x 10-6 (v/v). The Scenedesmus HY-D3 removes more than 50% of SO2. The light intensity is 1500-20000 lux. The pH value is 6-9 and cultured at 20-40 degrees C. The algae strain utilizes waste gas or flue gas containing CO2 and SO2 to perform autotrophic growth under light to obtain oil-rich biomass. The CO2 content in exhaust gas or flue gas does not exceed 40 vol.%. Preferred Components: The population comprises 4-8 cell. The cell comprises protein nucleus. Preferred Process: The algae strain is cultured with BG11 and SE fresh water medium. The gas with CO2 content of 5-40% is introduced from bottom of reactor.

AU SONG M

   WANG J

   HE L
AE SONG M (SONG-Individual)
GA 2019533319
AB    NOVELTY - Processing polystyrene foam insulation board doped with carbon material comprises preparing a foaming material and performing a foaming operation, where foaming material raw material contains alkaline carbon material and performing foaming operation by supercritical carbon dioxide treatment process.
   USE - The processing polystyrene foam insulation board doped with carbon material is useful for promoting effect on the electrical conductivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline carbon material is a carbon material treated with an alkali solution and the alkali solution is potassium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide solution. The carbon material is carbon nanotubes and/or activated carbon and/or graphene. Preferred Composition: The foaming material raw material comprises 90-100 pts. wt., preferably 95-98 pts. wt. polystyrene and 0.1-5 pts. wt., preferably 2.5-3.8 pts. wt. alkaline carbon material. Preferred Method: In the method, mixing the foaming material by a high shear emulsifier and foaming; the supercritical carbon dioxide treatment process comprises two process steps: stirring step and foaming step, where in the stirring step filling the raw material of foaming material with carbon dioxide, treating the raw material of foaming material by the high shear emulsifier, stirring, where stirring time is not less than 0.5 hours. The temperature of the foaming operation is not lower than 100 degrees C. The temperature of the foaming operation is 120-150 degrees C. The rotation speed of the high shear emulsifier for the foaming material raw material is not less than 3000 revolutions/minute, and the action time is not less than 5 hours.

AU SHEN W
   DU X
   ZHANG D
   XU G
   ZHANG B
   JI X
   TANG X
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 201880426H
AB    NOVELTY - Improving stability of steel slag aggregate                comprises screening and grading the slag grain                crushing iron to obtain obtaining steel slag with                different grain diameter, and then carrying out the                carbonized treatment using high pressure carbon                dioxide gas.
   USE - The method is useful for improving stability                of steel slag aggregate.
   ADVANTAGE - The steel slag aggregate has excellent                stability, and improved utilization rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of calcium                  oxide in the steel slag particles is above 20%,                  and the total content of R2O is not greater than                  3%. The conditions of carbonization treatment                  are: carbon dioxide partial pressure is 0.05-25                  MPa, the relative humidity of the environment is                  60-95%, and carbonization strength is 2-200 MPa.h                  at room temperature. The steel slag needs to be                  turned over many times during the carbonization                  process. The steel slag particles are screened by                  less than or equal to 2.36 mm, less than or equal                  to 4.75 mm, less than or equal to 9.5 mm,                  2.36-4.75 mm, 4.75-9.5 mm, 9.5-19 mm, 9.5-26.5                  mm, and 19-37.5 mm are respectively. The                  carbonization strength of steel slag particles                  below 2.36 mm is 2-10 MPa.h, the carbonization                  strength of steel slag particles below 4.75 mm is                  2-15 MPa.h, the carbonization strength of steel                  slag particles below 9.5 mm is 2-18 MPa.h, the                  carbonization strength of steel slag particles                  between 2.36 and 4.75 mm is 5-20 MPa.h, the                  carbonization strength of steel slag particles                  between 4.75-9.5 mm is 10-40 MPa.h, the                  carbonization strength of steel slag particles                  between 9.5-19 mm is 20-80 MPa.h, the                  carbonization strength of steel slag particles                  between 9.5-26.5 mm is 30-120 MPa.h, and the                  carbonization strength of the steel slag                  particles between 19-37.5 mm is 50-200 MPa.h. The                  carbonized steel slag is further formed into                  cement mortar, cement concrete, permeable                  concrete or asphalt concrete aggregate.

   FETVEDT J E
   FORREST B A
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 2022C1458J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves compressing a stream                comprising carbon dioxide to form a compressed                stream comprising carbon dioxide. The compressed                stream is heated in a recuperator heat exchanger                with heat withdrawn from a stream of combustion                products arising from combustion of a fuel source                e.g. natural gas, and another fuel source i.e.                dilute hydrocarbon stream, without combustion,                where the oxidizing is carried out separate from                the combustion of the former fuel source.                Concentration of hydrocarbons in the dilute stream                is below a lower explosive limit (LEL) of the                hydrocarbon.
   USE - Method for heating a stream i.e. gas stream                such as hydrocarbon and diluents, for energy                production in a petroleum industry and a geologic                formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables processing of a dilute                hydrocarbon stream such that the hydrocarbons in                the stream are oxidized without substantial                combustion and thusly impart added energy to the                power production cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of                  hydrocarbons in the dilute hydrocarbon stream is                  below the lower explosive limit (LEL) of the                  hydrocarbons. Preferred Components: The                  hydrocarbons in the dilute hydrocarbon stream are                  catalytically oxidized. The stream of combustion                  products is expanded in a turbine to produce                  power before being passed to the recuperator heat                  exchanger. The hydrocarbons in the dilute                  hydrocarbon stream are oxidized within the                  recuperator heat exchanger. The hydrocarbons in                  the dilute hydrocarbon stream are oxidized in a                  further heat exchanger. The dilute hydrocarbon                  stream is input to an oxidation reactor. The                  reaction stream exiting the oxidation reactor is                  input to the recuperator heat exchanger.

GA 202205669X
AB    NOVELTY - A perishable fresh food is packaged by (a) providing packaging material that creates a protective atmosphere by a non-stoichiometric reaction of a carbon dioxide generating activator included in the form of a polymer coating in a material forming the packaging material; (b) supplying perishable fresh food into the packaging; and (c) allowing contact of exudates produced by the packaged food with the coating while simultaneously preventing reabsorption of the exudates into the food.
   USE - The method is useful for packaging perishable fresh food.
   ADVANTAGE - Compared to the conventional gas replacement packaging method, the method does not require an external element and provides effective concentration of carbon dioxide for a long period of time. The packaging material can maintain the food and prevent the exudate eluted from the food from being reabsorbed into the food.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of how the packaging material functions by generating carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Carbon dioxide generating activator is a mixture of citric acid and sodium hydrogen carbonate. Active agent is a mixture of 30-50 wt.% sodium bicarbonate and 50-70 wt.% citric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Polymer coating applied to the packaging material is a coating solution obtained by dissolving a polymer selected from polyamide, polylactic acid, polycaprolactone, or polystyrene in a solvent selected from ethanol, acetone, or ethyl acetate. Packaging material further comprises a separation sheet on which food is supplied at the same time as a bottom surface of which the food is accommodated is formed in a cell pattern and forms a double bottom, and the separation sheet is on the side in contact with the food and has a funnel-shaped hole in the opposite direction to allow exudate to pass through the separation sheet only in the direction towards the packaging material.

TI Method to reduce content of methylene diurea in urea products involves eluting methanol flash vapor water to remove methanol, performing pressure swing adsorption decarburization, separating carbon dioxide in degassing, and washing carbon dioxide gas with desalinated water to remove methanol.
AU MA J
   HE S
   ZU Y
   XU T
   CHEN B
AE SHANDONG LEAGUE CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C7753J
AB    NOVELTY - Method to reduce content of methylene diurea in urea products involves eluting methanol flash vapor water to remove methanol, performing pressure swing adsorption decarburization, adsorbing and separating carbon dioxide in degassing, and washing carbon dioxide gas from pressure swing adsorption decarbonization with desalinated water to remove methanol.
   USE - Method to reduce content of methylene diurea in urea products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the content of methylene diurea in urea product from 15 mg/kg to 2 mg/kg to reach the vehicle urea standard, and reduce methanol content in the methanol flash gas from 2030 g/m3 to 50100 mg/m3. The removal of 99.5% and 99.8% of methanol in the gas not only greatly reduces the methanol content in the gas, but also recovers 782.79 tons of methanol, and increases the benefit by 1.8 million yuan.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The methanol flash vapor comprises 1.9-2.1 vol.% carbon monoxide, 4.5-4.9 vol.% carbon dioxide, 3.6-3.8 vol.% methane, 64.1-64.5 vol.% hydrogen, 25 g/m3 methanol and balance nitrogen. The component used for degassing comprises 7.4-7.6 vol.% carbon monoxide, 25-25.2 vol.% carbon dioxide, 0.3-0.5 vol.% methane, 54-54.5 %mass hydrogen, 12-13 vol.% nitrogen, 0.05-0.15 vol.% argon, 0.8-1.2 mg/m3 hydrogen sulfide, and 5.5-6.5 mg/m3 methanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: Adsorbents used in the pressure swing adsorption decarburization comprise HXAL-01 activated alumina adsorbent, silica gel HXSI-01 adsorbent, and activated carbon HXBC-15B adsorbent.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2021171317-A1; IN202021008375-A; IN396269-B
TI One pot synthesizing dimethyl ether comprises separating carbon dioxide from first stream comprising syngas, reacting second stream in presence of catalyst, cooling third stream and washing and conducting phase separation of fourth stream.
AU APTE A J
   BADHE R M
AE AMOL CARBONS PRIVATE LTD (AMOL-Non-standard)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 2021A2117N
AB    NOVELTY - One pot synthesizing dimethyl ether, comprises: separating carbon dioxide from a first stream (512) comprising syngas to produce a second stream (522), where the second stream is lean in carbon dioxide content; reacting the second stream in the presence of a catalyst to produce a third stream (532) comprising dimethyl ether, methanol, water, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen; cooling the third stream to a temperature of 10-40 degrees C to produce a fourth stream (542); and washing and conducting a phase separation of the fourth stream to produce a product stream (572) comprising at least 10 vol.% of dimethyl ether.
   USE - The process is useful for one pot synthesizing dimethyl ether.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is simple and economic; and eliminates the requirement of refrigeration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows process for the production of dimethyl ether from syngas.
   First stream (512)
   Second stream (522)
   Third stream (532)
   Fourth stream (542)
   Product stream (574)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process comprises: separating the carbon dioxide as a carbon dioxide stream from the first stream through a water wash followed by carbon dioxide stripping; reacting the second stream at an enhanced temperature of 200-300 degrees C and pressure of 20-30 bar in the presence of at least two catalysts; recycling at least a part of the sixth stream as a recycling stream to combine with the first stream for enhancing conversion of the syngas to dimethyl ether; removing methanol and water from a fifth stream through condensation, phase separation, and/or distillation; and washing the fourth stream before conducting the phase separation. The step of cooling does not comprise chilling or refrigerating. The step of washing comprises using a hydrocarbon solvent scrubber producing a sixth stream comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen. In the process, more than 60% of the sixth stream is recycled to combine with the first stream. The content of carbon dioxide in the second stream is less than 1% the total contents of the second stream.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of method generating dimethyl ether-diesel mixture that do not support the claims.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2021086483-A2; WO2021086483-A3; US2022297083-A1
TI Preparation of composition used for preparing            coordination polymer, involves providing supercritical            carbon dioxide and coordination polymer precursor(s) to            mixing section, and providing obtained mixture to            continuous flow reactor.
AU RASMUSSEN E G
   KRAMLICH J C
   NOVOSSELOV I V
AE UNIV WASHINGTON SEATTLE (UNIW-C)
   UNIV WASHINGTON SEATTLE (UNIW-C)
GA 2021479573
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of coordination polymer                composition involves providing a supercritical                carbon dioxide and one or more coordination polymer                precursors to a mixing section to obtain a mixture                of the supercritical carbon dioxide and one or more                coordination polymer precursors, and providing the                mixture to a continuous flow reactor for sufficient                period of time.
   USE - Preparation of composition used for preparing                coordination polymer (claimed). Uses include but                are not limited to catalysis application, gas                storage, drug deliver, and energy storage                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is rapid, efficient,                environmentally-friendly, and economical, and                enables preparation of coordination polymer                composition with increased surface area and                porosity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a system, which consists of a mixing                section containing an inlet (i) connected to a                precursor supply, an inlet (ii) connected to a                supercritical carbon dioxide supply, and an outlet                (o1), and a continuous flow reactor containing an                inlet connected to the outlet of the mixing section                and an outlet; and
   (2) the coordination polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The system further                  consists of a pressure regulator downstream from                  the outlet of the continuous flow reactor. The                  pressure regulator is a back pressure regulator,                  a heat exchanger in substantial thermal contact                  with the outlet of the continuous flow reactor, a                  collection vessel upstream from the back pressure                  regulator, a supercritical carbon dioxide supply                  (s2) connected to the inlet of the collection                  vessel, a collection vessel downstream (v2) from                  the back pressure regulator. The collection                  vessel is downstream from the heat exchanger. The                  collection vessel further consists of a filter,                  an inlet. The collection vessel (v2) is                  configured to collect effluent. The collection                  vessel (v2) comprises an outlet connected to the                  precursor supply.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-ion source                  is a metal oxide or metal salt e.g. an oxide or a                  salt of zirconium, copper, zinc, cobalt, indium,                  gallium, iron, nickel, aluminum, chromium,                  manganese, beryllium, and magnesium. The                  metal-ion source further comprises zirconyl                  chloride octahydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic linker is                  a carboxylic acid e.g. terephthalic acid,                  terephthalic acid with one or more                  additives/modulators such as nitrogen dioxide or                  azanide or methane or chlorine or formic acid,                  trimesic acid, 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid,                  2-hydroxyterephthalic acid, biphenyl-3, 3', 5,                  5'-tetracarboxylic acid,                  1,3,5-tris(4-carboxyphenyl)benzene,                  2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid,                  9,10-anthracenedicarboxylic acid,                  (1,1':4',1'')terphenyl-3,3'',5,5''tetracarboxylic                  acid, 3,3',5,5'-tetracarboxydiphenylmethane,                  1,2,4,5-tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)-benzene,                  malonic acid, butanedioic acid, pentanedioic                  acid, citric acid, oxalic acid, fumaric acid,                  pentetic acid, and                  1,3,5,7-adamantanetetracarboxylic acid, and                  adenosine diphosphate, an imidazole derivative                  e.g. imidazole, 2-methylimidazole, and                  tri(4-imidazolylphenyl)amine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The coordination                  polymer is metal-organic framework (MOF), and/or                  covalent organic framework (COF). The mixture of                  the supercritical carbon dioxide and one or more                  coordination polymer precursors comprise                  homogeneous medium. The coordination polymer                  precursor(s) comprises a metal-ion source and an                  organic linker. The coordination polymer                  precursor(s) further comprises a solvent.                  Preferred Process: The reactor is maintained at a                  temperature sufficient to obtain the coordination                  polymer composition and/or maintain supercritical                  conditions. The sufficient temperature is 30-600                  degrees C (for example 80-160 degrees C,                  preferably 120 degrees C). The reactor is                  maintained at a pressure sufficient to maintain                  supercritical condition. The sufficient pressure                  is 7.3-30 MPa (for example, 7.3-20 MPa,                  preferably 10 MPa). The reactor is maintained at                  a temperature of 90-150 degrees C (e.g., 110-130                  degrees C) and at a pressure of 8.5-11 MPa (e.g.,                  9-11 MPa). The mixture is provided to the reactor                  at a flow rate of 0.1-100 mL/minute (e.g., 0.1-50                  mL/minute, preferably 1-30 mL/minute). The period                  of time sufficient to obtain the coordination                  polymer composition is 0.01 second to 20 minutes,                  preferably 1-5 seconds. The period of time                  sufficient to obtain the coordination polymer                  composition is 0.01 seconds to 30 minutes,                  preferably 20-30 minutes. The method further                  involves providing gaseous carbon dioxide at a                  temperature and/or pressure sufficient to form                  liquid carbon dioxide, maintaining liquid carbon                  dioxide at pressure and/or temperature sufficient                  to form supercritical carbon dioxide, providing                  gaseous carbon dioxide at a temperature and/or                  pressure sufficient to form supercritical carbon                  dioxide, removing carbon dioxide after obtaining                  the coordination polymer composition, separating                  the unreacted coordination polymer precursors                  from the coordination polymer composition,                  treating the coordination polymer composition                  with additional supercritical carbon dioxide, and                  collecting the unreacted coordination polymer                  precursors are collected and provided to the                  mixing section. The separation is performed by                  filtration, centrifugation, sonication, gravity                  assist, or chromatography.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Device for sending air into the ground, comprises a fan sucking the air in order to send it into a pipe, the underground portion of which is pierced, pierced tubing makes it possible to recover the carbon dioxide released into the soil.
AU CHOQUET J
   DESVAUD J
   JEAN-LOUIS D
   JEAN-PIERRE C
AE CHOQUET J (CHOQ-Individual)
   DESVAUD J (DESV-Individual)
   CHOQUET J P (CHOQ-Individual)
   DESVAUD J L (DESV-Individual)
GA 202142665B
AB    NOVELTY - The device comprises a fan (2) sucking the air (3) in order to send it into a pipe (4), the underground portion (5) of which is pierced. The pierced tubing makes it possible to recover the carbon dioxide released into the soil by the roots, fauna and microorganisms, in order to reject it (6), through the flow generated by the fan, inside a closed greenhouse. The device supplies the leafy aerial portions with carbon dioxide for photosynthesis in the diurnal period. A photo voltaic cell provided with a voltage regulator supplies the fan.
   USE - Device for sending air into the ground.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide improves photosynthesis, by the leaves of plants, during the day, in the confined enclosure of the greenhouse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a device for sending air into the ground.

PT P
PN CN112480713-A
TI Preparation of nano calcium carbonate for filling polyethylene transparent films, involves introducing calcium hydroxide suspension into carbon dioxide mixed gas, adding monolauryl phosphate, and surface modifying.
AU LI H
   ZHU Y
   LU C
   LU Q
   YANG A
   FAN Y
   LIU Y
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021308565
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate, involves calcining limestone at 1050-1250 degrees C to obtain quicklime, digesting quicklime with water to obtain calcium hydroxide, sieving, leaving still, configuring into a calcium hydroxide suspension with a solid content of 6-11.5%, introducing the suspension into a mixed gas of 20-30% carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 0.005-0.015 m2/hour, adding monolauryl phosphate to the suspension when the pH is not more than 8, stirring, continuously introducing carbon dioxide for reaction, continuously introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas when the pH is 7 or less, ensuring that the pH is 7 or less and stable for 0.5-1.5 hours, adding an aluminate coupling agent solution to the suspension for wet surface modification, filtering, drying, and sieving.
   USE - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate for filling polyethylene transparent films.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides nano calcium carbonate having excellent dispersibility and compatibility with polyethylene, and complete particle shape, and provides polyethylene transparent films having excellent mechanical property and processability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The addition amount of monolauryl phosphate is 0.15-0.5% of the mass of calcium carbonate on dry basis. The addition amount of aluminate coupling agent solution is 5-15 %mass. The concentration of aluminate coupling agent solution is 50 %mass. The content of magnesium carbonate in limestone is 0.2% or less. Preferred Conditions: The carbonation reaction is carried out at 20-25 degrees C and 600-900 rpm. Surface modification is carried out at 70-90 degrees C and 300-500 rpm for 30-60 minutes. Drying is carried out for 12-18 hours at 120-135 degrees C.

TI Polymerizable composition useful for preparing            polymer, composite materials, casting compounds and            lacquers, comprises polyisocyanate composition-A,            trimerization catalyst-B i.e. carboxylate and carbon            dioxide.
AU MEISENHEIMER R D
   HEINZ P
   ACHTEN D
   GOLLING F
   LAAS H
   MAGER D
   MEISENHEIMER R D M
AE COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 2021064618
AB    NOVELTY - Polymerizable composition comprises (i) a                polyisocyanate composition-A i.e. oligomeric                polyisocyanate including at least 1 wt.% isocyanate                and at least 80 wt.% polyisocyanate, (ii) at least                one trimerization catalyst-B i.e. carboxylate; and                (iii) at least 150 ppm carbon dioxide. The molar                ratio of isocyanate groups to isocyanate-reactive                groups is 1.5:1.
   USE - The polymerizable composition is useful for                preparing polymer, composite materials, casting                compounds, lacquers, adhesives and                three-dimensional printed components.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is economical and avoids                waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) use of carbon dioxide to increase the pot                life of a polymerizable composition;
   (2) method for preparing polymerizable                composition with increased pot life; and
   (3) process for producing the polymer,                comprising (a) providing a reaction mixture of                polyisocyanate composition-A, (b) at least one                trimerization catalyst-B i.e. carboxylate, and (c)                at least 150 ppm carbon dioxide based liquid                constituents of the polymerizable composition, and                (d) curing the reaction mixture at 80-300 degrees                C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxylate                  includes a phosphonium, ammonium or a metal ion                  as a counter ion. The carboxylate has a pKa of at                  least 3.5-5. The polyisocyanate includes 20 wt.%                  oligomers having biuret groups. The amount of                  mono- and polyisocyanates with aromatically bound                  isocyanate group in the polyisocyanate                  component-A is 50 wt.%. The polyisocyanate                  composition-A and trimerization catalyst-B                  achieves the gel point at 23 degrees C within                  1-22 hours and carbon dioxide concentrations at                  90 ppm. The concentration of the polymerizable                  composition is achieved using a polyisocyanate                  composition-A having a suitable carbon dioxide                  content.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Serum carbon dioxide detection kit comprises reagent comprising tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane buffer salt, disodium edetate, magnesium acetate, sodium oxalate, glucose, phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, glucose dehydrogenase and water.
AU LIANG H
   XU Y
   LI C
   HU M
   ZHANG J
   WU Z
AE ANHUI EPNKAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C1295R
AB    NOVELTY - Serum carbon dioxide detection kit comprises reagent (R) comprising 50-150 mmol/l tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (Tris) buffer salt, 0.4-1 g/l disodium edetate, 1-3 g/l magnesium acetate, 1-3 g/l sodium oxalate, 10-20 g/l glucose, 0.3-1.7 g/l phosphoenolpyruvate, 0.2-2.2 g/l reduced 3-acetylpyridine adenine dinucleotide, 1.2-3.8 KU/l malate dehydrogenase, 0.5-2.5 KU/l phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, 1-2 KU/l glucose dehydrogenase, and purified water as solvent.
   USE - As serum carbon dioxide detection kit.
   ADVANTAGE - The kit has the beneficial effects of solving the problem of degradation of 3-acetylpyridine adenine dinucleotide under the pH of 7.5 condition, causing the quality of the reagent to decrease, and simultaneously improving the stability of the reagent kit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the serum carbon dioxide detection kit, involving preparing the reagent (R) in the kit, by mixing the raw materials based on the concentration to obtain reagent (R).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The reagent (R) includes 50 mmol/l tris buffer salt, 0.7g/L disodium edetate, 1 g/l magnesium acetate, 1 g/l sodium oxalate, 10 g/l glucose, 1 g/l phosphoenolpyruvate, 1.2 g/l 3-acetylpyridine adenine dinucleotide, 2.5 KU/l malate dehydrogenase, 1.5 KU/l phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, 1 KU/l glucose dehydrogenase, and purified water as solvent. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the reagent (R) includes mixing the raw materials based on the concentration to obtain the reagent (R). The preparation method of the reagent (R) includes: dissolving tris buffer salt in purified water, adding disodium edetate, magnesium acetate, sodium oxalate and glucose, after stirring, adding phosphoenolpyruvate and 3-acetylpyridine adenine dinucleotide; adjusting the pH value of the solution to 7.50 plus minus 0.05; and adding phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, malate dehydrogenase, and glucose dehydrogenase, and mixing to prepare the reagent (R). Preferred Properties: The pH of the reagent (R) is 7.5 plus minus 0.05. The pH of the tris buffer is 6-9.

TI Hydrogen storage method involves reacting carbon dioxide with amine to form an adduct comprising captured carbon dioxide and hydrogenating the captured carbon dioxide in the presence of hydrogenation solvent to form formates.
AU LIN H
AE UNIV WASHINGTON STATE (UNWS-C)
GA 2020090325
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen storage method involves (a) reacting carbon dioxide (CO2) with amine to form an adduct comprising captured CO2; and (b) hydrogenating the captured CO2 in the presence of a hydrogenation solvent to form formates.
   USE - A hydrogen storage method for producing formate for liquid hydrogen carrier (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method may result in a faster rate of hydrogen formation (as expressed as a lower or reduced time of hydrogen formation) as compared to conventional processes. The efficiency capturing and/or hydrogenation of CO2 is greater than conventional systems, which can increase the overall yield of hydrogen extracted. The dehydrogenation and release of hydrogen from the adduct can be faster than conventional systems. The method can result in a substantially pure hydrogen in the form of H2 and can, not only store hydrogen, but also release it in a recyclable or renewable fashion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for liquid hydrogen carrier comprising formate-piperidine adduct comprising hydrogenated CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Catalyst is a homogeneous catalyst. Catalyst is a heterogeneous catalyst. Catalyst comprises metal M. The catalyst is a component of a hydrogen generation system. The hydrogen generation system is capable of providing high-purity hydrogen gas to a fuel cell.M=Li, Be, Na, Mg, Al, K, Ca, Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Ga, Rb, Sr, Y, Zr, Nb, Mo, Tc, Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag, Cd, In, Sn, Cs, Ba, La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Pm, Sm, Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Rm, Yb, Lu, Hf, W, Re, Os, Ir, Pt, Au, Hg, Tl, Pb, Bi, Po, Ra, Ac, Th, Pa, U, Np, Am, Cm, Bk, Cf, Es, Fm, Md, No, Lr, Rf, Db, Sg, Bh, Hs, Mt, Ds, Rg, Cn, Nh, Fl, Me, and/or Lv.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further comprises (c) dehydrogenating the formates with a catalyst to generate hydrogen (H2). Preferred Compositions: Amine is selected from monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, ethane-1,2-diamine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, 2-amino-2-methyl-1,3-propanediol, 2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol, piperazine, piperidine, 1-methyl piperazine, and/or 2-methyl piperazine, preferably piperidine. Adduct formed in step (a) is a formate-pipridine adduct. Hydrogenation solvent comprises water, acetonitrile, or tetrahydrofuran. Hydrogenation solvent comprises an alcohol selected from methanol, ethanol, propanol (e.g. 1-propanol or 2-propanol), or butanol. Alcohol is present in an amount of 0-100 vol.% or wt.% of the hydrogenation solution. Water, acetonitrile, or tetrahydrofuran is present in an amount of 0-100 vol.% or wt.% of the hydrogenation solution. Preferred Conditions: Reaction temperature of steps (a), (b), (c), steps (a) and (b), steps (a) and (c), or steps (b) and (c) is 20-100 degrees C. Reaction temperature of step (a), step (b), or both is 20-40 degrees C, preferably 30 degrees C. Reaction temperature of step (c) is 60-100 degrees C, preferably 80-90 degrees C.

AU YAN G
AE XUZHOU RUIOUDA MACHINERY TECHNOLOGY CO (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201910210J
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of magnesite comprises calcining magnesite at 800 degrees C for 5 hours, where magnesium carbonate is decomposed to generate magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide, obtaining light burned magnesium oxide, adding the light burned magnesium oxide into water, introducing carbon dioxide gas under stirring condition, generating magnesium bicarbonate, using a plate-type filter to separate the magnesium bicarbonate solution and impurities, heating the magnesium bicarbonate solution at 100 degrees C, generating calcium carbonate precipitates, using the plate-type filter to separate the calcium carbonate precipitates and impurities, drying the calcium carbonate precipitates, calcining for 6 hours at 1000 degrees C to obtain high-purity calcium oxide, pressing the high-purity calcium oxide into magnesium oxide balls by a ball pressing machine, and calcining at 1800 degrees C for 8 hours to obtain high purity calcium oxide.

PN CN112661177-A
TI Environmentally friendly process for producing lithium carbonate by lithium polymer comprises subjecting lithium polymer to calcination transformation, leaching, causticizing, preforming carbonization reaction and packaging.
AU HE K
   HE D
   WU Z
   WANG L
   XU X
   YANG X
   DAI D
   HUANG J
AE GUANGXI TIANYUAN NEW ENERGY MATERIALS CO (FCET-C)
GA 202151687D
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally friendly process for producing lithium carbonate by lithium polymer comprises (i) subjecting lithium polymer to calcination transformation, recycling the smoke and dust generated during the calcination process; (ii) adding calcium carbonate slurry to slurry for stirring and leaching in a leaching tank; (iii) filtering pure liquid again to obtain a pure liquid and purified filter residue, causticizing purified liquid; (v) passing filtered causticizing liquid to freezing workshop to freeze and separate sodium sulfate decahydrate and lithium hydroxide solution, and freezing at -5 to -20 degrees C; (vi) concentrating sodium sulfate decahydrate and purifying by evaporation; (vii) centrifugally separating and dissolving refined lithium hydroxide slurry; (viii) centrifuging and separating carbonized reaction liquid; and (x) conveying centrifugal solid phase to a disc dryer, performing automatic packaging by system, stacking, allowing the finished product enter into warehouse.
   USE - The method is useful for environmentally friendly process for producing lithium carbonate by lithium polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes raw material, which is a new type of mineral source with high grade, great extraction value, and great economic benefits and double-shaft humidifying stirrer, for dust collecting position, humidify the dust, reducing the risk of dust emission and recycling and reusing effective components.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Environmentally friendly process for producing lithium carbonate by lithium polymer comprises (i) subjecting lithium polymer to calcination transformation at 950-1200 degrees C, recycling the smoke and dust generated during the calcination process to the dual-shaft humidifying stirrer for humidification through the exhaust gas treatment system, adding to the rotary kiln for calcination, cooling calcined lithium polymer, finely grinding, adding acid to react, and cooling to less than or equal to 90 degrees C, adjusting into a slurry with water until the solid content of the slurry is 10-70%; (ii) adding calcium carbonate slurry to the slurry for stirring and leaching in a leaching tank at less than or equal to 60 degrees C at a pH 5.5-6 for 20 minutes, adding calcium oxide to adjust the pH to 8-9, using a filter press to filter, purifying filtered supernatant with lithium hydroxide mother liquor or calcium oxide, adjusting the pH to 9-12, and removing the impurities of iron, manganese, aluminum and calcium in the filtered supernatant; (iii) filtering pure liquid again to obtain a pure liquidand a purified filter residue, causticizing pure liquid with a 50% alkaline solution or crude mother liquor, where the pH of the causticized solution is 11-14, and the temperature is normal temperature; (iv) filtering the solution after causticization to obtain causticizing liquid and causticizing filter residue, controlling equivalent content of lithium hydroxide in the causticizing liquid within 30-75 g/l, passing causticizing liquid through a precision filter and filtering to remove part of calcium ions; (v) passing filtered causticizing liquid to the freezing workshop to freeze and separate sodium sulfate decahydrate and lithium hydroxide solution, and freezing at -5 to -20 degrees C; (vi) concentrating sodium sulfate decahydrate and purifying by evaporation, heating crystal water to obtain anhydrous sodium sulfate at 200-800 degrees C, filtering lithium hydroxide solution through a precision filter to remove a portion of calcium ions, evaporating. concentrating, crystallizing concentrated crude lithium hydroxide solution, centrifuging, re-melting, filtering by a precision filter, filtering and then concentrating by mechanical vapor recompression (MVR) evaporation to obtain a fine lithium hydroxide slurry; (vii) centrifugally separating and dissolving refined lithium hydroxide slurry, allowing to enter into a carbonization kettle, and introducing carbon dioxide gas with a pressure of 0.5MPa for carbonization reaction, and keeping the gauge pressure in the carbonization kettle under 0.06-0.08 MPa at 48-52 degrees C, stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide when the pressure stabilizes, and opening a vent valve of the carbonization kettle to vent the excess carbon dioxide to obtain the carbonization reaction liquid; (viii) centrifuging and separating the carbonized reaction liquid to obtain solid lithium carbonate, drying to make the water content of the solid lithium carbonate reach less than or equal to 5%, where centrifugal liquid obtained by centrifugal separation contains a small amount of lithium carbonate and pumping the centrifugal liquid into the lithium sulfate finishing liquid production section of the slurry mixing process; and (x) conveying centrifugal solid phase to a disc dryer by a closed conveyor for drying until the moisture content of the material is less than 0.2%, passing dried material pneumatically to the lithium carbonate product warehouse, and the material from the disc dryer to a raw material distribution system, through a jet pulverization system, performing automatic packaging by system, stacking, allowing the finished product enter into warehouse, where the finished product control magnetic substance is not greater than 30 PP.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the fine grinding particle size is required to be 200 meshes, acid is added for reaction, sulfuric acid with a concentration of 98% is added, where the acid and material ratio is 2-5:1, acid reaction is cooled to below 60 degrees C by water cooling. In the step (ii), the solid content of the calcium carbonate slurry is 10-55%, the calcium oxide added to adjust the pH value is a powder with a mass fraction of more than 75% or a slurry with a solid content of 10-55%, the filter cake filtered by the filter press is rinsed with tap water or process water, and the filter cake is purged with compressed air to make the water content of the filter cake less than or equal to 20%, and the rinsing water is returned for slurry adjustment, if the lithium hydroxide mother liquor is added, the concentration of the lithium hydroxide mother liquor is 10-50% during purification, if calcium oxide is added, the calcium oxide is a powder with a mass fraction of more than 75% or a calcium oxide slurry with a solid content of 10-55%. In the step (iii), the purified filter residue is mixed with water to form a slurry with a solid content of 10-70%, and then returned to step (i) and the alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide solution, lithium hydroxide solution, or a mixed solution of two. In the step (iv), the method comprises returning to the purification process after adding water to the causticizing filter residue to adjust to a slurry with a solid content of 10-70%.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC A10-E05B; A12-W11; E11-W; E33-G

AU WANG S

   WANG W
   XING L
   HE Q
AE SHAANXI HANGUANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201903263Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ailanthone comprises e.g. (i) taking dried Ailanthus altissima fruit, Ailanthus altissima bark, Ailanthus altissima root bark, Ailanthus altissima twig, Ailanthus altissima leaf, Ailanthus altissima flower, Ailanthus altissima seeds and/or dried Ailanthus altissima bark in traditional Chinese medicine, and crushing to obtain substrate, (ii) mixing biological fermenting agent with the substrate prepared in step (i), adding 10-40% ethanol solution, carrying out fermentation, and filtering to obtain fermentation liquid, and concentrating to obtain fermentation product, (iii) processing the fermentation product obtained in step (ii) with chromatographic separation or chromatographic separation and supercritical carbon dioxide extracting to obtain ailanthone crude product, optionally (iv) re-crystallizing the ailanthone crude product obtained in step (iii) to obtain ailanthone pure product.
   USE - The ailanthone is useful for preparing antiviral drug for treating hepatitis B virus, rotavirus virus, Norwalk virus, Astroviridae virus, intestinal virus and influenza virus (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ailanthone comprises (i) taking dried Ailanthus altissima fruit, Ailanthus altissima bark, Ailanthus altissima root bark, Ailanthus altissima twig, Ailanthus altissima leaf, Ailanthus altissima flower, Ailanthus altissima seeds and/or dried Ailanthus altissima bark in traditional Chinese medicine, and crushing to obtain substrate, (ii) mixing biological fermenting agent with the substrate prepared in step (i), adding 10-40% ethanol solution, adjusting pH value to 5.5-8.2, carrying out fermentation at 38-65 degrees C for 8-48 hours, and filtering to obtain fermentation liquid, and concentrating to obtain fermentation product, (iii) processing the fermentation product obtained in step (ii) with chromatographic separation or chromatographic separation and supercritical carbon dioxide extracting to obtain ailanthone crude product, optionally (iv) re-crystallizing the ailanthone crude product obtained in step (iii) to obtain ailanthone pure product, where crushing in step (i) preferably is crushing to 10-24 meshes, in the step (ii), the mixing method of the biological fermenting agent and substrate is preferably (a) mixing biological fermenting agent with substrate prepared in step (i), uniformly stirring, then transferring to a fermentation tank; or (b) uniformly paving biological fermenting agent at every 5 cm of the thickness of substrate layer in the fermentation tank, the biological fermenting agent is food and medicine fermenting enzyme preparation, natural fungus (including plant endophyte), bio derived bacteria, the food and medicine fermenting enzyme preparation is preferably amyloglucosidase, alpha -acetolactate decarboxylase, cellulase, papain, subtilisin; the natural fungus is natural edible medicinal fungi (including Hericium erinaceus, Paecilomyces hepiali, penicillium mycelium, mycelium), plant endophyte (including endophytic fungi, preferably Pestalotiopsis sp, taxomyces andreanae, endophytic bacteria is preferably Bacillus subtilis, Klebsiella oxytoca, the endophytic actinomyces is preferably Actinomyces bovis, Streptomyces sp.Tc022), The bio-derived bacteria is preferably human Lactobacillus acidophilus, Lactobacillus plantarum, lactobacillus bifidus, Bifidobacterium longum, the dosage of biological fermenting agent is substrate quality of 1-5%, preferably 2-4%, the dosage of the ethanol solution is 20-80% of the substrate quality, in step (iii), the chromatographic separation is preferably silica gel column chromatography, gel column chromatography, macroporous resin column chromatography, reverse phase silica gel column chromatography, preferably the condition of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, optionally with methanol or ethanol as solvent, extracting pressure is 40 MPa, the temperature is 35 degrees C, separating kettle I pressure is 8 MPa and the temperature is 35 degrees C, separating kettle II pressure is 6 MPa, the temperature is 35 degrees C, in step (iv) the ailanthone with purity of more than 98% and yield of ailanthone is more than 2.5%.
   R1-R5 = H, OH;
   either R3, R4 = H, OH; or
   R3R4 = O;
   dotted line = single bond or non-existence; and
   X = vinyl-yl, ethane-1,1-diyl or oxirane-2,2-diyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) ailanthone crystal form I, where the X-ray powder diffraction pattern expressed in 2( theta ) d diffraction angle using Cu/K( alpha ) radiation is given as specification, where the ailanthone crystal form I by thermogravimetric analysis, at 184+1 degrees C to weightlessness, weightlessness is 93.4+2-643+1 degrees C, the ailanthone crystal form I prepared by crystallizing ailanthone crude product with suitable solvent, separating and drying, where the ailanthone product in mass content of more than 75%, the devitrification can be stationary, or can be stirring, separating comprises filtering or centrifuging and collecting the separated solid, drying temperature is 20-50 degrees C, can be normal pressure drying or can be vacuum drying, the solvent is dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, tetrahydrofuran, dioxane, ethyl acetate, methyl acetate, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, benzene, toluene, acetic acid, formic acid, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, glycol dimethyl ether, ether, isopropyl ether, acetonitrile, water, petroleum ether, normal hexane, pentane, acetone, preferably dichloromethane, chloroform, toluene, ethyl acetate, petroleum ether, normal hexane, pentane and/or ether;
   (2) ailanthone derivatives of formula (I); and
   (3) medicinal composition comprising ailanthone.

PN CN113464098-A
TI Method for carbon dioxide geological storage, involves sealing carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide reservoir, increasing pore space available space, and extracting salt water in carbon dioxide reservoir by alkaline extraction well to reduce pressure of carbon dioxide reservoir.
AU LI Q
   LI X
   ZHANG L
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 2021B7902E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a carbon dioxide reservoir (3) and a cover layer (2) according to a geological environment. A carbon dioxide injection well (1) and an alkaline water extraction well (4) are excavated. The carbon dioxide injection well is connected with the carbon dioxide reservoir. The carbon dioxide is injected into the carbon dioxide reservoir through the carbon dioxide injection well. The salt water in the carbon dioxide reservoir is extracted by the alkaline extraction well to reduce pressure of the carbon dioxide reservoir. A pore space available space is increased. The carbon dioxide is sealed in the carbon dioxide reservoir.
   USE - Method for carbon dioxide geological storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables excavating the carbon dioxideinjection well and the alkaline water extraction well at same time, so as to reduce the pressure of carbon dioxidereservoir and improve the pore space available space of the carbon dioxide reservoir, thus increasing the sealing coefficient of the reservoir, and hence improving the total amount of the carbon dioxide reservoir and improving the sequestingability of the carbon dioxide reservoir. The carbon dioxide geological sealing method ensures that the injected carbon dioxide is present in a supercritical state, thus ensuring the sealing amount of carbon dioxide. The method avoids the large distance caused by using the salt water extraction wells to collect water operation, thus effectively reducing the formation pressure, and effectively improving the injection of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a method for carbon dioxide geological storage.
   Carbon dioxide injection well (1)
   Cover layer (2)
   Carbon dioxide reservoir (3)
   Alkaline water extraction well (4)
   Packer (5)

PN DE102020102495-A1
TI Method for generating carbon dioxide snow from liquid carbon dioxide by expansion, involves discharging downward carbon dioxide snow that is separated out due to force of gravity and outputting from device for separating carbon dioxide snow from carbon dioxide gas.
AU GUDE J
   HAAS A
   JAGER F
AE ACP SYSTEMS AG (ACPS-Non-standard)
GA 202189727T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forming carbon dioxide gas/snow mixture. The carbon dioxide gas/snow mixture in an interior of a device (14) for separating carbon dioxide snow from carbon dioxide gas is brought onto an arcuate flow path, such that centrifugal forces arise, which drive carbon dioxide snow outwards more strongly than carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide snow is deposited on an inner wall of the device and carbon dioxide gas is discharged from an inner region of the device. The carbon dioxide snow that is separated out is discharged downward due to the force of gravity and is output from the device for separating carbon dioxide snow from carbon dioxide gas. The device for separating carbon dioxide snow from carbon dioxide gas is used at the same time as an expansion device (10) for liquid carbon dioxide to form the carbon dioxide gas/snow mixture.
   USE - Method for generating carbon dioxide snow from liquid carbon dioxide by expansion.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid phase of the carbon dioxide snow is separated economically and reliably from the carbon dioxide gas produced in the course of production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The liquid carbon dioxide or the carbon dioxide gas/snow mixture is fed to the device for separating carbon dioxide snow from carbon dioxide gas, which takes place essentially tangentially to the intended arcuate flow path. The device and inner wall delimiting an interior space are designed in a dome-shaped or dome-shaped manner in an inlet-side, in particular upper region. A pressure in the interior of the device is regulated to a predeterminable value using the regulating element. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for generating carbon dioxide snow from liquid carbon dioxide by expansion, has a device for separating carbon dioxide snow from carbon dioxide gas, the device is designed in a manner that the carbon dioxide gas/carbon dioxide snow mixture is brought onto an arcuately curved flow path in an interior of the device, so that centrifugal forces arise, which drive carbon dioxide snow outwards more strongly than carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide snow is deposited on an inner wall of the device and removed by gravity or by suction or mechanically, in particular downwards, and the carbon dioxide gas is removed from an inner area of the device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device components in the production of carbon dioxide snow.
   Storage tank (2)
   Conveying unit (6)
   Expansion nozzle (8)
   Expansion device (10)
   Device for separating carbon dioxide snow from carbon dioxide gas (14)

PN KR2459377-B1
TI Producing non-alcoholic protein beer by adding            water and malt for saccharification, adding thick            water, adding first hop for bittering and second hop            for aroma, separating boiled wort, adding whey protein            isolate and yeast, fermenting and aging obtained            product.
AU LEE Y
AE H2NUTRITION CO LTD (HTWO-Non-standard)
GA 2022D6098L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing non-alcoholic protein                beer involves adding water and malt for                saccharification, obtaining a saccharification                mixture at a saccharification temperature of                40-85&#176; C, adding thick water to the                saccharification mixture and separating wort,                heating the wort to obtain boiled wort by                sequentially adding a first hop for bittering and a                second hop for aroma at a boiling temperature of                90-105&#176; C, separating the boiled wort from                the hopskin to obtain a beer extract, sequentially                adding 50-100 pts. wt. whey protein isolate and                yeast to the beer extract based on 100 pts. wt. of                malt to obtain a boiling mixture, starting the                boiling mixture at a fermentation start temperature                of 14-18&#176; C, fermenting for 5 hours or                less, stopping the fermentation to obtain a mixture                for beer, injecting carbon dioxide into the beer                mixture and aging to obtain non-alcoholic protein                beer.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                non-alcoholic protein beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces non-alcoholic protein beer                having a relatively high protein content and                excellent palatability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing non-alcoholic protein                beer involves adding water and malt for                saccharification, obtaining a saccharification                mixture at a saccharification temperature of                40-85&#176; C, adding thick water to the                saccharification mixture and separating wort,                heating the wort to obtain boiled wort by                sequentially adding a first hop for bittering and a                second hop for aroma at a boiling temperature of                90-105&#176; C, separating the boiled wort from                the hopskin to obtain a beer extract, sequentially                adding 50-100 pts. wt. whey protein isolate and                yeast to the beer extract based on 100 pts. wt. of                malt to obtain a boiling mixture, starting the                boiling mixture at a fermentation start temperature                of 14-18&#176; C, fermenting for 5 hours or                less, stopping the fermentation to obtain a mixture                for beer, injecting carbon dioxide into the beer                mixture and aging to obtain non-alcoholic protein                beer. The water is purified water, and the malt and                the water for saccharification are applied in an                amount of 1:6-12. The thick water is purified                water, and the malt and the water for                saccharification are applied in an amount of                1:5-10. The first hop includes a citra hop as a                beater hop. The second hop includes a cascade hop                as an aroma hop, and the first hop and the second                hop are applied in a weight ratio of 1:0.7-1.3. The                malt is PALE ALE malt, MUNICH malt,                CARAMUNICH2(RTM: Drum-roasted caramel malt), VIENNA                malt, CARAPILS(RTM: Highly modified pale malt) malt                and their combinations. The fermentation step                involves sequentially lowering fermentation                temperature and stopping fermentation by making                temperature of mixture for beer 1&#176;                C.
