TI Welding Q620E high-strength steel used in crane,            heavy vehicle, vehicle frame of dumper and side plate            of carriage, has removing all kinds of dirt on groove            surface and nearby base metal until the metal shows            metallic luster, and preventing additional stress and            deformation due to welding heating.
AU BAI H
   HUANG L
   YANG X
   DONG L
   YUAN X
   WANG S
   LU X
AE BAOGANG GROUP CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2022041046
AB    NOVELTY - Welding Q620E high-strength steel comprises                (i) before the weldment is assembled, completely                removing all kinds of dirt on the groove surface                and the nearby base metal until the metal shows                metallic luster and ensure that there are no cracks                and defects within the cleaning range, (ii) where                the Q620E steel plate adopts 60degreeV-shaped                fracture, and no blunt edge, (iii) preheating                according to the calculation of carbon equivalent                at 75-125&#8451;, and the steel plate has                specific hardenability, (iv) using 90 wt.% argon, 8                wt.% carbon dioxide and 2 wt.% oxygen, (v) where                the welding torch is parallel to the groove, and                performing the welding from left to right along the                width of the plate, (vi) selecting the appropriate                gas shielded welding wire LA-100 according to the                principle of equal strength matching, and welding                wire diameter 1.1 mm, and (vii) where the welding                current is 250-310 A, arc voltage 25-28 V, welding                speed is 4.5-5 mm/s.
   USE - The steel is used in crane, heavy vehicle,                vehicle frame of dumper and side plate of                carriage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides a welding process of                high-strength steel with yield strength of 620 MPa                level; satisfies the welding sample mechanical                property, and matching degree with the base                material; reduces stress concentration; ensures the                flatness of the weld sample, and the performance of                welded component meets the design                requirement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Welding Q620E high-strength steel comprises                (i) before the weldment is assembled, completely                removing all kinds of dirt on the groove surface                and the nearby base metal until the metal shows                metallic luster and ensure that there are no cracks                and defects within the cleaning range, (ii) where                the Q620E steel plate adopts 60degreeV-shaped                fracture, and no blunt edge, (iii) preheating                according to the calculation of carbon equivalent                at 75-125&#8451;, and the steel plate has                specific hardenability, (iv) using 90 wt.% argon, 8                wt.% carbon dioxide and 2 wt.% oxygen, where the                protective gas flow is 18-20 l/minutes, (v) where                the welding torch is parallel to the groove, and                performing the welding from left to right along the                width of the plate, arranging tie bars under the                steel plate and placing the workpiece firmly,                preventing additional stress and deformation due to                welding heating, where the welding sequence is 8                welding on the front, and 2 welding on the back                after root cleaning, (vi) selecting the appropriate                gas shielded welding wire LA-100 according to the                principle of equal strength matching, and welding                wire diameter 1.1 mm, and (vii) where the welding                current is 250-310 A, arc voltage 25-28 V, welding                speed is 4.5-5 mm/s.

PN CN113693222-A
TI Preparing fermented fire chili sauce involves using fresh chili, dividing into two portions of supernatant and precipitate, subjecting precipitate to hot corn oil pouring to obtain oil phase and solid phase, and adding soybean lecithin into oil phase, emulsifying and homogenizing.
AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 2021E2189X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fermented fire chili sauce involves mixing fresh fructus Momordica charantia and herba houttuyniae, pulverizing, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction to obtain an extract, and uniformly dispersing the extract in 15-25 vol.% ethanol aqueous solution which is 4-6 times of the weight of the extract to obtain a water phase. The fresh chilies are cut into pieces, ground into slurry I, centrifuged to obtain a supernatant and a precipitate. The corn oil is heated to 120-130degrees Celsius and then quickly poured on the precipitate. The mixture is continuously stirred, and then centrifuged to obtain a solid phase and an oil phase. Soybean lecithin is added into the obtained oil phase, stirred until the soybean lecithin is completely dissolved and then the water phase is slowly added while stirring. The reaction mixture is emulsified and homogenized to obtain a water-in-oil microemulsion.
   USE - Method for preparing fermented fire chili sauce.
   ADVANTAGE - The chili sauce has strong flavor, is effective in preventing excessive internal heat, and is a healthy and delicious chili product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fermented fire chili sauce involves mixing fresh fructus Momordica charantia and herba houttuyniae, pulverizing, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction to obtain an extract, and uniformly dispersing the extract in 15-25 vol.% ethanol aqueous solution which is 4-6 times of the weight of the extract to obtain a water phase. The fresh chilies are cut into pieces, ground into slurry I, centrifuged to obtain a supernatant and a precipitate. The corn oil is heated to 120-130degrees Celsius and then quickly poured on the precipitate. The mixture is continuously stirred, and then centrifuged to obtain a solid phase and an oil phase. Soybean lecithin is added into the obtained oil phase, stirred until the soybean lecithin is completely dissolved and then the water phase is slowly added while stirring. The reaction mixture is emulsified and homogenized to obtain a water-in-oil microemulsion. The fresh tea leaves growing in the current year are withered, rolled, and stacked to obtain pre-treated tea leaves. The pre-treated tea leaves are mixed with fresh green plums and okra and the mixture is ground into slurry II. The slurry II is mixed with the supernatant and the solid phase to obtain mixed slurry. A mixed bacterial liquid I of Bacillus licheniformis and Acetobacter pasteurianus are inoculated into the mixed slurry and then subjected to aerobic fermentation followed by the addition of obtained microemulsion while stirring for uniform mixing. A mixed bacterial liquid II of Lactobacillus bulgaricus, Lactobacillus rhamnosus and Streptococcus thermophilus is inoculated into the obtained product and then subjected to anaerobic fermentation to obtain a fermentation product. The fermentation product is further seasoned and sterilized to obtain the fermented chili sauce.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fermented fire chili sauce.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The fructus Momordica charantia and herba houttuyniae are mixed in weight ratio of 1:0.2-0.3, and then supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed at 35-40degrees Celsius and 20-30 mega Pascals (MPa) for 3-4 hours to obtain the extract liquor. The weight ratio of chilies to corn oil is 1:2-3. The weight ratio of oil phase, soybean lecithin and water phase are 10:0.2-0.3:2-3. The mass ratio of the pre-treated tea leaves, green plums, okra and the supernatant is 1:0.20.3:0.1-0.2:35-45. Preferred Process: The pre-treated tea leaves are prepared by spreading fresh tea leaves flatly, and withering at 25-27degrees Celsius, at relative humidity of 60-70% for 14-15 hours. The air-kneading is carried out for 5 minutes, the light-kneading is carried out for 8 minutes, the heavy-kneading is carried out for 10 minutes and the air-kneading is carried out for 5 minutes in sequence. The tea leaves are put into a tile jar, piled up and placed for 1-2 days, and the ambient temperature in the tile jar is 27-30degrees Celsius.

TI Preparing stable reservoir reforming fluid used for preparing authigenic acid suspension, involves crushing paraformaldehyde, ammonium chloride, weighing product, adding raw materials to mix into reservoir modification fluid.
AU ZHENG C
AE BEIJING GUAERRUN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202171152A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stable reservoir reforming fluid involves crushing paraformaldehyde in certain container to obtain 5-200 mesh particles or powder, where crushing method includes normal temperature crushing and low temperature crushing, low temperature crushing room temperature is - 20-0 degrees C, crushing container includes open container and sealed container, gas atmosphere of container includes air, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and argon; crushing ammonium chloride in certain container to obtain 5-200 mesh particles or powder, where cryogenic crushing room temperature is - 20-0 degrees C, when paraformaldehyde or ammonium chloride is crushed, then quantitative weighing suspension aid is added, where mass ratio of paraformaldehyde or ammonium chloride to suspension aid is 1-1000:1; performing quantitatively weighing product to prepare self-generating acid suspension which comprises oil phase and aqueous phase.
   USE - Method for preparing stable reservoir reforming fluid used for preparing authigenic acid suspension (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide stable reservoir reforming fluid, which has ideal temperature-controllable, rapid and slow-release acid effect, which solves problem of poor stability of authigenic acid raw materials in reservoir reforming fluids, low solubility of active ingredients, and early release of acid; improves poor mixing and homogenization on site configuration, difficult process control, and low overall acid production; and improves acid production rate and oil and gas field production rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing stable reservoir reforming fluid involves crushing paraformaldehyde in certain container to obtain 5-200 mesh particles or powder, where crushing method includes normal temperature crushing and low temperature crushing, low temperature crushing room temperature is - 20-0 degrees C, crushing container includes open container and sealed container, gas atmosphere of container includes air, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and argon; crushing ammonium chloride in certain container to obtain 5-200 mesh particles or powder, where cryogenic crushing room temperature is - 20-0 degrees C, when paraformaldehyde or ammonium chloride is crushed, then quantitative weighing suspension aid is added, where mass ratio of paraformaldehyde or ammonium chloride to suspension aid is 1-1000:1; performing quantitatively weighing product to prepare self-generating acid suspension which comprises oil phase and aqueous phase, where oil phase comprises paraformaldehyde coated with suspension aid, aqueous phase comprises ammonium chloride solution, and standing liquid time T20 is greater than or equal to 30 minutes, when suspension appears with total volume of 20%, then 30d residual chlorine of suspension is greater than or equal to 90%; adding corresponding raw materials to mix into reservoir modification fluid in following proportions: 20-60% water, 0.1-1% thickener, 0.02-0.1% breaker glue, 0.30-0.50% composite additive one, 0-1% crosslinking agent, 0-80% autogenous acid suspension and 0-0.5% composite additive two in on-site sand mixer. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using stable reservoir reforming fluid, which involves pressurizing reservoir reforming fluid, and then pumped into target well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing stable reservoir reconstruction fluid involves mixing paraformaldehyde, ammonium chloride and suspension aid in solid phase; using proportional pump to add raw materials of composite additive one and composite additive two during mixing process of reservoir reforming fluid. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of paraformaldehyde and ammonium chloride is 0.33-3:1 and constitutes mixed component one. The mass ratio of suspension auxiliary agent and mixed component one is 1:1-3000. The dissolution temperature of autogenous acid suspension is controlled at 60-90 degrees C. The standing liquid time T20 of authigenic acid suspension with total volume of 20% is greater than or equal to 30 minutes; residual chlorine of authigenic acid suspension for 30 days is greater than or equal to 90%. Preferred Components: The suspension aid includes at least one of inverse polymerization polyacrylamide emulsion, oil-based suspension prepared from vegetable gum, oil-based suspension prepared from vegetable gum derivatives, and oil-based suspension prepared from xanthan gum Liquid, oil-based suspension prepared by velen gum, oil-based suspension prepared by xanthan gum derivatives, and oil-based suspension prepared by velen gum derivatives. The first composite additive includes clay stabilizer, drainage aid and demulsifier; and second composite additive includes corrosion inhibitor and iron ion stabilizer. The thickening agent is emulsion thickening agent; gel breaker is capsule gel breaker; and crosslinking agent is organometallic crosslinking agent. The reservoir reforming fluid is pressurized and pumped into target well by fracturing pump truck. The oil phase includes paraformaldehyde coated with suspension aid; the aqueous phase includes ammonium chloride solution.

TI Preparing complex catalyst in which zinc oxide nanoparticles complexed on copper selenide nanosheets useful for e.g. useful for preparing material comprises e.g. forming heterogeneous structure, and increasing active surface area.
AU YIN X
   ZHANG H
   MU M
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 2021446903
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing complex catalyst in which zinc oxide nanoparticles are complexed on copper selenide nanosheets comprises forming heterogeneous structure between one-dimensional nano-particles and two-dimensional nano-sheet, increasing the active surface area, the number of active sites, and the rate of electron transfer, and improving the performance of carbon dioxide electro-catalytic reduction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing material, which is useful in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide under applied potential conditions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is environmental friendly: has excellent electro-catalysis performance about 1.3Vvs, high selectivity and single and stable product, and obvious application prospect; reaches Faraday efficiency of carbon monoxide under RHE about 89.30%, and the current density about 10.44 mA/cm2; and utilizes cheap and easy to obtain materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the zinc oxide/copper selenide complex material, comprising (i) synthesizing hexagonal copper selenide nanosheets by hydrothermal method including taking 20 mg copper (II) acetate monohydrate and 11 mg selenium dioxide in beaker, adding 15 ml oleic acid and mixing to obtain a better hexagonal morphology of nanosheets, adjusting the amount of oleic acid from the perspective of solvents, where the formation of oleylamine to the anisotropy of the hexagonal crystal surface can play a good effect, adding oleylamine and adjusting the different ratios of the two on the basis of using oleic acid, placing in a vacuum drying box, heating at 180 degrees C for 8 hours, cooling, centrifuging, washing for 3 times with absolute ethanol, drying in a drying oven at 60 degrees C for 6 hours, and taking out, (ii) preparing zinc oxide nanosheets by precipitation method, and further transforming into nanoparticles including firstly respectively dissolving 0.79 g ammonium bicarbonate and 1.36 g zinc chloride in 20 ml and 15 ml distilled water, dropping the dissolved zinc chloride solution into ammonium bicarbonate, adding the surfactant sodium dodecyl sulfonate, forming a precipitate, washing with ethanol for 3 times, drying in the drying oven at 60 degrees C for 5 hours, placing the dried sample in a muffle furnace, and heating at 200 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain the sample, and (iii) synthesizing zinc oxide/copper selenide complex material by oil bath heating method including firstly adding different masses of zinc oxide and copper selenide into a beaker containing 15 ml absolute ethanol, stirring at 500 revolutions/minute under a magnetic stirrer for 30 minutes, transferring the two to 100 ml round bottom flask, stirring in an oil bath at 80 degrees C for 5 hours, centrifuging the product, washing with absolute ethanol and deionized water for 3 times, drying in the drying oven at 60 degrees C for 6 hours, and taking out.

PN CN112553144-A; CN112553144-B
TI Separating and in vitro culturing mouse cochlear spiral organs comprises cutting head skin of mouse, transferring to culture dish filled with sterile phosphate buffered saline, culturing cochlear spiral device, and culturing.
AU DUAN J
   DING Z
   HUANG Z
AE PENGLI BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO (PENG-Non-standard)
GA 202134330S
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and in vitro culturing mouse cochlear spiral organs comprises (i) cutting head skin of the mouse, cutting the head by surgical blade along the skull sagittal line, transferring to a culture dish filled with sterile phosphate buffered saline, removing brain tissue, (ii) removing the link of cochlea and temporal bone by fine tweezers, (iii) obtaining spiral ligament and spiral device around the worm shaft from the bottom of the cochlea to the peeling bone, using fine tweezers to clamp the spiral ligament and the bottom of the spiral device, (iv) culturing cochlear spiral device by taking proper collagen gel and dropping into the culture dish center, soaking the substrate film into collagen gel, adding serum-free culture solution to combine the glue block, culturing for 7-10 days.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and in vitro culturing mouse cochlear spiral organs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to operate and convenient to operate; uses immune staining mark Myo7a method to dye the cochlear screw device in the culture; has good dyeing effect; and can clearly observe the growth state of the hair cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and in vitro culturing mouse cochlear spiral organs comprises (i) cutting head skin of the mouse after cutting head, cutting the head by surgical blade along the skull sagittal line, transferring to a culture dish filled with sterile phosphate buffered saline, removing brain tissue, (ii) removing the link of cochlea and temporal bone by fine tweezers, cutting the connection of cochlea and vestibular tissue, taking out the cochlea, and transferring to a culture dish filled with DMEM/F12 culture solution, (iii) obtaining spiral ligament and spiral device around the worm shaft from the bottom of the cochlea to the peeling bone, using fine tweezers to clamp the spiral ligament and the bottom of the spiral device, unscrewing from the worm shaft, separating the spiral ligament and the substrate film containing the cochlea spiral device from the bottom, obtaining the substrate film containing the cochlear screw device, (iv) culturing cochlear spiral device by taking proper collagen gel and dropping into the culture dish center, soaking the substrate film into collagen gel, transferring the culture dish into carbon dioxide culture box for incubation for 20-30 minutes, taking out, solidifying collagen gel into rubber block, fixing the substrate film in the culture dish, adding proper serum-free culture solution to combine the glue block, finally adding back the carbon dioxide culture box to culture, replacing culturing liquid by feeding culture medium, culturing for 7-10 days, where the collagen gel comprises rat tail glue and the final concentration is 50 mM of phytic acid, adding the feeding culture medium on the basis of the serum-free culture solution with final concentration of 20mM of gap connecting protein 30.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The collagen gel is prepared by taking mouse tail glue, 10x BME and 2% sodium carbonate in a ratio of 9:1:1, adding final concentration 50 mM phytic acid, mixing uniformly, adding on the ice, and preventing coagulation. The step (iii) is performed under an anatomical microscope. The culturing days of the cochlear spiral device in step (4) are 10 days. The culture condition of carbon dioxide culture box in step (iv) is 37 degrees C and 5 % carbon dioxide. The incubation in the step (iv) is carried out for 20 minutes. The method uses immunostaining to mark Myo7a to observe the cochlear basement membrane helix tissue and hair cell activity during the culture stage of the cochlear helix. The immunostaining marker Myo7a method comprises taking cultured cochlear spiral removing culture liquid, adding 2 ml of 4% formalin fixing liquid, placing for 15 minutes at room temperature, washing by phosphate buffered saline, adding 2 ml phosphate buffered saline (0.1%) prepared Triton x-100 solution, placing for 15 minutes at room temperature, washing by phosphate buffered saline, adding 2ml of 2.5% phosphate buffered saline to prepare BSA solution, placing for 30 minutes at room temperature, washing with phosphate buffered saline, performing an anti-incubation, which is Rabbit anti- Myo7a 1: 200, 2.5 % BSA in phosphate buffered saline, 4 degrees C overnight, washing with phosphate buffered saline, adding secondary antibody as Alexa Fluor 546-anti-Rabbit IgG 1:1000, 2.5% BSA in phosphate buffered saline at room temperature for 2 hours, washing by secondary antibody, then observing with fluorescence microscope, and shooting. Preferred Components: The serum-free culture solution comprises 96.4 vol.% 1x DMEM, 2.4 vol.% glucose (20%), 0.2 vol.% penicillin G, and 1 vol.% glutamine. He mouse is the C57BL/6J mouse after being born 3-5 day after birth. The DMEM/F12 culture solution in the step (ii) comprises 1 vol.% N2 supplement, 2 vol.% B27 supplement, and 50 g/ml ampicillin with final concentration.

GA 202122063B
AB    NOVELTY - Method of performing an endothermic reaction                involves exposing an oxygen-containing stream to an                oxygen storage component in a combustion zone in a                reactor (120) to form an oxidized oxygen storage                component, reacting a fuel mixture comprising a                fuel stream and working fluid with the oxidized                oxygen storage component under combustion                conditions to form a flue gas (115) and to heat one                or more surfaces in a reaction zone to a                regenerated surface temperature of 600degrees                Celsius or more, recycling at least a portion of                the flue gas to form at least a portion of the                working fluid, and exposing an endothermic reagent                stream to the one or more surfaces in the reaction                zone at the regenerated surface temperature to form                an endothermic product stream. The oxygen storage                component comprises metal oxide system comprising                manganese oxide, iron oxide, copper oxide and/or                nickel oxide, and binder comprising magnesium                oxide, calcium oxide and/or yttrium oxide.
   USE - Method of performing endothermic                reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes binder which reduce or                minimize the amount of thermal expansion and                contraction, minimize or avoid                delamination/detachment of the bound oxygen storage                component from surfaces in the reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of performing an endothermic reaction                involves exposing an oxygen-containing stream to an                oxygen storage component in a combustion zone in a                reactor to form an oxidized oxygen storage                component, reacting a fuel mixture comprising a                fuel stream and working fluid with the oxidized                oxygen storage component under combustion                conditions to form a flue gas and to heat one or                more surfaces in a reaction zone to a regenerated                surface temperature of 600degrees Celsius or more,                recycling at least a portion of the flue gas to                form at least a portion of the working fluid, and                exposing an endothermic reagent stream to the one                or more surfaces in the reaction zone at the                regenerated surface temperature to form an                endothermic product stream, in which the direction                of flow for the endothermic reagent stream in the                reaction zone is reversed relative to a direction                of flow for the fuel mixture. The oxygen storage                component comprises metal oxide system comprising                manganese oxide, iron oxide, copper oxide and/or                nickel oxide, and binder comprising magnesium                oxide, calcium oxide and/or yttrium oxide. The fuel                mixture comprises 20 vol.% or more carbon                dioxide.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reverse                flow reactor system, which comprises reactor                comprising reactor inlet end, regenerator inlet end                and regeneration zone comprising an oxygen storage                component, and a recycle loop providing                intermittent fluid communication between the                reactor inlet end and regenerator inlet and                comprising a recycle compressor, fuel source inlet                an oxygen-containing gas inlet, and a carbon                dioxide-containing gas outlet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                oxycombustion to provide heat for a reverse flow                reactor.

   Flue gas (115)

   Air (122)
   Reforming effluent (135)

AU CHEN H
   GUAN X

   JI M
   WEN X
   HUANG F
   WANG J
   CHEN D
AE UNIV WENZHOU MEDICAL SECOND AFFILIATED (WEZN-C)
GA 2020C9048V
AB    NOVELTY - Testing the function of phthalates inhibiting                the differentiation of mesenchymal stem cells                comprise e.g. selecting a 90-day-old male Sprague                Dawley (SD) (RTM: Outbred model) rat, injecting                injection of ethylene dimethanesulfonate (EDS, 80                mg/kg body weight) into the intraperitoneal cavity                of the rat, and separating spermatogenesis from the                SD rat testis four days later Tubules and stem                Leydig cells (SLCs), dividing the seminiferous                tubules into multiple groups, adding different                concentrations (0-100 mu M) monoethylhexyl                phthalate (MEHP), culturing seminiferous tubules                for 4 weeks at 34 degrees C and at 5% carbon                dioxide, at the first weekend, adding 10 mu M                ethynyl deoxyuridine (EdU) label to part of                seminiferous tubule culture medium for 24 hours to                detect cell proliferation activity, counting the                positive cells on the surface of the seminiferous                tubules under a microscope, and expressing it as                the number of positive cells per square unit.
   USE - The method is useful for testing the function                of phthalates inhibiting the differentiation of                mesenchymal stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Testing the function of phthalates inhibiting                the differentiation of mesenchymal stem cells                comprise (1) selecting a 90-day-old male Sprague                Dawley (SD) (RTM: Outbred model) rat, injecting                injection of ethylene dimethanesulfonate (EDS, 80                mg/kg body weight) into the intraperitoneal cavity                of the rat, and separating spermatogenesis from the                SD rat testis four days later Tubules and stem                Leydig cells (SLCs), (2) dividing the seminiferous                tubules into multiple groups, adding different                concentrations (0-100 mu M) monoethylhexyl                phthalate (MEHP), culturing seminiferous tubules                for 4 weeks at 34 degrees C and at 5% carbon                dioxide, at the first weekend, adding 10 mu M                ethynyl deoxyuridine (EdU) label to part of                seminiferous tubule culture medium for 24 hours to                detect cell proliferation activity, counting the                positive cells on the surface of the seminiferous                tubules under a microscope, expressing it as the                number of positive cells per square unit, culturing                other seminiferous tubules until the fourth                weekend, performing beta -Hydroxysteroid                dehydrogenas (HSD3B) enzyme activity staining to                detect the effect of MEHP on the differentiation of                SLCs on the tubule surface into Leydig cells, or                protein extraction, and performing WesternBlots                (WB) analysis of Leydig cell-related protein                expression, and (3) treating seminiferous tubules                with collagenase to obtain a single cell                suspension, performing stream sorting to obtain                CD90+SLCs, amplifying CD90+SLCs in vitro, treating                with different microscope, calculating the positive                rate, culturing other cells, when the confluence                reaches 90%, placing in Leydig cell differentiation                induction medium (LIM) or adipocyte induction                medium (AIM), adding MEHP of different                concentrations (0-100 mu M) for 3 weeks, staining                cells with HSD3B activity and OilRed O, counting                positive cells, analyzing expression of Leydig cell                genes and adipogenic genes by qPCR to evaluate the                effect of MEHP on the differentiation of SLCs into                Leydig lineage and adipocyte lineage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  animal feeding condition is: the temperature is                  maintained at 22 degrees C and the light and dark                  switch is performed after every 12 hours to                  imitate the day and night alternation. In the step (3), during                  the differentiation of CD90+SLCs, the                  corresponding medium is collected each time for                  testosterone analysis.

PN CN111634896-A
TI Argon purification and recovery method includes            pressurizing crude argon, adding to argon purification            and recovery system, removing trace oil and/or heavy            hydrocarbons, heating, cooling, removing combustible            impurities, and oxidizing.
AU LI S
   JIANG H
   WANG Y
   SHEN H
AE UNIV BEIJING PEKING PIONEER TECHNOLOGY (UYBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020907395
AB    NOVELTY - An argon purification and recovery method                includes pressurizing crude argon, adding to argon                purification and recovery system, passing through                first stage temperature swing adsorption device                (TSA1) filled with adsorbent to remove trace oil                and/or heavy hydrocarbons, passing through heat                exchanger and oxygen-deficient catalytic tower,                heating until most of combustibles, such as                ammonia, carbon monoxide and hydrogen in crude                argon are consumed, and cooling argon; connecting                inlet of circulating oxidation tower to heater                while connecting outlet to second stage temperature                swing adsorption device (TSA2), removing all                combustible impurities that are not fully oxidized                in argon gas, flowing regeneration exhaust gas into                waste argon main pipe, adding to second oxidation                tower, and performing oxygen supplementation and                transition metal oxidation; and removing carbon                dioxide and water from argon through TSA2, and                connecting inlet of TSA2 to heat exchanger while                connecting outlet to VPSA.
   USE - The method is for argon purification and                recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses low-temperature catalysis to                burn hydrocarbons with a small amount of adsorption                and combustibility, fully utilizes heat, and has                low operating cost and high argon recovery                rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An argon purification and recovery method                comprises:
   (A) pressurizing crude argon to 0.3-2 MPa,                adding to argon purification and recovery system,                passing through TSA1 filled with adsorbent to                remove trace oil and/or heavy hydrocarbons, passing                through heat exchanger and oxygen-deficient                catalytic tower, heating using waste heat of argon                at the outlet of circulating oxidation tower until                most of combustibles, such as ammonia, carbon                monoxide and hydrogen in crude argon are consumed,                and cooling argon at the outlet of circulating                oxidation tower;
   (B) connecting inlet of circulating oxidation                tower to heater while connecting outlet to TSA2,                removing all combustible impurities that are not                fully oxidized in argon gas through circulating                oxidation tower, flowing regeneration exhaust gas                into waste argon main pipe, adding to second                oxidation tower when oxygen in the metal oxide in                first oxidation tower of circulating oxidation                tower is exhausted, and performing oxygen                supplementation and transition metal oxidation;                and
   (C) removing carbon dioxide and water from                argon through TSA2, connecting inlet of TSA2 to                heat exchanger while connecting outlet to VPSA,                indirectly heating the desorption tower to carry                out exchange heat reaction to gas flowing into                oxygen-deficient catalytic tower, subjecting to                adsorption, separating nitrogen and argon through                denitrification device, compressing desorbed air as                regeneration air while passing the rest through                waste argon buffer tank, and draining into waste                argon main pipe.
   The front end of oxygen-deficient catalytic                tower is provided with transition metal oxide                catalyst, while the back end is provided with                platinum, palladium, cobalt or nickel catalyst. The                circulating oxidation tower uses two recirculation                towers provided with transition metal oxide. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an argon                purification and recovery system comprising TSA1,                heat Exchanger, oxygen-deficient catalytic tower,                heater, circulating oxidation tower, TSA2,                denitrification device and vacuum pump connected by                pipelines and valve components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The TSA2 contains                  silica gel, activated carbon, 13X and/or                  activated alumina. The denitrification device is                  filled with 5A, 13X, GaLSX and LiLSX                  adsorbent.

PN CN111036228-A
TI Catalyst useful for producing hydrogen by thermal cracking of ammonia-containing tail gas in LED-MOCVD process, comprises coablt-molybdenum bimetallic catalyst for preparing precursor including cobalt nitrate and molybdenum nitrate.
AU WANG L
   ZHONG Y
   CHEN Y
   TANG J
   CAI Y
AE ZHEJIANG TIANCAI YUNJI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202034712G
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises e.g. coablt-molybdenum bimetallic catalyst for preparing precursor including cobalt nitrate and molybdenum nitrate mixed solution, where precursor of the promoter is a mixed solution of lanthanum nitrate and potassium nitrate, carrier is a mixture of pre-processed multi-layer carbon nanotubes (CNTs) and magnesium oxide and alcohol heating to obtain 10-30% of active component cobalt (wt./wt., similar below), 5-20% molybdenum containing 1-10% lanthanum as promoter component, 1-5% promoter potassium, and other mixed carrier carbon nanotubes and magnesium oxide, (1) the raw material gas suitable for the catalyst is LED- gallium nitride epitaxial wafer production, where the typical composition of the MOCVD epitaxial exhaust gas includes 60% nitrogen (vol./vol. similar to the following), 25% hydrogen, 14% ammonia, and trace metal ions, particulate matter, methane, oxygen and oxides (balance), e.g. carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and water.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for producing hydrogen by thermal cracking of ammonia-containing tail gas in light-emit- ting diodes -metal-organic chemical vapor deposition (LED-MOCVD) process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: fills the blank is green and the circulation economic development of the LED industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises cobalt-molybdenum bimetallic catalyst preparation including cobalt nitrate and molybdenum nitrate mixed solution, precursor of the promoter is a mixed solution of lanthanum nitrate and potassium nitrate, carrier is a mixture of pre-processed multi-layer carbon nanotubes (CNTs) and magnesium oxide and alcohol heating to obtain 10-30% of active component cobalt (wt./wt., similar below), 5-20% molybdenum containing 1-10% lanthanum as promoter component, 1-5% promoter potassium, and other mixed carrier carbon nanotubes and magnesium oxide, (1) the raw material gas suitable for the catalyst is LED- gallium nitride epitaxial wafer production, where the typical composition of the MOCVD epitaxial exhaust gas includes 60% nitrogen (vol./vol. similar to the following), 25% hydrogen, 14% ammonia, and trace metal ions, particulate matter, methane, oxygen and oxides (balance), e.g. carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, (2) the catalytic reaction temperature applicable to the above catalysts is 400-600 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 0.2 ~ 3 MPa, and the industrial scale of treatment tail gas is 50-50,000 Nm3/hours, and (3) the reaction mixture gas obtained by the above catalyst in the MOCVD tail gas ammonia catalytic thermal cracking reaction, and the ammonia content not greater than 3-5%, cooling or performing heat exchange and compression, introducing fine deamination composed of water washing tower and temperature swing adsorption tower, catalytic deoxygenation, pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction, and molecular sieve drying and hydrogen purification composed of metal getter, and finally obtaining a hydrogen product with a purity of more than 99.9999-99.99999% and returning to MOCVD section for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the pretreatment of carbon nanotubes, adding commercially available 10-20 nm carbon nanotubes carrier to an 10-30 wt.% nitric acid and 50-70 wt.% nitric acid mixed solution, heating and stirring, performing azeotropic reflux for 4-8 hours, cooling to ambient temperature, vacuum filtering, washing to neutrality with deionized water, drying the obtained filter cake at 120 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and grinding into 10-20 nm powder, mixing with magnesium oxide powder, roasting, cooling to obtain a mixed carrier of carbon nanotubes and magnesium oxide, where pretreatment also include sulfuric acid, dilute sulfuric acid and concentrated sulfuric acid mixed solution, sodium hydroxide solution, potassium hydroxide solution instead of nitric acid mixed solution for azeotropic reflux or using hydrogen peroxide, immersing phosphoric acid to replace the azeotropic reflux of the nitric acid mixed solution, or the above-mentioned azeotropic reflux, and using soaking combination to replace the azeotropic reflux of the nitric acid mixed solution, where the preferred pretreatment is azeotropic reflux of the nitric acid mixed solution The mixed carrier magnesium oxide is prepared by dissolving thermal decomposition of magnesium oxalate, or oxalic acid and magnesium acetate respectively in deionized water, pouring the magnesium acetate solution into the oxalic acid solution, stirring to produce a precipitate, taking out the precipitate, washing with deionized water many times, and drying for 1-4 hours, grinding into powder, roasting at 600-700 degrees C under nitrogen flow for 2-6 hours, controlling the heating rate of calcination at 100-140 degrees C/hours, or 100-140 degrees C/2 hours, or a controlling by the above-mentioned interval combination of heating rate. The alcohol heating method is to mixing pretreated multilayer carbon nanotubes and magnesium oxide mixed carrier, adding absolute ethanol solution, heating, stirring to obtain a slurry, heating concentration of cobalt-nickel bimetallic catalyst to obtain the precursor as a mixed solution of cobalt nitrate and molybdenum nitrate, where the precursor of the promoter is a mixed solution of lanthanum nitrate and potassium nitrate, and ethanol solution, adding a ammonia, adjusting the pH of the mixed solution to greater than 10, heating and stirring to form a slurry or precipitate again, and ultrasonically and dry the slurry or precipitate for 1-4 hours, grinding into powder, roasting at 600-700 degrees C under nitrogen flow for 6-10 hours, where the roasting temperature increase rate is controlled at 100-140 degrees C/hours, or 100-140 degrees C/2 hours, or a combination of the above temperature increase rates, and the equal volume impregnation, deposition and precipitation method can replace the above-mentioned alcohol heating method, preferably the alcohol heating method. The catalyst active components cobalt and molybdenum metals can be replaced by their oxides, single metals cobalt or molybdenum, or precious metals, other transition metals, rare metals, and/or corresponding metal oxides , preferably using cobalt and molybdenum bimetals as the catalyst active components.. The ratio of the mixed carrier carbon nanotubes to magnesium oxide is 1.0: 0.1-0.3: 1.0, according to a certain ratio, the carbon nanotubes and magnesium oxide powder is poured into the ethanol solution, heating, stirring, adding an ammonia solution, adjusting the solution pH to 9-10, drying for another 1-4 hours, conveying the mixed powder to a roaster, carrying out roasting at 600-700 degrees C under nitrogen flow for 2-4 hours, cooling the mixed carrier of carbon nanotubes and magnesium oxide is obtained to ambient temperature by hydrogen and nitrogen purge, where the magnesium oxide carrier can be replaced by one of aluminum oxide , silicon oxide, zirconium oxide or manganese oxide, preferably manganese oxide. The catalyst can be directly reduced online under the raw material atmosphere and the reaction temperature, or the catalyst is successively subjecting to online or offline temperature-dividing reduction at 400-500 degrees C and hydrogen flow and 600-700 degrees C and nitrogen flowing respectively, preferably the temperature-dividing reduction.

TI Preserving mixed green and red pepper comprises e.g. pre-harvest treating, harvesting, dipping medicine, bagging, ditch kiln temperature controlling, humidity controlling of ditch kiln, ditch kiln gas regulating and Anti-corrosion treating.
AU XIAO J
   WU Y
   WANG B
AE ANHUI JILONGSHAN FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202030537E
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving mixed green and red pepper comprises e.g. pre-picking green pepper plants and red pepper plants, stop irrigating, removing diseases, insects, and wounds, stacking picked green and red peppers, soaking, mixing dried green and red peppers into bacteriostatic, tying the mouth, placing it into strip ditch kiln, in autumn, the storage temperature is 9-11 degrees C, and the cold damage temperature is 8 degrees C, using two-temperature storage to extend storage period of green and red pepper, thoroughly pre-cooling green and red peppers before bagging, keeping relative humidity at 90-95%, packing in bag, using gas detector to detect gas index in bacteriostatic breathable film packaging bag, oxygen 2-7%, and carbon dioxide 1-2%, and treating with preservatives HS, LY or special preservatives for peppers CT-6 10-15 days before harvesting, sterilizing and disinfecting with lime water with concentration of 4-10% in chili shears during production.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving mixed green and red pepper.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prolongs storage and preservation time of single-class peppers; and greatly improves preservation effect of green and red peppers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preserving mixed green and red pepper comprises (i) pre-picking the pre-picked green pepper plants and red pepper plants by preservative treatment 3 days before harvest, and stop irrigating to avoid rainy days, (ii) choosing green ripe fruits that are fully enlarged, thick and hard, shiny and strong, and removing diseases, insects, and wounds, (iii) stacking picked green and red peppers in 2:1 ratio as push and dry them in dry house for 2 days, soaking them in sterilizing preservative for 10 minutes, and spreading them again in dry house until they dry, (iv) choosing strip ditch kiln storage bin, and using water-soluble disinfectant for sterilization, (v) mixing dried green and red peppers in 2:1 ratio into bacteriostatic type breathable film bag, and keeping green pepper handles upward, tying the mouth, and placing it into strip ditch kiln, (vi) in summer, the storage temperature of pepper is 10-12 degrees C, the cold damage temperature is 9 degrees C, in autumn, the storage temperature is 9-11 degrees C, and the cold damage temperature is 8 degrees C, using two-temperature storage to extend storage period of green and red pepper, (vii) thoroughly pre-cooling green and red peppers before bagging, keeping relative humidity at 90-95%, (viii) packing in bag, using gas detector to detect gas index in bacteriostatic breathable film packaging bag, oxygen 2-7%, and carbon dioxide 1-2%, and (ix) treating with preservatives HS, LY or special preservatives for peppers CT-6 10-15 days before harvesting, sterilizing and disinfecting with lime water with concentration of 4-10% in chili shears during production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of flowchart of preserving mixed green and red pepper. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the antiseptic agent is ACQ, the main components is copper and quaternary ammonium salts, the copper is calculated as copper oxide, the quaternary ammonium salt is calculated as didecyl dimethyl ammonium chloride, and its components and proportions includes copper 65, quaternary ammonium salt 35%. In the step (iii), the sterilization and fresh-keeping agent includes 2 pts. wt. p-coumarate, 55 pts. wt. mixture of methanol and ethanol, 15 pts. wt. mixture of polyoxyethylene sorbitan monolaurate and polyoxyethylene sorbitan monopalmitate, 15 pts. wt. edible vegetable oil, and 1000 pts. wt. water. In the step (iv), the water-soluble disinfectant includes 45 pts. wt. alkali metal chloroisocyanurate, 35 pts. wt. glucose, 1 pts. wt. hydrocarbon, 5 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 5 pts. wt. sodium hydroxymethyl cellulose, and 9 pts. wt. anhydrous sodium carbonate. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the picking specifically comprises cut the green and red peppers with flat-tipped sharp scissors or blades from the separation layer to ensure that there is another piece of pepper seedling above the separation layer, after harvesting, the green and red peppers are gently placed in a special storage wooden box, plastic box, carton, lined with paper or plastic bags, and the fruits are tightly packed.

PN CN110934964-A
TI Performing extraction method of alkaloids comprises selecting dry alkalescent raw rice, crushing with an ultrafine grinder, and adding deionized water for wetting, and putting moistened alkali raw rice material.
AU SUN Y
   JU W
   TANG X
   WANG S
   BI N
   QIU M
   WANG P
   PENG L
AE HOMEY GROUP CO LTD (HOME-Non-standard)
GA 202026487R
AB    NOVELTY - Performing extraction method of alkaloids comprises selecting dry alkalescent raw rice, crushing with an ultrafine grinder, and adding deionized water for wetting. The moistened alkali raw rice material is put into the steam blasting tank for steam blasting wall treatment for 5-8 minutes, and then the alkali raw rice material after steam blasting wall treatment is added to the ethanol solution, mixed uniformly and set aside. The mixed material obtained in the process is added with a compound strain of 2-5 wt.% mixed material, and fermented for 15-24 hours under the conditions of a temperature of 50-60 degrees C and a pH value of 3-6, and simultaneously subjected to ultrasonic treatment. Then after stopping the fermentation, and the fermentation broth is centrifuged and taken.
   USE - Method for performing extraction method of alkaloids.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform extraction method of alkaloids, which is simple and efficient, the preparation conditions are easy to control, does not contain toxic reagents, the raw material utilization rate is high, and the production cost is low, which is beneficial to large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing extraction method of alkaloids comprises selecting dry alkalescent raw rice, crushing with an ultrafine grinder, and adding deionized water for wetting. The moistened alkali raw rice material is put into the steam blasting tank for steam blasting wall treatment for 5-8 minutes, and then the alkali raw rice material after steam blasting wall treatment is added to the ethanol solution, mixed uniformly and set aside. The mixed material obtained in the process is added with a compound strain of 2-5 wt.% mixed material, and fermented for 15-24 hours under the conditions of a temperature of 50-60 degrees C and a pH value of 3-6, and simultaneously subjected to ultrasonic treatment. Then after stopping the fermentation, and the fermentation broth is centrifuged and taken. The fermentation broth obtained in the process is adjusted to a pH value of 7 to 13 with sodium hydroxide solution, centrifuged, the centrifuged liquid is taken, and spray-dried to obtain crude alkali raw rice alkaloid. The crude alkaloid obtained in the process is added to hydrochloric acid solution, stirred, and fully reacted to make the final pH value of the mixed solution less than 4. Then after centrifugation, the centrifuged liquid is lyophilized and put into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for extraction treatment, and residue is dissolved in deionized water, the pH value is adjusted to 7.0-8.9 with sodium hydroxide solution, the filter is filtered, and the filtrate is freeze-dried to obtain alkali raw rice alkaloid. The alkali raw rice alkaloid is put into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and an ethanol solution is used as a carrying agent for extraction treatment, and the extracted material is collected and freeze-dried to obtain a high-purity alkali raw rice alkaloid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of the ultrafinely crushed alkalescent raw rice to deionized water is 1: 0.2-0.5. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 0.1-0.15 mol / L. The concentration of the sodium solution is 0.01-0.03mol / L, and the pore size of the filter membrane is 0.2-0.4 microns.

PT P
PN CN110839889-A; CN110839889-B
TI Preparing composite microcapsule comprises            selecting immature and disease-free green bananas,            peeling them, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis, taking            raw wheat germ, drying, crushing, stirring homogenizing            and spray-drying.
AU GONG D
   DENG P
   WANG H
AE SHANDONG ACAD AGRIC SCI AGRIC RESOURCES (CAGS-C)
GA 2020203300
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite microcapsule comprises (i)                selecting immature and disease-free green bananas,                peeling them, adding 3-4 times the volume of ice                water, beatin at 2000-2500 revolutions/minute for                5-8 minutes, adding complex enzyme I, hydrolyzing                at 30-35 degrees C for 1.5 hours, introducing                carbon dioxide, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis                for 1.5 hours, filtering the residue with 3-4                layers of non-woven gauze to obtain green banana                pulp; (ii) adding sodium alginate and stachyose                into the green banana concentrated slurry; (iii)                taking raw wheat germ, drying it at 100-105 degrees                C for 15-20 minutes, superfinely crushing to                800-1000 mesh, cooling; (iv) adding Ganoderma                lucidum to the wheat germ hydrolysate, heating,                adding it to the mixture, heating up by program                temperature; and (v) grinding complex emulsion                through a colloid mill 4-5 times, homogenizing by a                high-pressure homogenizer 2-3 times to obtain a                homogeneous solution and spray-drying the                homogeneous solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing composite                microcapsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the stability, nutritional                value and burial rate; maintains functionality; has                a high extraction rate of resistant starch and high                pectin content; and ensures synergistic action with                sodium alginate and stachyose.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite microcapsule comprises (i)                selecting immature and disease-free green bananas,                peeling them, adding 3-4 times the volume of ice                water, beatin at 2000-2500 revolutions/minute for                5-8 minutes, adding complex enzyme I, hydrolyzing                at 30-35 degrees C for 1.5 hours, introducing                carbon dioxide, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis                for 1.5 hours, filtering the residue with 3-4                layers of non-woven gauze to obtain green banana                pulp, concentrating green banana pulp under vacuum                at 70-80 degrees C to a solid content of 30- 40%,                green banana concentrate; (ii) adding sodium                alginate and stachyose into the green banana                concentrated slurry and stirring uniformly to                obtain a mixed solution; (iii) taking raw wheat                germ, drying it at 100-105 degrees C for 15-20                minutes, superfinely crushing to 800-1000 mesh,                cooling, adding water to the material in a ratio of                1: 6, adding complex enzyme II to carry out                enzymatic hydrolysis for 2 hours of enzymatic                hydrolysis to obtain wheat germ hydrolysate; (iv)                adding Ganoderma lucidum to the wheat germ                hydrolysate, heating to 70-80 degrees C at a rate                of 100-200 revolutions/minute for 30-40 minutes to                obtain an emulsion, adding it to the mixture,                heating up by program temperature, stirring at                100-200 revolutions/minute during the heat                preservation period to obtain a double emulsion;                and (v) grinding complex emulsion through a colloid                mill 4-5 times, homogenizing by a high-pressure                homogenizer 2-3 times to obtain a homogeneous                solution and spray-drying the homogeneous                solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  compound enzyme is added in an amount of 2-3 wt.%                  of the green banana. The composite enzyme is                  pullulanase, cellulase and beer yeast in a mass                  ratio of 1:1:2. In the step (i), the amount of                  the carbon dioxide to be introduced has a foam                  slurry density of 0.6-0.8 g/cm3. In the step                  (ii), the mass ratio of the green banana                  concentrate slurry, sodium alginate, and                  stachyose is 1: 0.2: 0.1. In the step (iii), the                  compound enzyme II is added in an amount of 2.27                  wt.% of the wheat germ, the compound enzyme II is                  cellulase and protease in mass ratio of 1:5,                  where the protease is acidic protease, pawpaw                  protease, neutral protease and/or bromelain. In                  the step (iv), the Ganoderma lucidum spore powder                  accounts for 40-45 wt.% of wheat germ hydrolysate                  and the volume ratio of the emulsified liquid and                  the mixed liquid is 0.8-1.2. Preferred Method: In                  the step (iv), the programmed temperature                  increase and heat retention comprises heating to                  70-80 degrees C for 5 minutes and at 95-100                  degrees C for 2.5 minutes. In the step (v), the                  high-pressure homogenizing is carried out at                  60-70 degrees C under 20-25 MPa. The spray drying                  is carried out with a inlet air of 90-100 degrees                  C and the outlet air temperature of 40-50 degrees                  C at feed flow rate of 80-85 ml/minute.

PN CN110760325-A; CN110760325-B
TI Preparation of inorganic non-metal material used for energy conversion, involves separating raw coal, reducing content of ash and sulfur, feeding obtained mixture into coking chamber, air-drying and feeding obtained coke into furnace.
AU WU C
AE WU C (WUCC-Individual)
   TAIZHOU KEJIN ZHONGGAO TECHNOLOGY TRANSF (TAIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202014098W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of inorganic non-metal material involves separating raw coal with different components and different specific gravity, removing dust and waste rock, reducing content of ash and sulfur and filtering waste water, adding coking coal, lean coal and main coking coal into container, stirring, adding a small amount of boric acid, borax, boron oxide, titanium boride, titanium dioxide, asphalt, petroleum coke, tar residue and waste plastic and stirring, adding the obtained mixture to water for soaking, feeding the obtained blended mixture into the coking chamber of a coke oven, heating carbon dioxide through the combustion chambers on both sides under the condition of isolated air and obtaining coke, removing high-temperature coke from the carbonization chamber, spraying with high-pressure water, cooling, air-drying and evaporating the water in the coke, and feeding the coke into the furnace and recording the burning time of the coke and heat released during burning.
   USE - Preparation of inorganic non-metal material used for energy conversion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of improving heat dissipation efficiency of coke and reducing the time of quenching.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of inorganic non-metal material involves (i) providing 65-75 parts mass raw coking coal, 5-10 parts mass lean coal, 15-20 parts mass main coking coal, 3-8 parts mass lean coal, 0.1-0.2 part mass boric acid, 0.1-0.2 part mass borax, 0.1-0.2 part mass boron oxide, 0.4-0.5 part mass titanium boride, 1-3 parts mass titanium dioxide, 2-3 parts mass asphalt, 3-8 parts mass petroleum coke, 2-4 parts mass tar residue and 2-4 parts mass waste plastic, (ii) separating raw coal with different components and different specific gravity into different grades, removing dust and waste rock, reducing content of ash and sulfur and filtering waste water to remove the impurities, (iii) adding preset amount of coking coal, lean coal and main coking coal into the container, stirring, adding a small amount of boric acid, borax, boron oxide, titanium boride, titanium dioxide, asphalt, petroleum coke, tar residue and waste plastic and stirring, (iv) artificially open pores on the outer surface of the mixture and adding the obtained mixture to water for soaking, (v) feeding the obtained blended mixture into the coking chamber of a coke oven, heating carbon dioxide through the combustion chambers on both sides under the condition of isolated air and obtaining coke, (vi) removing high-temperature coke from the carbonization chamber, transporting the coke to the quenching tower, spraying with high-pressure water, cooling the high-temperature coke, air-drying and evaporating the water in the coke, and (vii) feeding the coke into the furnace for combustion and recording the burning time of the coke and heat released during burning.

TI Two-part reactive urethane resin composition            urethane resin composition is useful as sound-absorbing            material in substrate including e.g. vehicle bodies and            their parts, comprises polyisocyanate component and            polyol-containing component.
AU KATAOKA S
   KAWASE E
   TAKASAKI M
   WATANABE Y
   TAKEUCHI H
   SADO S
   IDO H
   NISHIKAWA S
   KINAMI S
   MURATA K
   WATANABE H
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   MAZDA MOTOR CORP (MAZD-C)
   NIPPON TOKUSHU TORYO CO LTD (NIUT-C)
   MAZDA MOTOR CORP (MAZD-C)
   NIPPON TOKUSHU TORYO CO LTD (NIUT-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   MAZDA KK (MAZD-C)
   NIPPON TOKUSHU TORYO CO LTD (NIUT-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 201983110V
AB    NOVELTY - Two-part reactive urethane resin composition                comprises polyisocyanate component and                polyol-containing component. The polyol-containing                component comprises a polyol component, a catalyst,                a foam stabilizer, an amine compound having primary                and secondary amino groups, and carbon dioxide. The                two-part reactive urethane resin composition is                open-celled soft polyurethane foam, when cured. The                average sound absorption coefficient of the                polyurethane foam is greater than or equal to 30%,                measured according to JIS A 1405-2-2007 at 63-5000                hertz, and the length of liquid drop is measured                within 300 mm by placing acrylic plate vertically                at position 10 cm away from the outlet position of                an injection molding machine, discharging mixture                of polyisocyanate component and polyol-containing                component from the injection molding machine to the                acrylic plate for 0.2 second at rate of 120                g/second to form injection molded product on the                acrylic plate.
   USE - The two-part reactive urethane resin                composition is useful as sound-absorbing material                in substrate including vehicle bodies (preferably                automobiles) and their parts, heavy machinery,                electric generators, office automation equipment,                household electronics, structural parts, and as                vehicle body frame filling material for applying to                vehicle body parts in assembly lines of vehicle                bodies, preferably in the production of vehicle                bodies noise.
   ADVANTAGE - The two-part reactive urethane resin                composition: has excellent sound absorption                property, stable density, good tensile strength and                hardness; is light in weight; and creates excellent                acoustic environment to reduce noise. The method                maintains stability of the polyurethane foam during                molding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Two-part reactive urethane resin composition                comprises polyisocyanate component and                polyol-containing component. The polyol-containing                component comprises a polyol component, a catalyst,                a foam stabilizer, an amine compound having primary                and secondary amino groups, and carbon dioxide. The                two-part reactive urethane resin composition is an                open-celled soft polyurethane foam, when cured. The                average sound absorption coefficient of the                polyurethane foam is greater than or equal to 30%,                measured according to JIS A 1405-2-2007 at 63-5000                hertz, and the length of liquid drop is measured                within 300 mm by placing acrylic plate vertically                at position 10 cm away from the outlet position of                an injection molding machine, discharging mixture                of polyisocyanate component and polyol-containing                component from the injection molding machine to the                acrylic plate for 0.2 second at rate of 120                g/second to form injection molded product on the                acrylic plate, 5 minutes after discharge, measuring                the length of the injection molded product from the                highest point to the lowest point in the vertical                direction on the acrylic plate as the length of the                liquid drops. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included                for two-part reactive urethane resin composition,                comprising spraying mixture of the polyisocyanate                component and polyol-containing component from the                injection molding machine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The amine compound                  comprises aliphatic amines, aromatic amines or                  alicyclic amines. The molecular weight of the                  amine compound is 33-220. The amine compound has                  primary or secondary amino groups. The                  polyol-containing component comprises 1-15 pts.                  wt. amine compound, 0.5-5 pts. wt. carbon                  dioxide. The content of the amine carbonate                  formed from the amine compound and carbon dioxide                  is 1.5-20 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. of the                  polyol component. The polyisocyanate component is                  diphenylmethane diisocyanate,                  polymethylene-polyphenyl polyisocyanate, or thier                  modifications. The cream time of the mixture of                  the polyisocyanate component and the                  polyol-containing component is less than or equal                  to 1 second. The core density of the polyurethane                  foam is 10-110 kg/m3. The air intake volume of                  the polyurethane foam is 3-60 l/minute, measured                  according to JIS K 6400-7: 2012. The average                  diameter of the cell size of the polyurethane                  foam is less than or equal to 400 nm, preferably                  less than or equal to 350 nm. The average sound                  absorption coefficient of the polyurethane foam                  is greater than or equal to 40% measured                  according to JIS A 1405-2: 2007 at 500-2500                  hertz.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109793229-A
TI Fat-soluble portion of rubber tree seed used as health food to improve memory, is prepared by mechanical extrusion method involving drying rubber tree seed, subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion and extruding to obtain liquid substance.
AU FAN J
   ZHU Y
   WANG D
   WANG R
   WANG Y
   YANG W
   PAN W
   LI Y
   HOU J
   CHEN Y
AE XISHUANGBANNA HUAKUN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (XISH-Non-standard)
GA 201947499J
AB    NOVELTY - Fat-soluble portion of a rubber tree seed is prepared by (a) a mechanical extrusion method involving obtaining rubber tree seed kernels, subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic screw extrusion and extruding to obtain liquid substance as the product, (b) a solvent leaching method involving expanding kernels of rubber tree seeds, adding to a sealed container for leaching, adding solvent comprising No.6 extraction solvent oil, petroleum ether, n-hexane and/or n-butanol, ultrasonically treating or mixing material and solvent, leaving mixture to precipitate and volatilizing the solvent to obtain remaining liquid substance as product, or (c) a supercritical extraction method involving granulating kernels of rubber tree seeds, adding to a sealed pressure-resistant supercritical extraction vessel, adding carbon dioxide, mixing material and solvent or ultrasonically treating mixture, leaving to precipitate, volatilizing solvent to obtain the remaining liquid as product.
   USE - The fat-soluble portion of rubber seed is useful for health food and food product for improving memory (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fat-soluble portion of a rubber tree seed is prepared by using (a) mechanical extrusion method involving drying and shelling fresh rubber tree seed, obtaining seed kernels, heating the seed material at 80-100 degrees C before extrusion, subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic screw extrusion, pressing and extruding to obtain liquid substance of the fat-soluble portion of the rubber tree seed as the product, (b) a solvent leaching method involving drying and shelling fresh rubber tree seeds, obtaining seed kernels, expanding the kernels, crushing or flaking and processing the kernels, adding kernels to a sealed container for leaching, adding solvent comprising No.6 extraction solvent oil, petroleum ether having a boiling point of 30-90 degrees C, n-hexane, diethyl ether, ethyl acetate and/or n-butanol, mixing the material and solvent or carrying out ultrasonic treatment at below the boiling point of the solvent under normal pressure, allowing the material to sufficiently contact the solvent, leaving the mixture to precipitate, draining the solvent, and volatilizing the solvent at a temperature higher than the boiling point of the solvent under vacuum to obtain remaining liquid substance as the product, or (c) a supercritical extraction method involving drying and shelling fresh rubber tree seeds, obtaining the kernels, expanding the kernels, granulating or milling the kernels, adding granulated kernels to a sealed pressure-resistant supercritical extraction vessel, adding carbon dioxide as solvent, stirring and mixing the material and the solvent or carrying out ultrasonic treatment under the condition that exceeds the critical temperature and critical pressure of the solvent, allowing the material to fully contact the solvent, leaving to precipitate, draining the solvent, reducing the pressure to room temperature, volatilizing the solvent and obtaining the remaining liquid substance as the product.
TF ACTIVITY - Nootropic.

PN KR1979423-B1
TI Processing high concentration organic wastewater, comprises e.g. mechanically pretreating organic wastewater, transferring wastewater to acid fermentation tank, performing acid fermentation, and performing wet digestion.
AU LEE J W
   KIM D W
   LIM B H
   WOO K J
   KANG S H
AE UNIV KONGJU NAT IND ACAD COOP GRO (UYKN-C)
   EDEN SBABE CO LTD (EDEN-Non-standard)
GA 201943211J
AB    NOVELTY - Processing high concentration organic wastewater, comprises (a) mechanically pretreating organic wastewater, (b) transferring wastewater subjected to mechanical pretreatment to an acid fermentation tank, and performing acid fermentation, (c) performing wet digestion by transferring acid fermented wastewater from acid fermentation tank into a wet digestion tank to decompose organic matter remaining in wastewater to generate methane gas and carbon dioxide, (d) supplying digested waste liquid into a waste heat recovery tank, recovering waste heat from digested waste liquid and supplying into an acid fermentation tank, (e) separating extinguishing waste liquid by solid-liquid centrifugal separator in a solid-liquid separator installed at rear end of waste heat recovery tank, and (f) cooling wastewater flowing into solid-liquid separator by heat-exchanging wastewater in waste heat recovery tank with waste water in acid fermentation tank using a heat exchange unit.
   USE - The method is useful for processing high concentration organic wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method optimizes biological wastewater treatment of digestion waste liquid to dispose of greenhouse gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing high concentration organic wastewater, comprises (a) mechanically pretreating the organic wastewater, (b) transferring the wastewater subjected to the mechanical pretreatment to an acid fermentation tank, and performing acid fermentation, (c) performing wet digestion by transferring the acid fermented wastewater from the acid fermentation tank into a wet digestion tank to decompose the organic matter remaining in the wastewater to generate methane gas and carbon dioxide, (d) supplying the digested waste liquid into a waste heat recovery tank, recovering waste heat from the digested waste liquid and supplying into an acid fermentation tank, (e) separating the extinguishing waste liquid by solid-liquid centrifugal separator in a solid-liquid separator installed at the rear end of the waste heat recovery tank, and (f) cooling the wastewater flowing into the solid-liquid separator by heat-exchanging the wastewater in the waste heat recovery tank with waste water in the acid fermentation tank using a heat exchange unit. The heat exchange unit comprises a first heat exchanger unit for transferring the waste heat of the waste heat recovery tank to the waste water of the acid fermentation tank using circulating refrigerant, and a second heat exchanger unit for circulating the wastewater of the acid fermentation tank and exchanging heat with the extinguishing waste liquid in the waste heat recovery tank. The second heat exchanger unit comprises a second low temperature heat exchange pipe provided in a staggered manner in the waste heat recovery tank, a second refrigerant pipe connected to the outlet of the second low temperature heat exchange pipe and inner side of the acid fermentation tank and the inlet of the second low temperature heat exchange pipe for circulating the wastewater of the acid fermentation tank, and transfer pump provided in the second refrigerant pipe to transfer wastewater from the acid fermentation tank to a second low temperature heat exchange tube.
