PN CN109652654-A; CN109652654-B
TI Recycling waste ternary-power lithium battery resource useful for recycling metal element, comprises crushing waste ternary-power lithium battery, immersing, filtering, pressurizing, subjecting to leaching reduction reaction and drying.
AU ZHOU M
   GUO Z
   HAN Y
   XU K
   JIA Z
   ZHANG L
   WEI H
   ZHANG X
   YUAN B
AE SHENYANG RES INST CHEM IND CO LTD (SNCM-C)
   SHENYANG ZHONGHUA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201939348M
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling waste ternary-power lithium battery resource comprises (i) crushing waste ternary-power lithium battery, immersing, filtering; (ii) adding calcium oxide or magnesium oxide into lithium-rich solution, filtering to obtain crude lithium carbonate solution, drying crude solution, adding water into crude product to prepare 2-8 wt.% lithium carbonate slurry, pressurizing, carrying out reaction, removing precipitate by filtration, heating filtrate and filtering to obtain refined lithium carbonate; (iii) subjecting anode and cathode mixed filter cake to leaching reduction reaction under acidic conditions, filtering reaction to obtain acid immersion liquid and carbon powder filter cake to obtain acid leaching solution, extracting acid leaching solution and adsorbing by activated carbon, carrying out complex reaction of adjusted acid immersion liquid with aqueous ammonia, adding sodium hydroxide solution, washing and drying filtered filter.
   USE - The recycling waste ternary-power lithium battery resource is useful for recycling metal element (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has no wastewater discharge during the treatment process and reduces pressure of wastewater treatment of lithium battery recycling enterprise.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling waste ternary-power lithium battery resource comprises (i) crushing waste ternary-power lithium battery, immersing by adding water of 2-10 times mass of battery debris under stirring conditions for 0.1-1 hours, filtering by water immersion, heating and pyrolyzing filter cake pieces in carbon dioxide gas environment under anaerobic conditions to obtain pyrolysis material, immersing by adding water of 2-20 times mass of pyrolysis material under stirring conditions for 1-5 hours, sieving and filtering to obtain copper aluminum foil mixed chips, anode and cathode mixed filter cake and lithium-rich solution; (ii) adding calcium oxide or magnesium oxide into lithium-rich solution, filtering to obtain crude lithium carbonate solution, drying crude solution, adding water into crude product to prepare 2-8 wt.% lithium carbonate slurry, pressurizing to 0.1-1 Mpa by carbon dioxide, carrying out reaction for 0.1-4 hours, removing precipitate by filtration, heating filtrate to 80-100 degrees C for 0.1-2 hours and filtering to obtain refined lithium carbonate, where amount of calcium oxide or magnesium oxide added is 0.1-1% of mass of lithium-rich solution; (iii) subjecting anode and cathode mixed filter cake to leaching reduction reaction under acidic conditions at 40-80 degrees C, filtering reaction to obtain acid immersion liquid and carbon powder filter cake to obtain acid leaching solution and subjecting to gradient adjustment of pH to remove impurities, extracting acid leaching solution and adsorbing by activated carbon, adjusting molar ratio of nickel, cobalt and manganese in acid immersion liquid to be 5-8:2-1:3-1, carrying out complex reaction of adjusted acid immersion liquid with aqueous ammonia, adding sodium hydroxide solution to adjust pH of reaction solution to 10-12, aging reaction solution at 40-80 degrees C for 5-50 hours under nitrogen atmosphere, washing and drying filtered filter cake to obtain nickel-cobalt-manganese three-element composite hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) crushing battery by subjecting to water immersion, leaching to obtain filter cake fragments, controlling filter cake fragments to have heating rate of 5-15 degrees C/minute in presence of carbon dioxide and under anaerobic conditions and raising temperature to 400-800 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours, hydrolyzing filtrate at 100-200 degrees C, adding calcium oxide or magnesium oxide to remove impurity ions, filtering the filtrate and used in water immersion, heating and pyrolyzing filter cake pieces in pyrolysis furnace in presence of carbon dioxide gas and under anaerobic conditions and discharging pyrolysis tail gas directly by thermal oxidation treatment, where waste power lithium battery is crushed into constant current discharge of collected waste power lithium battery, when current is less than 0.01 A, discharge is stopped, battery shell is stripped, battery core is crushed and used and tail gas produced during crushing process is mixed with tail gas generated during pyrolysis process and directly discharged after thermal oxidation treatment. In the step (iii), subjecting acid immersion liquid to gradient adjustment of pH to adjust pH value of acid immersion liquid to 4-6, filtering to remove precipitate, adjusting pH of acid immersion liquid to 2-4 and extracting impurities; subjecting anode and cathode mixed filter cake to leaching reduction reaction in mixed solution of sulfuric acid and hydrogen peroxide, filtering reaction to obtain acid immersion liquid and carbon powder filter cake, subjecting acid immersion liquid to gradient adjustment of pH to remove impurities and extracted using N902 (derivative of salicylal-doxime) as extracting agent, collecting extracted organic phase, recycling N902 further by sulfuric acid, where N902 is continuously used for extraction process and aqueous phase in extraction process is used for acid leaching reduction reaction, mass ratio of sulfuric acid and hydrogen peroxide in mixed solution of sulfuric acid and hydrogen peroxide is 8:1-2:1, and molar ratio of nickel and cobalt in sulfuric acid and mixed filter cake of anode and cathode is 1.1:1-2.0:1; carrying out complex reaction of adjusted acid immersion liquid with 0.3-2 mol/l aqueous ammonia; and after aging and filtering, filtrate is recovered by deamination to obtain ammonium sulfate and filtrate is concentrated and recycled to obtain sodium sulfate and concentrated distillate is used as process water.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN CN109433212-A
TI Magnetic oxygen carrier useful for coal chemical            chain gasification to produce hydrogen-rich syngas,            comprises a composite oxygen carrier, including nickel            ferrite and attapulgite.
AU GUO Q
   AN M
   HU X
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2019250144
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetic oxygen carrier comprises a composite                oxygen carrier, including 50-90 wt.% nickel ferrite                and 10-50 wt.% attapulgite. The nickel ferrite has                a spinel structure. The nickel ferrite interacts                with attapulgite, where nickel ferrite is dispersed                on surface of the attapulgite, the magnesium and                calcium in attapulgite inhibits nickel ferrite                reduction, and controls the reduction valence state                in ferrouso ferric oxide. The oxygen carrier is                added to coal chemical chain gasification process,                where the oxygen carrier carries heat to complete                coal gasification, when the oxygen carrier is in                reduced state, its acts as a catalyst for                catalyzing water vapor shift reaction, converting                carbon monoxide and steam to hydrogen and carbon                dioxide. The chemical chain gasification of coal                using oxygen carrier is carried out in a fuel                reactor at 800-1000 degrees C and in air reactor at                800-1000 degrees C.
   USE - The magnetic oxygen carrier is useful for coal                chemical chain gasification to produce                hydrogen-rich syngas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The magnetic oxygen carrier is simple to                prepare and has high oxygen stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Magnetic oxygen carrier comprises a composite                oxygen carrier, including 50-90 wt.% nickel ferrite                and 10-50 wt.% attapulgite. The nickel ferrite has                a spinel structure. The nickel ferrite interacts                with attapulgite, where nickel ferrite is dispersed                on surface of the attapulgite, the magnesium and                calcium in attapulgite inhibits nickel ferrite                reduction, and controls the reduction valence state                in ferrouso ferric oxide. The oxygen carrier is                added to coal chemical chain gasification process,                where the oxygen carrier carries heat to complete                coal gasification, when the oxygen carrier is in                reduced state, its acts as a catalyst for                catalyzing water vapor shift reaction, converting                carbon monoxide and steam to hydrogen and carbon                dioxide. The chemical chain gasification of coal                using oxygen carrier is carried out in a fuel                reactor at 800-1000 degrees C and in air reactor at                800-1000 degrees C. The oxygen carrier is prepared                by: (i) preparing a solution of nickel nitrate and                iron nitrate according to nickel trivalent ion and                iron trivalent ion molar ratio of 0.5-2; (ii)                preparing sodium borohydride solution according to                sodium borohydride and nickel trivalent ion-iron                trivalent ion molar ratio of 2-4; (iii) slowly and                uniformly adding the above two solutions into a                colloid mill, stirring rapidly for 3 minutes to                obtain nano metal particles-containing mixed                solution-A; (iv) continuing to stir rapidly for 1                minute according to nickel ferrite/soil unevenness                mass ratio of 1-5, and continuously stirring for 4                minutes, transferring the mixed solution into                polytetrafluoroethylene-lined autoclave and                carrying out hydrothermal crystallization at 150                degrees C for 12 hours; (v) naturally cooling to                room temperature, centrifuging and washing; and                (vi) naturally air-drying, transferring into a                drying oven at 100-140 degrees C for 12-24 hours,                placing in a muffle furnace and calcining at                800-1000 degrees C for 3-8 hours, crushing and                sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen carrier is                  in a shape of sphere, strip or microsphere. The                  size of the oxygen carrier particle is 10-2000 mu                  m, preferably 50-500 mu m.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN109167050-A
TI Production method of ternary anode material, involves mixing lithium carbonate and ternary precursor with metal oxide, sintering, crushing, aluminum coating, drying, sintering, mixing, removing iron, sieving, heat-sealing and packing.
AU CAO C
   GAO Y
   ZHANG J
   ZHAO B
   WANG L
AE HENAN FUSEN NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201906304E
AB    NOVELTY - The production method of 551530 ternary anode material involves (S1) preprocessing the raw materials, crushing the lithium carbonate raw material, and using with 551530 ternary precursor nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide compound, (S2) mixing lithium carbonate and 551530 ternary precursor, incorporating metal oxide into the compounding process, (S3) mixing lithium carbonate, 551530 ternary precursor and metal oxide, (S4) sintering, continuously introducing oxygen, timely discharging carbon dioxide and water vapor, crushing 551530 ternary semi-finished product after the sintering is completed, washing with water, coating with aluminum, drying, and sintering and (S5) processing the materials in a packaging room, mixing and pouring the sintered 551530 semi-finished product into a double-helix conical mixer, mixing and sieving, subjecting the sieved material to iron removal, heat-sealing the iron-removed material, packing, and warehousing.
   USE - Production method of 551530 ternary anode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of economical and high capacity 551530 ternary anode material with improved electrochemical capacity of 160-165 mAh/g, less than 20% 0.5 C charge-discharge cycle performance 2000-3000 times, and stable quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The production method of 551530 ternary anode material involves (S1) preprocessing the raw materials, crushing the lithium carbonate raw material, and using with 551530 ternary precursor nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide compound of formula: Ni0.55Co0.15Mn0.30(OH)2, (S2) mixing lithium carbonate and 551530 ternary precursor according to molar ratio of lithium and sum of nickel, cobalt and manganese of 1.03-1.10, incorporating metal oxide into the compounding process, where the content of metal in the metal oxide is calculated by 0.1-1 %mass according to the mass of theoretical product, (S3) uniformly mixing the lithium carbonate, 551530 ternary precursor and metal oxide in a high-speed mixer for 30-100 minutes to obtain raw material, (S4) sintering the obtained raw material in an oxygen-enriched kiln for one time, continuously introducing oxygen during the sintering process, timely discharging carbon dioxide and water vapor, where the oxygen content is more than 50%, orderly crushing 551530 ternary semi-finished product after the sintering is completed, washing with water, coating with aluminum, drying, and sintering, where the sintering temperature of the main temperature zone is 500-800 degrees C and the time is more than 8 hours, and (S5) processing the materials in a packaging room, where the humidity between the packages is less than 40%, mixing and pouring the sintered 551530 semi-finished product into a double-helix conical mixer for 60-180 minutes, mixing and sieving over 300-450 mesh, subjecting the sieved material to iron removal, where the magnetic substance content in the material after iron removal is less than 100 ppb, heat-sealing the iron-removed material, packing, and warehousing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: In the step (S1), the lithium carbonate has a particle size of 4-6 mu m. In the step (S4), the 551530 ternary semi-finished product after sintering is crushed to a particle size of 10-15 mu m. The metal oxide has a particle size of 200-800 nm. Preferred Conditions: In the step (S4), the raw material is sintered in an oxygen-enriched kiln at 650-700 degrees C for 2-6 hours, 750-850 degrees C for 4-10 hours and 860-930 degrees C for more than 10 hours, the oxygen is continuously supplied at 50 cubic meter per hour, the discharge gas is more than 30 meters per hour, the aluminum coating content is controlled at 800-1400 ppm after adding aluminum coating solvent, stirred for 30~100 minutes, transferred to a centrifuge, and vacuum dried. Preferred Composition: The residual alkali content of the sintered product is less than 0.5%. In the step (S4), the crushed material is washed with water, where the mass ratio of material to water is 1:(1-4). Preferred Components: The metal oxide is an oxide of titanium, aluminum, magnesium, zirconium, hafnium or tantalum.

TI Dragon fruit detoxifying and beautifying medicinal granules useful for regulating blood lipid and protecting heart comprises raw apple, Gynostemma pentaphyllum, Acanthopanax senticosus, grape seed, vitamin E1 and dragon fruit albumin.
AU LI H

(iv) taking grape seed, passing through 20 mesh sieve, degreasing with petroleum ether at volume ratio of 1:3, extracting for 48 hours using 95% ethanol, extract the volume at 1:7 at 50 degrees C for 2 times, adding supernatant with sodium chloride, extracting with ethyl acetate, distilling under reduced pressure, filtering, drying and pulverizing to obtain grape seed extract fine powder; and (vii) mixing apple polyphenol extract fine powder, Gynostemma pentaphyllum powder fine powder, Acanthopanax senticosus fine powder fine powder, grape seed extract fine powder, vitamin E1, dragon fruit albumin fine powder, and stirring uniformly to make fine particles, adding into a small bag and making a dragon fruit detoxifying nourishing granule and each small bag contains 1.2 grams of fine particles.

AU WU G
   LIU X
AE WU G (WUGG-Individual)
GA 2019055153
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pressure-resistant aerated brick comprises (i) mixing the paraffin and the Bacillus, adding a humic acid having a paraffin mass of 0.1-0.3 times, stirring and mixing, freezing and crushing to obtain a modified paraffin, (ii) obtaining an additive blank and (iii) obtaining a pretreatment additive after the additive blank is allowed to stand in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, mixing the pretreatment additive and the low melting point alloy, heating until the alloy is melted, soaking, filtering and freezing to obtain an additive and (iv) mixing cement with water, adding carbon powder, mullite powder, surfactant, bentonite, calcium stearate, urea, quicklime, modified paraffin and additives, mixing, injection molding the mixture, obtaining the blank, autoclaving the billet, curing, pretreating the pre-stressed aerated brick, calcining the pre-treatment anti-pressure aerated brick in an oxygen atmosphere, cooling to room temperature and discharging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pressure-resistant aerated brick.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pressure-resistant aerated brick comprises (i) mixing the paraffin and the Bacillus in a mass ratio of 5:1 to 6:1, adding a humic acid having a paraffin mass of 0.1-0.3 times, stirring and mixing, freezing and crushing to obtain a modified paraffin, (ii) adjusting the pH of the oxy graphene-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture to 8-9, adding a polyethylene oxide polyamine of 0.2-0.3 times the mass of the oxidized graphene-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture, stirring and mixing, ultrasonically dispersing the mixture is to obtain a pretreatment mixture, mixing the pretreatment mixture and the glucose beta lactone aqueous solution in a mass ratio of 30:1-30:3, stirring and mixing, ultrasonically dispersing, allowing to stand, filtering, washing, drying, and crushing to obtain an additive blank and (iii) obtaining a pretreatment additive after the additive blank is allowed to stand in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, mixing the pretreatment additive and the low melting point alloy in a mass ratio of 1:5 to 1:10, heating until the alloy is melted, soaking, filtering and freezing to obtain an additive and (iv) taking 15-30 pts. wt. cement, 10-12 pts. wt. quicklime, 2-3 pts. wt. carbon powder, 1-4 pts. wt. mullite powder, 0.5-1 pts. wt. surfactant, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. bentonite, 0.8-1 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 6-12 pts. wt. modified paraffin, 7-8 pts. wt. additive, 6-9 pts. wt. urea and 10-20 pts. wt. water, mixing cement with water, adding carbon powder, mullite powder, surfactant, bentonite, calcium stearate, urea, quicklime, modified paraffin and additives, mixing, injection molding the mixture, obtaining the blank, autoclaving the billet, curing, pretreating the pre-stressed aerated brick, calcining the pre-treatment anti-pressure aerated brick in an oxygen atmosphere, cooling to room temperature and discharging
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the paraffin wax is a paraffin mixture having a carbon number of 22-28. In the step (ii), the polyethene polyamine is triethylenetetramine, diethylenetriamine or ethylenediamine. In the step (iii), the low melting point alloy is a lead-bismuth alloy, where the mass fraction of bismuth is 52%, the mass fraction of lead is 40% and the mass fraction of cadmium is 8%. In the step (iv), the particle size of the carbon powder is 100 meshes. In the step (iv), the particle size of the mullite powder is 120 mesh. In the step (iv), the surfactant is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, sodium lauryl sulfate or Tween-80. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the graphene oxide-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture is obtained by mixing graphene oxide and water in a mass ratio of 1:250-1:300, adding a carboxylated carbon nanotube having a mass of 2-3 times of graphene oxide, stirring, mixing and ultrasonically dispersing.

AU YIN X
   TIAN M
AE YIN X (YINX-Individual)
GA 201895733N
AB    NOVELTY - Pharmaceutical composition comprises 50-150 pts. wt. life-saving king, 60-180 pts. wt. mustard, 40-120 pts. wt. ginseng, 50-150 pts. wt. radix zanthoxyli, 70-230 pts. wt. big leaf evergreen, 60-180 pts. wt. sinomenine, 40-120 pts. wt. folium artemisiae argyi, 50-150 pts. wt. pepper, 15-45 pts. wt. notoginseng, 15-45 pts. wt. pangolin, 10-30 pts. wt. artificial bezoar, 10-30 pts. wt. radix paeoniae alba, 5-15 pts. wt. rhizoma acorigraminei, 5-15 pts. wt. metronidazole, 5-15 pts. wt. vitamin B1, and 5-15 pts. wt. vitamin B6.
   USE - Pharmaceutical composition for treating pain (claimed), expelling wind, clearing away cold, freeing channels, and treating inflammation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing pharmaceutical composition, which involves:
   (A) taking half quantity of life-saving king, mustard, evergreen big leaf, and sinomenine;
   (B) crushing to obtain powder;
   (C) placing at (-)20 degrees C;
   (D) freezing;
   (E) performing super-fine crushing and ultra-micro crushing of more than 90% micro-powder particle, where diameter is not more than 6 microns;
   (F) adding rest half of life-saving king, mustard, evergreen big leaf, and sinomenine;
   (G) performing ultra-micro crushing to 40 meshes;
   (H) subjecting ultra-micro powder to carbon dioxide extraction kettle for 2 hours;
   (I) controlling pressure in extraction kettle to 30 megaPascals;
   (J) soaking in separation kettle;
   (K) controlling separation kettle separating pressure to 6 megaPascals;
   (L) heating to 60 degrees C;
   (M) keeping for 4 hours;
   (N) maintaining flow rate of carbon dioxide at 30 Kg/hour;
   (O) separating to obtain extract B;
   (P) taking ginseng, radix zanthoxyli, Artemisia leaf and pepper;
   (Q) adding 4 times water to soak for 20 minutes;
   (R) extracting for 1 hour for first time;
   (S) filtering to obtain filtrate;
   (T) adding medicine dregs with 2 times water to extract for 0.5 hours;
   (U) filtering to obtain filtrate;
   (V) combining filtrates of two times;
   (W) filtering;
   (X) concentrating filtrate to 80 degrees C to relative density of 1.15-1.18;
   (Y) adding ethanol until alcohol content reaches 65%;
   (Z) allowing to stand;
   (() taking supernatant;
   (\) recycling ethanol to obtain supernatant C;
   ()) pouring rest notoginseng, pangolin, artificial bezoar, radix paeoniae alba, rhizoma acori graminei with 6 times quantity of water to soak for 40 minutes;
   (^) extracting for 2 times, each time for 1 hour;
   (_) combining boiled liquid of two times filtrate;
   (`) filtering;
   (a) performing centrifugal separation at rotating speed of 3000 rotations//minutes for 10 minutes;
   (b) separating supernatant to obtain supernatant D;
   (c) crushing metronidazole, vitamin B1 and vitamin B6 to fine powder to obtain fine powder E;
   (d) adding obtaind extract B, supernatant C and supernatant D;
   (e) mixing uniformly;
   (f) adding fine powder A and powder E;
   (g) stirring uniformly;
   (h) adding 20 pts. wt. crospovidone;
   (i) stirring uniformly; and
   (j) preparing to granule, tablet, pill, or capsule to obtain the desired pharmaceutical composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: Pharmaceutical composition comprises 80-120 pts. wt., preferably, 100 pts. wt. life-saving king, 96-144 pts. wt., preferably, 120 pts. wt. mustard, 64-96 pts. wt., preferably, 80 pts. wt. ginseng, 80-120 pts. wt., preferably, 100 pts. wt. radix zanthoxyli, 120-180 pts. wt., preferably, 150 pts. wt. big leaf evergreen, 96-140 pts. wt., preferably, 120 pts. wt. sinomenine, 96-144 pts. wt., preferably, 120 pts. wt. folium artemisiae argyi, 64-96 pts. wt., preferably, 80 pts. wt. pepper, 80-120 pts. wt., preferably, 100 pts. wt. notoginseng, 24-36 pts. wt., preferably, 30 pts. wt. pangolin, 24-36 pts. wt., preferably, 30 pts. wt. artificial bezoar, 16-24 pts. wt., preferably, 20 pts. wt. radix paeoniae alba, 8-12 pts. wt., preferably, 20 pts. wt. rhizoma acorigraminei, 8-12 pts. wt., preferably, 10 pts. wt. metronidazole, 8-12 pts. wt., preferably, 10 pts. wt. vitamin B1, and 8-12 pts. wt., preferably, 10 pts. wt. vitamin B6. TI Manufacturing wearable ball useful in ball milling, comprises adding furnace material into induction furnace, heating, sampling, testing, molding, cooling, quenching, spraying coating powder and cooling.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHANG Y
   XIE H
   CHENG Y
AE ANHUI NINGGUO YACHEN GRINDS CASTING CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201879485J
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing wearable ball comprises (i) adding furnace material into induction furnace, introducing nitrogen, heating, and removing slag to obtain molten steel, (ii) sampling and testing the composition content of molten steel, (iii) placing leavening agent and inoculant on the ladle bottom, directly injecting the molten steel into the ladle for metamorphism and inoculation processing, pouring the molten steel into the mold, and cooling to obtain wearable ball blank, (iv) placing the wearable ball blank in high frequency quenching, placing the wearable ball blank in a heating furnace, and air cooling, (v) sandblasting the surface of the wear-resistant ball, spraying the coating powder, re-melting the coating by carbon dioxide laser, the laser power is 1200W, the spot diameter is 4 mm, the scanning speed is 8 mm/seconds, and cooling.
   USE - The wearable ball is useful in ball milling (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The wearable ball has sufficient strength, hardness, wear resistance, toughness and corrosion resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing wearable ball comprises (i) adding the furnace material into the induction furnace, introducing nitrogen into the induction furnace, heating the furnace material until it is completely melted, maintaining the temperature for 5-10 minutes, stirring, maintaining the vacuum and the negative pressure at 0.4-0.6 kPa, and removing slag to obtain molten steel, (ii) sampling and testing the composition content of molten steel, and adjusting it accordingly, where the adjusted elements of molten steel includes 1.5-1.8% carbon, 0.4-0.6% manganese, 0.9-1.2% silicon, 4.5-8.5% chromium, 1.4-1.9% nickel, 0.5-1% molybdenum, 0.5-1.5% rhenium, 0.05-0.08% niobium, and the balance is iron, (iii) placing leavening agent and inoculant on the ladle bottom, directly injecting the molten steel into the ladle for metamorphism and inoculation processing, pouring the molten steel into the mold, and cooling to obtain wearable ball blank, (iv) placing the wearable ball blank at 720-780 degrees C in high frequency quenching for one time quenching, reducing the temperature to 550-600 degrees C for the second time quenching, naturally cooling to room temperature, placing the wearable ball blank in a heating furnace at 350-400 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and air cooling, (v) sandblasting the surface of the wear-resistant ball, spraying the coating powder on the surface of the wear-resistant ball with a flame spray gun, the coating powder including alloy powder, tungsten carbide, and cobalt, and the alloy powder includes 18.5-25.6% chromium, 5.6-6.7% silicon, 35.6-50.9% nickel, 4.2-5.5% boron, and the balance is iron, the oxygen pressure is 2 MPa, the acetylene pressure is 0.08 MPa, the spraying distance is 150-180 mm, re-melting the coating by carbon dioxide laser, the laser power is 1200W, the spot diameter is 4 mm, the scanning speed is 8 mm/seconds, and cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (v), the mass ratio of the alloy powder, tungsten carbide, and cobalt is 7:2:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the flow rate of nitrogen is 2 l/seconds. In the step (iii), the casting temperature is 1540-1580 degrees C. In the step (iv), the first quenching time is 35-40 minutes, and the secondary quenching time is 20-30 minutes. The temperature rising rate during tempering is 40-60 degrees C/minute. In the step (v), the sandblast is roughened, cleaned and dried.

PN KR1895520-B1
TI Producing biodegradable oil comprises e.g. heating used oil under atmospheric pressure to separate sediments with density difference, removing primary and secondary foreign substances by microfiltration, and then volatile organic compounds.
AU BYUN H J
   CHOI K R
AE BYUN H J (BYUN-Individual)
   CHOI K R (CHOI-Individual)
GA 2018705872
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biodegradable oil comprises e.g. (a) heating used oil at 60-70 degrees C under atmospheric pressure to separate the sediment with a density difference due to a convection action, (b) removing a primary foreign substance having size of greater than or equal to 2000 mu m contained in the oil separated from the sediment by microfiltration, (c) removing a secondary foreign substance having size of greater than or equal to 20 mu m contained in the used oil from which the primary foreign matter is removed through microfiltration while maintaining the used oil at 70-80 degrees C, (d) removing volatile organic compounds and moisture under vacuum to form a first intermediate while maintaining the used oil from which the secondary foreign substances are removed to temperature of 60-70 degrees C, (e) mixing first intermediate material and biodegradation accelerator to form second intermediate material, and (f) reacting zeolite with second intermediate to exchange cations in the zeolite to form catalyst treatment product.
   USE - The method is useful to produce biodegradable oil for concrete (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces biodegradable oil by using oil having viscosity index ISO VG 32- 68 e.g. hydraulic oil, turbine oil, and mechanic oil, decomposes hydrocarbons into carbon dioxide and water, prevents damage due to the heat treatment of the raw material, removes harmful substances and improves color and odor by utilizing zeolite inorganic catalyst characteristics, and preserves safe and pleasant environment by allowing the hydrocarbons remaining on the concrete surface and the temporary equipment after the product is used to be biodegraded into carbon dioxide and water in a natural state.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing biodegradable oil comprises (a) heating used oil at 60-70 degrees C under atmospheric pressure to separate the sediment with a density difference due to a convection action, (b) removing a primary foreign substance having size of greater than or equal to 2000 mu m contained in the oil separated from the sediment by microfiltration, (c) removing a secondary foreign substance having size of greater than or equal to 20 mu m contained in the used oil from which the primary foreign matter is removed through microfiltration while maintaining the used oil at 70-80 degrees C, (d) removing volatile organic compounds and moisture under vacuum to form a first intermediate while maintaining the used oil from which the secondary foreign substances are removed to temperature of 60-70 degrees C, (e) mixing 80-95 wt.% first intermediate material and 5-20 wt.% biodegradation accelerator to form second intermediate material, (f) reacting 1-3 pts. wt. zeolite with 100 pts. wt. second intermediate to exchange cations in the zeolite to form catalyst treatment product, and (g) obtaining a biodegradable oil by separating the biodegradable oil from the catalyst treatment product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of production method (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The steps (a) to (g) are carried out by using heat transferred by an indirect heating method using a heating medium boiler or a steam boiler. In the step (d), the volatile organic compounds contained in the used oil are removed by condensing the water with the volatile organic compounds to remove the volatile organic compounds. The non-condensed volatile organic compounds are adsorbed to activated carbon. The residual hydrocarbons on the concrete surface are biodegraded with carbon dioxide and water after the step (g).

AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium bicarbonate comprises (i) adding heavy alkali raw material containing sodium hydrogencarbonate, sodium carbonate, ammonium hydrogencarbonate to a dissolution tank, (ii) passing slurry to the ammonia distillation tower, (iii) passing high temperature solution A1 into the mixer, mixing with the low temperature solution A2, (iv) discharging clear liquid at the top of the mixer to cooler or the flasher, (v) separating crystal slurry, passing solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal to a drying process, (vi) sending filtrate to the filtrate barrel, passing filtrate of the filtrate barrel to the carbonization tower and carbonizing the sodium carbonate in the filtrate and the carbon dioxide contained in the industrial gas in the carbonization tower, and (vii) discharging reaction liquid from the carbonization tower to the dissolution tank to dissolve the raw material thus completing the circulation of the liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short process flow, small energy consumption and low running cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing sodium bicarbonate comprises (i) adding heavy alkali raw material containing sodium hydrogencarbonate, sodium carbonate, ammonium hydrogencarbonate to a dissolution tank to prepare a raw material slurry containing sodium bicarbonate solid, (ii) passing slurry obtained in the step (i) to the ammonia distillation tower, where the residence time is 5-300 minutes the temperature inside the distillation ammonia column is 28-280 degrees C and the ammonia is evaporated and passing gas generated by the tower to recovery process through the top exhaust gas outlet, (iii) passing high temperature solution A1 containing the high concentration sodium hydrogencarbonate and the low concentration sodium carbonate in the steaming ammonia tower into the mixer, mixing with the low temperature solution A2 containing the low concentration sodium hydrogencarbonate in the mixer, (iv) discharging clear liquid at the top of the mixer to cooler or the flasher to cool down to obtain a low temperature solution A2, and the low temperature solution (ie, A2) is refluxed into the mixer, (v) separating crystal slurry at the bottom of the mixer by solid-liquid separator to obtain solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal and filtrate, passing solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal to a drying process to obtain dried sodium hydrogencarbonate is the target product sodium bicarbonate, (vi) sending filtrate separated by the solid-liquid separator to the filtrate barrel, passing filtrate of the filtrate barrel to the carbonization tower and carbonizing the sodium carbonate in the filtrate and the carbon dioxide contained in the industrial gas in the carbonization tower to form sodium hydrogencarbonate, and (vii) discharging reaction liquid from the carbonization tower to the dissolution tank to dissolve the raw material thus completing the circulation of the liquid. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for producing a sodium bicarbonate, comprising a dissolution tank, an ammonia distillation tower, a mixer, and a flasher which are sequentially connected or cooler, solid-liquid separator, filtrate tank and carbonization tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the control tank has a control temperature of 28-100 degrees C and the mass ratio of the water in the heavy alkali raw material to the raw material slurry is 1:2-14, In the step (ii), the carbon dioxide gas is also introduced into the lower part of the ammonia distillation tower, the mass ratio of the heavy alkali raw material to the carbon dioxide is 1:0.1-1, and the temperature inside the controlled ammoniating tower is 80-160 degrees C and the pressure is 0.2-2.2 MPa. The temperature of the high temperature solution containing the high concentration sodium hydrogencarbonate and the low concentration sodium carbonate from the ammonia column is 28-280 degrees C and the temperature of the low temperature solution A2 containing the low concentration sodium hydrogencarbonate and the low concentration sodium carbonate is 20-80 degrees C and the volume ratio of A1-A2 is 1:0.005-29. The temperature of the high temperature solution A1 is 80-120 degrees C, the temperature of the low temperature solution A2 is 30-80 degrees C and the volume ratio of A1-A2 is 1:1-5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device comprises dissolution tank, an ammonia tower, a mixer, a flasher, a solid-liquid separator, a filtrate tank. The bottom discharge port of the dissolution tank is connected to the feed port of the ammonia distillation tower through a pipe. The discharge port of the ammonia column is connected to the feed port of the mixer. The clear liquid nozzle is provided on each of the top sides of the mixer. The clear liquid nozzle passes through the clear liquid pipe and the flasher. The feed port is connected and the top of the flasher is provided with a flash steam outlet. The bottom outlet of the flasher is connected to the inlet of the mixer. The bottom discharge port of the mixer is connected to the solid-liquid separator through a pipe. The filtrate of the solid-liquid separator is connected to the filtrate tank through the liquid outlet. The filtrate barrel is in communication with the inlet of the upper portion of the carbonization tower. The inlet and the discharge port are respectively disposed at two sides of the lower portion of the carbonization tower. The carbonization tower discharge port is connected back to the dissolution tank through a pipe. The discharge port of the solid-liquid separator is connected to a drying device. The finished baking soda is obtained after the drying process. The flasher is replaced by a cooler that communicates with the bottom of the cooler through a clearing conduit. The top end of the cooler passes through a return conduit. The inlet of the mixer is connected. The cooler is connected to a cold energy refrigerant generating device which is a circulating water pipe, an air compressor or a refrigerator. The top of the carbonization tower and the ammonia distillation tower are provided with a tail gas outlet. The ammonia distillation tower is provided with a built-in or external heater. The carbonization tower is not provided with a built-in or an external cooler, i.e. an uncooled carbonization tower. The mixer is a thickener, a clarification tank, a crystallizer or a stirring device. The solid-liquid separator is a belt type vacuum filter, a drum vacuum filter, a disc filter, a plate and frame filter press, a centrifuge or a settling tank. The cooler is a coil heat exchanger, a tubular heat exchanger, a plate heat exchanger or a kettle heat exchanger. The flasher is a liquid top down spray type or a liquid bottom down spray type.

TI Low-temperature purification of biogas involves pressurizing biogas into the air compressor C-1 through biogas feed gas inlet, cooling, passing into the packed bed for capture and purification, pressurizing and purging.
AU SONG C
   FAN Z
   SUN Y
   XIE M
   QIU Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201861874X
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature purification of biogas comprises pressurizing biogas into the air compressor C-1 through biogas feed gas inlet to 4-5 bar, cooling to 25 degrees C by refrigerator, passing into the packed bed for capture and purification, pressurizing to 190-200 bar by air compressor C-2 where initial temperature of packed bed is - 100 to - 160 degrees C, entering into the regeneration stage when the filler in the packed bed is adsorbed and penetrated, pressurizing the dry air by air compressor C-3, passing through the refrigerator, entering into the packed bed, purging the attached carbon dioxide, regenerating the filler, passing the dry air to cool the packed bed to less than - 110 degrees C to prepare for the next capture stage and repeating steps to complete the alternate continuous operation of the packed bed to achieve the capture system and regeneration/cooling system.
   USE - Method for carrying out low-temperature purification of biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of low-temperature biogas comprises pressurizing biogas into the air compressor C-1 through biogas feed gas inlet to 4-5 bar, cooling to 25 degrees C by refrigerator, passing into the packed bed for capture and purification, pressurizing to 190-200 bar by air compressor C-2 where initial temperature of packed bed is - 100 to - 160 degrees C. entering into the regeneration stage when the filler in the packed bed is adsorbed and penetrated, pressurizing the dry air by air compressor C-3, passing through the refrigerator, entering into the packed bed, purging the attached carbon dioxide, regenerating the filler, passing the dry air to cool the packed bed to less than - 110 degrees C to prepare for the next capture stage and repeating steps to complete the alternate continuous operation of the packed bed to achieve the capture system and regeneration/cooling system. The packed bed is mainly composed of trap system, and the inlet and outlet terminals of packed bed are all connected with on-line detecting instrument. The capture system coupled with regeneration/cooling system constitutes coupling device to realize continuous operation in different operation stages. The inlet pipeline of coupling device is provided with inlet of biogas raw gas. The outlet pipeline of coupling device is provided with outlet of biogas raw gas. The capture system has air compressor C-1, refrigerator A, packed bed and outlet of biogas raw gas provided in air compressor C-2, which are imported by the biogas raw gas inlet and in series by pipeline. The bypass of outlet pipe of the coupling device is provided with regenerative inlet, and the bypass of entrance pipe of coupling device is provided with regenerative outlet. The regeneration/cooling system has air compressor C-3, refrigerator B, packed bed and regenerative outlet which are connected in series with regenerative inlet and pipeline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The biogas raw gas inlet flowrate is 1500-1600 standard L/minute. The dry air is 1 bar, 25 degrees C, and the total air flowrate is 2-3 kg/second.

TI Preparing human gallbladder cancer gemcitabine resistant cell line comprises e. g. seeding human gallbladder cancer cells of logarithmic growth phase in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing fetal bovine serum and incubating.
AU SUO T
   LIU H
   WANG G
AE UNIV FUDAN ZHONGSHAN HOSPITAL (UYFU-C)
   MEDI-TOOL MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO (MEDI-Non-standard)
GA 201859875N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing human gallbladder cancer gemcitabine resistant cell line comprises e. g. seeding human gallbladder cancer cells of logarithmic growth phase in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 20% fetal bovine serum, incubating at 37 degrees C in a saturated humidity incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide, adding gemcitabine into the Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 20% fetal bovine serum, incubating, where the initial concentration of gemcitabine is 0.2 mol/l, increasing the concentration to 0.1 mmol/l when the cells returned to normal growth, passing in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 0.5 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum, culturing the cells in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 1 mmol/l gemcitabine and containing 20% fetal bovine serum for 12 hours, culturing again in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 0.5 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum for 5-10 days.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing human gallbladder cancer gemcitabine resistant cell line.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, and has resistant index of more than 10.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing human gallbladder cancer gemcitabine resistant cell line comprises seeding human gallbladder cancer cells of logarithmic growth phase in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 20% fetal bovine serum, incubating at 37 degrees C in a saturated humidity incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide, adding gemcitabine into the Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 20% fetal bovine serum, incubating, where the initial concentration of gemcitabine is 0.2 mol/l, increasing the concentration to 0.1 mmol/l when the cells returned to normal growth, passing in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 0.5 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum, culturing the cells in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 1 mmol/l gemcitabine and containing 20% fetal bovine serum for 12 hours, culturing again in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 0.5 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum for 5-10 days, inducing the cells by Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 10 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum for 12 hours, culturing in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 4 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum for 5-10 days, culturing the cells again in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium 1 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum for 12 hours when the cells are stably grown, growing the cells in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 10 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum and growing the human gallbladder cancer gemcitabine-resistant cell line in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium containing 10 mmol/l gemcitabine and 20% fetal bovine serum.

TI Sewage treatment regeneration recycling system for            concrete mixing station production, has carbon dioxide            neutralizing device for injecting carbon dioxide in            neutralizing water to obtain mixed water that is            returned to dehydrating and separating system.
AU CHEN H
   WANG L
   LIU Q
   YUAN X
   LIU X
   LEI X
   ZENG Q
   LI Y
   ZENG C
   LUO W
AE SICHUAN RAILWAY CONSTR CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5003H
AB    NOVELTY - Sewage treatment regeneration recycling system                comprises a sewage stirring pool (1) for storing                the production sewage of the concrete mixing                station. The dehydrating and separating system (2)                is connected with the sewage stirring tank (1), for                obtaining sewage in the sewage mixing tank and                performing solid-liquid separation to obtain clean                water and water. A clean water storage tank (4)                connected to the dehydrated and separated system                for obtaining clean water of the dehydrate and                separation system and storing. A carbon dioxide                neutralization device (3) connected in sequence                with the dehydrates and separation systems (2),                used to obtain the neutralizing water of dehydrate                separation system. The carbon dioxide is injected                into the neutralization water to obtain mixed                water, and the mixed water is returned to the                dehydration separation system, where a closed cycle                is formed between the dehydrating separation system                ( 2) and the carbon dioxide neutrality                device.
   USE - The system is useful for recycling and                recycling sewage in production of concrete mixing                station. The sewage treatment regeneration                recycling system is used in concrete mixing                stations.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively establishes the closed                cycle between the carbon dioxide neutralizing                device and the dehydration separation system, so                that the neutralizing water in the circulation are                all processed as solid and clean water, greatly                improves the regeneration and recycling ratio of                the sewage, avoids the discharge of the waste                water, and improves the working efficiency. The                carbon dioxide property is stable, the harmfulness                is small, so it does not belong to the control                transportation product, and the risk in the                neutralization process is greatly reduced. The flow                guiding plate is horizontally arranged and                uniformly distributed from bottom to top. The                flowing length in the system is effectively                prolonged. The exhaust pipe is capable of stirring                the neutralized water liquid in the plate body                through the gas discharged by the screw                thread.

PN WO2018213214-A1; US2021322809-A1; US11478670-B2
TI Fire extinguisher capable of being used on variety            of different class of fire, has control valve to            control discharge of fire extinguishing media from            interior of storage vessel through discharge tube            though output of control valve.
AU CZARNEK R
AE CZARNEK R (CZAR-Individual)
   CZARNEK R (CZAR-Individual)
GA 2018930098
AB    NOVELTY - The extinguisher (100) has a control valve                (102) coupled to a storage vessel (101). A liquid                fire extinguishing media (109) comprising water in                the storage vessel. A carbon dioxide (112) charging                the storage vessel to a partial pressure less than                an operating pressure of the fire extinguisher. A                nitrogen (113) charging the storage vessel                including the fire extinguishing media and the                carbon dioxide to the operating pressure. The                carbon dioxide is order of magnitude more soluble                in the fire extinguishing media than the nitrogen.                The operating pressure a portion of the carbon                dioxide is dissolved in the fire extinguishing                media. A discharge tube (107) extending between an                input of the control valve and a location submerged                in the fire extinguishing media. The control valve                is operative to control a discharge of the fire                extinguishing media from the interior of the                storage vessel through the discharge tube though an                output of the control valve.
   USE - Fire extinguisher such as liquid/water-based                fire extinguisher portable fire extinguisher, hand                held or wheeled fire extinguisher and truck based                fire extinguisher, or a stationary fire                extinguisher capable of being used on variety of                different class of fire.
   ADVANTAGE - The portable extinguishers, propellant gas                used to pressurize the tank or vessel is used to                both propel the water through the nozzle and to mix                with the water stream to generate small bubble that                improve atomization of the water in the nozzle. The                design, requires large side openings and a large                amount of propellant gas to produce mist of                effective quality. The storage vessel strength                increases, and therefore, weight and cost, not make                it practical or cost effective for many                application. The pressure within vessel decreases                during discharge of fire extinguishing media, a                portion of the carbon dioxide dissolved in fire                extinguishing media forms bubbles in fire                extinguishing media and thus improving the                atomization efficiency of nozzle. The carbon                dioxide into the fire extinguishing media therefore                aids in decreasing the pressure drop in storage                vessel, improving the intensity and uniformity of                the discharge rate of the extinguishing                media.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for fire extinguisher.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of a                fluid charged fire extinguisher.
   Fire extinguisher (100)
   Storage vessel (101)
   Control valve (102)
   Discharge tube (107)
   Liquid fire extinguishing media (109)
   Carbon dioxide (112)
   Nitrogen (113)

PT P
PN WO2021007508-A1; US2021062349-A1; WO2021007508-A9
TI Electrolytic system for electrochemical reduction            of carbon dioxide, comprises an anode, and a cathode in            electrical communication with the anode, where cathode            comprises a first current collector and a porous            gas-diffusion membrane.
AU AGAPIE T
   ROSAS-HERNANDEZ A
   LI F
   PETERS J C
   SARGENT E H
   THEVENON A
AE CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
GA 2021055735
AB    NOVELTY - The electrolytic system comprises an anode,                and a cathode in electrical communication with the                anode. The cathode comprises a first current                collector, a porous gas-diffusion membrane having a                first side and a second side. The porous                gas-diffusion membrane is permeable to carbon                dioxide (CO2). The electrocatalyst layer is                adjacent to the second side of the gas-diffusion                membrane and is in electrical communication with                the first current collector. The electrocatalyst                layer comprises an electrically conductive catalyst                and a selectivity-determining organic material                attached to at least a portion of the electrically                conductive catalyst. The organic material is formed                of multiple oligomers, each oligomer comprises                multiple covalently bonded base units. Each base                unit comprises at least one heterocyclic group                having at least one nitrogen in its ring                structure.
   USE - Electrolytic system for electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular coating improve the                stabilization of a CO-bound intermediate in an a                top bound configuration for favoring the reduction                of CO2 to ethylene. The adhered molecules improve                the stabilization of an atop-bound CO intermediate                for favoring further reduction to ethylene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The electrolytic system comprises an anode,                and a cathode in electrical communication with the                anode. The cathode comprises a first current                collector, a porous gas-diffusion membrane having a                first side and a second side. The porous                gas-diffusion membrane is permeable to carbon                dioxide (CO2). The electrocatalyst layer is                adjacent to the second side of the gas-diffusion                membrane and is in electrical communication with                the first current collector. The electrocatalyst                layer comprises an electrically conductive catalyst                and a selectivity-determining organic material                attached to at least a portion of the electrically                conductive catalyst. The organic material is formed                of multiple oligomers, each oligomer comprises                multiple covalently bonded base units. Each base                unit comprises at least one heterocyclic group                having at least one nitrogen in its ring structure.                The anion exchange membrane is adjacent to the                electrocatalyst layer and is positioned between the                anode and the cathode. The anion exchange membrane                shows anion conductivity and the cathode is in                ionic communication with the anode via the anion                exchange membrane. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included                for the following:
   (1) a porous gas-diffusion membrane, which                comprises a first side and a second side, and an                electrocatalyst layer adjacent to the second side                of the gas-diffusion membrane, where the                electrocatalyst layer comprises an electrically                conductive catalyst and a selectivity-determining                organic material attached to at least a portion of                the electron conducting catalyst, where the organic                material is formed of multiple oligomers; porous                gas-diffusion membrane is permeable to carbon                dioxide CO2 gas, first side of the gas-diffusion                membrane is capable of providing transport of CO2                gas and electron conducting catalyst is                electrically connected to an electrode of an                electrolytic system;
   (2) a method of depositing an organic material                onto an electrically conductive catalyst, which                involves exposing the electrically conductive                catalyst to an electrolyte, applying a current                and/or voltage to the electrically conductive                catalyst, applying the current and/or voltage, and                reducing the additive at the electrically                conductive catalyst for depositing the organic                material onto the electrically conductive catalyst,                where the electrically conductive catalyst is                provided on a second side of a gas-diffusion                membrane which is electrically insulating, porous,                and CO2-permeable, electrolyte comprises a first                salt having a first concentration and at least one                additive salt having a second concentration, and                additive comprises an organic cation having at                least one heterocyclic group having at least one                nitrogen in its ring structure; and
   (3) a method for reducing CO2 gas using an                electrolytic system, which involves applying a                voltage between an anode and a cathode of the                electrolytic system, delivering the CO2 gas to the                first side of the gas-diffusion membrane such that                the CO2 gas diffuses through the gas-diffusion                membrane to the electrocatalyst layer, reducing the                CO2 gas at the electrocatalyst layer to produce at                least one product gas at the electrocatalyst layer,                and removing the product gas from the gas-diffusion                membrane, where product gas has a composition                comprising two carbon atoms, organic material                increases a selectivity for the product gas with                respect to reducing the CO2 gas at the electrically                conductive catalyst free of the organic                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The anion exchange                  membrane provides for transport of anions formed                  or dissociated at the electrocatalyst layer from                  the electrocatalyst layer to the first                  electrolyte. The electrically conductive catalyst                  comprises copper. The gas-diffusion membrane is                  hydrophobic. The electrolyte has a pH selected                  from the range of 6 to 14. Preferred Components:                  The gas-diffusion membrane comprises a                  fluorocarbon polymer. The electron conducting                  catalyst comprises copper. The oligomers                  comprises 2, 3, or 4 of the base units. Each of                  the oligomers is a dimer comprising 2 of the base                  units. Each of the base units comprises at least                  one substituted or unsubstituted pyridinium                  group, at least one substituted or unsubstituted                  arylpyridinium group, and/or at least one                  substituted or unsubstituted phenanthrolinium                  group. Each of the base unit is represented by                  group of formulae (I-II)..each of R1 and R5=substituted or                  unsubstituted 2-nC alkylene group, a substituted                  or unsubstituted 2-nC heteroalkylene group, a                  substituted or unsubstituted 2-nC alkenylene                  group, or substituted or unsubstituted 2-nC                  heteroalkenylene group;n=2 to 1 00;R2, R4, R6, R7 and R9=single bond to another                  base unit of the respective oligomer, a divalent                  linker group to another base unit of the                  respective oligomer, a hydrogen, a halogen, or                  selected from substituted or unsubstituted alkyl                  group, aryl group, cycloalkyl group, aryl group,                  heteroaryl group, hydroxyl group, alkoxy group,                  alkenyl group, acyl group, hydrocarbyl group,                  alkynyl group, alkynyl group, alkylaryl group,                  halocarbon group, thiol group, amine group, amide                  group, aminyl group, phosphorus-containing group,                  silicon-containing group, a boron-containing                  group, pyridinium and any combination of these;                  andeach of R3, R7 and R20-R21=hydrogen, a                  halogen, or selected from the group consisting of                  a substituted or unsubstituted alkyl group, aryl                  group, cycloalkyl group, aryl group, heteroaryl                  group, hydroxyl group, alkoxy group, alkenyl                  group, acyl group, hydrocarbyl group, alkynyl                  group, alkynyl group, alkylaryl group, halocarbon                  group, thiol group, amine group, aminyl group,                  phosphorus-containing group, silicon-containing                  group, a boron- containing group, pyridinium and                  any combination.The additive comprises substituted or                  unsubstituted pyridinium group, arylpyridinium                  group, and/or phenanthrolinium group.

PN CN112028385-A; CN213357242-U
TI Processing high-concentration organic wastewater            by cassava as raw material to produce alcohol involves            performing desanding by using cassava to produce            organic wastewater, and using desanding equipment to            remove sand and gravel.
AU ZHANG C
   DU S
   LU X
   ZHOU S
AE GOLD YIMENG GROUP CO LTD (GOLD-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4227E
AB    NOVELTY - Processing high-concentration organic                wastewater by cassava as a raw material to produce                alcohol involves performing desanding by using                cassava as raw material to produce                high-concentration organic wastewater from alcohol                production and using desanding equipment to remove                sand and gravel to obtain desanded wastewater. The                primary anaerobic fermentation is performed by                adjusting temperature of desanded wastewater to                58-60 degrees C, pumping it into first-stage                anaerobic tank for first-stage anaerobic                fermentation treatment to obtain methane, carbon                dioxide, sludge containing lignin organic matter,                and wastewater after primary anaerobic                fermentation. The secondary anaerobic fermentation                is performed by adjusting temperature of obtained                wastewater to 42-45 degrees C, and introducing it                into secondary anaerobic tank for secondary                anaerobic fermentation treatment to obtain methane,                carbon dioxide, settled sludge, and wastewater                after secondary anaerobic fermentation.
   USE - Method for processing high-concentration                organic wastewater by cassava as raw material to                produce alcohol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process                high-concentration organic wastewater by cassava as                raw material to produce alcohol, which operates                biochemical system effectively and stably, and                ensures that effluent after reaction and                precipitation reaches national first-level                discharge standard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing high-concentration organic                wastewater by cassava as a raw material to produce                alcohol involves performing desanding by using                cassava as raw material to produce                high-concentration organic wastewater from alcohol                production and using desanding equipment to remove                sand and gravel to obtain desanded wastewater. The                primary anaerobic fermentation is performed by                adjusting temperature of desanded wastewater to                58-60 degrees C, pumping it into first-stage                anaerobic tank for first-stage anaerobic                fermentation treatment to obtain methane, carbon                dioxide, sludge containing lignin organic matter,                and wastewater after primary anaerobic                fermentation. The secondary anaerobic fermentation                is performed by adjusting temperature of obtained                wastewater to 42-45 degrees C, and introducing it                into secondary anaerobic tank for secondary                anaerobic fermentation treatment to obtain methane,                carbon dioxide, settled sludge, and wastewater                after secondary anaerobic fermentation. The                three-stage anaerobic fermentation is performed by                introducing obtained wastewater into IC tank to                obtain methane, carbon dioxide, settled sludge, and                third-stage anaerobic fermented wastewater. The                obtained wastewater is introduced into air                flotation desilting tank and mixed with the                dissolved gas water. The suspended sludge in water                floats on upper surface under action of bubbles,                and then scraped from the sludge tank by the                scraper to obtain waste water. The obtained waste                water is introduced into primary aeration tank                treatment tank for primary aeration treatment to                obtain primary aeration wastewater. The obtained                wastewater is introduced into primary aeration tank                for precipitation, and portion of sedimented sludge                is returned to primary aeration tank to keep                primary aeration tank sludge concentration to                2000-3000mg/L to obtain treated wastewater. The                treated wastewater is introduced into A/O                biological denitrification unit for denitrification                treatment to obtain A/O biological denitrification                treatment wastewater, where in aerobic tank                wastewater is converted into a nitrification liquid                having nitrate nitrogen through the nitrification                of nitrifying bacteria. The nitrification liquid is                returned to anoxic tank through second aerobic tank                for denitrification, and nitrification liquid                reflux ratio is adjusted according to                denitrification effect which is 5:1, and dissolved                oxygen level of the anoxic tank is controlled to                below 0.5mg/L, and C/N molar ratio is controlled to                more then 3, and when source water does not meet                the demand then carbon source is artificially added                to the inlet of the anoxic tank. The wastewater                from A/O biological denitrification treatment is                introduced into secondary sedimentation tank for                sedimentation, where clear water and sludge are                separated, and clear water is discharged to the                outside, and sludge is returned to A/O biological                denitrification treatment unit to complete                processing. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                device for processing high-concentration organic                wastewater from cassava as a raw material to                produce alcohol, which comprises feed adjustment                tank, anaerobic system, aerobic system, sludge                blending tank and biogas tank, where anaerobic                system contains a primary anaerobic tank, a                secondary anaerobic tank, an IC tank and an air                flotation sludge removal tank, and aerobic system                includes a primary aeration tank, a primary                sedimentation tank, an A/O biological                denitrification unit, and a secondary sedimentation                tank, where feed regulating tank, primary anaerobic                tank, secondary anaerobic tank, IC tank, air                flotation sludge removal tank, primary aeration                tank treatment tank, primary sedimentation tank,                A/O biological denitrification unit and secondary                sedimentation tank are connected                sequentially.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The dissolved COD of                  high-concentration organic wastewater is more                  than 40000mg/L, and temperature of                  high-concentration organic wastewater produced by                  using cassava as a raw material is 58-60 degrees                  C. The sand and gravel are removed by using                  cyclonic de-sanding equipment to remove gravel in                  wastewater. The COD value of wastewater after                  primary anaerobic fermentation is less than                  4000mg/L. The COD value of the wastewater after                  secondary anaerobic fermentation is less than                  2000mg/L. The COD value of wastewater after                  three-stage anaerobic fermentation is less than                  1000mg/L. The COD concentration of wastewater                  after scraping mud is 800-1000 mg/L, ammonia                  nitrogen concentration is 250-300mg/L, total                  nitrogen is 350-400mg/L, and pH is 7.2-7.5. The                  COD of clean water is not more than 50mg/L,                  ammonia nitrogen concentration is less than                  5mg/L, total nitrogen is less than 15mg/L, and pH                  is 6.8-7.5. The A/O biological denitrification                  unit includes an anoxic tank, a first aerobic                  tank and a second aerobic tank. The dissolved                  oxygen level of wastewater in anoxic tank is                  0.5mg/L or less, C/N molar ratio of wastewater in                  anoxic tank is 3 or more, and treatment time of                  anoxic tank is 8 hours. The methane and carbon                  dioxide are introduced into biogas tank for                  biogas power generation, and if COD and SS                  discharged from secondary sedimentation tank                  exceed the standard then effluent enters                  flocculation sedimentation tank for treatment,                  and flocculant PAC or PAC and PAM are added. The                  PAC dosage is 80-120ppm/ton of waste water and                  PAM dosage is 0.5-1ppm/ton of wastewater.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
