PT P
PN CN109884221-A; CN109884221-B
TI Method for analzying water-soluble essential oil components in sweet-scented Osmanthus, involves preparing sweet-scented Osmanthus aroma water and fresh Osmanthus immersion water followed by performing liquid-liquid microextraction.
AU LI Z
   NIE J
   ZHAO Y
   LI S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201954839U
AB    NOVELTY - A water-soluble essential oil components in sweet-scented Osmanthus analyzing method involves preparing sweet-scented Osmanthus aroma water by mixing fresh sweet-scented Osmanthus with deionized water at a ratio of 1:5 to obtain a raw material liquid. Fresh Osmanthus immersion water is prepared by mixing fresh Osmanthus with water to obtain raw material liquid, immersing, filtering to obtain filtrate made up with deionized water to make up the volume to the raw material liquid to obtain Osmanthus immersion water, storing at 4 degrees C for use. Dispersive liquid-liquid microextraction is performed by diluting water sample to be tested with deionized water. The proton donor and the extraction solvent are premixed in the extracted sample, emulsified by ultrasonication, a carbon dioxide source is added, ultrasonically foaming and demulsification, and the upper extraction solvent phase is aspirated after drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate.
   USE - Method for analzying water-soluble essential oil components in sweet-scented Osmanthus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables analzying water-soluble essential oil components in short operation time, simple, easy and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A water-soluble essential oil components in sweet-scented Osmanthus analyzing method involves preparing sweet-scented Osmanthus aroma water by mixing fresh sweet-scented Osmanthus with deionized water at a ratio of 1:5 to obtain a raw material liquid, performing steam distillation at a microwave power of 200 W, when the volume of the distilled fraction is 1 and the volume of the raw material liquid is 1/4-1/3, the distillation is stopped, and the fraction is diluted for 3-4 times with deionized water to obtain a sweet-scented Osmanthus water, which is stored at 4 degrees C for use. Fresh Osmanthus immersion water is prepared by mixing fresh Osmanthus with deionized water of 85-95 degrees C at a ratio of 1:5 to obtain a raw material liquid, immersing for 30 minutes, filtering to obtain filtrate made up with deionized water to make up the volume to the raw material liquid to obtain Osmanthus immersion water, storing at 4 degrees C for use. Dispersive liquid-liquid microextraction is performed by diluting water sample to be tested with deionized water and used as an extraction sample. The proton donor and the extraction solvent are premixed in the extracted sample, emulsified by ultrasonication, a carbon dioxide source is added, ultrasonically foaming and demulsification, and the upper extraction solvent phase is aspirated after drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate, a sample solution is obtained, where water sample to be tested is the fresh sweet-scented osmanthus fragrance water or immersion water, volume of the diluted deionized water is 20 times, proton donor is hydrochloric acid, acetic acid, citric acid or sodium dihydrogen phosphate, extraction solvent is toluene, carbon dioxide source is sodium carbonate or sodium hydrogencarbonate, the ratio of the amount of the proton donor to the carbon dioxide source is from 1-6:1, the amount of the carbon dioxide source is 8-10 mol/l based on the volume of the water sample to be tested, volume ratio of the extraction solvent to the water sample to be tested is 5-7:100, power of the ultrasonic wave is at 250 w, the time of the phacoemulsification is 15-90 seconds and the time of the ultrasonic bubbling and demulsification is 60-180 seconds, injecting sample solution into a gas chromatography-mass spectrometer for analysis, and obtaining a total ion chromatogram of the sample gas chromatography mass spectrometry, and the components contained in the sample solution by comparison with a standard mass spectrum, where the gas chromatographic conditions having column is DB-5MS, inlet temperature is 250 degrees C, splitless injection, column temperature is maintained at 40 degrees C for 3 minutes, rise to 5 degrees .min-1 to 90 degrees C for 1 minutes, carrier gas is high purity helium, injection volume is 1 mu l, and mass spectrometry conditions are electron bombardment ion source, electron energy of 70eV, full scan mode, scanning range of 45-550m/z, ion trap temperature of 180 degrees C, transmission line temperature of 250 degrees C, and case temperature of 50 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The proton donor is sodium dihydrogen phosphate.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC D04-A01H; J04-B01A1; J04-B01C5; J04-C01; J04-C02; J04-C04; S03-E09C

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The carbonic acid produced from the carbon dioxide acts as a catalyst of the hydrothermal reaction. The ginseng extract is ginsenoside production method characterized in that the extraction using a volatile solvent. The intact ginsenoside is converted through one of deglycosylation or dehydration. The converted ginsenosides may include one or two or more of (S)-Rg3, (R)-Rg3, Rk1, and Rg5.

   SAYAMA S
   OMURA T
   OGASAWARA K
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 2020B32474
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed gas production apparatus (50) comprises adsorber (A, B) which can adsorb carbon dioxide, hydrogen supply unit (5) which supplies hydrogen to adsorber, gas ratio acquisition unit (4) which is arranged in the downstream side of the adsorber and acquires gas ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas from the adsorber, vacuum pump (6) which vacuates the inside of the adsorber, and control unit (1) which controls vacuum pump and hydrogen supply unit. After the inside of the adsorber is evacuated for a predetermined time by the vacuum pump, the hydrogen supply unit starts to supply hydrogen to the adsorber, and stops when the target ratio by which the gas ratio is preset is reached.
   USE - A mixed gas production apparatus for methane production system.
   ADVANTAGE - Even when desorption amount of carbon dioxide which detaches from adsorber fluctuates, surge tank and vacuum pump are suppressed from increasing in power, and target ratio of gas is generated or recycling rate is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for control method of mixed gas production apparatus which involves evacuating the inside of the adsorber which adsorbs carbon dioxide for a predetermined time with a vacuum pump, acquiring gas ratio of quantity of hydrogen and quantity of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas from the adsorber, starting supply of hydrogen from the hydrogen supply unit to the adsorber after the vacuum process, and stopping supply of hydrogen to the adsorber when the target ratio by which the gas ratio is preset is reached.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the methane production system containing mixed gas production apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Control unit (1)
   Gas ratio acquisition unit (4)
   Hydrogen supply unit (5)
   Vacuum pump (6)
   Mixed gas production apparatus (50)
   Adsorber (A, B)

PN KR2165954-B1
TI Environmentally friendly concrete structure mortar repair material useful for repairing damaged concrete structures comprises e.g. Portland cement, silica sand, polymer, fly ash, water-soluble epoxy, calcium carbonate, and strength enhancer.
AU HO Y J
AE SEONJU ENG CO LTD (SEON-Non-standard)
GA 2020A3433T
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally friendly concrete structure mortar repair material comprises 100 pts. wt. Portland cement, 50-200 pts. wt. silica sand, 1-10 pts. wt. polymer, 8-15 pts. wt. fly ash, 1-5 pts. wt. water-soluble epoxy, 0.5-2 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 1-5 pts. wt. calcium sulfoaluminate cement (CSA)-based expanded cement, 20-50 pts. wt. fiber, 1-8 pts. wt. strength enhancer, 0.5-7 pts. wt. anti-neutralization agent and 10-30 pts. wt. carbon dioxide adsorbent capsule. The carbon dioxide adsorbent capsule is configured by coating surface of carbon dioxide adsorbent with polymer coating solution. The surface of carbon dioxide adsorbent capsule is formed in shape in which first and second particles having different melting points are embedded, depending on external temperature, in which grains are sequentially melted and separated from surface or melted to form perforation in surface of capsule, and adsorbent adsorbs carbon dioxide through formed perforations.
   USE - The environmentally friendly concrete structure mortar repair material is useful for repairing damaged concrete structures (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally friendly concrete structure mortar repair material has improved service life, prevents main cause of greenhouse gas by reducing carbon dioxide, recycles discarded resources, contributes to reduction of waste due to resource recycling, exhibits excellent workability, and improves flame retardancy of concrete structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for concrete structure repair and construction method comprising (a) removing the deteriorated portion of the cross section of the concrete structure; (b) applying a primer to the cross section of the concrete structure from which the deteriorated part is removed; and (c) applying the mortar repair material to a cross section of a concrete structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows reference diagram for explaining the technical concept of a concrete structure mortar repair material (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The neutralization inhibitor is made of strontium chromate to prevent corrosion of construction surface due to deterioration of the repair material and intrusion of impurities outside the concrete.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer coating solution contains polypropylene, polyethylene or nylon polymer. The first grain is made of polymer having melting point of 25-70 degrees C. The second grains are made of polymers having melting point of 7090 degrees C. The first grains and the second grains are melted at different temperatures to form perforations. The first grains are made of a polyethylene oxide-based polymer. The second grains are made of polypropylethylene-based polymer. The base fiber is provided in a heterogeneous form of bead fiber and short fiber. The bead fiber is smaller than the diameter of the carbon dioxide adsorbent capsule. The short fiber fills the space between the capsule and the bead fiber to improve the density and compressive strength of the mortar repair material.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Polymer emulsion with carbon dioxide reversible regulation dispersibility, involves weighing each raw material according to parts by weight proportion, and mixing compound a, compound b and crosslinking agent to obtain monomer mixture.
AU FENG Y
   MU M
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201999022K
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer emulsion with carbon dioxide (CO2) reversible regulation dispersibility of 2-aminoethyl propanoate derivative is represented by (I), (II), (III) or (IV).
   USE - Polymer emulsion with carbon dioxide reversible regulation dispersibility.
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer emulsion with carbon dioxide reversible regulation dispersibility has simple preparation method, mild conditions, and strong applicability, and has application value for industrial large-scale production and low-cost storage and transportation of polymer products or polymer dry powder based on intelligent demulsification, and intelligent re-emulsification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polymer emulsion with carbon dioxide (CO2) reversible regulation dispersibility is 2-aminoethyl propanoate derivative is represented by formulae (I), (II), (III) or (IV).
   R1=CH3, CH2CH3 or CH2(CH3)2; and
   R2=CH3, CH2CH3 or CH2(CH2)3.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a polymer emulsion with carbon dioxide (CO2) reversible regulation dispersibility of 2-aminoethyl propanoate derivative is represented by (I), (II), (III) or (IV), which involves:
   (A) weighing each raw material according to parts by weight proportion, firstly uniformly mix compound a of formula (V), compound b of formula (VI) and crosslinking agent to obtain a monomer mixture;
   (B) taking a water-soluble initiator, dissolving it in water, and stirring to obtain an initiator solution;
   (C) adding solvent d to the container, then puting compound c of formula (VII) into the container, and continuously pass in nitrogen (N2) to remove oxygen at room temperature, and react for 0.5-1 hour;
   (D) dropping monomer mixture and the initiator solution into the container to obtain a reaction solution mixed solution, and dripping is completed in 3-5 minutes; and
   (E) stirring the reactions at a temperature of 40-80 degrees C for 1-7 hours, and cooling the mixture to normal temperature to obtain a polymer nanosphere emulsion having a copolymerized microsphere having a CO2 response.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polymer emulsion is a random copolymerized polymer emulsion obtained by emulsion polymerization of 0.65-5.05 pts. wt. compound a, 0-4.04 pts. wt. compound b, 0.1.1-0.12 pts. wt. compound c, 0.07-0.15 pts. wt. initiator, 110-130 pts. wt. solvent d and 10-20 pts. wt. water. The compound b is preferably 0.05-4.04 pts. wt.. The compound a is propyl 2-methylprop-2-enoate derivative of formula (V). The compound b is methyl prop-2-enoate derivative of formula (VI). The compound c is dodecyl methanesulfonate derivative of formula (VII). The raw material of the polymer emulsion further comprises 0.03-0.2 pts. wt. crosslinking agent.R=-CH3, CH2CH3, CH3(CH2)3.. Preferred Components: The compound a is an oil-soluble vinyl monomer having a tertiary amine group, and is diethylaminoethyl methacrylate or diisopropylaminoethyl methacrylate. The compound b is an oil-soluble monomer and is one of styrene, methyl methacrylate, butyl acrylate, butyl methacrylate, methyl acrylate, ethyl methacrylate and ethyl acrylate. The compound c is an anionic emulsifier and is one of sodium dodecylsulfonate, sodium lauryl sulfate, and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. The solvent d is pure water. The cross-linking agent is an oil-soluble diene-based monomer or other oil-soluble cross-linking agent, and is one of ethylene glycol diacrylate, polyethylene glycol dimethacrylate, and divinylbenzene.

TI Cadmium-based metal organic framework used in            carbon dioxide adsorption and separation, obtained by            self-assembly synthesis of cadmium salt and planar            aromatic ligand containing tetracarboxylic acid            functional groups.
AU LIN J
   LIN X
   LAN J
   CAI Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA NORMAL (USCN-C)
GA 201922672Q
AB    NOVELTY - Cadmium-based metal organic framework is                obtained by self-assembly synthesis of cadmium salt                and planar aromatic ligand containing                tetracarboxylic acid functional groups. The planar                aromatic ligand containing tetracarboxylic acid                functional group is pyrene-1,3,6,8-tetracarboxylic                acid (I), where each cadmium ion is coordinated                with four carboxyl groups from different ligands                and four carboxyl groups of each ligand are                respectively connected with four cadmium                ions.
   USE - The organic framework used in carbon dioxide                adsorption and separation (claimed) and used in the                fields of carbon dioxide capturing and clean energy                storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The organic framework has large specific                surface area, high porosity and large conjugate                center, enhances the interaction force between                carbon dioxide through large conjugation center                during the carbon dioxide adsorption process,                exhibits high selective adsorption performance for                carbon dioxide, has large adsorption capacity, is                prepared by simple synthesis method and has high                thermal stability and high chemical                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cadmium-based metal organic framework is                obtained by self-assembly synthesis of cadmium salt                and planar aromatic ligand containing                tetracarboxylic acid functional groups. The planar                aromatic ligand containing tetracarboxylic acid                functional group is pyrene-1,3,6,8-tetracarboxylic                acid of formula (I), where each cadmium ion is                coordinated with four carboxyl groups from                different ligands and four carboxyl groups of each                ligand are respectively connected with four cadmium                ions. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the organic framework involving (a)                dissolving 1,3,6,8-tetrabromo-pyrene and copper                cyanide in quinoline, heating and stirring to                obtain 1,3,6,8-tetracyano-pyrene, (b) dissolving                1,3,6,8-tetracyano-pyrene in a mixed solvent of                sodium hydroxide solution and absolute ethyl                alcohol, heating and refluxing to obtain the                compound (I) and (c) dissolving the compound (I)                and cadmium salt in nitric acid, dimethylformamide                and water, sealing and heating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  1,3,6,8-tetrabromo-pyrene to copper cyanide is                  1:(30-35). The volume ratio of sodium hydroxide                  solution and absolute ethyl alcohol is                  1:(1.5-1.8). The molar ratio of the compound (I)                  to cadmium salt is 1:(0.9-1.1). The volume ratio                  of nitric acid, dimethylformamide and water is                  1:(65-70):(30-35). Preferred Method: The step (a)                  further involves dissolving                  1,3,6,8-tetrabromo-pyrene and copper cyanide in                  quinoline, cooling to room temperature, adding                  absolute ethanol, stirring at 80-90 degrees C for                  0.8-1.2 hours, filtering and washing the obtained                  product with nitric acid and anhydrous ethanol.                  The step (b) further involves heating and                  refluxing at 105-115 degrees C for 20-28 hours,                  cooling to room temperature, acidifying with                  hydrochloric acid until the pH is 1, filtering                  the obtained product and washing with water and                  anhydrous ethanol. The step (c) further involves                  sealing and heating at 95-105 degrees C for 66-78                  hours, cooling to room temperature and filtering                  the obtained product with dimethylformamide to                  obtain the product.

TI Method for facilitating multi-target optimization            of bubble column carbon capture process based on proxy            model, involves comparing optimal solution obtained to            find out optimal design variable corresponding to            optimal design variables.
AU JIANG H
   XING L
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2022C3172P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inputting a sampling point                into a physical simulation model (S2). A proxy                model is constructed and evaluated (S3). An                adaptive hybrid agent model is constructed                according to a simulation result of the sampling                point. A mixed agent model is trained by using the                simulation result. A sobol global sensitivity                analysis is performed (S4) to different design                variables after accuracy and stability of a mixed                proxy model. A non-supportive genetic algorithm is                used (S5) to the selected agent model for                multi-target optimization solving after the                analysis. An optimal solution is obtained (S6)                according to a solution set drawing Pareto front                edge graph obtained in the algorithm. The optimal                solution is compared (S7) to find out an optimal                design variable of the variable corresponding to                the variable of a maximum carbon dioxide capture                rate and a minimum process energy consumption. A                satisfy-target optimization scheme of a                pre-specified process requirement is output.
   USE - Method for facilitating multi-target                optimization of a bubble column carbon capture                process based on a proxy model.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables applying the mixed agent                model to optimization of carbon dioxide capture                process, which greatly reduces a calculation amount                of a multi-variable optimization and improves                accuracy of a simulation optimization result. The                method enables utilizing the proxy model based on a                statistical basis and solving a fast simplified                model such that a physical model is close and has                high precision.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for facilitating multi-target optimization                of a bubble column carbon capture process based on                a proxy model. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   S1Constructing multi physical field                simulation model
   S2Inputting sampling point into physical                simulation model
   S3Constructing and evaluating proxy                model
   S4Performing sobol global sensitivity                analysis to different design variables after                accuracy and stability of mixed proxy model
   S5Using non-supportive genetic algorithm to                selected agent model for multi target optimization                solving after sensitivity analysis
   S6Obtaining optimal solution according to                solution set drawing Pareto front edge graph                obtained in algorithm
   S7Comparing optimal solution to find out                optimal design variable of optimal design variable                corresponding to optimal design variable of maximum                carbon dioxide capture rate and minimum process                energy consumption

GA 2022B8006R
AB    NOVELTY - Monodisperse ultra-fine particulate matter                generating device based on double closed-loop                control comprises an electric spraying module and a                double-closed-loop control unit, where the                double-closed-loop control unit comprising an                electric spraying module, a current measuring                module, an image collecting module, a high voltage                module, a logic operation module. The electric                spraying module comprising a micro sample injector                (1), a spray cavity, a capillary spray needle, air                and carbon dioxide inlet, a high voltage electrode,                a soft X ray neutralizer. The micro sample injector                is connected with the capillary spray needle                through 1/32 pipe, the air inlet (4) and the carbon                dioxide inlet (5) pass through the spray chamber                and ensure the air tightness between the air inlet                and the spray chamber (2), the high voltage                electrode (6) is mounted on the surface of the                capillary jet needle and is not contact with the                capillary spray needle (3).
   USE - Monodisperse ultra-fine particulate matter                generating device based on double closed-loop                control.
   ADVANTAGE - The device maintains stable work of the                electric spraying module, to stably occur                monodisperse superfine particulate matter.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is inluded for a                monodisperse ultra-fine particulate matter stable                generating method based on double closed-loop                control system, which involves:
   using micro sample injector to transmit the                solution with conductivity into the spray chamber                through the capillary spray needle at a set rate,                when the high voltage electrode does not apply                voltage, blowing the solution by air and carbon                dioxide to form small droplets, the following gas                flow leaves the spray chamber after passing through                the soft X ray irradiation region, controlling the                voltage on the high voltage electrode by the high                voltage module, increasing the voltage on the high                voltage electrode to the set range, separating                solution from the capillary spray needle by                cone-shaped jet model, forming charged liquid drop                with uniform size, charging liquid drop with air                flow into soft X ray irradiation area with electric                quantity is neutralized, the liquid drop solution                volatilization in the flying process, residual                solute to form electric neutral mono-dispersed                particles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                device.
   1Micro sample injector
   2Spray chamber
   3Capillary spray needle
   4Air inlet
   5Carbon dioxide inlet
   6High voltage electrode
   7Soft X ray neutralizer
   8Metal protective shell

The logic                  operation module comprises a single chip.                  Preferred Conditions: The top end of the                  capillary spray needle and the top end liquid                  drop shape are shot by the CCD camera-microscope                  lens, the image is transmitted to the FPGA in the                  form of digital signal to judge whether to form a                  conical jet, if it is a conical jet, FPGA                  calculates the vertex angle of the conical jet                  and then transmits to the logic operation                  module.

TI Preparing ternary hydrotalcite material in aspect            of simulating the performance of carbon dioxide            producing methanol under simulated sunshine, involves            dissolving cobalt chloride hexahydrate, aluminum            chloride hexahydrate and indium trichloride            tetrahydrate in ultrapure water to obtain solution            A.
AU GUO J
   YIN X
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 202255200K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ternary hydrotalcite material                involves dissolving 0.7138g cobalt chloride                hexahydrate, 0.0015 mole aluminum chloride                hexahydrate and indium trichloride tetrahydrate in                40 mL ultra-pure water to obtain solution A,                weighing and 0.3240g sodium hdyroxide and 0.3180g                sodium carbonate and dissolving in 40 mL ultra-pure                water to obtain solution B. The solution A and B at                the same time slowly dropping 20-20 in ultra-pure                water, with vigorous stirring to stable pH of 8.5                &#177; 0.5 range, after titrating completely,                the powder purple slurry is transferred to 175mL                poly-tetra-fluorine hydrothermal kettle at                110&#8451; for 24 hours after cooling to room                temperature, the obtained solid product with water                and absolute ethyl alcohol centrifugally washing                for more than three times, turning to                60&#8451;, and drying for 12 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing ternary hydrotalcite                material used in the aspect of simulating the                performance of carbon dioxide producing methanol                under the simulated sunshine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has proper forbidden band width,                which is good for photo-generated electron                transfer, effectively promotes the separation of                the electron hole, inhibits the recombination of                photo-generated carrier, and improves the                photo-catalytic reduction carbon dioxide                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ternary hydrotalcite material of                formula CoAlInx-LDH involves dissolving 0.7138g                cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 0.0015 mole aluminum                chloride hexahydrate and indium trichloride                tetrahydrate in 40 mL ultrapure water to obtain                solution A, weighing and 0.3240g sodium hydroxide                and 0.3180g sodium carbonate and dissolving in 40                mL ultrapure water to obtain solution B. AB    NOVELTY - Iron-containing spinel catalyst derived from                hydrotalcite is prepared by calcining and                carbonizing a catalyst precursor having a                hydrotalcite structure. The catalyst precursor is                mainly obtained by co-precipitation or hydrothermal                method including metal ion solution and alkali                solution with FeM, where, M is selected from M1,                M2, M3, the M1 includes copper ion (Cu2+), iron ion                (Fe3+), iron ion (Fe2+), cobalt ion (Co2+), and                nickel ion (Ni2+), theM2 is a divalent metal ion                except copper ion, iron ion (Fe2+), cobalt ion, and                nickel ion, the M3 is a trivalent or tetravalent                metal ion except iron ion (Fe3+), the molar ratio                of iron/M1 is: 1/0.05-1/10, the molar ratio of                iron/M2 is: 1/0.05-1/10, the molar ratio of iron/M3                is: 1/0.05-1/10, and the alkali solution is a mixed                solution of sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide                and carbonate.
   USE - Iron-containing spinel catalyst derived from                hydrotalcite used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide                to produce liquid fuel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The iron-containing spinel catalyst derived                from hydrotalcite has large specific surface area,                uniform distribution of active components,                difficult agglomeration and sintering, and has high                reactivity and c5+ hydrocarbon selectivity for                carbon dioxide hydrogenation to liquid fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing an iron-containing spinel catalyst                derived from hydrotalcite, which involves:                (a)preparing metal salt solution and alkali                solution of FeM in proportion;(b)subjecting the                prepared metal salt solution and alkali solution of                FeM to co-precipitation reaction at 10-80degrees                Celsius and a pH value of 4-13 after                precipitation;(c)curing at 30-80degrees Celsius for                3-24 hours at a constant temperature, or moving the                precipitated solution into a                polytetrafluoroethylene liner and react at                30-180degrees Celsius for 3-24 hours;(d)washing                until the pH value of the filtrate is neutral to                obtain sediment filter cake;(e)drying the                precipitate filter cake;(f)calcining the dried                precipitate filter cake at 500-900degrees Celsius                for 1-6 hours under an air atmosphere to obtain a                spinel catalyst;(g)carbonizing the obtained spinel                catalyst at 200-500degrees Celsius in a                carbon-containing atmosphere for 2-5 hours to                obtain an iron-containing spinel catalyst derived                from hydrotalcite.

AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)

   USE - Method for preparing InOOH/H-ATP photo-catalytic composite material used for preparing methanol by photocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing InOOH/H-ATP photo-catalytic composite material involves taking the purified attapulgite to acidify, acidifying, filtering, washing, drying to obtain the modified attapulgite, dissolving indium nitrate in a certain proportion of distilled water and N, N-dimethyl formamide mixed solution, ultrasonically dissolving, adding attapulgite modified, fully stirring to uniformly to obtain the mixed solution, where the volume ratio of the distilled water and the DMF is 1: 10 to 1: 30, the mass ratio of the indium nitrate and the modified attapulgite is 0.4: 1 to 1: 1, transferring the mixed solution into the reaction kettle, putting in the microwave hydrothermal reactor, reacting for 1-3h at 140-180degrees Celsius, ending the reaction and cooling, centrifuging and collecting the product, washing and removing impurity, finally drying in vacuum, putting the product dried in a tubular furnace, calcining at 250-450degrees Celsius, under nitrogen atmosphere, collecting the sample and grinding fully, obtaining InOOH/H-ATP photo-catalytic composite material.

   ZHANG Y
   WANG S
   GAO J
   SUN S
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202180411D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rice husk-derived functional carbon                based on novel ammonia method for carbon capture                and nano-silicon dioxide synthesis method comprises                (i) pickling rice husk raw material and then                pyrolyzing to obtain rice husk-based biochar, using                potassium hydroxide solution to grade rice                husk-based biochar to dissolve silicon, (ii) using                the dissolved silicon biochar prepared in the step                (i) as adsorbent and cross-linked with                ammonia-ethanol chemical absorbent to capture                carbon, obtaining intermediate product biochar,                ammonium bicarbonate crystal co-precipitation, and                performing the solid-liquid separation to obtain                the biochar, (iii) reacting the potassium silicate                solution prepared in step (i) with the intermediate                product ammonium bicarbonate of step (ii) to                prepare nano-silicon dioxide, and obtaining                potassium bicarbonate, and ammonia by-products, and                (iv) heating the potassium bicarbonate obtained in                step (iii) at medium and low temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rice                husk-derived functional carbon based on novel                ammonia method for carbon capture and nano-silicon                dioxide synthesis method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the rate of carbon                dioxide absorption; inhibits the escape of ammonia;                reduces system energy consumption; and provides the                polygeneration technology route of rice                husk-derived nanoporous carbon functional motif                sequence structure process synergistic ammonia                method to capture carbon nano-silica to synthesize                high-purity carbon dioxide separation and                preparation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing rice husk-derived functional carbon                based on novel ammonia method for carbon capture                and nano-silicon dioxide synthesis method comprises                (i) pickling rice husk raw material and then                pyrolyzing to obtain rice husk-based biochar, using                potassium hydroxide solution to grade rice                husk-based biochar to dissolve silicon to obtain                high-quality graded pore dissolved silicon biochar                and potassium silicate, (ii) using the dissolved                silicon biochar prepared in the step (i) as                adsorbent and cross-linked with ammonia-ethanol                chemical absorbent to capture carbon, obtaining                intermediate product biochar, ammonium bicarbonate                crystal co-precipitation, and after the second                water dissolution, performing the solid-liquid                separation to obtain the biochar, (iii) reacting                the potassium silicate solution prepared in step                (i) with the intermediate product ammonium                bicarbonate of step (ii) to prepare nano-silicon                dioxide, and obtaining potassium bicarbonate, and                ammonia by-products, and (iv) heating the potassium                bicarbonate obtained in step (iii) at medium and                low temperature to realize separation and                preparation of high-purity carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  graded silicon dissolving includes silicon                  dissolving pretreatment and activation pore                  formation, in the pretreatment process of                  dissolving silicon, the solid-liquid mass ratio                  of rice husk-based biochar and potassium                  hydroxide solution is 1:10, the concentration of                  potassium hydroxide solution is 5 wt.%, and the                  temperature of graded dissolving silicon is 20                  degrees C or 100 degrees C, during the activation                  process, mixing and grinding the rice husk-based                  biochar and potassium hydroxide powder pretreated                  by dissolving silicon in a mass ratio of 1:4,                  carrying out the activation in a muffle furnace                  quartz reactor at 900 degrees C, 2 l/min                  dinitrogen atmosphere, the heating rate is 20                  degrees C/minute, and the heat preservation time                  is 30 minutes. In the step (iii), the reaction                  temperature of potassium silicate and ammonium                  bicarbonate is 70 degrees C, and the magnetic                  stirrer stirs the reaction to complete the aging                  for 2 hours. In the step (iii), the obtained                  ammonia by-product is recovered and used in the                  novel ammonia method for carbon capture in step                  (ii).

TI Oral infantile paracetamol yellow-sensitive            granular composition comprises e.g. paracetamol,            chlorpheniramine maleate, artificial bezoar,            hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, food coloring,            sweetener, ethanol solution, sucrose and water.
AU GU Y
   JIANG L
   QIAN L
   GAO J
   DONG J
AE SHANGHAI SINE WANXIANG PHARM CO LTD (SHPH-C)
   SHANGHAI SINE YANAN PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021785066
AB    NOVELTY - Oral infantile paracetamol yellow-sensitive                granular composition comprises (i) raw material                coating comprising 110-140 pts. wt. paracetamol,                0.1-0.8 pts. wt. chlorpheniramine maleate, and 3-10                pts. wt. artificial bezoar, coating liquid                comprising 10-20 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl                methylcellulose (HPMC), 0.03-0.15 pts. wt. food                coloring, 28 pts. wt. sweetener, and 280-320 pts.                wt. ethanol solution, (ii) high-pressure carbon                dioxide aerated sucrose (popping candy) comprising                1800-1900 pts. wt. sucrose, 0.05-0.12 pts. wt. food                coloring, and 90-120 pts. wt. purified water, and                (iii) granule mixture comprising 125-180 pts. wt.                coating material (calculated based on amount of                paracetamol), 1800-1900 pts. wt. popping candy and                3-10 pts. wt. food flavor.
   USE - Used as oral infantile paracetamol                yellow-sensitive granular composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts powder coating technology                to cover main bitter taste, and incorporates                high-pressure carbon dioxide aerated sucrose;                avoids children's taste discomfort, which makes                children can accept medicine more. The granules is                directly taken orally without warm water, which is                convenient to carry and take.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing oral infantile paracetamol                yellow-sensitive granular composition, comprising                (i) passing paracetamol, chlorpheniramine maleate,                and artificial bezoar through 80-mesh sieve,                placing it in boiling coater, and start heating,                mixing HPMC, food coloring, sweetener and ethanol                solution to obtain coating liquid, start spraying                coating liquid when the temperature of material in                boiling coating machine reaches 30-40 degrees C,                and controlling temperature of material to less                than or equal to 40 degrees C during the entire                coating process until all spraying is completed,                after coating, passing coated material through a                40-mesh sieve to obtain coated granules, (ii)                placing sucrose, food color and purified water in                pressure vessel and heating to 150 degrees C,                firstly evacuating pressure vessel to vacuum and                maintaining for 5-10 minutes, injecting carbon                dioxide to 4-6 MPa, maintaining for 10-20 minutes,                and cooling to room temperature, opening air                release valve to normal pressure to obtain solid                candy, and granulating through 16 meshes, and (iii)                adding coated raw materials into flavor and mixing,                adding whole sugar cubes and mixing, and packing                into bags.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The oral infantile                  paracetamol yellow-sensitive granular composition                  comprises (i) raw material coating comprising 125                  pts. wt. paracetamol, 0.5 pts. wt.                  chlorpheniramine maleate, and 5 pts. wt.                  artificial bezoar, coating liquid comprising 15                  pts. wt. hydroxypropyl methylcellulose (HPMC),                  0.08 pts. wt. food coloring, 4 pts. wt.                  sweetener, and 300 pts. wt. ethanol solution,                  (ii) high-pressure carbon dioxide aerated sucrose                  (popping candy) comprising 1850 pts. wt. sucrose,                  0.1 pts. wt. food coloring, and 100 pts. wt.                  purified water, and (iii) granule mixture                  comprising 150 pts. wt. coating material                  (calculated based on amount of paracetamol), 1850                  pts. wt. popping candy and 6 pts. wt. food                  flavor. Preferred Components: The particle size                  of paracetamol, chlorpheniramine maleate and                  artificial bezoar is 80-200 mesh. The                  concentration of ethanol solution is 40-60 wt.%.                  The food coloring includes lemon yellow and/or                  amaranth. The sweetener includes aspartame and/or                  stevioside. The food flavor includes sweet orange                  oil and/or peppermint oil. Preferred Method: In                  the step (ii), the pressure vessel is evacuated                  to vacuum and maintained for 6-8 minutes, then                  carbon dioxide is injected to 5.2 MPa, maintained                  for 15-16 minutes, and then cooled to normal                  temperature.

TI Preparing barium zirconium sulfide used in carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst, involves adding zirconium chloride to N,N-dimethylformamide, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid, performing ultrasonication, and reacting.
AU MA D
   WU X
   XU Q
   LI Z
   QI C
AE UNIV SHAOXING (UNSX-C)
   UNIV WENZHOU (UNWZ-C)
GA 2021721639
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing barium zirconium sulfide (BaZrS3) nanocrystals involves synthesizing zirconium (Zr)-metal organic framework (MOF) nanoparticles by first adding zirconium chloride to N,N-dimethylformamide, and then adding concentrated hydrochloric acid; performing ultrasonicating of mixed solution finally for 5 minutes to fully dissolve; adding terephthalic acid to mixed solution; ultrasonicating mixed solution for 20 minutes to fully dissolve until solution becomes colorless and transparent solution; adding mixed solution to 50 mL centrifuge tube, sealing centrifuge tube and placing in dry box, and reacting at constant temperature at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling to room temperature naturally, washing obtained product with N,N-dimethylformamide for multiple times, then washing multiple times with acetone, and drying in vacuum drying oven for 12 hours to obtain Zr-MOF nanoparticles; mixing Zr-MOF nanoparticles with barium sulfide (BaS).
   USE - Method for preparing BaZrS3 used in carbon dioxide (CO2) reduction photocatalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare BaZrS3, which are small and effectively remove carbon dioxide for reduced products, such as carbon monoxide and methane with good practical value and application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing barium zirconium sulfide (BaZrS3) nanocrystals involves synthesizing zirconium (Zr)-metal organic framework (MOF) nanoparticles by first adding zirconium chloride to N,N-dimethylformamide, and then adding concentrated hydrochloric acid; performing ultrasonicating of mixed solution finally for 5 minutes to fully dissolve; adding terephthalic acid to mixed solution; ultrasonicating mixed solution for 20 minutes to fully dissolve until solution becomes colorless and transparent solution; adding mixed solution to 50 mL centrifuge tube, sealing centrifuge tube and placing in dry box, and reacting at constant temperature at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling to room temperature naturally, washing obtained product with N,N-dimethylformamide for multiple times, then washing multiple times with acetone, and drying in vacuum drying oven for 12 hours to obtain Zr-MOF nanoparticles; mixing Zr-MOF nanoparticles with barium sulfide (BaS), placing in mortar and grinding thoroughly to make uniformly mixed; placing obtained powder by grinding in magnetic boat, placing magnetic boat in quartz tube, and then placing quartz tube in tube furnace, and allowing argon gas flow for 30 minutes to remove air in quartz tube, then using bubbler to bring carbon disulfide (CS2) into quartz tube, raising temperature to 1000 degrees C, and after reacting for 4 hours, turning off CS2 and lowering temperature to obtain resulting powder which is BaZrS3 nanocrystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing BaZrS3 nanocrystals involves placing 0.123 g zirconium chloride in 15 mL of N,N-dimethylformamide, and then adding 1 mL of 12 Molar concentrated hydrochloric acid; adding 0.125 g of terephthalic acid to mixed solution; mixing Zr-MOF nanoparticles and BaS at molar ratio of 1:1 and placing into mortar to fully grind. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of argon gas is 200 standard cubic centimeter per minute (sccm) when air is removed, flow rate of argon gas is 400 sccm when CS2 is blowing, and heating rate is 10 degrees C/minute.

PN JP6868923-B1; WO2022034703-A1; JP2022032907-A; JP2022032945-A
TI Preparing effervescent sake by performing            secondary fermentation of sake using carbon dioxide gas            in distribution container by mixing liquor, filling in            container, performing secondary fermentation and            extruding sake.
AU WATANABE D
   IBARAKI M
   HIROSHI W
AE CLIP CREATIVE GK (CLIP-Non-standard)
   CLIP CREATIVE GK (CLIP-Non-standard)
GA 2021522913
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing effervescent sake                involves (a) performing secondary fermentation of                sake using carbon dioxide gas in a distribution                container, (b) mixing to produce a mixed liquor in                which raw liquor and mash are mixed such that the                pressure in the second container reaches a                predetermined pressure higher than the atmospheric                pressure, and the fluid has the same as the                temperature in the third container, (c) sealing                fluid around the first container in the second                container, (d) filling the mixed liquor into the                distribution container, (e) filling the first                container with a pressure higher than the                predetermined pressure while stirring the mixed                liquor, (f) performing secondary fermentation in                which the mixed liquor is secondarily fermented in                the distribution container and sealed in the first                container, and (g) extruding the sake in the first                container by the pressure in the second                container.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                effervescent sake.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents deterioration of refined                sake quality, and does not impair original taste                and aroma of refined sake due to oxidation,                irradiation of sun and temperature change.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing effervescent sake                involves (a) performing secondary fermentation of                sake using carbon dioxide gas in a distribution                container comprising first container of soft                packaging material, a second container that                accommodates the first container and is configured                to seal a predetermined fluid around the first                container and a third container in which the second                container is housed and the inside is maintained at                a predetermined low temperature, (b) mixing to                produce a mixed liquor in which raw liquor and mash                are mixed such that the pressure in the second                container reaches a predetermined pressure higher                than the atmospheric pressure, and the fluid has                the same as the temperature in the third container,                (c) sealing fluid around the first container in the                second container, (d) filling the mixed liquor into                the distribution container, (e) filling the first                container with a pressure higher than the                predetermined pressure while stirring the mixed                liquor, (f) performing secondary fermentation in                which the mixed liquor is secondarily fermented in                the distribution container and sealed in the first                container, and (g) extruding the sake in the first                container by the pressure in the second                container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the filling step, the                  mixed liquor is pumped to the first container and                  sealed around the first container in the second                  container based on the amount of the mixed liquor                  filled in the first container. The fluid                  discharged is discharged to the outside of the                  second container. In the sealing step, the fluid                  outside the third container is cooled to a same                  temperature with respect to temperature inside                  the third container, and the fluid is sealed                  around the first container in the second                  container.

   WANG Z
   FAN X
   WANG J

AE POWERCHINA STECOL CORP (CNPW-C)
GA 2021174651
AB    NOVELTY - A streamlined steel arch with special-shaped section box steel structure manufacturing method comprises staggering adjacent welds, staggering segmented butt joints bottom plates, dividing segments into 13 sections, utilizing computer three-dimensional lofting technology to accurately loft the components of the steel box girder, optimizing pre-camber in the blanking programming, carrying out unit parts production, performing parts inspection, carrying out manhole stiffening ring assembly welding, performing single assembly correction, carrying out unit inspection and marking, performing steel arch assembly, carrying out steel arch linear control, manufacturing joint line type between the steel arch and the arch beam, performing steel arch tire frame manufacturing, performing steel arch matching inspection, carrying out steel arch trial installation, performing steel arch welding, carrying out anti-corrosion coating, packing, and transporting.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A streamlined steel arch with special-shaped section box steel structure manufacturing method comprises staggering adjacent welds with a spacing greater than 200 mm, staggering segmented butt joints bottom plates by at least 200 mm, dividing segments into 13 sections as sections A to section M; utilizing computer three-dimensional lofting technology to accurately loft the components of the steel box girder, optimizing pre-camber in the blanking programming, carrying out unit parts production, performing parts inspection, carrying out manhole stiffening ring assembly welding, performing single assembly correction, carrying out unit inspection and marking, performing steel arch assembly, carrying out steel arch linear control, manufacturing joint line type between the steel arch and the arch beam, performing steel arch tire frame manufacturing, performing steel arch matching inspection, carrying out steel arch trial installation, performing steel arch welding using submerged arc automatic welding and carbon dioxide gas shielded welding for the production of partition unit parts and the butt welds of the top and bottom webs, performing carbon dioxide gas shielded welding for the corner welds and the filet welds of the transverse diaphragm, performing carbon dioxide shielded welding and manual arc welding are used for tack welding and auxiliary facility welding, carrying out anti-corrosion coating, packing, and transporting.

PT P
PN CN112233896-A
TI Pre-sintering high permeability magnetic cores involves taking iron oxide, manganese oxide, and zinc oxide, fully mixing raw materials to obtain the primary raw material, adding ten polyglycerol monostearate to magnetic core raw materials.
AU QIAN L
   QIAN M
   LIN H
   SUN H
   WU H
AE HAINING XINGHUO ELECTRONICS CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 202110264J
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-sintering high permeability magnetic cores involves taking iron oxide, manganese oxide, and zinc oxide in the ratio of 5:4:4, and fully mixing raw materials to obtain the primary raw material, adding 2-3 pts. wt. ten polyglycerol monostearate to every 100 pts. wt. magnetic core raw materials to obtain secondary raw materials. The secondary raw materials are processed in a high-speed ball mill for 15-20 minutes and then mixed after treatment. The raw materials are dried and crushed and then passed through a 200-400 mesh sieve, and filtered to obtain three raw materials. The three raw materials are heated to 890-930 degrees C under 70% carbon dioxide and 20% hydrogen and 10% carbon monoxide atmosphere, kept for 4-5 hours, lowered to 610-660 degrees C in 75% nitrogen and 25% argon atmosphere, incubated at 660 degrees C for 2-3 hours.
   USE - Method for pre-sintering high permeability magnetic cores.
   ADVANTAGE - The pre-sintering treatment technique of high magnetic permeability magnetic core has high stability and the raw material mixing mold has high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pre-sintering high permeability magnetic cores involves taking iron oxide, manganese oxide, and zinc oxide in the ratio of 5:4:4, and fully mixing raw materials to obtain the primary raw material, adding 2-3 pts. wt. ten polyglycerol monostearate to every 100 pts. wt. magnetic core raw materials to obtain secondary raw materials. The secondary raw materials are processed in a high-speed ball mill for 15-20 minutes and then mixed after treatment. The raw materials are dried and crushed and then passed through a 200-400 mesh sieve, and filtered to obtain three raw materials. The three raw materials are heated to 890-930 degrees C under 70% carbon dioxide and 20% hydrogen and 10% carbon monoxide atmosphere, kept for 4-5 hours, lowered to 610-660 degrees C in 75% nitrogen and 25% argon atmosphere, incubated at 660 degrees C for 2-3 hours, then increased the temperature to 900-950 degrees C under 60% hydrogen and 40% carbon monoxide atmosphere, kept for 5-6 hours, then decreased the temperature to 590-630 degrees C under 50% argon and 50% carbon dioxide atmosphere C, heat preserved for 2-3 hours to obtain product.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V02 (Inductors and Transformers); X12 (Power Distribution/Components/Converters)

TI Dry cosmetic and/or skin care composition,            comprises mixture of natural ground calcium carbonate            or precipitated calcium carbonate, and calcium            carbonate surface-reacted with carbon dioxide and            hydronium ion donor.
AU SHARMA L
   BUDDE T
   LANDER S
   BADER T
   SHARMALLITE
   LANDERSSTEPHAN
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA DEV AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2021051214
AB    NOVELTY - Dry cosmetic and/or skin care composition                comprises a mixture of a first component being a                natural ground calcium carbonate or a precipitated                calcium carbonate, and a second component being a                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction                product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide                and at least one hydronium ion donor, the carbon                dioxide is formed in situ by at least one hydronium                ion donor treatment and/or is supplied from an                external source.
   USE - The dry cosmetic and/or skin care composition                is used as a powder, preferably a baby powder                and/or a body powder, which is used for absorbing                fluids, for decreasing skin friction, for modifying                the skin feel, and/or for modifying the skin                appearance (all claimed), where the powder is used                as a cosmetic powder including eyeshadow, powder                makeup, lip powder, face powder, body powder or                blusher, and as a skin care powder including                shaving powder, body powder, baby powder, foot                powder and a deodorant powder. The dry cosmetic                and/or skin care composition is used for bleaching                and/or whitening of the skin.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry cosmetic and/or skin care composition                effectively decreases the friction of the skin with                an object being in contact with than known                products, is better or easier applicable to the                skin than known products, is free of components                being considered as health concern such as talc or                talc-containing material, has excellent skin                adherence, is well spreadable and has little                resistance during application onto the skin, leaves                the skin with a soft and dry touch, and does not                contain a silicate- and/or aluminate-containing                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a process for preparing the dry cosmetic                and/or skin care composition, comprising (a)                providing the first component, (b) providing the                second component, and (c) mixing the first                component with the second component;
   (2) use of the dry composition as a cosmetic                and/or skin care composition such as a cosmetic                and/or skin care powder, preferably a baby powder                and/or a body powder; and
   (3) a use of the mixture comprising the first                component and the second component as a replacement                for talc or a talc-containing material in a dry                cosmetic and/or skin care composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 1-99 wt.%, preferably 70-90 wt.% first                  component and 1-99 wt.%, preferably 10-30 wt.%                  second component, based on the total weight of                  the first component and the second component.                  Preferred Components: The first component is a                  natural ground calcium carbonate chosen from                  marble, chalk, limestone, and their mixtures, or                  the first component is a precipitated calcium                  carbonate chosen from precipitated calcium                  carbonates having an aragonitic, vateritic or                  calcitic crystal form, and their mixtures. The                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction                  product of natural ground calcium carbonate                  chosen from marble, chalk, limestone, and their                  mixtures, with carbon dioxide and at least one                  hydronium ion donor, or the surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate is a reaction product of                  precipitated calcium carbonate chosen from                  precipitated calcium carbonates having an                  aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic crystal form,                  and their mixtures, with carbon dioxide and at                  least one hydronium ion donor. The hydronium ion                  donor is chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric                  acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, citric                  acid, oxalic acid, an acidic salt, acetic acid,                  formic acid, and their mixtures, preferably                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  phosphoric acid, oxalic acid or phosphoric acid,                  being at least partially neutralized by a cation                  chosen from lithium, sodium and/or potassium ion,                  hydrogenphosphate, being at least partially                  neutralized by a cation chosen from lithium ion,                  sodium ion, potassium ion, magnesium ion, and/or                  calcium ion, and their mixtures. The hydronium                  ion donor is chosen from hydrochloric acid,                  sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid                  (preferred), oxalic acid, and their mixtures. The                  first component has a volume median particle size                  of 0.1-50 pm, preferably 0.8-8 pm, and/or the                  first component has a specific surface area of                  0.5-30 m2/g, preferably 2-15 m2/g, measured using                  nitrogen and the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET)                  method. The second component has a volume median                  particle size of 0.5-50 pm, preferably 3-10 pm,                  and/or the second component has a specific                  surface area of 15-200 m2/g, measured using                  nitrogen and the BET method. The dry cosmetic                  and/or skin care composition is free of talc or a                  talc-containing material. The dry cosmetic and/or                  skin care composition comprises one or more                  further components chosen from a fragrance, an                  aroma, an antibacterial and/or an antimicrobial                  agent, a fatty acid or their salt, a fatty                  alcohol, a vegetable or a synthetic oil, a                  polymeric carbohydrate, a mineral additive, a                  pigment, a salt, and their mixtures. Preferred                  Method: The first component of step (a) and the                  second component of step (b) is provided in dry                  form and the mixing step (c) is a dry blending                  step, or the first component of step (a) and/or                  the second component of step (b) is provided in                  form of an aqueous suspension, preferably in form                  of a slurry, and the process further comprises a                  step (d) of drying the mixture obtained in step                  (c), where the step (d) is a spray drying step                  (preferred) or a superheated steam drying                  step.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN CN111422889-A
TI Preparing highly active lithium carbonate comprises e.g. preparing lithium salt to lithium solution, obtaining alkaline solution, preparing ammonium bicarbonate, convecting lithium solution, alkali solution and ammonium bicarbonate solution.
AU JIANG Y
AE JIANG Y (JIAN-Individual)
GA 202069429L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly active lithium carbonate comprises e.g. preparing lithium salt into a lithium solution, obtaining alkaline solution, preparing ammonium bicarbonate solution, convecting the lithium solution, alkali solution, and ammonium bicarbonate solution into the reactor, and adding the bottom liquid to the reactor, maintaining the pH of the solution during the convective addition process, when the reaction is complete, after filtering, washing, and then drying, performing microwave drying, during the drying process, introducing carbon dioxide gas for activation, and stopping, drying, sieving, iron removal, and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing highly active lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can purifies lithium salt solution, ammonium bicarbonate and alkali solution added to the reactor at a flow rate to react, can control the reaction temperature, pH, CO32-concentration, ensures high activity, small primary particle size, has high battery grade lithium carbonate with high sphericity, during the microwave drying process, uses carbon dioxide to activate the lithium carbonate surface, thus further improves the activity of lithium carbonate, after sieving and removing iron, obtains high-activity lithium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing highly active lithium carbonate comprises (i) preparing lithium salt into a lithium solution with a lithium concentration of 10-22 g/l, obtaining alkaline solution, and the concentration of the alkaline solution is 7-9 mol/l, preparing ammonium bicarbonate solution, the concentration of ammonium bicarbonate is 180-200 g/l; (ii) convecting the lithium solution, alkali solution, and ammonium bicarbonate solution into the reactor, and adding the bottom liquid to the reactor, maintaining the pH of the solution during the convective addition process of 10.5-11.5, the time of convective addition is 30-60 minutes, continue to react for 1-2 hours after the addition, the temperature of the entire convective addition process and the reaction process is 40-55 degrees C; and (iii) when the reaction is complete, after filtering, washing until the conductivity of the washing water is less than or equal to 100 mu S/cm, and then drying, performing microwave drying, during the drying process, introducing carbon dioxide gas for activation, and stopping the drying until the free water mass fraction of the material is less than or equal to 1%, after drying, sieving, iron removal, and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium salt is at least one of lithium sulfate, lithium nitrate, lithium chloride or lithium acetate. In the step (i), the alkali solution is at least one of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or ammonia. In the step (ii), the volume of the base solution added is 0.1-0.15 times the total volume of the added lithium solution, alkali solution and ammonium bicarbonate solution. Preferred Method: In the method, adjusting the pH of the bottom solution to 11-11.5, the bottom solution is an alkaline solution, and the temperature of the bottom solution is 40-65 degrees C. In the step (ii), in the countercurrent addition, the carbonate concentration of the supernatant in the reaction slurry is maintained at 0.5-1.5 g/l. In the step (iii), deionized water is used for washing at 40-55 degrees C, and the conductivity of the deionized water is less than or equal to 20 mu S/cm. In the step (iii), the temperature of the microwave drying is 85-110 degrees C, and the carbon dioxide gas is introduced so that the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the gas in the microwave dryer is greater than 70%. In the step (iii), the 60-mesh sieve is used for screening, and an electromagnetic separator is used for iron removal, and the magnetic induction intensity of the electromagnetic separator is 15000-20000 Gauss.

TI Induced pluripotent stem cell-complete neural cell induction culture liquid used for preparing e.g. glutamatergic neurons, comprises basic culture solution of cells, cell culture additives, nerve growth factor and inhibitors.
AU ZHANG L
   HE C
AE GUANGZHOU RUIZHEN REGENERATIVE MEDICINE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU RUIZHEN REGENERATIVE MEDICINE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020141188
AB    NOVELTY - Induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC)-complete neural cell induction culture liquid comprises basic culture solution of cells, cell culture additives, nerve growth factor and inhibitors.
   USE - The iPSC complete neural cell induction culture liquid is useful for culturing neural cell, which is used for preparing cerebral cortical nerve stem cell and glutamatergic neurons (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The culture liquid prepares high-quality cerebral cortical nerve stem cell and glutamatergic neurons in a simple and cost-effective manner without causing risk of contamination, and introduction of exogenous substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) iPSC neural cell culture liquid comprising basic culture solution of cells, cell culture additives, and neurotrophic factor; and
   (2) method for preparing cerebral cortical nerve stem cell and glutamatergic neurons involves (a) culturing human iPSC, and passaging the cells for every 7 days using ReLeSR (1:6), (b) preparing iPSCs for neural induction by preparing iPSCs single cells, adding single cell suspension to the wells of polymer matrix pretreated culture plate to make cells evenly distributed, and culturing at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide under humid environment, (c) inducing neural stem cells by pre-warming iPSC complete nerve induction medium as described above at room temperature, aspirating old pre-culture medium on the first day, when the iPSCs cell density is 70%, adding pre-warmed iPSCs complete neural induction medium to each well in the culture plate, returning to 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide in a humid environment, from second to sixth day, using a sterile pipette to aspirate old complete nerve induction culture solution, adding new preheated complete nerve induction culture solution, adding it back in the incubator for cultivation, and taking certain amount of cells On the seventh day for immunofluorescence staining to detect expression of NPC markers (paired box protein (Pax)-6, orthodenticle homeobox (Otx)-2, nestin or their combinations) to determine NPC concentration, and (d) performing neuron differentiation by adding nerve cell culture solution to the well of the polymer plate pretreated by the polymer matrix, adding obtained NPC single cell suspension to the well of the culture plate, evenly distributing the cells, culturing the cells at 37 degrees C in a humid environment of 5% carbon dioxide, and finally identifying the neuron cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The cell basal culture medium is Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM)/F12 and/or NeuroBasal (RTM: Basal medium for long-term maintenance and maturation of pure pre-natal and embryonic neuronal cell populations). The cell culture additive is one of B27, non-essential amino acids (NEAA), Glutamax (RTM: Supplement that does not spontaneously break down to form ammonia) or nitrogen (N2) or their combinations, where the concentration of B27 is 0.5-2X, the concentration of NEAA is 0.5-2X, the concentration of Glutamax is 0.5-2X, and the concentration of N2 is 0.5-2X, where "X" is concentration multiple. The nerve growth factor is one of 40-150 ng/ml medium-term factor, 40-120 ng/ml pleiotropic growth factor, 20-100 ng/ml insulin-like growth factor-1 or their combinations. The inhibitor is one of 2 nM to 5 mu M RepSox, 40 nM to 4 mu M SB431542, 20 nM to 4 mu M dorsomorphin, 100 nM to 80 mu M KY02111, 1-40 micrograms/ml dickkopf wingless-type MMTV integration site family (Wnt) signaling pathway inhibitor 1 (DKK1) or their combinations. The nerve cell culture solution further comprises epidermal growth factor at a concentration of 5-100 ng/ml.

TI System for generation of electricity by            incineration of solid waste, has collection chamber            that is provided for collecting hydrogen, and generator            that is attached to collection chamber to generate            electricity from combustion of hydrogen.
AU DHYLLON A
AE DHYLLON A (DHYL-Individual)
   SERENDIPITY TECHNOLOGIES LLC (SERE-Non-standard)
GA 2020088227
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fuel input that is provided                for delivering solid fuel into a first plasma                chamber. The first plasma chamber is designed to                contain a plasma and combust fuel. A first heating                portion is formed within a combustion chamber. A                thermoelectric generator (TEG) (401) is contained                within the combustion chamber for generating                electricity from heat. A second plasma chamber                contains a second heating portion. A vent is                attached to the second plasma chamber in                communication with a separating membrane adapted to                separate hydrogen from as mixture of hydrogen and                carbon dioxide. A collection chamber is provided                for collecting hydrogen gas. A generator is                attached to the collection chamber adapted to                generate electricity from the combustion of                hydrogen. A gas input vent is about an exterior                surface of the vent and magnets adapted to induce a                dipole in oxygen molecules. An electron-donation                element comprises titanium and/or platinum. A metal                coils are brass, and are coated with                zirconia.
   USE - System for generation of electricity by                incineration of solid waste such as food waste,                yard waste, container and product packaging and                other miscellaneous inorganic wastes from                residential, commercial, institutional and                industrial sources.
   ADVANTAGE - The complete destruction of waste is provided                without producing harmful byproducts, and thus                providing a method of cheap and effective energy                generation by the incineration of waste using a                plasma ignition system. The incineration process is                efficient, produces low carbon dioxide and sulphur                compounds in the flue gasses, and thus, resulting                in elimination of dioxin emissions. By adjusting                the magnetic fields of the inlet tubes, the system                is programmed to control the precise quantity of                oxygen atoms entering the plasmic box, and adjusted                based upon the type of waste entering the plasmic                box. The Oxygen molecules are negatively charged                and the waste molecules are positively charged and                their density is directly proportional to each                other, therefore resulting in true complete                combustion. The production of liquid carbon dioxide                eliminates the emission into the atmosphere                guaranteeing overall benefit for the planet and                fully respecting the Kyoto agreement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                plasma boxes connected together through heaters and                fans.
   TEG box (401)
   Fan (403)
   Heater (404)
   Flow of gasses (405,406)
