TI Manufacture of barium carbonate used in composition for e.g. electronic material, involves adding citric acid to e.g. barium hydroxide, reacting with carbon dioxide and/or carbonate compound, and adding polybasic carboxylic acid(s).
AU TSUJITA H
   NISHIDA H
   OGATA H
AE SAKAI CHEM IND CO LTD (SAKI-C)
   SAKAI KAGAKU KOGYO KK (SAKI-C)
   SAKAI CHEM IND CO LTD (SAKI-C)
   SAKAI CHEM IND CO (SAKI-C)
GA 2019904283
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of barium carbonate involves adding citric acid and/or its salts to at least one barium compound chosen from barium hydroxide, barium chloride, barium nitrate, barium acetate, and barium oxide, reacting with carbon dioxide and/or carbonate compound, and adding at least one polybasic carboxylic acid (I) or its acid anhydride or salt with respect to 100 mol% barium atoms in the barium compound. The total of addition amount (mol%) (A) of citric acid and/or its salts and addition amount (mol%) (B) of compound (I) is 6-16. The ratio of addition amounts (mol%) (A) and (B) is 0.01-7.
   USE - Manufacture of barium carbonate used in composition (claimed) for forming material such as laminated ceramic capacitor of electronic device. Can also be used for air-pole-material of solid oxide fuel cell, oxide superconductor material, optical glass, glaze, ferrite, pigment, dye, and fluorescent substance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically produces barium carbonate having small aspect ratio and excellent pulverization property, without using a large amount of crystal growth inhibitor and with little load to environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of barium carbonate involves adding citric acid and/or its salts to at least one barium compound chosen from barium hydroxide, barium chloride, barium nitrate, barium acetate, and barium oxide, reacting with carbon dioxide and/or carbonate compound, and adding at least one polybasic carboxylic acid of formula: HOOC-X-(COOH)n (I) or its acid anhydride or salt with respect to 100 mol% barium atoms in the barium compound. The total of addition amount (mol%) (A) of citric acid and/or its salts and addition amount (mol%) (B) of compound (I) is 6-16. The ratio of addition amounts (mol%) (A) and (B) is 0.01-7.
   X = divalent or trivalent linking group optionally comprising hydroxy as substituent, 1 or 2 carbon atoms or unsaturated hydrocarbyl containing 2 carbon atoms, or group derived from phenyl;and
   n = 1 or 2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for composition, which comprises barium carbonate having a BET method specific surface area of more than 45 m2/g and a particle aspect ratio of 1-1.5, the citric acid and/or its salt, and compound (I) or its acid anhydride or salts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The citric acid and/or its salt is added in an amount of 0.1-9 mol%. Compound (I) is preferably at least one chosen from tartaric acid, succinic acid, malic acid, maleic acid, malonic acid, and trimellitic acid. The barium carbonate composition has a ratio of total number of moles of citric acid and/or its salt, and compound (I) or its acid anhydride or salt to number of moles of barium atoms of 6-16 mol%. Preferred Process: Manufacture of barium carbonate alternately involves adding citric acid and/or its salt, and compound (I) or its acid anhydride or salt to the barium compound, and adding carbon dioxide and/or carbonate compound.

AU GUTIERREZ ORTEGA A E
   NOMEN RIBE R
   GONZALEZ OLMOS R
   MARTINEZ HUGUET O
AE GAS N2ITROGEN SL (GASN-Non-standard)
   GAS N2ITROGEN SL (GASN-Non-standard)
GA 201982409W
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering heavy gas component from multicomponent gas mixture involves using heavy gas component which is carbon dioxide and light gas component which is nitrogen, cycle carrying out in bed system, introducing multicomponent gas mixture at pressure from 100-600 kilopascal into bed system which is hollow structure enclosing interior volume which contains adsorbent and which has length which is higher than its diameter, adsorbent that retains heavy gas component of multicomponent gas mixture, while letting out light-component-enriched- stream from bed system, pressurizing bed system by introducing heavy-component-enriched- stream into bed system, until pressure which is from 1.1-3 times pressure, pressure is from 110-900 kilopascal and then stopping feeding, depressurizing bed system by letting out light-component-enriched- stream from bed system, until pressure which is from 0.5-1 times pressure, pressure is from 100-600 kilopascal.
   USE - Method for recovering heavy gas component from multicomponent gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The metrhod enables to recover heavy gas component from multicomponent gas mixture that has good yields and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering heavy gas component from multicomponent gas mixture involves using heavy gas component which is carbon dioxide and light gas component which is nitrogen, cycle carrying out in bed system, introducing multicomponent gas mixture at pressure from 100-600 kilopascal into bed system which is hollow structure enclosing interior volume which contains adsorbent and which has length which is higher than its diameter, adsorbent that retains heavy gas component of multicomponent gas mixture, while letting out light-component-enriched- stream from bed system, pressurizing bed system by introducing heavy-component-enriched- stream into bed system, until pressure which is from 1.1-3 times pressure, pressure is from 110-900 kilopascal and then stopping feeding, depressurizing bed system by letting out light-component-enriched- stream from bed system, until pressure which is from 0.5-1 times pressure, pressure is from 100-600 kilopascal and then stopping removal of light-component-enriched-stream, repeating until relative concentration of heavy gas, component in light-component-enriched-stream with respect to concentration of heavy gas component in heavy-component-enriched- stream is equal to or higher than 5 mol.%, desorbing heavy gas component from bed system to give heavy-component-enriched-stream containing heavy gas component and regenerating adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The multi component gas mixture comprises nitrogen in amount from 50-95 mol.%, and carbon dioxide in amount from 7-20 mol.%. The removal of heavy- component-enriched-stream (6A) is stopped when pressure in bed system is from 0.2-0.9 times pressure. The second portion of heavy-component-enriched-stream is sucked through outlet located along axial portion of bed system. Preferred Process: The method involves feeding multicomponent gas mixture, and removal of light-component-enriched-stream is stopped when relative concentration of heavy gas component in light-component-enriched-stream with respect to concentration of the heavy gas component in feed mixture is from 1-30 mol.%. The multicomponent gas mixture is introduced through inlet located at axial portion of bed system, light-component- enriched-stream is let out through outlet located at bottom of bed system. The method involves depressurizing bed system by letting out first portion of heavy-component-enriched-stream, applying vacuum to bed system and sucking second portion of heavy-component-enriched-stream, purging bed system by introducing light-component-enriched-stream into bed system and letting out light-component-enriched-stream. The introduction of light-component-enriched-stream, and removal of light-component-enriched- stream is stopped when relative concentration of heavy gas component in light-component-enriched-stream with respect to concentration of heavy gas component in feed mixture is equal to or lower than 80 mol.%. The light- component-enriched-stream is introduced through two inlets located at top and at bottom of bed system, and light-component-enriched-stream is let out through inlet located at axial portion of bed system. The method involves pressurizing adsorption bed by introducing light-component-enriched-stream until the pressure in the adsorption bed is from 0.2-1 times pressure and then stopping feeding. Preferred Components: The multicomponent gas mixture is flue gas. The adsorbent is selected from activated carbon, zeolite, aluminosilicates, silica gel, activated alumina, carbon molecular sieves (CMSs), and combinations.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109513909-A; CN109513909-B
TI Cooling cast aluminum product useful for preventing steam explosion comprises e.g. cooling well main body, passing carbon dioxide or nitrogen gas into lower chamber through the through hole, and forming air cushion layer in lower chamber.
AU SHEN Z
   CHEN H
   WANG D
   SHEN J
   JI H
   NIU Y
   CEN Z
   CHEN D
   ZHU G
AE NINGBO SPECIAL EQUIP INSPECTION RES INST (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201929551W
AB    NOVELTY - Cooling cast aluminum product comprises cooling the well main body, passing carbon dioxide or nitrogen gas having a pressure of 0.01-0.05 MPa into the lower chamber through the through hole, forming an air cushion layer in the lower chamber, allowing gas in the lower chamber to continuously passing into the upper chamber through each of the through holes, forming continuous overflow or bubble flow in the upper chamber, pumping the cooling water in the upper chamber, and passing it to the cooling passage of the casting mold for cooling the aluminum product in the mold, and returning the heat-exchanged cooling water into the upper chamber of the cooling well body through the circulation passage. The cooling well main body is provided with clapboard for dividing inner chamber of the cooling well main body into an upper chamber, and lower chamber, and the upper chamber is stored with cooling water. The clapboard is provided with multiple through holes connected with the upper chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for cooling cast aluminum product for preventing steam explosion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cooling cast aluminum product comprises cooling the well main body, passing carbon dioxide or nitrogen gas having a pressure of 0.01-0.05 MPa into the lower chamber through the through hole, forming an air cushion layer in the lower chamber, allowing gas in the lower chamber to continuously passing into the upper chamber through each of the through holes, forming continuous overflow or bubble flow in the upper chamber, pumping the cooling water in the upper chamber, and passing it to the cooling passage of the casting mold for cooling the aluminum product in the mold, and returning the heat-exchanged cooling water into the upper chamber of the cooling well body through the circulation passage. The cooling well main body is internally provided with the clapboard for dividing the inner chamber of the cooling well main body into an upper chamber, and lower chamber, and the upper chamber is stored with cooling water. The clapboard is provided with multiple through holes connected with the upper chamber, and lower chamber. The side wall of cooling well main body is provided with an air inlet hole connected with lower chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The height of the cooling water in the upper chamber is less than or equal to 5 meters. The surface finish of the clapboard is less than or equal to Sa2.5. The thickness of the clapboard is 2-3 mm. The clapboard is made of stainless steel plate or aluminum alloy plate.

PN US2018106138-A1
TI System for efficiently recovering geothermal heat,            has energy recovery apparatus which is in fluid            communication with productions wells and energy            contained in production fluid is converted to            electricity, heat in energy recovery apparatus.
AU RANDOLPH J B
AE TERRACOH INC (TERR-Non-standard)
GA 2018299494
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has injection wells (14) for                accessing underground reservoirs (1). The                production wells (28) have a production well                reservoir opening (16) in fluid with one of                reservoirs. The exposure of a working fluid (12) to                first temperatures heats working fluid to a third                temperature that is higher than second temperature.                A second portion of working fluid is at third                temperature enters production well reservoir                openings. An energy recovery apparatus is in fluid                communication with productions wells. The energy                contained in the production fluid is converted to                electricity and/or heat.
   USE - System for efficiently recovering geothermal                heat from reservoirs by injecting a non-water based                working fluid, such as carbon dioxide, into the                reservoir to extract geothermal heat used in power                plants, such as a fossil fuel power plants e.g.,                coal plant, natural gas plant, and the like, a                plant capable of producing fuel, such as biofuel                e.g., ethanol plant, or an industrial plant, such                as a cement manufacturer, steel manufacturer.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovery of methane from the reservoir and                subsequent combustion of a portion of the methane                to boost production fluid temperature or pressure,                or both, can increase the overall system efficiency                and the overall power produced compared to                geothermal capture alone. The system and method can                be configured to take advantage of the methane that                is produced to the surface in order to improve the                efficiency of the geothermal energy recovery by                recovering another form of energy from the                reservoir namely, a portion of the chemical energy                stored in the methane. The formation of methane                thermosiphon can reduce or minimize parasitic power                loses, such as those that can occur due to fluid                injection or production pumps. The geothermal heat                recovered with the carbon dioxide -based working                fluid and the outside fuel source can combine in                much the same fashion as carbon dioxide-based                energy recovery and combusted produced methane, to                increase the overall efficiency of conversion                energy to electricity and to produce more                electricity than either system could provide                alone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a simplified schematic view                of a geothermal energy recovery system.

PN CN112981045-A; CN112981045-B
TI Vanadium-phosphorus-containing molten iron dephosphorization and vanadium preservation method involves draining vanadium-containing molten iron after desulfurization into induction furnace, insulating, and adding dephosphorization slag.
AU ZHANG T
   DOU Z
   LV G
   YANG H
   LIU Y
   ZHAO Q
   ZHANG Z
   ZHANG W
   FU D
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 202172338H
AB    NOVELTY - A vanadium-phosphorus-containing molten iron dephosphorization and vanadium preservation method comprises draining vanadium-containing molten iron after desulfurization into the induction furnace, maintaining temperature of the vanadium-containing molten iron in the induction furnace at 1400-1450 degrees C, adding dephosphorization slag, spraying carbon dioxide gas at injection rate of 0.50-5.0 Nm3/ton for 10-60 minutes.
   USE - The vanadium-phosphorus-containing molten iron dephosphorization and vanadium preservation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes selective oxidation of vanadium-containing molten iron and resource utilization of carbon dioxide by blowing carbon dioxide. The smelting process of vanadium steel is reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The vanadium-containing phosphorus molten iron used is molten iron obtained by smelting vanadium-titanium magnetite in a blast furnace or a non-blast furnace, and after desulfurization, the vanadium content is 0.05-0.60%, and the phosphorus content is 0.05-0.15%. In the blowing process, pure carbon dioxide gas is used for blowing, or a mixed gas with carbon dioxide gas with volume fraction of not less than 60% and balance oxygen and argon; and the blowing method adopts top blowing, bottom blowing, side blowing or top and bottom combined blowing; and the carbon monoxide gas generated by carbon dioxide dephosphorization is returned to the steel smelting process for recycling. Preferred Components: The dephosphorization slag used in the injection process is calcium oxide slag, and the basicity of the initial slag is 2-4, including lime, fluorite, quartz sand, iron ore or iron oxide scale.

PN KR2020141123-A; KR2213381-B1
TI Manufacture of magnesium oxide hollow sphere used as adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide, involves preparing precursor solution by dissolving magnesium salt, polystyrene, and carboxylic acid in solvent, and pyrolyzing formed droplets.
AU KIM J S
   LIMKYUNGMIN
   KIM W S
AE UNIV KYUNGHEE IND COOP (UYKY-C)
GA 2020D0227H
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of magnesium oxide hollow sphere involves preparing a precursor solution by dissolving magnesium salt, polystyrene and carboxylic acid in a solvent, applying ultrasonic waves to the precursor solution to generate droplets, and spraying the droplets into a reactor using a carrier gas, and pyrolyzing the droplets.
   USE - Manufacture of magnesium oxide hollow spheres (claimed) used as adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces magnesium oxide hollow spheres having large specific surface area and carbon dioxide adsorptivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for magnesium oxide hollow spheres having bimodal pores.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium salts comprise magnesium nitrate, magnesium acetate, magnesium chloride, magnesium sulfate, magnesium bromide, and magnesium iodide. Preferred Process: The method further involves annealing the magnesium oxide hollow spheres at 100-500 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: The pyrolysis is carried out at 300-1000 degrees C. Preferred Components: The magnesium oxide is at least partially modified with carboxylic acid. Preferred Properties: The pore size distribution of the magnesium oxide hollow spheres shows a peak (P1) of 1-10 nm and a peak (P2) of 10-50 nm. The magnesium oxide hollow sphere has BET specific surface area of 160 m2/g or more, and pore volume of 0.30 cm3/g or more. Preferred Composition: The amount of carbon dioxide adsorption of the magnesium oxide hollow sphere is 0.150 mol/kg or more.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxylic acid is one chosen from oxalic acid, succinic acid, tartaric acid, glutamic acid, adipic acid, terephthalic acid, phthalic acid, malonic acid, maleic acid, and fumaric acid. The solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, propanol, iso-propanol, tert-butanol, n-butanol, methoxyethanol, ethoxyethanol, dimethylacetamide, dimethylformamide, n-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), formic acid, nitromethane, acetic acid, and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The polystyrene has average particle size of 50-500 nm.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AU FUELLER K
   STENGEL T
AE DAIMLER AG (DAIM-C)
GA 2020C0404B
AB    NOVELTY - Plastic (1) comprises a negative carbon dioxide footprint, and a plastic base material (3), where the plastic base material is mixed with biochar (5).
   USE - The plastic is useful in vehicle having a component (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing a plastic, comprising (a) mixing a plastic base material with biochar, (b) preserving the plastic, preferably the plastic with a negative carbon dioxide footprint;
   (2) component (7), preferably for a vehicle, comprising plastic; and
   (3) vehicle having a component.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the component.
   Plastic (1)
   Plastic base material (3)
   Biochar (5)
   Component (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The plastic base material comprises polypropylene (PP), polyethylene (PE), polyethylene terephthalate (PET) and polycarbonate (PC). The biochar with which the plastic base material is compounded is a pyrocarbon or a biochar. The biochar has a carbon mass content of at least 50%, preferably at least 70%, based on a total mass of biochar. The biochar has a molar hydrogen-carbon ratio of 0.1-0.6, preferably 0.3-0.4. Preferred Method: The biochar is mixed in powdered form with the plastic base material. The mixing according to process step (a) is carried out at a temperature of 100-250 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN110922605-A; CN110922605-B
TI Preparation of covalent organic framework material involves subjecting aromatic amines and aromatic aldehydes to aldol condensation using supercritical carbon dioxide as reaction medium under specific reaction conditions.
AU GAO Y
   WANG S
   XU H
   YAN Q
   HU H
AE UNIV HAINAN (UYNN-C)
GA 202025898A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of covalent organic framework material (I) involves subjecting aromatic amines and aromatic aldehydes to aldol condensation using supercritical carbon dioxide as reaction medium under specific reaction conditions.
   USE - Preparation of covalent organic framework material (I) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, and environmentally-friendly, and provides covalent organic framework material (I) with large specific surface area and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of covalent organic framework material of formula (I) involves subjecting aromatic amines and aromatic aldehydes to aldol condensation using supercritical carbon dioxide as reaction medium under specific reaction conditions.
   A = group of formula (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation method preferably involves mixing aromatic amine and aromatic aldehyde to obtain raw material mixture, placing raw material mixture in closed reactor using carbon dioxide as protective gas, heating, raising pressure of reactor, performing aldol condensation reaction under specific temperature and pressure conditions to obtain a covalent organic framework crude product, and post-processing. The mixing of raw material preferably involves placing aromatic amine and aromatic aldehyde in vials, sealing the opening of the vial with filter paper, shaking to mix the aromatic amine and aromatic aldehyde uniformly to obtain a raw material mixture, and placing the vial in the reactor. Preferred Condition: The reactor is maintained at a temperature 32-120 degrees C and a pressure of 7.4-20 MPa. The aldol condensation reaction is performed for 12-120 hours, preferably 12-48 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the aromatic amine to the aromatic aldehyde is 1:(1-20), preferably (1:1)-(1:18). Preferred Component: The aromatic amine is one or more of tetra(4-aminophenyl)methane, tetra(4-aminophenyl)adamantane, tetra(4aminophenyl)pyrene and p-phenylenediamine. The aromatic aldehyde is one or more of terephthalaldehyde, 4,4'-biphenyldialdehyde, tetrakis(4-formylphenyl)pyrene, 5,6-bis(4-formylbenzyl)-1-methyl-1H-benzimidazole, and 2',3',5',6'-tetrafluoro-(1,1': 4',1''-terphenyl)-4,4''-dicarbaldehyde.

MC A02-A; A05-J11; A10-D

TI New N-dimethyldiglycolamine bridged diaryloxy rare            earth-zinc hetero-bimetallic complex useful in reducing            carbon dioxide emission.
AU YAO Y
   HUA L
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2019113831
AB    NOVELTY - N-dimethyldiglycolamine bridged diaryloxy rare                earth-zinc hetero-bimetallic complex (I) are                new.
   USE - (I) is useful in reducing carbon dioxide                emission (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - N-dimethyldiglycolamine bridged diaryloxy rare                earth-zinc hetero-bimetallic complex of formula (I)                are new. The structure is not given in the                specification (SIC).
   Ln = rare earth metal ion.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) preparing cyclic carbonate comprising                subjecting alkylene oxide compound and carbon                dioxide to an addition reaction under the action of                a catalyst in the presence of a quaternary ammonium                salt to obtain a cyclic carbonate compound, where                catalyst is (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I) comprises (i) reacting diglycolamine and                  2,4-di-tert-butylphenol in aqueous formaldehyde                  solution to obtain diethylene glycol amine                  diaryloxy ligand precursor H3L, (ii) reacting                  diglycolamine bisaryloxy ligand precursor H3L                  obtained in step (i) and trilanyl rare earth                  compound (LnCp3) in tetrahydrofuran under                  anhydrous and anaerobic conditions to obtain                  diethylene glycol amine bridged diaryloxy rare                  earth metal complex (L2Ln2), and (iii) reacting                  bis(ethoxy)zinc ((Zn(OCH2Ph)2)n) with diethylene                  glycol amine bridged diaryloxy rare earth metal                  complex (L2Ln2) obtained in step (ii) to obtain                  diethylene glycol amine bridged diaryloxy rare                  earth- zinc heterobimetallic complex                  (L2Ln2Zn(OCH2Ph)2). Preferred Components: The                  quaternary ammonium salt is tetrabutylammonium                  iodide, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetraoctyl                  ammonium bromide, benzyl trioctyl ammonium                  bromide or bis(triphenylphosphine) ammonium                  chloride. The alkylene oxide is (III). The molar                  ratio between diethylene glycol amine bridged                  diaryloxy rare earth-zinc heterobimetallic                  complex and alkylene oxide is 1:200-500. The                  molar amount of quaternary ammonium salt is 1-4                  times the diethylene glycol amine bridged                  diaryloxy rare earth-zinc heterobimetallic                  complex.R1, R2 = alkyl, alkoxy, halo or ether,                  preferably methyl, chloromethyl, n-butyl, phenyl,                  phenoxymethyl, enbutyloxy, ethoxymethyl,                  cyclohexyl or cyclopentyl.Preferred Method: The reaction temperature                  of the addition reaction is 25-100 degrees C, and                  reaction pressure is 0.08-0.15 MPa.

PN CN108794298-A; CN108794298-B
TI Synthesizing methanol at low temperature method            comprises pre-heating catalyst in a reducing gas            atmosphere, cooling, switching mixed atmosphere of            carbon dioxide and hydrogen and carrying out a            reaction.
AU KANG J
   HU J
   CHENG K
   DENG J
   ZHANG Q
   DENG D
   WANG Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201892729T
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing methanol at low temperature                method comprises pre-heating the catalyst in a                reducing gas atmosphere, cooling to the room                temperature, switching to the mixed atmosphere of                carbon dioxide and hydrogen and carrying out a                reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing methanol                at low temperature method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the phenomenon of sintering                reaction process, improves the stability of the                catalyst, adopts reducing atmosphere pre-processing                catalyst, promotes the selective generation of                methanol and is simple and controllable preparation                process and easy to amplifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing gas is                  hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, hydrogen                  sulfide, nitric oxide, ammonia, or mixed gas is                  hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, hydrogen                  sulfide, nitric oxide, ammonia and inert gas, the                  volume ratio of reducing gas is 1-60 % and inert                  gas (balance). The inert gas is nitrogen, helium                  gas and argon gas. The catalyst is metal sulfide                  and metal oxide or its load-type catalyst. The                  metal sulfide is indium(iii) sulfide,                  bis(sulfanylidene)tungsten, tantalum(iv) sulfide,                  zinc sulfide, molybdenum disulfide, carbonyl                  sulfide, copper sulfide, nickel sulfide, iron(ii)                  sulfide or vanadium sulfide. The metal oxide is                  zirconium dioxide, zinc oxide, gallium(iii)                  oxide, chromium(iii) oxide, molybdenum trioxide,                  aluminum oxide, tungsten trioxide, manganese(ii)                  oxide, manganese(iv) oxide and cerium(iv) oxide.                  The supported catalyst is a metal sulfide or a                  metal oxide supported on an oxide, a molecular                  sieve, a carbon material, and a silicon carbide.                  The pre-heating condition is as follows: the                  temperature is 150-350 degrees C, the time is                  0.5-50 hours, the gas pressure is 1-100bar and                  airspeed is 500-5000 ml/hour/g. The carbon                  dioxide hydrogenation reaction conditions are as                  follows: the pressure is 1-100bar, the reaction                  temperature is 80-200 degrees C, the reaction                  airspeed is 500-60000 ml/h/g. The volume ratio of                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed gas is                  1-5.

TI Preparation of graphene-ceramic composite material            involves mixing magnesium powder and ceramic powder,            carrying out combustion reaction of mixture in carbon            dioxide gas, and sintering obtained powder.
AU HE G
   LU N
   LI H
   LI J
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201840972X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a graphene-ceramic composite                material involves uniformly mixing magnesium powder                and ceramic powder in the mass ratio of 1:3-9,                carrying out combustion reaction of the obtained                mixture in carbon dioxide gas with pressure of                0.5-5 MPa, and sintering the obtained powder.
   USE - Preparation of graphene-ceramic composite                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, short, economical and                produces graphene-ceramic composite material having                uniform component structure and excellent                performance. The process uses in-situ deposition                composite magnesium and carbon dioxide gas-solid                reaction characteristics, which enables uniform                mixing of the graphene and ceramic powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Component: The ceramic powder                  comprises alumina, silica, magnesium oxide,                  calcium oxide, titania, zirconia, yttria,                  lanthanum oxide, cerium oxide, silicon nitride,                  aluminum nitride, boron nitride, titanium                  nitride, zirconium nitride, silicon carbide,                  boron carbide, tungsten carbide, titanium                  carbide, zirconium carbide, hafnium carbide,                  titanium boride and/or hafnium boride. The                  sintering is carried out in vacuum, nitrogen or                  inert atmosphere. Preferred Property: The grain                  diameter of magnesium powder is 1-100 mu m. The                  grain diameter of ceramic powder is 10 nm-10 mu                  m.

PN CN114887658-A
TI Dendritic mesoporous silica microsphere supported            carbene catalyst used for carbon dioxide cycloaddition            reaction with epoxide to produce target compound cyclic            carbonate, which is prepared by synthesize the            dendritic mesoporous silica microspheres, carrying out            chemical modification on silica.
AU DING H
   CUI S
   LIU W
   LIU X
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 2022A75548
AB    NOVELTY - Dendritic mesoporous silica microsphere                supported carbene catalyst,prepared by grafting                carbene groups on surface of a dendritic mesoporous                silica microsphere.
   USE - Dendritic mesoporous silica microsphere                supported carbene catalyst used for carbon dioxide                cycloaddition reaction with epoxide to produce                target compound cyclic carbonate(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has the unique advantages of high                specific surface area and high active site, and can                catalyze CO under conditions of normal pressure, no                solvent and no promoter. The catalyst has higher                selectivity and conversion rate for cycloaddition                reaction, and has stable structure and good cyclic                usability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:(1)                preparation method of the dendritic mesoporous                silica microsphere supported carbene catalyst,                involves taking tetraethyl silicate, hexadecyl                trimethyl ammonium bromide, sodium salicylate and                triethanolamine are used as raw materials to                synthesize dendritic mesoporous silica                microspheres; then carrying out chemical                modification on dendritic mesoporous silica                microspheres by using triethoxy-3-                (2-imidazoline-1-yl) propyl silane to graft                imidazoline functional groups on the surfaces of                the dendritic mesoporous silica microspheres; and                finally, putting chemically modified mesoporous                silica microspheres into dimethyl carbonate for                solvothermal reaction to obtain dendritic                mesoporous silica microsphere supported carbene                catalyst.(2)application of dendritic mesoporous                silica microsphere supported carbene                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method:The method involves                  dissolving 0.1-0.5g of triethanolamine in                  50-400mL of water, adding 1-3g hexadecyl                  trimethyl ammonium bromide and 0.5-3g of sodium                  salicylate, stirring for 0.5-3h, then adding                  10-20mL of tetraethyl silicate, reacting for 4-8h                  at normal temperature, washing and drying the                  obtained product, then adding product into mixed                  solution of ethanol and hydrochloric acid,                  stirring for 2-6h at 50-100 &#8451;,                  filtering, then adding the product into mixed                  solution of ethanol and hydrochloric acid again,                  stirring for 2-6h at 50-100 &#8451;,                  filtering and drying to obtain dendritic                  mesoporous silica microspheres, dispersing                  0.1-0.8g dendritic mesoporous silica microspheres                  in 20-50mL of dry acetonitrile, uniformly                  stirring, adding 100 muL of triethoxy-3-                  (2-imidazoline-1-yl) propyl silane, and adding                  the mixture into Nitrogen Reflux reaction at                  50-100 deg.c for 24-72 hr, cooling to room                  temperature, centrifugal separation of solid                  matter, washing with alcohol and drying to obtain                  mesoporous dioxide with imidazoline functional                  group grafted on the surface Silicon                  microspheres, and putting 0.1-0.5g mesoporous                  silica microsphere with imidazoline functional                  group grafted on surface and 10-50mL dimethyl                  carbonate into a stainless steel autoclave with a                  polytetrafluoroethylene lining, carrying out                  solvent heat treatment at 80-150 &#8451; for                  12-48 hours, cooling, filtering and drying to                  obtain dendritic mesoporous silica microsphere                  loaded carbene catalyst..Preferred Components: The epoxide is                  epichlorohydrin, epoxy bromopropane, styrene                  oxide or epoxy propyl phenyl ether.The dosage of                  dendritic mesoporous silica microsphere loaded                  carbene catalyst accounts for 2-10% of the mass                  of the epoxide.The cycloaddition reaction is                  carried out under environment of normal pressure,                  no solvent and no promoter.The temperature of                  cycloaddition reaction is 25-120 &#8451;, and                  reaction time is 8-48 h.After the reaction, the                  catalyst can be recovered and recycled through                  centrifugal separation.

PN CN114538478-A
TI Preparing sodium bicarbonate particle, comprises            adding additives into mixed solution of sodium            carbonate and sodium bicarbonate, continuously            introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting, using            ultrafiltration membrane to remove salt, and analyzing            concentration of additives in reaction solution.
AU CHEN G
   LIU C
AE NANJING ANHUAI INNOVATIVE DRUG RES INST (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022749397
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sodium bicarbonate particle,                comprises adding additives into the mixed solution                of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate to                obtain a salt solution, heating the above-mentioned                salt solution temperature to 50-80&#8451;,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas with                flow speed of 10-40 ml/minute, reacting for 6-10                hours, and filtering to obtain the reaction liquid                and reaction product, drying the reaction product                at 40&#8451; for 12 hours to obtain sodium                bicarbonate particles, taking 1 ml reaction                solution and diluting with water to 100 ml, using                nanofiltration membrane or ultrafiltration membrane                to remove salt, and using UV-visible                spectrophotometry to analyze the concentration of                additives in the reaction solution after the                reaction, adding additive and sodium carbonate to                the required concentration into the remaining                filtered reaction solution, and repeating to                continue the reaction to prepare sodium bicarbonate                particles.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sodium                bicarbonate particle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of sodium                bicarbonate particles by controlling the                concentration of the additive to obtain the                expected particle diameter of the particles. TI Gas diffusion electrode used in device for electrolyzing carbon dioxide, comprises current collector, and coating layers comprising catalytic conductive layers, in which each catalytic conductive layer has porous structure containing catalyst and hydrophobic agent.

   XU B
   LU Q
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
   UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202261561S
AB    NOVELTY - A gas diffusion electrode comprises a current collector (10), a coating layer (A) provided on one surface of the current collector near the gas side, and a coating layer (B) provided on other surface of the current collector near electrolyte side. The coating layers (A and B) comprise catalytic conductive layers (11, 12, 13). Each catalytic conductive layer has a porous structure containing a catalyst and a hydrophobic agent, and permeable to gas. The mass content of the hydrophobic agent in each catalytic conductive layer decreases sequentially from the direction of the coating layer (A) to the coating layer (B).
   USE - Gas diffusion electrode is used in device for electrolyzing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas diffusion electrode adopts the design of at least two layers of catalytic conductive layer. The main components of each catalyst and hydrophobic agent are the same. The electrode performance can be kept stable under the different electrolytic liquid penetration depth. The electrolytic stability is better.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the gas diffusion electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas diffusion electrode. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Current collector (10)
   Catalytic conductive layers (11, 12, 13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalytic conductive layer of coating layer (A) comprises 40-100 %mass hydrophobic agent and 5-40 %mass catalyst. The catalytic conductive layer of coating layer (B) comprises 30 %mass or less hydrophobic agent and 60-100 %mass catalyst. The catalyst can conduct electricity and is stable, and does not decompose at 300-360degrees Celsius. The catalyst is a composite material of carbon and transition metal, a composite material of carbon and transition metal oxide, an alloy of noble metal and transition metal, and/or a composite material of noble metal and transition metal oxide. The total loading amount of catalyst in the gas diffusion electrode is 100-250 mg/cm2.Preferred Property: The gas diffusion electrode has porosity of 30-50%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: the hydrophobic agent is polytetrafluoroethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride and/or polyvinylidene fluoride-hexafluoropropylene copolymer.

PN CN114177354-A; CN114177354-B
TI Preparation method of natural ceramic bone involves adding hydrogen peroxide to bone blocks treated to carry out deproteinization and sterilization treatment, washing with pure water, extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide, and subjecting to gradient temperature calcination.
AU SUN B
   HUANG G
   ZHANG W
   LI G
AE TIANXINFU BEIJING MEDICAL APPLIANCE CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202243127Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of natural ceramic bone, which involves: i. cleaning the surface of animal bones, remove the attachments on the surface, cutting into small bone pieces, soakinng in pure water, and changing the water every 0.8-1.2 hours until no blood seeps out; ii. adding hydrogen peroxide with a concentration of 2-4% to the bone blocks treated in step (1) to carry out deproteinization and sterilization treatment, then ultrasonically treating, washing with pure water, and drying; iii. extracting dried bone blocks with supercritical carbon dioxide, and then subjecting to gradient temperature calcination under nitrogen protection; and iv. washing calcined bone material with 4-6% sodium hydroxide solution, washing with water, finally adjusting to neutrality with phosphate-buffered saline solution, and drying.
   USE - Preparation method of natural ceramic bone
   ADVANTAGE - Preparation method of natural ceramic bone adopts hydrogen peroxide to remove most of protein before supercritical extraction, loosens bone blocks, facilitates further supercritical degreasing and deproteinization, has no harmful substance residue after the treatment of hydrogen peroxide and supercritical extraction, and does not damage the mechanical properties of materials.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an infrared spectrum of the natural ceramicized bone. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: In step (2), the ultrasonic treatment is to wash in a water bath at 45-55 degreesC for 25-35 minutes, and the ultrasonic frequency is 35-45 kilohertz. The ultrasonic treatment in step (2) is to wash in a water bath at 50 degreesC for 30 minutes. The drying temperature in step (2) is 45-55 degreesC. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction treatment conditions in step (3) are: extraction pressure 20-30 megapascal, extraction temperature 30-40 degreesC, flow rate 25-35 liter per hour, extraction time 3.5-4.5 hours. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction treatment conditions in step (3) are: extraction pressure 25 megapascal, extraction temperature 35 degreesC, flow rate 30 liter per hour, and extraction time 4 hours. The gradient temperature calcination in step (4) is specifically: calcination at 600-800 degreesC for 45-55 minutes, calcination at 800-1000 degreesC for 45-55 minutes, and calcination at 1000-1200 degreesC for 45-55 minutes. The gradient temperature calcination in step (4) is specifically: calcination at 600-800 degreesC for 50 minutes, calcination at 800-1000 degreesC for 50 minutes, and calcination at 1000-1200 degreesC for 50 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Entrainer is alcohol, preferably, entrainment agent is absolute ethanol. Animal bones in step (1) are selected from femoral diaphysis and rib bones of large mammals such as cattle, sheep and pigs.

PN CN114158566-A
TI Slow-release chlorine dioxide-release type single-component gel comprises carbomer, alkaline compound, chlorite and water.
AU MA J
   JI T
   SHI L
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2022421049
AB    NOVELTY - Slow-release chlorine dioxide-release type single-component gel comprises carbomer, alkaline compound, chlorite and water.
   USE - Slow-release chlorine dioxide-release type single-component gel as disinfectant for applying in the environment air sterilization in sealed places such as room, toilet, carriage, refrigerator or refrigerator by placing the sealed container with open lid in a relatively closed place, and the sealed container for holding or storing the disinfectant (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a method for preparaing the single-component gel slow-release chlorine dioxide release-type disinfectant, which involves formulating the gel by aqueous solution of carbomer and aqueous solution of alkaline compound, and then adding the aqueous solution of chlorite and stirring uniformly to obtain the disinfectant, (2) a method for using the single-component gel slow-release chlorine dioxide disinfectant to releases chlorine dioxide gas, which involves opening the lid of airtight container, or controlling the opening of lid of airtight container, contacting the single-component gel slow-release chlorine dioxide disinfectant in the airtight container with air, gradually absorbing the gel of one-component gel slow-release chlorine dioxide disinfectant and carbon dioxide in the air and reacting with water to form carbonic acid, at room temperature, using carbon dioxide or carbonic acid as an activator for activation reaction, releasing chlorine dioxide gas, holding or storing by sealed container single-component gel slow-release chlorine dioxide disinfectant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: Slow-release chlorine dioxide-release type single-component gel comprises carbomer, alkaline compound, chlorite and water in 1:(0.2 to 0.7):(0.5 to 3.0):(30 to 80) ratio. Preferred Components: The alkaline compound is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. The chlorite is sodium chlorite or potassium chlorite. Preferred Conditions: Carbomer is added to the reaction container, followed by adding water, stirring to fully dissolve, then adding alkaline compound aqueous solution, stirring uniformly to obtain gel, then adding chlorite aqueous solution, and stirring uniformly. The amount of chlorite aqueous solution is 5-30 wt.%, alkaline compound aqueous solution is 5-20 vol.%. The preparation method involves adding chlorite aqueous solution to stir uniformly and then sealing, or, adding chlorite aqueous solution and stirring uniformly, and then storing under inert atmosphere or vacuum condition, adding the carbomer into the reaction container, adding water, stirring and fully dissolving, then adding 5-20 vol.% alkaline compound water solution, stirring uniformly to obtain gel, then 5-30 vol.% adding chlorite aqueous solution, and stirring uniformly.
   ACTIVITY - Antimicrobial.

PN CN113353962-A
TI Preparing active nano calcium carbonate useful in rubber, plastic and industrial fields, comprises e.g. calcining limestone by high temperature radiation, performing carbonization reaction, obtaining calcium carbonate, filtering and drying.
AU LI P
   LU M
   SUN H
   LI J
   WANG Z
   LIU G
   LU X
AE XIANGYANG XINGFA CHEM CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A9988F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active nano calcium carbonate comprises (i) calcining limestone by high temperature radiation to generate calcium oxide and carbon dioxide gas, subjecting calcium oxide and water to digestion reaction at 25-30 degrees C to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry; (ii) adding calcium hydroxide slurry into the carbonization reaction kettle after adding water, stirring under 25-35 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction; (iii) adding crystal control agent before carbonization for 30 minutes, surfactant 1 and stirring uniformly, then continuously reacting, until the conductivity value of the carbonization reaction kettle slurry is reduced to the lowest point, stopping the carbonization reaction under 0.3-0.5 ms/cm, obtaining calcium carbonate; and (iv) adding surfactant 2 to calcium carbonate after carbonization reaction in the step (iii) for stirring and activating, filtering and drying the obtained slurry and crushing to obtain active nano-calcium carbonate.
   USE - The active nano calcium carbonate is useful in rubber, plastic and industrial fields, industrial fields, namely silicone rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can prepares active nano calcium carbonate, which has cubic shape, primary particle size 40-60 nm, 80-100 nm, apparent particle size to D97 = greater than or equal to 9 mu m as a functional filler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate content is more than 97.5% limestone. The surfactant 1 in the step (iii) is 12-18C fatty acids and the addition amount is 1-3% calcium hydroxide slurry. The surfactant 2 in the step (v) is 12-18C fatty acid sodium salt, and the addition amount is 0.5-2% of the calcium hydroxide slurry. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide gas generated by the limestone calcination in the step (i) is used as carbon dioxide gas source in the step (iii), and the volume fraction of the carbon dioxide gas is 20-40%. The calcium hydroxide slurry in the step (ii) is mixed with water to prepare calcium hydroxide slurry with a mass concentration of 12-18%. The crystal form control agent is sodium citrate, sucrose, and polyphosphate, and the total added amount is 0.5-2% of calcium hydroxide slurry. The crystal form control agent in the step (iii) is sodium citrate, sucrose, polyphosphate, and the total added amount is 0.5-2% of calcium hydroxide slurry. The addition time of crystal form control agent and surfactant 1 is before the carbonization proceeds i.e. calcium hydroxide slurry is placed into the carbonization reactor after adding water, adding crystal form control agent, surfactant 1, passing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction. The addition time of crystal form control agent and surfactant 1 is 10 minutes before carbonization i.e. calcium hydroxide slurry is added with water and then put into the carbonization reactor, and carbon dioxide gas is introduced for carbonization reaction for 10 minutes before adding the crystal form control agent, surfactant 1. The drying temperature is carried out at 110-150 degrees C.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

GA 2021946055

PT P
PN CN112973625-A; CN112973625-B
TI Preparing lignin-based carbon nanotubes used as adsorbents comprises mixing santa barbara amorphous and transition metal salt, freeze-drying, pyrolyzing and carbonizing precursor and subjecting to acid treatment and alkali treatment.
AU SHAO L
   CHEN J
   LUO W
   ZHAN P
   ZHANG L
   LIU N
   SHENG Z
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH FORESTRY & TECHNOLOGY (UCSF-C)
GA 202171943F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lignin-based carbon nanotubes comprises mixing Santa Barbara Amorphous-15 and transition metal salt in liquid phase to obtain mixed solution A, freeze-drying and roasting treatment to obtain transition metal oxide/SBA-15 supported catalyst; (ii) liquid phase mixing of lignin and transition metal oxide/santa barbara amorphous-15 supported catalyst to obtain mixed solution B, freeze-drying treatment to obtain a precursor, and (iii) pyrolyzing and carbonizing precursor in a protective atmosphere, and sequentially subjecting carbonized product to acid treatment and alkali treatment.
   USE - The lignin-based carbon nanotubes is used as adsorbents to remove gaseous pollutants in the environment especially pollutants are carbon dioxide, iodine vapor, and volatile organic compounds more preferably carbon dioxide in flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanotubes has hierarchical pore structure of 10-20% micropores (less than 2 nm), 75-85% mesopores (2-50nm) and 3-8% macropores (less than 50nm), has 200-800 m2/g specific surface area, 0.6-1.40 cm3/g pore volume, and 3-10 nm average pore diameter, realizes one-step carbonization of lignin to obtain carbon nanotubes with hierarchical pores, and the material has a better gas adsorption effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for lignin-based carbon nanotube prepared by above mentioned preparation method
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal element in the transition metal salt is iron, cobalt, and/or nickel; preferably, the transition metal salt is a water-soluble salt of a transition metal; preferably nitrate, chloride and/or acetate. The mass ratio of SBA-15 to transition metal salt is 1:1-3:1. The mixed solution A is an aqueous solution. The total concentration of the SBA-15 and the transition metal salt in the mixed solution A is 2-4 g/20-40 ml. In step (ii), the lignin is water-soluble lignin and water-soluble derivatives; preferably, the lignin is sodium lignin sulfonate, potassium lignosulfonate, and calcium lignin sulfonate. The mixed solution B is an aqueous solution, where the lignin is 1-4 g, the catalyst is 0.5-2 g, and the water is 5-40 ml. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the pyrolysis and carbonization is carried out in a tube furnace; preferably, the protective atmosphere is nitrogen and/or inert gas; preferably, the flow rate of the protective gas in the pyrolysis and carbonization process is 20-100 ml/minutes; preferably, the heating rate is 2-6 degrees C/minutes; preferably, the pyrolysis temperature is 800-1100 degrees C; preferably the pyrolysis carbonization time is 1-4 hours. In step (i), the sintering process is carried out under a protective atmosphere; preferably, the protective atmosphere is nitrogen and/or an inert gas; preferably, the firing temperature is 500-800 degrees C; preferably, the firing time for 1-4 hours. The carbonized product is placed in an acid solution for acid treatment, performing solid-liquid separation, washing and drying to obtain acid-treated materials; preferably, the acid solution is aqueous solutions of hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, or sulfuric acid; preferably, the concentration of the acid solution is 2-5 mol/l preferably, in the acid treatment process, the solid-to-liquid ratio of the carbonized product to the acid solution is 1 g/5-20 ml;preferably, the time of the acid treatment process is 20-60 minutes. The acid-treated material is placed in an alkali solution, and subjecting to alkali treatment, performing solid-liquid separation, washing and drying to obtain the lignin-based carbon nanotubes; preferably the alkali solution is alkali metal hydrogen aqueous solution of oxide; preferably, the alkali concentration of the alkali solution is 4-8 mol/l preferably, the solid-to-liquid ratio of the acid-treated material to the alkali solution is 1 g/10-30 ml during the alkali treatment, preferably, the temperature of the alkali treatment process is 50-80 degrees C; preferably the time of the alkali treatment process is 12-24 hours.

   USE - (I) is useful for preparing N-heterocyclic carbene functionalized organic polymer as a catalyst in the catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide and N-formylation of amine (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low production cost and simple preparation method; and utilizes cheap and easily available divinylbenzene as a polymerization crosslinking agent; and produces (I) which has excellent catalytic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Alkene functionalized IPr-HCl monomer i.e. 1,3-bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-yl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium chloride of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) preparing N-heterocyclic carbene functionalized organic polymer, comprising adding (I), divinylbenzene, and azobisisobutyronitrile in an organic solvent F under a protective atmosphere, allowing to stand for polymerization at 50-200 degrees C for 12-48 hours, cooling to room temperature to obtain candy, subjecting obtained candy to post-treatment F to obtain N-heterocyclic carbene functionalized organic polymer, the mass ratio between (I) and divinylbenzene is 1:1-40, the mass ratio between (I) and azobisisobutyronitrile is 1:0.01-0.20.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (V), comprises (i) adding (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline), 4-vinylphenylboronic acid, palladium acetate, tripotassium phosphate trihydrate, tricyclohexylphosphine tetrafluoroborate into organic solvent D under nitrogen protection, reacting at 80 degrees C for 4-12 hours, subjecting resultnt reaction solution D to post-treatment C to obtain mixture containing (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine), the ratio between (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline), 4-vinylphenylboronic acid, palladium acetate and tripotassium phosphate trihydrate is 1:2-4:0.01-0.05:4-8, and (ii) mixing mixture containing (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine), paraformaldehyde and organic solvent E, stirring for 45 minutes at 70 degrees C, then adding trimethyl chlorosilane, reacting for 8-12 hours to obtain reaction liquid E, and then carrying out post-treatment to obtain (I), the purity of the intermediate (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine) in the mixture containing (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine) is detected by liquid phase mass spectrometry, calculate the theoretical substance amount of the (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine) as the amount of the (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine), the intermediate (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine) in the mixture containing (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine), paraformaldehyde and trimethylchlorosilane is 1:1-2:1-2. Preferred Method: In step (i): the post-treatment comprises adding basic alumina to the reaction solution D, stirring uniformly at room temperature, filtering, and rotating under reduced pressure to remove the organic solvent in the filtrate to obtain mixture containing (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine). The (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline) is prepared by (a) adding 2,6-diisopropylaniline, a saturated solution of sodium bicarbonate and organic solvent A, adding organic solvent B solution of elemental iodine within 1.5-2 hours at room temperature, continuously stirring and reacting for 2-8 hours, and post-treating the obtained reaction solution A to obtain 4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline, the mass ratio between 2,6-diisopropylaniline and elemental iodine in the organic solvent B solution of elemental iodine is 1:1-1.5, and (b) adding 4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline, glyoxal, formic acid into organic solvent C, and stirring for 8-12 hours at room temperature to obtain reaction solution B, then post-treating to obtain final product, the mass ratio between 2,6-diisopropylaniline, glyoxal and formic acid is 1:0.5-1.0:0.4-0.8. In step (i) (a): the post-treatment comprises adding sodium thiosulfate to the reaction solution A to remove excess iodine in the reaction solution, extracting with dichloromethane, combining the upper extracts, drying with anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering, and collecting the filtrate, rotary evaporating under reduced pressure to remove the organic solvent in the filtrate to obtain 4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline, and the post-treatment in (i) (b) comprises filtering the reaction solution B, taking the filter cake and washing with methanol, collecting the solid, and drying it in a vacuum drying oven at 60 degrees C to obtain (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline). The organic solvent F is methanol, acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide or dichloromethane, the volume of the organic solvent F is 15-50 ml/g based on (I), the post-treatment F includes soaking after the candy and washing in methanol, and drying under vacuum to obtain N-heterocyclic carbene functionalized organic polymer. Preferred Components: In step (i): the organic solvent D is anhydrous toluene, and the volume of the organic solvent D is 10.6 ml/g based on the mass of the (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(4-iodo-2,6-diisopropylaniline), and in step (ii), the organic solvent E is ethyl acetate, and the volume of the organic solvent E is 6-20 ml/g based on the mass of the mixture containing (N,N'E,N,N'E)-N,N'-(ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(3,5-diisopropyl-4'-vinylbiphenyl-4-amine). In step (i) (a): the volume of the saturated solution of sodium bicarbonate in step (a) is 16.7 ml/g based on the mass of 2,6-diisopropylaniline, the organic solvent A and the organic solvent B are both diethyl ether, the volume of the organic solvent A is 2 ml/g based on the mass of 2,6-diisopropylaniline, in step (b), the organic solvent C is methanol, and the volume of the organic solvent C is 13.7 ml/g based on the mass of the 2,6-diisopropylaniline.

PT P
PN TW202120193-A
TI Nozzle device for connecting outer tube for conveying compressed gas and inner tube for conveying carbon dioxide gas, has outer cover connected to body and surrounding guide section and part of cone section of nozzle annulus to define communicating with orifice.
AU XU W
   TSAO K J
   CHEN J
   LIU Q
AE HABOR PRECISION INC (HABO-Non-standard)
GA 2021A3279H
AB    NOVELTY - A nozzle device includes a body connected to an outer tube, a nozzle connected to the body, and an outer cover connected to the body and surrounding the nozzle and defining an annular chamber. The body includes a surrounding wall defining a passage. This channel is suitable for inserting an inner tube. The surrounding wall has a hole communicating with the channel and the outside. The nozzle includes a large-diameter hole and a small-diameter hole communicating with the channel, a tapered section connecting the end surface of the adjacent small-diameter hole, and a guiding section connecting the end surface of the adjacent large-diameter hole. The large diameter hole is sleeved with the inner tube. The small diameter hole is used to generate particles due to the phase change of the passing carbon dioxide gas. When the compressed gas enters the annulus through the orifice, it will follow the conical section to flow, and entrain the external airflow and particles to be ejected forward. In this way, the annular chamber restricts the air flow to flow only along the tapered surface section, which can not only increase the flow rate, but also improve the collimation during spraying.

   LUO F
   SHI L
   YAO X
   MEI Q
   GU J
AE SHANGHAI YAOMING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021405171
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hybridomas by enriching mouse plasma                cells using CD138+ biomarkers comprises (i)                enriching mouse plasma cells by mouse CD138                positive selection kit, (ii) collecting the                enriched mouse plasma cells, centrifuging the mouse                plasma cell suspension and the mouse myeloma cell                suspension to discard the supernatant, and                performing cell electrofusion according to the                ratio of mouse plasma cells and mouse myeloma cells                of 1:1-1:3 after washing, (iii) connecting the                fused cells to HAT medium and culturing in a carbon                dioxide incubator, and (iv) placing the fused cells                in a carbon dioxide incubator for continuous                cultivation, and performing high-throughput                screening of specific antibodies after 7-14                days.
   USE - The hybridoma is useful in preparing                monoclonal antibody medicines (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enriches mouse plasma cells by                using CD138+ biomarkers on the surface of plasma                cells; greatly improves the purity of plasma cells;                obtains large number of hybridoma cells secreting                specific antibodies, which improves the positive                rate of specific antibodies during the screening                process; reduces the number of cells fused with the                initial cells and mouse myeloma cells; and greatly                reduces the number of plating and high-throughput                screening and labor and material costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                hybridoma prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The specific method of                  enrichment in step (i) is: (a) preparing buffer,                  filtering and sterilizing the mixed cells of                  immune tissue according to the density of 1x 108                  cells/ml, (b) adding mouse FcR blocker and mouse                  CD138 positive selection mixture to the cell                  suspension obtained in step (a) at a volume ratio                  of 50 mu l/ml, and incubating after mixing, (c)                  adding magnetic beads at a volume ratio of 50 mu                  l/ml, and mixing and incubating, (d) filling up                  the corresponding volume of the solution obtained                  in step (c) with buffer and uniformly mixing,                  placing it in a magnet, pouring out irrelevant                  cell suspension after incubation, washing the                  tube wall with buffer solution to fill up the                  corresponding volume and uniformly mixing, then                  placing the solution in a magnet to incubate,                  pouring out the irrelevant cell suspension and                  collect the cells enriched in the tube wall. In                  the step (b), the specific method of incubation                  is 2-8 degrees C for 3-10 minutes. In the step                  (c), the specific method of incubation is 2-8                  degrees C for 3-10 minutes. In the step (d), the                  specific incubation method is to incubate at room                  temperature for 5-10 minutes. The method                  comprises before performing the cell                  electrofusion described in step (ii), adding 1 ml                  pronase buffer per 1-2x 108 cells to treat mouse                  plasma cells and mouse myeloma cells, the                  processing time is 10-60 seconds, and adding more                  than 10 times the volume of fetal bovine serum                  interferes with the reaction. In the step (iii),                  the carbon dioxide concentration in the carbon                  dioxide incubator is 5%, the culture temperature                  is 37 degrees C, and the culture time is 1-3                  hours. Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  buffer contains phosphate buffered saline, 2%                  fetal bovine serum, and 1 mM EDTA, there is no                  calcium ion and magnesium ion in the phosphate                  buffered saline. In step (iv), the carbon dioxide                  concentration in the carbon dioxide incubator is                  5%, and the culture temperature is 37 degrees                  C.

TI Preparing ceramic material as an absorbent material for acid gases, involves solubilizing alkaline carbonate in water, adding alkaline earth metal oxide or hydroxide, adding binding agent, homogenizing the mixture.
AU BELCHIOR J C
   VOGA PEREIRA G
   DE CARVALHO PINTO P C
   MAIA DE ARAUJO L
   SCHIAVO REZENDE L
   CAFFARO VICENTINI P
   NEVES LOUREIRO L

   LUCIANA L
   CAFFARO V P
   SCHIAVO R L
   MAIA D A L
   DE CARVALHO P P C
   VOGA P G
AE PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   UNIV FEDERAL MINAS GERAIS (UFMG-C)
   FUNDACAO AMPARO A PESQUISA MINAS GERAIS (AMPA-Non-standard)
   FCA FIAT CHRYSLER AUTOMOVEIS BRASIL LTDA (FCAF-Non-standard)
   FAPESP FUNDACAO AMPARO A PESQUISA ESTADO (FAPE-Non-standard)
   FCA FIAT CHRYSLER AUTOMOVEIS BRASIL LTDA (FCAF-Non-standard)
   UNIV FEDERAL MINAS GERAIS (UFMG-C)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
GA 202059412J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ceramic material involves solubilizing 23 to 360 g, preferably 87 g alkaline carbonate in 500 to 1000 ml of water, adding 200 to 800 g, preferably 300 g alkaline earth metal oxide or hydroxide, adding binding agent, homogenizing the mixture for 5 to 30 minutes, preferably 10 minutes and removing shape the mass for 1 to 3 hours, preferably 1 hour. The material is dried at 80 degrees C for 24 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing ceramic material is used as an absorbent material for acid gases, such as carbon dioxide (CO2), nitric oxide (NO), nitrogen dioxide (NO2), sulfur dioxide (SO2), sulfur trioxide (SO3), where absorption process occur in a bed filled with the precast solid material or in a fluidized bed with excess water and source of acid gases, includes carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare ceramic material that increases network energy, and has fast absorption kinetics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for regeneration of ceramic material, which involves thermally decomposing saturated absorbent ceramic at 350-800 degrees C for 2 and 8 hours to release the acid gas, cooling ceramic material to 80 degrees C, adding water under constant agitation to correct the moisture content between 7 and 30%, preferably 17% of the absorbent material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline earth metal is selected from magnesium oxide (MgO), magnesium hydroxide (Mg(OH)2), oxide of calcium (CaO) and calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2). The binding agent being selected from calcium oxide (CaO), Portland cement, calcium sulfate (CaS04) and ferrous sulfate (FeS04). The heating occurring in the presence of nitrogen and in the absence of air and carbon dioxide in a closed chamber. The material is molded into compact block, hollow block (brick) or pelletized material formats.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for detecting and analyzing molar ratio of sulfate and carboxylate in enoxaparin sodium, comprises adopting anion exchange resin and cation exchange resin for elution and conducting conductivity titration of eluent.
AU LUO X
   HE R
   LU N
AE HUBEI YINUORUI BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202051757V
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting and analyzing molar ratio of sulfate and carboxylate in enoxaparin sodium comprises adopting anion exchange resin and cation exchange resin for elution and conducting conductivity titration of the eluent with 0.1 N sodium hydroxide solution including (i) rinsing the anion and cation exchange columns with carbon dioxide water, collecting the eluent at the outlet of the cation exchange column; (ii) injecting sample solution from the anion exchange column sampling end, and rinsing the anion and cation exchange columns with carbon dioxide-free water and collecting the eluate with a triangular flask at the outlet of the cation exchange column; (iii) transferring the collected solution to a triangular flask with a magnetic stir bar; (v) adding sodium hydroxide solution into the conductivity cell; and (v) drawing the curve with the conductivity value on the y-axis and the volume of sodium hydroxide as the x-axis and calculating the molar ratio of sulfate and carboxylate.
   USE - Method for detecting and analyzing molar ratio of sulfate and carboxylate in enoxaparin sodium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method only requires traditional equipment e.g. peristaltic pumps and conductivity meters and has low detection cost and convenient operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the experiment device.

PN CN111053285-A
TI Improving the quality of tobacco leaves involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of tobacco leaves to obtain extracted tobacco leaves and tobacco leaf extracts, and dissolving tobacco leaf extract in an organic solvent.
AU WANG Y
   LI C
   LIU R
   QIU G
   TAO W
   MA M
   ZHOU S
   ZHOU Y
   ZHU C
   ZHAO Y
   SONG Q
AE CHINA TOBACCO CHONGQING IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
   HUABAO FLAVOURS & FRAGRANCES CO LTD (HUAB-Non-standard)
GA 2020367227
AB    NOVELTY - Improving the quality of tobacco leaves involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction of tobacco leaves to obtain extracted tobacco leaves and tobacco leaf extracts, and dissolving the tobacco leaf extract in an organic solvent, centrifuging to remove insoluble materials, and then concentrating under reduced pressure, after removing the organic solvent, obtaining a refined tobacco leaf extract. The multi-stage molecular distillation is performed on the refined tobacco leaf extract to obtain various groups of fractions. The sensory evaluation is performed on various groups of fractions, left the fractions that have a positive effect on sensory quality. The fractions that have a positive effect on the sensory quality are formulated according to the characteristics and design goals of the tobacco leaves. The fraction is dissolved in ethanol, sprayed onto the extracted tobacco leaves, and adjusted the moisture content of the tobacco leaves.
   USE - Method for improving the quality of tobacco leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve the quality of tobacco leaves, that can significantly improve the sensory quality of tobacco leaves, so that the impurity in the tobacco leaves is significantly reduced, the stimulation is reduced, the aroma quality and aroma amount are significantly improved, the energy is moderate, and the aftertaste is more comfortable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The process conditions of the supercritical CO2 extraction comprises temperature at 30-50 degrees C, extraction kettle pressure 10-30 megapascal, separation kettle pressure 5-10 megapascal, and extraction time 1-4 hours. The mass ratio of the organic solvent and the tobacco leaf extract is (2-10): 1, and the temperature of the reduced-pressure concentration is 30-50 degrees C, and the vacuum degree is 0.080-0.090 megapascal. The molecular distillation times are 2-5 times, the molecular distillation temperature is 70-180 degrees C, and the vacuum degree is 1-100 pascal. The moisture content of the tobacco leaves is adjusted to 11-13%. Preferred Components: The organic solvent is any one of ethanol, ethyl acetate, and n-hexane.

PN TW592346-U
TI Nozzle device for injecting carbon dioxide particles, has outer cover connected to body and surrounding guide section and part of conical surface section of nozzle to define ring chamber communicating with channel.
AU XU W
   TSAO K J
   CHEN J
   LIU Q
AE HABOR PRECISION INC (HABO-Non-standard)
GA 202151421A
AB    NOVELTY - A nozzle device includes a body connected to an outer tube, a nozzle connected to the body, and a cover connected to the body and surrounding the nozzle and defining a ring chamber. The body includes a surrounding wall defining a channel. The channel is suitable for passing an inner tube. The surrounding wall has a hole communicating with the channel and the outside. The nozzle includes a large-diameter hole and a small-diameter hole connected to the channel, a tapered section connected to the end surface adjacent to the small-diameter hole, and a guide section connected to the end surface adjacent to the large-diameter hole. The large-diameter hole sleeves the inner tube. The small-diameter hole is used to cause the passing carbon dioxide gas to generate particles due to phase change. When the compressed gas enters the annular chamber from the hole, it will follow the conical section flow, and entrain the external air flow and particles to spray forward. In this way, the air flow restricted by the annular chamber can only flow along the conical section, which can not only increase the flow velocity, but also improve the collimation during injection.
