PN CN108424162-A; CN108424162-B
TI Producing ultra-high temperature ceramic modified carbon/carbon composite material, comprises e.g. washing carbon dioxide or low-density C/C composite, drying, injecting precursor solution, and wrapping in graphite paper.
AU LIU T
   FU Q
   LI H
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
   UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
GA 201866730A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ultra-high temperature ceramic modified carbon/carbon composite material, comprises (a) washing carbon dioxide or low-density C/C composite, and drying in a dry box, (b) injecting precursor solution containing mixed solution of 10-60 wt.% organic ceramic precursor and 40-90 wt.% organic solvent into region selected by carbon fiber preform or low density C/C composite using a syringe, placing in an infrared drying oven at 80-120 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain dried modified C/C composite material, (c) wrapping dried modified C/C composite material in graphite paper, placing in a graphite crucible and high temperature furnace, raising temperature of high temperature furnace to 1500-2100 degrees C at a heating rate of 2 degrees C/minute for 2-6 hours, lowering temperature to 1000 degrees C, naturally cooling to room temperature, and introducing high purity argon gas at a rate of 50 cm3/minute under atmospheric pressure of 1, and (d) densifying material by thermal gradient chemical vapor deposition.
   USE - The method is useful for producing ultra-high temperature ceramic modified carbon/carbon composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens producing period, reduces cost, and improves erosion-resistant performance of material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The injection rate using the syringe to inject the precursor solution is 0.1-1.5 ml/minute. The injection of the precursor solution in the step (b) is performed at a depth of 0.5-5 mm when the precursor solution is injected. Preferred Components: The carbon fiber preform is of 0.2-0.6 g/cm3. The low density C/C composite material is low density C/C composite material having density of 0.4-1.3 g/cm3. The organic ceramic precursor includes organic zirconium precursor, organic hafnium precursor, organic hafnium precursor, silicone precursor and/or organoboron zirconium precursor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is xylene, toluene, cyclohexane or kerosene.

   (B) subjecting the powder mixture to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction in an extraction vessel having a temperature of 35-45 degrees C, a pressure of 3-6 MPa, and a carbon dioxide flow rate of 24-28 L/hour, and extracting for 30-60 minutes to obtain an extract; and

AE UNIV HUNAN AGRIC (UYAG-C)
   UNIV HUNAN AGRIC (UYAG-C)
GA 201818150X
AB    NOVELTY - Conditioner comprises 40-55 pts. wt., preferably 45-50 pts. wt. carbon capturing plant material, 20-30 pts. wt., preferably 20-25 pts. wt. humic acid, 2-8 pts. wt., preferably 3-4 pts. wt. potassium silicate, 5-15 pts. wt., preferably 8-10 pts. wt. calcium-magnesium phosphate fertilizer, 1-5 pts. wt., preferably 2-3 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 5-15 pts. wt., preferably 5-10 pts. wt. slaked lime and 5-10 pts. wt. binder.
   USE - The conditioner is useful for reducing cadmium content in soil for cultivation of crops chosen from rice and melon (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The soil conditioner is cost-effective and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing conditioner involves (a) soaking the carbon capturing plant into zinc sulfate solution, drying, crushing and sieving, (b) separately sieving humic acid, calcium-magnesium phosphate fertilizer and slaked lime and uniformly mixing mixture obtained in step (a) and step (b), adding binder, uniformly mixing, making into dry materials, evenly spraying potassium silicate to the dried material, granulating and drying the granulated material; and
   (2) method for fixing carbon dioxide in air using carbon capturing plant involves planting carbon capturing plant to absorb carbon dioxide in air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The conditioner comprises 45 or 50 pts. wt. carbon capturing plant material, 20 or 25 pts. wt. humic acid, 4 or 6 pts. wt. potassium silicate, 5 or 8 pts. wt. calcium-magnesium phosphate fertilizer, 2 or 3 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 5 or 7 pts. wt. slaked lime and 10 pts. wt. binder. The soil conditioner further comprises 10 pts. wt. modified cassava starch or charcoal carbon. Preferred Components: The carbon capturing plant comprises one or more chosen from Miscanthus, Pennisetum, reed, bamboo reed, switchgrass and elephant grass. The Miscanthus is chosen from Miscanthus sacchariflorus, Miscanthus giganteus and hybrid varieties of Miscanthus (Xiangnandi 1-3 and Xiangzamang 1-3). The carbon capturing plant material comprises N content of 20-40 g/kg, phosphorus pentoxide content of 10-15 g/kg and potassium oxide content of 15-25 g/kg. The carbon capturing plant material has pH value of 9-11.3, diameter of 2-9 mu m and surface area of 80-200 m2/g. Preferred Method: The carbon capturing plant material is prepared by crushing carbon capturing plant initially, performing oxygen-limited pyrolysis at 400-500 degrees C for 2-3 hours, adding water as gasification agent, oxidizing at 500-600 degrees C and reducing to 600-800 degrees C.
   ADMINISTRATION - The soil conditioner is applied at a rate of 300-500 kg/acre (claimed).

PT P
PN CN107674116-A; CN107674116-B
TI Purifying bovine pneumocandin B0, involves filtering pneumocandin fermentation liquid, extracting mushroom residue, filtering, adding adsorbent to filtrate, extracting, crystallizing extract, separating, and collecting product.
AU HE Y
   YUE G
   ZHANG K
AE UNIV PEKING FOUNDER GROUP CORP (BFEB-C)
   CHONGQING DAXIN PHARM CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
   PKU HEALTHCARE IND GROUP CO LTD (BFEB-C)
   UNIV PEKING FOUNDER GROUP CO LTD (BFEB-C)
GA 201814513W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for purifying bovine pneumocandin B0, involves filtering pneumocandin fermentation liquid, taking mushroom residue and alcoholic solvent, extracting, subjecting to membrane filtration, collecting filtrate, adding adsorbent to the filtrate to enrich pneumocandin B0 on the adsorbent, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, obtaining extract, crystallizing the extract to obtain crystal containing pneumocandin B0, performing column chromatography separation, and collecting pneumocandin B0 enriched effluent.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying bovine pneumocandin B0 (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates pneumocandin B0 and pneumocandin C0, enables to obtain purified pneumocandin B0 stably with good quality and HPLC purity of more than 99.4% and pneumocandin C0 isomer purity of as low as 0.01-0.04%, and is performed simply.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for purifying bovine pneumocandin B0, involves (a) filtering pneumocandin fermentation liquid, taking mushroom residue and alcoholic solvent as extracting agent, extracting the residue, subjecting the obtained extract to pass through membrane to perform membrane filtration and collecting filtrate, (b) adding adsorbent to the filtrate to enrich pneumocandin B0 on the adsorbent, (c) subjecting the enriched adsorbent to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, obtaining extract and crystallizing the extract to obtain crystal containing pneumocandin B0, and (d) using hydrophilic interaction chromatography (HILIC) mode reversed-phase bonded silica gel as stationary phase, and aqueous ethanol as mobile phase to perform column chromatography separation, and collecting pneumocandin B0 enriched effluent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The alcoholic solvent is 70-80% aqueous methanol solution or 60-70% aqueous ethanol solution. The filtration membrane is chosen from ultrafiltration membrane and nanofiltration membrane with pore size of 4500-5500 Dalton and 900-980 Dalton, respectively. The adsorbent is chosen from diatomite and activated aluminum oxide which is added in an amount of 5-6 times with respect to total weight of the filtrate. Preferred Method: The step (b) involves before adding the adsorbent, removing the water in the filtrate, by replacing the water in the filtrate by nanofiltration with methanol or ethanol as a displacer, where the concentration of methanol or ethanol in the resulting solution is replaced by more than 95%, and the content of pneumocandin B0 is 200-250 g/l. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed by taking ethanol as entraining agent, extracting at 35-40 degrees C under pressure of 19-20 MPa, discarding extract, taking the remaining solids after extraction by adding entrainer, and extracting at 45-50 degrees C under pressure of 29-30 MPa to obtain ethanol extract containing pneumocandin B0, where the ratio of the absorbent and the entrainer is 100 g:100-150 ml, and the ratio of the solids and the entrainer is 100 g:180-220 ml. The step (c) involves crystallizing using crystallizing agent chosen from acetonitrile, acetone and ethyl acetate. The step (d) involves using 40-50% aqueous ethanol solution as mobile phase. The step (d) further involves performing post-treatment of the effluent to obtain solid pneumocandin B0, by nano-filtering the effluent using membrane with pore size of 900-980 Dalton, freeze-drying the obtained concentrated liquid or crystallizing to obtain product.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN115044544-A
TI Resuscitating dental pulp mesenchymal stem cells            involves taking out cryopreserved dental pulp            mesenchymal stem cells from liquid nitrogen, thawing in            water bath, adding resuscitation solution for dilution,            centrifuging, collecting supernatant cell precipitate,            recovering stem cells.
AU FANG X
   ZHANG J
AE ZHANG J (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2022C0584V
AB    NOVELTY - Resuscitating dental pulp mesenchymal stem                cells involves taking out cryopreserved dental pulp                mesenchymal stem cells from liquid nitrogen,                thawing in water bath at 37-40 &#8451; by                ultrasonic oscillation. Resuscitation solution is                added to dilute and centrifuge, the lower layer of                cell pellet is removed, resuscitation solution is                added to resuspend, let stand at 37 &#8451;, 5%                carbon dioxide for 10-12 hours, and the recovery is                completed. The composition of the resuscitation                fluid is base fluid, lecithin, muscone,                irisin.
   USE - Method for resuscitating dental pulp                mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables solving the problems of low                cell activity and poor proliferation ability after                cryopreservation, having good cell activity that                helps to improve the application effect of dental                pulp mesenchymal stem cells, lecithin, muskone and                irisin in the resuscitation fluid acting                synergistically to increase the recovery rate of                the frozen dental pulp mesenchymal stem cells,                maintaining good proliferative activity, and the                revived dental pulp mesenchymal stem cells are                directly used for conventional expansion and                culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The base fluid is                  &#945;-minimum essential medium. The density                  of dental pulp mesenchymal stem cells in the                  resuscitation fluid is 1-4&#215;105cells/mL.                  The resuscitation solution is preheated to 37                  &#8451;. and then added to thawed cells for                  dilution. The cryopreserved dental pulp                  mesenchymal stem cells are P1-P7 generation cells                  obtained by subculture.Preferred Composition: The resuscitating                  solution comprises 70-80 pts. wt. base solution,                  2.2-4.5 pts. wt. lecithin, 0.5-1 pts. wt. muscone                  and 1.8-3.1 pts. wt. irisin.

PN CN114702410-A
TI Preparation of guanidinoacetic acid by mixing            calcium cyanamide and water, separating solid-liquid,            mixing obtained cyanamide aqueous solution and glycine            aqueous solution, mixing obtained guanidinoacetic acid            crude product and water, stirring and separating            solid-liquid and drying.
AU LV M
AE HANGZHOU FENGHE BIO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2022911713
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of guanidinoacetic acid                involves (i) mixing calcium cyanamide and water for                the first reaction, continuously introducing carbon                dioxide gas, and carrying out solid-liquid                separation after the reaction to obtain a cyanamide                aqueous solution and the pH value of the first                reaction is 5-6, (ii) mixing the cyanamide aqueous                solution and glycine aqueous solution to carry out                a second reaction to obtain a guanidinoacetic acid                crude product and the pH value of the second                reaction is 7-8 and the temperature is                65-75&#8451; and (iii) mixing the                guanidinoacetic acid crude product and water,                stirring and carrying out solid-liquid separation                and drying the obtained solid product to obtain                guanidinoacetic acid.
   USE - As preparation method of guanidinoacetic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method yields over 88% of 99.3% high                purity guanidinoacetic acid, where the process                steps are simple, the raw materials are simple and                easy to obtain, the preparation cost is low, and                the industrial production is easy, and has obvious                pharmaceutical market competitiveness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the                  calcium cyanamide to water is 1:(2-2.5). The                  molar ratio of cyanamide in the cyanamide aqueous                  solution to glycine in the glycine aqueous                  solution is 1:(0.99-1). The mass ratio of the                  guanidinoacetic acid crude product to water is                  1:2-5.Preferred Method: The second reaction                  involves dripping cyanamide aqueous solution into                  glycine water solution at 20-30&#8451;.Preferred Conditions: The temperature of                  first reaction is room temperature. The dripping                  time is 20-45 minutes. The second reaction is                  carried out for 2-5 hours. The stirring is                  carried out at 85-90&#8451; for 1-2                  hours.Preferred Components: The reagent used for                  adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution of                  first reaction is sulfuric acid solution or                  phosphoric acid solution. The reagent used for                  adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution of                  second reaction is an aqueous sodium bicarbonate                  solution.

PN IN202021044827-A
TI Preparing monohydrated calcium nitrate used as fertilizer includes mixing calcium hydroxide in water, slowly adding nitric acid, reacting with nitric acid, separating unreacted calcium hydroxide and drying to obtain crystalline form product.
AU VALODKAR M C
   OTHAYOTH S P
   JOSHI A B
   VAISHNAV P B
AE GUJARAT STATE FERTILIZERS & CHEM LTD (GUJA-Non-standard)
GA 202257632C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing monohydrated calcium nitrate comprises (a) mixing a predetermined amount of calcium hydroxide in a predetermined amount of water to obtain a suspension, (b) slowly adding nitric acid to the suspension and reacting the suspension with nitric acid at a first predetermined temperature for a predetermined time period to obtain a reaction mixture comprising calcium nitrate in a solution form and an un-reacted calcium hydroxide, (c) separating the unreacted calcium hydroxide to obtain a product mixture comprising calcium nitrate, and (d) drying the product mixture at a second predetermined temperature to obtain monohydrated calcium nitrate in a crystalline form.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing monohydrated calcium nitrate which is used as a fertilizer (all claimed) for tomato crop.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe, simple, requires less energy consumption, and is environment friendly (does not produce greenhouse emissions (carbon dioxide)), uses reactants which are recyclable and hence, the process is cost-effective. The monohydrated calcium nitrate has enhanced shelf life. The monohydrated calcium nitrate increases growth and yield of the crops. Test details are described but no results given.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first predetermined temperature is 60-90 degrees C and second predetermined temperature is 110-140 degrees C. The slow addition of the nitric acid into the suspension is carried out for 30-180 minutes. The predetermined time period is 3-5 hours. The drying in the step (d) is carried out by using a spray dryer or rotary dryer. Preferred Components: The monohydrated calcium nitrate in a crystalline form is granulated into an extruder-granulator to obtain a granulated monohydrated calcium nitrate. The calcium hydroxide has a purity of 85-95%. The nitric acid is a spent nitric acid. The concentration of nitric acid is 15-65%. The predetermined amount of calcium hydroxide is 5-30 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the raw material. The predetermined amount of water is 10-20 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the raw material and predetermined amount of nitric acid is 70-95 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the raw material. The ratio of calcium hydroxide to nitric acid is 1:4-1:8. The monohydrated calcium nitrate: has a total nitrogen content of 10-20 wt.%, a nitrate nitrogen content of 10-20 wt.%, a water soluble calcium content of 15-30 wt.% and an insoluble matter content of 0-0.2 wt.%; has a crushing strength of 1-5 kg/granule.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Application of the monohydrated calcium nitrate is directly into the soil and to crops in a solid form or in water soluble form. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - 80 g Calcium hydroxide (90% pure) was added in 80 g water to obtain a suspension of calcium hydroxide. 500 g nitric acid having a concentration of 17-20% was added slowly to the suspension over a period of 1 hour followed by reaction at 80 degrees C for 4 hours and a reaction mixture comprising calcium nitrate in a solution form and an un-reacted calcium hydroxide were obtained. The reaction mixture was filtered to remove the un-reacted calcium hydroxide to obtain a product mixture comprising calcium nitrate in a solution form. The separated unreacted calcium hydroxide was reused further. The product mixture comprising calcium nitrate solution was spray dried at 130 degrees C to and monohydrated calcium nitrate in a crystalline form was obtained. The calcium nitrate monohydrated powder already containing one water molecule of crystallization was processed to an extruder-granulator and granulated monohydrated calcium nitrate was obtained.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Preparing rutile titanium oxide and dry ice by using chlorination process titanium white process waste gas in production of titanium dioxide mainstream process, involves reacting titanium white production process waste gas.
AU LU P
   LI D
   DU M
   ZHOU A
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 202216230B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rutile titanium oxide and dry ice by using chlorination process titanium white process waste gas involves reacting titanium white production process waste gas and oxidation process waste gas under the condition of the crystal conversion agent with thermal oxygen reaction, separating the reaction product to obtain rutile titanium dioxide particles, and the separated gas to spray to obtain diluted hydrochloric acid solution. The gas after spraying treatment is made into dry ice.
   USE - Method for preparing rutile titanium oxide and dry ice by using chlorination process titanium white process waste gas used in the production of titanium dioxide mainstream process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively recycles the waste gas produced by chlorination process titanium white industrial production, obtains hydrochloric acid solution, rutile titanium dioxide and dry ice, and controls the greenhouse gas emission generated in the chlorination of titanium white process production, meets the national requirement of carbon emission peak and carbon neutralization standard, and solves the problem of recycling of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in exhaust gas discharged by chlorine white production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The waste gas of the chlorination process and the oxidation process in the process of titanium white production are reacted with the thermal oxygen under the condition of the crystal conversion agent includes introducing the waste gas of the chlorination process and the waste gas of the oxidation process into the oxidation reactor, introducing the hot oxygen into the oxidation reactor, adding the crystal conversion agent into the oxidation reactor, the chlorination process waste gas and the oxidation process waste gas and the hot oxygen at 1550-1750degrees Celsius under the temperature condition of oxidation reaction, the reaction product in the rutile titanium dioxide particles, gaseous carbon dioxide and gaseous chlorine. The reaction product is separated to obtain rutile titanium dioxide includes sending the reaction product into the gas-solid separation bag filter, separating the rutile titanium dioxide particles, and gaseous carbon dioxide and gaseous chlorine. The separated gas is sprayed to obtain diluted hydrochloric acid solution includes introducing the separated gaseous carbon dioxide and gaseous chlorine into the leaching tower, spraying water in the leaching tower. The gaseous chlorine is absorbed to form diluted hydrochloric acid solution, the gaseous carbon dioxide is discharged out of the leaching tower and separated the diluted hydrochloric acid solution. The gas after spraying treatment is made into dry ice includes introducing the gaseous carbon dioxide discharged after spraying in the leaching tower into the dry ice generator to prepare dry ice. The hot oxygen is the oxygen preheated to 850-950degrees Celsius. The oxidation reactor is electromagnetic induction oxidation reactor. The method involves adding spraying water and diluted hydrochloric acid in leaching tower to absorb gaseous chlorine gas. Preferred Components: The waste gas of chlorination process comprises carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, titanium tetrachloride and chlorine. The waste gas of oxidation process comprises chlorine gas and carbon dioxide.

PN CN113812420-A
TI Rapidly preparing boron nitride/carbon            nitride/silver single-atom antibacterial agent            comprises e.g. grinding and crushing two-dimensional            boron nitride/carbon nitride nano heterojunction,            adding ionized water and silver salt, stirring, placing            under irradiation of xenon lamp, reacting and            drying.
AU DENG X
AE SHANGHAI TENGLING HVAC EQUIP ENG CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202210180X
AB    NOVELTY - Rapidly preparing boron nitride/carbon                nitride/silver single-atom antibacterial agent                comprises (i) dissolving nitrogen source, boron                source and carbon source into solvent, placing into                supercritical reaction kettle, introducing carbon                dioxide gas, maintaining pressure, vacuum                extracting, preparing boron nitride/carbon nitride                heterojunction precursor by supercritical fluid                rapid expansion method, drying and placing into a                ceramic crucible and covering cover, placing into a                muffle furnace and heating, maintaining the                temperature for roasting, naturally cooling to                obtain two-dimensional boron nitride/carbon nitride                nano-heterojunction; and (ii) grinding and crushing                two-dimensional boron nitride/carbon nitride nano                heterojunction, adding ionized water and silver                salt, stirring in the dark, placing under the                irradiation of a xenon lamp, and reacting under                stirring condition, filtering, washing and                drying.
   USE - The method is useful for rapidly preparing                boron nitride/carbon nitride/silver single-atom                antibacterial agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate. The                antibacterial agent has high antibacterial and                antiviral efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen source                  comprises melamine, cyanuric acid, dicyandiamide,                  cyanamide, urea and/or melamine cyanurate. The                  boron source comprises boric acid, sodium borate,                  borane amine and/or borax. The carbon source                  comprises chitosan, butanediol, sucrose,                  polystyrene and/or phenolic resin. The solvent                  comprises water, ethanol, glycol, glycerol and/or                  acetone. In the step (i), the mass ratio of                  nitrogen source and boron source is 1:0.01-100,                  the mass ratio of nitrogen source and carbon                  source is 1:0.01-100 and the mass ratio of                  nitrogen source to solvent is 1:1-10000. In the                  step (ii), the mass ratio of the two-dimensional                  boron nitride/carbon nitride nanoheterojunction                  to deionized water is 1:1-10000 and the mass                  ratio of the two-dimensional boron nitride/carbon                  nitride nanoheterojunction to silver salt is                  1:0.001-100. Preferred Method: In the step (i),                  the temperature of the supercritical reaction                  kettle is 20-100degrees Celsius, the pressure is                  20-400 bar, and the pressure-holding time is 2-60                  hours. In the step (i), the heating rate of the                  muffle furnace heating is 1-20degrees                  Celsius/minute, the temperature is heated to                  500-750degrees Celsius, and the heat preservation                  time is 0.1-15 hours. In the step (ii), the                  stirring is carried out in the dark for 2-72                  hours and reacting for 0.1-12 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Frostbite ointment composition, comprises antipruritic agent, traditional Chinese medicine ointment including safflower, hawthorn, golden cypress, mirabilite, orange peel, comfrey, Rehmannia glutinosa, and red pepper, and emu oil.
   USE - Used as frostbite ointment composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has good therapeutic effect and shortens the treatment cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the ointment composition, comprising (i) crushing safflower, hawthorn, golden cypress, mirabilite, orange peel, lithospermum, Rehmannia glutinosa, red pepper, drying and uniformly mixing the powder, (ii) mixing the mixed powder obtained in the step (i) and the ethanol solution according to mass ratio of 1: (4-8), extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide, collecting the extracting liquid, distilling and recycling ethanol, and collecting the residue, (iii) washing the remainder using deionized water and drying, (iv) evaporating and concentrating the extracting solution to 1/3 of the original volume, (v) mixing and stirring the dried residue and the concentrated extracting solution according to the mass ratio of 3: 1 to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine ointment, and (vi) mixing antipruritic agent, traditional Chinese medicine ointment and emu oil uniformly and sterilizing to obtain chilblain cream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 1.5-3 wt.% antipruritic agent, 10-30 wt.%, preferably 15-20 wt.% traditional Chinese medicine ointment and emu oil (remaining amount). Preferred Components: The antipruritic agent comprises fructus cnidii, radix sophorae flavescentis, shrubalthea bark and menthol in a ratio of 4:4:2:1; or lithargite, Bletilla striata and dried alum in a ratio of 30:6:4:1. In the step (v), the moisture content of the remainder is less than 5%. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the antipruritic agent is as follows: grinding the fructus cnidii, radix sophorae flavescentis, and shrubalthea bark into coarse powder, mixing, soaking the mixed coarse powder in 10 pts. wt. ethanol solution, allowing to stand for 4-8 hours, adding the rest ethanol solution into the solution by diacolation and adding method to obtain tincture, and adding the menthol into the tincture to obtain the antipruritic agent. The preparation method of the antipruritic agent is as follows: grinding the lithargite, Bletilla striata, and dried alum to grain diameter less than 1000 meshes, and uniformly mixing to obtain the antipruritic agent.
   ACTIVITY - Cardiovascular-Gen.

PT P
PN US2021197180-A1; TW202124044-A; TW739254-B1
TI Catalyst used to converting carbon oxide into            methanol, comprises copper, zinc, aluminum, silicon,            and metal comprising palladium or gold and palladium            and gold are based on total weight of copper, zinc,            aluminum and silicon in catalyst.
AU CHENG N
   CHEN Y
   LO M
AE IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
   IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
GA 202176220J
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises: 40-60 pts. wt. copper;                25-40 pts. wt. zinc; 2-15 pts. wt. aluminum; 0.1-3                pts. wt. silicon; and a metal, where the metal                comprises palladium or gold, and the palladium and                the gold are 0.1-5 wt.% based on total weight of                copper, zinc, aluminum, and silicon in the                catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbon                oxide into methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provide catalyst for converting                carbon oxide into methanol and process of utilizing                catalyst for converting carbon oxide into methanol,                which may effectively increase conversion rate from                carbon dioxide to methanol at lower reaction                temperature, thus improving overall efficiency of                process and reducing energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                converting carbon oxide into methanol, comprising                putting the catalyst into a fixed bed reactor and                introducing a gas mixture of hydrogen and the                carbon oxide into the fixed bed reactor, and                performing a hydrogenation reaction under the                effect of the catalyst to form the methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrogenation reaction                  is performed at a gas hourly space velocity of                  3600-20000/hour. The hydrogenation reaction is                  performed at a temperature of 180-250degrees                  Celsius. The hydrogenation reaction is performed                  under a pressure of 30-80 kg/cm2.Preferred Components: The carbon oxide                  comprises carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide.                  The palladium and the gold are 0.25-1 wt.% based                  on the total weight of copper, zinc, aluminum,                  and silicon in the catalyst. The carbon oxide                  comprises carbon dioxide. The hydrogenation                  reaction further forms carbon monoxide and water.                  The molar ratio of the hydrogen to the carbon                  oxide is 3/1-10/1.
   EXAMPLE - 54.3 g of Copper(II) nitrate, 39.1 g of zinc                nitrate, 6.6 g aluminum nitrate, and 0.95 g of                sodium silicate were dissolved in 1500 ml of                de-ionized water to form Liquid A. 140 g of                Bicarbonate of soda was dissolved in 500 ml of                de-ionized water to form liquid B1. Liquid A1 and                liquid B1 were added into 400 g of de-ionized water                with constant stirring at an addition rate of 10                ml/minutes. After stirring for 24 hours, the                mixture was filtered and a filter cake was                obtained. Then, the filter cake was washed with                de-ionized water several times to remove the sodium                ions. The filter cake was baked at 110degrees                Celsius and then calcinated at 600degrees Celsius                to obtain catalyst precursor I. Catalyst precursor                I was cooled down to room temperature, and 10 g of                catalyst precursor I was put into a pear-shaped                bottle. A solution of 0.211 g of palladium(II)                acetate was added to the pear-shaped bottle. The                mixture was then dried in a rotary evaporator and                then baked at 110degrees Celsius to obtain                copper-zinc- aluminum-silicon-palladium catalyst I                with a palladium content of 1 wt.%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN113004585-A; CN113004585-B
TI Natural latex foaming process comprises e.g.            preparing latex raw material, stirring,            pre-vulcanizing, injecting latex mixture into foaming            machine, molding, vacuumizing, freezing, cleaning,            washing ultrasonic wave hot water and drying.
AU BEI Z
AE ZHEJIANG PUNALA TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202176034W
AB    NOVELTY - Natural latex foaming process comprises (1)                preparing the latex raw material, stirring                uniformly, pre-vulcanizing, (2) injecting the latex                mixture into the foaming machine, foaming to obtain                foam latex, (3) injecting the foam latex into the                mold, vacuumizing, freezing treatment, (4)                injecting carbon dioxide into the mold, (5)                vulcanizing by heating the mold to form the foaming                latex, (6) cleaning by opening the mold, taking out                the latex, using ultrasonic wave hot water to wash,                (7) adding the cleaned latex into a drying box to                dry.
   USE - The natural latex foaming process is useful in                one-time molding natural latex bra cup pad.
   ADVANTAGE - The natural latex foaming process reduces the                chemical residue in the latex by physical foaming,                and improves the uniformity, so that the latex                product has more elastic supporting effect and                ventilation property, and it is more more suitable                for personal use latex products e.g. latex                brasiers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                one-time molding natural latex bra cup pad                comprising foaming and molding by the latex foaming                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The latex raw                  material comprises 300-320 pts. wt. natural                  latex, 5-10 pts. wt. sulfur, 5-10 pts. wt.                  accelerant, 8-15 pts. wt. plant oleic acid, 4-10                  pts. wt. castor oil, 10-15 pts. wt. zinc oxide,                  3-15 pts. wt. potassium pyrophosphate and 3-25                  pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide. Preferred                  Components: The accelerant comprises 25-40 pts.                  wt. accelerant MZ, 25-40 accelerant (Zinc                  diethyldithiocarbamate) ZDC and 20-40 accelerant                  DPG (diphenyl guanidine). The foaming machine                  comprises a foaming tank and a driving device.                  The stirring shaft and a stirring blade, the                  stirring shaft is rotatably set in the foaming                  tank and driven to rotate by the driving device;                  the stirring blade is fixedly connected to the                  periphery of the stirring shaft, the stirring                  paddle is fixedly connected with multiple                  stirring rods for stirring the latex raw material                  in the foaming tank. The inner part of the                  foaming tank is provided with a foaming plate.                  The foaming plate is connected with the external                  air source, for injecting bubbles into the                  foaming tank. Preferred Method: In the step (2),                  when foaming, injecting carbon dioxide gas into                  the latex mixture, and at the same time the                  foaming machine is used to disperse the latex                  mixture to form a foam. In the step (3),                  injection molding, controlling vacuum pressure                  above 4 kg, controlling freezing temperature is                  -25 degrees C to -35 degrees C, and maintaining                  temperature at 10 degrees C for 30 minutes, make                  the foam latex condense and immobilizing. In the                  step (4), when the gel is gelled, the air                  pressure is controlled to be 1-3 kg, and the                  injection time is 5-10 minutes. In the step (5),                  vulcanization temperature is heated to 100-120                  degrees C for 20-120 minutes and heat transfer                  oil is used for vulcanization. In the step (5),                  vulcanizing, heating mold to 50 degrees C and                  maintaining for 6-20 minutes to preheat the latex                  product and maintaining temperature at 100-120                  degrees C for 10-80 minutes.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.); P21 (Wearing apparel (A41, A42).); V06 (Electromechanical Transducers and Small Machines)

PT P
PN CN112864014-A
TI Reducing reverse leakage of sapphire-based gallium            nitride quasi-vertical Schottky diode, involves            preparing metal nickel pickling film on gallium nitride            sample by photoetching technique.
AU GENG L
   LIU J
   YANG M
   LIU W
   HAO Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021630865
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing reverse leakage of sapphire-based                gallium nitride quasi-vertical Schottky diode                comprises preparing metal nickel pickling film on                gallium nitride sample by photoetching technology,                using induction coupling plasma dry etching process                to etch gallium nitride sample until n+ gallium                nitride layer, evaporating                titanium/aluminum/nickel/gold metal layer on etched                n+ gallium nitride sample by combining electron                beam process, and performing rapid thermal                annealing treatment under high temperature nitrogen                environment to form ohmic contact using                photoetching and electron beam to finish                preparation of nickel/gold Schottky contact on                n-gallium nitride table surface, using wet etching                process for cathode opening window, leakage                electrode, and putting gallium nitride sample into                supercritical reaction kettle, adding isopropanol                and de-ionized water after sealing to react to                obtain sapphire-based gallium nitride                quasi-vertical Schottky diode based on                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   USE - Method for reducing reverse leakage of a                sapphire-based gallium nitride (GaN) quasi-vertical                schottky diode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve the reverse leakage of                the sapphire-based GaN quasi-vertical Schottky                diode, and realize the obvious reduction of the                reverse leakage of the diode.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)
